ref: After Jesus had finished instructing his twelve disciples, he left and began teaching and preaching in the towns.
text: Yeesuu a sa se̱ŋsa gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱ kudu anyɔ‑ɔ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ yɔ e̱maŋ ne̱ e̱ maa mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ na ɔ ya kaapo̱ bamo̱ atɔ, na ɔ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ gywii bamo̱.

ref: God will bless everyone who doesn't reject me because of what I do.”
text: Ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ maa yɔwe̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱ mo̱ gyi‑o e̱ nya ŋyure.”

ref: What kind of man did you go out to see? Was he someone dressed in fine clothes? People who dress like that live in the king's palace.
text: Nte̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a le̱e̱ ya de̱e̱re̱? Se̱sɛ ne̱ o buŋ waagya timaa‑o ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a yɔ ya de̱e̱re̱ aaa? Ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ buŋ ane̱ŋ a atɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ bamo̱ kakye̱na‑rɔ bo̱ kɔne̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ atɔ awuye ne̱ bo̱ te e̱lɔŋ dame̱naŋsɛ dabe̱ dabe̱‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: What did you really go out to see? Was he a prophet? He certainly was. I tell you that he was more than a prophet.
text: Mo̱nꞌ tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱, nsɛ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a yɔ a mo̱nꞌ ya de̱e̱re̱? Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ aaa? Kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱e̱, amaa n gye̱ Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ‑ɔ wo̱re̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a ŋu. Mo̱ne̱ a ŋu ɔko̱ na ɔ kyɔ bo̱ tii si.

ref: I tell you no one ever born on this earth is greater than John the Baptist. But whoever is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than John.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ bo̱ tii si fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ se̱sɛ ko̱ mo̱ŋ kye̱na swe̱e̱re̱ mɔ se̱ na ɔ kyɔ Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱‑ɔ, amaa mò̱ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ kagyingyii Wuribware̱ se̱ a kuwure‑o‑ro‑o kyɔ Yohanee.

ref: From the time of John the Baptist until now, violent people have been trying to take over the kingdom of heaven by force.
text: Le̱e̱ saŋ ne̱ Yohanee a tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ lo̱we̱‑ɔ bo̱ fo̱ ndɔɔ, Wuribware̱ se̱ a kuwure‑o‑ro a ba ne̱ kaa daŋ waa ko̱kyɔ, ne̱ ɔke̱maa i neŋ mò̱ bɛɛko̱ ɔ le̱e̱‑rɔ, na ɔ nya nya kpa a ɔ bo̱ tii si.

ref: “We played the flute, but you would not dance! We sang a funeral song, but you would not mourn!”
text: ‘Ane̱ a da ko̱ko̱fɔ‑rɔ akɔye̱ sa mo̱ne̱, amaa mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ kyaa ooo. Ne̱ ane̱ a be̱e̱ waa kale, amaa mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ su ooo.’

ref: But the Son of Man goes around eating and drinking, and you say, “That man eats and drinks too much! He is even a friend of tax collectors and sinners.” Yet Wisdom is shown to be right by what it does. The Unbelieving Towns (Luke
text: Amaa saŋ ne̱ mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, a ba‑ɔ, mo̱ a gyi, ne̱ mo̱ a nuu, ne̱ bo̱ be̱e̱ bo̱ yɛ, ‘Mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱ ɔnyare̱ amo̱, ɔ gye̱ oteesekpapo̱, na ɔtanuupo̱, na leŋpoo akɔɔre̱po̱ bamo̱ nyare̱, na e̱bɔye̱ awaapo̱ bamo̱ nyare̱ ne̱e̱.’ Amaa fo̱ ya ŋu ɔko̱ ne̱ mò̱ e̱ gya Wuribware̱ a kanyiase̱ŋ a e̱kpa‑ɔ se̱, fo̱ i ŋu fe̱yɛ mò̱ kanyiase̱ŋ‑ɔ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ.” Ke̱se̱bɔgyiiri ne̱ e̱maŋ awuye ne̱ baa kine Yeesuu‑o ye̱re̱ bo̱ sa‑ɔ (Lukas

ref: In the towns where Jesus had worked most of his miracles, the people refused to turn to God. So Jesus was upset with them and said:
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu akatɔ mo̱ŋ gyi mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ Isireelii awuye bo̱ko̱ se̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔɔ waa akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ bamo̱ e̱maŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ nu bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ kiŋŋi le̱e̱ bamo̱ e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ.

ref: So I tell you on the day of judgment the people of Sodom will get off easier than you. Come to Me and Rest (Luke
text: Mo̱ i yii mo̱ne̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ kake ne̱ Wuribware̱ i gyi ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ, o gyiiri mo̱ne̱ Kapaaniyum awuye ke̱se̱bɔ a ɔ kyo̱ŋ Sɔdɔm awuye‑o.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ i yure awo̱rɔba na bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ (Lukas

ref: At that moment Jesus said: My Father, Lord of heaven and earth, I am grateful that you hid all this from wise and educated people and showed it to ordinary people.
text: Saŋ amo̱‑ɔ, Yeesuu yɛ, “N se̱, Wuribware̱, so̱so̱ na swe̱e̱re̱ Owure, mo̱ e̱ sa fo̱ aŋsɛ fe̱yɛ fo̱ a taa fo̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o bo̱ kwe̱e̱rɔ anyiase̱ŋpo̱ se̱, ne̱ fo̱ a taa bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ.

ref: My Father has given me everything, and he is the only one who knows the Son. The only one who truly knows the Father is the Son. But the Son wants to tell others about the Father, so they can know him too.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “N se̱ a taa ke̱tɔ ke̱maa bo̱ waa mo̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ. Ɔke̱maa mɔ mo̱ŋ nyi ane̱ŋ ne̱ n du‑o, amɔ n se̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ wo̱re̱, ne̱ ɔke̱maa mɔ mo̱ŋ nyi ane̱ŋ ne̱ n se̱ du‑o, amɔ mo̱ aa bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ du fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ a lee fe̱yɛ bo̱ gyii mò̱‑ɔ wo̱re̱ kpeŋ.”

ref: Some Pharisees noticed this and said to Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do this!”
text: Farisii awuye ko̱ a ŋu amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ gywii Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Ke̱e̱ ɛ, fo̱ agyase̱po̱ a ke̱gyɛɛ abwaye fee‑o gye̱ kusuŋ. Ne̱ a kye ane̱ mbraa fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ a waa kusuŋ ko̱ kukyure kake.”

ref: But when I force out demons by the power of God's Spirit, it proves that God's kingdom has already come to you.
text: Amaa amo̱ a gye̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o ke̱yaale̱ŋ ne̱ n de mo̱ e̱ gya ibrisi‑o si‑o, a kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kuwure‑o a kyɔ ba mo̱ne̱ ase̱.

ref: Haven't you read in the Law of Moses that the priests are allowed to work in the temple on the Sabbath? But no one says they are guilty of breaking the law of the Sabbath.
text: Bɛɛɛ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ kare̱ ane̱ mbraa a wo̱re̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ kukyure kake ke̱maa Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ i suŋ bamo̱ ke̱bware̱suŋ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa aaa? Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ ba kye mbraa ne̱ ŋ yɛ ane̱ kyure kukyure kake‑o, bɛɛɛ? Na ɔko̱ ma taare̱ taa ke̱mo̱ fe̱yɛ ba waa bɔye̱, bɛɛɛ?

ref: Don't you know what the Scriptures mean when they say, “Instead of offering sacrifices to me, I want you to be merciful to others?” If you knew what this means, you would not condemn these innocent disciples of mine.
text: Mo̱nꞌ nyiŋŋi si ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Wuribware̱ yɛ, N gye̱ mbo̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa a mo̱nꞌ mɔɔ sa mo̱. Amaa ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa a mo̱nꞌ waa mo̱ne̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana.’ Mo̱ne̱ dɛɛ mo̱ne̱ i nu amo̱‑ɔ kaase̱ kyɛɛkyɛɛ ne̱e̱‑ɔ, weetee mo̱ne̱ maa po̱rɔ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ waa e̱bɔye̱‑ɔ.

ref: Jesus left and went into one of their synagogues,
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ ya lwee ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ ko̱ ne̱ ke̱ maa mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ.

ref: Then Jesus told the man, “Hold out your hand.” The man did, and it became as healthy as the other one.
text: Ne̱ ŋkee ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii ke̱sare̱e̱ wusɛ wuye‑o fe̱yɛ, “Teyi fo̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ.” Mò̱ a teyi‑ro‑o, ne̱ kaa kpaare̱ nya ke̱yaale̱ŋ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ko̱ko̱‑ɔ du‑o.

ref: When Jesus found out what was happening, he left there and large crowds followed him. He healed all of their sick,
text: Yeesuu a nu kikpuni ne̱ baa kpa a bo̱ da mò̱ kuŋu si‑o ase̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ, ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ ɔ naa ifuri baŋbaŋ se̱. Ne̱ ase̱sɛ biribiri a gya mò̱ se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ kya bamo̱ alɔpo̱ pɛɛɛ.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as Isaiah the prophet had said,
text: Ɔɔ waa amo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱ na ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ bo̱ bo̱‑rɔ mò̱ kyaamɛɛ Isaya si‑o nya ba kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ,

ref: “Here is my chosen servant! I love him, and he pleases me. I will give him my Spirit, and he will bring justice to the nations.
text: “Wuribware̱ yɛ, Mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱ mo̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱ ne̱ mo̱ a lee‑o, mò̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa mò̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ akatɔ a gyi mò̱ se̱‑ɔ. N de mo̱ kufwiiŋe‑o mo̱ e̱ waa mò̱‑rɔ. Mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ baa kase̱ŋtiŋ kigyi nde̱ se̱ awuye pɛɛɛ se̱.

ref: If you are not on my side, you are against me. If you don't gather in the harvest with me, you scatter it.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱ i yii mo̱ne̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ ye̱re̱ mo̱ kamɛɛ‑ɔ gye̱ mo̱ do̱ŋ ne̱e̱. Ne̱ ɔke̱maa mɔ ne̱ ɔ maa kya mo̱‑rɔ na ŋ kpɔwe̱ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ bo̱ tii mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ se̱‑ɔ mɔ e̱ brawe̱ bamo̱‑rɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: Some people brought to Jesus a man who was blind and could not talk because he had a demon in him. Jesus healed the man, and then he was able to talk and see.
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ ko̱ a baa ɔnyare̱ ko̱ Yeesuu ase̱. Ɔnyare̱ amo̱ mɔ mo̱ŋ ke̱e̱, na ɔ be̱e̱ ɔ maa taare̱ a ɔ sa se̱ŋsa, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ibrisi ko̱ te mò̱ se̱. Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya ɔnyare̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱ akatɔ a buŋŋi, ne̱ mò̱ kanɔ a buŋŋi, ne̱ ɔ taare̱ a ɔ sa se̱ŋsa.

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he said to them: Any kingdom where people fight each other will end up ruined. And a town or family that fights will soon destroy itself.
text: Amɔ Yeesuu a kyɔ ŋu ke̱tɔ ne̱ ba fa‑ɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ da bamo̱ ke̱kpare̱ mɔ fe̱yɛ, “E̱kɔ ya lwee maŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ maŋ‑ɔ ya ke‑ro, amɔ maŋ amo̱ i bwee ne̱e̱, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ kasuro bɛɛɛ kabuno ya ke‑ro, ne̱ ka bwee.

ref: If I use the power of Beelzebul to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? Your followers are the ones who will judge you.
text: Mo̱ne̱ yɛ n de Biil-sibul ne̱ ɔ gye̱ Ɔbɔnsam‑ɔ e̱le̱ŋ mo̱ e̱ gya ibrisi. Amaa ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ e̱ gya ibrisi ba ko̱so̱ ase̱sɛ se̱. Nte̱tɔ e̱le̱ŋ ne̱ bo̱ de? Ɔbɔnsam a e̱le̱ŋ‑ɔ bɛɛɛ Wuribware̱ lee‑o? A gye̱ Wuribware̱ a e̱le̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱ bo̱ de, bɛɛɛ? To, mo̱ne̱ agyase̱po̱ amo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ gbaa a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ a fo̱ kpa.

ref: it goes off and finds seven other evil spirits even worse than itself. They all come and make their home there, and the person ends up in worse shape than before. That's how it will be with you evil people of today. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Mark
text: Na e̱ le̱e̱ ya kpa ibrisi isunoo ne̱ e̱ bo̱ le̱ŋ e̱ kyɔ e̱mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ‑ɔ, na e̱ bo̱ kye̱na mfe̱ŋ. Ibrisi lɔŋ e̱ gye̱ se̱sɛ. To, amɔ e̱mo̱ a waa isunoo i te se̱sɛ amo̱ se̱ se̱‑ɔ, amɔ se̱sɛ a ko̱lɔ‑ɔ a waa ko̱kyɔ ko̱ kyo̱ŋ saŋ ne̱ ibrisi ko̱ŋko̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ki te mò̱ se̱‑ɔ. Mfaanɛɛ a ase̱ŋ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ a ba ndɔɔ a kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ se̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ bamo̱ e̱bɔye̱ se̱.” Yeesuu mò̱ nyi na mò̱ tire‑ana ase̱ŋ (Maak

ref: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit. You can tell what a tree is like by the fruit it produces.
text: Ŋ yɛ, fo̱ ya ŋu kiyii ko̱ a swɛɛ agyi timaa, a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kiyii‑o bo̱ daŋ. Ne̱ fo̱ ya ŋu kiyii suyo ko̱ mɔ a swɛɛ agyi preepreesɛ, a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kiyii amo̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱ daŋ. Kiyii agyi ne̱ bo̱ de ba pini kiyii suyo ne̱ ke̱ gye̱‑ɔ.

ref: You are a bunch of evil snakes, so how can you say anything good? Your words show what is in your hearts.
text: Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi ase̱ŋ kpe̱yaa e̱tɔwe̱. Mo̱ne̱ awɔ mɔ, e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ waa a mo̱nꞌ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ kpe̱yaa na ke̱bwɛɛ bo̱ mo̱ne̱ nnɔ‑rɔ? A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ bo̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ ne̱ kanɔ e̱ tɔwe̱.

ref: I promise you on the day of judgment, everyone will have to account for every careless word they have spoken.
text: Mo̱nꞌ nyiŋŋi si fe̱yɛ kake nsi ne̱ Wuribware̱ i gyi ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ, o bise ɔke̱maa ke̱se̱ŋkpare̱gyi ke̱maa ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱, ne̱ ke̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱ daŋ‑ɔ ase̱ŋ, na ɔke̱maa lee mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ kanɔ gywii Wuribware̱.

ref: On that day they will be told they are either innocent or guilty because of the things they have said. A Sign from Heaven (Mark
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ ase̱ŋkpare̱gyi ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ de̱e̱re̱‑rɔ a ɔ bo̱ gyi fo̱ ase̱ŋ na o bu fo̱ ke̱be̱ŋ bɛɛɛ ke̱pɔ.” Atɔ dabe̱ ko̱waa ase̱ŋ (Lukas

ref: Some Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses said, “Teacher, we want you to show us a sign from heaven.”
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Farisii awuye ko̱ na Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱ ko̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Ɔkaapo̱po̱, ane̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ fo̱ waa akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ ko̱ na fo̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ bo̱ fo̱ ase̱.”

ref: But Jesus replied: You want a sign because you are evil and won't believe! But the only sign you will get is the sign of the prophet Jonah.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ndɔɔ a kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ gye̱ ase̱sɛ bɔye̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ maa kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ mo̱ne̱ i gyi‑o. Mo̱ne̱ yɛ ŋ waa akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ bo̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱. Amaa ma waa, amɔ Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ Yonaa a akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ wo̱re̱.

ref: He was in the stomach of a big fish for three days and nights, just as the Son of Man will be deep in the earth for three days and nights.
text: A bo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ baa taa mò̱ bo̱ twe̱e̱ ɔpo̱o̱‑rɔ, ne̱ kakiŋgyi dabe̱ ko̱ a kra mò̱ mee, ne̱ oo sii kamo̱ kame‑ro ŋke nsa pwɛɛ ne̱ kakiŋgyi‑o a dɛɛ kpuu mò̱ bo̱ be̱ya nkyu kɛɛ na ɔ ke̱e̱. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, i gyi ŋke nsa e̱se̱‑rɔ.

ref: While Jesus was still speaking to the crowds, his mother and brothers came and stood outside because they wanted to talk with him.
text: Yeesuu saŋ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa o gywii lamaŋ amo̱, ne̱ mò̱ nyi na mò̱ tire‑ana a ba. Ne̱ baa ye̱re̱ kawu suŋ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ ba kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ ba a mò̱ aa bamo̱ a sa se̱ŋsa.

ref: Someone told Jesus, “Your mother and brothers are standing outside and want to talk with you.”
text: Ne̱ ɔko̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ke̱e̱ fo̱ nyi na fo̱ tire‑ana ye̱re̱ kawu ba te̱e̱ fo̱‑ɔ.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Nsɛ e̱ gye̱ n na? Ne̱ nsɛ‑ana mɔ e̱ gye̱ mo̱ tire‑ana?”

ref: Then he pointed to his disciples and said, “These are my mother and my brothers!
text: Ne̱ oo teyi mò̱ ke̱sare̱e̱ bo̱ kyɔ mò̱ agyase̱po̱, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱, n na na mo̱ tire‑ana i te mo̱ ase̱ mfe̱e̱‑ɔ.

ref: Then he taught them many things by using stories. He said: A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ ba itee na akpare̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱ ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Ane̱ŋ a itee mɔ ko̱ko̱ŋko̱ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ, “N gye̱ ɔdɔɔpo̱ ko̱ e̱ bo̱‑rɔ aaa? Ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ a ɔ ya ŋo̱nyaŋ mò̱ yaabraa.

ref: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
text: Ne̱ amo̱ ako̱ a le̱e̱ da kagyaase̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ swe̱e̱re̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ bo̱ daŋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ‑ɔ. Swe̱e̱re̱‑ɔ a mo̱ŋ bo̱ keŋ‑o si‑o, ne̱ aa kwɛɛ me̱naŋ.

ref: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked the plants.
text: Ayaabraagyi‑o ako̱ ne̱ aa le̱e̱ da e̱fa‑rɔ‑ɔ mɔ a ko̱so̱ daŋ. Amaa e̱fa a daŋ mwiire amo̱ se̱.

ref: Jesus' disciples came to him and asked, “Why do you use stories to speak to the people?”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a bo̱ bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “E̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ fo̱ de itee na akpare̱ fo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ fo̱ i gywii lamaŋ‑ɔ?”

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “These people will listen and listen, but never understand. They will look and look, but never see.
text: Ane̱ŋ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ Isaya a tɔwe̱‑ɔ a waa kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ne̱ ya nu mo̱ ase̱ŋ kpo̱ra‑rɔ iluwi bwe̱e̱tɔ gbaa, mo̱ne̱ maa nu amo̱ kaase̱. Mo̱ne̱ be̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ de̱e̱re̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ na mo̱nꞌ fa amo̱ mfɛɛre̱ kpo̱ne̱, mo̱ne̱ maa nu kaase̱.’

ref: Many prophets and good people were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear. Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Mark
text: Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ akyaamɛɛ na mò̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ baa saŋ‑ɔ ne̱ baa dɛɛ kye̱na‑ɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ a yo̱rɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ŋu atɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i ŋu ndɔɔ a kake mɔ‑ɔ, na bo̱ nu ase̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i nu mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ. Amaa ɔ mo̱ŋ sa bamo̱ kpa.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kaapo̱ ɔdɔɔpo̱ kitee a kaase̱‑ɔ (Maak

ref: The seeds that fell among the thornbushes are also people who hear the message. But they start worrying about the needs of this life and are fooled by the desire to get rich. So the message gets choked out, and they never produce anything.
text: Ayaabraagyi amo̱ ne̱ aa le̱e̱ da e̱fa‑rɔ‑ɔ mɔ ye̱re̱ bo̱ sa se̱sɛ ne̱ o nu abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ atɔ tɔɔraasɛ, na katanne̱kpa ne̱ ka taare̱ a ka pe̱nna se̱sɛ‑ɔ, ya lwee mò̱ mfɛɛre̱‑rɔ, amɔ aa waa ko̱kyɔ kyo̱ŋ abware̱se̱ŋ ne̱ a bo̱ mò̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ. Amɔ mò̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi‑o maa waa mò̱ sɛye̱.

ref: Jesus then told them this story: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a farmer scattered good seed in a field.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ kitee ko̱ gywii bamo̱. Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ se̱ a kuwure‑o du‑o. N gye̱ ɔdɔɔpo̱ ko̱ e̱ bo̱‑rɔ aaa? Ne̱ ɔɔ ŋo̱nyaŋ ayaabraagyi timaa ko̱ mò̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑rɔ.

ref: But while everyone was sleeping, an enemy came and scattered weed seeds in the field and then left.
text: Kanye ko̱ saŋ ne̱ ɔke̱maa a di idi‑o, ne̱ mò̱ do̱ŋ a ywii ya ŋo̱nyaŋ afagyi bɔye̱ ko̱ bo̱ waa mò̱ yaabraa ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: When the plants came up and began to mature, the farmer's servants could see the weeds.
text: Ne̱ ayaabraagyi amo̱ a kwɛɛ, ne̱ aa bo̱ daŋ ko̱we̱, ne̱ ŋkee afagyi bɔye̱ amo̱ mɔ a bo̱ kwɛɛ lee amo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ifuri.

ref: The servants came and asked, “Sir, didn't you scatter good seed in your field? Where did these weeds come from?”
text: Mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱ ɔdɔɔpo̱‑ɔ ayaafɔre̱ a bo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱, ne̱e̱ fo̱ a ŋo̱nyaŋ ayaabraagyi timaa ne̱e̱ ɛ? Ne̱ mfe̱ne̱ ne̱ e̱fa bɔye̱ mɔ a le̱e̱?’

ref: “An enemy did this,” he replied. His servants then asked, “Do you want us to go out and pull up the weeds?”
text: Ne̱ oo lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ɔdo̱ŋ ko̱ ya waa amo̱‑ɔ.’ Ne̱ baa be̱e̱ bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Amɔ fo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ane̱ ya kyuwi e̱fa‑ɔ le̱e̱ yaabraa amo̱‑rɔ aaa?’

ref: Jesus used stories when he spoke to the people. In fact, he did not tell them anything without using stories.
text: Akpare̱ na itee ne̱ Yeesuu a bo̱ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ gywii lamaŋ‑ɔ. Ɔ mo̱ŋ tɔwe̱ ke̱se̱ŋ ko̱ gywii bamo̱ na a mo̱ŋ gye̱ ke̱kpare̱ bɛɛɛ kitee.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “I will use stories to speak my message and to explain things hidden since the creation of the world.” Jesus Explains the Story about the Weeds
text: Ɔɔ waa ane̱ŋ ne̱e̱ na ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ ko̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ waa kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ, “Akpare̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ da a m bo̱ sa se̱ŋsa gywii bamo̱, na m bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ aa kwe̱e̱rɔ pwɛɛ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a dɛɛ twe̱e̱ kaye̱‑ɔ.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kaapo̱ e̱fa kitee‑o kaase̱‑ɔ

ref: After Jesus left the crowd and went inside, his disciples came to him and said, “Explain to us the story about the weeds in the wheat field.”
text: Ne̱ ŋkee Yeesuu a le̱e̱ lamaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ lwee lɔŋ‑nɔ. Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a ba mò̱ ase̱ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Kaapo̱ ane̱ e̱fa kitee‑o kaase̱.”

ref: Jesus answered: The one who scattered the good seed is the Son of Man.
text: Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Yooo. Ɔdɔɔpo̱ ne̱ ɔɔ ŋo̱nyaŋ ayaabraagyi timaa amo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o.

ref: The field is the world, and the good seeds are the people who belong to the kingdom. The weeds are those who belong to the evil one,
text: Mò̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ mɔ e̱ gye̱ kaye̱ mɔ. Ne̱ ayaabraagyi‑o mɔ e̱ gye̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ tii Wuribware̱ se̱ a kuwure‑o si‑o. E̱fa‑ɔ mɔ e̱ gye̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ gya Ɔbɔnsam a e̱kpa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ.

ref: and the one who scattered them is the devil. The harvest is the end of time, and angels are the ones who bring in the harvest.
text: Ne̱ ɔdo̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ ŋo̱nyaŋ afagyi amo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ Ɔbɔnsam fɔŋfɔŋ. Yaabraa ke̱te̱ŋŋe̱be̱e̱‑ɔ mɔ e̱ gye̱ saŋ ne̱ kaye̱ mɔ e̱ gye̱ kɛɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ ba te̱ŋŋe̱ yaabraa‑ɔ mɔ e̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ a mbɔɔ‑ɔ.

ref: The Son of Man will send out his angels, and they will gather from his kingdom everyone who does wrong or causes others to sin.
text: Mo̱ i suŋ mo̱ mbɔɔ a m bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ na bo̱ ba bo̱ kpɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ke̱ gye̱ bɔye̱‑ɔ na abɔye̱waapo̱ pɛɛɛ.

ref: Then he will throw them into a flaming furnace, where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.
text: Na mo̱ mbɔɔ‑ɔ lee bamo̱ le̱e̱ mo̱ kuwure‑o‑ro, na bo̱ twe̱e̱ bamo̱ waa ke̱de̱e̱kpɛɛtekii‑ro. Mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱ ba su, na ba duŋ nnɔ.

ref: But everyone who has done right will shine like the sun in their Father's kingdom. If you have ears, pay attention! A Hidden Treasure
text: Na ŋkee a saŋ ase̱sɛ timaa‑o, na bamo̱ itimaa‑o sa a bo̱ laŋŋe̱‑rɔ fe̱yɛ kyɔwe̱‑ɔ bamo̱ se̱ Wuribware̱ a kuwure‑o‑ro. Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ saase̱bɛɛ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ɔke̱maa ne̱ o de e̱se̱bɔ‑ɔ, o nu ase̱ŋ mɔ.” Kalɔgyii na atanne̱ kitee

ref: After finding a very valuable one, the owner goes and sells everything in order to buy that pearl. A Fish Net
text: Ne̱ kake ŋko̱ ɔɔ ya ŋu ɔko̱ de ane̱ŋ a ataŋgyi‑o ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ ko̱. Amaa ke̱mo̱ yawo̱ bo̱ le̱ŋ fɛɛ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ oo kperi ke̱mo̱ se̱ yɔ pe̱ ya fe mò̱ kapo̱tɛɛ pɛɛɛ ya sɔɔ ke̱taŋgyi amo̱.” Kisawu kitee

ref: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a net is thrown into a lake and catches all kinds of fish.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ ba kitee ko̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ se̱ a kuwure‑o du‑o. Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Ŋ gye̱ ŋkiŋgyi akrapo̱ ko̱ e̱ bo̱‑rɔ aaa? Ne̱ baa yɔ kisawu‑ro, ne̱ baa ya twe̱e̱ kra ŋkiŋgyi bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: When the net is full, it is dragged to the shore, and the fishermen sit down to separate the fish. They keep the good ones, but throw the bad ones away.
text: Bamo̱ a nya ŋkiŋgyi bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa de̱e̱ kideŋbe ya kye̱na tesi ŋkiŋgyi‑o‑ro. Ne̱ baa swii ntimaa‑o waa bamo̱ atwe̱e̱twe̱e̱‑rɔ. Ne̱ baa swii mmo̱ ne̱ ba kisi kigyi‑o bo̱ twe̱e̱.

ref: That's how it will be at the end of time. Angels will come and separate the evil people from the ones who have done right.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ kaye̱ bo̱ gye̱ kɛɛ‑ɔ, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ke̱tɔ e̱ ba. Wuribware̱ a mbɔɔ‑ɔ e̱ ba a bo̱ bo̱ lee ase̱sɛ bɔye̱‑ɔ le̱e̱ atimaa‑o‑ro.

ref: Then those evil people will be thrown into a flaming furnace, where they will cry and grit their teeth in pain. New and Old Treasures
text: Na bo̱ taa abɔye̱‑ɔ twe̱e̱ waa ke̱de̱e̱kpɛɛtekii‑o‑ro. Mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱ ba di‑ro, na ba su, na ba duŋwi nnɔ.”

ref: Jesus asked his disciples if they understood all these things. They said, “Yes, we do.”
text: Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ itee na akpare̱ amo̱ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ oo bise mò̱ agyase̱po̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ne̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ a nu itee mɔ kaase̱ ɛ?” Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Ɔɔŋ, ane̱ a nu kaase̱.”

ref: So he told them, “Every student of the Scriptures who becomes a disciple in the kingdom of heaven is like someone who brings out new and old treasures from the storeroom.” The People of Nazareth Turn against Jesus (Mark
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱ kyɔ bo̱ nyi Wuribware̱ a atɔ‑ɔ ke̱kaapo̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ bamo̱ ɔko̱ ya be̱e̱ kii fe̱yɛ sukuu kigyi ne̱ oo suye Wuribware̱ se̱ a kuwure‑o e̱kpa‑ɔ ke̱gya se̱‑ɔ le̱e̱ mo̱ ase̱, ɔ waa ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ atɔ wuye ko̱ ne̱ o de atɔ dame̱naŋsɛ po̱pwɛɛ na ade̱daa mò̱ ke̱kyaŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ oo lee amo̱ pɛɛɛ bo̱ kaapo̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Nasarɛt awuye a kine Yeesuu‑o (Maak

ref: When Jesus had finished telling these stories, he left
text: Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ itee na akpare̱ amo̱ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ.

ref: and went to his hometown. He taught in their synagogue, and the people were so amazed that they asked, “Where does he get all this wisdom and the power to work these miracles?
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ mò̱ aye̱ Nasarɛt. Mò̱ a bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ kaapo̱ ase̱sɛ abware̱se̱ŋ maŋ amo̱ a ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ. Bamo̱ a nu mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ aa kpe̱ŋ bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱. Ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ bamo̱ wo̱re̱ wo̱re̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mfe̱ne̱ ne̱ ɔnyare̱ mɔ a nya mfɛɛre̱ mɔ le̱e̱? Ne̱ nsɛ ya sa mò̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ waa akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ mɔ ne̱ ane̱ a nu amo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ?

ref: Isn't he the son of the carpenter? Isn't Mary his mother, and aren't James, Joseph, Simon, and Judas his brothers?
text: Ane̱ aye̱po̱ fɔŋfɔŋ e̱ gye̱ mò̱. N gye̱ kaape̱ŋtaa‑ɔ mò̱ gyi‑o ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱? N gye̱ mò̱ nyi e̱ gye̱ Mariya aaa? N gye̱ mò̱ tire‑ana e̱ gye̱ Yakubu na Yo̱sɛf na Simɔŋ na Yudas aaa?

ref: Don't his sisters still live here in our town? How can he do all this?”
text: Mò̱ pe̱kye̱e̱‑ana mo̱ŋ te mfe̱e̱ aaa? Ne̱ ŋkee mfe̱ne̱ ne̱ ɔɔ nya amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ le̱e̱?”

ref: So the people were upset because of what he was doing. But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their own family.”
text: Ne̱ baa kine mò̱. Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ba bu Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ to̱ŋ ke̱maa, amaa mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ aye̱ awuye na mò̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ awuye fe̱raa mɔ mo̱ŋ de mò̱ bo̱ te̱e̱ sɛye̱.”

ref: And because the people did not have any faith, Jesus did not work many miracles there.
text: Bamo̱ a mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi‑o si‑o, ɔ mo̱ŋ waa akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ mfe̱ŋ.

ref: “Come on!” Jesus said. Peter then got out of the boat and started walking on the water toward him.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ba!” Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ Peetroo a le̱e̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ le̱e̱ ɔ naa nkyu‑o si ɔ yɔ Yeesuu ase̱.

ref: and told his officials, “This is John the Baptist! He has come back from death, and that's why he has the power to work these miracles.”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ ayaafɔre̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ a n gyi fe̱yɛ ɔnyare̱ mɔ e̱ gye̱ Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱ ne̱ mo̱ a mɔɔ mò̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro. Amo̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ ane̱ŋ fe̱raa, ɔ maa taare̱ a ɔ waa ane̱ŋ a akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ mɔ.”

ref: Herod had earlier arrested John and had him chained and put in prison. He did this because John had told him, “It isn't right for you to take Herodias, the wife of your brother Philip.”
text: Mfaanɛɛ ne̱ Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱‑ɔ a wu. Saŋ ko̱, ne̱ Owure Hɛrɔd a kɔɔre̱ mò̱ tire Filipo mò̱ ka Hɛrɔde̱ya waare̱e̱. Ne̱ Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱‑ɔ mɔ a yii owure‑o si iluwi bwe̱e̱tɔ fe̱yɛ, “A mo̱ŋ bware fe̱yɛ fo̱ a kɔɔre̱ fo̱ tire mò̱ ka waare̱e̱.”

ref: John's followers took his body and buried it. Then they told Jesus what had happened. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Mark
text: Ne̱ Yohanee a agyase̱po̱‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ a bo̱ so̱rɔ mò̱ kifuniŋ‑o yaa pure. Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ baa ya tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ kaa waa Yohanee‑o gywii Yeesuu. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a sa ase̱sɛ ŋkpe̱ŋ nnuu ateese‑o (Maak

ref: But when Peter saw how strong the wind was, he was afraid and started sinking. “Save me, Lord!” he shouted.
text: Amaa afwii‑o a da mò̱ nkyu si mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, kufu a nya mò̱, ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ o kirewi. Ne̱ ɔɔ faa‑rɔ tɔwe̱ ɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, mo̱rɔwe̱ mo̱!”

ref: But they said, “We have only five small loaves of bread and two fish.”
text: Ne̱ baa lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Bodobodoo akuri anuu na ŋkiŋgyi ŋnyɔ wo̱re̱ kpeŋ ne̱ ane̱ de mfe̱e̱.”

ref: Jesus asked his disciples to bring the food to him,
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ taa baa mò̱ a ɔ ke̱e̱.

ref: and he told the crowd to sit down on the grass. Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples, and they gave it to the people.
text: Ne̱ ɔ yɛ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ kye̱na e̱fa se̱ swe̱e̱re̱ mfe̱ŋ. Ne̱ ɔɔ taa bodobodoo akuri anuu‑o na ŋkiŋgyi ŋnyɔ‑ɔ waa mò̱ asare̱e̱‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ de̱e̱re̱ so̱so̱, ne̱ ɔɔ sa Wuribware̱ aŋsɛ. Ne̱ ŋkee ɔɔ te̱ŋŋe̱‑rɔ bo̱ sa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa ke‑ro sa ase̱sɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ.

ref: After everyone had eaten all they wanted, Jesus' disciples picked up twelve large baskets of leftovers.
text: Bamo̱ pɛɛɛ a gyi bodobodoo‑o kpo̱ne̱, ne̱ baa wo̱ ŋkiŋgyi‑o kpo̱ne̱. Ne̱ ŋkee mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a taa ake kudu anyɔ swii bodobodoo na ŋkiŋgyi mpupuriase̱e̱ ne̱ ŋ ya saŋ‑ɔ bo̱ bo̱rɔ.

ref: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into a boat and start back across the lake. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
text: Amo̱‑ɔ kamɛɛ‑rɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu a sa ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a lwee ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ gye̱ mò̱ ŋkpɛɛ yɔ nkyu‑o nno̱ŋ a bo̱ŋbe̱‑ɔ se̱, ne̱ mò̱‑ɔ mò̱ a sii a ɔ sa lamaŋ‑ɔ kpa a bo̱ yɔ pe̱.

ref: Then he went up on a mountain where he could be alone and pray. Later in the evening, he was still there.
text: Mò̱ a brawe̱ bamo̱‑rɔ pɛɛɛ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ bamo̱ e̱pe̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ ke̱be̱e̱ ko̱ se̱ ya ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ mò̱ wo̱re̱ kpeŋ. Bo̱ fo̱ kanye‑o, Yeesuu wo̱re̱ bo̱ ke̱be̱e̱‑ɔ se̱ mfe̱ŋ.

ref: By this time the boat was a long way from the shore. It was going against the wind and was being tossed around by the waves.
text: Na mò̱ agyase̱po̱ a ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ a fo̱ ke̱fɔ. Amɔ bo̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ ba taare̱ bo̱ pare̱ yɔ akatɔ‑rɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ a pare̱ ba gyaŋŋaa afwii‑o si‑o, alaŋkpare̱ e̱ da bamo̱ a kiŋŋi.

ref: A little while before morning, Jesus came walking on the water toward his disciples.
text: Bo̱ fo̱ gye̱gyaye̱ kese‑ro, ne̱ Yeesuu a nare̱ nkyu‑o si fe̱yɛ mò̱ a naa swe̱e̱re̱‑ɔ ya to̱ bamo̱.

ref: When they saw him, they thought he was a ghost. They were terrified and started screaming.
text: Mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a ŋu mò̱ a naa nkyu‑o si‑o, bo̱ mo̱ŋ pini mò̱, amo̱se̱‑ɔ kufu a nya bamo̱ pɛɛɛ, ne̱ baa fɛɛ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, “Kye̱ŋaŋpo̱ ne̱e̱!”

ref: At once, Jesus said to them, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ ne̱e̱. Mo̱nꞌ nya kakpo̱nɔ. Mo̱ne̱ ma sa a kufu nya mo̱ne̱.”

ref: Peter replied, “Lord, if it really is you, tell me to come to you on the water.”
text: Ne̱ Peetroo yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, amo̱ e̱ gye̱ fo̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ si, amo̱ fe̱raa sa kanɔ na n nare̱ nkyu‑o si ba fo̱ ase̱.”

ref: At once, Jesus reached out his hand. He helped Peter up and said, “You surely don't have much faith. Why do you doubt?”
text: Puri amo̱‑rɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu a fo̱ mò̱ ase̱, ne̱ ɔɔ twe̱e̱ kra mò̱‑rɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi ne̱ fo̱ de‑o mo̱ŋ kyɔ pɛɛɛ! E̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ fo̱ mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ gyi?”

ref: When Jesus and Peter got into the boat, the wind died down.
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yeesuu na Peetroo a lwee ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ afwii‑o a yɔwe̱.

ref: The men in the boat worshiped Jesus and said, “You really are the Son of God!” Jesus Heals Sick People in Gennesaret (Mark
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a gyɔŋŋɔ suŋ mò̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ. Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Kase̱ŋtiŋ si, Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑o e̱ gye̱ fo̱.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya alɔpo̱ Ginɛsarɛt ifuri si‑o (Maak

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and came to shore near the town of Gennesaret.
text: Ne̱ baa me̱raa se̱ ba yɔ nkyu‑o si. Ne̱ ŋkee bamo̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ a bo̱ daŋ Ginɛsarɛt ifuri si.

ref: The people found out he was there, and they sent word to everyone who lived in this part of the country. So they brought all the sick people to Jesus.
text: Mfe̱ŋ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ a de̱ŋ ŋu Yeesuu‑o, ne̱ baa da kiboŋboŋ mmaŋgyii na e̱maŋ ne̱ e̱ maa mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ ifuri‑o si a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ a taa bamo̱ alɔpo̱ pɛɛɛ baa Yeesuu ase̱.

ref: They begged him just to let them touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: Ne̱ baa ko̱re̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ dabo̱rɔ bamo̱ na bo̱ kpaare̱, bɛɛɛ ɔ sa alɔpo̱‑ɔ kpa na bo̱ dabo̱rɔ mò̱ waagya kanɔ gbaa. Bamo̱ ne̱ baa dabo̱rɔ mò̱ waagya‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a kpaare̱.

ref: Jesus answered, “Every plant that my Father in heaven did not plant will be pulled up by the roots.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a da ke̱kpare̱ mɔ bo̱ lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ke̱tɔdwii ke̱maa ne̱ Wuribware̱, n se̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ, mo̱ŋ dwii‑o o kyuwi.

ref: “Why don't your disciples obey what our ancestors taught us to do? They don't even wash their hands before they eat.”
text: “Nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ fo̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ maa gya e̱kpa ne̱ ane̱ ade̱daapo̱ a kaapo̱ ane̱‑ɔ se̱? Ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ ane̱ a bise amo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ maa fwe̱e̱ asare̱e̱, pwɛɛ dɛɛ gyi ateese.”

ref: It is useless for you to worship me, when you teach rules made up by humans.” What Really Makes People Unclean (Mark
text: Bamo̱ kusuŋ mɔ ne̱ ba suŋ mo̱‑ɔ gye̱ kwaa, ke̱ e̱ gye̱ ke̱gya mbraa na amaŋbe̱re̱ ne̱ dimaadi a kaapo̱‑ɔ se̱ dooo.’ ” Atɔ ne̱ a waa se̱sɛ iyisi‑o (Maak

ref: Stay away from those Pharisees! They are like blind people leading other blind people, and all of them will fall into a ditch.”
text: Farisii awuye‑o fe̱raa, mo̱ne̱ ma kaŋ be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱. Bo̱ yɛ bo̱ gye̱ te̱napo̱ kayii‑ro akrapo̱ ne̱e̱. Amaa bamo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ mo̱ŋ ke̱e̱. Te̱napo̱ ya kra te̱napo̱ kiyii‑ro a ɔ gye̱ mò̱ ŋkpɛɛ, bamo̱ pɛɛɛ e̱ le̱e̱ a bo̱ le̱e̱ da ke̱maŋtaŋ‑nɔ.”

ref: Jesus did not say a word. But the woman kept following along and shouting, so his disciples came up and asked him to send her away.
text: Amaa Yeesuu mo̱ŋ be̱ŋŋaa mò̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ oo su ɔ ko̱re̱ mò̱ ane̱ŋ‑aaa. Ne̱ ŋkee Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a bo̱ ko̱re̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱, gya ɔkye̱e̱ amo̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ gya ane̱ se̱ ane̱ŋ‑aaa, na ɔ maa yɔwe̱ e̱lawo̱ mɔ ko̱waa.”

ref: Then his disciples came over to him and asked, “Do you know you insulted the Pharisees by what you said?”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a ba mò̱ ase̱ bo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ ase̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, Farisii awuye‑o iduŋ a fwii fo̱ se̱ aaa?”

ref: Peter replied, “What did you mean when you talked about the things that make people unclean?”
text: Ne̱ Peetroo yɛ, “Fo̱ yɛ n gye̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ se̱sɛ i gyi ɔ waa kame‑ro‑o e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ sa a ɔ waa iyisi, kaapo̱ ane̱ ke̱mo̱ kaase̱.”

ref: Jesus then said: Don't any of you know by now what I am talking about?
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Ntɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ du fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ amo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱?

ref: Don't you know that the food you put into your mouth goes into your stomach and then out of your body?
text: Ntɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nu ase̱ŋ amo̱ kaase̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱? Mo̱nꞌ nu fe̱yɛ ke̱tɔ ne̱ se̱sɛ i gyi‑o i lwee mò̱ kanɔ‑rɔ ne̱e̱ na ko̱ yɔ mò̱ kame‑ro na ŋkee ɔ kɔɔ bo̱ twe̱e̱.

ref: But the words that come out of your mouth come from your heart. And they are what make you unfit to worship God.
text: Amaa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ le̱e̱ mò̱ kanɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ fe̱raa a le̱e̱ mò̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ ne̱e̱. Ke̱mo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ sa ne̱ ɔ waa iyisi.

ref: These are what make you unclean. Eating without washing your hands will not make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Mark
text: Ane̱ŋ a atɔ mɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ a sa ne̱ se̱sɛ e̱ waa iyisi. Amaa mò̱ ya gyi ateese na ɔ mo̱ŋ fwe̱e̱ asare̱e̱, a maa taare̱ a a sa a ɔ waa iyisi.” Ɔkye̱e̱ ko̱ ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaanyi‑o a ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi‑o (Maak

ref: Large crowds came and brought many people who were paralyzed or blind or lame or unable to talk. They placed them, and many others, in front of Jesus, and he healed them all.
text: Mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱ ase̱sɛ biribiri a ba mò̱ ase̱. Bamo̱ bo̱ko̱ a baa agyabɔɔ na ate̱napo̱ na asare̱e̱ wusɛ awuye na mmu na bamo̱ ne̱ ba lɔ alɔgyi e̱wo̱re̱se̱ e̱wo̱re̱se̱‑ɔ mò̱ akatɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ kya bamo̱.

ref: The woman came closer. Then she knelt down and begged, “Please help me, Lord!”
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ a bo̱ kpuni aŋurii Yeesuu ayaa‑rɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ fo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ bo̱ kya mo̱‑rɔ.”

ref: Jesus replied, “It isn't right to take food away from children and feed it to dogs.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “N de ateese mo̱ e̱ sa Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑ana. Ne̱ mo̱ ya taa ako̱ sa bamo̱ ne̱ ane̱ ase̱sɛ e̱ te̱e̱ fe̱yɛ igyono‑o, a bware aaa?”

ref: “Lord, this is true,” the woman said, “but even puppies get the crumbs that fall from their owner's table.”
text: Ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ yɛ, “To, mo̱ a nu, mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, amaa igyono maa kisi bamo̱ anyaŋpe̱ a anɔwo̱re̱ ne̱ a le̱e̱ a da swe̱e̱re̱‑ɔ kigyi.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Dear woman, you really do have a lot of faith, and you will be given what you want.” At that moment her daughter was healed. Jesus Heals Many People
text: Mò̱ a tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu mɔ a be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔpe̱kye̱e̱, fo̱ a kɔɔre̱ mo̱ gyi bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ke̱tɔ ne̱ fo̱ a ko̱re̱ mo̱‑ɔ, fo̱ e̱ nya.” Puri amo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ mò̱ gyi kye̱e̱sɛ‑ɔ a kpaare̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ kya alɔpo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ

ref: From there, Jesus went along Lake Galilee. Then he climbed a hill and sat down.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ kiŋŋi yɔ Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ ɔ naa mfe̱ŋ a ke̱pare̱ dabe̱‑ɔ e̱kɛɛkɛɛ se̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ de̱e̱ ke̱be̱e̱ ko̱ kafwe̱e̱ ya kye̱na.

ref: Everyone was amazed at what they saw and heard. People who had never spoken could now speak. The lame were healed, the paralyzed could walk, and the blind were able to see. Everyone was praising the God of Israel. Jesus Feeds Four Thousand (Mark
text: Lamaŋ ne̱ baa ba Yeesuu ase̱‑ɔ a ŋu ane̱ŋ ne̱ puri amo̱‑rɔ mmu a sa se̱ŋsa, ne̱ asare̱e̱ wusɛ awuye a asare̱e̱‑ɔ a kpaare̱, ne̱ agyabɔɔ a ko̱so̱ nare̱ bamo̱ ayaa se̱, ne̱ ate̱napo̱ akatɔ a buŋŋi‑o, ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ bamo̱, ne̱ baa kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱, ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Ane̱ Isireelii awuye a nyaŋpe̱‑ɔ ke̱yaale̱ŋ kyɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a sa ase̱sɛ ŋkpe̱ŋ nna ateese‑o (Maak

ref: Jesus called his disciples together and told them, “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat. I don't want to send them away hungry. They might faint on their way home.”
text: Ne̱ ŋkee Yeesuu a te̱e̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ baa mò̱ ase̱, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ase̱sɛ mɔ ase̱ŋ bo̱ mo̱ e̱wɛɛ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ a ba mo̱ ase̱ mfe̱e̱‑ɔ ŋke nsa ne̱e̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ bamo̱ ateese pɛɛɛ a lo̱we̱. Ne̱ mo̱‑ɔ mo̱ ya sa ne̱ baa yɔ e̱pe̱ na mo̱ŋ sa bamo̱ ateese, ako̱ŋ e̱ mɔɔ bo̱ko̱ kpa‑rɔ.”

ref: His disciples said, “This place is like a desert. Where can we find enough food to feed such a crowd?”
text: Ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Kimukee mɔ‑rɔ mfe̱ne̱ ne̱ ɔko̱ e̱ nya ase̱sɛ de̱maŋte̱ mɔ kanɔ se̱ ateese?”

ref: Jesus asked them how much food they had. They replied, “Seven small loaves of bread and a few little fish.”
text: To, ne̱ Yeesuu a bise bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Bodobodoo akuri afe̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ de?” Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Ane̱ de bodobodoo akuri asunoo na ŋkiŋgyi ngyingyii kafwe̱e̱.”

ref: After Jesus had told the people to sit down,
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a sa ne̱ lamaŋ‑ɔ a kye̱na swe̱e̱re̱.

ref: he took the seven loaves of bread and the fish and gave thanks. He then broke them and handed them to his disciples, who passed them around to the crowds.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ taa bodobodoo akuri asunoo amo̱ na ŋkiŋgyi amo̱, ne̱ ɔɔ sa Wuribware̱ aŋsɛ we̱e̱ se̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ te̱ŋŋe̱‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ taa sa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ke‑ro sa bamo̱. Ne̱ baa ke‑ro sa ase̱sɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ.

ref: Everyone ate all they wanted, and the leftovers filled seven large baskets.
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ a gyi ateese amo̱ kpo̱ne̱. Ne̱ ŋkee mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a ya swii ateese mpupuriase̱e̱‑ɔ bo̱ bo̱rɔ ake adabe̱ asunoo.

ref: After Jesus had sent the crowds away, he got into a boat and sailed across the lake. He came to shore near the town of Magadan.
text: Yeesuu a sa ne̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ mɔ a brawe̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ oo lwee ko̱re̱e̱‑rɔ ya daŋ nkyu‑o nno̱ŋ a bo̱ŋbe̱‑ɔ se̱ me̱raa maŋ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Magadaŋ‑ɔ.

ref: Don't you know by now that I am not talking to you about bread? Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees!
text: Ne̱ e̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ sa Farisii awuye na Sadukii awuye a yiisi‑o si, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ maa nu kaase̱ fe̱yɛ n gye̱ bodobodoo ase̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱?”

ref: When Jesus and his disciples were near the town of Caesarea Philippi, he asked them, “What do people say about the Son of Man?”
text: Saŋ ko̱ ne̱ Yeesuu a yɔ ifuri ko̱ ne̱ e̱ maa Kaye̱sare̱ya Filipii maŋ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ. Bamo̱ a naa ba yɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ oo bise mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “E̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ ase̱sɛ e̱ te̱e̱ mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o?”

ref: Finally, the disciples understood that Jesus wasn't talking about the yeast used to make bread, but about the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees. Who Is Jesus? (Mark
text: Ne̱ ŋkee mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a nu kaase̱ fe̱yɛ n gye̱ yiisi ne̱ bo̱ de ba waa bodobodoo‑ro‑o ase̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ tɔwe̱. Amaa oo yii bamo̱ se̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Farisii awuye‑o na Sadukii awuye‑o maa kaapo̱ ke̱bware̱suŋ kpa se̱‑ɔ se̱ ne̱e̱. Mò̱ ne̱ Peetroo yɛ Yeesuu gye̱‑ɔ (Maak

ref: He told them: If the sky is red in the evening, you say the weather will be good.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Kyɔwe̱ e̱ twɛɛ‑rɔ, ne̱ ke̱tɔ ya pee‑ro‑o, mo̱ne̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ nyaŋe̱ ane̱ e̱ nya kaye̱‑rɔ kaye̱e̱yuri.

ref: But if the sky is red and gloomy in the morning, you say it is going to rain. You can tell what the weather will be like by looking at the sky. But you don't understand what is happening now.
text: Gye̱gyaye̱ tututu mɔ awo̱re̱ ya biri, mo̱ne̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bware̱ e̱ kpa a ɔ ba. Mo̱ne̱ a taare̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ de̱e̱re̱ so̱so̱ mo̱ne̱ i pini ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ ba kaye̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, e̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ maa taare̱ a mo̱nꞌ kaapo̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ waa kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ kaase̱ fe̱raa?

ref: The disciples answered, “Some people say you are John the Baptist or maybe Elijah or Jeremiah or some other prophet.”
text: Ne̱ baa lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Bo̱ko̱ yɛ fo̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ a akyaamɛɛ ne̱ baa dɛɛ kye̱na‑ɔ ɔko̱ ne̱ oo kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro, fe̱yɛ Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱‑ɔ, bɛɛɛ Iliya, bɛɛɛ Yɛrɛme̱ya, bɛɛɛ bamo̱‑rɔ ɔko̱‑ɔ.”

ref: From then on, Jesus began telling his disciples what would happen to him. He said, “I must go to Jerusalem. There the nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses will make me suffer terribly. I will be killed, but three days later I will rise to life.”
text: Le̱e̱ kake amo̱ bo̱ yɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu a le̱e̱ ɔ kaapo̱‑rɔ ɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “A tiri fe̱yɛ ŋ yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ. Na mfe̱ŋ a maŋ abre̱sɛ na Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ na Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ sa a n gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Na bo̱ mɔɔ mo̱. Amaa ke̱mo̱ ŋke nsa, Wuribware̱ i kyiŋŋi mo̱ a ɔ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro.”

ref: The disciples talked this over and said to each other, “He must be saying this because we didn't bring along any bread.”
text: To, ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a le̱e̱ ba tɔwe̱ ba gywii abɛɛ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ mo̱ŋ de bodobodoo‑o si ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ, bɛɛɛ?”

ref: So I will call you Peter, which means “a rock.” On this rock I will build my church, and death itself will not have any power over it.
text: Fo̱ ke̱ŋasɛnyare̱ e̱ gye̱ Peetroo, ne̱ ke̱mo̱ kaase̱ e̱ gye̱ ke̱fo̱re̱‑ɔ bɛɛɛ? Fo̱ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱fo̱re̱‑ɔ. Mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii fo̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱fo̱re̱ mɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ pwɛɛ mo̱ asɔre̱e̱ ke̱kyaŋ a m bo̱ ye̱ra, na Ɔbɔnsam na mò̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ awuye ma taare̱ a bo̱ nye̱ra ke̱mo̱.

ref: Then Jesus asked, “But who do you say I am?”
text: Ne̱ oo bise mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ne̱ mo̱ne̱ mɔ mo̱ne̱ yɛ nsɛ e̱ gye̱ mo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱?”

ref: Simon Peter spoke up, “You are the Messiah, the Son of the living God.”
text: Ne̱ Simɔŋ Peetroo a be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ e̱ gye̱ Kristoo, Wuribware̱ ne̱ mò̱ wo̱re̱ dooo i de ŋkpa‑ɔ mò̱ gyi ne̱ Wuribware̱ yɛ o suŋ ane̱ ase̱‑ɔ.”

ref: Jesus told him: Simon, son of Jonah, you are blessed! You didn't discover this on your own. It was shown to you by my Father in heaven.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Yohanee mò̱ gyi Simɔŋ mɔ, fo̱ de ŋyure, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ n gye̱ nyiŋkpasɛ ya tɔwe̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ mɔ gywii fo̱, amaa n se̱ Wuribware̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ ya tɔwe̱ gywii fo̱.

ref: I will give you the keys to the kingdom of heaven, and God in heaven will allow whatever you allow on earth. But he will not allow anything you don't allow.
text: Fo̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ taa Wuribware̱ se̱ a e̱safo̱wa‑ɔ a n sa. Po̱ne̱ ke̱maa ne̱ fo̱ e̱ taye̱ swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ mfe̱e̱‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ a taye̱. Ne̱ po̱ne̱ ke̱maa ne̱ fo̱ e̱ twe̱e̱‑rɔ swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ mfe̱e̱‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ a twe̱e̱‑rɔ‑ɔ.”

ref: Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that. He said, “God would never let this happen to you, Lord!”
text: Ne̱ Peetroo a lee mò̱ keri si, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Wuribware̱ sa a a ma ba ane̱ŋ. Amo̱‑ɔ ma ba fo̱ se̱ daa.”

ref: Jesus turned to Peter and said, “Satan, get away from me! You're in my way because you think like everyone else and not like God.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a buruwaa tɔwe̱ gywii Peetroo fe̱yɛ, “Ɔbɔnsam, nare̱ le̱e̱ mo̱ ase̱. Peetroo, fo̱ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱fo̱re̱ ne̱ ki i tii mo̱ e̱kpa‑ɔ. Fo̱ mfɛɛre̱ mo̱ŋ le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱, Se̱sɛ mfɛɛre̱ ne̱ fo̱ de.”

ref: Then Jesus said to his disciples: If any of you want to be my followers, you must forget about yourself. You must take up your cross and follow me.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ kpa a ɔ gya mo̱ se̱‑ɔ yɔwe̱ ko̱waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ pre̱ mò̱‑ɔ, na ɔ so̱rɔ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ gya mo̱ se̱.

ref: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me, you will find it.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ kpa a ɔ nya mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ le̱e̱ lowi ase̱‑ɔ, lowi e̱ nya mò̱ nsu pɛɛɛ. Ɔko̱ mɔ ne̱ o kperi mo̱ se̱ na o wu‑o e̱ nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself? What would you give to get back your soul?
text: To̱nɔ mo̱ ne̱ se̱sɛ e̱ nya fe̱yɛ mò̱ a nya kaye̱‑rɔ atɔ pɛɛɛ, na bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ se̱ na ɔ paŋ kye̱nakpa Wuribware̱ ase̱? A mo̱ŋ de to̱nɔ. Sɛye̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ɔ taare̱ a ɔ bo̱ sɔɔ kye̱nakpa amo̱.

ref: I promise you some of those standing here will not die before they see the Son of Man coming with his kingdom.
text: Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ bo̱ko̱ bo̱ mfe̱e̱ na bo̱ ma wu, amɔ baa ŋu mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, a ba fe̱yɛ owure‑o pwɛɛ.”

ref: There in front of the disciples, Jesus was completely changed. His face was shining like the sun, and his clothes became white as light.
text: Bamo̱ a bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa ŋu fe̱yɛ Yeesuu a kyurowi. Mò̱ akatɔ‑rɔ a to̱re̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ kyɔwe̱, ne̱ mò̱ atɔ buŋsɛ a fwiiri pare̱pare̱pare̱.

ref: Six days later Jesus took Peter and the brothers James and John with him. They went up on a very high mountain where they could be alone.
text: Ŋke nsiye a kyo̱ŋ fe̱yɛ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ mɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ oo lee Peetroo na Yakubu na Yakubu mò̱ tire Yohanee, ne̱ mò̱ aa bamo̱ wo̱re̱ a yɔ ke̱be̱e̱ so̱swe̱e̱ ko̱ so̱so̱.

ref: So Peter said to him, “Lord, it is good for us to be here! Let us make three shelters, one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”
text: Ne̱ Peetroo a tɔwe̱ gywii Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, a bware fɛɛ fe̱yɛ ane̱ a bo̱ mfe̱e̱. Fo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱raa, mo̱ e̱ waa abwii asa, fo̱ ko̱ŋko̱, na Mosis ko̱ŋko̱, na Iliya ko̱ŋko̱.”

ref: While Peter was still speaking, the shadow of a bright cloud passed over them. From the cloud a voice said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him. Listen to what he says!”
text: Peetroo a saŋ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa‑ɔ, ne̱ ko̱wo̱re̱ to̱re̱se̱sɛ kuyayu a le̱e̱ so̱so̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ bamo̱ se̱. Ne̱ bo̱re̱ ko̱ a le̱e̱ ko̱wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱ mo̱ gyi timaa ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa mò̱ ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ, ne̱ mo̱ akatɔ a gyi mò̱ se̱‑ɔ. Mo̱nꞌ nu mò̱ ase̱ŋ.”

ref: When the disciples heard the voice, they were so afraid they fell flat on the ground.
text: Agyase̱po̱ asa amo̱ a nu bo̱re̱ amo̱‑ɔ, kufu a nya bamo̱ fɛɛ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa le̱e̱ da bamo̱ mme si, ne̱ baa be̱ra akatɔ bo̱ buŋ swe̱e̱re̱.

ref: But Jesus came over and touched them. He said, “Get up and don't be afraid!”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a bo̱ dabo̱rɔ bamo̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ ko̱so̱, mo̱ne̱ ma sa a kufu nya mo̱ne̱.”

ref: When they opened their eyes, they saw only Jesus.
text: Bamo̱ a yase̱ akatɔ de̱e̱re̱‑ɔ, bo̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ ŋu ɔko̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ, amɔ Yeesuu wo̱re̱ kpeŋ.

ref: The disciples asked Jesus, “Don't the teachers of the Law of Moses say Elijah must come before the Messiah does?”
text: Ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱ asa‑ɔ a bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ Iliya amo̱ ne̱ ane̱ a ŋu‑o e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ Kristoo ŋkpɛɛ a ɔ ba‑ɔ ne̱e̱?”

ref: In fact, he has already come. But the people did not recognize him and treated him just as they wanted to. They will soon make the Son of Man suffer in the same way.”
text: Amaa mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ Iliya a kyɔ ba, ne̱ ase̱sɛ mo̱ŋ pini mò̱. Ne̱ baa waa mò̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ba kpa‑ɔ. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ba waa mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, bo̱rɔkraa.”

ref: and said, “Lord, have pity on my son! He has a bad case of epilepsy and often falls into a fire or into water.
text: “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ fo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ ŋu mo̱ gyi nyaŋsɛ mɔ e̱wɛɛ na fo̱ kya mò̱. Ɔ lɔ kigbuŋgbuŋ ne̱e̱, ne̱ ke̱ e̱ tɔɔraa mò̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Saŋ ke̱maa, ke̱ e̱ twe̱e̱ mò̱ ke̱ e̱ da de̱e̱kpa‑rɔ bɛɛɛ nkyu‑ro.

ref: I brought him to your disciples, but none of them could heal him.”
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ ne̱ mo̱ a taa mò̱ baa fo̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ ase̱ mfe̱e̱, amaa bo̱ mo̱ŋ taare̱ kya mò̱.”

ref: Jesus said, “You people are too stubborn to have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you? Bring the boy here.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ke̱se̱bɔrɔle̱ŋ awuye, mo̱ne̱ maa kɔɔre̱ mo̱ mo̱ne̱ i gyi. Nsu mfe̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱ aa mo̱ne̱ a kye̱na? Bɛɛɛ nsu mfe̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ŋ nyite mo̱ne̱ pwɛɛ na mo̱nꞌ dɛɛ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ gyi‑o ne̱e̱? Mo̱nꞌ taa kayaagyi‑o baa mo̱ ase̱ mfe̱e̱.”

ref: Then Jesus spoke sternly to the demon. It went out of the boy, and right then he was healed.
text: Bamo̱ a taa mò̱ baa‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu a sa ibrisi ne̱ i te kayaagyi‑o si‑o kanɔ, ne̱ e̱ ya ko̱so̱ le̱e̱ mò̱ se̱. Puri amo̱‑rɔ ne̱ kanyaŋsɛɛ‑ɔ a kpaare̱.

text: Amo̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a ba mò̱ ase̱ keri si to̱ŋ ko̱ bo̱ bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ ane̱ mo̱ŋ taare̱ gya ibrisi amo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱?”

ref: Jesus replied: It is because you don't have enough faith! But I can promise you this. If you had faith no larger than a mustard seed, you could tell this mountain to move from here to there. And it would. Everything would be possible for you. Jesus Again Speaks about His Death (Mark
text: Amaa ane̱ŋ a ibrisi amo̱, mo̱ne̱ maa taare̱ a mo̱nꞌ gya bo̱ ko̱so̱ se̱sɛ se̱, amɔ mo̱ne̱ a ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ na mo̱ne̱ a kra ako̱ŋ pwɛɛ.]” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ mò̱ lowi ase̱ŋ‑ɔ (Maak

text: Yeesuu mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a naa ba muruwi Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ ba yɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ lɔŋ e̱se̱bɔ na mo̱nꞌ nu mo̱ ase̱. Ba taa mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, a bo̱ waa swe̱e̱re̱ mɔ se̱ ayaale̱ŋpo̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ.

ref: And when you welcome one of these children because of me, you welcome me. Temptations To Sin (Mark

text: Ne̱ Peetroo a bo̱ bise Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “To, mo̱ nyaŋpe̱. N taa fe̱yɛ mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ e̱ waa mo̱ bɔye̱ saŋ ke̱maa, waa iluwi e̱fe̱ne̱ ne̱ n taa mò̱ e̱bɔye̱‑ɔ bo̱ ke mò̱ na ŋ yɔwe̱? Iluwi isunoo bɛɛɛ e̱fe̱ne̱?”

ref: This story will show you what the kingdom of heaven is like: One day a king decided to call in his officials and ask them to give an account of what they owed him.
text: Mo̱nꞌ nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ a du Wuribware̱ se̱ a kuwure‑o‑ro‑o. N gye̱ owure ko̱ ya dɛɛ kye̱na aaa? Ne̱ mò̱ ayaafɔre̱‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ de mò̱ ko̱kɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ waa mò̱ mfɛɛre̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ te̱e̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ a ɔ bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱ na ɔ nya ŋu mò̱ aa bamo̱ mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ atanne̱ ne̱ a bo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ na ɔ kɔɔre̱ le̱e̱ bamo̱ ase̱.

ref: As he was doing this, one official was brought in who owed him
text: Mò̱ a le̱e̱ kaase̱ ɔ waa amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa baa mò̱ ayaafɔre̱‑ɔ ɔko̱ ne̱ o de mò̱ ko̱kɔ siidii ŋkpe̱ŋkɛɛ kakpe̱ŋ (GHC

ref: But he didn't have any money to pay what he owed. The king ordered him to be sold, along with his wife and children and all he owned, in order to pay the debt.
text: Owure a kpa a ɔ kɔɔre̱ atanne̱‑ɔ le̱e̱ mò̱ ase̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔ yɛ ɔ maa taare̱ a ɔ ka. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ owure‑o yɛ bo̱ taa mò̱ kapo̱tɛɛ pɛɛɛ na mò̱ ka na bamo̱ ŋyaagyi fe sa ɔko̱ na bo̱ kii se̱sɛ‑ɔ anya. Na bo̱ taa atanne̱ ne̱ baa nya‑ɔ baa bo̱ ka ko̱kɔ‑ɔ.

ref: The official got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you every cent I owe!”
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱ amo̱ a kpuni aŋurii owure‑o ayaa‑rɔ‑ɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ fo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ nyite mo̱, na mo̱ e̱ ka fo̱ ko̱kɔ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ.’

ref: The king felt sorry for him and let him go free. He even told the official that he did not have to pay back the money.

ref: The man got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you back.”
text: Ne̱ mò̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ɔ a kpuni aŋurii mò̱ ayaa‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, ‘Mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ fo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ nyite mo̱, na mo̱ e̱ ka fo̱ ko̱kɔ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ.’

ref: But the first official refused to have pity. Instead, he went and had the other official put in jail until he could pay what he owed.
text: Amaa ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ nu ko̱ko̱re̱ ne̱ mò̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ɔ a ko̱re̱ mò̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ ɔ yɛ bo̱ kra mò̱ tii tiikpa bo̱ fo̱ saŋ ne̱ ɔ ka atanne̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a ɔ bo̱ sa mò̱‑ɔ.

ref: When some other officials found out what had happened, they felt sorry for the man who had been put in jail. Then they told the king what had happened.
text: Mò̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana ayaafɔre̱ ne̱ baa saŋ‑ɔ a ŋu ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ waa‑ɔ, ne̱ bamo̱ iduŋ a fwii bwe̱e̱tɔ, ne̱ baa ya tɔwe̱ gywii owure‑o.

ref: The king called the first official back in and said, “You're an evil man! When you begged for mercy, I said you did not have to pay back a cent.
text: Amo̱se̱ ne̱ owure‑o a te̱e̱ ane̱ŋ a ke̱yaafɔre̱ amo̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Kuŋurole̱ŋ wuye ke̱yaafɔre̱ e̱ gye̱ fo̱. Ko̱kɔ ne̱ fo̱ de mo̱‑ɔ kyɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ bɛɛɛ? Amaa fo̱ a ko̱re̱ mo̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ nyite fo̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ a taa fo̱ ko̱kɔ amo̱ bo̱ ke fo̱.

ref: The king was so angry that he ordered the official to be tortured until he could pay back everything he owed.
text: Ne̱ owure‑o duŋ a fwii mò̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱ amo̱ se̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ sa kanɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ya tii mò̱ tiikpa waa mò̱ bo̱rɔkraa bo̱ fo̱ saŋ ne̱ ɔ taare̱ a ɔ ka atanne̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ‑ɔ. Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ saase̱bɛɛ.

ref: That is how my Father in heaven will treat you, if you don't forgive each of my followers with all your heart.
text: Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱, n se̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ, e̱ waa mo̱ne̱ ɔke̱maa‑ɔ ne̱e̱, amɔ fo̱ a lee fo̱ kame‑ro taa fo̱ bɛɛko̱ e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ ke mò̱ fe̱raa.”

ref: When Jesus finished teaching, he left Galilee and went to the part of Judea east of the Jordan River.
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ yɔ Yude̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ a ke̱be̱gya ne̱ ke̱ bo̱ Yo̱o̱daŋ bo̱ŋ nno̱ŋ a bo̱ŋbe̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ.

ref: Some people are unable to marry because of birth defects or because of what someone has done to their bodies. Others stay single in order to serve God better. Anyone who can accept this teaching should do so.” Jesus Blesses Little Children (Mark
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ anyare̱ bo̱ko̱ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ baa ko̱we̱ bamo̱ na bo̱ maa taare̱ a bo̱ ko̱we̱ ŋyaagyi sa bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱. Bo̱ko̱ mɔ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ baa lee bamo̱ awɛɛ. Ne̱ bo̱ko̱ mɔ be̱e̱ bo̱ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ bamo̱ a pee akatɔ Wuribware̱ se̱ a kuwure‑o e̱kpa se̱ ke̱gya‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, bo̱ yɛ bo̱ maa waare̱e̱. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ɔko̱ e̱ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ mò̱ e̱ taare̱ a ɔ waa ane̱ŋ, mo̱nꞌ sa a ɔ waa.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a yure ŋyaagyi‑o (Maak

ref: Large crowds followed him, and he healed their sick.
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ biribiri a gya mò̱ se̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ kya bamo̱ alɔpo̱‑ɔ mfe̱ŋ.

ref: Some Pharisees wanted to test Jesus. They came up to him and asked, “Is it right for a man to divorce his wife for just any reason?”
text: Mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, Farisii awuye ko̱ a ba mò̱ ase̱ a bo̱ lɔɔre̱ mò̱ kanɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Ane̱ mbraa‑ɔ e̱ sa kpa fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ kine mò̱ ka bo̱ le̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ɔnyare̱‑ɔ e̱ fa fe̱yɛ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ a waa‑ɔ se̱ aaa?”

ref: Jesus answered, “Don't you know in the beginning the Creator made a man and a woman?
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ kare̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ saŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a twe̱e̱ kaye̱‑rɔ atɔ pɛɛɛ‑ɔ, ɔɔ twe̱e̱ ɔnyare̱ na ɔkye̱e̱ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱?

ref: That's why a man leaves his father and mother and gets married. He becomes like one person with his wife.
text: Ne̱ a be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Amo̱se̱ ne̱ ɔnyare̱ e̱ le̱e̱ mò̱ se̱ na mò̱ nyi lɔŋ‑nɔ na ɔ waare̱e̱, na mò̱ aa mò̱ ka a waa kanɔ kye̱na kii ko̱ko̱ŋko̱.’

ref: Then they are no longer two people, but one. And no one should separate a couple God has joined together.”
text: Amo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ bo̱ gye̱ abɛɛ anyɔ. Amaa Wuribware̱ a sa ne̱ baa kii se̱sɛ ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱e̱. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ se̱sɛ mo̱ŋ de kpa fe̱yɛ mò̱ a brawe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a bo̱ waa abɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ.”

ref: Jesus replied, “You are so heartless! That's why Moses allowed you to divorce your wife. But from the beginning God did not intend it to be that way.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mosis a sa mo̱ne̱ kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ kine mo̱ne̱ aka, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ aŋu‑ro bo̱ le̱ŋ ne̱e̱ fɛɛ. Amaa mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ hare̱e̱ le̱e̱ saŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a twe̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa‑ɔ, a mo̱ŋ waa mò̱ kɔne̱ fe̱yɛ ɔnyare̱ a kine mò̱ ka.

ref: I say if your wife has not committed some terrible sexual sin, you must not divorce her to marry someone else. If you do, you are unfaithful.”
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ɔnyare̱ ma kine mò̱ ka ke̱tɔ ke̱maa se̱, amɔ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ a kyɔ le̱e̱ mò̱ kamɛɛ fe̱raa. Ne̱ n gye̱ ane̱ŋ, ɔnyare̱‑ɔ ya waare̱e̱ ɔkye̱e̱ baŋbaŋ, ɔ waa bɔye̱. Mò̱ ke̱be̱e̱ waare̱e̱ ɔkye̱e̱ baŋbaŋ‑ɔ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ a waa kakye̱e̱kpa‑ɔ.”

ref: Jesus told them, “Only those people who have been given the gift of staying single can accept this teaching.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “N gye̱ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ba taare̱ a bo̱ sure ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ se̱, amɔ bamo̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a sa bamo̱ ane̱ŋ a e̱ye̱e̱ ke̱kra‑ɔ.

ref: Some people brought their children to Jesus, so he could place his hands on them and pray for them. His disciples told the people to stop bothering him.
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ ko̱ a baa bamo̱ ŋyaagyi Yeesuu ase̱, na ɔ ba mò̱ asare̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ bamo̱ se̱, na ɔ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ sa bamo̱. Amaa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ yɛ bo̱ nare̱, na bo̱ ma lɛɛ tɔɔraa mò̱.

ref: A man came to Jesus and asked, “Teacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life?”
text: Ne̱ ɔnyare̱ ko̱ a ba Yeesuu ase̱, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Ɔkaapo̱po̱, nte̱tɔ ke̱tɔ timaa ne̱ ŋ waa na ŋ nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ?”

ref: “Which ones?” the man asked. Jesus answered, “Do not murder. Be faithful in marriage. Do not steal. Do not tell lies about others.
text: Ne̱ ɔnyare̱‑ɔ a bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ mbraa?” Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mbraa ne̱ ŋ yɛ, ‘Ɔko̱ ma mɔɔ mò̱ bɛɛko̱, ɔko̱ ma waa kakye̱e̱kpa, ɔko̱ ma kaŋ ywii, ɔko̱ ma kaŋ te̱ŋ mò̱ bɛɛko̱ kanɔ,

ref: Jesus said to him, “Why do you ask me about what is good? Only God is good. If you want to have eternal life, you must obey his commandments.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ fo̱ i bise mo̱ atɔ timaa si? Ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ otimaa be̱e̱ bo̱ tii Wuribware̱ se̱. To, fo̱ e̱ kpa a fo̱ nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ, gya Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ se̱.”

ref: But Jesus said, “Let the children come to me, and don't try to stop them! People who are like these children belong to God's kingdom.”
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ Yeesuu fe̱raa yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ma kaŋ gya ŋyaagyi mɔ le̱e̱ mo̱ ase̱. Mo̱nꞌ sa a bo̱ ba mo̱ ase̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ a ŋyaagyi mɔ na bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ du fe̱yɛ ŋyaagyi mɔ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ bo̱ tii Wuribware̱ se̱ a kuwure‑o si.”

ref: But many who are now first will be last, and many who are last will be first.
text: Amaa ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ i kii nsii-amɛɛ, na bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ nsii-amɛɛ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ mɔ kii agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱.”

ref: Respect your father and mother. And love others as much as you love yourself.”
text: na ɔke̱maa bu mò̱ se̱ na mò̱ nyi,’ na ‘Ɔke̱maa kpa mò̱ bɛɛko̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ kpa mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱‑ɔ.’ ”

ref: The young man said, “I have obeyed all of these. What else must I do?”
text: Ne̱ ɔnyare̱ amo̱ a be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ a gya mbraa mɔ pɛɛɛ se̱. Nte̱tɔ ya saŋ ne̱ a bware fe̱yɛ m be̱e̱ waa?”

ref: Jesus replied, “If you want to be perfect, go sell everything you own! Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come and be my follower.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ fo̱ kii se̱sɛ timaa lo̱we̱, amo̱ fe̱raa, nare̱ ya fe ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ fo̱ de‑o, na fo̱ taa atanne̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ bo̱ sa atiripo̱. Fo̱ ya waa ane̱ŋ fe̱raa, Wuribware̱ e̱ kpa kapo̱tɛɛ a ɔ bo̱ ye̱ra so̱so̱ sa fo̱. Na fo̱ bo̱ gya mo̱ se̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ.”

ref: When the young man heard this, he went away sad, because he was very rich.
text: Ɔnyare̱ amo̱ a nu mfaanɛɛ‑ɔ, a mo̱ŋ waa mò̱ kɔne̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ o de kapo̱tɛɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱re̱ le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ yɔ mò̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ.

ref: Jesus said to his disciples, “I tell you, it's terribly hard for rich people to get into the kingdom of heaven!
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “A waa le̱ŋ fe̱yɛ atɔ wuye a sure si fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ gye̱ mò̱ owure.

ref: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
text: Nyɔma e̱ taare̱ a o lwee basa bɔ‑rɔ dare̱e̱ aaa? Amaa mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ atɔ wuye kusure si fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ gye̱ mò̱ owure bo̱ le̱ŋ a kyɔ nyɔma kulwee basa bɔ‑rɔ dare̱e̱.”

ref: Peter replied, “Remember, we have left everything to be your followers! What will we get?”
text: Ne̱ Peetroo yɛ, “Ke̱e̱ ɛ, ane̱ a yɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ane̱ gya fo̱ se̱‑ɔ, nte̱tɔ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ nya?”

ref: Jesus answered: Yes, all of you have become my followers. And so in the future world, when the Son of Man sits on his glorious throne, I promise you will sit on twelve thrones to judge the twelve tribes of Israel.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ saŋ ko̱ e̱ ba a ɔ bo̱ ba, na kaye̱ mɔ gye̱ kɛɛ, na kaye̱ po̱pwɛɛ ba. Na saŋ amo̱‑ɔ, mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, nya ke̱dabe̱ na wuraa na n kye̱na mo̱ kuwuregya se̱ gyi mo̱ kuwure. Na mo̱ne̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a gya mo̱ se̱‑ɔ kye̱na ŋwuregya kudu ŋnyɔ se̱, na mo̱nꞌ gyi ane̱ Isireelii awuye a nsuro kudu ŋnyɔ‑ɔ kuwure.

ref: After he had agreed to pay them the usual amount for a day's work, he sent them off to his vineyard.
text: Ne̱ ɔ yɛ ɔ sa bamo̱ siidii kudu na bo̱ yɔ ya suŋ sa mò̱ kake amo̱ pɛɛɛ. Ne̱ baa sure si, ne̱ baa yɔ mò̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ ya suŋ.

ref: About nine that morning, the man saw some other people standing in the market with nothing to do.
text: Kyɔwe̱ a daŋ kafwe̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ yɔ kawu be̱e̱ ya to̱ anyare̱ ko̱ na bo̱ ye̱re̱ ko̱lɔŋde̱ se̱ bo̱ maa waa sɛye̱.

ref: So they went. At noon and again about three in the afternoon he returned to the market. And each time he made the same agreement with others who were loafing around with nothing to do.
text: Mpase̱ a fo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ yɔ kawu ya ŋu bo̱ko̱, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ dɛɛ gywii bamo̱, ne̱ baa sure si. Kyɔwe̱ kikpeerebe̱e̱ a fo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ ya ŋu bo̱ko̱, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ dɛɛ gywii bamo̱, ne̱ baa sure si.

ref: Finally, about five in the afternoon the man went back and found some others standing there. He asked them, “Why have you been standing here all day long doing nothing?”
text: Ke̱de̱e̱pwɛta a fo̱ ne̱ kyɔwe̱ e̱ kpa a ɔ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ yɔ kawu be̱e̱ ya to̱ anyare̱ ko̱ ye̱re̱ na bo̱ maa waa sɛye̱. Ne̱ oo bise bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘E̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a ye̱re̱ mfe̱e̱ le̱e̱ gye̱gyaye̱ bo̱ fo̱ ke̱de̱e̱pwɛta na mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ waa sɛye̱?’

ref: “Because no one has hired us,” they answered. Then he told them to go work in his vineyard.
text: Ne̱ baa be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ taa ane̱ a ane̱ suŋ sa mò̱.’ Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, ‘To, mo̱ne̱ gbaa, mo̱nꞌ nare̱ mo̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ ya suŋ sa mo̱.’ Ne̱ baa yɔ.

ref: That evening the owner of the vineyard told the man in charge of the workers to call them in and give them their money. He also told the man to begin with the ones who were hired last.
text: Kyɔwe̱ a lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ wuye‑o a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ ke̱paafo̱wɔ bre̱sɛ fe̱yɛ, ‘Te̱e̱ mo̱ apafo̱wɔ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱ na fo̱ ka bamo̱ ko̱kɔ. Na fo̱ ka bamo̱ ne̱ baa sii kamɛɛ yɔ mo̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ ko̱kɔ pwɛɛ na fo̱ dɛɛ ka bamo̱ ne̱ baa gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ yɔ‑ɔ.’

ref: When the workers arrived, the ones who had been hired at five in the afternoon were given a full day's pay.
text: Ne̱ apafo̱wɔ ne̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ wuye‑o a taa bamo̱ lalalo̱we̱ bo̱ suŋ mò̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ a ba akatɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ ka bamo̱ ɔke̱maa siidii kudu.

ref: they began complaining to the owner of the vineyard.
text: Bamo̱ a ŋu atanne̱ ne̱ baa nya‑ɔ, ne̱ baa tɛɛ.

ref: They said, “The ones who were hired last worked for only one hour. But you paid them the same that you did us. And we worked in the hot sun all day long!”
text: Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, ‘Anyare̱ mɔ ne̱ baa ba fo̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ mfe̱e̱ saŋ ne̱ kyɔwe̱ e̱ kpa a ɔ lo̱we̱‑ɔ a suŋ kusuŋ dɔŋhwe̱re̱e̱ ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱e̱. Ne̱ ane̱ mɔ ane̱ a kpo̱ne̱ suŋ kusuŋ ibiri mɔ pɛɛɛ na kyɔwe̱ a da ane̱. Amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ gbaa fo̱ a ka ane̱ aa bamo̱ atanne̱ ko̱ŋko̱ dɛɛ ne̱e̱.’

ref: Take your money now and go! What business is it of yours if I want to pay them the same that I paid you?
text: To, taa fo̱ kakɔka a fo̱ nare̱ pe̱. Mo̱ ya ka fo̱ aa ɔnyare̱ mɔ ne̱ oo sii kamɛɛ bo̱ dɔɔ mo̱‑ɔ atanne̱ ko̱ŋko̱ gbaa ooo, a mo̱ŋ gye̱ fo̱ ase̱ŋ.

ref: Don't I have the right to do what I want with my own money? Why should you be jealous, if I want to be generous?”
text: Mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ atanne̱ ne̱e̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ n de kpa fe̱yɛ m ba amo̱ bo̱ waa ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ. Bɛɛɛ a bo̱ fo̱ gya fe̱yɛ mo̱ a waa ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱?’ ”

ref: Jesus then said, “So it is. Everyone who is now last will be first, and everyone who is first will be last.” Jesus Again Tells about His Death (Mark
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ saase̱bɛɛ. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ nsii-amɛɛ i kii agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱, na agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ mɔ kii nsii-amɛɛ.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ mò̱ lowi ase̱ŋ sase̱po̱‑ɔ (Maak

ref: When Jesus heard them, he stopped and asked, “What do you want me to do for you?”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a ye̱re̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ bamo̱ baa mò̱ ase̱. Bamo̱ a ba‑ɔ, ne̱ oo bise bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ŋ waa sa mo̱ne̱?”

ref: Jesus felt sorry for them and touched their eyes. At once they could see, and they became his followers.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a ŋu bamo̱ e̱wɛɛ, ne̱ ɔɔ dabo̱rɔ bamo̱ akatɔ. Puri amo̱‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ bamo̱ akatɔ a buŋŋi. Ne̱ baa gya mò̱ se̱.

ref: The crowd told them to be quiet, but they shouted even louder, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ a fɛɛ bamo̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ laatɔ. Amaa ŋkee gbaa ne̱ baa me̱raa se̱ ba fɛɛ‑rɔ keŋkeŋ‑o ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱, Deefid mò̱ nana, ŋu ane̱ e̱wɛɛ.”
