text: Amaa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ kaapo̱‑ɔ kaase̱, bamo̱ mɔ kufu de bamo̱ a bo̱ bise mò̱. Agyase̱po̱‑ɔ‑rɔ nsɛ e̱ gye̱ bre̱sɛ? (Matiyo

ref: If your foot causes you to sin, chop it off. You would be better off to go into life lame than to have two feet and be thrown into hell.
text: Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Aso̱ŋsɔɔ ne̱ a wo̱ mfe̱ŋ a awuye‑o maa wu daa, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ mɔ ne̱ de̱e̱kpa ne̱ ɔ kywɛɛ bamo̱‑ɔ maa duŋ.’]

ref: Jesus and his disciples went to his home in Capernaum. After they were inside the house, Jesus asked them, “What were you arguing about along the way?”
text: Ne̱ baa ba Kapaaniyum maŋ‑nɔ. Bamo̱ a lwee Yeesuu lɔŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ oo bise mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ntɔ ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i gyiiri ikii kpa‑rɔ‑ɔ ne̱e̱?”

ref: They had been arguing about which one of them was the greatest, and so they did not answer.
text: Amaa bo̱ mo̱ŋ be̱ŋŋaa Yeesuu, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ baa gyiiri ikii kpa‑ɔ‑rɔ ne̱e̱, baa kpa bamo̱‑rɔ mò̱ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ bre̱sɛ‑ɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: After Jesus sat down and told the twelve disciples to gather around him, he said, “If you want the place of honor, you must become a slave and serve others!”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kye̱na, ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱ kudu anyɔ‑ɔ baa mò̱ ase̱, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ kpa fe̱yɛ o kii mo̱ne̱‑rɔ ɔbre̱sɛ‑ɔ waa mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ pɛɛɛ‑rɔ kayaagyi na o suŋ mo̱ne̱.”

ref: Salt is good. But if it no longer tastes like salt, how can it be made salty again? Have salt among you and live at peace with each other.
text: Mfɔre̱ bo̱ kɔne̱, amaa mmo̱ kɔne̱‑ɔ ya le̱e̱ mmo̱‑rɔ, mmo̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ n gye̱ mfɔre̱. Kɔne̱ amo̱ a le̱e̱‑ɔ, ɔko̱ maa taare̱ a ɔ bo̱rɔ kpa ko̱ se̱ be̱e̱ sa a ŋ kii mfɔre̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ kye̱na kaye̱e̱yuri‑ro, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kaye̱e̱yuri du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mfɔre̱, ne̱ ŋ waa ateese kɔne̱‑ɔ.”

ref: John said, “Teacher, we saw a man using your name to force demons out of people. But he wasn't one of us, and we told him to stop.”
text: Ne̱ Yohanee ne̱ ɔ gye̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ ɔko̱‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ gywii Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Ɔkaapo̱po̱, ane̱ a ŋu ɔko̱ de fo̱ ke̱nyare̱ ɔ gya ibrisi ɔ ko̱so̱ ase̱sɛ se̱. Ne̱ ane̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ yɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ ko̱waa, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ mo̱ŋ tii ane̱ se̱.”

ref: Jesus said to his disciples: Don't stop him! No one who works miracles in my name will soon turn and say something bad about me.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ma lɛɛ gya mò̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ ne̱ o de mo̱ ke̱nyare̱ ɔ waa atɔ dabe̱‑ɔ maa lɛɛ taare̱ a ɔ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ bɔye̱ bo̱ kye mo̱ puri amo̱‑rɔ.

ref: Anyone who isn't against us is for us.
text: Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ ane̱ ɔdo̱ŋ‑ɔ bo̱ ane̱ ke̱be̱gya ne̱e̱.

ref: And anyone who gives you a cup of water in my name, just because you belong to me, will surely be rewarded. Temptations To Sin (Matthew
text: Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ sa mo̱ne̱ nkyu a mo̱nꞌ nuu bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ a gye̱ Kristoo agyase̱po̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ e̱ nya ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ kakɔka.” Ko̱kyɔrɔke̱e̱ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ baa e̱bɔye̱ ko̱waa‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: It will be terrible for people who cause even one of my little followers to sin. Those people would be better off thrown into the ocean with a heavy stone tied around their necks.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya se̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔko̱ ne̱ mò̱ ya sa ne̱ ŋyaagyi ngyingyii mɔ ne̱ ba kɔɔre̱ mo̱ ba gyi‑o ɔko̱ ya waa e̱bɔye̱, amo̱ fe̱raa bamo̱ ya ba kibu dabe̱ bo̱ da se̱sɛ‑ɔ ke̱bɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ baa twe̱e̱ mò̱ waa ɔpo̱o̱‑rɔ, a bɔ fe̱yɛ mò̱ a ŋu ke̱se̱bɔgyiiri ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ taa a ɔ bo̱ laye̱ ane̱ŋ a e̱bɔye̱ awaapo̱‑ɔ.

ref: So if your hand causes you to sin, cut it off! You would be better off to go into life paralyzed than to have two hands and be thrown into the fires of hell that never go out.
text: Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Aso̱ŋsɔɔ ne̱ a wo̱ mfe̱ŋ a awuye‑o maa wu daa, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ mɔ ne̱ de̱e̱kpa ne̱ ɔ kywɛɛ bamo̱‑ɔ maa duŋ.’]

ref: If your eye causes you to sin, get rid of it. You would be better off to go into God's kingdom with only one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into hell.
text: Ne̱ fo̱ ko̱ mɔ ne̱ fo̱ ke̱katɔ ko̱ e̱ sa ne̱ fo̱ e̱ waa bɔye̱‑ɔ, lee ke̱mo̱ bo̱ twe̱e̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ ya kii ke̱katɔ ko̱ŋko̱ wuye, ne̱ fo̱ a nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ, a bɔ fe̱yɛ fo̱ akatɔ anyɔ a te fo̱ se̱, ne̱ fo̱ a bo̱ lwee awo̱re̱fɔɔ de̱e̱kpa‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: After Jesus left, he went to Judea and then on to the other side of the Jordan River. Once again large crowds came to him, and as usual, he taught them.
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ Yude̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱. Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ te̱ŋ Yo̱o̱daŋ bo̱ŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ yɔ nno̱ŋ a bo̱ŋbe̱‑ɔ se̱. Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ase̱sɛ a le̱e̱ ba ba mò̱ ase̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ kaapo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ kyɔ ɔ naa ɔ kaapo̱‑ɔ.

text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱ ya kine mò̱ kuri ya waare̱e̱ ɔnyare̱ baŋbaŋ, ne̱ ɔɔ waa kanyare̱kpa.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a yure ŋyaagyi‑o (Matiyo

ref: Some Pharisees wanted to test Jesus. So they came up to him and asked if it was right for a man to divorce his wife.
text: Ne̱ Farisii awuye ko̱ a ba mò̱ ase̱ mfe̱ŋ a bo̱ lɔɔre̱ mò̱ kanɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ baa bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “To, tɔwe̱ gywii ane̱. Ane̱ mbraa‑ɔ e̱ sa kpa fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ kine mò̱ ka aaa?”

ref: Jesus asked them, “What does the Law of Moses say about this?”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a ba se̱ŋbise bo̱ be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mbraa mo̱ ne̱ Mosis a sa mo̱ne̱?”

ref: But in the beginning God made a man and a woman.
text: Amaa saŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a twe̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa‑ɔ, ɔɔ twe̱e̱ ɔnyare̱ na ɔkye̱e̱ ne̱e̱, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ.

ref: And no one should separate a couple that God has joined together.”
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, se̱sɛ mo̱ŋ de kpa fe̱yɛ mò̱ a brawe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a bo̱ waa abɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ.”

ref: When Jesus and his disciples were back in the house, they asked him about what he had said.
text: Saŋ ne̱ baa kiŋŋi yɔ lɔŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a bise Yeesuu ase̱ŋ bo̱ le̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱‑ɔ se̱.

ref: He told them, “A man who divorces his wife and marries someone else is unfaithful to his wife.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔnyare̱ ya kine mò̱ ka, ne̱ ɔɔ ya waare̱e̱ ɔkye̱e̱ baŋbaŋ, ɔɔ waa bɔye̱. Mò̱ ke̱be̱e̱ waare̱e̱ ɔkye̱e̱ baŋbaŋ‑ɔ a sa ne̱ ɔɔ waa kakye̱e̱kpa.

ref: Some people brought their children to Jesus so he could bless them by placing his hands on them. But his disciples told the people to stop bothering him.
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ ko̱ a baa bamo̱ ŋyaagyi Yeesuu ase̱ na ɔ ba mò̱ asare̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ bamo̱ se̱ yure bamo̱. Amɔ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ e̱ kpa a bo̱ gya bamo̱ bo̱ kiŋŋi.

ref: I promise you that you cannot get into God's kingdom, unless you accept it the way a child does.”
text: Na mo̱nꞌ nyiŋŋi si fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ maa sure si fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ gyi kuwure fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ŋyaagyi mɔ a sure si‑o, maa tii Wuribware̱ a kuwure‑o si.”

ref: When Jesus saw this, he became angry and said, “Let the children come to me! Don't try to stop them. People who are like these little children belong to the kingdom of God.
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a ŋu na ba waa ane̱ŋ‑ɔ, a mo̱ŋ waa mò̱ kɔne̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ma gya ŋyaagyi‑o. Mo̱nꞌ sa a bo̱ ba mo̱ ase̱, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ a ŋyaagyi mɔ na bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ du fe̱yɛ bamo̱ mɔ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ bo̱ tii Wuribware̱ a kuwure‑o si.

ref: Jesus looked closely at the man. He liked him and said, “There's one thing you still need to do. Go sell everything you own. Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come with me.”
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a de̱e̱re̱ ɔnyare̱ amo̱, ne̱ mò̱ ase̱ŋ a waa mò̱ kɔne̱, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Ke̱tɔ ko̱ŋko̱ ya saŋ fo̱ a fo̱ waa na fo̱ ya wu na fo̱ kii atɔ wuye Wuribware̱ se̱. Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ ya fe fo̱ kapo̱tɛɛ pɛɛɛ, na fo̱ taa atanne̱‑ɔ sa atiripo̱, na fo̱ bo̱ gya mo̱ se̱.”

ref: When the man heard Jesus say this, he went away gloomy and sad because he was very rich.
text: Ɔnyare̱ amo̱ a nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱‑ɔ, a mo̱ŋ waa mò̱ kɔne̱, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ o de kapo̱tɛɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱re̱ o yii mò̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ.

ref: Jesus looked around and said to his disciples, “It's hard for rich people to get into God's kingdom!”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kii de̱e̱re̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “A waa le̱ŋ fe̱yɛ atɔ wuye a sure si fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ gye̱ mò̱ owure.”

ref: The disciples were shocked to hear this. So Jesus told them again, “It's terribly hard to get into God's kingdom!
text: Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ, aa waa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ ase̱ŋ dabe̱. Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ kya se̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ gyi‑ana, a waa le̱ŋ fɛɛ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ a sure si fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ gye̱ mò̱ owure.

ref: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
text: Nyɔma e̱ taare̱ a o lwee basa bɔ‑rɔ dare̱e̱ aaa? Amaa atɔ wuye kusure si fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ gye̱ mò̱ owure bo̱ le̱ŋ ko̱ kyɔ nyɔma kulwee basa bɔ‑rɔ dare̱e̱.”

ref: Jesus looked at them and said, “There are some things that people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a de̱e̱re̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Se̱sɛ fe̱raa maa taare̱ a ɔ nya ane̱ŋ a ŋkpa‑ɔ, amɔ Wuribware̱ a sa mò̱ kpa. Ko̱tɔko̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ na Wuribware̱ maa taare̱ a ɔ waa ke̱mo̱.”

ref: The disciples were confused as Jesus led them toward Jerusalem, and his other followers were afraid. Once again, Jesus took the twelve disciples aside and told them what was going to happen to him. He said:
text: Kamɛɛ‑rɔ ne̱ Yeesuu mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a me̱raa se̱ ba yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ, na Yeesuu gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ. Bamo̱ a yɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ iduŋ saŋ e̱ te̱ŋŋe̱ bamo̱. Kufu gbaa a nya ase̱sɛ ne̱ bamo̱‑ɔ gbaa bo̱ gya bamo̱ se̱‑ɔ. Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ lee mò̱ agyase̱po̱ kudu anyɔ‑ɔ keri si, ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ ɔ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ a ba a a bo̱ to̱ mò̱‑ɔ o gywii bamo̱.

ref: We are now on our way to Jerusalem where the Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses. They will sentence him to death and hand him over to foreigners,
text: Ɔɔ tɔwe̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ nu mo̱ ase̱. Ane̱ e̱ yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ba ba mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, a bo̱ bo̱ waa Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ na Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ‑ɔ. Bamo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ba gyi mo̱ ase̱ŋ na bo̱ bu mo̱ ke̱pɔ, na ŋkee bo̱ taa mo̱ sa Roma awuye ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ ane̱ maŋ‑ɔ, a bo̱ mɔɔ mo̱.

ref: and they answered, “When you come into your glory, please let one of us sit at your right side and the other at your left.”
text: Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Fo̱ ya kye̱na fo̱ kuwure dabe̱‑ɔ se̱, na fo̱ sa a ane̱ kye̱na fo̱ ase̱, ɔko̱ kye̱na fo̱ kigyise si na ɔko̱ mɔ kye̱na fo̱ ke̱be̱na se̱.”

ref: Jesus told them, “You don't really know what you're asking! Are you able to drink from the cup that I must soon drink from or be baptized as I must be baptized?”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi ane̱ŋ ne̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i bise mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ du‑o. Mo̱ne̱ e̱ taare̱ a mo̱nꞌ gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ ba a m bo̱ gyi‑o ooo, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ taare̱ a mo̱nꞌ nyite ase̱ŋ ne̱ ase̱sɛ e̱ taa a bo̱ bo̱ laye̱ mo̱‑ɔ ɔɔɔ?”

ref: “Yes, we are!” James and John answered. Then Jesus replied, “You certainly will drink from the cup from which I must drink. And you will be baptized just as I must!
text: Ne̱ baa be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ e̱ taare̱.” Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ i gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ na ase̱sɛ taa ase̱ŋ bo̱ laye̱ mo̱‑ɔ, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ a waa mo̱ne̱‑ɔ.

ref: But it isn't for me to say who will sit at my right side and at my left. This is for God to decide.”
text: Amaa n gye̱ mo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ mo̱ i lee mò̱ ne̱ ɔ kye̱na mo̱ kigyise si‑o na mò̱‑ɔ mò̱ ne̱ ɔ kye̱na mo̱ ke̱be̱na se̱‑ɔ. Wuribware̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ o lee e̱kye̱nakpa amo̱ a ɔ bo̱ sa bamo̱ ne̱ ɔɔ lɔŋŋɔ mfe̱ŋ bo̱ ye̱ra bamo̱‑ɔ.”

ref: But Jesus called the disciples together and said: You know that those foreigners who call themselves kings like to order their people around. And their great leaders have full power over the people they rule.
text: Yeesuu a ŋu fe̱yɛ bamo̱ iduŋ a fwii‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ne̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ nyi fe̱yɛ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ase̱sɛ‑rɔ fe̱raa, bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ de maŋ‑ɔ na agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ bo̱ de ke̱yaale̱ŋ ba gyi ɔke̱maa se̱ ɛ?

ref: And if you want to be first, you must be everyone's slave.
text: Ne̱ mò̱‑ɔ mò̱ ne̱ ɔ kpa fe̱yɛ o kii mo̱ne̱‑rɔ ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ waa mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ pɛɛɛ‑rɔ ke̱nya.

ref: The Son of Man did not come to be a slave master, but a slave who will give his life to rescue many people. Jesus Heals Blind Bartimaeus (Matthew
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, gbaa mo̱ŋ ba fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ waa asuŋ sa mo̱ ne̱e̱. Mo̱ a ba a m bo̱ waa asuŋ sa ase̱sɛ ne̱e̱, na ŋ wu bo̱ sa ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ, na ŋ ka ko̱kɔ sa bamo̱, na bo̱ nya nya bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a buŋŋi te̱napo̱ Bar‑Timiyus akatɔ‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: Jesus and his disciples went to Jericho. And as they were leaving, they were followed by a large crowd. A blind beggar by the name of Bartimaeus son of Timaeus was sitting beside the road.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a bo̱rɔ Yɛrikoo maŋ‑nɔ. Bamo̱ a le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ a gya bamo̱ se̱. Timiyus mò̱ gyi te̱napo̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Bar‑Timiyus‑o mɔ bo̱ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ o te kabo̱re̱ kɛɛ saŋ ke̱maa ɔ ko̱re̱ atɔ.

ref: When he heard that it was Jesus from Nazareth, he shouted, “Jesus, Son of David, have pity on me!”
text: Te̱napo̱ amo̱ a te kabo̱re̱ kɛɛ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ oo nu fe̱yɛ Yeesuu Nasarɛte̱nyi e̱ kyo̱ŋ kabo̱re̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ ɔ fɛɛ‑rɔ keŋkeŋ fe̱yɛ, “Yeesuu, Deefid mò̱ nana, ŋu mo̱ e̱wɛɛ.”

ref: Many people told the man to stop, but he shouted even louder, “Son of David, have pity on me!”
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ a fɛɛ mò̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ laatɔ. Amaa ŋkee gbaa ne̱ ɔɔ me̱raa se̱ ɔ fɛɛ‑rɔ keŋkeŋ‑o ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “Deefid mò̱ nana, ŋu mo̱ e̱wɛɛ.”

ref: Jesus stopped and said, “Call him over!” They called out to the blind man and said, “Don't be afraid! Come on! He is calling for you.”
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa ne̱ Yeesuu a ye̱re̱, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ te̱e̱ mò̱.” Ne̱ baa te̱e̱ te̱napo̱ amo̱, ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ko̱so̱ na fo̱ sa a fo̱ akatɔ gyi, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ yɛ fo̱ ba.”

ref: The man threw off his coat as he jumped up and ran to Jesus.
text: Ne̱ oo buŋŋi mò̱ waagya, ne̱ oo fuwi ywe̱e̱, ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ Yeesuu ase̱.

ref: Jesus asked, “What do you want me to do for you?” The blind man answered, “Master, I want to see!”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ŋ waa sa fo̱?” Ne̱ te̱napo̱‑ɔ yɛ, “Ɔkaapo̱po̱, mo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ fo̱ buŋŋi mo̱ akatɔ ne̱e̱, na n de̱e̱ nya ŋu atɔ.”

ref: Jesus told him, “You may go. Your eyes are healed because of your faith.” At once the man could see, and he went down the road with Jesus.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Fo̱ a kɔɔre̱ mo̱ gyi‑o si‑o, fo̱ a kpaare̱, amo̱se̱‑ɔ nare̱.” Puri amo̱‑rɔ ne̱ te̱napo̱ amo̱ akatɔ a buŋŋi, ne̱ ɔɔ gya Yeesuu si kabo̱re̱ amo̱ se̱.

ref: After Jesus had gone to Jerusalem, he went into the temple and looked around at everything. But since it was already late in the day, he went back to Bethany with the twelve disciples. Jesus Puts a Curse on a Fig Tree (Matthew
text: Yeesuu a kye̱na mò̱ kuruma‑o si ya ye̱re̱ kpo̱ro̱we̱ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ oo lwee Wuribware̱ suŋkpa mfe̱ŋ de̱e̱re̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa waa kamo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ. Amaa kyɔwe̱ a kyɔ lo̱we̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱ kudu anyɔ‑ɔ a kiŋŋi yɔ Bɛtaniya. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a daa figi kiyii ko̱ yii‑o (Matiyo

ref: That evening, Jesus and the disciples went outside the city. A Lesson from the Fig Tree (Matthew
text: Bo̱ fo̱ ke̱de̱e̱pwɛta‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a le̱e̱ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ. Figi kiyii‑o kuwu ase̱ŋ (Matiyo

ref: They asked, “What right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
text: Ne̱ baa bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nsɛ ya sa fo̱ kpa fe̱yɛ fo̱ gya ase̱sɛ le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa a kabuno‑o mfe̱e̱? Nsɛ ya sa fo̱ kpa fe̱yɛ fo̱ kaapo̱ atɔ mfe̱e̱?”

ref: If anyone asks why you are doing this, say, ‘The Lord needs it and will soon bring it back.’ ”
text: Ne̱ ɔko̱ ya bise mo̱ne̱ ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ saŋŋe̱ kuruma‑o, mo̱nꞌ tɔwe̱ gywii se̱sɛ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ kpa. Amaa ke̱tɔ maa kyee na ɔ be̱e̱ kiŋŋaa mò̱ baa.’ ”

ref: The disciples left and found the donkey tied near a door that faced the street. While they were untying it,
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa yɔ mfe̱ŋ ya ŋu fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ a ŋure mò̱ kuruma kibunogyi ase̱ kabo̱re̱ se̱. Ne̱ baa me̱raa se̱ ba saŋŋe̱.

ref: They told them what Jesus had said, and the people let them take it.
text: Ne̱ baa be̱ŋŋaa ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ bamo̱ ya bise a bo̱ be̱ŋŋaa‑ɔ. Ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ ye̱re̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ a yɔwe̱ bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ nare̱.

ref: The disciples led the donkey to Jesus. They put some of their clothes on its back, and Jesus got on.
text: Ne̱ baa taa kuruma‑o baa Yeesuu ase̱. Ne̱ baa buŋŋi bamo̱ awaagya bo̱ buŋ kuruma‑o si, ne̱ Yeesuu a de̱e̱ kye̱na se̱.

ref: The chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses heard what Jesus said, and they started looking for a way to kill him. They were afraid of him, because the crowds were completely amazed at his teaching.
text: Amɔ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ na Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ a nu ke̱tɔ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa le̱e̱ ba buwi ba kpa e̱kpa ne̱ ba ye̱re̱ se̱ a bo̱ bo̱ mɔɔ mò̱‑ɔ. Amaa kufu de bamo̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ ke̱kaapo̱‑ɔ a kpe̱ŋ lamaŋ ne̱ baa gyaŋŋe̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ e̱ye̱e̱.

ref: So Jesus said to the tree, “Never again will anyone eat fruit from this tree!” The disciples heard him say this. Jesus in the Temple (Matthew
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii kiyii‑o fe̱yɛ, “Ɔko̱ ma lɛɛ gyi fo̱ agyi ŋke ŋko̱ se̱ daa.” Ne̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱‑ɔ. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ Yeesuu a waa na ɔ bo̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: Then he taught the people and said, “The Scriptures say, ‘My house should be called a place of worship for all nations.’ But you have made it a place where robbers hide!”
text: Ne̱ ŋkee ɔɔ kaapo̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ suŋkpa a kabuno‑o mfe̱ŋ. Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Ne̱e̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ yɛ, ‘Mo̱ lɔŋ‑ɔ i kii ke̱kyaŋ ne̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ e̱ ba a bo̱ bo̱ nya ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱‑ɔ.’ Amaa mo̱ne̱ a taa mfe̱ŋ bo̱ kii ‘ayu lɔŋ ne̱e̱,’ bɛɛɛ?”

ref: Jesus told his disciples: Have faith in God!
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ gyi.

ref: Jesus would not let anyone carry things through the temple.
text: Ne̱ ŋkee ɔ yɛ ɔke̱maa ma lɛɛ so̱rɔ tɔso̱rɔ bo̱ kyo̱ŋ kabuno‑o mfe̱ŋ.

ref: Whenever you stand up to pray, you must forgive what others have done to you. Then your Father in heaven will forgive your sins. A Question about Jesus' Authority (Matthew
text: Amaa fo̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ taa fo̱ bɛɛko̱ e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ ke mò̱, Wuribware̱ gbaa maa taa fo̱ lee a ɔ bo̱ ke fo̱.]” Yudaa awuye agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ a se̱ŋbise ko̱ ne̱ Yeesuu a kine kilee kanɔ‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: Jesus and his disciples returned to Jerusalem. And as he was walking through the temple, the chief priests, the nation's leaders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses came over to him.
text: Ne̱ baa be̱e̱ kiŋŋi ya lwee Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ. Yeesuu a yɔ ɔ naa Wuribware̱ suŋkpa a kabuno‑o, ne̱ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ na Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ na maŋ‑ɔ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ a yɔ mò̱ ase̱.

ref: Jesus answered, “I have just one question to ask you. If you answer it, I will tell you where I got the right to do these things.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “To, mo̱ i bise mo̱ne̱ ase̱ŋ ko̱ŋko̱ kpe̱ŋ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ ya taare̱ be̱ŋŋaa mo̱ nɛɛnɛɛ fe̱raa, mo̱‑ɔ mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ kpa ne̱ n de fe̱yɛ m bo̱ waa ane̱ŋ a atɔ‑ɔ.

ref: Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?”
text: Mo̱nꞌ kaapo̱ mo̱ mò̱ ne̱ ɔɔ sa Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱‑ɔ kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ bɔ asuu‑o. Wuribware̱ ya sa mò̱ bɛɛɛ dimaadi?”

ref: They thought it over and said to each other, “We can't say that God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ bamo̱ wo̱re̱ wo̱re̱ a le̱e̱ ba gyiiri ikii fe̱yɛ, “E̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ ane̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱? Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ ya tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ ya sa Yohanee kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ bɔ asuu, o bise ane̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱e̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ Yohanee gyi?’

ref: On the other hand, these people think that John was a prophet. So we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize.” They were afraid of the crowd
text: Ne̱ ane̱ mɔ ane̱ ya tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ dimaadi ya sa mò̱ kpa, e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ ase̱sɛ mɔ e̱ waa ane̱?” Bo̱ se̱re̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ɔke̱maa a kɔɔre̱ gyi fe̱yɛ Yohanee gye̱ Wuribware̱ a akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ ɔko̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: It is also true that we must love God with all our heart, mind, and strength, and that we must love others as much as we love ourselves. These commandments are more important than all the sacrifices and offerings that we could possibly make.”
text: Na ane̱ lee ane̱ mme‑ro ba ane̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ na ane̱ mfɛɛre̱ na ane̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ pɛɛɛ bo̱ kpa ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱ŋ, na ane̱ be̱e̱ kpa ane̱ abɛɛko̱ ase̱ŋ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ kpa ane̱ e̱ye̱e̱‑ɔ. A tiri fe̱yɛ ane̱ bu mbraa ŋnyɔ mɔ kyo̱ŋ ane̱ baa alɔŋŋɔtɔ bɛɛɛ ane̱ mɔɔ mbo̱ bo̱ kywɛɛ bo̱ lɔŋŋɔ Wuribware̱.”

ref: Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.

text: ‘Na akye̱e̱‑e̱‑kpa‑agyi kyuwi le̱e̱ daye̱ bwee‑ro pupuri, na ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ke̱ bo̱ awo̱re̱‑rɔ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ ma lɛɛ waa kyɛɛkyɛɛ.’

ref: Learn a lesson from a fig tree. When its branches sprout and start putting out leaves, you know summer is near.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya se̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ sa a figi kiyii kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ kanyiase̱ŋ. Mo̱ne̱ nyi fe̱yɛ ke̱mo̱ ya ba ke̱ e̱ kpa a ke̱ pɔwe̱, amɔ ke̱kyaare̱ e̱ kpa a ke̱ fo̱.

ref: Then the Son of Man will be seen coming in the clouds with great power and glory.
text: Ŋkee na mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, le̱e̱ so̱so̱ n te awo̱re̱‑rɔ mo̱ e̱ ba ke̱yaale̱ŋ dabe̱‑rɔ na ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ‑rɔ.

ref: He will send his angels to gather his chosen ones from all over the earth. A Lesson from a Fig Tree (Matthew
text: Na n suŋ mo̱ mbɔɔ le̱e̱ so̱so̱ ba swe̱e̱re̱ mɔ se̱ mfe̱e̱, na bo̱ yɔ ke̱bare̱ ke̱maa ya kpɔwe̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a lee sa mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ le̱e̱ swe̱e̱re̱ ke̱maa se̱ bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱ to̱ŋ ko̱ŋko̱.”

ref: No one knows the day or the time. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ bo̱ gye̱ kɛɛ fe̱yɛ, “Amaa ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ nyi kake nsi ne̱ mo̱ e̱ ba a m bo̱ kiŋŋi ba‑ɔ. Wuribware̱ mbɔɔ‑ɔ, bɛɛɛ mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, gbaa, mo̱ŋ nyi kake suyo‑o. Amɔ ane̱ se̱ Wuribware̱ wo̱re̱ kpeŋ i nyi.

ref: So be alert! You don't know when the master of the house will come back. It could be in the evening or at midnight or before dawn or in the morning.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ sa se̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ nyi saŋ ne̱ lɔŋ wuye‑o de ɔ ba‑ɔ, nsaŋse̱ kade̱e̱pwɛta bɛɛɛ kanye bɛɛɛ kaye̱ nse̱na bɛɛɛ gye̱gyaye̱ kese‑ro ne̱ ɔ ba.

ref: So watch out and be ready! You don't know when the time will come.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ɔke̱maa sa se̱ na ɔ waa siraa, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ nyi saŋ amo̱.

ref: You can be sure that some of the people of this generation will still be alive when all this happens.
text: Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ ndɔɔ a kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ i ŋu saŋ ne̱ atɔ mɔ e̱ ba‑ɔ.

ref: But if he comes suddenly, don't let him find you asleep.
text: Mò̱ ya fu‑ro ba, mo̱ne̱ ma sa a ɔ bo̱ to̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ a di idi.

ref: It is like what happens when a man goes away for a while and places his servants in charge of everything. He tells each of them what to do, and he orders the guard to keep alert.
text: A du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kawurenyare̱ ko̱ a kpa a o tu kpa, ne̱ oo ke kusuŋ‑no sa mò̱ ayaafɔre̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ waa, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ lɔŋ ɔde̱e̱re̱se̱po̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ de̱e̱re̱ mò̱ lɔŋ se̱ bo̱ fo̱ saŋ ne̱ ɔ ba‑ɔ.

ref: Corinthians
text: Korintoo awuye

ref: Jesus was led off to the high priest. Then the chief priests, the nation's leaders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses all met together.
text: To, ne̱ baa taa Yeesuu yaa Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ lɔŋ‑nɔ, mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ na maŋ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ na Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ e̱ gyaŋŋe̱‑ɔ.

ref: She has done all she could by pouring perfume on my body to prepare it for burial.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔ taare̱‑ɔ ne̱ ɔɔ waa. Mò̱ a twiiri laŋpe̱ŋtaa‑ɔ we̱e̱ mo̱ se̱‑ɔ, a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ lɔŋŋɔ mo̱ kayo̱wɔre̱ o gywii saŋ ne̱ mo̱ i wu na bo̱ pure mo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: This made some of the guests angry, and they complained, “Why such a waste?
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ iduŋ a fwii, ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ e̱ gye̱ laŋpe̱ŋtaa amo̱ ke̱nye̱ra ane̱ŋ?

ref: You may be sure that wherever the good news is told all over the world, people will remember what she has done. And they will tell others. Judas and the Chief Priests (Matthew
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ kaye̱ mɔ pɛɛɛ‑rɔ to̱ŋ ke̱maa ne̱ ba tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ, bo̱ le̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱ mɔ a waa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, mò̱ ke̱nyare̱ maa muri amo̱‑rɔ daa.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yudas a sure si fe̱yɛ o gyi Yeesuu kidiburo‑o (Matiyo

ref: The two disciples went into the city and found everything just as Jesus had told them. So they prepared the Passover meal.
text: Ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱ anyɔ amo̱ a me̱raa se̱ yɔ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ. Bamo̱ a yɔ‑ɔ, ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱‑ɔ dɛɛ ne̱ baa ŋu. Ne̱ baa waa Wuribware̱-a‑kya-ane̱-yɔwe̱ a ateese‑o siraa mfe̱ŋ. Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ a ateese‑o (Matiyo

ref: Judas Iscariot was one of the twelve disciples. He went to the chief priests and offered to help them arrest Jesus.
text: Ne̱ Yudas Kariyotinyi ne̱ ɔ gye̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱ kudu anyɔ‑ɔ ɔko̱‑ɔ a yɔ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ ase̱ na ɔ ya gyi Yeesuu kidiburo gywii bamo̱.

ref: The Son of Man will die, just as the Scriptures say. But it is going to be terrible for the one who betrays me. That man would be better off if he had never been born.” The Lord's Supper (Matthew
text: Mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, i wu fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ mo̱ ase̱ŋ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ. Amaa ɔko̱ ne̱ o gyi mo̱ kidiburo‑o i ŋu ase̱ŋ ne̱e̱ fɛɛ. Bo̱ dɛɛ bo̱ mo̱ŋ ko̱we̱ ane̱ŋ a se̱sɛ‑ɔ ne̱e̱, weetee a bɔ.”

ref: Jesus picked up a cup of wine and gave thanks to God. He gave it to his disciples, and they all drank some.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ taa nta ko̱to̱re̱nkyu bo̱rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ sa Wuribware̱ aŋsɛ we̱e̱ se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ taa sa bamo̱, ne̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ a nuu ke̱mo̱‑rɔ.

ref: Jesus replied, “This very night before a rooster crows twice, you will say three times that you don't know me.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa Peetroo fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii fo̱ fe̱yɛ ndɔɔ a kanye mɔ fo̱ e̱ swe̱e̱ iluwi e̱sa fe̱yɛ fo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi mo̱ pwɛɛ na kyaŋare̱ dɛɛ fo̱re̱ iluwi e̱nyɔ.”

ref: He answered, “It is one of you twelve men who is eating from this dish with me.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ kudu anyɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ ɔko̱ ne̱e̱, ɔko̱ ne̱ mo̱ aa mò̱ ke̱sare̱e̱ di gyare̱ ko̱ŋko̱‑rɔ ane̱ i gyi‑o.

ref: But Peter was so sure of himself that he said, “Even if I have to die with you, I will never say I don't know you!” All the others said the same thing. Jesus Prays (Matthew
text: Ne̱ Peetroo a be̱e̱ be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ, “Hare̱e̱ amo̱ e̱ gye̱ mo̱ aa fo̱ kuwu gbaa, ma tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ŋ nyi fo̱.” Ne̱ agyase̱po̱ ne̱ baa saŋ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ mɔ a tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ dɛɛ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a ya ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ Gɛte̱sɛmane̱e̱‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: told them, “I am so sad that I feel as if I am dying. Stay here and keep awake with me.”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ bo̱re̱‑rɔ i duŋwi mo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ, hare̱e̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa a ŋ wu. To, mo̱nꞌ kyiŋŋi kye̱na mfe̱e̱.”

ref: Jesus walked on a little way. Then he knelt down on the ground and prayed, “Father, if it is possible, don't let this happen to me! Father, you can do anything. Don't make me suffer by drinking from this cup. But do what you want, and not what I want.”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ kya se̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ fe̱yɛ, “N se̱, n se̱, ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ fo̱ e̱ taare̱ a fo̱ waa ke̱tɔ ke̱maa. Mbe̱yɔmɔ mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ fo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ sa a mo̱ awo̱re̱fɔɔ ne̱ mo̱ i gyi mɔ‑ɔ gye̱ kɛɛ na m ma wu. Amaa kanɔ ŋke̱maa ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ bware sa mo̱.”

ref: But after I am raised to life, I will go ahead of you to Galilee.”
text: Amaa mo̱ ya bo̱ kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro‑o, mo̱ e̱ gye̱ mo̱ne̱ ŋkpɛɛ a ŋ yɔ Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱.”

ref: Peter spoke up, “Even if all the others reject you, I never will!”
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Peetroo a be̱ŋŋaa Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Hare̱e̱ mo̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana ya se̱re̱, m fe̱raa ma se̱re̱ a ŋ yɔwe̱ fo̱.”

ref: and the men grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
text: Ne̱ baa kra Yeesuu, ne̱ baa kra mò̱‑rɔ keŋkeŋ.

ref: Jesus went back and prayed the same prayer.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ kiŋŋi ya ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ ko̱re̱ ke̱gye̱ŋkpɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane, and he told them, “Sit here while I pray.”
text: Amo̱‑ɔ kamɛɛ‑rɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a ba bane̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Gɛte̱sɛmane̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ kye̱na mfe̱e̱ na ŋ ya ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱.”

ref: When Jesus came back and found the disciples sleeping, he said to Simon Peter, “Are you asleep? Can't you stay awake for just one hour?
text: Ne̱ oo kiŋŋi ya to̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ agyase̱po̱ asa‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a di idi. Ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ Peetroo ɛ, “Simɔŋ, fo̱ a di idi ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱? Amɔ fo̱ mo̱ŋ taare̱ kye̱na dɔŋhwe̱re̱e̱ ko̱ŋko̱ na fo̱ mo̱ŋ di idi aaa?”

ref: But when he returned to the disciples, he found them sleeping again. They simply could not keep their eyes open, and they did not know what to say.
text: Mò̱ a ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ kiŋŋi bo̱ to̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a be̱e̱ di idi. Bo̱ mo̱ŋ taare̱ kra idi. Ŋkee bo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi ane̱ŋ ne̱ ba tɔwe̱ a bo̱ gywii Yeesuu‑o.

ref: When Jesus returned to the disciples the third time, he said, “Are you still sleeping and resting? Enough of this! The time has come for the Son of Man to be handed over to sinners.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ ya ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ sase̱po̱. Saŋ ne̱ oo kiŋŋi ba‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ saŋ mo̱ne̱ i di idi mo̱ne̱ i kyure ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱? To, aa baare ane̱ŋ. Saŋ a fo̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ. Mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱re̱, ba lee mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, ba sa e̱bɔye̱ awaapo̱ na bo̱ nya mo̱ se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ.

ref: Get up! Let's go. The one who will betray me is already here.” Jesus Is Arrested (Matthew
text: Mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱, ɔko̱ ne̱ o gyi mo̱ kidiburo‑o e̱ ba‑ɔ. To, mo̱nꞌ ko̱so̱ na ane̱ yɔ ya gyaŋŋaa mò̱.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kra Yeesuu‑o (Matiyo

ref: Someone standing there pulled out a sword. He struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his ear.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ ɔko̱ a gyiiri ke̱paŋ lee, ne̱ oo ŋe Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ a anya‑ɔ ɔko̱ ke̱se̱bɔ te̱ŋ.

ref: Jesus was still speaking, when Judas the betrayer came up. He was one of the twelve disciples, and a mob of men armed with swords and clubs were with him. They had been sent by the chief priests, the nation's leaders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: Yeesuu saŋ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa, ne̱ Yudas ne̱ ɔ gye̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱ kudu anyɔ‑ɔ ɔko̱‑ɔ a fo̱. Mò̱ aa ase̱sɛ kukwii ko̱ na bamo̱ apaŋ na akyokuŋ ya ba. Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ na Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ na maŋ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ ya suŋ bamo̱.

ref: Judas had told them ahead of time, “Arrest the man I greet with a kiss. Tie him up tight and lead him away.”
text: Amɔ odiburopo̱‑ɔ a kyɔ kaapo̱ bamo̱ ne̱ ba gya mò̱ se̱ ba‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔko̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ yɔ a ŋ ya ka mò̱ kanɔ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ mò̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ. Na mo̱nꞌ kra mò̱, na mo̱nꞌ taa mò̱ nare̱!”

ref: Jesus said to the mob, “Why do you come with swords and clubs to arrest me like a criminal?
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a bise ase̱sɛ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Amɔ mo̱ne̱ a ba apaŋ na akyokuŋ a mo̱nꞌ bo̱ kra mo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ oyu‑o ooo?

ref: Day after day I was with you and taught in the temple, and you didn't arrest me. But what the Scriptures say must come true.”
text: Ŋke mfe̱ne̱ mɔ‑rɔ mo̱ aa mo̱ne̱ ne̱e̱ na mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa kabuno, na mo̱ne̱ mɔ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ kra mo̱? Amaa amo̱‑ɔ a ba ane̱ŋ ne̱e̱ na mo̱ awo̱re̱fɔɔ na lowi ne̱ mo̱ i wu‑o, ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ amo̱ ase̱ŋ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, nya waa kase̱ŋtiŋ.”

ref: All of Jesus' disciples ran off and left him.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a agyase̱po̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a se̱re̱ yɔwe̱ mò̱.

ref: One of them was a young man who was wearing only a linen cloth. And when the men grabbed him,
text: Amaa ke̱yaafɔre̱ ko̱ mɔ a kpuriti waagya laare̱‑rɔ ɔ gya bamo̱ se̱. Ne̱ ba kpa a bo̱ kra mò̱ gbaa mò̱.

ref: he left the cloth behind and ran away naked. Jesus Is Questioned by the Council (Matthew
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ ŋwɛɛnaŋ, ne̱ mò̱ waagya‑ɔ a saŋŋe̱ le̱e̱ da, ne̱ ɔɔ se̱re̱ ke̱te̱bɔŋburoŋ yɔwe̱ mò̱ waagya‑ɔ bo̱ sii. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa yaa Yeesuu Yudaa awuye a iwurekpa‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: Finally, some men stood up and lied about him. They said,
text: Ne̱ ŋkee bo̱ko̱ a ko̱so̱ ba aye̱ba bo̱ po̱rɔ Yeesuu fe̱yɛ,

ref: Peter replied, “That isn't true! I don't know what you're talking about. I don't have any idea what you mean.” He went out to the gate, and a rooster crowed.
text: Ne̱ Peetroo a swe̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ŋ nyi ase̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ kaase̱.” Ne̱ ɔɔ nare̱ ya ye̱re̱ lɔŋ‑ɔ kibunogyi‑ro. Puri amo̱‑rɔ ne̱ kyaŋare̱ a fo̱re̱.

ref: Peter had followed at a distance, and when he reached the courtyard of the high priest's house, he sat down with the guards to warm himself beside a fire.
text: Ne̱ Peetroo a sii kamɛɛ ɔ gya bamo̱ se̱. Ɔɔ gya bamo̱ se̱ ane̱ŋ‑aaa ya bo̱ fo̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ a lɔŋ amo̱‑rɔ. Ne̱ mò̱ aa ade̱e̱re̱se̱po̱ ko̱ te lɔŋ‑ɔ kabuno ase̱ ba wo̱re̱ de̱e̱kpa.

ref: At once the high priest ripped his robe apart and shouted, “Why do we need more witnesses?
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa ne̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ‑ɔ a ba kaduŋfwii bo̱ kyaŋ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ waagya‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Ane̱ maa lɛɛ kpa a ɔko̱ tɔwe̱ ko̱tɔko̱ pwɛɛ na ane̱ dɛɛ ŋu fe̱yɛ oo gyi ke̱pɔ.

ref: “We heard him say he would tear down this temple that we built. He also claimed that in three days he would build another one without any help.”
text: “Ane̱ a nu fe̱yɛ mò̱ a tɔwe̱ ɛ, ‘Mo̱ i bwee Wuribware̱ suŋkpa ne̱ ase̱sɛ a ba asare̱e̱ bo̱ pwɛɛ‑ɔ, na ŋke nsa kamɛɛ‑rɔ na m pwɛɛ ke̱mo̱ ne̱ se̱sɛ mo̱ŋ ba asare̱e̱ bo̱ pwɛɛ‑ɔ.’ ”

ref: But even then they did not agree on what they said.
text: Amaa bamo̱ gbaa bamo̱ aye̱ba‑ɔ mo̱ŋ taare̱ ye̱re̱ abɛɛ se̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu i gyi ke̱pɔ‑ɔ.

ref: The high priest stood up in the council and asked Jesus, “Why don't you say something in your own defense? Don't you hear the charges they are making against you?”
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ‑ɔ a ko̱so̱ ye̱re̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ akatɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ oo bise Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Amɔ fo̱ mo̱ŋ de ase̱ŋ mɔ pɛɛɛ ne̱ ba tɔwe̱ ba kye fo̱‑ɔ kanɔlee aaa?”

ref: But Jesus kept quiet and did not say a word. The high priest asked him another question, “Are you the Messiah, the Son of the glorious God?”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a laatɔ, kanɔ ɔ mo̱ŋ buŋŋi. Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ e̱ gye̱ mò̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ mò̱ gyi Kristoo ne̱ oo suŋ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱‑ɔ, bɛɛɛ fo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱?”

ref: You heard him claim to be God! What is your decision?” They all agreed he should be put to death.
text: Mo̱ne̱ a nu ase̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ bo̱ kye Wuribware̱‑ɔ, ɔ yɛ mò̱ aa Wuribware̱ ta. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ fa fe̱yɛ ane̱ waa mò̱?” Ne̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ a sure si fe̱yɛ oo gyi ke̱pɔ, amo̱se̱‑ɔ bo̱ mɔɔ mò̱.

ref: Some of the people started spitting on Jesus. They blindfolded him, hit him with their fists, and said, “Tell us who hit you!” Then the guards took charge of Jesus and beat him. Peter Says He Doesn't Know Jesus (Matthew
text: Mfe̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ bo̱ko̱ a le̱e̱ ba twe̱e̱ akyɔne̱ ba we̱e̱ Yeesuu si. Ne̱ baa ba kaprakɛɛ bo̱ buŋ mò̱ akatɔ se̱, na ba da mò̱ akuŋtiŋ. ref: While Peter was still in the courtyard, a servant girl of the high priest came up
text: Bamo̱ a gyi Yeesuu ase̱ŋ ke̱kyaŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, na Peetroo saŋ o te kabuno mfe̱ŋ. Ne̱ Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ‑ɔ mò̱ kabre ko̱ a ba ɔ kyo̱ŋ.

ref: and saw Peter warming himself by the fire. She stared at him and said, “You were with Jesus from Nazareth!”
text: Saŋ ne̱ oo ŋu Peetroo na ɔ wo̱re̱ de̱e̱kpa‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ de̱e̱re̱ Peetroo akatɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Fo̱ gbaa fo̱ aa Yeesuu Nasarɛte̱nyi mɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ naa.”

ref: “No, I'm not!” Peter replied. A little while later some of the people said to Peter, “You certainly are one of them. You're a Galilean!”
text: Amaa Peetroo a be̱e̱ swe̱e̱. Ke̱tɔ a be̱e̱ waa kafwe̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ ye̱re̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ a be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ gywii Peetroo fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ maa taare̱ a fo̱ gyiiri ikii fe̱yɛ fo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yeesuu a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ ɔko̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ fo̱ gbaa fo̱ gye̱ Galile̱yanyi ne̱e̱.”

ref: At once the rooster crowed a second time. Then Peter remembered that Jesus had told him, “Before a rooster crows twice, you will say three times that you don't know me.” So Peter started crying.
text: Mò̱ ke̱de̱ŋ tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ kyaŋare̱ a fo̱re̱ nyɔse̱po̱. Ne̱ oo nyiŋŋi ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱‑ɔ se̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ e̱ swe̱e̱ iluwi e̱sa fe̱yɛ fo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi mo̱ pwɛɛ na kyaŋare̱ dɛɛ fo̱re̱ iluwi e̱nyɔ.” Ne̱ ɔɔ ya su bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: Early the next morning the chief priests, the nation's leaders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses met together with the whole Jewish council. They tied up Jesus and led him off to Pilate.
text: Kaye̱ ŋke gye̱gyaye̱ tututu‑o, ne̱ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ na maŋ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ na Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a gyaŋŋe̱ kra ane̱ŋ ne̱ ba waa Yeesuu‑o. text: Amaa kanɔ Yeesuu be̱e̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ buŋŋi. Ne̱ gominaa‑o e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ mò̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Romanyi gominaa‑o a sure si fe̱yɛ bo̱ mɔɔ Yeesuu‑o (Matiyo

text: Gominaa‑o a kpa a ɔ waa bamo̱ ke̱pre̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ oo lee Bar‑Abas bo̱ sa bamo̱. text: Saŋ ne̱ asoogyaa awuye‑o de Yeesuu ba yɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa gyaŋŋaa ɔnyare̱ ko̱ na ɔ kyo̱ŋ o lwee maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ. Ɔɔ le̱e̱ Kireenee maŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Simɔŋ, ne̱ ɔ gye̱ Alɛsanda na Rufus bamo̱ se̱‑ɔ. Simɔŋ mɔ ne̱ asoogyaa awuye‑o a ye̱re̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ so̱rɔ Yeesuu a kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ.

text: Mo̱ e̱ sa ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ kɔɔre̱ ase̱ŋ timaa‑o a o gyi‑o e̱le̱ŋ na ɔ ba mo̱ ke̱nyare̱ bo̱ gya ibrisi bo̱ ko̱so̱ ase̱sɛ se̱, na ɔ sa e̱se̱ŋsa ne̱ ɔko̱ maa nu e̱mo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: Both of them were good people and pleased the Lord God by obeying all that he had commanded.
text: Abɛɛ anyɔ mɔ kakye̱na‑rɔ a waa Wuribware̱ daŋ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ baa gya mò̱ mbraa‑ɔ pɛɛɛ se̱ kyɛɛkyɛɛ.

ref: But they did not have children. Elizabeth could not have any, and both Zechariah and Elizabeth were already old.
text: Amaa bo̱ mo̱ŋ de gyi, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Ilisabɛt maa ko̱we̱. Amɔ bamo̱ anyɔ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a kyɔ be̱re̱ lo̱we̱.

ref: According to the custom of the priests, he had been chosen to go into the Lord's temple that day and to burn incense,
text: Saŋ a fo̱ fe̱yɛ Abiya kabuno‑o suŋ Wuribware̱ mò̱ suŋkpa. Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ bamo̱ ade̱daase̱ŋ bo̱ le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ se̱ e̱ kaapo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa twe̱e̱ abu bo̱ lee Sakariya fe̱yɛ mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ o lwee Wuribware̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ ya kywɛɛ nyoŋku sa Wuribware̱.

ref: All at once an angel from the Lord appeared to Zechariah at the right side of the altar.
text: Nyoŋku mɔ bo̱ kakyaŋse̱gyii ne̱ ka ye̱re̱ lɔŋŋɔkpa se̱‑ɔ‑rɔ ɔ kywɛɛ. Ne̱ Sakariya ye̱re̱ lɔŋŋɔkpa‑ɔ akatɔ‑rɔ. Puri amo̱‑rɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ a le̱e̱ mò̱ se̱, na ɔ ye̱re̱ mò̱ kigyisesare̱e̱ se̱.

ref: Zechariah was confused and afraid when he saw the angel.
text: Amɔ Sakariya a ŋu Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ amo̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱ duŋ a te̱ŋ mò̱, ne̱ kufu a nya mò̱.

ref: But the angel told him: Don't be afraid, Zechariah! God has heard your prayers. Your wife Elizabeth will have a son, and you must name him John.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ‑ɔ yɛ, “Sakariya, fo̱ ma sa a kufu nya fo̱. Wuribware̱ a nu fo̱ a ko̱re̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ sa fo̱ kayaagyi. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ fo̱ aa fo̱ ka Ilisabɛt e̱ ko̱we̱ ɔnyare̱. Na fo̱ sa mò̱ ke̱nyare̱ fe̱yɛ Yohanee.

ref: His birth will make you very happy, and many people will be glad.
text: Mò̱ ya ko̱we̱ mò̱, a sa a fo̱ akatɔ gyi bwe̱e̱tɔ na a sa a ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ gbaa akatɔ gyi,

ref: Your son will be a great servant of the Lord. He must never drink wine or beer, and the power of the Holy Spirit will be with him from the time he is born.
text: a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ o kii se̱sɛ dabe̱ a ɔ bo̱ sa Wuribware̱. O kisi nta ke̱maa. Na Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o sa mò̱ mò̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ pwɛɛ gbaa na mo̱ne̱ dɛɛ ko̱we̱ mò̱.

ref: John will lead many people in Israel to turn back to the Lord their God.
text: Na ɔ kpɔwe̱ ane̱ Isireelii awuye ne̱ baa te̱ŋŋe̱ bamo̱ aa ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Wuribware̱ mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ baa Wuribware̱ ase̱.

ref: He will go ahead of the Lord with the same power and spirit that Elijah had. And because of John, parents will be more thoughtful of their children. And people who now disobey God will begin to think as they ought to. This is how John will get people ready for the Lord.
text: Na ɔ nya ke̱yaale̱ŋ le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o ase̱, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ Iliya a nya‑ɔ dɛɛ. Na ɔ gye̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ ŋkpɛɛ, na ɔ nya waa Wuribware̱ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ siraa gywii mò̱. Na ɔ sa a ŋyaagyi na bamo̱ ase̱ a be̱e̱ kpa abɛɛ, na bo̱ waa kanɔ ko̱ŋko̱. Na ɔ sa a katɔmaabu awuye kyurowi bamo̱ mfɛɛre̱ bo̱ gya ase̱sɛ timaa e̱kpa se̱.”

ref: Zechariah said to the angel, “How will I know this is going to happen? My wife and I are both very old.”
text: Ne̱ Sakariya a bise Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ aa mo̱ ka pɛɛɛ mɔ a be̱re̱. Ne̱ e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa a m bo̱ ŋu fe̱yɛ amo̱‑ɔ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱e̱?”

ref: The angel answered, “I am Gabriel, God's servant, and I was sent to tell you this good news.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ gye̱ Gabriyɛl ne̱ ŋ ye̱re̱ Wuribware̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ase̱‑ɔ, ne̱ oo suŋ mo̱ fe̱yɛ m bo̱ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ kɔne̱kɔne̱sɛ mɔ gywii fo̱.

ref: You have not believed what I have said. So you will not be able to say a thing until all this happens. But everything will take place when it is supposed to.”
text: Amaa fo̱ a mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ ase̱ŋ mɔ ne̱ amo̱ ke̱be̱e̱ maa paŋ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, fo̱ i kii kpawu na kamu ya bo̱ fo̱ ŋke nsi ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ ko̱we̱ kayaagyi‑o, ne̱ fo̱ i ŋu fe̱yɛ ase̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii fo̱‑ɔ e̱ ba kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o.”

ref: The crowd was waiting for Zechariah and kept wondering why he was staying in the temple so long.
text: Saŋ mɔ pɛɛɛ na ase̱sɛ‑ɔ ye̱re̱ bo̱ gywii Sakariya, na ba fa ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ ɔɔ ko̱ŋ kyee Wuribware̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ.

ref: When he did come out, he could not speak, and they knew he had seen a vision. He motioned to them with his hands, but did not say a thing.
text: Saŋ ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ le̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ ye̱re̱ ba asare̱e̱ bo̱ sa se̱ŋsa. Ne̱ baa pini fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a lee mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ mò̱ mò̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ mfe̱ŋ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ taare̱ yaye̱ kanɔ sa se̱ŋsa gywii bamo̱.

ref: When Zechariah's time of service in the temple was over, he went home.
text: Ŋke ne̱ Sakariya e̱ waa mò̱ kusuŋ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ a kyo̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ ŋkee ɔɔ yɔ mò̱ aye̱.

ref: Soon after this, his wife was expecting a baby, and for five months she did not leave the house. She said to herself,
text: Bo̱ fo̱ saŋ ko̱‑ɔ ne̱ mò̱ ka a nya kame, ne̱ ŋkee ɔɔ kwe̱e̱rɔ ke̱kyaŋ‑nɔ aferi anuu, na ɔ maa le̱e̱ kawu.

ref: “What the Lord has done for me will keep people from looking down on me.” An Angel Tells about the Birth of Jesus
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ŋkee fe̱raa Wuribware̱ a kya mo̱‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ lee mo̱ le̱e̱ ipeere‑ro ase̱sɛ ase̱!” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a suŋ tɔwe̱ Yeesuu ko̱ko̱we̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ

ref: One month later God sent the angel Gabriel to the town of Nazareth in Galilee
text: Amɔ Ilisabɛt a kame‑o aferi asiye‑o‑ro‑o, ne̱ Wuribware̱ a be̱e̱ suŋ mò̱ kabɔɔ Gabriyɛl fe̱yɛ ɔ yɔ Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ a maŋ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Nasarɛt‑ɔ‑rɔ,

ref: with a message for a virgin named Mary. She was engaged to Joseph from the family of King David.
text: na ɔ yɔ kabregyii ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Mariya‑o ase̱. Kabregyii mɔ ne̱ baa ke̱re̱ mò̱ sa ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Yo̱sɛf ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ Yudaa awuye ɔde̱daapo̱ Deefid a kabuno‑o.

ref: The angel greeted Mary and said, “You are truly blessed! The Lord is with you.”
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa kabɔɔ‑ɔ a yɔ kabregyii amo̱ ase̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ bo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ ka mò̱ kanɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Wuribware̱ a gya fo̱ kamɛɛ, ne̱ oo yure fo̱.”

ref: Then the angel told Mary, “Don't be afraid! God is pleased with you,
text: Ne̱ kabɔɔ amo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mariya, fo̱ ma sa a kufu nya fo̱. Wuribware̱ a waa fo̱ atɔ.

ref: and you will have a son. His name will be Jesus.
text: Fo̱ e̱ nya kame na fo̱ ko̱we̱ ɔnyare̱, na fo̱ sa mò̱ ke̱nyare̱ fe̱yɛ Yeesuu.

ref: He will be great and will be called the Son of God Most High. The Lord God will make him king, as his ancestor David was.
text: Na o kii se̱sɛ dabe̱, na bo̱ te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Nyiŋkpe̱ŋ-kyo̱ŋ-atɔ‑pɛɛɛ-Bware̱ mò̱ gyi‑o. Na Nyaŋpe̱ Wuribware̱ sa a ɔ kye̱na mò̱ nana Deefid a kuwure‑o si.

ref: He will rule the people of Israel forever, and his kingdom will never end.”
text: Na o kii mo̱ne̱ ɔde̱daapo̱ Yakubu a ananagyi‑o owure nsu pɛɛɛ. Mò̱ kuwure‑o maa gye̱ kɛɛ daa!”

ref: Mary asked the angel, “How can this happen? I am not even married!”
text: Ne̱ Mariya yɛ, “Mo̱ŋ nyi anyare̱, ne̱ e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ a waa na a ba ane̱ŋ?”

ref: The angel answered, “The Holy Spirit will come down to you, and God's power will come over you. So your child will be called the holy Son of God.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ‑ɔ mɔ yɛ, “Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o e̱ ywe̱e̱ fo̱ se̱, na Nyiŋkpe̱ŋ-kyo̱ŋ-atɔ‑pɛɛɛ-Bware̱ a ke̱yaale̱ŋ‑ɔ sii fo̱ se̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ fo̱ kayaagyi‑o, ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ ke̱yaafɔre̱ timaa, Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑o.

ref: Your relative Elizabeth is also going to have a son, even though she is old. No one thought she could ever have a baby, but in three months she will have a son.
text: Nu, fo̱ ko̱we̱bɛɛ Ilisabɛt ne̱ ɔɔ be̱re̱, ne̱ bo̱ yɛ ɔ maa ko̱we̱‑ɔ, de ɔnyare̱ kame. Kamo̱ aferi asiye ne̱e̱.

ref: Mary said, “I am the Lord's servant! Let it happen as you have said.” And the angel left her. Mary Visits Elizabeth
text: Ne̱ Mariya yɛ, “Wuribware̱ kabre e̱ gye̱ mo̱, amo̱se̱‑ɔ a waa sa mo̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ mɔ dɛɛ.” Ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ amo̱ a le̱e̱ mò̱ ase̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Mariya a ya ka Ilisabɛt kanɔ‑ɔ

ref: A short time later Mary hurried to a town in the hill country of Judea.
text: Ke̱tɔ mo̱ŋ kyee ne̱ Mariya a waa siraa, ne̱ ɔɔ da e̱ye̱e̱ se̱ yɔ Yude̱ya maŋ ko̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ abe̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Sakariya na mò̱ ka Ilisabɛt te‑o.

ref: She went into Zechariah's home, where she greeted Elizabeth.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ Sakariya lɔŋ‑nɔ ya ka Ilisabɛt kanɔ.

ref: Then in a loud voice she said to Mary: God has blessed you more than any other woman! He has also blessed the child you will have.
text: Ne̱ Ilisabɛt a tɔwe̱ keŋkeŋ gywii Mariya fe̱yɛ, “Akye̱e̱‑rɔ pɛɛɛ fo̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a yure bwe̱e̱tɔ, Wuribware̱ a yure kayaagyi ne̱ fo̱ e̱ ba a fo̱ bo̱ ko̱we̱‑ɔ.

ref: Why should the mother of my Lord come to me?
text: Mo̱ a nya kuŋu timaa fe̱yɛ mo̱ nyaŋpe̱ Kristoo mò̱ nyi a ba na ɔ bo̱ ka mo̱ kanɔ.

ref: As soon as I heard your greeting, my baby became happy and moved within me.
text: Mo̱ a de̱ŋ nu fo̱ kanɔka‑ɔ, ne̱ kayaagyi ne̱ ɔ bo̱ mo̱ kame‑ro‑o a dabo̱rɔ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ aa waa mò̱ kɔne̱.

ref: The Lord has blessed you because you believed that he will keep his promise. Mary's Song of Praise
text: Fo̱ a kɔɔre̱ gyi fe̱yɛ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a suŋ bo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii fo̱‑ɔ e̱ ba‑rɔ kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o si‑o, o yure fo̱.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Mariya a kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱‑ɔ

ref: God All-Powerful has done great things for me, and his name is holy.
text: Bo̱ le̱e̱ atɔ dabe̱ ne̱ Ɔyaale̱ŋpo̱-Bware̱ a waa sa mo̱‑ɔ se̱, mo̱ e̱ kyo̱rɔ mò̱ ke̱nyare̱ kpe̱yaa.

ref: He always shows mercy to everyone who worships him.
text: Le̱e̱ de̱daa‑ɔ bo̱ fo̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ ɔ kpa ke̱nana ke̱maa‑rɔ ase̱sɛ ne̱ ba bu mò̱‑ɔ ase̱ŋ.

ref: The Lord has used his powerful arm to scatter those who are proud.
text: Ne̱ o teyi mò̱ ke̱sare̱e̱ dabe̱‑ɔ bo̱ waa atɔ dabe̱, ne̱ ɔɔ brawe̱ bamo̱ ne̱ ba kaapo̱ bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: God drags strong rulers from their thrones and puts humble people in places of power.
text: Ɔɔ baa awure kaase̱, ne̱ ɔɔ yase̱ ase̱sɛ laŋgyaŋ so̱so̱.

ref: God gives the hungry good things to eat, and sends the rich away with nothing.
text: Ɔɔ sa atiripo̱ atɔ timaa, ne̱ ɔɔ gya atɔ awuye bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ asare̱e̱ kpaŋkpaŋ.

ref: Mary stayed with Elizabeth about three months. Then she went back home. The Birth of John the Baptist
text: Ne̱ Mariya a kye̱na mfe̱ŋ ka Ilisabɛt kanɔ waa aferi asa pwɛɛ ne̱ ɔɔ dɛɛ kiŋŋi yɔ pe̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa ko̱we̱ Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱‑ɔ

ref: When Elizabeth's son was born,
text: Ilisabɛt ko̱ko̱we̱ saŋ a fo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ ko̱we̱ ɔnyare̱.

ref: her neighbors and relatives heard how kind the Lord had been to her, and they too were glad.
text: Mò̱ akye̱nabɛɛpo̱ na mò̱ ako̱we̱bɛɛ a nu fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a waa atɔ timaa sa mò̱‑ɔ, ne̱ aa waa bamo̱ aa mò̱ kakatɔgyi.

ref: Eight days later they did for the child what the Law of Moses commands. They were going to name him Zechariah, after his father.
text: Bamo̱ a ko̱we̱ kayaagyi‑o, ke̱mo̱ kake buruwase̱po̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa te̱ŋ mò̱ ko̱twe̱e̱tu, ne̱ bo̱ yɛ bo̱ ba mò̱ se̱ Sakariya ke̱nyare̱ bo̱ sa mò̱.

ref: But Elizabeth said, “No! His name is John.”
text: Ne̱ mò̱ nyi yɛ, “Daabii. Weetee mò̱ ke̱nyare̱ e̱ gye̱ Yohanee.”

ref: The people argued, “No one in your family has ever been named John.”
text: Ne̱ baa be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ne̱e̱ bo̱ maa te̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ ako̱we̱bɛɛ‑ɔ ɔko̱ mɔ ane̱ŋ ɛ?”

ref: So they motioned to Zechariah to find out what he wanted to name his son.
text: Mò̱ se̱ a mo̱ŋ tɛɛ taare̱ sa se̱ŋsa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa ba asare̱e̱ bo̱ bise mò̱ ke̱nyare̱ ne̱ ɔ kpa fe̱yɛ bo̱ bo̱ sa mò̱ gyi amo̱‑ɔ.

ref: Zechariah asked for a writing tablet. Then he wrote, “His name is John.” Everyone was amazed.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ ba asare̱e̱ bo̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ sa mò̱ kataabo̱o̱ ne̱ ba kyo̱rɛɛ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ kyo̱rɛɛ fe̱yɛ, “Mò̱ ke̱nyare̱ e̱ gye̱ Yohanee.” Ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ.

ref: All the neighbors were frightened because of what had happened, and everywhere in the hill country people kept talking about these things.
text: Ne̱ aa waa mò̱ akye̱nabɛɛpo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ kufu, ne̱ ase̱ŋ amo̱ a lweero fo̱ Yude̱ya abe̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ a e̱maŋ‑ɔ ɔke̱maa se̱.

ref: Everyone who heard about this wondered what this child would grow up to be. They knew the Lord was with him. Zechariah Praises the Lord
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ oo nu ase̱ŋ amo̱‑ɔ a fa amo̱ mfɛɛre̱, ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Ntɔ se̱sɛ ne̱ kayaagyi mɔ e̱ ba a ɔ bo̱ kii‑o ne̱e̱?” A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa a ŋu fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ bo̱ mò̱‑rɔ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Sakariya a kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱‑ɔ

ref: Praise the Lord, the God of Israel! He has come to save his people.
text: “Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ Isireelii awuye kyo̱rɔ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Wuribware̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔɔ bo̱ lee mò̱ ase̱sɛ le̱e̱ ase̱ŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ baa nya bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱.

ref: Our God has given us a mighty Savior from the family of David his servant.
text: Ɔɔ sa ane̱ ɔmo̱rɔwe̱po̱ dabe̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ mò̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱, ane̱ ɔde̱daapo̱ Deefid, a ke̱nana‑ɔ‑rɔ,

ref: You, my son, will be called the prophet of God Most High. You will go ahead of the Lord to get everything ready for him.
text: Ne̱ Sakariya a sa se̱ŋsa gywii mò̱ gyi‑o fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ gyi, fo̱ fe̱raa ba te̱e̱ fo̱ fe̱yɛ Nyiŋkpe̱ŋ-kyo̱ŋ-atɔ‑pɛɛɛ-Bware̱ a kyaamɛɛ‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ e̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ ŋkpɛɛ, na fo̱ ya lɔŋŋɔ mò̱ kpa bo̱ be̱ya mò̱.

ref: As John grew up, God's Spirit gave him great power. John lived in the desert until the time he was sent to the people of Israel.
text: Kayaagyi‑o a daŋ‑ɔ, ɔɔ nya kanyiase̱ŋ na daa timaa. Ne̱ ŋkee ɔɔ yɔ ya kye̱na kimukee‑ro ane̱ŋ‑aaa, bo̱ fo̱ saŋ ne̱ o lee mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ifuri bo̱ kaapo̱ Isireelii awuye‑o.

ref: About that time Emperor Augustus gave orders for the names of all the people to be listed in record books.
text: Ŋke amo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ ne̱ Roma awuye owure dabe̱ Kaye̱saa Agustus a sa mbraa fe̱yɛ bo̱ kyo̱rɛɛ na bo̱ kare̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mò̱ e̱se̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ. Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ ke̱gye̱ŋkpɛɛ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ a sa ane̱ŋ a mbraa‑ɔ.

ref: At this time a man named Simeon was living in Jerusalem. Simeon was a good man. He loved God and was waiting for him to save the people of Israel. God's Spirit came to him
text: Saŋ amo̱ mɔ ɔnyare̱ ko̱ bo̱ Yɛro̱salɛm mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Simeyɔŋ. Ɔnyare̱ amo̱ gye̱ se̱sɛ timaa, ne̱ o suŋ Wuribware̱ nɛɛnɛɛ. Ne̱ ŋkee o te o gywii saŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ i suŋ ɔmo̱rɔwe̱po̱ a ɔ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ Isireelii awuye‑o. Ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o te mò̱ se̱.

ref: Everyone had to go to their own hometown to be listed.
text: Ne̱ baa sa mbraa fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa yɔ mò̱ aye̱, na ɔ kyo̱rɛɛ mò̱ ke̱nyare̱ mfe̱ŋ.

ref: and while they were there,
text: Bamo̱ a bo̱ Bɛte̱lahɛm mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ Mariya ko̱ko̱we̱ a fo̱.

ref: That night in the fields near Bethlehem some shepherds were guarding their sheep.
text: Ke̱mo̱ kanye amo̱ e̱sanne̱ ade̱e̱re̱se̱po̱ ko̱ mɔ bo̱ swe̱e̱re̱ amo̱ se̱, ne̱ bo̱ da e̱fa‑rɔ ba de̱e̱re̱ bamo̱ mbo̱ se̱.

ref: All at once an angel came down to them from the Lord, and the brightness of the Lord's glory flashed around them. The shepherds were frightened.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ a lee mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱. Ne̱ Wuribware̱ se̱ a ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ‑ɔ a we̱e̱ bamo̱ se̱, ne̱ kufu a nya bamo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: But the angel said, “Don't be afraid! I have good news for you, which will make everyone happy.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ‑ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ma sa a kufu nya mo̱ne̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱ŋ kɔne̱kɔne̱sɛ ko̱ ne̱ a baa ɔke̱maa mɔ kakatɔgyi‑o ne̱ mo̱ a baa mo̱ne̱.

ref: You will know who he is, because you will find him dressed in baby clothes and lying on a bed of hay.”
text: Na ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ, mo̱ne̱ ya yɔ, mo̱ne̱ i ŋu fe̱yɛ baa ba waagya bo̱ miri mò̱ bo̱ be̱ya ke̱dakaa ne̱ bo̱ de mbo̱ ateese ba waa‑rɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ.”

ref: Suddenly many other angels came down from heaven and joined in praising God. They said:
text: Puri amo̱‑rɔ, ne̱ Wuribware̱ mbɔɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ a le̱e̱ so̱so̱ bo̱ tii ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛsɛ‑ɔ se̱ e̱sanne̱ ade̱e̱re̱se̱po̱ amo̱ ase̱ mfe̱ŋ, na ba waa iliŋ ba kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱ fe̱yɛ,

ref: After the angels had left and gone back to heaven, the shepherds said to each other, “Let's go to Bethlehem and see what the Lord has told us about.”
text: Amɔ Wuribware̱ mbɔɔ amo̱ a kiŋŋi bo̱ yii so̱so̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ŋkee e̱sanne̱ ade̱e̱re̱se̱po̱ amo̱ yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ yɔ maŋ amo̱‑rɔ ya ke̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ kaa waa mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a suŋ bo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii ane̱‑ɔ.”

ref: They hurried off and found Mary and Joseph, and they saw the baby lying on a bed of hay.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa waa me̱naŋ yɔ Bɛte̱lahɛm ya ŋu Mariya na Yo̱sɛf na kayaagyi‑o, na ɔ da ke̱dakaa ne̱ bo̱ de mbo̱ ateese ba waa‑rɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: When the shepherds saw Jesus, they told his parents what the angel had said about him.
text: Ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ kayaagyi amo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ gywii bamo̱.

ref: But Mary kept thinking about all this and wondering what it meant.
text: Mariya mɔ a taa ase̱ŋ mɔ pɛɛɛ bo̱ be̱ya mò̱ kuŋu‑ro, ne̱ ɔɔ fa amo̱ mfɛɛre̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: As the shepherds returned to their sheep, they were praising God and saying wonderful things about him. Everything they had seen and heard was just as the angel had said.
text: Ne̱ e̱sanne̱ ade̱e̱re̱se̱po̱‑ɔ a kiŋŋi ba yɔ, na ba waa iliŋ ba kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ baa nu ne̱ baa ŋu‑o si. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ‑ɔ a de̱ŋ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱‑ɔ dɛɛ ya waa. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa sa kayaagyi‑o ke̱nyare̱‑ɔ

ref: Eight days later Jesus' parents did for him what the Law of Moses commands. And they named him Jesus, just as the angel had told Mary when he promised she would have a baby. Simeon Praises the Lord
text: Kayaagyi‑o a gyi ŋke mburuwa‑o, ne̱ saŋ a fo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ te̱ŋ mò̱ ko̱twe̱e̱tu‑o. Ne̱ baa te̱ŋ mò̱ ko̱twe̱e̱tu. Ne̱ baa sa mò̱ ke̱nyare̱ ɛ Yeesuu, ne̱ ke̱ gye̱ ke̱nyare̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ‑ɔ a sa mò̱ pwɛɛ ne̱ Mariya a dɛɛ nya mò̱ kame‑o. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa yaa Yeesuu Wuribware̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ

ref: When Mary and Joseph brought Jesus to the temple to do what the Law of Moses says should be done for a new baby, the Spirit told Simeon to go into the temple.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a sa ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ kabuno kake nsi ne̱ Yo̱sɛf na Mariya a baa Yeesuu mfe̱ŋ na bo̱ bo̱ waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ e̱ kaapo̱‑ɔ.

ref: Simeon took the baby Jesus in his arms and praised God,
text: Ne̱ Simeyɔŋ a kɔɔre̱ keegyi‑o o de mò̱ asare̱e̱‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ sa Wuribware̱ aŋsɛ fe̱yɛ,

ref: “Lord, I am your servant, and now I can die in peace, because you have kept your promise to me.
text: “O, Nyaŋpe̱ Wuribware̱, mo̱ mfɛɛre̱ a di mo̱, ŋkee fe̱raa sa a ŋ wu,

ref: Your mighty power is a light for all nations, and it will bring honor to your people Israel.”
text: O kii fe̱yɛ fetiri a ɔ bo̱ sa nde̱ se̱ awuye, na bo̱ nya gyii fo̱ e̱kpa‑ɔ. Na fo̱ ase̱sɛ Isireelii awuye kaapo̱ bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ mò̱ ke̱dabe̱ na mò̱ wuraa‑o si.”

ref: After Joseph and Mary had done everything that the Law of the Lord commands, they returned home to Nazareth in Galilee.
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yo̱sɛf na mò̱ ka Mariya a waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ e̱ kaapo̱‑ɔ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa kiŋŋi yɔ bamo̱ kye̱nakpa Nasarɛt maŋ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: The child Jesus grew. He became strong and wise, and God blessed him. The Boy Jesus in the Temple
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ bamo̱ gyi amo̱ a ya daŋ nya e̱le̱ŋ. Ne̱ Wuribware̱ a sa mò̱ kanyiase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ sa mò̱ ŋyure. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu ŋyaagyi‑ro, ɔɔ yɔ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ

ref: After Passover his parents left, but they did not know that Jesus had stayed on in the city.
text: Bamo̱ a lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ŋkee ba kiŋŋi ba yɔ pe̱‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu wo̱re̱ a sii Yɛro̱salɛm, na mò̱ ako̱we̱po̱ mo̱ŋ nyi.

ref: When they could not find him with their relatives and friends, they went back to Jerusalem and started looking for him there.
text: Amaa baa de̱e̱re̱ kpo̱ne̱, bo̱ mo̱ŋ ŋu mò̱. Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa kiŋŋi be̱e̱ yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm ba buwi ba kpa mò̱.

ref: Everyone who heard him was surprised at how much he knew and at the answers he gave.
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa ŋu ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a nu ase̱ŋ‑ɔ kaase̱ na mò̱ gbaa e̱ be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱‑ɔ, e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ bamo̱.

ref: When his parents found him, they were amazed. His mother said, “Son, why have you done this to us? Your father and I have been very worried, and we have been searching for you!”
text: Mò̱ se̱ na mò̱ nyi a ya ŋu mò̱‑ɔ, ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ bamo̱, ne̱ mò̱ nyi yɛ, “Mo̱ gyi, nte̱tɔ ne̱e̱ ne̱ fo̱ a waa ane̱ mfaanɛɛ‑ɔ ne̱e̱? Fo̱ a sa ne̱ mo̱ aa fo̱ se̱ akatɔ a pee ane̱ i buwi ane̱ e̱ kpa fo̱.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Why did you have to look for me? Didn't you know that I would be in my Father's house?”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ nyi na mò̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ naa mo̱ne̱ i buwi mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa mo̱ to̱ŋ ko̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱? Mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ a tiri fe̱yɛ n kye̱na n se̱ a lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱?”

ref: But they did not understand what he meant.
text: Amaa bo̱ mo̱ŋ nu mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ kaase̱.

ref: Jesus went back to Nazareth with his parents and obeyed them. His mother kept on thinking about all that had happened.
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ aa Yeesuu a kiŋŋi yɔ Nasarɛt maŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ oo bu bamo̱ nɛɛnɛɛ. Ne̱ mò̱ nyi a taa ase̱ŋ mɔ pɛɛɛ bo̱ be̱ya mò̱ kuŋu‑ro.

ref: Jesus became wise, and he grew strong. God was pleased with him and so were the people.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a daŋ, ne̱ mò̱ kanyiase̱ŋ a waa ko̱kyɔ. Ne̱ Wuribware̱ na ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ a kpa mò̱ ase̱ŋ.

ref: When tax collectors came to be baptized, they asked John, “Teacher, what should we do?”
text: Ne̱ leŋpoo akɔɔre̱po̱ ko̱ a ba a bo̱ bo̱ bɔ asuu, ne̱ baa bise Yohanee fe̱yɛ, “Ɔkaapo̱po̱, nte̱tɔ ne̱ ane̱ waa?”

ref: Isaiah the prophet wrote about John when he said, “In the desert someone is shouting, ‘Get the road ready for the Lord! Make a straight path for him.
text: Yohanee mɔ ase̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ Isaya a kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔko̱ e̱ fɛɛ‑rɔ kimukee‑ro fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱nꞌ lɔŋŋɔ kpa nɛɛnɛɛ bo̱ be̱ya Wuribware̱, na mo̱nꞌ waa kpa kye̱rarasɛ sa mò̱ a ɔ bo̱ bo̱rɔ se̱.

ref: Fill up every valley and level every mountain and hill. Straighten the crooked paths and smooth out the rough roads.
text: Ke̱maŋtaŋ ke̱maa mɔ e̱ bo̱rɔ a ki tii, na ke̱be̱e̱ na ke̱be̱e̱gyi ke̱maa mɔ buburi ba kaase̱. E̱kpa kɔre̱sɛ e̱ waa kyɛye̱, na swe̱e̱re̱ ne̱ o du sake̱sake̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ mɔ gyiiri si.

ref: Then everyone will see the saving power of God.’ ”
text: Na kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ ŋu ɔmo̱rɔwe̱po̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ i suŋ a ɔ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱‑ɔ.’ ”

ref: The crowds asked John, “What should we do?”
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ a bise Yohanee fe̱yɛ, “Ne̱ nte̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ kpa a ane̱ waa?”

ref: Some soldiers asked him, “And what about us? What do we have to do?” John told them, “Don't force people to pay money to make you leave them alone. Be satisfied with your pay.”
text: Ne̱ asoogyaa ko̱ mɔ a bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ne̱ ane̱ mɔ ane̱ ɛɛɛ? Nte̱tɔ ne̱ ane̱ waa?” Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ma le̱ŋ ɔko̱ kɔɔre̱ atanne̱, na mo̱ne̱ ma po̱rɔ ɔko̱ na ɔ mo̱ŋ waa bɔye̱ kɔɔre̱ mò̱ atanne̱. Mo̱ne̱ atanne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i suŋ ne̱ ba ka mo̱ne̱‑ɔ a baare. Mo̱ne̱ ma tɛɛ fe̱yɛ a mo̱ŋ kyɔ.”

ref: John said, “I am just baptizing with water. But someone more powerful is going to come, and I am not good enough even to untie his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.
text: Ne̱ Yohanee a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ pɛɛɛ fe̱yɛ, “Nkyu ne̱ n de mo̱ e̱ bɔ mo̱ne̱ asuu, amaa ɔko̱ saŋ ɔ ba na ɔ kyɔ mo̱, na mo̱ŋ fo̱ fe̱yɛ n lee mò̱ ase̱be̱ta gbaa na m fwe̱e̱ mò̱ ayaapapaa se̱. Mò̱ fe̱raa ɔ sa a Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o ywe̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱e̱ na mo̱nꞌ nya ke̱yaale̱ŋ fe̱yɛ de̱e̱kpa‑ɔ. Na ɔ ba kufwiiŋe timaa‑o bo̱ gyee mo̱ne̱.

ref: While everyone else was being baptized, Jesus himself was baptized. Then as he prayed, the sky opened up,
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yohanee a bɔ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ asuu‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ bɔ Yeesuu asuu. Mò̱ a bɔ mò̱ asuu lo̱we̱, ne̱ Yeesuu e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱‑ɔ, ne̱ awo̱re̱‑rɔ a buŋŋi‑ro.

ref: and the Holy Spirit came down upon him in the form of a dove. A voice from heaven said, “You are my own dear Son, and I am pleased with you.” The Ancestors of Jesus (Matthew
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a ba bo̱ ywe̱e̱ Yeesuu si fe̱yɛ kuwurele̱pɔ‑ɔ. Ne̱ bo̱re̱ ko̱ a le̱e̱ so̱so̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ e̱ gye̱ mo̱ gyi timaa, ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa fo̱ ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ, ne̱ mo̱ akatɔ a gyi fo̱ se̱.” Yeesuu ke̱nana‑rɔ (Matiyo

ref: Enosh, and Seth. The family of Jesus went all the way back to Adam and then to God.
text: Ne̱ Inɔs mɔ mò̱ se̱ e̱ gye̱ Sɛt, ne̱ Sɛt mɔ mò̱ se̱ e̱ gye̱ Adam, ne̱ ŋkee Adam mɔ mò̱ se̱ e̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱.

ref: The Scriptures say: ‘God will tell his angels to take care of you. They will catch you in their arms, and you will not even hurt your feet on the stones.’ ”
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ baa be̱e̱ kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Wuribware̱ e̱ sa a mò̱ mbɔɔ‑ɔ bo̱ kuŋ fo̱ nɛɛnɛɛ, na bo̱ so̱rɔ fo̱ bamo̱ asare̱e̱ se̱, na fo̱ ma nya da ke̱yaa kibu si.’ ”

ref: After the sun had set, people with all kinds of diseases were brought to Jesus. He put his hands on each one of them and healed them.
text: Saŋ ne̱ kyɔwe̱ e̱ twɛɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ ase̱sɛ a baa bamo̱ anyipo̱ ne̱ ba lɔ alɔgyi alɔgyi‑o Yeesuu ase̱, ne̱ ɔɔ ba mò̱ asare̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ bamo̱ se̱ ako̱ ako̱, ne̱ bamo̱ ɔke̱maa a kpaare̱.

ref: The devil said to Jesus, “If you are God's Son, tell this stone to turn into bread.”
text: Ne̱ Ɔbɔnsam a tɔwe̱ gywii Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ i nyi fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑o e̱ gye̱ fo̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ, amo̱ fe̱raa sa a kibu mɔ buruwi bodobodoo sa fo̱ a fo̱ gyi.”

ref: Then the devil led Jesus up to a high place and quickly showed him all the nations on earth.
text: Ne̱ Ɔbɔnsam a yaa mò̱ so̱so̱ to̱ŋ ko̱, puri amo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ɔɔ ba kaye̱‑rɔ e̱maŋ pɛɛɛ bo̱ kaapo̱ Yeesuu.

ref: Just worship me, and you can have it all.”
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ fo̱ ya kpuni aŋurii suŋ mo̱, e̱mo̱ pɛɛɛ mɔ i kii fo̱ lee.”

ref: Finally, the devil took Jesus to Jerusalem and had him stand on top of the temple. The devil said, “If you are God's Son, jump off.
text: Ne̱ Ɔbɔnsam a be̱e̱ yaa mò̱ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ taa mò̱ yaa bo̱ ye̱ra Wuribware̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ katwe̱e̱‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ i nyi fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑o e̱ gye̱ fo̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ, amo̱ fe̱raa fuwi ywe̱e̱‑rɔ a ŋ ke̱e̱.

ref: He taught in the Jewish synagogues, and everyone praised him. The People of Nazareth Turn against Jesus (Matthew
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ kaapo̱ ase̱sɛ abware̱se̱ŋ mfe̱ŋ a ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ akyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔke̱maa a kyo̱rɔ mò̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Nasarɛt awuye a kine Yeesuu‑o (Matiyo

ref: Jesus went back to Nazareth, where he had been brought up, and as usual he went to the synagogue on the Sabbath. When he stood up to read from the Scriptures,
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a yɔ Nasarɛt maŋ‑nɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ baa be̱ra mò̱‑ɔ. Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ kyɔ ɔ waa‑ɔ, kukyure kake a fo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ mfe̱ŋ a ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ baa ko̱re̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ kare̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ gywii bamo̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ ko̱so̱ a ɔ kare̱.

ref: “The Lord's Spirit has come to me, because he has chosen me to tell the good news to the poor. The Lord has sent me to announce freedom for prisoners, to give sight to the blind, to free everyone who suffers,
text: “Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o te mo̱ se̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ oo lee mo̱ suŋ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ ya tɔwe̱ mò̱ ase̱ŋ timaa gywii atiripo̱. Oo suŋ mo̱ fe̱yɛ m bo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ ne̱ baa tii bamo̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ ba nya bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱, na n tɔwe̱ gywii ate̱napo̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ akatɔ i buŋŋi. Oo suŋ mo̱ fe̱yɛ m bo̱ kya bamo̱ ne̱ baa waa bamo̱ bo̱rɔkraa‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: and to say, ‘This is the year the Lord has chosen.’ ”
text: Na n tɔwe̱ gywii ɔke̱maa fe̱yɛ saŋ a fo̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ e̱ ba a ɔ bo̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ mò̱ ase̱sɛ.”

ref: Jesus closed the book, then handed it back to the man in charge and sat down. Everyone in the synagogue looked straight at Jesus.
text: Yeesuu a kare̱ gywii bamo̱ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ bɔɔ wo̱re̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ laŋŋaa sa ɔbre̱sɛ ne̱ ɔɔ taa sa mò̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ ŋkee ɔɔ kye̱na ko̱re̱ bamo̱ se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ ɔ kaapo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ. Ne̱ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ bo̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ e̱ de̱e̱re̱ mò̱ diŋŋ.

ref: All the people started talking about Jesus and were amazed at the wonderful things he said. They kept on asking, “Isn't he Joseph's son?”
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ akatɔ a gyi mò̱ se̱, ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ bamo̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ o nyi se̱ŋsa e̱sa‑ɔ se̱. Ne̱ baa kuri gywii abɛɛ, “N gye̱ ane̱ aye̱po̱ Yo̱sɛf mò̱ gyi‑o ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱?”

ref: Jesus answered: You will certainly want to tell me this saying, “Doctor, first make yourself well.” You will tell me to do the same things here in my own hometown that you heard I did in Capernaum.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ a n gyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱ da ke̱kpare̱ mɔ a mo̱nꞌ bo̱ kye mo̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ɔkyapo̱, kya fo̱ e̱ye̱e̱,’ na mo̱nꞌ tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ waa atɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a nu fe̱yɛ mo̱ a waa Kapaaniyum maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ aye̱ mfe̱e̱.

ref: But you can be sure that no prophets are liked by the people of their own hometown.
text: Mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ ba bu Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ to̱ŋ ke̱maa, amɔ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ aye̱ wo̱re̱.

ref: Once during the time of Elijah there was no rain for three and a half years, and people everywhere were starving. There were many widows in Israel,
text: Bɛɛɛ mo̱ e̱ ba aye̱ba aaa? Mo̱nꞌ nyiŋŋi Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ Iliya ke̱be̱e̱‑ɔ se̱, saŋ‑ɔ bware̱ a ye̱re̱ e̱wo̱re̱ nsu nsa na ke̱be̱gya, ne̱ ako̱ŋ a ba bwe̱e̱tɔ swe̱e̱re̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ se̱‑ɔ.

ref: but Elijah was sent only to a widow in the town of Zarephath near the city of Sidon.
text: Saŋ amo̱‑ɔ akpenlekye̱e̱ kyɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ Isireelii swe̱e̱re̱ se̱. Amaa Wuribware̱ mo̱ŋ suŋ Iliya fe̱yɛ ɔ kya bamo̱‑rɔ. Kpenlekye̱e̱ ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Isireeliinyi ne̱ o te Sarafat maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ ase̱ wo̱re̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a suŋ mò̱.

ref: During the time of the prophet Elisha, many men in Israel had leprosy. But no one was healed, except Naaman who lived in Syria.
text: Ne̱ saŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ Ilisa be̱e̱ ɔ bo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ e̱ lɔ ke̱bwatɔ ane̱ Isireelii swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ, Ilisa mo̱ŋ sa a ɔko̱ kpaare̱, amɔ ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Naamaŋ ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ Siriya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ wo̱re̱ kpeŋ.”

ref: But Jesus slipped through the crowd and got away. A Man with an Evil Spirit (Mark
text: Amaa Yeesuu a bo̱rɔ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ kyo̱ŋ ne̱e̱, me̱raa mò̱ kpa se̱ o yii yɔwe̱ bamo̱ mfe̱ŋ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ibrisi te mò̱ se̱‑ɔ (Maak

ref: Jesus went to the town of Capernaum in Galilee and taught the people on the Sabbath.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a yɔ Kapaaniyum maŋ‑nɔ Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱. Kukyure kake‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ ya kaapo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ mfe̱ŋ a ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: There in the synagogue was a man with an evil spirit. He yelled out,
text: Amɔ saŋ amo̱ ɔnyare̱ ko̱ bo̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ, ne̱ ibrisi te mò̱ se̱. Ne̱ ɔnyare̱‑ɔ a faa‑rɔ keŋkeŋ fe̱yɛ,

ref: They all were amazed and kept saying to each other, “What kind of teaching is this? He has power to order evil spirits out of people!”
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ bamo̱, ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ ase̱ŋ ne̱e̱, ne̱ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa ane̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱e̱? Ɔnyare̱ amo̱ de ibrisi pɛɛɛ mɔ se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ, hare̱e̱ mò̱ ya tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ, na e̱ ko̱so̱ se̱sɛ se̱.”

ref: News about Jesus spread all over that part of the country. Jesus Heals Many People (Matthew
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu ke̱nyare̱ a yɔ mfe̱ŋ a ifuri‑o pɛɛɛ se̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya alɔpo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: Jesus left the synagogue and went to Simon's home. When Jesus got there, he was told that Simon's mother-in-law was sick with a high fever.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a le̱e̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ yɔ Simɔŋ lɔŋ‑nɔ. Simɔŋ mɔ gye̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ gya Yeesuu si‑o ɔko̱ ne̱e̱. Saŋ amo̱ na Simɔŋ mò̱ saa kye̱e̱sɛ e̱ lɔ fekyu, ne̱ mò̱ kayo̱wɔre̱ a be̱re̱ gya bwe̱e̱tɔ. Ne̱ baa ko̱re̱ Yeesuu fe̱yɛ ɔ kya mò̱‑rɔ.

ref: The next morning Jesus went out to a place where he could be alone, and crowds came looking for him. When they found him, they tried to stop him from leaving.
text: Ke̱mo̱ kaye̱ ŋke gye̱gyaye̱ kese‑ro, ne̱ Yeesuu a le̱e̱ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ yɔ kɛɛ se̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱, ne̱ a du diŋŋ‑o. Amo̱ fe̱raa ne̱ ase̱sɛ a le̱e̱ ba buwi ba kpa mò̱. Bamo̱ a ya ŋu mò̱‑ɔ, ŋkee bo̱ ma lɛɛ kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ le̱e̱ bamo̱ ase̱ mfe̱ŋ.

ref: But Jesus said, “People in other towns must hear the good news about God's kingdom. This is why I was sent.”
text: Amaa ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “A tiri fe̱yɛ n tɔwe̱ Wuribware̱ a kuwure‑o‑ro a ase̱ŋ timaa‑o gywii e̱maŋ ko̱ gbaa se̱ awuye, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a suŋ mo̱ a m bo̱ waa‑ɔ ne̱e̱.”

ref: So he kept on preaching in the synagogues in Judea.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ ɔɔ me̱raa se̱ ɔ naa ɔ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ Yudaa awuye e̱maŋ se̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ akyaŋ‑nɔ.

ref: But Jesus would often go to some place where he could be alone and pray. Jesus Heals a Man Who Could Not Walk (Matthew
text: Amaa mò̱ ya nya kpa ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ taare̱‑ɔ, ɔ yɔ ɔ naa mò̱ wo̱re̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ a du diŋŋ‑o na ɔ nya ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya ke̱gyabɔɔ ko̱‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: When Jesus saw how much faith they had, he said to the man, “My friend, your sins are forgiven.”
text: Ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa pee akatɔ baa ke̱gyabɔɔ amo̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, Yeesuu a ŋu fe̱yɛ baa kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi bwe̱e̱tɔ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii ke̱gyabɔɔ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ nyare̱, mo̱ a taa fo̱ e̱bɔye̱‑ɔ bo̱ ke fo̱.”

ref: Jesus got into the boat that belonged to Simon and asked him to row it out a little way from the shore. Then Jesus sat down in the boat to teach the crowd.
text: Ne̱ oo lwee e̱ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ ɔko̱‑rɔ. Simɔŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya mò̱ saa kye̱e̱sɛ fekyu‑o lee ne̱e̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ ko̱re̱ Simɔŋ fe̱yɛ o neŋ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ nkyu si kafwe̱e̱, ne̱ Yeesuu a kye̱na ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ kaapo̱ ase̱sɛ abware̱se̱ŋ.

ref: “Master,” Simon answered, “we have worked hard all night long and have not caught a thing. But if you tell me to, I will let the nets down.”
text: Ne̱ Simɔŋ yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, ane̱ a waa kusuŋ kanye‑o pɛɛɛ, ane̱ mɔ ane̱ mo̱ŋ kra sɛye̱. Amaa fo̱ e̱ yɛ ane̱ŋ fe̱raa, mo̱ e̱ twe̱e̱ asawu a ŋ waa‑rɔ.”

ref: They did this and caught so many fish that their nets began ripping apart.
text: Ne̱ baa twe̱e̱ asawu‑o waa‑rɔ. Ne̱ baa kra ŋkiŋgyi bwe̱e̱tɔ, hare̱e̱ asawu‑o e̱ kpa a a kyaye̱‑rɔ.

ref: Then they signaled for their partners in the other boat to come and help them. The men came, and together they filled the two boats so full that they both began to sink.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa ya bamo̱ ke̱sare̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana ne̱ bamo̱ gbaa bo̱ bo̱ bamo̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑rɔ‑ɔ bo̱ kya bamo̱‑rɔ. Ne̱ baa bo̱ kya bamo̱‑rɔ, ne̱ baa lee ŋkiŋgyi‑o bo̱ bo̱rɔ e̱ko̱re̱e̱ e̱nyɔ‑ɔ, hare̱e̱ e̱ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ se̱ e̱ kpa a e̱ gya nkyu.

ref: The men pulled their boats up on the shore. Then they left everything and went with Jesus. Jesus Heals a Man (Matthew
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ baa gyiiri bamo̱ e̱ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ bo̱ be̱ya ke̱pare̱‑ɔ kideŋbe si, ne̱ baa yɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa, ne̱ baa gya Yeesuu si. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya bwatɔpo̱ ko̱‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: News about Jesus kept spreading. Large crowds came to listen to him teach and to be healed of their diseases.
text: Amo̱ gbaa ne̱ Yeesuu ke̱nyare̱ a yɔ e̱maŋ se̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱. Ne̱ ase̱sɛ biribiri a ba a bo̱ bo̱ nu ase̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ tɔwe̱‑ɔ, na ɔ kya bamo̱ alɔ.

ref: Is it easier for me to tell this man that his sins are forgiven or to tell him to get up and walk?
text: Ke̱mo̱‑ɔ ke̱mo̱ e̱ bo̱ tɔwe̱ lɔ, ‘Mo̱ a taa fo̱ e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ ke fo̱’ bɛɛɛ ‘Ko̱so̱ nare̱’?

ref: But now you will see that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” Jesus then said to the man, “Get up! Pick up your mat and walk home.”
text: To, mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, de ke̱yaale̱ŋ swe̱e̱re̱ mɔ se̱ na n taare̱ taa ɔke̱maa e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ ke mò̱.” Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii ke̱gyabɔɔ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ke̱e̱ ɛ, ko̱so̱ taa fo̱ kraŋ a fo̱ nare̱ yɔ pe̱.”

ref: At once the man stood up in front of everyone. He picked up his mat and went home, giving thanks to God.
text: Puri amo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ke̱gyabɔɔ amo̱ a ko̱so̱ lamaŋ amo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ bɔɔ mò̱ kraŋ, ne̱ ɔɔ nare̱ mò̱ ayaa anyɔ se̱ o yii pe̱, na ɔ kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱.

ref: Everyone was amazed and praised God. What they saw surprised them, and they said, “We have seen a great miracle today!” Jesus Chooses Levi (Matthew
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ mɔ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ bamo̱. Ne̱ kufu a nya bamo̱, ne̱ baa kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱ na ba tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ase̱ŋ dabe̱ ko̱ ne̱ ane̱ a ŋu ndɔɔ.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a te̱e̱ Lewii, ne̱ ɔɔ ba bo̱ gya mò̱ se̱‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: Later, Jesus went out and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at the place for paying taxes. Jesus said to him, “Follow me.”
text: Amo̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu a le̱e̱ ya ŋu leŋpoo ɔkɔɔre̱po̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Lewii‑o na o te mò̱ leŋpoo kɔɔre̱kpa. Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ko̱so̱ gya mo̱ se̱.”

ref: Levi left everything and went with Jesus.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Lewii a ko̱so̱ yɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa, ne̱ ɔɔ gya Yeesuu si.

ref: In his home Levi gave a big dinner for Jesus. Many tax collectors and other guests were also there.
text: Ne̱ Lewii a ya waa ateese mò̱ aye̱ sa Yeesuu a o gyi. Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ nyiŋŋi leŋpoo akɔɔre̱po̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ na ase̱sɛ ko̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ba na bo̱ gyi ateese amo̱.

ref: The Pharisees and some of their teachers of the Law of Moses grumbled to Jesus' disciples, “Why do you eat and drink with these tax collectors and other sinners?”
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa ne̱ Farisii awuye‑o na Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱ ko̱ ne̱ bo̱ tii bamo̱ se̱ bo̱ gya bamo̱ ananatɔ se̱‑ɔ a le̱e̱ ba tɛɛ ba gywii Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ, ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ aa leŋpoo akɔɔre̱po̱ na e̱bɔye̱ awaapo̱ i gyi abɛɛ ase̱?”

ref: Jesus answered, “Healthy people don't need a doctor, but sick people do.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a nya me̱naŋ be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ lɔ‑ɔ ne̱ ba sa kadwii. N gye̱ ɔko̱ ne̱ o de alaŋfiya‑o.

ref: I didn't come to invite good people to turn to God. I came to invite sinners.” People Ask about Going without Eating (Matthew
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ n gye̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ e̱bɔye̱ awaapo̱‑ɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ a ba, amaa bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ e̱bɔye̱ awaapo̱‑ɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ a ba, na m bo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ nu bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ kiŋŋi le̱e̱ bamo̱ e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ.” Ako̱ŋ ke̱kra ase̱ŋ (Matiyo

ref: Some people said to Jesus, “John's followers often pray and go without eating, and so do the followers of the Pharisees. But your disciples never go without eating or drinking.”
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ ko̱ a bo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Yohanee a agyase̱po̱‑ɔ na Farisii awuye a bo̱mo̱‑ɔ e̱ kra ako̱ŋ iluwi bwe̱e̱tɔ na ba ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱, amaa fo̱ fe̱raa fo̱ bo̱mo̱‑ɔ i gyi ba nuu saŋ ke̱maa.”

ref: Jesus told them, “The friends of a bridegroom don't go without eating while he is still with them.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ e̱ fa fe̱yɛ saŋ ne̱ ɔko̱ e̱ kpa a ɔ waare̱e̱‑ɔ, ɔko̱fɔ mò̱ kuri mò̱ nyare̱‑ana e̱ kye̱na ako̱ŋ na ɔ bo̱‑rɔ aaa? Mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ gyi.

ref: Jesus then told them these sayings: No one uses a new piece of cloth to patch old clothes. The patch would shrink and make the hole even bigger.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ ke̱kpare̱ mɔ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔko̱ maa kyaŋ waagya po̱pwɛɛ na ɔ bo̱ taa waagya de̱daa‑rɔ. Ɔko̱ ne̱ mò̱ ya waa ane̱ŋ, ɔ nye̱ra waagya po̱pwɛɛ‑ɔ, na mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ ba waagya‑ɔ bo̱ taa‑rɔ‑ɔ na waagya de̱daa‑ɔ maa waa ko̱ŋko̱.

ref: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The new wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined.
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ nyi fe̱yɛ bo̱ de nta ba waa praŋto̱wa ne̱ ba ba kabo̱ wo̱re̱ bo̱ waa‑ɔ‑rɔ. Bo̱ de nta po̱pwɛɛ ba waa praŋto̱wa po̱pwɛɛ‑rɔ. Nta po̱pwɛɛ ya lwee praŋto̱wa de̱daa‑rɔ, nta‑ɔ i fwii na praŋto̱wa‑ɔ bwee nye̱ra na nta‑ɔ twiiri we̱e̱.

ref: New wine must be put only into new wineskins.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, bo̱ de nta po̱pwɛɛ ba waa praŋto̱wa po̱pwɛɛ‑rɔ.

ref: No one wants new wine after drinking old wine. They say, “The old wine is better.”
text: Ne̱ ɔko̱ mɔ ya nuu nta de̱daa, ɔ maa lɛɛ kpa mpo̱pwɛɛ kunuu. Ɔ yɛ nde̱daa‑ɔ bɔ mpo̱pwɛɛ‑ɔ. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ mo̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ maa kra ako̱ŋ.”

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he told the man to stand up where everyone could see him. And the man stood up.
text: Amaa Yeesuu a kyɔ ŋu bamo̱ mfɛɛre̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii ke̱sare̱e̱ wusɛ wuye amo̱ ɛ, “Ko̱so̱ ba akatɔ‑rɔ mfe̱e̱.” Ne̱ ɔnyare̱‑ɔ a ko̱so̱ ye̱re̱ ba akatɔ‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ.

ref: Ask God to bless anyone who curses you, and pray for everyone who is cruel to you.
text: Na mo̱nꞌ yure bamo̱ ne̱ bamo̱ fe̱raa ba da mo̱ne̱ yii‑o, na mo̱nꞌ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ sa bamo̱ ne̱ ba waa mo̱ne̱ bo̱rɔkraa‑ɔ.

ref: One was Simon, and Jesus named him Peter. Another was Andrew, Peter's brother. There were also James, John, Philip, Bartholomew,
text: Bamo̱ e̱ gye̱ Simɔŋ na mò̱ tire Andruu, na Yakubu na Yohanee na Filipo na Bar‑Tolomiyu,

ref: On another Sabbath Jesus was teaching in a synagogue, and a man with a paralyzed right hand was there.
text: Kukyure kake ko̱ be̱e̱ ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ yɔ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ‑nɔ ya kaapo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ. Saŋ amo̱‑ɔ ɔnyare̱ ko̱ mɔ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ na mò̱ kigyisesare̱e̱ a wu.

ref: The teachers and the Pharisees were furious and started saying to one another, “What can we do about Jesus?” Jesus Chooses His Twelve Apostles (Matthew
text: Bo̱ le̱e̱ mfaanɛɛ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ Farisii awuye‑o na Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ iduŋ a fwii bwe̱e̱tɔ Yeesuu si, ne̱ baa le̱e̱ ba fa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ba waa mò̱‑ɔ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a lee mò̱ ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱ kudu anyɔ‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: The next morning he called his disciples together and chose twelve of them to be his apostles.
text: Kaye̱ a ke‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ baa mò̱ ase̱, ne̱ oo lee bamo̱‑rɔ kudu anyɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ waa mò̱ ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱.

ref: Everyone was trying to touch Jesus, because power was going out from him and healing them all. Blessings and Troubles (Matthew
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a le̱ŋ e̱ye̱e̱ ba kpa fe̱yɛ bo̱ dabo̱rɔ mò̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱yaale̱ŋ ne̱ o de‑o ɔ taare̱ a ɔ bo̱ kya bamo̱ pɛɛɛ. Ke̱be̱e̱ se̱ abware̱se̱ŋ ko̱tɔwe̱ (Matiyo

ref: Matthew, Thomas, and James the son of Alphaeus. The rest of the apostles were Simon, known as the Eager One,
text: na ŋkee Matiyo na Tomas na Alafiyus mò̱ gyi Yakubu, na Simɔŋ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Sɛlɔt ,
