ref: You well-fed people are in for trouble. You will go hungry! You people who are laughing now are in for trouble. You are going to cry and weep!
text: Ase̱ŋ dabe̱ e̱ to̱ mo̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a gyi ke̱e̱paa mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ako̱ŋ saŋ a ba a bo̱ nya mo̱ne̱. Ase̱ŋ dabe̱ e̱ to̱ mo̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ mɔse̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱re̱‑rɔ saŋ ɔ ba a ɔ bo̱ duŋwi mo̱ne̱ na mo̱nꞌ su.

ref: These people had come to listen to Jesus and to be healed of their diseases. All who were troubled by evil spirits were also healed.
text: Baa ba ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ bo̱ nu abware̱se̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ na ɔ be̱e̱ kya bamo̱ alɔ. Bamo̱ ne̱ ibrisi te bamo̱ se̱ e̱e̱ tɔɔraa bamo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ mɔ a ba mfe̱ŋ. Ne̱ ɔɔ gya e̱mo̱ bo̱ ko̱so̱ bamo̱ se̱.

ref: God will bless you hungry people. You will have plenty to eat! God will bless you people who are now crying. You will laugh!
text: Mo̱ne̱ ne̱ ako̱ŋ e̱ mɔɔ mo̱ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ de ŋyure, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ saŋ mo̱ne̱ e̱ ke̱e̱paa. Mo̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱bo̱re̱‑rɔ i duŋwi mo̱ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ de ŋyure, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ saŋ mo̱ne̱ e̱ mɔse̱.

ref: You are in for trouble when everyone says good things about you. That is what your own people said about those prophets who told lies. Love for Enemies (Matthew
text: Ase̱ŋ dabe̱ e̱ to̱ mo̱ne̱ ne̱ ase̱sɛ e̱ sa mo̱ne̱ ke̱nyare̱ timaa mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ bamo̱ ade̱daapo̱ a sa bamo̱ ne̱ baa bo̱ pe̱nna fe̱yɛ bo̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ ke̱nyare̱ timaa.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ kpa ane̱ ado̱ŋ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: If you give to others, you will be given a full amount in return. It will be packed down, shaken together, and spilling over into your lap. The way you treat others is the way you will be treated.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ya sa mo̱ne̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana atɔ, Wuribware̱ mɔ e̱ sa mo̱ne̱ mo̱ne̱ lee. Kase̱ŋtiŋ Wuribware̱ e̱ ba atɔ a ɔ bo̱ bo̱rɔ mò̱ kyuŋputiŋ‑o, na o yee si, na ɔ be̱e̱ nya bo̱ bo̱rɔ se̱, na a bo̱rɔwe̱, na ɔ taa sa mo̱ne̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ sa mo̱ne̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana‑ɔ dɛɛ ne̱ Wuribware̱ mɔ i kiŋŋaa a ɔ bo̱ sa mo̱ne̱.”

ref: If someone slaps you on one cheek, don't stop that person from slapping you on the other cheek. If someone wants to take your coat, don't try to keep back your shirt.
text: Fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ ya da fo̱ ko̱ ke̱se̱bɔ‑rɔ ke̱baa, na fo̱ be̱e̱ buruwaa ke̱nyɔse̱po̱‑ɔ sa mò̱ a ɔ da. Ɔko̱ ya taa fo̱ waagya, na fo̱ taa fo̱ kaare̱ bo̱ tii mò̱ si na ɔ taa nare̱.

ref: Give to everyone who asks and don't ask people to return what they have taken from you.
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ ko̱re̱ fo̱ ko̱tɔko̱‑ɔ, fo̱ i de, sa mò̱ ke̱tɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔko̱ ya taa fo̱ ke̱tɔ, fo̱ ma tɔɔraa mò̱ fe̱yɛ o kiŋŋaa ke̱mo̱ baa fo̱.

ref: If you love only someone who loves you, will God praise you for that? Even sinners love people who love them.
text: Mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa bamo̱ ne̱ ba kpa mo̱ne̱‑ɔ wo̱re̱ ase̱ŋ, e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ waa a mo̱nꞌ nya ŋyure le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱? A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ gbaa, bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ e̱bɔye̱ awaapo̱‑ɔ, e̱ kpa bamo̱ ne̱ ba kpa bamo̱‑ɔ ase̱ŋ.

ref: Have pity on others, just as your Father has pity on you. Judging Others (Matthew
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱nꞌ ŋu ase̱sɛ e̱wɛɛ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ se̱ Wuribware̱ i ŋu ase̱sɛ e̱wɛɛ‑ɔ.” Mo̱ne̱ ma po̱rɔ mo̱ne̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana (Matiyo

ref: Jesus also used some sayings as he spoke to the people. He said: Can one blind person lead another blind person? Won't they both fall into a ditch?
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a da ke̱kpare̱ mɔ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ e̱ gye̱ te̱napo̱, fo̱ e̱ taare̱ a fo̱ kaapo̱ fo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ te̱napo̱ kpa aaa? Mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ gyi. Fo̱ ya waa ane̱ŋ, mo̱ne̱ anyɔ‑ɔ e̱ le̱e̱ a mo̱nꞌ le̱e̱ da ke̱maŋtaŋ‑nɔ.

ref: Are students better than their teacher? But when they are fully trained, they will be like their teacher.
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ sukuu kigyi mo̱ŋ de kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ kaapo̱ atɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ mo̱ŋ ta fe̱yɛ mò̱ ɔkaapo̱po̱, amɔ ɔɔ lo̱we̱ mò̱ sukuu.

ref: You can see the speck in your friend's eye, but you don't notice the log in your own eye.
text: Bɛɛɛ e̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ de̱e̱re̱ kakpafɛɛ ne̱ ka dɔŋ fo̱ bɛɛko̱ ke̱katɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ fo̱ mo̱ŋ ke̱e̱ ke̱be̱ŋtiŋ ne̱ ke̱ dɔŋ fo̱ lee si‑o?

ref: How can you say, “My friend, let me take the speck out of your eye,” when you don't see the log in your own eye? You show-offs! First, get the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye. A Tree and Its Fruit (Matthew
text: Bɛɛɛ e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ waa na fo̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ, sa a n lee kakpafɛɛ ne̱ ka dɔŋ fo̱ ke̱katɔ se̱‑ɔ,’ na fo̱ fe̱raa fo̱ mo̱ŋ ke̱e̱ ke̱be̱ŋtiŋ ne̱ ke̱ dɔŋ fo̱ lee si‑o? Nnɔ ŋnyɔ ŋnyɔ wuye, lee ke̱be̱ŋtiŋ ne̱ ke̱ dɔŋ fo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ke̱katɔ se̱‑ɔ pwɛɛ na fo̱ dɛɛ taare̱ ŋu dame̱naŋsɛ lee kakpafɛɛ ne̱ ka dɔŋ fo̱ bɛɛko̱ lee si‑o. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ mo̱ne̱ ma po̱rɔ mo̱ne̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana. Amaa mo̱nꞌ lee e̱bɔye̱ le̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ pwɛɛ na mo̱nꞌ dɛɛ taare̱ kya mo̱ne̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana‑rɔ na bo̱ lee bamo̱ lee.” Kiyii na ke̱mo̱ agyi (Matiyo

ref: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu be̱e̱ ɔ yɛ, “Kiyii timaa maa swɛɛ agyi bɔye̱, ne̱ kiyii ne̱ ke̱ mo̱ŋ de alaŋfiya‑o mɔ maa swɛɛ agyi timaa.

ref: You can tell what a tree is like by the fruit it produces. You cannot pick figs or grapes from thornbushes.
text: Kiyii agyi ne̱ bo̱ de ba pini kiyii suyo ne̱ ke̱ gye̱‑ɔ. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ le̱ŋkre̱ŋ maa swɛɛ kimaŋgoo agyi, ne̱ kuruŋbree mɔ maa swɛɛ kukuti agyi.

ref: Good people do good things because of the good in their hearts, but bad people do bad things because of the evil in their hearts. Your words show what is in your heart. Two Builders (Matthew
text: Se̱sɛ timaa kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ ne̱ atɔ timaa e̱ le̱e̱, ne̱ atɔ bɔye̱ e̱ le̱e̱ se̱sɛ bɔye̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ bo̱ se̱sɛ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ a le̱e̱ mò̱ kanɔ‑rɔ.” Lɔŋ apwɛɛpo̱ anyɔ‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: Why do you keep on saying that I am your Lord, when you refuse to do what I say?
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya se̱ bise fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ te̱e̱ mo̱ ɛ ‘Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, mo̱ nyaŋpe̱,’ na mo̱ne̱ mɔ mo̱ne̱ maa waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱?

ref: But anyone who hears what I say and doesn't obey me is like someone whose house wasn't built on solid rock. As soon as the river rushed against that house, it was smashed to pieces!
text: Amaa ɔko̱ ne̱ o nu mo̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱‑ɔ mò̱ maa gya amo̱ se̱‑ɔ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ pwɛɛ mò̱ lɔŋ ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ kwii talii‑o na ɔ waa keŋ‑o. Ne̱ nkyu a twe̱e̱ kufwiiri gyaa bo̱ to̱waa lɔŋ amo̱. Ne̱ lɔŋ amo̱ a bwee le̱e̱ da wuuruum. Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ saase̱bɛɛ.”

ref: I have officers who give orders to me, and I have soldiers who take orders from me. I can say to one of them, ‘Go!’ and he goes. I can say to another, ‘Come!’ and he comes. I can say to my servant, ‘Do this!’ and he will do it.”
text: Ŋ gbaa mo̱ ne̱ n gye̱ se̱sɛ laŋgyaŋ, ne̱ n de abre̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ kyɔ mo̱‑ɔ, de asoogyaa awuye mo̱ kaase̱, ne̱ mo̱ ya tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ ɔko̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Nare̱,’ ɔ yɔ ne̱e̱, ne̱ mo̱ ya tɔwe̱ gywii ɔko̱ mɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ba,’ puri amo̱‑rɔ amɔ ɔ ba. Ne̱ mo̱ ya tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Waa ke̱mo̱‑ɔ,’ amɔ ɔ waa.”

ref: He loves our nation and even built us a synagogue.”
text: Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ kpa ane̱ ase̱sɛ ase̱ŋ, ne̱ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ a be̱e̱ pwɛɛ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ sa ane̱.”

ref: When the Lord saw the woman, he felt sorry for her and said, “Don't cry!”
text: Amɔ Yeesuu ke̱de̱ŋ ŋu ɔkye̱e̱ amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱ ase̱ŋ a waa mò̱ e̱wɛɛ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ ma lɛɛ su.”

ref: The leaders went to Jesus and begged him to do something. They said, “This man deserves your help!
text: Ne̱ baa ba Yeesuu ase̱ bo̱ ko̱re̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ ba. Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Hare̱e̱ ɔnyare̱ amo̱ a mo̱ŋ gye̱ Isireeliinyi‑o gbaa ooo, a bware fe̱yɛ fo̱ bo̱ kya mò̱‑rɔ.

ref: Simon answered, “I suppose it would be the one who had owed more and didn't have to pay it back.” “You are right,” Jesus said.
text: Ne̱ Simɔŋ Farisiinyi‑o a be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ a n gyi fe̱yɛ mò̱ ne̱ mò̱ ko̱kɔ kyɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ taa bo̱ ke mò̱‑ɔ.” Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “A gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ.”

ref: And when he heard about Jesus, he sent some Jewish leaders to ask him to come and heal the servant.
text: Soogyaa amo̱ a nu Yeesuu si maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ oo suŋ Yudaa awuye abre̱sɛ ko̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ya ko̱re̱ Yeesuu na ɔ bo̱ kya mò̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱‑ɔ sa mò̱.

ref: So Jesus went with them. When Jesus wasn't far from the house, the officer sent some friends to tell him, “Lord, don't go to any trouble for me! I am not good enough for you to come into my house.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ bamo̱ aa Yeesuu a taa bo̱ yɔ. Bamo̱ a kpa a bo̱ fo̱ lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ soogyaa‑o a suŋ mò̱ nyare̱‑ana fe̱yɛ bo̱ ya gyaŋŋaa Yeesuu. Ne̱ baa ya tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, fo̱ ma kpo̱ne̱ fo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ba mo̱ ase̱. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ŋ gye̱ se̱sɛ timaa ne̱ m bware fe̱yɛ fo̱ ba mo̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ.

ref: And I am certainly not worthy to come to you. Just say the word, and my servant will get well.
text: Ne̱ mo̱‑ɔ m be̱e̱ mo̱ŋ fo̱ fe̱yɛ m ba fo̱ ase̱ mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ fo̱ ya de̱ŋ sa kanɔ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ gbaa, mo̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱‑ɔ e̱ kpaare̱.

ref: As they came near the gate of the town, they saw people carrying out the body of a widow's only son. Many people from the town were walking along with her.
text: Saŋ ne̱ ba kpa a bo̱ lwee maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ na ase̱sɛ ko̱ so̱ kifuniŋ, na ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ gya se̱ ba le̱e̱ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ ba yɔ purekpa. Asaa kpenlekye̱e̱ ko̱ mɔ kagyinyaŋsɛɛ wo̱re̱ kpeŋ ne̱ o de‑o ya wu, ne̱ bo̱ de mò̱ a bo̱ ya pure, ne̱ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ ase̱sɛ gya se̱ ane̱ŋ.

ref: Jesus went over and touched the stretcher on which the people were carrying the dead boy. They stopped, and Jesus said, “Young man, get up!”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a nare̱ ya dabo̱rɔ kifuniŋ‑o, ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ so̱‑ɔ a sii ye̱re̱. Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Ke̱yaafɔre̱, ko̱so̱.”

ref: The boy sat up and began to speak. Jesus then gave him back to his mother.
text: Ne̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱ ne̱ oo wu‑o a ko̱so̱ kye̱na, ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa. Ne̱ Yeesuu a taa mò̱ sa mò̱ nyi.

ref: Everyone was frightened and praised God. They said, “A great prophet is here with us! God has come to his people.”
text: Ne̱ kufu a nya bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ, ne̱ baa kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱. Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ dabe̱ ko̱ a ba ane̱ ase̱. Wuribware̱ a ba a ɔ bo̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ mò̱ ase̱sɛ.”

ref: News about Jesus spread all over Judea and everywhere else in that part of the country. John the Baptist (Matthew
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu ase̱ŋ a yɔ e̱maŋ ne̱ e̱ maa mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ na ifuri amo̱ pɛɛɛ se̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱‑ɔ a suŋ mbɔɔ Yeesuu ase̱‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: John's followers told John everything that was being said about Jesus. So he sent two of them to ask the Lord, “Are you the one we should be looking for? Or must we wait for someone else?”
text: Ne̱ Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱ a agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ atɔ ne̱ Yeesuu a waa‑ɔ pɛɛɛ ase̱ŋ gywii Yohanee. Ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ bamo̱ abɛɛ anyɔ suŋ bamo̱ Yeesuu ase̱ na bo̱ ya bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ e̱ gye̱ Kristoo ne̱ Yohanee yɛ ɔ ba a ɔ bo̱ le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱‑ɔ ɔɔɔ, bɛɛɛ se̱sɛ baŋbaŋ ko̱ be̱e̱ ɔ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ane̱ ke̱e̱ mò̱ kpa aaa?”

ref: When these messengers came to Jesus, they said, “John the Baptist sent us to ask, ‘Are you the one we should be looking for? Or are we supposed to wait for someone else?’ ”
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa bamo̱ a yɔ Yeesuu ase̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱‑ɔ ya suŋ ane̱ na ane̱ bo̱ bise fo̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ e̱ gye̱ Kristoo ne̱ ɔ yɛ ɔ ba a ɔ bo̱ le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱‑ɔ ɔɔɔ, bɛɛɛ se̱sɛ baŋbaŋ ko̱ be̱e̱ ɔ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ane̱ ke̱e̱ mò̱ kpa aaa?”

ref: At that time Jesus was healing many people who were sick or in pain or were troubled by evil spirits, and he was giving sight to a lot of blind people.
text: Saŋ amo̱ mɔ‑ɔ, Yeesuu a kyɔ kya alɔpo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ na bamo̱ ne̱ ibrisi te bamo̱ se̱‑ɔ. Ate̱napo̱ pɛɛɛ, oo buŋŋi bamo̱ akatɔ.

ref: Jesus said to the messengers sent by John, “Go and tell John what you have seen and heard. Blind people are now able to see, and the lame can walk. People who have leprosy are being healed, and the deaf can now hear. The dead are raised to life, and the poor are hearing the good news.
text: To, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱ŋŋaa Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱ a mbɔɔ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ kiŋŋi nare̱ ya tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a nu mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱, na ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a ŋu mo̱ e̱ waa‑ɔ gywii Yohanee. Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ ko̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ bo̱ le̱e̱ mò̱ ne̱ ɔ ba a ɔ bo̱ ba‑ɔ kuŋu si ase̱ŋ‑ɔ, amo̱ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ, mbe̱yɔmɔ ate̱napo̱ ke̱e̱, ne̱ agyabɔɔ naa, ne̱ abwatɔpo̱ ke̱bwatɔ a lo̱we̱, ne̱ akpawu i nu ase̱ŋ, ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa wu‑o a kyiŋŋi, ne̱ atiripo̱ i nu ase̱ŋ timaa‑o.

ref: God will bless everyone who doesn't reject me because of what I do.”
text: Ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ maa yɔwe̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱ mo̱ gyi‑o e̱ nya ŋyure.”

ref: What kind of man did you really go out to see? Was he someone dressed in fine clothes? People who wear expensive clothes and live in luxury are in the king's palace.
text: Nte̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a le̱e̱ a mo̱nꞌ ya de̱e̱re̱? Se̱sɛ ne̱ o buŋ waagya timaa‑o ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a yɔ a mo̱nꞌ ya de̱e̱re̱ aaa? Ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ buŋ ane̱ŋ a atɔ ne̱ bamo̱ kakye̱na‑rɔ bo̱ kɔne̱‑ɔ gye̱ atɔ awuye ne̱ bo̱ te e̱lɔŋ dame̱naŋsɛ dabe̱ dabe̱‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: What then did you go out to see? Was he a prophet? He certainly was! I tell you that he was more than a prophet.
text: Mo̱nꞌ tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱, nsɛ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a yɔ a mo̱nꞌ ya de̱e̱re̱? Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ aaa? Kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱e̱, amaa n gye̱ Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ‑ɔ wo̱re̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a ya ŋu. Mo̱ne̱ a ŋu ɔko̱ na ɔ kyɔ bo̱ tii si.

ref: In the Scriptures, God calls John his messenger and says, “I am sending my messenger ahead of you to get things ready for you.”
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱‑ɔ ase̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ o gywii Kristoo fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ i suŋ mo̱ kabɔɔ a m bo̱ gye̱ fo̱ ŋkpɛɛ, na ɔ ya lɔŋŋɔ kpa bo̱ ye̱ra fo̱.’ ”

ref: No one ever born on this earth is greater than John. But whoever is least important in God's kingdom is greater than John.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ bo̱ tii si fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ se̱sɛ ko̱ mo̱ŋ kye̱na swe̱e̱re̱ mɔ se̱ ne̱ ɔ kyɔ Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱‑ɔ, amaa mò̱ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ kagyingyii Wuribware̱ se̱ a kuwure‑o‑ro kyɔ Yohanee.”

ref: Everyone had been listening to John. Even the tax collectors had obeyed God and had done what was right by letting John baptize them.
text: Ase̱sɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ, fɔŋfɔŋ leŋpoo akɔɔre̱po̱‑ɔ, a nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱‑ɔ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ bamo̱ akatɔ a gyi, ne̱ bo̱ yɛ Wuribware̱ bware. Ase̱sɛ mɔ ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ gye̱ bamo̱ ne̱ Yohanee a bɔ asuu‑o ne̱e̱.

ref: But the Pharisees and the experts in the Law of Moses refused to obey God and be baptized by John.
text: Amaa Farisii awuye‑o na Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bamo̱ fe̱raa baa kine kpa ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ bo̱rɔ se̱ a ɔ bo̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa kine fe̱yɛ Yohanee bɔ bamo̱ asuu‑o.

ref: Jesus went on to say: What are you people like? What kind of people are you?
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya se̱ ɛ, “Mbe̱yɔmɔ, nte̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ nya a m bo̱ biri ndɔɔ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ ne̱e̱? E̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ bo̱ du?

ref: You are like children sitting in the market and shouting to each other, “We played the flute, but you would not dance! We sang a funeral song, but you would not cry!”
text: Bo̱ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ŋyaagyi a te ba soori ko̱lɔŋde̱ se̱, ne̱ bamo̱ katuŋ ko̱ e̱ fɛɛ‑rɔ ba kyo̱ŋwe̱ katuŋ ko̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ane̱ a da ko̱ko̱fɔ‑rɔ akɔye̱ sa mo̱ne̱, amaa mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ kyaa ooo. Ne̱ ane̱ a be̱e̱ waa kale, amaa mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ su ooo.’

ref: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “John has a demon in him!”
text: To, Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱‑ɔ a ba‑ɔ, ɔɔ kra ako̱ŋ, ne̱ oo kisi nta kunuu, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ yɛ, ‘Ɔbɔnsam te mò̱ se̱.’

ref: But because the Son of Man goes around eating and drinking, you say, “Jesus eats and drinks too much! He is even a friend of tax collectors and sinners.”
text: Amaa mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, a ba, ne̱ mo̱ a gyi nuu fe̱raa, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ be̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ yɛ, ‘Mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱ ɔnyare̱ amo̱, ɔ gye̱ oteesekpapo̱, na ɔtanuupo̱, na leŋpoo akɔɔre̱po̱ bamo̱ nyare̱, na e̱bɔye̱ awaapo̱ bamo̱ nyare̱ ne̱e̱.’

ref: A Pharisee invited Jesus to have dinner with him. So Jesus went to the Pharisee's home and got ready to eat.
text: Ne̱ Farisii awuye‑o ɔko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Simɔŋ‑ɔ a nyiŋŋi Yeesuu fe̱yɛ ɔ ba mò̱ aye̱ bo̱ gyi ateese. Ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ mfe̱ŋ ya kye̱na a o gyi.

ref: Then she came and stood behind Jesus. She cried and started washing his feet with her tears and drying them with her hair. The woman kissed his feet and poured the perfume on them.
text: Amo̱ a gye̱ Yudaa awuye mbraa‑ɔ se̱, ne̱ bo̱ da ba gyi. Ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ a bo̱ ye̱re̱ Yeesuu ayaa ase̱. Ne̱ o su o de mò̱ akyukyu ɔ pɔ mò̱ ayaapapaa. Ne̱ ŋkee ɔɔ ba mò̱ ipwii bo̱ kpɛɛ akyukyu‑o Yeesuu ayaapapaa‑ɔ se̱, ne̱ ŋkee ɔɔ kyo̱kywe̱e̱ amo̱ se̱, ne̱ oo twiiri laŋpe̱ŋtaa‑ɔ bo̱ we̱e̱ se̱.

ref: The Pharisee who had invited Jesus saw this and said to himself, “If this man really were a prophet, he would know what kind of woman is touching him! He would know that she is a sinner.”
text: Ne̱ Farisiinyi ne̱ Yeesuu te mò̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ a ŋu ane̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ fa mò̱ mfɛɛre̱‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔnyare̱ mɔ dɛɛ ɔ gye̱ Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱e̱, weetee o ŋu ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱ mɔ ne̱ ɔ dabo̱rɔ mò̱‑ɔ gye̱‑ɔ. Na o ŋu ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱ mɔ te ɔ waa e̱bɔye̱‑ɔ.”

ref: Jesus said to the Pharisee, “Simon, I have something to say to you.” “Teacher, what is it?” Simon replied.
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Simɔŋ, n de ko̱tɔko̱ a n tɔwe̱ gywii fo̱.” Ne̱ Simɔŋ yɛ, “Yooo, ɔkaapo̱po̱, tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱.”

ref: But Jesus told the woman, “Because of your faith, you are now saved. May God give you peace!”
text: Amaa Yeesuu a be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ gywii ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ a kɔɔre̱ mo̱ gyi‑o si‑o, Wuribware̱ a mo̱rɔwe̱ fo̱. To, nare̱ dame̱naŋsɛ.”

ref: Since neither of them could pay him back, the moneylender said that they didn't have to pay him anything. Which one of them will like him more?”
text: Bamo̱ anyɔ‑ɔ mɔ pɛɛɛ mo̱ŋ taare̱ ko̱kɔ‑ɔ ka, amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ ɔɔ taa bo̱ ke bamo̱. To, bamo̱ abɛɛ anyɔ mɔ‑rɔ, nsɛ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ mò̱ akatɔ i gyi atɔ wuye amo̱ se̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ ne̱e̱?”

ref: He turned toward the woman and said to Simon, “Have you noticed this woman? When I came into your home, you didn't give me any water so I could wash my feet. But she has washed my feet with her tears and dried them with her hair.
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa ne̱ oo buruwaa de̱e̱re̱ ɔkye̱e̱ amo̱ a keri‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii Simɔŋ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ a ŋu ɔkye̱e̱ mɔ aaa? Mo̱ a ba fo̱ aye̱‑ɔ, fo̱ mo̱ŋ kra mo̱ nɛɛnɛɛ, fe̱yɛ fo̱ mo̱ŋ sa mo̱ nkyu na m bo̱ fwe̱e̱ ayaapapaa se̱, amaa ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ fe̱raa a ba mo̱ akyukyu bo̱ fwe̱e̱ mo̱ ayaapapaa se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ ba mò̱ ipwii bo̱ kpɛɛ amo̱ se̱.

ref: You didn't greet me with a kiss, but from the time I came in, she has not stopped kissing my feet.
text: Fo̱ mo̱ŋ kyo̱kywe̱e̱ mo̱ ke̱daabɔŋ bo̱ sa mo̱ aŋsɛ na ke̱ba, amaa ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ fe̱raa mo̱ŋ yɔwe̱ mo̱ ayaapapaa se̱ ko̱kyo̱kywe̱e̱ le̱e̱ hare̱e̱ saŋ ne̱ mo̱ a ba mfe̱e̱‑ɔ.

ref: You didn't even pour olive oil on my head, but she has poured expensive perfume on my feet.
text: Fo̱ mo̱ŋ kyiine mo̱ kuŋu‑ro mfɔ, amaa mò̱ fe̱raa a twiiri laŋpe̱ŋtaa we̱e̱ mo̱ ayaapapaa se̱.

ref: So I tell you that all her sins are forgiven, and that is why she has shown great love. But anyone who has been forgiven for only a little will show only a little love.”
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii fo̱ fe̱yɛ, hare̱e̱ ɔkye̱e̱ mɔ e̱bɔye̱ a kyɔ gbaa ooo, Wuribware̱ a taa e̱mo̱ pɛɛɛ bo̱ ke mò̱. Ke̱kpa de̱maŋte̱ ne̱ ɔ kpa mo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a taa mò̱ e̱bɔye̱‑ɔ bo̱ ke mò̱. Amaa ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ waa e̱bɔye̱ kafwe̱e̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a taa bo̱ ke mò̱‑ɔ e̱ kpa Wuribware̱ kafwe̱e̱ ne̱e̱.”

ref: Then Jesus said to the woman, “Your sins are forgiven.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ a taa fo̱ e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ ke fo̱.”

ref: The seeds that fell on rocky ground are the people who gladly hear the message and accept it. But they don't have deep roots, and they believe only for a little while. As soon as life gets hard, they give up.
text: Ayaabraagyi ne̱ aa le̱e̱ da kagyaase̱‑ɔ mɔ ye̱re̱ bo̱ sa ase̱sɛ ne̱ ba nu abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ a waa bamo̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ kɔne̱‑ɔ. Amaa bamo̱ akatɔ mo̱ŋ pee si‑o si‑o, Ɔbɔnsam ya kyɔ bamo̱‑rɔ ke̱e̱ kafwe̱e̱, amɔ baa kyɔ yɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱ ke̱gya.

ref: Other seeds fell on rocky ground and started growing. But the plants did not have enough water and soon dried up.
text: Ne̱ yaabraa amo̱ ako̱ a le̱e̱ da kagyaase̱. A bo̱ kwɛɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ aa wu, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ swe̱e̱re̱ amo̱ mfe̱ŋ mo̱ŋ de yuri.

ref: So he answered: I have explained the secrets about God's kingdom to you. But for others I use stories, so they will look, but not see, and they will hear, but not understand. Jesus Explains the Story about a Farmer (Matthew
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ wo̱re̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kuwure‑o‑ro du‑o mo̱ i gywii, amaa bamo̱ ne̱ ba saŋ‑ɔ fe̱raa itee‑ro na akpare̱‑rɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ ke̱mo̱ ase̱ŋ a m bo̱ gywii bamo̱, ‘na bo̱ de̱e̱re̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ, na bo̱ fa amo̱ mfɛɛre̱ kpo̱ne̱, na bo̱ nu ke̱mo̱ ase̱ŋ kpo̱ra‑rɔ, na bo̱ ma nu kaase̱.’ ” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kaapo̱ ɔdɔɔpo̱ kitee kaase̱‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: There is nothing hidden that will not be found. There is no secret that will not be well known.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ sɛye̱ maa kwe̱e̱rɔ. Ke̱tɔ ke̱maa na ase̱ŋ ke̱maa e̱ le̱e̱ ifuri na ɔke̱maa ŋu.

ref: and while they were sailing across, he went to sleep. Suddenly a storm struck the lake, and the boat started sinking. They were in danger.
text: Bamo̱ a pare̱ ba yɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu a di idi ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ. Ne̱ afwii dabe̱ ko̱ a ko̱so̱ a kya nkyu‑o si mfe̱ŋ, ne̱ alaŋkpare̱ a le̱e̱ a da nkyu bo̱ waa bamo̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔ kpa a ɔ bo̱rɔ.

ref: The seeds that fell among the thornbushes are also people who hear the message. But they are so eager for riches and pleasures that they never produce anything.
text: Amo̱ ne̱ aa le̱e̱ da e̱fa‑rɔ‑ɔ mɔ ye̱re̱ bo̱ sa bamo̱ ne̱ ba nu abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ atɔ tɔɔraasɛ na katanne̱kpa na atɔ biribiri ke̱kpa ya lwee bamo̱ mfɛɛre̱‑rɔ, amɔ aa waa ko̱kyɔ kyo̱ŋ abware̱se̱ŋ ne̱ a bo̱ bamo̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ. Amɔ bamo̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi‑o maa waa bamo̱ sɛye̱.

ref: Those seeds that fell on good ground are the people who listen to the message and keep it in good and honest hearts. They last and produce a harvest. Light (Mark
text: Ŋkee ayaabraagyi ne̱ aa le̱e̱ da swe̱e̱re̱ timaa si, ne̱ aa ko̱so̱ daŋ bo̱ ko̱we̱ waa aŋu adabe̱ adabe̱‑ɔ mɔ ye̱re̱ bo̱ sa ase̱sɛ timaa ne̱ ba nu ase̱ŋ timaa‑o, ne̱ ba kɔɔre̱ ba gyi, ne̱ ba le̱ŋ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ gya amo̱ se̱ nɛɛnɛɛ, ne̱ ba waa itimaa saŋ ke̱maa‑ɔ.” Mo̱nꞌ nu ase̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ dame̱naŋsɛ (Maak

ref: Then Jesus asked the disciples, “Don't you have any faith?” But they were frightened and amazed. They said to each other, “Who is this? He can give orders to the wind and the waves, and they obey him!” A Man with Demons in Him (Matthew
text: Ŋkee ne̱ oo bise mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ maa kɔɔre̱ mo̱ mo̱ne̱ i gyi‑o ne̱e̱?” Ne̱ kufu a nya bamo̱, ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ bamo̱, ne̱ ba ŋwiini ba gywii abɛɛ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ se̱sɛ e̱ gye̱ ɔnyare̱ mɔ? Mò̱ ya sa kanɔ, afwii na alaŋkpare̱ gya kamo̱ se̱ ne̱e̱.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ibrisi te mò̱ se̱‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: No one lights a lamp and puts it under a bowl or under a bed. A lamp is always put on a lampstand, so people who come into a house will see the light.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya se̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔko̱ maa kyaŋŋe̱ mò̱ fetiri si na ɔ ko̱ŋ be̱ra kike bo̱ buŋ si, bɛɛɛ ɔ taa bo̱ kwe̱e̱rɔ mpaa kaase̱. Kabuno ne̱ ɔ taa a ɔ bo̱ ye̱ra, na ɔ laŋŋe̱‑rɔ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ase̱sɛ i lwee lɔŋ‑nɔ ne̱ ba nya bo̱ ŋu‑ro‑o.

ref: But Jesus answered, “Someone touched me, because I felt power going out from me.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Amaa mo̱ a pini fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ a dabo̱rɔ mo̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ e̱le̱ŋ a le̱e̱ mo̱‑rɔ kya ɔko̱.”

ref: When Jesus heard this, he told Jairus, “Don't worry! Have faith, and your daughter will get well.”
text: Amaa Yeesuu a nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii Yayerus fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ ma sa a fo̱ duŋ te̱ŋ fo̱, fo̱ fe̱raa, fo̱ ya kɔɔre̱ mo̱ gyi, ɔ kpaare̱.”

ref: The people went out to see what had happened, and when they came to Jesus, they also found the man. The demons had gone out of him, and he was sitting there at the feet of Jesus. He had clothes on and was in his right mind. But the people were terrified.
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ a le̱e̱ a bo̱ bo̱ ke̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ kaa ba‑ɔ. Ne̱ baa ba Yeesuu ase̱ bo̱ to̱ fe̱yɛ ɔnyare̱ ne̱ ibrisi dɛɛ i te mò̱ se̱‑ɔ te Yeesuu ayaa‑rɔ o buŋ atɔ, ne̱ ŋkee fe̱raa mò̱ mfɛɛre̱ du mò̱ kyɛɛkyɛɛ. Ne̱ kufu a nya bamo̱ pɛɛɛ.

ref: Jesus' mother and brothers went to see him, but because of the crowd they could not get near him.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu mò̱ nyi na mò̱ tire‑ana a ba mò̱ ase̱ a bo̱ bo̱ ŋu mò̱. Amaa bo̱ le̱e̱ ase̱sɛ ko̱kyɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ bo̱ mo̱ŋ nya kpa yɔ mò̱ ase̱.

ref: Just then the man in charge of the synagogue came and knelt down in front of Jesus. His name was Jairus, and he begged Jesus to come to his home
text: Ne̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ ɔko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Yayerus‑o a ba Yeesuu ase̱ mfe̱ŋ bo̱ kpuni aŋurii Yeesuu ayaa‑rɔ ko̱re̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ sa a bo̱ yɔ mò̱ aye̱,

ref: One day, Jesus and his disciples got into a boat, and he said, “Let's cross the lake.” They started out,
text: Kake ŋko̱ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ te̱ŋ ke̱pare̱ dabe̱‑ɔ‑rɔ yɔ nno̱ŋ a bo̱ŋbe̱‑ɔ se̱.” Ne̱ baa lwee ko̱re̱e̱‑rɔ me̱raa se̱ ba yɔ.

ref: So they went to Jesus and woke him up, “Master, Master! We are about to drown!” Jesus got up and ordered the wind and waves to stop. They obeyed, and everything was calm.
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa ne̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a ya kyiŋŋi mò̱, ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱, ane̱ nyaŋpe̱, ane̱ e̱ kpa a ane̱ wu.” Ne̱ Yeesuu a ko̱so̱ sa kanɔ fe̱yɛ afwii‑o na alaŋkpare̱‑ɔ yɔwe̱, ne̱ aa yɔwe̱, ne̱ ke̱tɔ a waa surumm.

ref: When the men taking care of the pigs saw this, they ran to spread the news in the town and on the farms.
text: Bamo̱ ne̱ ba de̱e̱re̱ aprako amo̱ se̱‑ɔ a ŋu ke̱tɔ ne̱ kaa waa‑ɔ, ne̱ baa ŋwɛɛnaŋ yɔ maŋ‑nɔ na mmaŋgyii‑ro na adɔɔ‑rɔ ya da ase̱ŋ ne̱ aa ba‑ɔ kiboŋboŋ.

ref: Jesus asked the man, “What is your name?” He answered, “My name is Lots.” He said this because there were “lots” of demons in him.
text: To, ne̱ Yeesuu a bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ ke̱nyare̱ e̱ gye̱ e̱me̱ne̱?” Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ gye̱ Ane̱-kyɔ-mfe̱e̱”, a kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ ibrisi bwe̱e̱tɔ te mò̱ se̱.

ref: A large herd of pigs was feeding there on the hillside. So the demons begged Jesus to let them go into the pigs, and Jesus let them go.
text: Saŋ amo̱ mɔ aprako bwe̱e̱tɔ ko̱ ye̱re̱ kabe̱e̱gyi ko̱ se̱ mfe̱ŋ ba gyi. Ne̱ ibrisi‑o a ko̱re̱ Yeesuu fe̱yɛ ɔ sa a bo̱ le̱e̱ ɔnyare̱ amo̱ se̱ ya kye̱na aprako amo̱ se̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ sa e̱mo̱ kpa.

ref: Then the demons left the man and went into the pigs. The whole herd rushed down the steep bank into the lake and drowned.
text: Ne̱ e̱ ya ko̱so̱ ɔnyare̱ amo̱ se̱ ya kye̱na aprako‑o si, ne̱ aprako de̱maŋte̱ amo̱ a ŋwɛɛnaŋ kpo̱ro̱we̱ kabe̱e̱gyi‑o si ya le̱e̱ da nkyu‑ro wu wu.

ref: Then all who had seen the man healed told about it.
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa ŋu ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔnyare̱ amo̱ a kpaare̱‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ amo̱ ase̱ŋ gywii bamo̱ ne̱ baa sii kamɛɛ ba‑ɔ.

ref: Everyone from around Gerasa begged Jesus to leave, because they were so frightened. When Jesus got into the boat to start back,
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ swe̱e̱re̱ amo̱ se̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a tɔwe̱ gywii Yeesuu fe̱yɛ ɔ le̱e̱ bamo̱ ifuri si, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kufu de bamo̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ lwee ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ ɔ kpa a o kiŋŋi yɔ.

ref: the man who had been healed begged to go with him. But Jesus sent him off and said,
text: Ne̱ ɔnyare̱ ne̱ Yeesuu a gya ibrisi bo̱ ko̱so̱ mò̱ se̱‑ɔ a ko̱re̱ Yeesuu fe̱yɛ ɔ sa a mò̱ aa mò̱ a yɔ. Amaa Yeesuu a kiŋŋaa mò̱ ne̱e̱, tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ,

ref: “Go back home and tell everyone how much God has done for you.” The man then went all over town, telling everything that Jesus had done for him. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Matthew
text: “Kiŋŋi nare̱ pe̱ ya tɔwe̱ atɔ dabe̱ dabe̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a waa sa fo̱‑ɔ gywii fo̱ aye̱ awuye.” Ne̱ ɔnyare̱ amo̱ a kiŋŋi yɔ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ ya tɔwe̱ atɔ dabe̱ dabe̱ ne̱ Yeesuu a waa sa mò̱‑ɔ ase̱ŋ gywii mfe̱ŋ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kyiŋŋi kakye̱e̱sɛɛ ko̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ kya ɔkye̱e̱ ko̱‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: In the crowd was a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years. She had spent everything she had on doctors, but none of them could make her well.
text: Amɔ ɔkye̱e̱ ko̱ mɔ bo̱ bamo̱‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔ lɔ ke̱kye̱e̱tɔ bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kiferi ke̱maa‑rɔ ɔ taare̱ bwiino waa iluwi kanɔ ŋko̱. Waa nsu kudu ŋnyɔ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ a lɔ ane̱ŋ a ko̱lɔ‑ɔ. Ɔko̱ mɔ mo̱ŋ taare̱ kya mò̱ ke̱mo̱.

ref: Everyone had been waiting for Jesus, and when he came back, a crowd was there to welcome him.
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kiŋŋi ba bo̱ŋbe̱ ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ mò̱ se̱ yɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ ye̱re̱ bo̱ gywii mò̱‑ɔ a sa mò̱ aŋsɛ na ke̱ba.

ref: because his twelve-year-old child was dying. She was his only daughter. While Jesus was on his way, people were crowding all around him.
text: a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ kagyikye̱e̱sɛɛ ko̱ ne̱ oo gyi nsu kudu ŋnyɔ‑ɔ e̱ lɔ ɔ kpa a o wu. Kakye̱e̱sɛɛ‑ɔ wo̱re̱ kpeŋ e̱ gye̱ mò̱ gyi. Yeesuu a yɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ ase̱sɛ a kpɔwe̱ miri bo̱ gya mò̱ se̱.

ref: As soon as she came up behind Jesus and barely touched his clothes, her bleeding stopped.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ oo lwee ase̱sɛ amo̱‑rɔ yɔ Yeesuu kamɛɛ ya dabo̱rɔ mò̱ waagya kanɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ kpaare̱ puri amo̱‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ.

ref: “Who touched me?” Jesus asked. While everyone was denying it, Peter said, “Master, people are crowding all around and pushing you from every side.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a bise fe̱yɛ, “Nsɛ ya dabo̱rɔ mo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱?” Ɔke̱maa a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ n gye̱ mò̱ ne̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ Peetroo yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ fo̱ i bise ane̱ŋ, na ase̱sɛ de̱maŋte̱ mɔ a muruwaa fo̱?”

ref: The woman knew that she could not hide, so she came trembling and knelt down in front of Jesus. She told everyone why she had touched him and that she had been healed at once.
text: Ɔkye̱e̱ amo̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ ɔ maa taare̱ a ɔ taa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ waa‑ɔ a ɔ bo̱ kwe̱e̱rɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ ba Yeesuu ase̱ na ɔ yo̱ŋ, ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ kpuni aŋurii mò̱ ayaa‑rɔ. Ase̱sɛ amo̱‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii Yeesuu ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ ɔɔ dabo̱rɔ mò̱‑ɔ na ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ kpaare̱ puri amo̱‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ.

ref: Jesus said to the woman, “You are now well because of your faith. May God give you peace!”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Ɔpe̱kye̱e̱, bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ a kɔɔre̱ mo̱ gyi‑o si‑o, fo̱ a kpaare̱. To, nare̱ dame̱naŋsɛ.”

ref: While Jesus was speaking, someone came from Jairus' home and said, “Your daughter has died! Why bother the teacher anymore?”
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yeesuu e̱ tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ a le̱e̱ Yayerus a lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ bo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii Yayerus fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ kakye̱e̱sɛɛ‑ɔ a wu, amo̱se̱‑ɔ fo̱ ma lɛɛ sa a ɔkaapo̱po̱‑ɔ kpo̱ne̱ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱.”

ref: Jesus went into the house, but he did not let anyone else go with him, except Peter, John, James, and the girl's father and mother.
text: Saŋ ne̱ baa fo̱ Yayerus a lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, Yeesuu mo̱ŋ sa a ɔko̱ lwee mfe̱ŋ ne̱ kakye̱e̱sɛɛ‑ɔ a wu ɔ da‑ɔ bo̱ tii mò̱ se̱, amɔ Peetroo na Yohanee na Yakubu na kakye̱e̱sɛɛ‑ɔ mò̱ se̱ na mò̱ nyi.

ref: Everyone was crying and weeping for the girl. But Jesus said, “The child isn't dead. She is just asleep.”
text: Bamo̱ ne̱ baa saŋ bo̱ bo̱ lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ de̱ŋ ba su kale ba sa kakye̱e̱sɛɛ‑ɔ ne̱e̱. Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ma su, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kakye̱e̱sɛɛ‑ɔ mo̱ŋ wu, oo di idi ne̱e̱.”

ref: The people laughed at him because they knew she was dead.
text: Mò̱ a tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ, baa mɔse̱ mò̱ ne̱e̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ nyi kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ kakye̱e̱sɛɛ‑ɔ a wu.

ref: Jesus took hold of the girl's hand and said, “Child, get up!”
text: To, ne̱ Yeesuu a kra kakye̱e̱sɛɛ ne̱ oo wu ɔ da‑ɔ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ gyi, kyiŋŋi ko̱so̱.”

ref: She came back to life and got right up. Jesus told them to give her something to eat.
text: Puri amo̱‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ oo kyiŋŋi ko̱so̱ ŋkpa na alaŋfiya‑ro. Ne̱ Yeesuu a sa kanɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ sa kakye̱e̱sɛɛ‑ɔ ko̱tɔko̱ a o gyi.

ref: Her parents were surprised, but Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened.
text: Ne̱ kakye̱e̱sɛɛ‑ɔ mò̱ se̱ na mò̱ nyi e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ bamo̱. Amaa Yeesuu yɛ bo̱ ma kaŋ tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ waa‑ɔ gywii ɔko̱.

ref: The apostles came back and told Jesus everything they had done. He then took them with him to the village of Bethsaida, where they could be alone.
text: To, ne̱ Yeesuu a agyase̱po̱ ne̱ oo suŋ bamo̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ e̱maŋ se̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ya tɔwe̱ Wuribware̱ a kuwure‑o‑ro ase̱ŋ‑ɔ a kiŋŋi ba bo̱ tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ baa waa‑ɔ gywii Yeesuu. Ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ mò̱ aa bamo̱ wo̱re̱ a yɔ maŋ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Bɛtɛsaye̱da‑ɔ se̱.

ref: Herod the ruler heard about all that was happening, and he was worried. Some people were saying John the Baptist had come back to life.
text: Saŋ ne̱ Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ Owure Hɛrɔd a nu atɔ mɔ pɛɛɛ ne̱ Yeesuu a waa‑ɔ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ oo turi, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ko̱ yɛ Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱‑ɔ ya be̱e̱ kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro ba.

ref: He told them, “Don't take anything with you! Don't take a walking stick or a traveling bag or food or money or even a change of clothes.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ a yɔ‑ɔ, ɔko̱ ma kaŋ taa ko̱tɔko̱. Mo̱ne̱ ma taa kayii nare̱se̱sɛ bɛɛɛ ko̱re̱e̱ bɛɛɛ ateese bɛɛɛ atanne̱ bɛɛɛ e̱kaare̱ ko̱ bo̱ tii e̱ko̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ ɔke̱maa mɔ buŋ‑o si.

ref: When you are welcomed into a home, stay there until you leave that town.
text: Mo̱ne̱ a yɔ‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ kye̱na lɔŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ so̱we̱ mò̱‑rɔ ne̱ ba kra mo̱ne̱ nɛɛnɛɛ‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ bo̱ fo̱ ŋke nsi ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ le̱e̱ maŋ amo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: Late in the afternoon the twelve apostles came to Jesus and said, “Send the crowd to the villages and farms around here. They need to find a place to stay and something to eat. There is nothing in this place. It's like a desert!”
text: Amɔ kyɔwe̱ a kpa a ɔ twɛɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱ kudu anyɔ‑ɔ a bo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mfe̱e̱ a gye̱ e̱fa‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, sa a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ yɔ mmaŋgyii na e̱maŋ ne̱ e̱ maa mfe̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ, na bo̱ ya kpa ateese gyi, na bo̱ buwi kpa bamo̱ e̱so̱we̱kpa.”

ref: While he was praying, his face changed, and his clothes became shining white.
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yeesuu e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱ akatɔ‑rɔ a kyurowi, ne̱ mò̱ atɔ buŋsɛ a buruwi pare̱pare̱pare̱ a to̱re̱ se̱.

ref: “Pay close attention to what I am telling you! The Son of Man will be handed over to his enemies.”
text: “Mo̱ne̱ ma kaŋ taŋ ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa a n tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ne̱‑ɔ se̱. Ba taa mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, a bo̱ waa swe̱e̱re̱ mɔ se̱ ayaale̱ŋpo̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ.”

ref: But a lot of people found out about this and followed him. Jesus welcomed them. He spoke about God's kingdom and healed everyone who was sick.
text: Ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ a nu mò̱ se̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa gya mò̱ se̱. Ne̱ Yeesuu a sa bamo̱ aŋsɛ na ke̱ba, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ Wuribware̱ a kuwure‑o‑ro ase̱ŋ gywii bamo̱, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ kya alɔpo̱ ne̱ baa ba mò̱ ase̱‑ɔ.

ref: “Don't stop him!” Jesus said. “Anyone who isn't against you is for you.” A Samaritan Village Refuses To Receive Jesus
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ aa mò̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ma lɛɛ gya mò̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ mo̱ne̱ ɔdo̱ŋ‑ɔ bo̱ mo̱ne̱ ke̱be̱gya ne̱e̱.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Samariya awuye a kine Yeesuu ko̱so̱we̱ bamo̱ aye̱‑ɔ

ref: About eight days later Jesus took Peter, John, and James with him and went up on a mountain to pray.
text: Waa ŋke mburuwa a kyo̱ŋ fe̱yɛ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ mɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ oo lee Peetroo na Yohanee na Yakubu, ne̱ mò̱ aa bamo̱ a yɔ ke̱be̱e̱ ko̱ se̱ na bo̱ ya ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱.

ref: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me, you will save it.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ kpa a ɔ nya mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ le̱e̱ lowi ase̱‑ɔ, lowi e̱ nya mò̱ nsu pɛɛɛ. Ɔko̱ mɔ ne̱ o kperi mo̱ se̱ na o wu‑o e̱ nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself or waste your life?
text: To̱nɔ mo̱ ne̱ se̱sɛ e̱ nya fe̱yɛ mò̱ a nya kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ atɔ pɛɛɛ, na bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ se̱ na ɔ paŋ kye̱nakpa Wuribware̱ ase̱? A mo̱ŋ de to̱nɔ.

ref: While Peter was still speaking, a shadow from a cloud passed over, and they were frightened as the cloud covered them.
text: Na Peetroo saŋ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa‑ɔ, ne̱ ko̱wo̱re̱ ko̱ kuyayu a le̱e̱ so̱so̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ bamo̱ se̱. Ne̱ kufu a nya Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ saŋ ne̱ ko̱wo̱re̱ amo̱ i buŋ bamo̱ se̱‑ɔ.

ref: From the cloud a voice spoke, “This is my chosen Son. Listen to what he says!”
text: Ne̱ bo̱re̱ ko̱ a le̱e̱ ko̱wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱ mo̱ gyi ne̱ mo̱ a lee‑o. Mo̱nꞌ nu mò̱ ase̱ŋ.”

ref: After the voice had spoken, Peter, John, and James saw only Jesus. For some time they kept quiet and did not say anything about what they had seen. Jesus Heals a Boy (Matthew
text: Saŋ ne̱ bo̱re̱ amo̱ a laatɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ aa saŋ Yeesuu wo̱re̱. Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a laa amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ tɔ. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ baa ŋu saŋ amo̱‑ɔ, bo̱ mo̱ŋ tɔwe̱ gywii ɔko̱, amɔ ke̱tɔ a bo̱ kyee‑o. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya kanyaŋsɛɛ ko̱ ne̱ ibrisi te mò̱ se̱‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: A demon often attacks him and makes him scream. It shakes him until he foams at the mouth, and it won't leave him until it has completely worn the boy out.
text: Ibrisi ko̱ te mò̱ se̱, ne̱ e̱mo̱ ya ba mò̱ se̱, amɔ ɔ fɛɛ‑rɔ, na e̱ twe̱e̱ mò̱ bo̱ da swe̱e̱re̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ a lɔ kigbuŋgbuŋ‑o, na ɔ twe̱e̱ mò̱ asare̱e̱ na ayaa, na mò̱ kanɔ‑rɔ e̱ le̱e̱ afwii. Saŋ ke̱maa mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ se̱ e̱ swaa swaa pwɛɛ na e̱ dɛɛ yuri mò̱.

ref: Jesus said to them, “You people are stubborn and don't have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you?” Then Jesus said to the man, “Bring your son to me.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ke̱se̱bɔrɔle̱ŋ awuye, mo̱ne̱ maa kɔɔre̱ mo̱ mo̱ne̱ i gyi. Nsu mfe̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱ aa mo̱ne̱ a kye̱na? Bɛɛɛ nsu mfe̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ŋ nyite mo̱ne̱ pwɛɛ na mo̱nꞌ dɛɛ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ gyi?” Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii ɔnyare̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Taa fo̱ gyi nyaŋsɛ‑ɔ baa mfe̱e̱.”

ref: While the boy was being brought, the demon attacked him and made him shake all over. Jesus ordered the demon to stop. Then he healed the boy and gave him back to his father.
text: Kanyaŋsɛɛ amo̱ a yɔ Yeesuu ase̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ibrisi amo̱ ne̱ i te mò̱ se̱‑ɔ a twe̱e̱ mò̱ bo̱ da swe̱e̱re̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ a lɔ kigbuŋgbuŋ‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ twe̱e̱ mò̱ asare̱e̱ na ayaa. Ne̱ Yeesuu a sa kanɔ fe̱yɛ ibrisi‑o ko̱so̱ kanyaŋsɛɛ‑ɔ se̱. Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya mò̱, ne̱ ɔɔ kpaare̱, ne̱ Yeesuu a kiŋŋaa mò̱ sa mò̱ se̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ mò̱ lowi ase̱ŋ‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: But the disciples did not know what he meant. The meaning was hidden from them. They could not understand it, and they were afraid to ask. Who Is the Greatest? (Matthew
text: Amaa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ nu ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱‑ɔ kaase̱. Ne̱ kufu mɔ de bamo̱ na bo̱ bise mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ kaase̱. Agyase̱po̱‑ɔ‑rɔ nsɛ e̱ gye̱ bre̱sɛ? (Matiyo

ref: Jesus' disciples were arguing about which one of them was the greatest.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ wo̱re̱ wo̱re̱ i gyiiri bamo̱ ke̱bre̱sɛ se̱ ikii.

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking, and he had a child stand there beside him.
text: Yeesuu mɔ nyi bamo̱ mfɛɛre̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ kra kayaagyi ko̱ baa bo̱ ye̱ra mò̱ ase̱.

ref: Then he said to his disciples, “When you welcome even a child because of me, you welcome me. And when you welcome me, you welcome the one who sent me. Whichever one of you is the most humble is the greatest.” For or against Jesus (Mark
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ ɔ kra ane̱ŋ a kayaagyi mɔ nɛɛnɛɛ‑ɔ e̱ kra mo̱ nɛɛnɛɛ ne̱e̱, ne̱ mò̱‑ɔ mò̱ ne̱ ɔ kra mo̱ nɛɛnɛɛ‑ɔ a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔɔ kra mò̱ ne̱ oo suŋ mo̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱‑ɔ nɛɛnɛɛ dɛɛ ne̱e̱. ref: John said, “Master, we saw a man using your name to force demons out of people. But we told him to stop, because he isn't one of us.”
text: Ne̱ Yohanee, ne̱ ɔ gye̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ ɔko̱‑ɔ, yɛ, “Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱, ane̱ a ŋu ɔko̱ de fo̱ ke̱nyare̱ ɔ gya ibrisi ɔ ko̱so̱ ase̱sɛ se̱. Ne̱ ane̱ a gya mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ yɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ ko̱waa bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ mo̱ŋ tii ane̱ se̱.”

ref: Not long before it was time for Jesus to be taken up to heaven, he made up his mind to go to Jerusalem.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu nyi dame̱naŋsɛ fe̱yɛ amo̱ ya waa kafwe̱e̱‑ɔ, saŋ e̱ fo̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ, na o wu, na o kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro, na ɔ yɔ Wuribware̱ se̱. Amo̱se̱ ne̱ ɔɔ waa mò̱ mfɛɛre̱ ne̱ ɔɔ de̱e̱ kpa se̱ ɔ yɔ mfe̱ŋ.

ref: He sent some messengers on ahead to a Samaritan village to get things ready for him.
text: Ne̱ oo suŋ mbɔɔ bo̱ gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ, ne̱ baa yɔ Samariya kamaŋgyii ko̱‑rɔ na bo̱ ya kpa to̱ŋ ko̱ lɔŋŋɔ bo̱ ye̱ra mò̱.

ref: But he was on his way to Jerusalem, so the people there refused to welcome him.
text: Amaa kamaŋgyii amo̱‑rɔ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ maa kra mò̱ nɛɛnɛɛ, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ baa de̱ŋ ŋu fe̱yɛ ɔ kpa a ɔ bo̱rɔ yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: When the disciples James and John saw what was happening, they asked, “Lord, do you want us to call down fire from heaven to destroy these people?”
text: Amɔ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ Yakubu na Yohanee a ŋu ane̱ŋ ne̱ ase̱sɛ amo̱ a waa‑ɔ, ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱, ane̱ ya sa ne̱ de̱e̱kpa a le̱e̱ so̱so̱ bo̱ kywɛɛ bamo̱ kpuri, a pre̱ fo̱ aaa?”

ref: But Jesus turned and corrected them for what they had said.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a buruwaa tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ a mfɛɛre̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ bo̱ daŋ.

ref: Then they all went on to another village. Three People Who Wanted To Be Followers (Matthew
text: Ne̱ mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱ amo̱ a kyo̱ŋ be̱e̱ yɔ kamaŋgyii ko̱ se̱. Bamo̱ ne̱ ba kpa fe̱yɛ bo̱ gya Yeesuu si‑o (Matiyo

ref: Jesus said, “Foxes have dens, and birds have nests, but the Son of Man doesn't have a place to call his own.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii ɔnyare̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Sabraŋ‑nɔ igyono de idikpa, ne̱ mbo̱gyii de asaa bo̱ da‑rɔ, amaa mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, fe̱raa mo̱ŋ de mfe̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ i di a n kyure‑o.”

ref: Jesus told someone else to come with him. But the man said, “Lord, let me wait until I bury my father.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ gywii ɔnyare̱ ko̱ fe̱yɛ, “Gya mo̱ se̱.” Ne̱ ɔnyare̱‑ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, sa a ŋ kiŋŋi yɔ pe̱ na ŋ ya pure n se̱ pwɛɛ na n dɛɛ be̱e̱ bo̱ gya fo̱ se̱.”

ref: Then someone said to Jesus, “I want to follow you, Lord, but first let me go back and take care of things at home.”
text: Ne̱ ɔnyare̱ ko̱ mɔ a be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ gywii Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, mo̱ e̱ gya fo̱ se̱, amaa sa a ŋ ya kra mo̱ ko̱we̱bɛɛ‑ana pwɛɛ.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Anyone who starts plowing and keeps looking back isn't worth a thing to God's kingdom!”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ naa na ɔ de̱e̱re̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ maa taare̱ a ɔ waa Wuribware̱ a kuwure‑o kusuŋ.”

ref: He said to them: A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers. Ask the Lord in charge of the harvest to send out workers to bring it in.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Yaabraa ke̱te̱ŋŋe̱ kyɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ, ate̱ŋŋe̱po̱ mɔ mo̱ŋ kyɔ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱nꞌ ko̱re̱ ko̱dɔɔ wuye‑o na o buwi ate̱ŋŋe̱po̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ mo̱ne̱ na bo̱ kya mo̱ne̱‑rɔ.

ref: Now go, but remember, I am sending you like lambs into a pack of wolves.
text: To, mo̱nꞌ nare̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ a kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱‑ɔ. Amaa mo̱nꞌ nyiŋŋi si fe̱yɛ mo̱ i suŋ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ nsanne̱gyii ne̱ ba ya lwee akwaare̱‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: Don't take along a moneybag or a traveling bag or sandals. And don't waste time greeting people on the road.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ma kaŋ taa atanne̱ kabɔɔte̱e̱, na ko̱re̱e̱, na ase̱be̱ta bo̱ gya mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ se̱. Na mo̱ne̱ ma be̱e̱ ye̱re̱ kpa‑rɔ ka ɔko̱ kanɔ.

ref: As soon as you enter a home, say, “God bless this home with peace.”
text: Pwɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i lwee lɔŋ ke̱maa‑rɔ‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ ka kanɔ. Na mo̱nꞌ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Wuribware̱ yure lɔŋ mɔ‑rɔ ase̱sɛ.’

ref: If the people living there are peace-loving, your prayer for peace will bless them. But if they are not peace-loving, your prayer will return to you.
text: Ne̱ se̱sɛ timaa e̱ bo̱ lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ mò̱ ya kɔɔre̱ mo̱ne̱ kanɔka fe̱raa, na mo̱nꞌ so̱we̱ lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ. Na Wuribware̱ yure lɔŋ wuye amo̱. Ne̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ bamo̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ne̱ kanɔka fe̱raa, na mo̱nꞌ kyo̱ŋ lɔŋ amo̱ se̱. Wuribware̱ maa yure lɔŋ wuye amo̱.

ref: Stay with the same family, eating and drinking whatever they give you, because workers are worth what they earn. Don't move around from house to house.
text: Mo̱nꞌ so̱we̱ lɔŋ ne̱ ba kɔɔre̱ mo̱ne̱ kanɔka nɛɛnɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ, na mo̱nꞌ gyi ateese ke̱maa ne̱ ba nya a bo̱ sa mo̱ne̱‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa ne̱ o suŋ kusuŋ‑o de kakɔka. Na mo̱nꞌ so̱we̱ lɔŋ ko̱ŋko̱‑rɔ, mo̱ne̱ ma nare̱ so̱we̱ e̱lɔŋ‑nɔ kaa.

ref: Heal their sick and say, “God's kingdom will soon be here!”
text: Na mo̱nꞌ kya alɔpo̱ mfe̱ŋ, na mo̱nꞌ tɔwe̱ gywii ase̱sɛ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Wuribware̱ a kuwure‑o e̱ kpa a ko̱ tɔ‑rɔ.’

ref: But if the people of a town refuse to welcome you, go out into the street and say,
text: Amaa mo̱ne̱ ya yɔ maŋ ko̱ se̱ ne̱ bamo̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ kra mo̱ne̱ nɛɛnɛɛ fe̱raa, mo̱nꞌ le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ yɔ kabo̱re̱ se̱, na mo̱nꞌ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ,

ref: But don't be happy because evil spirits obey you. Be happy that your names are written in heaven! Jesus Thanks His Father (Matthew
text: Mo̱ne̱ ma sa a mo̱ne̱ akatɔ gyi fe̱yɛ ibrisi a bu mo̱ne̱, amaa mo̱nꞌ sa a mo̱ne̱ akatɔ gyi bwe̱e̱tɔ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ mo̱ne̱ anyare̱ bo̱ be̱ya so̱so̱.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ i yure awo̱rɔba na bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: Many prophets and kings were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear.” The Good Samaritan
text: Mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ akyaamɛɛ na awure ko̱ gbaa a kpa fe̱yɛ bo̱ ŋu ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i ŋu‑o, bamo̱‑ɔ bo̱ mo̱ŋ nya ke̱mo̱ ŋu. Ne̱ baa kpa fe̱yɛ bo̱ nu ase̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i nu‑o, bamo̱‑ɔ bo̱ mo̱ŋ nya amo̱ mɔ nu.” Ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ ne̱ Samariyanyi ko̱ a waa‑ɔ kitee

ref: On the day of judgment the people of Tyre and Sidon will get off easier than you will.
text: Kake ne̱ Wuribware̱ i gyi ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ, o gyiiri mo̱ne̱ ke̱se̱bɔ a ɔ kyo̱ŋ Tiro na Sidɔŋ e̱maŋ awuye.

ref: People of Capernaum, do you think you will be honored in heaven? Well, you will go down to hell!
text: Mo̱ne̱ Isireelii awuye ne̱ mo̱ne̱ te Kapaaniyum maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ mɔ, mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ yase̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ yaa Wuribware̱ se̱ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱? Wuribware̱ e̱ twe̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ a ɔ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ o gyiiri e̱bɔye̱ awaapo̱ ke̱se̱bɔ‑ɔ.”

ref: I have given you the power to trample on snakes and scorpions and to defeat the power of your enemy Satan. Nothing can harm you.
text: To, mo̱nꞌ nu mfe̱e̱. Mo̱ a sa mo̱ne̱ Ɔbɔnsam se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ na mo̱nꞌ kɔ mò̱ gyi si, na mo̱nꞌ ya kyikye awɔ na agyapureepu si saŋ ke̱maa, na sɛye̱ ma waa mo̱ne̱.

ref: An expert in the Law of Moses stood up and asked Jesus a question to see what he would say. “Teacher,” he asked, “what must I do to have eternal life?”
text: Saŋ ko̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ mbraa ɔkaapo̱po̱ ko̱ a ba Yeesuu ase̱ na ɔ bo̱ kyɔ mò̱‑rɔ ke̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ abware̱se̱ŋ ke̱kaapo̱ bo̱ daŋ aaa. Ne̱ ɔnyare̱ amo̱ a bise fe̱yɛ, “Ɔkaapo̱po̱, e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa na ŋ nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ ne̱e̱?”

ref: The man replied, “The Scriptures say, ‘Love the Lord your God with all your heart, soul, strength, and mind.’ They also say, ‘Love your neighbors as much as you love yourself.’ ”
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ mbraa ɔkaapo̱po̱‑ɔ yɛ, “Baa kyo̱rɛɛ fe̱yɛ, ‘Lee fo̱ kame‑ro ba fo̱ kakpo̱nɔ na fo̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ na fo̱ mfɛɛre̱ pɛɛɛ bo̱ kpa fo̱ nyaŋpe̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱ŋ.’ ‘na fo̱ ke̱maa kpa fo̱ bɛɛko̱ ase̱ŋ, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kpa fo̱ e̱ye̱e̱‑ɔ.’ ”

ref: At that same time, Jesus felt the joy that comes from the Holy Spirit, and he said: My Father, Lord of heaven and earth, I am grateful that you hid all this from wise and educated people and showed it to ordinary people. Yes, Father, this is what pleased you.
text: Saŋ amo̱‑ɔ, amɔ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a sa ne̱ Yeesuu akatɔ a gyi, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “N se̱, so̱so̱ na swe̱e̱re̱ owure, mo̱ e̱ sa fo̱ aŋsɛ fe̱yɛ fo̱ a taa ke̱tɔ ne̱ fo̱ a bo̱ kwe̱e̱rɔ anyiase̱ŋpo̱ se̱‑ɔ bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ. N se̱, mo̱ a ŋu kase̱ŋtiŋ si, ane̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ a kpa fe̱yɛ fo̱ waa‑ɔ ne̱e̱.”

ref: My Father has given me everything, and he is the only one who knows the Son. The only one who really knows the Father is the Son. But the Son wants to tell others about the Father, so they can know him too.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “N se̱ a taa ke̱tɔ ke̱maa bo̱ waa mo̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ. Ɔke̱maa mɔ mo̱ŋ nyi ane̱ŋ ne̱ n du‑o, amɔ n se̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ wo̱re̱, ne̱ ɔke̱maa mɔ mo̱ŋ nyi ane̱ŋ ne̱ n se̱ du‑o, amɔ mo̱ aa bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ du fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ a lee fe̱yɛ bo̱ gyii mò̱‑ɔ wo̱re̱ kpeŋ.”

ref: Jesus then turned to his disciples and said to them in private, “You are really blessed to see what you see!
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ keri si, na ɔko̱ ma nya nu. Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ de ŋyure fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ a bo̱ mfe̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ i ŋu atɔ mɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i ŋu‑o.

ref: Jesus answered, “What is written in the Scriptures? How do you understand them?”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “E̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ baa tɔwe̱? Mo̱nꞌ tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ amo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ.”

ref: Jesus said, “You have given the right answer. If you do this, you will have eternal life.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱ fo̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ. Fo̱ ya waa ane̱ŋ, fo̱ e̱ nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ.”

ref: But the man wanted to show that he knew what he was talking about. So he asked Jesus, “Who are my neighbors?”
text: Wuribware̱ mbraa ɔkaapo̱po̱ amo̱ a kpa a ɔ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ se̱ŋbise‑o a baare‑o si‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ bise Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Ne̱ nsɛ e̱ gye̱ mo̱ bɛɛko̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱?”

ref: A priest happened to be going down the same road. But when he saw the man, he walked by on the other side.
text: Kafwe̱e̱‑ɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ ko̱ a ba ɔ kyo̱ŋ kpa amo̱ se̱ mfe̱ŋ dɛɛ. Mò̱ a ŋu ɔnyare̱ amo̱ da kpa‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ kwaye̱ mò̱ kyo̱ŋ.

ref: Later a temple helper came to the same place. But when he saw the man who had been beaten up, he also went by on the other side.
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ Lewii awuye ne̱ ba kya Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ bamo̱ kusuŋ‑no‑o ɔko̱ a bo̱ to̱ ɔnyare̱ amo̱ mfe̱ŋ, ne̱ ɔɔ ya de̱e̱re̱ mò̱ kwaye̱ kyo̱ŋ.

ref: and went over to him. He treated his wounds with olive oil and wine and bandaged them. Then he put him on his own donkey and took him to an inn, where he took care of him.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ da‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ ba nta na mfɔ bo̱ waa kadwii bo̱ me̱ra mò̱ e̱lɔ‑ɔ se̱, ne̱ oo ŋure e̱mo̱ se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ yase̱ ɔnyare̱‑ɔ bo̱ kye̱na mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ a kuruma ne̱ ɔ de̱e̱‑ɔ se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ taa mò̱ yaa afɔ a so̱we̱kpa‑ɔ ya ke̱e̱ mò̱.

ref: The next morning he gave the innkeeper two silver coins and said, “Please take care of the man. If you spend more than this on him, I will pay you when I return.”
text: Ke̱mo̱ kaye̱ ŋke‑o ne̱ oo lee siidii adunyɔ bo̱ sa afɔ lɔŋ ɔde̱e̱re̱se̱po̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ bo̱ de̱e̱re̱ ɔlɔpo̱ amo̱ se̱ sa mò̱. Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, ‘Mo̱ ke̱bo̱ kiŋŋi ba‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ fo̱ ya be̱e̱ nye̱ra atanne̱ kanɔ ŋke̱maa mò̱ kuŋu si bo̱ tii si, mo̱ e̱ ka fo̱.’ ”

ref: Then Jesus asked, “Which one of these three people was a real neighbor to the man who was beaten up by robbers?”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ bo̱ gye̱ kɛɛ fe̱yɛ, “To, abɛɛ asa mɔ‑rɔ, bamo̱ nsɛ ya kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ ɔnyare̱ ne̱ ayu‑o a da mò̱‑ɔ gye̱ mò̱ bɛɛko̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱?”

ref: The expert in the Law of Moses answered, “The one who showed pity.” Jesus said, “Go and do the same!” Martha and Mary
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ mbraa ɔkaapo̱po̱‑ɔ yɛ, “Mò̱ ne̱ ɔɔ waa ɔnyare̱ amo̱ ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ‑ɔ.” Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Amo̱ fe̱raa, nare̱ na fo̱ ya waa ane̱ŋ.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a ya ka Maataa na Mariya kanɔ‑ɔ

ref: The Lord and his disciples were traveling along and came to a village. When they got there, a woman named Martha welcomed him into her home.
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yeesuu mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a kya bamo̱ kpa‑ɔ se̱ ba yɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa ya fo̱ maŋ ko̱ ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Maataa‑ɔ te mò̱‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱. Ne̱ Yeesuu a so̱we̱ ɔkye̱e̱ amo̱ aye̱, ne̱ ɔɔ kra mò̱ nɛɛnɛɛ.

ref: She had a sister named Mary, who sat down in front of the Lord and was listening to what he said.
text: Maataa‑ɔ mɔ de mò̱ tire ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Mariya, ne̱ o te Yeesuu ayaa‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ o nu ase̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ.

ref: Martha was worried about all that had to be done. Finally, she went to Jesus and said, “Lord, doesn't it bother you that my sister has left me to do all the work by myself? Tell her to come and help me!”
text: Ane̱ŋ ne̱ mò̱ afɔ kyɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ateese ke̱de̱ŋa a tɔɔraa Maataa. Ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ Yeesuu ase̱ ya tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, amɔ fo̱ ase̱ a bware fe̱yɛ mo̱ tire a yɔwe̱ kusuŋ mɔ pɛɛɛ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ mo̱ se̱ aaa? Tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ ko̱so̱ bo̱ kya mo̱‑rɔ.”

ref: The Lord answered, “Martha, Martha! You are worried and upset about so many things,
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Oo! Oo! Maataa, fo̱ e̱ tɔɔraa fo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ atɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ se̱.

ref: but only one thing is necessary. Mary has chosen what is best, and it will not be taken away from her.”
text: Ke̱tɔ ko̱ŋko̱ e̱ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ki tiri, ne̱ fo̱ taa fo̱ mfɛɛre̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ se̱. Ke̱mo̱ ne̱ Mariya e̱ waa mbe̱yɔmɔ. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ oo lee a ɔ waa‑ɔ ne̱e̱. Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ ma tɔwe̱ a n gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ yɔwe̱.”

ref: So I tell you to ask and you will receive, search and you will find, knock and the door will be opened for you.
text: Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ, mo̱ne̱ ya ko̱re̱ Wuribware̱ atɔ, mo̱ne̱ e̱ nya ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ. Mo̱ne̱ ya buwi, mo̱ne̱ i ŋu ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ. Mo̱ne̱ ya ye̱re̱ kawu te̱e̱ mò̱, ɔ taye̱ po̱ne̱‑ɔ na mo̱nꞌ lweero.

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give the Holy Spirit to anyone who asks. Jesus and the Ruler of Demons (Matthew
text: Mo̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ gye̱ ase̱sɛ bɔye̱-ɔ gbaa nyi e̱sa mo̱ne̱ gyi-ana atɔ timaa, saŋbo̱to̱ Wuribware̱, mo̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱-ɔ, e̱ gye̱ ne̱, mo̱ne̱ ya ko̱re̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ sa mo̱ne̱ mò̱ kufwiiŋe timaa-o, ne̱ ɔ maa taare̱ a ɔ sa mo̱ne̱ aaa?” Yeesuu na Biil-sibul (Matiyo

ref: And suppose your friend answers, “Don't bother me! The door is bolted, and my children and I are in bed. I cannot get up to give you something.”
text: To, mo̱nꞌ be̱e̱ taa ke̱mo̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ nyare̱ ne̱ fo̱ a ko̱re̱ mò̱‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Fo̱ ma tɔɔraa mo̱. Mo̱ a kyɔ twe̱e̱ mo̱ ke̱kyaŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ mo̱ aa mo̱ gyi‑ana pɛɛɛ a di. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ma taare̱ a ŋ ko̱so̱ sa fo̱ ko̱tɔko̱.’

ref: Which one of you fathers would give your hungry child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
text: M be̱e̱ taa fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ nsɛ mò̱ gyi e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ kpa kakiŋgyi a ɔ wo̱, ne̱ ɔ taa ko̱wɔ a ɔ bo̱ sa mò̱?

ref: So Jesus told them, “Pray in this way: ‘Father, help us to honor your name. Come and set up your kingdom.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱, mo̱nꞌ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ane̱ se̱ Wuribware̱, sa a bo̱ kyo̱rɔ fo̱ ke̱nyare̱ timaa. Na fo̱ baa fo̱ kuwure‑o bo̱ gyi mfe̱e̱.

ref: Everyone who asks will receive, everyone who searches will find, and the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ ko̱re̱‑ɔ e̱ nya, ne̱ ɔke̱maa ne̱ o buwi‑o mɔ i ŋu, na po̱ne̱ taye̱ sa ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ ye̱re̱ kawu ɔ te̱e̱‑ɔ.

ref: Just as Jonah was a sign to the people of Nineveh, the Son of Man will be a sign to the people of today.
text: Ane̱ŋ ne̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ kaa waa Yonaa‑o a lee Wuribware̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ bo̱ kaapo̱ Niniwee awuye ne̱ baa kye̱na Yonaa mbe̱e̱ se̱‑ɔ, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ waa mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, i lee Wuribware̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ a m bo̱ kaapo̱ ndɔɔ a kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ.

ref: While Jesus was still talking, a woman in the crowd spoke up, “The woman who gave birth to you and nursed you is blessed!”
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ ye̱re̱ ba nu Yeesuu ase̱‑ɔ amo̱‑rɔ ɔkye̱e̱ ko̱ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔkye̱e̱ ne̱ ɔɔ nya kame ko̱we̱ fo̱, ne̱ ɔɔ nyapo̱ fo̱‑ɔ de ŋyure.”

ref: Jesus forced a demon out of a man who could not talk. And after the demon had gone out, the man started speaking, and the crowds were amazed.
text: Saŋ ko̱ Yeesuu a gya ibrisi ko̱ ne̱ e̱ maa taare̱ a e̱ sa se̱ŋsa, ne̱ i te ɔnyare̱ ko̱ se̱‑ɔ le̱e̱ mò̱ se̱. Ibrisi amo̱ a le̱e̱ mò̱ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔnyare̱‑ɔ a le̱e̱ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa. Ne̱ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ bamo̱.

ref: Others wanted to put Jesus to the test. So they asked him to show them a sign from God.
text: Bo̱ko̱ mɔ a kpa a bo̱ kyɔ Yeesuu‑ro ke̱e̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ waa akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ ko̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ ya sa mò̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ.

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking, and he said: A kingdom where people fight each other will end up in ruin. And a family that fights will break up.
text: Amaa Yeesuu a ŋu ke̱tɔ ne̱ ba fa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “E̱kɔ ya lwee maŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ maŋ‑ɔ ya ke‑ro, amɔ maŋ amo̱ i bwee ne̱e̱, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ kabuno ya ke‑ro, ne̱ ka bwee.

ref: If Satan fights against himself, how can his kingdom last? Yet you say that I force out demons by the power of Beelzebul.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ amo̱ e̱ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ Ɔbɔnsam na mò̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ awuye a ke‑ro, ke̱ maa kyee na mò̱ kuwure‑o kpuri. Mo̱ne̱ yɛ n de Biil-sibul ne̱ ɔ gye̱ Ɔbɔnsam‑ɔ e̱le̱ŋ mo̱ e̱ gya ibrisi.

ref: If I use his power to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? They are the ones who will judge you.
text: Amaa ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ e̱ gya ibrisi ba ko̱so̱ ase̱sɛ se̱. Nte̱tɔ e̱le̱ŋ ne̱ bo̱ de? Ɔbɔnsam a e̱le̱ŋ‑ɔ bɛɛɛ Wuribware̱ lee‑o? A gye̱ Wuribware̱ a e̱le̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱ bo̱ de, bɛɛɛ? To, mo̱ne̱ agyase̱po̱ amo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ gbaa a kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ a fo̱ kpa.

ref: You Pharisees are in for trouble! You love the front seats in the synagogues, and you like to be greeted with honor in the market.
text: Mo̱ne̱ Farisii awuye, mo̱ne̱ i ŋu ase̱ŋ ŋke ŋko̱ fɛɛ, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ngya dabe̱ dabe̱ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa ke̱kye̱na mo̱ne̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ akyaŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ be̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ gyɔŋŋɔ ka mo̱ne̱ nnɔ lamaŋ‑nɔ.

ref: A teacher of the Law of Moses spoke up, “Teacher, you said cruel things about us.”
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ ɔko̱ yɛ, “Ɔkaapo̱po̱, fo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ amo̱ ane̱ŋ fe̱raa, fo̱ e̱ saare̱ ane̱ fo̱ i tii si ne̱e̱.”

ref: When an evil spirit leaves a person, it travels through the desert, looking for a place to rest. But when it doesn't find a place, it says, “I will go back to the home I left.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Ibrisi ya ko̱so̱ se̱sɛ se̱, amɔ e̱ naa e̱ kaa e̱fa‑rɔ e̱e̱ kpa to̱ŋ ko̱ a e̱ kye̱na kyure. E̱mo̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ nya to̱ŋ ko̱, amɔ e̱e̱ fa e̱mo̱ mfɛɛre̱ fe̱yɛ ii kiŋŋi a e̱ yɔ lɔŋ ne̱ baa gya e̱mo̱ le̱e̱ mò̱‑rɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: When it gets there and finds the place clean and fixed up,
text: Na i kiŋŋi ya to̱ fe̱yɛ lɔŋ amo̱‑rɔ bo̱ daŋ ɔ mo̱ŋ de iyisi.

ref: it goes off and finds seven other evil spirits even worse than itself. They all come and make their home there, and that person ends up in worse shape than before. Being Really Blessed
text: Na e̱ le̱e̱ ya kpa ibrisi isunoo ne̱ e̱ bo̱ le̱ŋ e̱ kyɔ e̱mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ‑ɔ, na e̱ bo̱ kye̱na mfe̱ŋ. Ibrisi lɔŋ e̱ gye̱ se̱sɛ. To, amɔ e̱mo̱ a waa isunoo i te se̱sɛ amo̱ se̱ se̱‑ɔ, amɔ se̱sɛ a ko̱lɔ‑ɔ a waa ko̱kyɔ kyo̱ŋ saŋ ne̱ ibrisi ko̱ŋko̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ki te mò̱ se̱‑ɔ.” Ŋyure kase̱ŋtiŋ

ref: Jesus replied, “That's true, but the people who are really blessed are the ones who hear and obey God's message!” A Sign from God (Matthew
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱e̱. Amaa bamo̱ ne̱ ba nu abware̱se̱ŋ ne̱ ba bu amo̱, ne̱ bo̱ gya se̱‑ɔ de Wuribware̱ ŋyure bo̱ kyo̱ŋ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ.” Atɔ dabe̱ ko̱waa ase̱ŋ (Matiyo

ref: The Pharisee was surprised that he did not wash his hands before eating.
text: Ne̱ Farisiinyi‑o e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ mò̱ fe̱yɛ Yeesuu a mo̱ŋ fwe̱e̱ asare̱e̱ bamo̱ mbraa‑ɔ kpa se̱ pwɛɛ ne̱ ɔ dɛɛ o gyi‑o.

ref: You Pharisees are in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your gardens, such as mint and rue. But you cheat people, and you don't love God. You should be fair and kind to others and still give a tenth to God.
text: Mo̱ne̱ Farisii awuye, mo̱ne̱ i ŋu ase̱ŋ ŋke ŋko̱ fɛɛ. Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ gya Wuribware̱ mbraa ne̱ ŋ yɛ mo̱nꞌ taa mo̱ne̱ adɔɔteese ke̱maa hare̱e̱ alɛɛfo̱ gbaa ntuŋkare̱ kudu ke̱maa‑rɔ katuŋ ko̱ŋko̱ na mo̱nꞌ bo̱ lɔŋŋɔ Wuribware̱ saŋ ke̱maa, amaa mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ de kase̱ŋtiŋ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ be̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ maa kpa Wuribware̱ ase̱ŋ pɛɛɛ‑ɔ. Ane̱ŋ a atɔ mɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ a bware fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ waa, na mo̱ne̱ ma yɔwe̱ adɔɔteese a ntuŋkare̱ kudu‑ro kako̱ŋko̱‑ɔ ke̱taa sa Wuribware̱.

ref: As crowds were gathering around Jesus, he said: You people of today are evil! You keep looking for a sign from God. But what happened to Jonah is the only sign you will be given.
text: Saŋ amo̱, ase̱sɛ a tii si ne̱ bo̱ ye̱re̱ muruwaa Yeesuu‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ndɔɔ a kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ase̱sɛ gye̱ ase̱sɛ bɔye̱. Bo̱ yɛ ŋ waa akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ ko̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱. Amaa ma waa, amɔ Yonaa akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ wo̱re̱.

ref: No one lights a lamp and then hides it or puts it under a clay pot. A lamp is put on a lampstand, so everyone who comes into the house can see the light.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔko̱ maa kyaŋŋe̱ mò̱ fetiri si na ɔ taa bo̱ kwe̱e̱rɔ, bɛɛɛ ɔ be̱ra kike bo̱ buŋ si. Kabuno ne̱ ɔ taa a ɔ bo̱ ye̱ra, na ɔ laŋŋe̱‑rɔ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ase̱sɛ i lwee lɔŋ‑nɔ ne̱ ba nya bo̱ ŋu‑ro‑o.

ref: Your eyes are the lamp for your body. When your eyes are good, you have all the light you need. But when your eyes are bad, everything is dark.
text: Fo̱ fetiri e̱ bo̱ daŋ, ɔ laŋŋe̱ lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ pɛɛɛ. Ne̱ mò̱ ya nye̱ra fe̱raa, kibugyii e̱ da fo̱ lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ fo̱ akatɔ e̱ bo̱ daŋ, fo̱ ke̱e̱ nɛɛnɛɛ. Amaa amo̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ ke̱e̱ fe̱raa, a du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ a te kibugyii‑ro‑o. Ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ maa taare̱ a ki yii fo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ.

ref: You fools! Didn't God make both the outside and the inside?
text: Mfɛɛre̱ ke̱‑mo̱ŋ‑de awuye. N gye̱ Wuribware̱ ne̱ ɔɔ waa se̱sɛ kayo̱wɔre̱ se̱‑ɔ dɛɛ ya waa mò̱ kayo̱wɔre̱‑rɔ aaa?

ref: Yes, you are really in for trouble. You build monuments to honor the prophets your own people murdered long ago.
text: Mo̱ne̱ i ŋu ase̱ŋ ŋke ŋko̱ fɛɛ. Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ akyaamɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ ade̱daapo̱ a mɔɔ‑ɔ agye̱raŋta ne̱ mo̱ne̱ mɔ mo̱ne̱ de siimeetii mo̱ne̱ e̱ laa amo̱ nɛɛnɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: When Jesus finished speaking, a Pharisee invited him home for a meal. Jesus went and sat down to eat.
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a sa se̱ŋsa lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ Farisiinyi ko̱ a te̱e̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ ba mò̱ aye̱ na ɔ bo̱ gyi ateese. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ mfe̱ŋ o te o gyi.

ref: So the Lord said to him: You Pharisees clean the outside of cups and dishes, but on the inside you are greedy and evil.
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ Farisii awuye fe̱raa, mo̱ne̱ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ ne̱ ba fwe̱e̱ bamo̱ ato̱re̱nkyu na e̱gyare̱ mmɛɛ, amaa mo̱ne̱ maa fwe̱e̱ amo̱ mme‑ro. Mo̱ne̱ e̱ lɔŋŋɔ mo̱ne̱ ŋyo̱wɔre̱, amaa apoo na bo̱ro̱kɔɔne̱ ya bo̱rɔ mo̱ne̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ.

ref: If you would only give what you have to the poor, everything you do would please God.
text: Mo̱nꞌ ke atiripo̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ de‑o na a mo̱ŋ bo̱ mo̱ne̱ gya. Mo̱ne̱ ya waa ane̱ŋ fe̱raa, mo̱ne̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ n de iyisi.

ref: But you are in for trouble! You are like unmarked graves that people walk on without even knowing it.
text: Mo̱ne̱ i ŋu ase̱ŋ ŋke ŋko̱ fɛɛ. Mo̱ne̱ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱gye̱raŋta ne̱ ase̱sɛ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ bo̱ nyi ke̱mo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ. Ɔko̱ ya kyikye ke̱gye̱raŋta se̱, ne̱ mò̱ gbaa mo̱ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ ke̱gye̱raŋta ne̱e̱, ɔ waa mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ iyisi.”

ref: You must think that was the right thing for your people to do, or else you would not have built monuments for the prophets they murdered.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱ne̱ gbaa mo̱ne̱ a sure ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ ade̱daapo̱ a waa‑ɔ se̱, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ya mɔɔ Wuribware̱ a akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ mɔ mo̱ne̱ a waa bamo̱ agye̱raŋta.

ref: Because of your evil deeds, the Wisdom of God said, “I will send prophets and apostles to you. But you will murder some and mistreat others.”
text: Fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ mò̱ kanyiase̱ŋ se̱ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ i suŋ mo̱ akyaamɛɛ na mo̱ ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱ a m bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ bamo̱ ase̱, amaa ba mɔɔ bamo̱ bo̱ko̱, na bo̱ waa bo̱ko̱ mɔ awo̱re̱fɔɔ.’

ref: You teachers of the Law of Moses are really in for trouble! You carry the keys to the door of knowledge about God. But you never go in, and you keep others from going in.
text: Mo̱ne̱, Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱ mɔ, mo̱ne̱ i ŋu ase̱ŋ ŋke ŋko̱ fɛɛ. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ a twe̱e̱ ke̱kyaŋ ne̱ kanyiase̱ŋ bo̱ ke̱mo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a kɔɔre̱ safo̱wa‑ɔ bo̱ be̱ya. Mo̱ne̱ maa lweero, mo̱ne̱ mɔ mo̱ne̱ a waa ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ a tii bamo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ ne̱ ba kpa a bo̱ taare̱ lweero mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ kpa.”

ref: As thousands of people crowded around Jesus and were stepping on each other, he told his disciples: Be sure to guard against the dishonest teaching of the Pharisees! It is their way of fooling people.
text: Saŋ ne̱ ase̱sɛ ŋkpe̱ŋ ŋkpe̱ŋ a bo̱ gyaara abɛɛ se̱ Yeesuu ase̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ sa Farisii awuye si, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ yiisi ne̱ bo̱ de ba waa bodobodoo‑ro‑o mo̱ŋ bo̱ kɔne̱. Amo̱‑ɔ e̱ kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ de mfɛɛre̱ bɔye̱ ba kwe̱e̱rɔ. Mo̱ne̱ ma kaŋ kii nnɔ ŋnyɔ ŋnyɔ awuye‑o fe̱yɛ bamo̱‑ɔ.

ref: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. Won't he do even more for you? You have such little faith!
text: Wuribware̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ lo̱ waagya o buŋ e̱fa ne̱ e̱ ye̱re̱ ndɔɔ, ne̱ nyaŋe̱ bo̱ fo̱‑ɔ e̱ ya wu‑o, ne̱ bo̱ de e̱mo̱ ba kure de̱e̱kpa na ba tɔ bodobodoo‑o. Amɔ mo̱ne̱ fe̱raa mo̱ne̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ maa pee ke̱katɔ a ɔ sa mo̱ne̱ atɔ buŋsɛ aaa? Mo̱ne̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi‑o mo̱ŋ kyɔ.

ref: Even the hairs on your head are counted. So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Matthew
text: Mo̱nꞌ nu fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kare̱ ipwii ne̱ i te mo̱ne̱ aŋu si‑o, ne̱ oo ŋu e̱mo̱ kanɔ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱ne̱ ma sa a kufu nya mo̱ne̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ nte̱ye̱te̱ye̱ ŋkpe̱ŋ ŋkpe̱ŋ mo̱ŋ fo̱ mo̱ne̱ ɔko̱.” Yeesuu kipini na Yeesuu kikine (Matiyo

ref: Sell what you have and give the money to the poor. Make yourselves moneybags that never wear out. Make sure your treasure is safe in heaven, where thieves cannot steal it and moths cannot destroy it.
text: Mo̱nꞌ fe mo̱ne̱ kaye̱‑rɔ mfe̱e̱ a mpo̱tɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ de‑o, na mo̱nꞌ taa atanne̱‑ɔ sa atiripo̱, na mo̱nꞌ buwi kpa Wuribware̱ se̱ atanne̱ mbɔɔte̱e̱ ne̱ m maa nye̱ra‑ɔ na mo̱nꞌ buwi kpa mpo̱tɛɛ ne̱ m bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mfe̱ŋ fe̱raa sɛye̱ maa lee mmo̱ se̱, ne̱ oyu maa taare̱ a o ywii, ne̱ nsekreeke na igyeeri mɔ maa taare̱ a ŋ nye̱ra mpo̱tɛɛ amo̱ Wuribware̱ se̱ mfe̱ŋ.

ref: At that time the Holy Spirit will tell you what to say. A Rich Fool
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o e̱ kaapo̱ ke̱tɔ bɛɛɛ ase̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱nꞌ tɔwe̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ.” Atɔ wuye ko̱ kitee

ref: If you tell others that you belong to me, the Son of Man will tell God's angels that you are my followers.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya se̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ, ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ nya kakpo̱nɔ na ɔ tɔwe̱ lamaŋ‑nɔ fe̱yɛ o tii mo̱ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, gbaa e̱ waa mò̱ ane̱ŋ Wuribware̱ a mbɔɔ‑ɔ akatɔ‑rɔ.

ref: But if you reject me, you will be rejected in front of them.
text: Ne̱ ɔko̱ ne̱ mò̱ ya kine mo̱ lamaŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, gbaa i kine mò̱ Wuribware̱ a mbɔɔ‑ɔ akatɔ‑rɔ.

ref: When you are brought to trial in the synagogues or before rulers or officials, don't worry about how you will defend yourselves or what you will say.
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ ya yaa mo̱ne̱ se̱ŋgyikpa, hare̱e̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ abre̱sɛ ase̱ bɛɛɛ awure ase̱ bɛɛɛ maŋ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ ase̱ ooo, mo̱ne̱ ma kaŋ sa a ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ ya kya kanɔ‑ɔ na ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ ya tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ tɔɔraa mo̱ne̱.

ref: A man in a crowd said to Jesus, “Teacher, tell my brother to give me my share of what our father left us when he died.”
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ ba nu Yeesuu ase̱‑ɔ amo̱‑rɔ ɔnyare̱ ko̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Ɔkaapo̱po̱, tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ daa fe̱yɛ ɔ sa a mo̱ aa mò̱ ke kapo̱tɛɛ ne̱ ane̱ se̱ a wu yɔwe̱ sa ane̱‑ɔ‑rɔ.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Who gave me the right to settle arguments between you and your brother?”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ nyare̱, nsɛ ya sa mo̱ kpa fe̱yɛ n gyi amo̱‑ɔ ase̱ŋ bɛɛɛ ŋ ke mo̱ne̱ kapo̱tɛɛ‑rɔ sa mo̱ne̱?”

ref: and he said to himself, “What can I do? I don't have a place large enough to store everything.”
text: Kake ŋko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ fa mò̱ mfɛɛre̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ akyeŋkyeŋku mo̱ŋ kyɔ a m ba mo̱ adɔɔteese bo̱ waa‑rɔ. E̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa?’

ref: Later, he said, “Now I know what I'll do. I'll tear down my barns and build bigger ones, where I can store all my grain and other goods.
text: Mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ɔ be̱e̱ ɔ yɛ, ‘Ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ i kyuwi mo̱ akyeŋkyeŋku de̱daa‑ɔ na ŋ yii adabe̱ na n taa mo̱ adɔɔteese pɛɛɛ waa‑rɔ, na m pwɛɛ akyaŋ dabe̱ na n taa mo̱ ate̱tɔmo̱ŋta waa‑rɔ.

ref: Jesus said to his disciples: I tell you not to worry about your life! Don't worry about having something to eat or wear.
text: To, ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ, mo̱ne̱ ma sa a ateese ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i gyi‑o bɛɛɛ atɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i buŋ‑o ase̱ŋ tɔɔraa mo̱ne̱.

ref: Life is more than food or clothing.
text: Ŋkpa ase̱ŋ tiri a kyɔ ateese, ne̱ kayo̱wɔre̱ mɔ tiri ka kyɔ atɔ buŋsɛ.

ref: Look how the wild flowers grow! They don't work hard to make their clothes. But I tell you Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of these flowers.
text: To, mo̱nꞌ be̱e̱ ke̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ afe̱raagyi a daŋ‑ɔ, a maa suŋ, ne̱ a maa lo̱ atɔ buŋsɛ a sa amo̱ e̱ye̱e̱. Amaa mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ Owure Solomɔŋ a dɛɛ kye̱na‑ɔ, mò̱ aa mò̱ atɔ gbaa, ɔ mo̱ŋ nya waagya timaa na ɔ bo̱ daŋ fe̱yɛ afe̱raagyi amo̱.

ref: Don't keep worrying about having something to eat or drink.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱ne̱ ma sa a ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i gyi‑o, na ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i nuu‑o, ase̱ŋ tɔɔraa mo̱ne̱.

ref: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father knows what you need.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ba tɔɔraa bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ mfaanɛɛ a atɔ‑ɔ se̱. Mo̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ nyi fe̱yɛ atɔ pɛɛɛ tiri mo̱ne̱.

ref: Do you think that I came to bring peace to earth? No indeed! I came to make people choose sides.
text: Mo̱ne̱ nyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ a ba a m bo̱ baa kaye̱e̱yuri kaye̱‑rɔ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱? Daabii, mo̱ a ba a m bo̱ nye̱ra ase̱sɛ mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ ne̱e̱, n gye̱ kaye̱e̱yuri.

ref: Servants are fortunate if their master finds them awake and ready when he comes! I promise you he will get ready and let his servants sit down so he can serve them.
text: Mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ ayaafɔre̱‑ɔ e̱ ke̱e̱, ne̱ bamo̱ nyaŋpe̱ ya ba, bamo̱ akatɔ i gyi bwe̱e̱tɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ o buŋŋi mò̱ atɔ buŋsɛ, na o buŋ amo̱ ne̱ bo̱ buŋ ba waa ateese‑o, na ɔ sa a bo̱ kye̱na kyure, na mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ de̱ŋa, na mò̱ aa bamo̱ kye̱na gyi.

ref: Those servants are really fortunate if their master finds them ready, even though he comes late at night or early in the morning.
text: Hare̱e̱ bamo̱ nyaŋpe̱‑ɔ ya ba kaye̱ nse̱na bɛɛɛ kamɛɛ‑rɔ bo̱ to̱ fe̱yɛ baa waa siraa bo̱ gywii mò̱ gbaa, bamo̱ akatɔ i gyi.

ref: You would surely not let a thief break into your home, if you knew when the thief was coming.
text: Mo̱ne̱ gbaa mo̱ne̱ nyi fe̱yɛ lɔŋ wuye dɛɛ o nyi saŋ ne̱ oyu de ɔ ba‑ɔ ne̱e̱, weetee ɔ maa yɔwe̱ mò̱ lɔŋ a ɔ sa oyu‑o na ɔ bo̱ lweero ya ywii mò̱.

ref: Peter asked Jesus, “Did you say this just for us or for everyone?”
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Peetroo yɛ, “Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱, ane̱ wo̱re̱ ne̱ fo̱ a da ke̱kpare̱ mɔ bo̱ sa bɛɛɛ ɔke̱maa mɔ?”

ref: A servant who is always faithful will surely be put in charge of everything the master owns.
text: Mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ, a waa kanɔ ŋke̱maa, mò̱ nyaŋpe̱ e̱ taa mò̱ kapo̱tɛɛ pɛɛɛ na ɔ bo̱ sa mò̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱ amo̱ a ɔ de̱e̱ de̱e̱re̱ se̱ sa mò̱.

ref: But servants who don't know what their master wants them to do will not be beaten so hard for doing wrong. If God has been generous with you, he will expect you to serve him well. But if he has been more than generous, he will expect you to serve him even better. Not Peace, but Trouble (Matthew
text: Amaa ke̱yaafɔre̱ ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ nyi ke̱tɔ ne̱ mò̱ nyaŋpe̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱‑ɔ mò̱ a waa adase̱ŋ‑ɔ, ba ba ko̱kyo̱kywe̱e̱ ke̱da kafwe̱e̱ a bo̱ bo̱ gyiiri mò̱ ke̱se̱bɔ. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ɔko̱ ne̱ mo̱ a sa mò̱ atɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ fe̱yɛ ɔ de̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ, amo̱ ya fo̱, mò̱ ne̱ mo̱ i bise ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Ne̱ ɔko̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ sa mò̱ atɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ fe̱yɛ ɔ de̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ, amo̱ ya fo̱, mò̱ ne̱ mo̱ i bise ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu e̱ nye̱ra ase̱sɛ mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: I am going to be put to a hard test. And I will have to suffer a lot of pain until it is over.
text: Awo̱re̱fɔɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ dɔŋ mo̱ se̱ ne̱e̱, amo̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ kyo̱ŋ, mo̱ kakpo̱nɔ maa di mo̱.

ref: A family of five will be divided, with two of them against the other three.
text: Le̱e̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ bo̱ yɔ mbuno ke̱maa i ke‑ro, na abɛɛ asa na abɛɛ anyɔ kɔ abɛɛ.

ref: Fathers and sons will turn against one another, and mothers and daughters will do the same. Mothers-in-law and daughters-in-law will also turn against each other. Knowing What To Do (Matthew
text: Na ase̱ na bamo̱ gyi‑ana kɔ na bamo̱ mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ te̱ŋ‑nɔ, na agyi na bamo̱ anyi kɔ na bamo̱ mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ te̱ŋ‑nɔ, na ase̱sɛ anyi na bamo̱ gyi‑ana aka kɔ na bamo̱ mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ te̱ŋ‑nɔ.” Ndɔɔ a kake mɔ kaase̱ kunu (Matiyo

ref: Jesus said to all the people: As soon as you see a cloud coming up in the west, you say, “It's going to rain,” and it does.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a buruwaa tɔwe̱ gywii lamaŋ ne̱ ba nu mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ya ŋu awo̱re̱ a le̱e̱ kyɔwe̱ twɛɛkpa, amɔ mo̱ne̱ yɛ bware̱ e̱ kpa a ɔ ba, na kase̱ŋtiŋ ɔ ba.

ref: When the south wind blows, you say, “It's going to get hot,” and it does.
text: Ne̱ mo̱ne̱ ya be̱e̱ ŋu afwii e̱ da kyɔwe̱ le̱e̱kpa kigyise si, amɔ mo̱ne̱ yɛ kipuripuri e̱ kpa a ke̱ ba, na kase̱ŋtiŋ ke̱ ba.

ref: When someone accuses you of something, try to settle things before you are taken to court. If you don't, you will be dragged before the judge. Then the judge will hand you over to the jailer, and you will be locked up.
text: Ane̱ taa fe̱yɛ fo̱ i de ɔko̱ ko̱kɔ bɛɛɛ fo̱ aa fo̱ bɛɛko̱ i de ase̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ yaa fo̱ se̱ŋgyikpa, fo̱ ko̱ ne̱ fo̱ a baa ase̱ŋ‑ɔ wuree de̱e̱re̱ se̱ na fo̱ aa ase̱ŋ wuye‑o lɔŋŋɔ amo̱ pwɛɛ na mo̱nꞌ dɛɛ fo̱ se̱ŋgyikpa‑ɔ mfe̱ŋ. N gye̱ ane̱ŋ, o gyiiri fo̱ a ɔ yaa ɔse̱ŋgyipo̱‑ɔ ase̱ na mò̱ mɔ taa fo̱ sa apurisii awuye na bo̱ tii fo̱.

ref: Jesus replied: Do you think that these people were worse sinners than everyone else in Galilee just because of what happened to them?
text: Ne̱ baa bise Yeesuu fe̱yɛ lowi suyo ne̱ baa wu‑o e̱ kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ gye̱ abɔye̱waapo̱ bo̱ kyɔ bamo̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana Galile̱ya awuye aaa?

ref: Not at all! But you can be sure that if you don't turn back to God, every one of you will also be killed.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Daabii. A mo̱ŋ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ko̱ kyɔ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ i yii mo̱ne̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ nu mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱, na mo̱nꞌ kiŋŋi le̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ, Wuribware̱ i gyiiri mo̱ne̱ gbaa mo̱ne̱ ke̱se̱bɔ, na mo̱nꞌ wu.

ref: Not at all! But you can be sure that if you don't turn back to God, every one of you will also die. A Story about a Fig Tree
text: A mo̱ŋ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ. Na m be̱e̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ nu mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ na mo̱nꞌ kiŋŋi le̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ, mo̱ne̱ i wu fe̱yɛ bamo̱‑ɔ dɛɛ.” Figi kiyii ne̱ ke̱ maa swɛɛ‑ɔ kitee

ref: Jesus then told them this story: A man had a fig tree growing in his vineyard. One day he went out to pick some figs, but he didn't find any.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ kitee mɔ gywii bamo̱. Ɔ yɛ, “N gye̱ ɔnyare̱ ko̱ e̱ bo̱‑rɔ aaa? Ne̱ oo dwii figi kiyii ko̱ mò̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ kake ŋko̱ ɔɔ yɔ a ɔ ya kpa agyi kiyii‑o si, mò̱‑ɔ mò̱ mo̱ŋ nya.

ref: The gardener answered, “Master, leave it for another year. I'll dig around it and put some manure on it to make it grow.
text: Ne̱ mò̱ ko̱dɔɔ a ɔde̱e̱re̱se̱po̱‑ɔ a be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, yɔwe̱ ke̱mo̱. Sa ke̱mo̱ kasu ko̱ŋko̱ kpeŋ. Mo̱ i kwii a m bo̱ muruwaa ke̱mo̱ na n swii e̱naate̱ igyini bo̱ waa‑rɔ.

ref: Maybe it will have figs on it next year. If it doesn't, you can have it cut down.” Healing a Woman on the Sabbath
text: Ne̱ mo̱ ya waa ane̱ŋ, ne̱ su-a-ba ke̱mo̱ ya swɛɛ fe̱raa, a bware. Ne̱ ke̱mo̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ swɛɛ, amo̱ fe̱raa na fo̱ ŋe bo̱ twe̱e̱.’ Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ saase̱bɛɛ.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya ɔkye̱e̱ ke̱gyabɔɔ ko̱ kukyure kake‑o

ref: One Sabbath, Jesus was teaching in a synagogue,
text: Kukyure kake ko̱ ne̱ Yeesuu e̱ kaapo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ ko̱‑rɔ.

ref: and a woman was there who had been crippled by an evil spirit for
text: Ɔkye̱e̱ ko̱ mɔ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ, ne̱ Ɔbɔnsam a waa mò̱ ko̱lɔ nsu kudu mburuwa. Ko̱lɔ amo̱ se̱, ɔ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ ɔ naa kyɛɛkyɛɛ, oo buŋ‑no ne̱e̱ ɔ naa.

ref: When Jesus saw the woman, he called her over and said, “You are now well.”
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a ŋu mò̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ mò̱ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔpe̱kye̱e̱, ŋkee fe̱raa fo̱ a nya fo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ le̱e̱ fo̱ ko̱lɔ‑ɔ se̱.”

ref: The Lord replied, “Are you trying to fool someone? Won't any one of you untie your ox or donkey and lead it out to drink on a Sabbath?
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nnɔ ŋnyɔ ŋnyɔ awuye mɔ. Mo̱ne̱ ke̱maa e̱ saŋŋe̱ mò̱ e̱naate̱ bɛɛɛ ikuruma ɔ yaa nkyu nuukpa kake ke̱maa, hare̱e̱ kukyure kake gbaa. Amo̱‑ɔ fe̱raa mo̱ŋ gye̱ kusuŋ ko̱waa ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱?

ref: Jesus' words made his enemies ashamed. But everyone else in the crowd was happy about the wonderful things he was doing. A Mustard Seed and Yeast (Matthew
text: Ne̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa‑ɔ a sa ne̱ ipeere a nya mò̱ ado̱ŋ. Ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa saŋ‑ɔ akatɔ a gyi bo̱ le̱e̱ akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ ne̱ Yeesuu a waa‑ɔ se̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a ba kupurutugyi bo̱ da ke̱kpare̱‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: At that time some Pharisees came to Jesus and said, “You had better get away from here, because Herod wants to kill you!”
text: Saŋ amo̱ dɛɛ ne̱ Farisii awuye ko̱ a bo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “A tiri fe̱yɛ fo̱ a le̱e̱ mfe̱e̱ yɔ to̱ŋ ko̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Owure Hɛrɔd e̱ kpa fo̱ a ɔ mɔɔ.”

ref: But I am going on my way today and tomorrow and the next day. After all, Jerusalem is the place where prophets are killed. Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Matthew
text: A saŋ a tiri fe̱yɛ ŋ yɔ mo̱ kpa‑ɔ ndɔɔ na nyaŋe̱ na kraye̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ e̱ kpa a bo̱ mɔɔ Wuribware̱ a akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ, Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ ne̱ ba mɔɔ bamo̱. Ne̱ mo̱ saŋ‑ɔ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ fo̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ yɔ mfe̱ŋ.’ ”

ref: There the ones who are now least important will be the most important, and those who are now most important will be least important. Jesus and Herod
text: Na bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ nsii-amɛɛ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ kii agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ mfe̱ŋ, na bo̱ko̱ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ mɔ kii nsii-amɛɛ mfe̱ŋ.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu e̱ kpa Yɛro̱salɛm awuye ase̱ŋ‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: Jesus said to them: Go tell that fox, “I am going to force out demons and heal people today and tomorrow, and three days later I'll be through.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ nare̱ ya tɔwe̱ gywii naŋsɛ amo̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ndɔɔ mo̱ e̱ gya ibrisi mo̱ e̱ ko̱so̱ ase̱sɛ se̱ ne̱e̱, na mo̱ e̱ kya alɔpo̱, na bo̱ fo̱ nyaŋe̱ na kraye̱‑ana‑ɔ mo̱ e̱ lo̱we̱ mo̱ kusuŋ.

ref: It is like what happens when someone plants a mustard seed in a garden. The seed grows as big as a tree, and birds nest in its branches.”
text: To, mfaanɛɛ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kuwure‑o‑ro du. Ku du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kupurutugyi‑o. Kigyi mɔ du kagyingyii, amaa se̱sɛ ya dwii ke̱mo̱, amɔ ke̱ e̱ kwɛɛ a ke̱ daŋ kii kikiree, ne̱ ke̱ bo̱ e̱swe̱e̱ ko̱ kyo̱ŋ ayii pɛɛɛ. Ne̱ mbo̱gyii a bo̱ kye̱na ke̱mo̱ se̱ waa bamo̱ asaa.” Yiisi a ke̱kpare̱‑ɔ (Matiyo
