ref: As Jesus was on his way to Jerusalem, he taught the people in the towns and villages.
text: Ne̱ ŋkee Yeesuu naa e̱maŋ se̱ na mmaŋgyii‑ro ɔ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ o gywii ase̱sɛ na ɔ yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm a keri‑o.

ref: Do all you can to go in by the narrow door! A lot of people will try to get in, but will not be able to.
text: “Wuribware̱ a kuwure‑o du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱kyaŋ ne̱ ke̱mo̱ kabunogyii du bɔŋbɔŋ‑ɔ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱nꞌ le̱ŋ e̱ye̱e̱, na mo̱nꞌ taare̱ nya lwee ke̱kyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ akatɔ i pee ke̱mo̱ kulweero si, amaa n gye̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ba nya kpa a bo̱ lweero.

ref: Once the owner of the house gets up and locks the door, you will be left standing outside. You will knock on the door and say, “Sir, open the door for us!” But the owner will answer, “I don't know a thing about you!”
text: Saŋ ko̱ e̱ ba ne̱ lɔŋ wuye‑o e̱ twe̱e̱ po̱ne̱‑ɔ‑rɔ, na mo̱ne̱ te mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ lweero. Na mo̱nꞌ sii kawu mo̱ne̱ ye̱re̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ daye̱ po̱ne̱‑ɔ se̱, na mo̱ne̱ e̱ te̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱, taye̱ ane̱,’ na ɔ be̱ŋŋaa mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ŋ nyi mo̱ne̱, ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi mfe̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a le̱e̱‑ɔ.’

ref: Then you will start saying, “We dined with you, and you taught in our streets.”
text: Na mo̱nꞌ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ane̱ aa fo̱ ya dɛɛ gyi na ane̱ i nuu, ne̱ fo̱ a nare̱ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ ane̱ aye̱.’

ref: But he will say, “I really don't know who you are! Get away from me, you evil people!”
text: Na mò̱‑ɔ mò̱ be̱e̱ be̱ŋŋaa mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ŋ nyi mo̱ne̱, ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi mfe̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a le̱e̱‑ɔ. Ase̱ŋ bɔye̱ awuye mɔ, mo̱nꞌ le̱e̱ mo̱ ase̱.’

ref: Then when you have been thrown outside, you will weep and grit your teeth because you will see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in God's kingdom.
text: Wuribware̱ ya kine mo̱ne̱, ne̱ ɔɔ gya mo̱ne̱ le̱e̱ mò̱ kuwure‑o‑ro, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ ya ŋu mo̱ne̱ nana‑ana Abraham na Isak na Yakubu na Wuribware̱ akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ Wuribware̱ ase̱ fe̱raa, n gye̱ e̱wo̱gya ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i su na mo̱nꞌ duŋ nnɔ.

ref: People will come from all directions and sit down to feast in God's kingdom.
text: Ase̱sɛ e̱ le̱e̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ to̱ŋ ke̱maa, na bamo̱ ɔke̱maa kye̱na Wuribware̱ a lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ, na bamo̱ aa mò̱ gyi ateese, na bamo̱ akatɔ gyi bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu be̱e̱ ɔ yɛ, “Yɛro̱salɛm awuye, Yɛro̱salɛm awuye. Saŋ ke̱maa ne̱ Wuribware̱ a suŋ mò̱ akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ ɔko̱ mo̱ne̱ ase̱‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ a daye̱ mò̱ abu mɔɔ. Saŋ ke̱maa mo̱ a kpa fe̱yɛ ŋ kpɔwe̱ mo̱ne̱ bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱, na ŋ kuŋ mo̱ne̱, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ kyaase̱ i buŋ mò̱ agyi si‑o, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ mɔ mo̱ne̱ a kine.

ref: Now your temple will be deserted. You won't see me again until the time when you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
text: Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ e̱ yɔwe̱ mo̱ne̱ ase̱ŋ a ɔ sa mo̱ne̱, na mo̱ne̱ maŋ mɔ kii ke̱taakpaŋ. Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ maa lɛɛ ŋu mo̱, amɔ saŋ a fo̱ fe̱yɛ m ba, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Wuribware̱ yure mò̱ ne̱ ɔ ba mò̱ ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ‑ɔ.’ ”

ref: All of a sudden a man with swollen legs stood up in front of him.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a ŋu ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ mò̱ ayaa na asare̱e̱ a puŋŋe‑o ye̱re̱ mò̱ akatɔ‑rɔ.

ref: Jesus turned and asked the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses, “Is it right to heal on the Sabbath?”
text: To, ne̱ Yeesuu a bise Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ na Farisii awuye‑o fe̱yɛ, “Mbraa ne̱ Wuribware̱ a sa ane̱‑ɔ e̱ sa kpa fe̱yɛ ane̱ kya alɔpo̱ kukyure kake aaa?”

ref: But they did not say a word. Jesus took hold of the man. Then he healed him and sent him away.
text: Amaa bo̱ mo̱ŋ be̱ŋŋaa mò̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ dabo̱rɔ ɔnyare̱ amo̱ kya mò̱ ko̱lɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ nare̱ pe̱.

ref: There was nothing they could say. How To Be a Guest
text: Amaa bo̱ be̱e̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ taare̱ lee mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ kanɔ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ baa ane̱ e̱ye̱e̱ kaase̱‑ɔ na kafɔkra ase̱ŋ

ref: Jesus saw how the guests had tried to take the best seats. So he told them:
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a ŋu ane̱ŋ ne̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ baa te̱e̱ bamo̱ a bo̱ bo̱ gyi‑o i lee e̱kye̱nakpa timaa‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ,

ref: If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa ne̱ o bu mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ke̱dabe̱‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ e̱ baa mò̱ kaase̱, ne̱ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ baa mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ kaase̱‑ɔ mɔ, Wuribware̱ e̱ sa mò̱ ke̱dabe̱.”

ref: Then Jesus said to the man who had invited him: When you give a dinner or a banquet, don't invite your friends and family and relatives and rich neighbors. If you do, they will invite you in return, and you will be paid back.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii lɔŋ wuye‑o fe̱yɛ, “Amo̱se̱‑ɔ fo̱ ya te̱e̱ ase̱sɛ ateesegyikpa, fo̱ ma te̱e̱ fo̱ nyare̱‑ana bɛɛɛ fo̱ adaa na fo̱ atire bɛɛɛ fo̱ ko̱we̱bɛɛ‑ana bɛɛɛ atɔ awuye ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ fo̱ akye̱nabɛɛpo̱‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ŋke ŋko̱ ba waa ane̱ŋ dɛɛ a bo̱ bo̱ teere, saŋ‑ɔ a waa ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ baa waa bo̱ ka ke̱tɔ ne̱ fo̱ a waa sa bamo̱‑ɔ ko̱kɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: When the servant returned, he said, “Master, I've done what you told me, and there is still plenty of room for more people.”
text: Ke̱tɔ bo̱ waa kafwe̱e̱‑ɔ, mò̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱‑ɔ a kiŋŋi bo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, mo̱ a waa ane̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ, amaa kye̱nakpa saŋ ɔ bo̱‑rɔ.’

ref: After Jesus had finished speaking, one of the guests said, “The greatest blessing of all is to be at the banquet in God's kingdom!”
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ mò̱ gbaa te gyikpa amo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ a nu ke̱tɔ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Bamo̱ ne̱ ba kye̱na Wuribware̱ a kuwure‑o‑ro a gyikpa‑ɔ de ŋyure.”

ref: Jesus told him: A man once gave a great banquet and invited a lot of guests.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ kitee mɔ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “N gye̱ ɔnyare̱ ko̱ e̱ dɛɛ ɔ bo̱‑rɔ aaa? Ne̱ kake ŋko̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ba gyikpa mò̱ aye̱.

ref: When the banquet was ready, he sent a servant to tell the guests, “Everything is ready! Please come.”
text: Saŋ a fo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ bo̱ gyi‑o, ne̱ oo suŋ mò̱ ayaafɔre̱‑ɔ ɔko̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ ya tɔwe̱ gywii ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ mò̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱ yɛ mo̱nꞌ ba, na ɔɔ waa siraa.’

ref: One guest after another started making excuses. The first one said, “I bought some land, and I've got to look it over. Please excuse me.”
text: Amaa bamo̱ ɔke̱maa yɛ ɔ maa taare̱ a ɔ yɔ. Mò̱ ne̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱ amo̱ a gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ yɔ mò̱ ase̱‑ɔ yɛ, ‘Mo̱ a de̱ŋ sɔɔ swe̱e̱re̱ ko̱, ne̱ a tiri fe̱yɛ ŋ ya de̱e̱re̱, amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ fo̱, ma taare̱ a m ba.’

ref: Another guest said, “I bought five teams of oxen, and I need to try them out. Please excuse me.”
text: Ne̱ ɔko̱ mɔ yɛ, ‘Mo̱ a de̱ŋ sɔɔ mo̱ e̱naate̱ kudu ko̱, amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱ e̱ ya ke̱e̱ bamo̱. Ma taare̱ a m ba.’

ref: The servant told his master what happened, and the master became so angry he said, “Go as fast as you can to every street and alley in town! Bring in everyone who is poor or paralyzed or blind or lame.”
text: To, ne̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱‑ɔ a kiŋŋi ya tɔwe̱ amo̱ pɛɛɛ gywii mò̱ nyaŋpe̱. Ne̱ mò̱ nyaŋpe̱‑ɔ duŋ a fwii, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ gywii ke̱yaafɔre̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Waa me̱naŋ nare̱ yɔ maŋ‑nɔ mbo̱re̱ se̱ na alɔŋde̱ se̱ ya te̱e̱ atiripo̱ na agyabɔɔ na ate̱napo̱ baa.’

ref: His master then told him, “Go out along the back roads and make people come in, so my house will be full.
text: Ne̱ mò̱ nyaŋpe̱‑ɔ be̱e̱ ɔ yɛ, ‘Nare̱ mbo̱re̱ se̱, na maŋ‑nɔ e̱kpa se̱, na e̱fa‑rɔ be̱e̱ ya ko̱re̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ fo̱ i ŋu‑o baa, na mo̱ lɔŋ‑ɔ nya bo̱rɔ.

ref: Not one of the guests I first invited will get even a bite of my food!” Being a Disciple (Matthew
text: Amaa agye̱ŋkpɛɛsɛ ne̱ mo̱ a te̱e̱, ne̱ baa kine‑o, ɔko̱ maa lɛɛ nya mo̱ ateese a o gyi daa.’ ” Yeesuu maa kpa bamo̱ ne̱ ba gya mò̱ se̱ katiŋ, na bo̱ kiŋŋi kamɛɛ‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: You cannot be my disciple, unless you love me more than you love your father and mother, your wife and children, and your brothers and sisters. You cannot follow me unless you love me more than you love your own life.
text: “Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ ba mo̱ ase̱‑ɔ e̱ mo̱ŋ kpa mo̱ ase̱ŋ kyo̱ŋ mò̱ se̱ na mò̱ nyi, na mò̱ ka na agyi, na mò̱ daa‑ana na mò̱ tire‑ana na mò̱ pe̱kye̱e̱‑ana na mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱, amo̱ fe̱raa ɔ maa taare̱ a ɔ bo̱ kii mo̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ ɔko̱.

ref: You cannot be my disciple unless you carry your own cross and follow me.
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ maa taare̱ a o kperi mo̱ se̱ so̱rɔ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ gya mo̱ se̱‑ɔ maa taare̱ a o kii mo̱ ɔgyase̱po̱.”

ref: Otherwise, you will start building the tower, but not be able to finish. Then everyone who sees what is happening will laugh at you.
text: Mò̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ fa lɔŋ‑ɔ mfɛɛre̱ pwɛɛ, ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ taare̱ pwɛɛ lo̱we̱, ase̱sɛ ne̱ ba ŋu‑o e̱ mɔse̱ mò̱.

ref: They will say, “You started building, but could not finish the job.”
text: Na bo̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ke̱e̱, ɔnyare̱ mɔ a le̱e̱ ɔ pwɛɛ lɔŋ, ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ taare̱ lo̱we̱.’ ”

ref: If he thinks he won't be able to defend himself, he will send messengers and ask for peace while the other king is still a long way off.
text: Mò̱ ya ŋu fe̱yɛ ɔ maa taare̱ a ɔ kɔ gyi si, o suŋ mbɔɔ a ɔ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ owure ko̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱, na ɔ saŋ ɔ bo̱ ke̱fɔ ɔ ba, na bo̱ ya ko̱re̱ mò̱ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ kpa a bo̱ kii anyare̱.”

ref: So then, you cannot be my disciple unless you give away everything you own. Salt and Light (Matthew
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ bo̱ gye̱ kɛɛ fe̱yɛ, “Ase̱ŋ mɔ katiŋ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ maa yɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ o de‑o na ɔ gya mo̱ se̱‑ɔ maa taare̱ a o kii mo̱ ɔgyase̱po̱.

ref: It is no longer good for the soil or even for the manure pile. People simply throw it out. If you have ears, pay attention!
text: M mo̱ŋ lɛɛ m bware bo̱ sa swe̱e̱re̱ gbaa. Ba taa a bo̱ bo̱ twe̱e̱ ne̱e̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ de e̱se̱bɔ‑ɔ mo̱nꞌ nu ase̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ.”

ref: I will go to my father and say to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against God in heaven and against you.
text: Mo̱ i kiŋŋi a ŋ yɔ n se̱ ase̱, na ŋ ya tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, N se̱, mo̱ a waa bɔye̱ bo̱ kye fo̱ aa Wuribware̱.

ref: The younger son said to his father, “Give me my share of the property.” So the father divided his property between his two sons.
text: Kake ŋko̱ ne̱ mò̱ gyi kagyingyii‑o a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘N se̱, ke fo̱ kapo̱tɛɛ‑rɔ, na fo̱ taa mo̱ lee sa mo̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ.’ Ne̱ mò̱ se̱ a ke‑ro sa bamo̱ abɛɛ anyɔ‑ɔ.

ref: He had spent everything, when a bad famine spread through that whole land. Soon he had nothing to eat.
text: Saŋ ne̱ oo gyi amo̱ pɛɛɛ lo̱we̱, ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ o de sɛye̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ako̱ŋ dabe̱ ko̱ a ba swe̱e̱re̱ amo̱ se̱.

ref: So the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses started grumbling, “This man is friendly with sinners. He even eats with them.”
text: Ne̱ Farisii awuye na Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱ ko̱ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ a ko̱so̱ ba tɛɛ fe̱yɛ, “Ke̱e̱, ɔnyare̱ mɔ e̱ sa ne̱ abɔye̱waapo̱ e̱ ba mò̱ ase̱, hare̱e̱ mò̱ aa bamo̱ i gyi ateese.”

ref: Then Jesus told them this story:
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ kitee mɔ gywii bamo̱.

ref: And when you find it, you will be so glad that you will put it on your shoulder
text: Mò̱ ya ŋu mò̱, amɔ mò̱ akatɔ a gyi, na ɔ taa mò̱ bo̱ da abaakpa‑rɔ,

ref: and carry it home. Then you will call in your friends and neighbors and say, “Let's celebrate! I've found my lost sheep.”
text: na ɔ so̱rɔ yaa pe̱. Na ɔ te̱e̱ mò̱ nyare̱‑ana na mò̱ akye̱nabɛɛpo̱ bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱, na ɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ a ŋu mo̱ sanne̱ ne̱ ɔɔ fo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ akatɔ a gyi, amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ gyi akatɔ.’

ref: Jesus told the people another story: What will a woman do if she has ten silver coins and loses one of them? Won't she light a lamp, sweep the floor, and look carefully until she finds it?
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “To, mo̱nꞌ be̱e̱ taa fe̱yɛ ɔkye̱e̱ ko̱ de asare̱e̱‑rɔ mpini kudu, ne̱ mmo̱‑rɔ kako̱ŋko̱ ya fo̱, e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ ɔ waa? To, ɔ kyaŋŋe̱ fetiri ne̱e̱, na ɔ kpa pesi na ɔ kpane̱ mò̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ pɛɛɛ ɔ de̱e̱re̱ dame̱naŋsɛ ɔ kpa kamo̱ ane̱ŋ‑aaa, amɔ ɔ bo̱ ŋu.

ref: Not long after that, the younger son packed up everything he owned and left for a foreign country, where he wasted all his money in wild living.
text: Ke̱tɔ mo̱ŋ kyee, ne̱ kagyingyii‑o a fe mò̱ kapo̱tɛɛ ne̱ ɔɔ nya‑ɔ pɛɛɛ, ne̱ ɔɔ taa atanne̱‑ɔ. Amo̱ fe̱raa, ɔ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ kye̱na pe̱, ne̱ oo tu kpa yɔ to̱ŋ ko̱ ke̱fɔ ke̱fɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ ya gyi atanne̱‑ɔ kanɔ‑rɔ lo̱we̱.

ref: He went to work for a man in that country, and the man sent him out to take care of his pigs.
text: Sɛye̱ ke̱‑mo̱ŋ‑lɛɛ kide se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ mfe̱ŋ a maŋ‑ɔ kigyi ko̱ ase̱ ya kpa kusuŋ. Ne̱ ɔnyare̱ amo̱ a taa mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ de̱e̱re̱ mò̱ aprako si.

ref: He would have been glad to eat what the pigs were eating, but no one gave him a thing.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ yo̱rɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ de̱e̱ nya ateese ne̱ aprako‑o i gyi‑o gyi. Amaa aprako ateese amo̱ gbaa, ɔ mo̱ŋ nya ako̱ gyi.

ref: Finally, he came to his senses and said, “My father's workers have plenty to eat, and here I am, starving to death!
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa bo̱ fo̱ saŋ ko̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mfɛɛre̱ timaa a ba mò̱ kuŋu‑ro, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, ‘N se̱ a ayaafɔre̱ ne̱ ba suŋ mò̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ e̱ nya ateese ba gyi ba kpo̱ne̱, na m bo̱ mfe̱e̱ mo̱ i wu ako̱ŋ.

ref: I am no longer good enough to be called your son. Treat me like one of your workers.’ ”
text: Fo̱ ma lɛɛ te̱e̱ mo̱ ɛ fo̱ gyi, taa mo̱ bo̱ waa fo̱ ayaafɔre̱‑ɔ ɔko̱.’

ref: The younger son got up and started back to his father. But when he was still a long way off, his father saw him and felt sorry for him. He ran to his son and hugged and kissed him.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ ko̱so̱ me̱raa se̱ kiŋŋi ɔ yɔ mò̱ se̱ ase̱. Saŋ ne̱ ɔɔ fo̱ pe̱, ne̱ ɔ tɔ lɔŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱ se̱ a ŋu mò̱ le̱e̱ ke̱fɔ. Ne̱ mò̱ gyi‑o ase̱ŋ a waa mò̱ e̱wɛɛ, ne̱ ɔɔ ŋwɛɛnaŋ ya biri mò̱‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ kyo̱kywe̱e̱ mò̱ ke̱daabɔŋ se̱ sa mò̱ aŋsɛ na ke̱ba pwɛɛ ne̱ mò̱ gyi‑o a dɛɛ ka mò̱ kanɔ.

ref: The son said, “Father, I have sinned against God in heaven and against you. I am no longer good enough to be called your son.”
text: Ne̱ mò̱ gyi‑o yɛ, ‘N se̱, mo̱ a waa bɔye̱ bo̱ kye fo̱ aa Wuribware̱. Fo̱ ma lɛɛ te̱e̱ mo̱ ɛ fo̱ gyi.’

ref: But his father said to the servants, “Hurry and bring the best clothes and put them on him. Give him a ring for his finger and sandals for his feet.
text: Ne̱ mò̱ se̱ a sa ne̱ ɔɔ yɔwe̱ se̱ŋsa, ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ mò̱ ayaafɔre̱. Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, ‘Mo̱nꞌ waa me̱naŋ taa awaagya timaa ne̱ n de‑o baa bo̱ buŋ mò̱, na mo̱nꞌ ba kapini bo̱ waa mò̱ ke̱sare̱e̱gyi‑ro, na mo̱nꞌ waa mò̱‑rɔ ase̱be̱ta.

ref: Get the best calf and prepare it, so we can eat and celebrate.
text: Na ŋkee mo̱nꞌ kra kakpe̱ŋnyare̱e̱ ne̱ ka de mfɔ‑ɔ bo̱ mɔɔ, na ane̱ gyi ateese bo̱ gyi akatɔ.

ref: This son of mine was dead, but has now come back to life. He was lost and has now been found.” And they began to celebrate.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ a du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ gyi mɔ a wu, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro‑o. Ɔɔ dɛɛ fo̱, amaa mbe̱yɔmɔ, mo̱ a ŋu mò̱.’ Ne̱ baa le̱e̱ ba gyi akatɔ.

ref: The older son had been out in the field. But when he came near the house, he heard the music and dancing.
text: Amo̱‑ɔ ne̱ a yɔ se̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ na mò̱ daa ne̱ mò̱ fe̱raa a sii mò̱ se̱ ase̱ pe̱‑ɔ bo̱ ndɔɔ‑rɔ. Ɔɔ le̱e̱ ndɔɔ‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ ɔ ba ɔ tɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ oo nu iliŋ na isoori si bamo̱ lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: So he called one of the servants over and asked, “What's going on here?”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ ayaafɔre̱‑ɔ ɔko̱, ne̱ oo bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Nte̱tɔ ase̱ŋ ya ba lɔŋ‑nɔ?’

ref: The servant answered, “Your brother has come home safe and sound, and your father ordered us to kill the best calf.”
text: Ne̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱‑ɔ yɛ, ‘Fo̱ tire ya kiŋŋi ba pe̱ ŋkpa na alaŋfiya‑ro, ne̱ fo̱ se̱ a mɔɔ kakpe̱ŋnyare̱e̱ ne̱ ka de mfɔ‑ɔ sa mò̱.’

ref: The older brother got so angry that he would not even go into the house. His father came out and begged him to go in.
text: Ne̱ mò̱ daa‑ɔ duŋ a fwii, ne̱ oo sii ke̱laŋbɛɛ. Ɔ yɛ ɔ maa lwee lɔŋ‑nɔ. Bamo̱ a tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱‑ɔ gywii mò̱ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱ se̱ a le̱e̱ kawu ya ko̱re̱ mò̱ na o lwee lɔŋ‑nɔ.

ref: But he said to his father, “For years I have worked for you like a slave and have always obeyed you. But you have never even given me a little goat, so that I could give a dinner for my friends.
text: Amaa ɔɔ be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ se̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ke̱e̱ nsu de̱maŋte̱ mɔ ne̱ mo̱ a suŋ sa fo̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a gye̱ ke̱nya‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ a waa ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ fo̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ. Nte̱tɔ gbaa ne̱ fo̱ a sa mo̱? Fo̱ mo̱ŋ mɔɔ kate̱e̱re̱gyii gbaa sa mo̱, na mo̱ aa mo̱ nyare̱‑ana a wo̱ bo̱ gyi akatɔ.

ref: This other son of yours wasted your money on prostitutes. And now that he has come home, you ordered the best calf to be killed for a feast.”
text: Amaa fo̱ gyi mɔ ne̱ ɔɔ ya nye̱ra fo̱ kapo̱tɛɛ akye̱e̱ se̱, ne̱ oo kiŋŋi ba pe̱‑ɔ fe̱raa, fo̱ a mɔɔ kakpe̱ŋnyare̱e̱ ne̱ ka de mfɔ‑ɔ sa mò̱.’

ref: His father replied, “My son, you are always with me, and everything I have is yours.
text: Ne̱ mò̱ se̱ a be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ gyi, fo̱ fe̱raa, fo̱ bo̱ mfe̱e̱ saŋ ke̱maa, ne̱ mo̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa gye̱ fo̱ lee.

ref: But we should be glad and celebrate! Your brother was dead, but he is now alive. He was lost and has now been found.”
text: Amaa a bware fe̱yɛ ane̱ gyi akatɔ, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ a du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ tire mɔ a wu ne̱e̱, ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ ɔɔ be̱e̱ kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro. Ɔɔ fo̱ ne̱e̱, amaa mbe̱yɔmɔ mo̱ a ŋu mò̱.’ ” Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ saase̱bɛɛ.”

ref: Jesus said to his disciples: A rich man once had a manager to take care of his business. But he was told that his manager was wasting money.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ kitee mɔ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “N gye̱ atɔ wuye ko̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱‑rɔ aaa? Ne̱ o de mò̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱ ko̱ ne̱ ɔ de̱e̱re̱ mò̱ kapo̱tɛɛ pɛɛɛ se̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ kake ŋko̱ baa tɔwe̱ gywii atɔ wuye‑o fe̱yɛ mò̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱ amo̱ e̱ nye̱ra mò̱ atanne̱.

ref: So the rich man called him in and said, “What is this I hear about you? Tell me what you have done! You are no longer going to work for me.”
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa, ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ i nu ba tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ e̱ nye̱ra mo̱ atanne̱. Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ taa mo̱ kapo̱tɛɛ pɛɛɛ bo̱ waa mo̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ma lɛɛ sa a fo̱ de̱e̱re̱ mo̱ kapo̱tɛɛ‑ɔ se̱.’

ref: The manager said to himself, “What shall I do now that my master is going to fire me? I can't dig ditches, and I'm ashamed to beg.
text: Ne̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱‑ɔ a fa mò̱ mfɛɛre̱‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱ e̱ kpa a o lee mo̱ kusuŋ‑no. Ne̱ e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa? Mo̱ŋ bo̱ le̱ŋ a n taare̱ dɔɔ, ne̱ ipeere de mo̱ ke̱nare̱ ko̱re̱ atɔ.

ref: I know what I'll do, so that people will welcome me into their homes after I've lost my job.”
text: To, ŋ nyi ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa, na, mo̱ ya paŋ kusuŋ, na ŋ nya anyare̱ ne̱ mo̱ ya yɔ bamo̱ e̱lɔŋ‑nɔ na bo̱ kra mo̱ nɛɛnɛɛ‑ɔ.’

ref: “A hundred barrels of olive oil,” the man answered. So the manager said, “Take your bill and sit down and quickly write ‘
text: Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, ‘Mfɔ aŋko̱re̱ ke̱lafa bo̱rɔ bo̱‑rɔ.’ Ne̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱‑ɔ yɛ, ‘Ke̱e̱, ko̱kɔ a wo̱re̱ ne̱ fo̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ sa mo̱ nyaŋpe̱‑ɔ. Taa na fo̱ kye̱na na fo̱ kyurowi ke̱lafa‑ɔ kyo̱rɛɛ fe̱yɛ amo̱ kanɔ bo̱ adunuu.’

ref: The manager asked someone else who was in debt to his master, “How much do you owe?” “A thousand sacks of wheat,” the man replied. The manager said, “Take your bill and write ‘
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ bise ɔko̱ ɛ, ‘Ne̱ fo̱, afe̱ne̱ ne̱ fo̱ de mo̱ nyaŋpe̱ ko̱kɔ?’ Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, ‘Yaabraa e̱bɔɔte̱ kakpe̱ŋ.’ Ne̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱‑ɔ yɛ, ‘Ke̱e̱ ko̱kɔ a wo̱re̱ ne̱ fo̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ sa mo̱ nyaŋpe̱‑ɔ mɔ. Taa na fo̱ kyurowi kakpe̱ŋ‑ɔ kyo̱rɛɛ fe̱yɛ amo̱ kanɔ gye̱ alafa aburuwa.’

ref: The master praised his dishonest manager for looking out for himself so well. That's how it is! The people of this world look out for themselves better than the people who belong to the light.
text: Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ saŋ ne̱ apoo wuye mɔ mò̱ nyaŋpe̱ a nu ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ waa‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ kyo̱rɔ mò̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ o nyi ase̱ŋ. Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, ‘Kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ase̱sɛ de kanyiase̱ŋ bo̱ de bamo̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana bo̱ kyɔ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱sɛ de bamo̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana Wuribware̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ.’ ”

ref: My disciples, I tell you to use wicked wealth to make friends for yourselves. Then when it is gone, you will be welcomed into an eternal home.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya se̱ fe̱yɛ, “Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ, mo̱nꞌ ba mo̱ne̱ kaye̱‑rɔ a kapo̱tɛɛ‑ɔ bo̱ buwi kpa anyare̱ swe̱e̱re̱ mɔ mfe̱e̱, na mo̱ne̱ ya wu, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ kapo̱tɛɛ a kyo̱ŋ, Wuribware̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ne̱ kakatɔgyi‑ro, na ɔ sa mo̱ne̱ akyaŋ so̱so̱ mfe̱ŋ, na mo̱nꞌ kye̱na nsu pɛɛɛ.

ref: So Abraham said, “If they won't pay attention to Moses and the prophets, they won't listen even to someone who comes back from the dead.”
text: Ne̱ Abraham be̱e̱ ɔ yɛ, ‘Bamo̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ gya Mosis a kanɔ‑ɔ se̱ na Wuribware̱ a akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ ase̱ŋ tɔwe̱sɛ se̱, ɔko̱ ya kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro yɔ gbaa, bo̱ maa kyurowi bamo̱ mfɛɛre̱.’ ” Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “To, ane̱ŋ ne̱ a du‑o ne̱e̱.”

ref: The Pharisees really loved money. So when they heard what Jesus said, they made fun of him.
text: Amɔ Farisii awuye‑o a nu ase̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa mɔse̱ mò̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ba kpa atanne̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: But Jesus told them: You are always making yourselves look good, but God sees what is in your heart. The things that most people think are important are worthless as far as God is concerned.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ e̱ waa atɔ ne̱ a kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱ waa nɛɛnɛɛ‑ɔ ase̱sɛ ase̱, amaa Wuribware̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ o nyi ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ bo̱ mo̱ne̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱tɔ ne̱ se̱sɛ i bu fe̱yɛ ke̱ gye̱ kitimaa‑o, Wuribware̱ fe̱raa mo̱ŋ de ke̱mo̱ ɔ te̱e̱ sɛye̱.

ref: Until the time of John the Baptist, people had to obey the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets. But since God's kingdom has been preached, everyone is trying hard to get in.
text: Mbraa ne̱ Wuribware̱ a bo̱rɔ Mosis si bo̱ sa mò̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ na ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ a kyo̱rɛɛ‑ɔ a fo̱ Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱‑ɔ mbe̱e̱ se̱ ne̱e̱. Le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ase̱sɛ a le̱e̱ ba nu Wuribware̱ a kuwure a ase̱ŋ timaa‑o. Ne̱ ɔke̱maa e̱ le̱ŋ e̱ye̱e̱ ɔ kpa a o tii kuwure‑o si.

ref: It is a terrible sin for a man to divorce his wife and marry another woman. It is also a terrible sin for a man to marry a divorced woman. Lazarus and the Rich Man
text: N taa fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa ne̱ oo kine mò̱ ka ya waare̱e̱ ɔkye̱e̱ baŋbaŋ‑ɔ a waa kakye̱e̱kpa, ne̱ ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ waare̱e̱ ɔkye̱e̱ ne̱ mò̱ kuri a kine mò̱‑ɔ mɔ a waa kakye̱e̱kpa‑ɔ dɛɛ.” Atɔ wuye ko̱ na otiripo̱ ko̱ kitee

ref: There was once a rich man who wore expensive clothes and every day ate the best food.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ kitee mɔ fe̱yɛ, “N gye̱ atɔ wuye ko̱ ya dɛɛ kye̱na aaa? Ne̱ ɔɔ dɛɛ buŋ awaagya dame̱naŋsɛ timaa, ne̱ oo gyi kɔne̱ saŋ ke̱maa.

ref: The poor man died, and angels took him to the place of honor next to Abraham. The rich man also died and was buried.
text: Ne̱ otiripo̱ amo̱ a bo̱ wu, ne̱ Wuribware̱ a mbɔɔ‑ɔ a bo̱ so̱rɔ mò̱ yaa so̱so̱ yaa bo̱ kye̱na Abraham keri‑ro kakatɔgyi‑ro. Ne̱ atɔ wuye‑o mɔ a bo̱ wu, ne̱ baa pure mò̱, ne̱ baa waa mò̱ kale.

ref: He went to hell and was suffering terribly. When he looked up and saw Abraham far off and Lazarus at his side,
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ abɔye̱waapo̱ ya wu, ne̱ baa yɔ ya gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ‑ɔ. Ne̱ o gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ mfe̱ŋ, ne̱ ɔɔ de̱e̱re̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ ŋu Abraham te ke̱fɔ, na Lasarus te mò̱ keri‑ro.

ref: he said to Abraham, “Have pity on me! Send Lazarus to dip his finger in water and touch my tongue. I'm suffering terribly in this fire.”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ keŋkeŋ fe̱yɛ, ‘N nana Abraham, ŋu mo̱ e̱wɛɛ, na fo̱ sa a Lasarus ba ke̱sare̱e̱gyi bo̱ waa nkyu ko̱‑rɔ, na ɔ ba bo̱ dwe̱e̱ mo̱ gyeepu si, na e̱ye̱e̱ yuri mo̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ mo̱ gya de̱e̱kpa mɔ‑rɔ.’

ref: Abraham answered, “My friend, remember that while you lived, you had everything good, and Lazarus had everything bad. Now he is happy, and you are in pain.
text: Ne̱ Abraham a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘N nana, nyiŋŋi si fe̱yɛ fo̱ a kyɔ gyi fo̱ kɔne̱ saŋ ne̱ fo̱ te kaye̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ Lasarus mɔ a gyi preepree. Amaa mbe̱yɔmɔ o gyi kɔne̱ mfe̱e̱ ne̱e̱, na fo̱ gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ.

ref: But the rich man said, “Abraham, then please send Lazarus to my father's home.
text: Ne̱ atɔ wuye‑o yɛ, ‘Amo̱ fe̱raa, n nana Abraham, mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ fo̱ ne̱e̱, suŋ Lasarus bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ n se̱ a lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: Let him warn my five brothers, so they won't come to this horrible place.”
text: N de atire na adaa anuu mfe̱ŋ. Sa a ɔ yɔ na ɔ ya yii bamo̱ se̱, na bamo̱ mɔ bo̱ ma be̱e̱ ba kaye̱e̱gya awo̱re̱fɔɔ mɔ‑rɔ.’

ref: Abraham answered, “Your brothers can read what Moses and the prophets wrote. They should pay attention to that.”
text: Ne̱ Abraham yɛ, ‘Bo̱ kyɔ bo̱ de abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, sa a bo̱ bu ke̱tɔ ne̱ Mosis na Wuribware̱ a akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ a kyo̱rɛɛ bo̱ waa mò̱‑rɔ‑ɔ.’

ref: Then the rich man said, “No, that's not enough! If only someone from the dead would go to them, they would listen and turn to God.”
text: Ne̱ atɔ wuye‑o yɛ, ‘N nana Abraham, a mo̱ŋ kyɔ. Amaa ɔko̱ a kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro yɔ bamo̱ ase̱ ne̱e̱, weetee ba nu bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱, na bo̱ kiŋŋi le̱e̱ bamo̱ e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ.’

ref: Why was this foreigner the only one who came back to thank God?”
text: Nte̱tɔ ne̱e̱, ne̱ mò̱ ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Isireeliinyi‑o fe̱raa wo̱re̱ ya kiŋŋi bo̱ sa Wuribware̱ aŋsɛ?”

ref: Jesus replied: If you had faith no bigger than a tiny mustard seed, you could tell this mulberry tree to pull itself up, roots and all, and to plant itself in the ocean. And it would!
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ dɛɛ mo̱ne̱ de ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi kafwe̱e̱ gbaa fe̱yɛ kiyii kigyi kagyingyii ne̱e̱, weetee mo̱ne̱ ya tɔwe̱ gywii nno̱ŋ a kiyii amo̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ko̱so̱ kyuwi fo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ya bo̱ ye̱ra ɔpo̱o̱‑rɔ,’ ke̱ e̱ waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ.” Ke̱yaafɔre̱ kusuŋ

ref: If your servant comes in from plowing or from taking care of the sheep, would you say, “Welcome! Come on in and have something to eat”?
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ taa fe̱yɛ fo̱ gye̱ nyaŋpe̱, ne̱ fo̱ de ke̱yaafɔre̱, ne̱ ɔ dɔɔ fo̱, bɛɛɛ ɔ de̱e̱re̱ fo̱ e̱sanne̱ se̱. Mò̱ ya le̱e̱ ndɔɔ‑rɔ ke̱de̱e̱pwɛta ba, fo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ a fo̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Aŋsɛ na ndɔɔ‑rɔ, bo̱ gyi ateese’ aaa?

ref: Jesus said to his disciples: The time will come when you will long to see one of the days of the Son of Man, but you will not.
text: Ne̱ oo buruwaa tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Saŋ ko̱ e̱ ba ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ ŋu kake ko̱ŋko̱ gbaa ne̱ mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, i gyi kuwure‑o. Amaa mo̱ne̱ maa ŋu kake amo̱.

ref: Servants don't deserve special thanks for doing what they are supposed to do.
text: Ke̱yaafɔre̱ ya waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ mò̱ nyaŋpe̱ e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ, ɔ mo̱ŋ de aŋsɛ na kusuŋ, bɛɛɛ mo̱ e̱ pe̱nna aaa?

ref: Jesus said to his disciples: There will always be something that causes people to sin. But anyone who causes them to sin is in for trouble. A person who causes even one of my little followers to sin
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “A waa dɛɛ atɔ ne̱ a sa a se̱sɛ waa bɔye̱‑ɔ e̱ ba, amaa ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ sa ne̱ a ba‑ɔ i ŋu ase̱ŋ fɛɛ.

ref: would be better off thrown into the ocean with a heavy stone tied around their neck.
text: Se̱sɛ amo̱ fe̱raa, bamo̱ ya ba kibu dabe̱ bo̱ da mò̱ ke̱bɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ baa twe̱e̱ mò̱ waa ɔpo̱o̱‑rɔ, a bɔ fe̱yɛ mò̱ a sa ne̱ ŋyaagyi mɔ ɔko̱ a waa bɔye̱.

ref: So be careful what you do. Correct any followers of mine who sin, and forgive the ones who say they are sorry.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱nꞌ sa ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ waa‑ɔ se̱. Fo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ ya waa fo̱ bɔye̱, tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔɔ waa bɔye̱, ne̱ mò̱ ya nu mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱, na fo̱ taa bo̱ ke mò̱.

ref: Even if one of them mistreats you seven times in one day and says, “I am sorry,” you should still forgive that person.
text: Ne̱ ɔko̱ ya waa fo̱ e̱bɔye̱, hare̱e̱ iluwi isunoo kake ko̱ŋko̱‑rɔ gbaa, ne̱ ke̱mo̱ ke̱ke̱maa mɔ ɔɔ ba fo̱ ase̱ bo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii fo̱ fe̱yɛ ɔɔ waa bɔye̱, a tiri fe̱yɛ fo̱ taa bo̱ ke mò̱.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi du‑o

ref: No, you wouldn't say that. You would say, “Prepare me something to eat. Get ready to serve me, so I can have my meal. Then later on you can eat and drink.”
text: Daabii, fo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ a fo̱ gywii mò̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Kyo̱ŋ ya de̱ŋa mo̱ ateese, na fo̱ taa baa mo̱ pwɛɛ, ne̱ mo̱ ya gyi lo̱we̱, na ŋkee fo̱ dɛɛ nya kpa ya waa fo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ateese.’

ref: And that's how it should be with you. When you've done all you should, then say, “We are merely servants, and we have simply done our duty.” Ten Men with Leprosy
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ gbaa mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ de. Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ mo̱ne̱ ya waa ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ nyaŋpe̱ Wuribware̱ yɛ mo̱nꞌ waa‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘A mo̱ŋ tiri fe̱yɛ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ sa ane̱ aŋsɛ na kusuŋ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii ane̱‑ɔ dooo ne̱ ane̱ a waa.’ ” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya abwatɔpo̱ kudu ko̱‑ɔ

ref: On his way to Jerusalem, Jesus went along the border between Samaria and Galilee.
text: To, saŋ ne̱ Yeesuu maa se̱ ɔ yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱rɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Samariya na Galile̱ya e̱swe̱e̱re̱ e̱ gyaŋŋe̱‑ɔ.

ref: As he was going into a village, ten men with leprosy came toward him. They stood at a distance
text: Saŋ ne̱ ɔ kpa a o lwee kamaŋgyii ko̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ abwatɔpo̱ kudu ko̱ a tɔ mò̱‑rɔ, ne̱ baa ye̱re̱ ke̱fɔ.

ref: and shouted, “Jesus, Master, have pity on us!”
text: Ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ keŋkeŋ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu, ŋu ane̱ e̱wɛɛ.”

ref: When one of them discovered that he was healed, he came back, shouting praises to God.
text: Amɔ bamo̱ ɔko̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ ɔɔ kpaare̱‑ɔ, ne̱ oo kiŋŋi ɔ ba, na ɔ kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱ keŋkeŋ.

ref: He bowed down at the feet of Jesus and thanked him. The man was from the country of Samaria.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ kpuni aŋurii Yeesuu ayaa‑rɔ sa mò̱ aŋsɛ na ko̱waa. Samariyanyi e̱ gye̱ ɔnyare̱ amo̱.

ref: Jesus asked, “Weren't ten men healed? Where are the other nine?
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Ase̱sɛ kudu ko̱lɔ ne̱ mo̱ a kya, ne̱ ŋke̱e̱ akpanɔɔ ne̱ baa saŋ‑ɔ?

ref: Then Jesus told the man, “You may get up and go. Your faith has made you well.” God's Kingdom (Matthew
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ko̱so̱, nare̱ dame̱naŋsɛ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ a kɔɔre̱ mo̱ gyi‑o si ne̱ fo̱ a kpaare̱.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ ba na ɔ bo̱ gyi mò̱ kuwure‑o (Matiyo

ref: When people say to you, “Look there,” or “Look here,” don't go looking for him.
text: Bo̱ko̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ a bo̱ gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱re̱ nno̱ŋ’ bɛɛɛ ‘Mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱re̱ mfe̱e̱,’ mo̱ne̱ ma se̱re̱ gya bamo̱ se̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ de̱e̱re̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa.

ref: Then Jesus' disciples spoke up, “But where will this happen, Lord?” Jesus said, “Where there is a corpse, there will always be vultures.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱, mfe̱ne̱ ne̱ amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ e̱ waa?” Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ, “Ɔko̱ ya buwi kpa bamo̱ ne̱ baa mɔɔ bamo̱‑ɔ afuniŋ, o ŋu apate̱yɛ pwɛɛ.”

ref: But first he must suffer terribly and be rejected by the people of today.
text: Amaa pwɛɛ na saŋ‑ɔ dɛɛ fo̱‑ɔ, mo̱ i ŋu ase̱ŋ, na ndɔɔ a kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ase̱sɛ kine mo̱.

ref: When the Son of Man comes, things will be just as they were when Noah lived.
text: Ane̱ŋ ne̱ aa dɛɛ waa ane̱ ɔde̱daapo̱ Nowaa mbe̱e̱ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ saŋ ne̱ mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, e̱ ba‑ɔ e̱ waa.

ref: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day when Noah went into the big boat. Then the flood came and drowned everyone on earth.
text: Saŋ amo̱‑ɔ ɔke̱maa maa se̱ o gyi ateese na o nuu nta, na anyare̱ na akye̱e̱ e̱ waare̱e̱ abɛɛ ane̱ŋ‑aaa, na ba fa fe̱yɛ sɛye̱ maa waa bamo̱, ya bo̱ fo̱ kake ne̱ Nowaa a lwee mò̱ ko̱re̱e̱ dabe̱ dabe̱ ne̱ ɔɔ waa‑ɔ, ne̱ kufwiiri‑o a bo̱ mɔɔ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: But on the very day Lot left Sodom, fiery flames poured down from the sky and killed everyone.
text: Ke̱mo̱ kake ne̱ Lɔt a de̱ŋ le̱e̱ bamo̱ maŋ amo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ ne̱ de̱e̱kpa na kafirewuta a le̱e̱ so̱so̱ fe̱yɛ bware̱‑ɔ bo̱ mɔɔ maŋ amo̱ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ.

ref: The same will happen on the day when the Son of Man appears.
text: Kake ne̱ mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, e̱ ba‑ɔ, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ke̱tɔ e̱ waa‑ɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: At that time no one on a rooftop should go down into the house to get anything. No one in a field should go back to the house for anything.
text: Kake amo̱ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ bo̱ kawu‑o ma kpo̱ne̱ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ kiŋŋi ba lɔŋ‑nɔ fe̱yɛ ɔ ba a ɔ bo̱ swii mò̱ atɔ. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ ndɔɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ ma kpo̱ne̱ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ kiŋŋi yɔ pe̱.

ref: Remember what happened to Lot's wife.
text: Mo̱nꞌ nyiŋŋi Lɔt mò̱ ka na lowi suyo ne̱ oo wu‑o si.

ref: People who try to save their lives will lose them, and those who lose their lives will save them.
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ kpa a ɔ me̱raa se̱ kye̱na mò̱ kakye̱na ne̱ o te mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ maa nya ŋkpa. Amaa ɔke̱maa ne̱ o kperi mo̱ se̱ na o wu‑o e̱ nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: On that night two people will be sleeping in the same bed, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
text: Mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ kake amo̱ ne̱ mo̱ i kiŋŋi a m ba‑ɔ kanye ase̱sɛ anyɔ da ke̱kyaŋ ko̱ŋko̱‑rɔ ba di idi, na m bo̱ lee ɔko̱ŋko̱ yaa mo̱ ase̱ yɔwe̱ ɔko̱ŋko̱ mɔ bo̱ sii.

ref: Two women will be together grinding wheat, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
text: Na anyare̱ anyɔ bo̱ ndɔɔ‑rɔ ba dɔɔ, na m bo̱ lee ɔko̱ŋko̱ yɔwe̱ mò̱ bɛɛko̱‑ɔ bo̱ sii.]”

ref: Jesus told his disciples a story about how they should keep on praying and never give up:
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ kitee mɔ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ de̱e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ saŋ ke̱maa, na bo̱ ma yɔwe̱.

ref: In a town there was once a judge who didn't fear God or care about people.
text: Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “N gye̱ ɔse̱ŋgyipo̱ ko̱ ya dɛɛ kye̱na maŋ ko̱ se̱ aaa? Ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ se̱re̱ Wuribware̱, ne̱ mò̱‑ɔ mò̱ maa bu se̱sɛ ke̱maa.

ref: In that same town there was a widow who kept going to the judge and saying, “Make sure that I get fair treatment in court.”
text: Amɔ kpenlekye̱e̱ ko̱ mɔ bo̱ maŋ amo̱‑rɔ dɛɛ, ne̱ ɔ naa ɔse̱ŋgyipo̱ amo̱ ase̱ saŋ ke̱maa ɔ ko̱re̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ bɛɛko̱ ko̱ a waa mo̱ bɔye̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ fo̱ te̱e̱ mo̱ aa mò̱ na ane̱ tɔwe̱ amo̱ ase̱ŋ.’

ref: The Lord said: Think about what that crooked judge said.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya se̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ nu ase̱ŋ ne̱ apoo ɔse̱ŋgyipo̱ amo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱‑ɔ.

ref: He will surely hurry and help them. But when the Son of Man comes, will he find on this earth anyone with faith? A Pharisee and a Tax Collector
text: Mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ ba me̱naŋ, na ɔ bo̱ kya mò̱ ase̱sɛ‑rɔ, na bo̱ nya bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ le̱e̱ bamo̱ ado̱ŋ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ. Amaa mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, ya ba swe̱e̱re̱ mɔ se̱ nyɔse̱po̱, mo̱ i ŋu fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ saŋ ba gyi Wuribware̱ ba gyi aaa?” Farisiinyi na leŋpoo ɔkɔɔre̱po̱ kitee

ref: Jesus told a story to some people who thought they were better than others and who looked down on everyone else:
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ kitee mɔ gywii bamo̱ ne̱ ba fa fe̱yɛ bamo̱ wo̱re̱ e̱ gye̱ ase̱sɛ timaa, ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ bo̱ de ɔko̱ ba te̱e̱ sɛye̱‑ɔ.

ref: The Pharisee stood over by himself and prayed, “God, I thank you that I am not greedy, dishonest, and unfaithful in marriage like other people. And I am really glad that I am not like that tax collector over there.
text: Ne̱ Farisiinyi‑o a ye̱re̱ mò̱ ko̱kwe̱e̱, ne̱ ɔɔ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Wuribware̱, mo̱ e̱ sa fo̱ aŋsɛ na kusuŋ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a mo̱ŋ gye̱ ogyimɔre̱po̱ na apoonyi na ɔkye̱e̱kpapo̱ fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa‑ɔ. Mo̱ e̱ sa fo̱ aŋsɛ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a mo̱ŋ du fe̱yɛ leŋpoo ɔkɔɔre̱po̱ mɔ ne̱ ɔ ye̱re̱ nno̱ŋ‑ɔ.

ref: I go without eating for two days a week, and I give you one tenth of all I earn.”
text: Ŋke nsunoo ke̱maa‑rɔ mo̱ e̱ kra ako̱ŋ ŋke ŋnyɔ, mo̱ i lee ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ a nya‑ɔ‑rɔ ntuŋkare̱ kudu‑ro katuŋkare̱ ko̱ŋko̱ bo̱ lɔŋŋɔ fo̱, Wuribware̱.’

ref: Then Jesus said, “When the two men went home, it was the tax collector and not the Pharisee who was pleasing to God. If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.” Jesus Blesses Little Children (Matthew
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ bo̱ gye̱ kɛɛ fe̱yɛ, “Leŋpoo ɔkɔɔre̱po̱‑ɔ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ ya gyi Wuribware̱ akatɔ, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa ne̱ o bu mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ke̱dabe̱‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ e̱ baa mò̱ kaase̱, ne̱ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ baa mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ kaase̱‑ɔ mɔ, Wuribware̱ e̱ sa mò̱ ke̱dabe̱.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a yure ŋyaagyi‑o (Matiyo

ref: Some people brought their little children for Jesus to bless. But when his disciples saw them doing this, they told the people to stop bothering him.
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ ko̱ a baa bamo̱ ŋyaagyi Yeesuu ase̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ ba mò̱ asare̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ bamo̱ se̱ yure bamo̱. Amɔ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a ŋu ane̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa kpa fe̱yɛ bo̱ gya bamo̱.

ref: Jesus took the twelve apostles aside and said: We are now on our way to Jerusalem. Everything that the prophets wrote about the Son of Man will happen there.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee mò̱ agyase̱po̱ kudu anyɔ‑ɔ keri si, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ nu mo̱ ase̱. Ane̱ e̱ yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ ne̱e̱, mfe̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, ase̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ a kyo̱rɛɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ e̱ ba‑rɔ kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o.

ref: An important man asked Jesus, “Good Teacher, what must I do to have eternal life?”
text: Ne̱ Yudaa awuye ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ ko̱ a bise Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Ɔkaapo̱po̱ timaa, e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa, na ŋ nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ ne̱e̱?”

ref: He told Jesus, “I have obeyed all these commandments since I was a young man.”
text: Ne̱ ɔnyare̱ amo̱ a be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Le̱e̱ mo̱ ŋyaagyi‑ro ne̱ mo̱ a le̱e̱ gya Wuribware̱ a mbraa mɔ pɛɛɛ se̱ bo̱ fo̱ ndɔɔ.”

ref: When Jesus heard this, he said, “There is one thing you still need to do. Go and sell everything you own! Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come and be my follower.”
text: Yeesuu a nu ane̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Ke̱tɔ ko̱ŋko̱ ya saŋ fo̱ a fo̱ waa, na fo̱ ya wu na fo̱ kii atɔ wuye Wuribware̱ se̱. Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ ya fe fo̱ kapo̱tɛɛ pɛɛɛ, na fo̱ taa atanne̱‑ɔ sa atiripo̱, na fo̱ bo̱ gya mo̱ se̱.”

ref: When the man heard this, he was sad, because he was very rich.
text: Amaa ɔnyare̱ amo̱ a nu mfaanɛɛ‑ɔ, a mo̱ŋ waa mò̱ kɔne̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ o de kapo̱tɛɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: Jesus saw how sad the man was. So he said, “It's terribly hard for rich people to get into God's kingdom!
text: Yeesuu a ŋu fe̱yɛ a mo̱ŋ waa ɔnyare̱ amo̱ kɔne̱, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “A waa le̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ fe̱yɛ atɔ wuye a sure si fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ gye̱ mò̱ owure.

ref: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
text: Nyɔma e̱ taare̱ a o lwee basa bɔ‑rɔ dare̱e̱ aaa? Ma kɔɔre̱ a n gyi. Amaa atɔ wuye kusure si fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ gye̱ mò̱ owure bo̱ le̱ŋ ko̱ kyɔ nyɔma kulwee basa bɔ‑rɔ dare̱e̱.”

ref: Jesus replied, “There are some things that people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ, “Se̱sɛ fe̱raa maa taare̱ a ɔ nya ane̱ŋ a ŋkpa‑ɔ, amɔ Wuribware̱ a sa mò̱ kpa.”

ref: Peter said, “Remember, we left everything to be your followers!”
text: Ne̱ Peetroo yɛ, “Ke̱e̱ ɛ, ane̱ a yɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ane̱ gya fo̱ se̱.”

ref: He will be handed over to foreigners, who will make fun of him, mistreat him, and spit on him.
text: Ba taa mo̱ a bo̱ bo̱ sa Roma awuye ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ ane̱ maŋ‑ɔ, na bo̱ mɔse̱ mo̱, na bo̱ saare̱ mo̱, na bo̱ twe̱e̱ akyɔne̱ we̱e̱ mo̱ se̱,

ref: They will beat him and kill him, but three days later he will rise to life.
text: na bo̱ da mo̱ paara, na bo̱ mɔɔ mo̱. Amaa ke̱mo̱ ŋke nsa, mo̱ i kyiŋŋi a n le̱e̱ lowi‑ro.”

ref: When Jesus was coming close to Jericho, a blind man sat begging beside the road.
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yeesuu e̱ kpa a ɔ fo̱ Yɛrikoo maŋ, na te̱napo̱ nyare̱ ko̱ te kpa kɛɛ ɔ ko̱re̱ atɔ.

ref: The man heard the crowd walking by and asked what was happening.
text: Mò̱ a nu ase̱sɛ biribiri ko̱ e̱ kyo̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ oo bise fe̱yɛ, “Ntɔse̱ŋ ne̱e̱?”

ref: Some people told him that Jesus from Nazareth was passing by.
text: Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Yeesuu Nasarɛte̱nyi‑o e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ kyo̱ŋ.”

ref: So the blind man shouted, “Jesus, Son of David, have pity on me!”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ faa‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, “Yeesuu, Deefid mò̱ nana, ŋu mo̱ e̱wɛɛ.”

ref: The people who were going along with Jesus told the man to be quiet. But he shouted even louder, “Son of David, have pity on me!”
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ‑ɔ e̱ fɛɛ mò̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ laatɔ. Ŋkee gbaa ne̱ ɔ maa se̱ ɔ faa‑rɔ keŋkeŋ fe̱yɛ, “Deefid mò̱ nana, ŋu mo̱ e̱wɛɛ.”

ref: Jesus stopped and told some people to bring the blind man over to him. When the blind man was getting near, Jesus asked,
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa ne̱ Yeesuu a sii ye̱re̱, ne̱ ɔ yɛ bo̱ baa ɔnyare̱‑ɔ mò̱ ase̱. Mò̱ a ba‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu a bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ,

ref: “What do you want me to do for you?” “Lord, I want to see!” he answered.
text: “Nte̱tɔ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ŋ waa sa fo̱?” Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, mo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ fo̱ buŋŋi mo̱ akatɔ, na n de̱e̱ nya ŋu atɔ.”

ref: Jesus replied, “Look and you will see! Your eyes are healed because of your faith.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ akatɔ a buŋŋi. Fo̱ a kɔɔre̱ mo̱ gyi‑o si‑o, fo̱ a kpaare̱.”

ref: At once the man could see, and he went with Jesus and started thanking God. When the crowds saw what happened, they praised God.
text: Puri amo̱‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔnyare̱ amo̱ akatɔ a buŋŋi nɛɛnɛɛ, ne̱ ɔɔ gya Yeesuu si, ne̱ ɔɔ sa Wuribware̱ aŋsɛ. Ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ ne̱ bo̱ gya Yeesuu si‑o a ŋu amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱.

ref: Jesus was heading his way, and Zacchaeus wanted to see what he was like. But Zacchaeus was a short man and could not see over the crowd. So he ran ahead and climbed up into a sycamore tree.
text: Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ ɔɔ ŋwɛɛnaŋ yɔ ase̱sɛ akatɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ ya de̱e̱ kuroŋku kiyii ne̱ ke̱ ye̱re̱ kpa kɛɛ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu e̱ ba a ɔ bo̱ kyo̱ŋ‑ɔ, na ɔ nya ŋu Yeesuu.

ref: When Jesus got there, he looked up and said, “Zacchaeus, hurry down! I want to stay with you today.”
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a bo̱ fo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ de̱e̱re̱ so̱so̱, ne̱ oo ŋu Sakiiyus, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Sakiiyus, waa me̱naŋ kpo̱ro̱we̱, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ aye̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ so̱we̱ ndɔɔ.”

ref: Everyone who saw this started grumbling, “This man Zacchaeus is a sinner! And Jesus is going home to eat with him.”
text: Ne̱ ɔke̱maa ne̱ oo ŋu‑o a le̱e̱ ɔ tɛɛ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔnyare̱ mɔ a ya so̱we̱ nyiŋkpasɛ bɔye̱ mɔ aye̱.”

ref: Later that day Zacchaeus stood up and said to the Lord, “I will give half of my property to the poor. And I will now pay back four times as much to everyone I have ever cheated.”
text: Ne̱ Sakiiyus a ko̱so̱ ye̱re̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ akatɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, nu mfe̱e̱. Mo̱ e̱ taa mo̱ kapo̱tɛɛ‑ɔ ke̱be̱gya na m bo̱ ke atiripo̱. Ne̱ mo̱ ya gyi ɔko̱ kɔɔre̱ atanne̱ kyo̱ŋ amo̱ ne̱ a bware‑o, mo̱ i kiŋŋaa ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ a gyi mò̱ ke̱mo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ iluwi e̱na na m bo̱ sa se̱sɛ amo̱, ne̱ a kyo̱ŋ amo̱ ne̱ ane̱ mbraa yɛ oyu e̱ ka a ɔ bo̱ teere‑o.”

ref: The Son of Man came to look for and to save people who are lost.” A Story about Ten Servants (Matthew
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, a ba a m bo̱ de̱e̱re̱ kpa abɔye̱waapo̱ ne̱e̱ na m mo̱rɔwe̱ bamo̱.” Ayaafɔre̱ timaa anyɔ na ke̱yaafɔre̱ wo̱rɔgyapo̱ ko̱ kitee (Matiyo

text: Amaa baa be̱ŋŋaa ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱, ɔ kyɔ o de siidii ŋkpe̱ŋ kudu!’]

ref: But the people of his country hated him, and they sent messengers to the foreign country to say, “We don't want this man to be our king.”
text: Amɔ ɔnyare̱ amo̱ aye̱ awuye‑o mɔ i kisi mò̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, saŋ ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa suŋ mbɔɔ bo̱ gya mò̱ se̱, na bo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ ne̱ ba ba mò̱ a bo̱ bo̱ gyi kuwure‑o fe̱yɛ, ‘Ane̱ maa kpa a ɔnyare̱ mɔ waa ane̱ owure.’

ref: The first servant came and said, “Sir, with the money you gave me I have earned ten times as much.”
text: Ne̱ ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛsɛ‑ɔ a bo̱ ye̱re̱, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, ‘Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, mo̱ a ba siidii kakpe̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ a sa mo̱‑ɔ bo̱ nya siidii ŋkpe̱ŋ kudu bo̱ tii si.’

ref: The second one came and said, “Sir, with the money you gave me, I have earned five times as much.”
text: Ne̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱ nyɔse̱po̱‑ɔ mɔ a ba bo̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, mo̱ a ba siidii kakpe̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ a sa mo̱‑ɔ bo̱ nya siidii ŋkpe̱ŋ nnuu bo̱ tii si.’

ref: Why didn't you put my money in the bank? On my return, I could have had the money together with interest.”
text: Ne̱ e̱me̱ne̱ ya waa se̱ ne̱ fo̱ mo̱ŋ taa mo̱ atanne̱‑ɔ yaa bo̱ be̱ya baŋke̱‑rɔ, na mo̱ a kiŋŋi ba‑ɔ, na ŋ nya atanne̱ be̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ se̱?’

ref: Then he said to some other servants standing there, “Take the money away from him and give it to the servant who earned ten times as much.”
text: Ne̱ ŋkee ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ ye̱re̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱nꞌ kɔɔre̱ siidii kakpe̱ŋ ne̱ e̱ bo̱ mò̱ ase̱‑ɔ sa mò̱ ne̱ ɔɔ nya siidii ŋkpe̱ŋ kudu‑o.’ [

ref: The king replied, “Those who have something will be given more. But everything will be taken away from those who don't have anything.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ɔko̱ ne̱ o de ke̱tɔ‑ɔ, ba taa bwe̱e̱tɔ na bo̱ bo̱ tii mò̱ se̱, amaa ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ de‑o fe̱raa, ba kɔɔre̱ kafwe̱e̱ ne̱ o de‑o na bo̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ mò̱ ase̱.

ref: Now bring me the enemies who didn't want me to be their king. Kill them while I watch!” Jesus Enters Jerusalem (Matthew
text: Amaa mo̱ ado̱ŋ‑ɔ fe̱raa ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ kpa fe̱yɛ n gyi kuwure‑o, mo̱nꞌ baa bamo̱ mfe̱e̱, na mo̱nꞌ bo̱ mɔɔ bamo̱ mo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ.’ ” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a de̱e̱ kye̱na kuruma si lwee Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: When Jesus had finished saying all this, he went on toward Jerusalem.
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ kya se̱ ɔ yɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ gye̱ ase̱sɛ ŋkpɛɛ ɔ yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ.

ref: If anyone asks why you are doing this, just say, ‘The Lord needs it.’ ”
text: Ɔko̱ ya bise mo̱ne̱ ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ saŋŋe̱ kuruma‑o, na mo̱nꞌ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ kpa.’ ”

ref: They went off and found everything just as Jesus had said.
text: Bamo̱ a yɔ‑ɔ, ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a de̱ŋ kaapo̱ bamo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa ya ŋu.

ref: While they were untying the donkey, its owners asked, “Why are you doing that?”
text: Saŋ ne̱ bo̱ maa se̱ ba saŋŋe̱ kuruma‑o, ne̱ mò̱ wuye‑ana a bise fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ saŋŋe̱ kuruma‑o?”

ref: They answered, “The Lord needs it.”
text: Ne̱ baa be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ kpa.”

ref: Then they led the donkey to Jesus. They put some of their clothes on its back and helped Jesus get on.
text: Ne̱ baa taa mò̱ yaa Yeesuu. Ne̱ baa buŋŋi bamo̱ awaagya bo̱ buŋ kuruma‑o si, ne̱ baa kra Yeesuu‑ro, ne̱ ɔɔ de̱e̱ kye̱na se̱.

ref: And as he rode along, the people spread clothes on the road in front of him.
text: Yeesuu a de̱e̱ kuruma‑o ɔ yɔ‑ɔ, na ase̱sɛ e̱ brawe̱ bamo̱ awaagya‑rɔ ba be̱ya kabo̱re̱‑ɔ se̱ na ɔ nare̱ se̱ kyo̱ŋ.

ref: When Jesus started down the Mount of Olives, his large crowd of disciples were happy and praised God because of all the miracles they had seen.
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a fo̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ kpa‑ɔ bɔ Mfɔ-Ayii ke̱be̱e̱‑ɔ kaase̱, ne̱ ɔ kpo̱ro̱we̱‑rɔ ɔ yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ ase̱sɛ de̱maŋte̱ ne̱ bo̱ gya mò̱ se̱‑ɔ a le̱e̱ ba sa Wuribware̱ aŋsɛ, na ba kyo̱rɔ mò̱ keŋkeŋ bo̱ le̱e̱ akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ baa ŋu‑o pɛɛɛ se̱.

ref: They shouted, “Blessed is the king who comes in the name of the Lord! Peace in heaven and glory to God.”
text: Ba fɛɛ‑rɔ keŋkeŋ ba tɔwe̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “ ‘Wuribware̱ yure owure ne̱ ɔ ba mò̱ ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ‑ɔ.’ Ke̱tɔ ke̱maa a yuri Wuribware̱ se̱. Mò̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ nya ke̱dabe̱ na wuraa ooo!”

ref: When Jesus came closer and could see Jerusalem, he cried
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔ‑rɔ Yɛro̱salɛm, ne̱ oo ŋu ane̱ŋ ne̱ maŋ‑ɔ du‑o, ne̱ oo su sa maŋ‑ɔ ase̱sɛ.

ref: and said: It is too bad that today your people don't know what will bring them peace! Now it is hidden from them.
text: Na ɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Yɛro̱salɛm awuye, mo̱ne̱ dɛɛ mo̱ne̱ nyi kpa ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ bo̱rɔ se̱ na mo̱nꞌ nya kaye̱e̱yuri kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱e̱ ooo, weetee ase̱ŋ maa to̱ mo̱ne̱. Amaa mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi kpa amo̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ.

ref: Jerusalem, the time will come when your enemies will build walls around you to attack you. Armies will surround you and close in on you from every side.
text: Saŋ ko̱ e̱ ba ne̱ mo̱ne̱ ado̱ŋ e̱ ba, na bo̱ bo̱ tii mo̱ne̱ e̱kpa, na bo̱ kyaa muruwaa mo̱ne̱ bo̱ sii nse̱na.

ref: They will level you to the ground and kill your people. Not one stone in your buildings will be left on top of another. This will happen because you did not see that God had come to save you. Jesus in the Temple (Matthew
text: Na bo̱ bo̱ kpuri mo̱ne̱ pɛɛɛ mɔ. Bo̱ maa yɔwe̱ mo̱ne̱ agyi gbaa. Na bo̱ bwee mo̱ne̱ akyaŋ pɛɛɛ. Ke̱fo̱re̱ serisɛ ko̱ŋko̱ gbaa maa saŋ na ke̱ ye̱re̱ ke̱mo̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ a ye̱re̱kpa‑ɔ, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ pini fe̱yɛ ndɔɔ Wuribware̱ a ba, na ɔ bo̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ mo̱ne̱.” Ke̱tɔ ne̱ Yeesuu a waa na ɔ bo̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: He told them, “The Scriptures say, ‘My house should be a place of worship.’ But you have made it a place where robbers hide!”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Baa kyo̱rɛɛ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ yɛ, ‘Mo̱ lɔŋ‑ɔ i kii ke̱kyaŋ ne̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ e̱ ba a bo̱ bo̱ nya ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱‑ɔ.’ Amaa mo̱ne̱ a taa mfe̱ŋ bo̱ kii ‘ayu lɔŋ ne̱e̱’ bɛɛɛ?”

ref: Each day, Jesus kept on teaching in the temple. So the chief priests, the teachers of the Law of Moses, and some other important people tried to have him killed.
text: To, kake ke̱maa na Yeesuu naa Wuribware̱ suŋkpa a kabuno‑o ɔ kaapo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ. Ne̱ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ na Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ na maŋ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ e̱ kpa a bo̱ mɔɔ mò̱.

ref: But they could not find a way to do it, because everyone else was eager to listen to him.
text: Amaa bo̱ mo̱ŋ nya kpa, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ mɔ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ ba nu ase̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ tɔwe̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱, na bo̱ maa kpa fe̱yɛ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ ko̱ko̱ŋko̱ gbaa e̱ kyo̱ŋ bamo̱ se̱ na bo̱ mo̱ŋ nu.

ref: The spies said to him, “Teacher, we know you teach the truth about what God wants people to do. And you treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are.
text: Ne̱ akyɔŋŋɔpo̱ amo̱ a yɔ Yeesuu ase̱. Ne̱ baa bo̱ tɔwe̱ kɔne̱ kɔne̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔkaapo̱po̱, ane̱ nyi fe̱yɛ ase̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ na fo̱ e̱ kaapo̱‑ɔ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ. Fo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ se̱sɛ mɔ gye̱ se̱sɛ dabe̱ bɛɛɛ ɔ gye̱ se̱sɛ kagyingyii, fo̱ fe̱raa fo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ atɔ kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ se̱sɛ dimaadi ke̱maa waa‑ɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: They talked this over and said to each other, “We can't say God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John.
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ wo̱re̱ wo̱re̱ a le̱e̱ ba gyiiri ikii. Bo̱ yɛ, “E̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ ane̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱? Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ ya tɔwe̱ ɛ Wuribware̱ ya sa Yohanee kpa a ɔ bɔ asuu, o bise ane̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱e̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ Yohanee gyi?’

ref: And we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize. The crowd will stone us to death, because they think John was a prophet.”
text: Ne̱ ane̱ mɔ ane̱ ya tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ dimaadi ya sa mò̱ kpa, ase̱sɛ mɔ e̱ twe̱e̱ ane̱ abu, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ baa kɔɔre̱ gyi fe̱yɛ Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱‑ɔ gye̱ Wuribware̱ a akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ ɔko̱ ne̱e̱.”

ref: So they told Jesus, “We don't know who gave John the right to baptize.”
text: Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa be̱ŋŋaa Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ mo̱ŋ nyi ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ sa Yohanee kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ bɔ asuu‑o.”

ref: Jesus replied, “Then I won't tell you who gave me the right to do what I do.” Renters of a Vineyard (Matthew
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu mɔ yɛ, “Ane̱ŋ fe̱raa ŋ gbaa ma tɔwe̱ ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ sa mo̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ŋ waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa‑ɔ a n gywii mo̱ne̱.” Apafo̱wɔ bɔye̱ ko̱ kitee (Matiyo

ref: Guard against the teachers of the Law of Moses! They love to walk around in long robes, and they like to be greeted in the market. They want the front seats in the synagogues and the best seats at banquets.
text: “Mo̱nꞌ sa Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ se̱. Bamo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ bo̱ naa bo̱ buŋ e̱kaare̱ so̱swe̱e̱ lamaŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ ba kpa fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ de̱e̱ nya gyɔŋŋɔ ka bamo̱ nnɔ, ne̱ bo̱ te ngya dabe̱ se̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ akyaŋ‑nɔ na ateese gyikpa.

ref: When the renters saw the owner's son, they said to one another, “Someday he will own the vineyard. Let's kill him! Then we can have it all for ourselves.”
text: Saŋ ne̱ apafo̱wɔ amo̱ a ŋu kayaagyi‑o e̱ ba‑ɔ, ne̱ ŋkee ba tɔwe̱ ba gywii abɛɛ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ke̱e̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ wuye‑o mò̱ gyi‑o ne̱ mò̱ se̱ ya wu o gyi mò̱ kapo̱tɛɛ‑ɔ e̱ ba‑ɔ. Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ mɔɔ mò̱, na mò̱ se̱ a ko̱dɔɔ mɔ kii ane̱ lee.’

ref: I'll tell you what. He will come and kill those renters and let someone else have his vineyard.” When the people heard this, they said, “This must never happen!”
text: Mo̱ e̱ fa fe̱yɛ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ e̱ yɔ ne̱e̱ na ɔ ya mɔɔ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ, na ɔ be̱e̱ taa ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ sa ase̱sɛ baŋbaŋ, na bo̱ de̱e̱re̱ se̱ sa mò̱.” Amɔ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ a nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱‑ɔ, ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Daabii. A mo̱ŋ bo̱ daŋ.”

ref: The chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses knew that Jesus was talking about them when he was telling this story. They wanted to arrest him right then, but they were afraid of the people. Paying Taxes (Matthew
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ na Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ e̱ kpa a bo̱ kra Yeesuu mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ kitee‑o, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ baa ŋu fe̱yɛ ɔɔ ba kitee‑o bo̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ du fe̱yɛ apafo̱wɔ bɔye̱ amo̱‑ɔ. Amaa bamo̱ a se̱re̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, bo̱ mo̱ŋ taare̱.

ref: Jesus' enemies kept watching him closely, because they wanted to hand him over to the Roman governor. So they sent some men who pretended to be good. But they were really spies trying to catch Jesus saying something wrong.
text: Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ ba buwi ba kpa Yeesuu e̱kpa. Ne̱ baa suŋ ase̱sɛ ko̱, na bo̱ waa fe̱yɛ bo̱ gye̱ ase̱sɛ timaa‑o, na bo̱ yɔ Yeesuu ase̱, na bo̱ ya bise mò̱ ase̱ŋ lɔɔre̱ mò̱ kanɔ‑rɔ, na ɔ tɔwe̱ ke̱bɔye̱ ko̱, na bo̱ nya kra mò̱ yaa bo̱ sa gominaa‑o.

ref: Then he told them, “Give the Emperor what belongs to him and give God what belongs to God.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “To, amo̱ fe̱raa mo̱nꞌ ka ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ gye̱ Kaye̱saa lee‑o sa Kaye̱saa, na mo̱nꞌ ka ke̱mo̱ ne̱ ke̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ lee‑o sa Wuribware̱.”

ref: Jesus' enemies could not catch him saying anything wrong there in front of the people. They were amazed at his answer and kept quiet. Life in the Future World (Matthew
text: Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a lee bamo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ kanɔ bo̱rɔ‑ɔ a sa ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ bamo̱. Bo̱ mo̱ŋ taare̱ waa mò̱ ko̱tɔko̱ na ɔ nya tɔwe̱ ke̱bɔye̱ ko̱ lamaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ, na bo̱ nya ye̱re̱ ke̱mo̱ se̱ bo̱ po̱rɔ mò̱. Ne̱ baa laatɔ. Kikyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro se̱ŋbise (Matiyo

ref: and said: Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.
text: “Ɔkaapo̱po̱, ane̱ ɔde̱daapo̱ Mosis ya kyo̱rɛɛ mbraa mɔ sa ane̱ fe̱yɛ, ɔko̱ ya wu yɔwe̱ mò̱ ka na bo̱ mo̱ŋ ko̱we̱ agyi, se̱sɛ‑ɔ mò̱ tire taa kpenlekye̱e̱‑ɔ waare̱e̱, na bo̱ nya ko̱we̱ kayaagyi, na bo̱ taa kamo̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ daa ne̱ oo wu‑o mò̱ gyi ne̱e̱.

ref: There were once seven brothers. The first one married, but died without having any children.
text: To, ɔnyare̱ ko̱ agyi nyaŋsɛ asunoo e̱ dɛɛ bo̱ bo̱‑rɔ. Ne̱ bamo̱‑rɔ ɔbre̱sɛ‑ɔ a waare̱e̱, ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ wu yɔwe̱ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ, na mò̱ aa mò̱ mo̱ŋ ko̱we̱.

ref: The second one married his brother's widow, and he also died without having any children.
text: Ne̱ ɔnyɔse̱po̱‑ɔ a bo̱ taa mò̱ be̱e̱ waare̱e̱, na mò̱ aa mò̱ be̱e̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ ko̱we̱, ne̱ ɔnyare̱‑ɔ a bo̱ wu.

ref: The same thing happened to the third one. Finally, all seven brothers married this woman and died without having any children.
text: Ɔsase̱po̱‑ɔ gbaa ane̱ŋ dɛɛ. Bamo̱ asunoo‑o pɛɛɛ a waare̱e̱ bamo̱ daa a kpenlekye̱e̱ amo̱ gyaŋŋe̱ se̱, ne̱ baa wu yɔwe̱ mò̱, na mò̱ aa bamo̱ ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ ko̱we̱ gyi ko̱ŋko̱ gbaa.

ref: Jesus answered: The people in this world get married.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ndɔɔ a kaye̱‑ɔ anyare̱ na akye̱e̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ba waare̱e̱.

ref: Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses said, “Teacher, you have given a good answer!”
text: Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ yɛ, “Ɔkaapo̱po̱, fo̱ a lee kanɔ nɛɛnɛɛ.”

ref: While everyone was listening to Jesus, he said to his disciples:
text: Na ase̱sɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ saŋ ba nu ke̱tɔ ne̱ Yeesuu e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ,

ref: But they cheat widows out of their homes and then pray long prayers just to show off. These teachers will be punished most of all.
text: Bamo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ba puni akpenlekye̱e̱ ba kɔɔre̱ bamo̱ e̱lɔŋ, na ba ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ ane̱ŋ‑aaa na ase̱sɛ nya ŋu saŋ amo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ tɛɛ bo̱ gya Wuribware̱ se̱ nɛɛnɛɛ. Mfaanɛɛ a ase̱sɛ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ i gyiiri ke̱se̱bɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ.”

ref: Before all this happens, you will be arrested and punished. You will be tried in your synagogues and put in jail. Because of me you will be placed on trial before kings and governors.
text: Amaa pwɛɛ na atɔ mɔ pɛɛɛ dɛɛ waa‑ɔ, ba kperi ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ gye̱ mo̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ se̱, na bo̱ daye̱ mo̱ne̱, na bo̱ waa mo̱ne̱ awo̱re̱fɔɔ. Na bo̱ kra mo̱ne̱ yaa ane̱ Yudaa awuye ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ akyaŋ abre̱sɛ ase̱, na bo̱ tii mo̱ne̱. Na bo̱ po̱rɔ mo̱ne̱ awure na agominaa akatɔ‑rɔ.

ref: Some people were talking about the beautiful stones used to build the temple and about the gifts that had been placed in it. Jesus said,
text: Yeesuu mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a maa se̱ bo̱ bo̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa a kabuno‑o, ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ a tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ bo̱ daŋ‑ɔ ase̱ŋ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ ko̱ a taa afo̱re̱ dame̱naŋsɛ bo̱ sa Wuribware̱, na apwɛɛpo̱‑ɔ bo̱ pwɛɛ mò̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ na ɔ nya waa daŋ.

ref: “Do you see these stones? The time is coming when not one of them will be left in place. They will all be knocked down.” Warning about Trouble (Matthew
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ mɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a ŋu‑o, saŋ ko̱ e̱ ba a ɔ bo̱ ba, na afo̱re̱ amo̱ ne̱ baa bo̱ pwɛɛ akyaŋ amo̱‑ɔ ko̱ŋko̱ gbaa maa sii mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ke̱ ye̱re̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ. Ba gye̱ra amo̱ pɛɛɛ a bo̱ bo̱ da.” Ase̱ŋ ne̱ a ba pwɛɛ na kaye̱ dɛɛ gye̱ kɛɛ‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: When you hear about wars and riots, don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but this isn't the end.
text: Saŋ amo̱ mo̱ne̱ ya nu e̱kɔ na anaa se̱, mo̱ne̱ ma sa a kufu nya mo̱ne̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ a waa kanɔ ŋke̱maa a ba ane̱ŋ pwɛɛ, amaa n gye̱ amo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ a kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ kaye̱ e̱ kpa a ka gye̱ kɛɛ.

ref: Nations will go to war against one another, and kingdoms will attack each other.
text: Mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ e̱maŋ e̱ kɔ abɛɛ na swe̱e̱re̱ ko̱ se̱ ase̱sɛ kɔ swe̱e̱re̱ ko̱ se̱ mɔ ase̱sɛ.

ref: There will be great earthquakes, and in many places people will starve to death and suffer terrible diseases. All sorts of frightening things will be seen in the sky.
text: Swe̱e̱re̱ e̱ le̱e̱ a ɔ le̱ŋkpaŋ, na ako̱ŋ ba, na alɔgyi e̱wo̱re̱se̱ e̱wo̱re̱se̱ ba e̱swe̱e̱re̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ se̱, na mo̱nꞌ ŋu afuse̱ŋ na afutɔ a le̱e̱ awo̱re̱‑rɔ a ba.

ref: You will be betrayed by your own parents, brothers, family, and friends. Some of you will even be killed.
text: Mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ ako̱we̱po̱ na adaa na atire na ako̱we̱bɛɛ na anyare̱ e̱ taa mo̱ne̱ a bo̱ sa a bo̱ mɔɔ mo̱ne̱ bo̱ko̱.

ref: Because of me, you will be hated by everyone.
text: Ase̱sɛ i kperi mo̱ se̱, na bo̱ kisi mo̱ne̱.

ref: If you are living in Judea at this time, run to the mountains. If you are in the city, leave it. And if you are out in the country, don't go back into the city.
text: Saŋ amo̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Yude̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ, a tiri fe̱yɛ bo̱ se̱re̱ yɔ abe̱e̱‑rɔ, na bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ se̱re̱ le̱e̱, na bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ mɔ ma lɛɛ kiŋŋi yɔ mfe̱ŋ.

ref: When you see Jerusalem surrounded by soldiers, you will know that it will soon be destroyed.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Saŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ ya ŋu asoogyaa awuye a muruwaa Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ, amo̱ fe̱raa mo̱nꞌ pini fe̱yɛ a saŋ kafwe̱e̱ na maŋ‑ɔ kpuri.

ref: This time of punishment is what is written about in the Scriptures.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ saŋ amo̱ e̱ gye̱ ke̱se̱bɔgyiiri ŋke ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ mmo̱ ase̱ŋ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: It will be an awful time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children! Everywhere in the land people will suffer horribly and be punished.
text: Saŋ amo̱ adaŋpo̱kye̱e̱ na atrapo̱ i ŋu ase̱ŋ ne̱e̱ fɛɛ. Swe̱e̱re̱ amo̱ se̱ ase̱sɛ ase̱ŋ e̱ waa e̱wɛɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ, na Wuribware̱ be̱e̱ gyiiri ase̱sɛ‑ɔ ke̱se̱bɔ.

ref: Then Jesus told them a story: When you see a fig tree or any other tree
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ sa a figi kiyii‑o kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ kanyiase̱ŋ.

ref: So, when you see these things happening, you know that God's kingdom will soon be here.
text: To, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ ya ŋu fe̱yɛ atɔ mɔ e̱ waa, na mo̱nꞌ pini fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kuwure‑o saŋ e̱ kpa a ke̱ fo̱.

ref: Then the Son of Man will be seen, coming in a cloud with power and great glory.
text: To, na mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, le̱e̱ so̱so̱ n te awo̱re̱‑rɔ mo̱ e̱ ba ke̱yaale̱ŋ‑nɔ na ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ dabe̱‑rɔ.

ref: Strange things will happen to the sun, moon, and stars. The nations on earth will be afraid of the roaring sea and tides, and they won't know what to do.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya se̱ fe̱yɛ, “Saŋ amo̱‑ɔ ane̱ŋ ne̱ kyɔwe̱ na akye̱e̱‑e̱‑kpa‑agyi i kyurowi‑o i puruwe mo̱ne̱. Na swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ mɔ e̱maŋ ke̱maa se̱ ase̱sɛ ase̱ŋ waa e̱wɛɛ, na ɔpo̱o̱ kusu na mò̱ alaŋkpare̱ dabe̱ dabe̱‑ɔ sa a kufu nya ɔke̱maa.

ref: People will be so frightened that they will faint because of what is happening to the world. Every power in the sky will be shaken.
text: Ŋkee fe̱raa ase̱sɛ ya ŋu fe̱yɛ awo̱re̱‑rɔ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa a kyurowi ke̱mo̱ kye̱nakpa na nare̱kpa‑ɔ, ba ye̱re̱ a bo̱ gywii kaye̱ a ka gye̱ kɛɛ, na amo̱ kufu sa a bo̱ kiriŋ.

ref: You can be sure that some of the people of this generation will still be alive when all of this takes place.
text: Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ ndɔɔ a kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ i ŋu saŋ ne̱ atɔ mɔ e̱ ba‑ɔ.

ref: putting out leaves, you know that summer will soon come.
text: Mo̱ne̱ nyi fe̱yɛ ke̱mo̱ ya ba ke̱ e̱ kpa a ke̱ pɔwe̱, amɔ ke̱kyaare̱ e̱ kpa a ke̱ fo̱.

ref: The sky and the earth won't last forever, but my words will. A Warning
text: So̱so̱ na swe̱e̱re̱ pɛɛɛ ase̱ŋ e̱ kyo̱ŋ, amaa mo̱ ase̱ŋ fe̱raa maa kyo̱ŋ daa.” Ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ nyi kake ne̱ Yeesuu i kiŋŋi a ɔ ba‑ɔ

ref: Watch out and keep praying that you can escape all that is going to happen and that the Son of Man will be pleased with you.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱nꞌ waa siraa, na mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ saŋ ke̱maa fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ lwee ase̱ŋ mɔ‑rɔ le̱e̱, na sɛye̱ ma waa mo̱ne̱, na mo̱nꞌ be̱e̱ nya taare̱ ye̱re̱ mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, akatɔ‑rɔ kake amo̱ na kufu mo̱ŋ de mo̱ne̱.”

ref: Jesus taught in the temple each day, and he spent each night on the Mount of Olives.
text: Ŋke amo̱ saŋ ke̱maa Yeesuu naa Wuribware̱ suŋkpa a kabuno‑o ɔ kaapo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ, na amo̱ ya fo̱ ke̱de̱e̱pwɛta‑ɔ na ɔ ya di Mfɔ-Ayii ke̱be̱e̱‑ɔ se̱.

ref: Everyone got up early and came to the temple to hear him teach.
text: Na gye̱gyaye̱ tututu ke̱maa ase̱sɛ‑ɔ e̱ gyaŋŋaa mò̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa a kabuno‑o ba nu ase̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ kaapo̱‑ɔ.

ref: They were very pleased and offered to pay Judas some money.
text: Amo̱ a waa bamo̱ kɔne̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ bo̱ yɛ ba ba atanne̱ a bo̱ ka mò̱ ko̱kɔ.

ref: Jesus told them, “As you go into the city, you will meet a man carrying a jar of water. Follow him into the house
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ya yɔ mo̱ne̱ i lwee maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ, mo̱ne̱ e̱ gyaŋŋaa ɔnyare̱ ko̱ na ɔ so̱ kalɔ na nkyu, na mo̱nꞌ gya mò̱ se̱ yɔ lɔŋ ne̱ o lwee mò̱‑rɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ.

text: Ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ bo̱ saare̱ mò̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa yaa Yeesuu Yudaa awuye a iwurekpa‑ɔ (Matiyo

text: Ne̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ yɛ, “Amo̱ fe̱raa, amɔ fo̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑o ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱?” Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ.”

ref: They started accusing him and said, “We caught this man trying to get our people to riot and to stop paying taxes to the Emperor. He also claims that he is the Messiah, our king.”
text: Ne̱ baa le̱e̱ ba po̱rɔ mò̱ mfe̱ŋ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ a kra ɔnyare̱ mɔ na ɔ naa ɔ pe̱nna ase̱sɛ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ma ka leŋpoo sa Kaye̱saa, mo̱ne̱ Roma awuye owure dabe̱‑ɔ. Na mò̱ gbaa gye̱ owure ne̱e̱. Ne̱ ɔ be̱e̱ ɔ yɛ mò̱ e̱ gye̱ Kristoo ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ o suŋ a ɔ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ.”

ref: Herod and his soldiers made fun of Jesus and insulted him. They put a fine robe on him and sent him back to Pilate.
text: Ŋkee Owure Hɛrɔd na mò̱ asoogyaa awuye a saare̱ Yeesuu, ne̱ baa waa mò̱ kanɔ ŋke̱maa mɔ ne̱ ba kpa‑ɔ, ne̱ baa ba waagya dame̱naŋsɛ ko̱ bo̱ buŋ mò̱, ne̱ baa kiŋŋaa mò̱ yaa Gominaa Pilat ase̱.

ref: He told them, “You brought Jesus to me and said he was a troublemaker. But I have questioned him here in front of you, and I have not found him guilty of anything that you say he has done.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ a baa ɔnyare̱ mɔ mo̱ ase̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ naa ɔ pe̱nna ase̱sɛ ne̱e̱. Mbe̱yɔmɔ, mo̱ a bise mò̱ de̱e̱re̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ mo̱ne̱ akatɔ‑rɔ mfe̱e̱, ne̱ mo̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ ɔ mo̱ŋ gyi ase̱ŋ pɛɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ po̱rɔ mò̱ amo̱ se̱‑ɔ ke̱pɔ.

ref: But they all kept on saying, “He has been teaching and causing trouble all over Judea. He started in Galilee and has now come all the way here.” Jesus Is Brought before Herod
text: Amaa ŋkee gbaa ne̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ a pee akatɔ ba po̱rɔ mò̱. Bo̱ yɛ “Mò̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ kaapo̱‑ɔ e̱ kpa a a baa ase̱ŋ Yude̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱. Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ ɔ kaapo̱ amo̱, ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ ɔɔ taa baa bo̱ fo̱ mfe̱e̱‑ɔ.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa yaa Yeesuu Hɛrɔd akatɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ

ref: But the whole crowd shouted, “Kill Jesus! Give us Barabbas!”
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ a faa‑rɔ keŋkeŋ fe̱yɛ, “Mɔɔ mò̱! Na fo̱ lee Bar‑Abas le̱e̱ tiikpa‑ɔ sa ane̱.”

ref: After Pilate learned that Jesus came from the region ruled by Herod, he sent him to Herod, who was in Jerusalem at that time.
text: Gominaa‑o a nu amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ baa yaa Yeesuu Hɛrɔd ase̱.

ref: For a long time Herod had wanted to see Jesus and was very happy because he finally had this chance. He had heard many things about Jesus and hoped to see him work a miracle.
text: Owure Hɛrɔd a ŋu Yeesuu‑o, ne̱ aa waa mò̱ kɔne̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ oo nu mò̱ ase̱ŋ, ne̱ ɔ kyɔ ɔ kpa mò̱ kuŋu ke̱de̱daa. Ɔ kpa a o ŋu akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ ne̱ ɔ waa‑ɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: Herod asked him a lot of questions, but Jesus did not answer.
text: Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ Hɛrɔd a bise Yeesuu ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Amaa Yeesuu mo̱ŋ be̱ŋŋaa mò̱.

ref: Then the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses stood up and accused him of all kinds of bad things.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱‑ɔ na Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ a le̱e̱ yɔ akatɔ‑rɔ ya po̱rɔ Yeesuu bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: As Jesus was being led away, some soldiers grabbed hold of a man named Simon who was from Cyrene. He was coming in from the fields, but they put the cross on him and made him carry it behind Jesus.
text: Saŋ ne̱ asoogyaa awuye‑o de Yeesuu ba yɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa gyaŋŋaa ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ Kireenee maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Simɔŋ na ɔ kyo̱ŋ o lwee maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ baa ye̱re̱ mò̱ taa kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ bo̱ so̱rɔ mò̱, na ɔ gya Yeesuu si.

ref: One of the criminals hanging there also insulted Jesus by saying, “Aren't you the Messiah? Save yourself and save us!”
text: Ne̱ abɔye̱waapo̱ ne̱ baa da bamo̱ aa Yeesuu bo̱ me̱ra ayii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱ kake ko̱ŋko̱‑ɔ ɔko̱ a tu asaagyi we̱e̱ Yeesuu si fe̱yɛ, “N gye̱ fo̱ e̱ gye̱ Kristoo‑o ooo? Mo̱rɔwe̱ fo̱ e̱ye̱e̱, na fo̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ ane̱.”

ref: Pilate called together the chief priests, the leaders, and the people.
text: Ne̱ Gominaa Pilat a te̱e̱ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ na maŋ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ na ase̱sɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱.

ref: Pilate wanted to set Jesus free, so he spoke again to the crowds.
text: Amɔ gominaa‑o saŋ ɔ kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ yɔwe̱ Yeesuu bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱. Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ bise ase̱sɛ‑ɔ.

ref: Pilate spoke to them a third time, “But what crime has he done? I have not found him guilty of anything for which he should be put to death. I will have him beaten with a whip and set free.”
text: Ne̱ gominaa‑o a be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ sase̱po̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ bɔye̱ gbaa ne̱ ɔɔ waa? M fe̱raa mo̱ŋ ke̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ waa ne̱ o kperi ke̱mo̱ se̱ a o wu‑o. Mo̱ e̱ sa a bo̱ da mò̱ paara ne̱e̱ a bo̱ yɔwe̱ mò̱ a ɔ nare̱ ya kyure.”

ref: He freed the man who was in jail for rioting and murder, because he was the one the crowd wanted to be set free. Then Pilate handed Jesus over for them to do what they wanted with him. Jesus Is Nailed to a Cross (Matthew
text: Ne̱ gominaa‑o a lee Bar‑Abas ne̱ ɔɔ baa ase̱ŋ maŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ mɔɔ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ le̱e̱ tiikpa bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱, ne̱ ɔɔ taa Yeesuu sa mò̱ asoogyaa awuye fe̱yɛ bo̱ ya waa mò̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ e̱ kpa‑ɔ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa da Yeesuu bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: If this can happen when the wood is green, what do you think will happen when it is dry?
text: Kayii bo̱bwɛɛ ne̱ baa kywɛɛ‑ɔ, saŋbo̱to̱ kamo̱ ne̱ kaa wo̱re̱‑ɔ. Bamo̱ a waa mo̱ amo̱‑ɔ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ ba ba a bo̱ bo̱ waa mo̱ne̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ?”

ref: Two criminals were led out to be put to death with Jesus.
text: Amɔ anyare̱ anyɔ ko̱ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ abɔye̱waapo̱‑ɔ mɔ tii si, ne̱ ba yaa bamo̱ aa Yeesuu a bo̱ mɔɔ.

ref: Jesus said, “Father, forgive these people! They don't know what they're doing.” While the crowd stood there watching Jesus, the soldiers gambled for his clothes. The leaders insulted him by saying, “He saved others. Now he should save himself, if he really is God's chosen Messiah!”
text: Ne̱ ŋkee ase̱sɛ‑ɔ ye̱re̱ ba de̱e̱re̱, na Yudaa awuye agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ e̱ mɔse̱ mò̱ ba tɔwe̱ ba kyo̱ŋwe̱ Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Ɔɔ mo̱rɔwe̱ bo̱ko̱, amaa mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ ke̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ a tɔwe̱ ɛ mò̱ e̱ gye̱ Kristoo ne̱ Wuribware̱ a suŋ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ɔ taare̱ a ɔ mo̱rɔwe̱ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ aaa.”

ref: The soldiers made fun of Jesus and brought him some wine.
text: Ne̱ asoogyaa awuye‑o gbaa a saare̱ mò̱, ne̱ baa yɔ Yeesuu ase̱ ya sa mò̱ nta.

ref: They said, “If you are the king of the Jews, save yourself!”
text: Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Fo̱ e̱ gye̱ Yudaa awuye owure kase̱ŋtiŋ, amo̱ fe̱raa mo̱rɔwe̱ fo̱ e̱ye̱e̱.”

ref: But the other criminal told the first one off, “Don't you fear God? Aren't you getting the same punishment as this man?
text: Ne̱ abɔye̱waapo̱ amo̱ ɔnyɔse̱po̱‑ɔ a faa mò̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ɔ se̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ mo̱ŋ se̱re̱ Wuribware̱ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱? Ke̱pɔ ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱ baa bu ane̱ aa mò̱.

ref: Jesus replied, “I promise that today you will be with me in paradise.” The Death of Jesus (Matthew
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ i gyi fo̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ ndɔɔ mo̱ aa fo̱ e̱ yɔ Wuribware̱ se̱.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a wu kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: Then they went to prepare some sweet-smelling spices for his burial. But on the Sabbath they rested, as the Law of Moses commands.
text: Ne̱ baa kiŋŋi yɔ pe̱ ya kpa kadwii na kakyanne̱ na bo̱ ba bo̱ kyiine Yeesuu kifuniŋ‑o. Ne̱ baa kyure kukyure kake‑o fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ e̱ kaapo̱‑ɔ.

ref: A crowd had gathered to see the terrible sight. Then after they had seen it, they felt brokenhearted and went home.
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa ase̱sɛ ne̱ baa gyaŋŋe̱ mfe̱ŋ ba de̱e̱re̱ atɔ‑ɔ a ŋu ke̱tɔ ne̱ kaa waa‑ɔ, ne̱ baa kiŋŋi yɔ pe̱, na bamo̱ asare̱e̱ gya bamo̱ aŋu ba su.

ref: All of Jesus' close friends and the women who had come with him from Galilee stood at a distance and watched. Jesus Is Buried (Matthew
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ nyi Yeesuu nɛɛnɛɛ‑ɔ na akye̱e̱ ne̱ baa gya mò̱ se̱ le̱e̱ Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ ye̱re̱ ke̱fɔ ba de̱e̱re̱ ba kyo̱ŋwe̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa pure Yeesuu‑o (Matiyo

ref: There was a man named Joseph, who was from Arimathea in Judea. Joseph was a good and honest man, and he was eager for God's kingdom to come. He was also a member of the council, but he did not agree with what they had decided.
text: Amɔ ɔnyare̱ ko̱ mɔ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Yo̱sɛf ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ Arimate̱ya maŋ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ Yude̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ. Ɔ gye̱ se̱sɛ timaa, ne̱ ase̱sɛ mɔ be̱e̱ ba bu mò̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Mò̱ fe̱raa gywii saŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ i gyi kuwure swe̱e̱re̱ mɔ se̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱. O tii iwurekpa ase̱ŋgyipo̱‑ɔ se̱, amaa n gye̱ mò̱ aa bamo̱ kanɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ bu Yeesuu ke̱pɔ.

ref: He took the body down from the cross and wrapped it in fine cloth. Then he put it in a tomb that had been cut out of solid rock and had never been used.
text: Amɔ ke̱gye̱raŋta ko̱ mɔ ne̱ baa ŋeri ke̱fo̱re̱ lee bɔ fe̱yɛ ke̱kyaŋ‑ɔ maa mfe̱ŋ. Amaa bo̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ pure ɔko̱ ke̱mo̱‑rɔ. Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ Yo̱sɛf a ya maye̱ kifuniŋ‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ ba kyefuri bo̱ miri mò̱, ne̱ ɔɔ waa me̱naŋ taa mò̱ waa ke̱gye̱raŋta‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: The women who had come with Jesus from Galilee followed Joseph and watched how Jesus' body was placed in the tomb.
text: Ne̱ akye̱e̱ ne̱ baa gya Yeesuu si le̱e̱ Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ a gya Yo̱sɛf se̱ ya ŋu ke̱gye̱raŋta‑ɔ na ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ ba Yeesuu kifuniŋ‑o bo̱ be̱ya‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and some other women were the ones who had gone to the tomb. When they returned, they told the eleven apostles and the others what had happened.
text: Amɔ akye̱e̱ amo̱ anyare̱ e̱ gye̱ Mariya Magadalanyi na Yohana na Yakubu mò̱ nyi Mariya. Akye̱e̱ mɔ na bo̱ko̱ ne̱ bo̱ tii bamo̱ se̱‑ɔ ya yɔ ya tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ gywii Yeesuu ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ.

ref: they went in. But they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus,
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa lweero. Amaa bo̱ mo̱ŋ ŋu ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu kifuniŋ‑o da‑rɔ.

ref: and they did not know what to think. Suddenly two men in shining white clothes stood beside them.
text: Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ bamo̱. Puri amo̱‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ baa ŋu anyare̱ anyɔ ko̱, na bo̱ buŋ e̱kaare̱ ne̱ e̱ to̱re̱ se̱ pare̱pare̱pare̱‑ɔ bo̱ ye̱re̱ bamo̱ ase̱.

ref: The women were afraid and bowed to the ground. But the men said, “Why are you looking in the place of the dead for someone who is alive?
text: Kufu a nya akye̱e̱ amo̱‑ɔ se̱, ne̱ baa buŋŋe anyare̱ anyɔ amo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ anyare̱ anyɔ‑ɔ a bise bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i buwi alowipo̱‑rɔ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa ɔko̱ ne̱ o te‑o?

ref: ‘The Son of Man will be handed over to sinners who will nail him to a cross. But three days later he will rise to life.’ ”
text: ‘A tiri fe̱yɛ bo̱ taa mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, bo̱ waa abɔye̱waapo̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ, na bo̱ da mo̱ bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱, na ke̱mo̱ kake sase̱po̱‑ɔ na n kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro.’ ”

ref: That same day two of Jesus' disciples were going to the village of Emmaus, which was about eleven kilometers from Jerusalem.
text: Kake amo̱ dɛɛ, ne̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱ anyɔ ko̱ a ko̱so̱ le̱e̱ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ bo̱ naa ba yɔ kamaŋgyii ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ Imayus‑o. Mfe̱ŋ na Yɛro̱salɛm e̱ waa fe̱yɛ e̱maye̱re̱ isunoo.

ref: Jesus asked them, “What were you talking about as you walked along?” The two of them stood there looking sad and gloomy.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a bise bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ ase̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ naa mo̱ne̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ne̱ i gywii abɛɛ‑ɔ ne̱e̱?” Ne̱ baa sii ye̱re̱, ne̱ oo ŋu fe̱yɛ bamo̱ akatɔ a pee.

ref: We had hoped that he would be the one to set Israel free! But it has already been three days since all this happened.
text: Na weetee ane̱ mɔ ane̱ de mò̱ te̱maa fe̱yɛ mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ ba na ɔ bo̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ Isireelii awuye. Amɔ ŋke nsa e̱ gye̱ ndɔɔ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ a waa mò̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ.

ref: Some women in our group surprised us. They had gone to the tomb early in the morning,
text: Amaa ane̱ akye̱e̱ ne̱ bo̱ tii ane̱ se̱‑ɔ a sa ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ ane̱, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ baa ya de̱e̱re̱ ke̱gye̱raŋta‑ɔ‑rɔ gye̱gyaye̱ kese‑ro.

ref: Some men from our group went to the tomb and found it just as the women had said. But they didn't see Jesus either.
text: Ne̱ ane̱‑rɔ bo̱ko̱ a ya de̱e̱re̱ ke̱gye̱raŋta‑ɔ‑rɔ, bo̱ mo̱ŋ ŋu Yeesuu‑o fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ akye̱e̱ amo̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ.”

ref: Then Jesus asked the two disciples, “Why can't you understand? How can you be so slow to believe all that the prophets said?
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ de mfɛɛre̱. Mo̱ne̱ saŋ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ akyaamɛɛ a kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ gyi mo̱ne̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ.

ref: Didn't you know that the Messiah would have to suffer before he was given his glory?”
text: Mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ nu kaase̱ fe̱yɛ a tiri fe̱yɛ Kristoo ne̱ Wuribware̱ yɛ o suŋ‑o i ŋu awo̱re̱fɔɔ na o wu, pwɛɛ na ɔ dɛɛ nya wuraa na ke̱dabe̱ Wuribware̱ se̱.”

ref: Jesus then explained everything written about himself in the Scriptures, beginning with the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets.
text: Bamo̱ aa Yeesuu a kya kpa se̱ bo̱ naa‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu a le̱e̱ o lee ke̱tɔ ne̱ Mosis na Wuribware̱ akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ a kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ bo̱ kaapo̱ mò̱ kuŋu si ase̱ŋ‑nɔ pɛɛɛ gywii bamo̱.

ref: They begged him, “Stay with us! It's already late, and the sun is going down.” So Jesus went into the house to stay with them.
text: Amaa bo̱ mo̱ŋ sure si fe̱yɛ ɔ kyo̱ŋ yɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ bo̱ yɛ ɔ bo̱ so̱we̱ bamo̱ ase̱ mfe̱e̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kyɔwe̱ a lo̱we̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ Yeesuu a ya so̱we̱ bamo̱ ase̱.

ref: After Jesus sat down to eat, he took some bread. He blessed it and broke it. Then he gave it to them.
text: Ne̱ mò̱ aa bamo̱ a kye̱na na bo̱ gyi ateese, ne̱ ɔɔ taa bodobodoo‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ sa Wuribware̱ aŋsɛ we̱e̱ se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ te̱ŋŋe̱‑rɔ sa bamo̱ a bo̱ gyi.

ref: They said to each other, “When he talked with us along the road and explained the Scriptures to us, didn't it warm our hearts?”
text: Ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ gywii abɛɛ fe̱yɛ, “Saŋ ne̱ ɔɔ kaapo̱ ane̱ abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, aa waa ane̱ kɔne̱, ne̱ ane̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ a yuri ane̱, bɛɛɛ?”

ref: So they got up and returned to Jerusalem. The two disciples found the eleven apostles and the others gathered together.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ baa ko̱so̱ puri amo̱‑rɔ kiŋŋi yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ ya to̱ fe̱yɛ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱ kudu kako̱‑ɔ na bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ tii si‑o a gyaŋŋe̱. Ne̱ abɛɛ anyɔ‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ baa ŋu Yeesuu na ɔ ke̱e̱.

ref: And they learned from the group that the Lord was really alive and had appeared to Peter.
text: Ne̱ baa be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Kase̱ŋtiŋ, ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ a kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro. Oo lee mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ Simɔŋ.”

ref: Then the disciples from Emmaus told what happened on the road and how they knew he was the Lord when he broke the bread. What Jesus' Followers Must Do (Matthew
text: Ne̱ anyare̱ anyɔ‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ kaa ba kpa‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ na ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa pini Yeesuu saŋ ne̱ ɔɔ te̱ŋŋe̱ bodobodoo‑o‑ro sa bamo̱‑ɔ gywii bamo̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a lee mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ (Maak

ref: They were frightened and terrified because they thought they were seeing a ghost.
text: Ne̱ kufu a nya bamo̱, ba fa fe̱yɛ ba ŋu kye̱ŋaŋpo̱ ko̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: But Jesus said, “Why are you so frightened? Why do you doubt?
text: Ne̱ oo bise bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ntɔ i de mo̱ne̱ kufu? Nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ maa kɔɔre̱ mo̱ne̱ i gyi?

ref: Look at my hands and my feet and see who I am! Touch me and find out for yourselves. Ghosts don't have flesh and bones as you see I have.”
text: Mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱re̱ mo̱ asare̱e̱ na mo̱ ayaapapaa, na mo̱nꞌ ŋu fe̱yɛ mo̱ ne̱e̱. Na mo̱nꞌ kra mo̱‑rɔ a mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱. Kye̱ŋaŋpo̱ de ayeegyi na abowii fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a ŋu n de‑o ooo?”

ref: After Jesus said this, he showed them his hands and his feet.
text: Mò̱ a tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ taa mò̱ asare̱e̱ na ayaapapaa bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱.

ref: The disciples were so glad and amazed that they could not believe it. Jesus then asked them, “Do you have something to eat?”
text: Bo̱ saŋ bo̱ mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu a ase̱ŋ‑ɔ gyi kyɛɛkyɛɛ. Amaa bamo̱ akatɔ a gyi, ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ bamo̱. To, ne̱ Yeesuu a bise bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ de ateese ko̱ mfe̱e̱ aaa?”

ref: They gave him a piece of broiled fish.
text: Ne̱ baa taa kakiŋgyi de̱ŋasɛ katiŋ ko̱ sa mò̱.

ref: He took it and ate it as they watched.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ taa wo̱ bamo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ.

ref: Jesus said to them, “While I was still with you, I told you that everything written about me in the Law of Moses, the Books of the Prophets, and in the Psalms had to happen.”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Saŋ ne̱ n te na mo̱ aa mo̱ne̱ e̱ sa se̱ŋsa‑ɔ, mo̱ a tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ ko̱ gywii mo̱ne̱, ne̱ m be̱e̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ amo̱‑rɔ.
