Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ Mosis a kyo̱rɛɛ waa mò̱ mbraa wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ Wuribware̱ akyaamɛɛ a kyo̱rɛɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ Deefid a kyo̱rɛɛ waa mò̱ iliŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ bo̱ le̱e̱ mo̱ kuŋu si‑o e̱ ba kase̱ŋtiŋ.”

ref: He told them: The Scriptures say that the Messiah must suffer, then three days later he will rise from death.
text: Ne̱ ŋkee ɔɔ tɔwe̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “Baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Kristoo ne̱ Wuribware̱ yɛ o suŋ a ɔ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ i gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ, na o wu, na ke̱mo̱ ŋke nsa o kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro.

ref: They also say that all people of every nation must be told in my name to turn to God, in order to be forgiven. So beginning in Jerusalem,
text: Na mo̱nꞌ yɔ mfe̱e̱ a Yɛro̱salɛm awuye‑o na kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ ase̱ ya ba mo̱ ke̱nyare̱ bo̱ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ timaa‑o gywii bamo̱. Na mo̱nꞌ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa nu mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱, na o kiŋŋi le̱e̱ mò̱ e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ, na Wuribware̱ nya taa mò̱ e̱bɔye̱‑ɔ bo̱ ke mò̱.

ref: you must tell everything that has happened.
text: Mo̱ne̱ fɔŋfɔŋ e̱ gye̱ amo̱ aye̱re̱rɔpo̱.

ref: I will send you the one my Father has promised, but you must stay in the city until you are given power from heaven. Jesus Returns to Heaven (Mark
text: Na mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ sa a Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o ywe̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ se̱, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ n se̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ ɔ waa‑ɔ. Amaa mo̱nꞌ gywii maŋ‑nɔ mfe̱e̱ bo̱ fo̱ saŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ nya mfaanɛɛ a ke̱yaale̱ŋ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ le̱e̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu yii so̱so̱‑ɔ (Maak

ref: Jesus led his disciples out to Bethany, where he raised his hands and blessed them.
text: Ne̱ ŋkee Yeesuu a sa ne̱ mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a yɔ hare̱e̱ Bɛtaniya kamaŋgyii, ne̱ ɔɔ yase̱ mò̱ asare̱e̱ so̱so̱, ne̱ oo yure bamo̱ mfe̱ŋ.

ref: As he was doing this, he left and was taken up to heaven.
text: Mò̱ a maa se̱ o yure bamo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ Wuribware̱ a bo̱ yaa mò̱ so̱so̱.

ref: After his disciples had worshiped him, they returned to Jerusalem and were very happy.
text: Ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a sii mfe̱ŋ suŋ mò̱, ne̱ baa kiŋŋi yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ, na bamo̱ akatɔ a gyi bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: They spent their time in the temple, praising God.
text: Ne̱ baa yɔ bo̱ bo̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ kabuno ba kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱ kake ke̱maa.

ref: From the very beginning the Word was with God.
text: Saŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a twe̱e̱ kaye̱‑ɔ, Abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ na Wuribware̱ kyɔ bo̱ bo̱‑rɔ.

ref: received its life from him, and his life gave light to everyone.
text: Abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱ ne̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ye̱re̱ ke̱ e̱ nya ŋkpa. Ŋkpa pɛɛɛ a le̱e̱ mò̱ ase̱ ne̱e̱. Ŋkpa mɔ a taa ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ baa ase̱sɛ.

ref: The light keeps shining in the dark, and darkness has never put it out.
text: Ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ mɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ laŋŋe̱ kibugyii‑ro, ne̱ kibugyii‑o maa taare̱ a ko̱ kyo̱ŋ ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: who came to tell about the light and to lead all people to have faith.
text: Mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔɔ ba bo̱ tɔwe̱ ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ‑ɔ ase̱ŋ gywii ase̱sɛ, na bamo̱ pɛɛɛ nu ase̱ŋ‑ɔ, na bo̱ kɔɔre̱ gyi.

ref: The true light that shines on everyone was coming into the world.
text: Abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ kase̱ŋtiŋ amo̱ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ sa ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: The Word was in the world, but no one knew him, though God had made the world with his Word.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ baa ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ kaye̱‑rɔ. Amaa saŋ ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ kye̱na kaye̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ mo̱ŋ pini mò̱, amaa mò̱ se̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a twe̱e̱ kaye̱‑ɔ bo̱ bo̱rɔ.

ref: He came into his own world, but his own nation did not welcome him.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ ba kaye̱ ne̱ ɔɔ twe̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ, amaa mò̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ ɔɔ twe̱e̱‑ɔ mɔ a kine mò̱.

ref: They were not God's children by nature or because of any human desires. God himself was the one who made them his children.
text: Amaa se̱sɛ ya kii Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑ana‑ɔ ɔko̱, n gye̱ se̱sɛ dimaadi e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔɔ kpa ne̱ ɔɔ ko̱we̱ gyi, Wuribware̱ fɔŋfɔŋ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔɔ ko̱we̱ mò̱.

ref: The Word became a human being and lived here with us. We saw his true glory, the glory of the only Son of the Father. From him the complete gifts of undeserved grace and truth have come down to us.
text: To, ne̱ Abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ a bo̱ kii nyiŋkpasɛ, ne̱ ɔɔ kye̱na ane̱ ase̱ kafo̱ŋfwe̱e̱. Ɔ bo̱ ane̱ ase̱, ne̱ oo ŋu ane̱ e̱wɛɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ, ne̱ o de kase̱ŋtiŋ ɔ kyɔ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa. Ne̱ ane̱ a ŋu mò̱ wuraa na mò̱ ke̱dabe̱ ne̱ a kaapo̱ ane̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ e̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi ko̱ŋko̱ kpeŋ‑o.

ref: Because of all that the Son is, we have been given one blessing after another.
text: To, mò̱ a ŋu ane̱ e̱wɛɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ sa ane̱ ŋyure bwe̱e̱tɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: No one has ever seen God. The only Son, who is truly God and is closest to the Father, has shown us what God is like. John the Baptist Tells about Jesus (Matthew
text: Amaa fe̱raa ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ ŋu Wuribware̱, mò̱ gyi ko̱ŋko̱ amo̱ ne̱ o te me̱raa mò̱ se̱ Wuribware̱‑ɔ, mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔɔ kaapo̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ du‑o. Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ

ref: The religious authorities in Jerusalem sent priests and temple helpers to ask John who he was. He told them plainly, “I am not the Messiah.”
text: Yohanee mo̱ŋ kine bamo̱ ke̱be̱ŋŋaa, ne̱ ɔɔ kaapo̱ bamo̱‑rɔ nɛɛnɛɛ fe̱yɛ, “Daabii. N gye̱ mo̱ e̱ gye̱ Kristoo amo̱.”

ref: Finally, they said, “Who are you then? We have to give an answer to the ones who sent us. Tell us who you are!”
text: To, ne̱ bo̱ be̱e̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Amo̱ fe̱raa, tɔwe̱ gywii ane̱ mò̱ ne̱ fo̱ gye̱‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ e̱ ya lee kanɔ bamo̱ ne̱ baa suŋ ane̱‑ɔ ase̱. E̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ fo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ase̱ŋ?”

ref: John answered in the words of the prophet Isaiah, “I am only someone shouting in the desert, ‘Get the road ready for the Lord!’ ”
text: Ne̱ Yohanee a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ gye̱ ɔko̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ Isaya a kyo̱rɛɛ mò̱ ase̱ŋ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ɔko̱ e̱ fɛɛ‑rɔ kimukee‑ro ɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, Mo̱nꞌ lɔŋŋɔ kpa kye̱rarasɛ nɛɛnɛɛ bo̱ be̱ya ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ a ɔ bo̱rɔ se̱!’ ”

ref: They asked him, “Why are you baptizing people, if you are not the Messiah or Elijah or the Prophet?”
text: Ne̱ mbɔɔ ne̱ baa suŋ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ Yohanee ase̱‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ gye̱ Farisii awuye. Ne̱ baa bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Kristoo bɛɛɛ Iliya bɛɛɛ Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ amo̱, ne̱ e̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ bɔ ase̱sɛ asuu?”

ref: John told them, “I use water to baptize people. But here with you is someone you don't know.
text: Ne̱ Yohanee a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “M fe̱raa, nkyu ne̱ n de mo̱ e̱ bɔ asuu, amaa ɔko̱ bo̱ mo̱ne̱‑rɔ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi mò̱.

ref: Even though I came first, I am not good enough to untie his sandals.”
text: Mo̱ a gye̱ mò̱ ŋkpɛɛ ba, amaa mo̱ŋ fo̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ kra mò̱ ase̱be̱ta‑rɔ lee gbaa, na m fwe̱e̱ mò̱ ayaapapaa se̱.”

ref: John said this as he was baptizing east of the Jordan River in Bethany. The Lamb of God
text: Amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a waa Bɛtaniya kamaŋgyii ne̱ ɔ bo̱ Yo̱o̱daŋ bo̱ŋ kyɔwe̱ le̱e̱kpa a ke̱be̱gya‑ɔ‑rɔ, mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yohanee e̱ bɔ asuu‑o ne̱e̱. Wuribware̱ a kasanne̱gyii‑o

ref: The next day, John saw Jesus coming toward him and said: Here is the Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!
text: Kaye̱ ŋke‑o ne̱ Yohanee a ŋu Yeesuu na ɔ ba mò̱ ase̱, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱ ɔko̱ ne̱ o lee kaye̱‑rɔ e̱bɔye̱, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ba mɔɔ kasanne̱gyii ko̱ bo̱ de ba lee e̱bɔye̱‑ɔ. Mò̱ e̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ a kasanne̱gyii‑o.

ref: He is the one I told you about when I said, “Someone else will come, who is greater than I am, because he was alive before I was born.”
text: Se̱sɛ mɔ ase̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ ba mo̱ kamɛɛ‑ɔ. Ɔ kyɔ mo̱ ke̱dabe̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ kyɔ o te kyee pwɛɛ ne̱ baa dɛɛ ko̱we̱ mo̱.’ ”

ref: John's two followers heard him, and they went with Jesus.
text: Mò̱ agyase̱po̱ anyɔ‑ɔ a nu fe̱yɛ ɔ tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa yɔ Yeesuu ase̱.

ref: When Jesus turned and saw them, he asked, “What do you want?” They answered, “Rabbi, where do you live?” The Hebrew word “Rabbi” means “Teacher.”
text: Yeesuu a nyɔŋ ke̱e̱, ne̱ oo ŋu fe̱yɛ bo̱ gya mò̱ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ oo bise bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i buwi mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa?” Ne̱ baa bise Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Ɔkaapo̱po̱, mfe̱ne̱ ne̱ fo̱ te?”

ref: Jesus replied, “Come and see!” It was already about four o'clock in the afternoon when they went with him and saw where he lived. So they stayed on for the rest of the day.
text: Ne̱ oo lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ bo̱ ke̱e̱.” Saŋ amo̱‑ɔ kyɔwe̱ a kyɔ kpeere, ne̱ mò̱ aa bamo̱ a yɔ, ne̱ baa ya ŋu mfe̱ŋ ne̱ o te‑o, ne̱ baa nya se̱ sii mò̱ ase̱ kake amo̱ pɛɛɛ.

ref: One of the two men who had heard John and had gone with Jesus was Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter.
text: Agyase̱po̱ ne̱ baa gya Yeesuu si‑o ɔko̱ ke̱nyare̱ e̱ gye̱ Andruu, ne̱ o de daa ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Simɔŋ.

ref: The first thing Andrew did was to find his brother and tell him, “We have found the Messiah!” The Hebrew word “Messiah” means the same as the Greek word “Christ.”
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ oo buwi kpa mò̱ daa, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ a ŋu Masiya‑o.”

ref: Andrew brought his brother to Jesus. And when Jesus saw him, he said, “Simon son of John, you will be called Cephas.” This name can be translated as “Peter.” Jesus Chooses Philip and Nathanael
text: Ne̱ Andruu a yaa Simɔŋ Yeesuu ase̱. Ne̱ Yeesuu a de̱e̱re̱ mò̱ diŋŋ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ ke̱nyare̱ e̱ gye̱ Yohanee mò̱ gyi Simɔŋ, amaa le̱e̱ ndɔɔ bo̱ yɔ fo̱ ke̱ŋasɛnyare̱ e̱ gye̱ Ke̱e̱fas.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a te̱e̱ Filipo na Nataniyɛl‑ɔ

ref: The next day Jesus decided to go to Galilee. There he met Philip, who was from Bethsaida, the hometown of Andrew and Peter. Jesus said to Philip, “Follow me.”
text: Kaye̱ a be̱e̱ ke‑o, ne̱ Yeesuu a kra fe̱yɛ ɔ yɔ Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱. Ne̱ oo ŋu ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Filipo, ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ Bɛtɛsaye̱da maŋ‑nɔ, Andruu na Peetroo‑ana aye̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ba a mo̱ aa fo̱ a yɔ.”

ref: When Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him, he said, “Here is a true descendant of our ancestor Israel. And he isn't deceitful.”
text: Yeesuu a ŋu Nataniyɛl e̱ ba mò̱ ase̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱ Isireeliinyi kase̱ŋtiŋ, ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ de kaye̱ba‑ɔ.”

ref: Nathanael said, “Rabbi, you are the Son of God and the King of Israel!”
text: Ne̱ Nataniyɛl yɛ, “Ɔkaapo̱po̱, Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑o e̱ gye̱ fo̱. Isireelii swe̱e̱re̱ owure‑o e̱ gye̱ fo̱!”

ref: Jesus answered, “Did you believe me just because I said that I saw you under the fig tree? You will see something even greater.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii fo̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a taare̱ ŋu fo̱, na m bo̱ mfe̱e̱, saŋ ne̱ fo̱ bo̱ kiyii‑o kaase̱‑ɔ se̱, ne̱ fo̱ a kɔɔre̱ mo̱ gyi aaa? Saŋ ko̱ e̱ ba na fo̱ ŋu amo̱ ne̱ a kyɔ amo̱‑ɔ.”

ref: I tell you for certain you will see heaven open and God's angels going up and coming down on the Son of Man.”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ awo̱re̱‑rɔ i buŋŋi‑ro, na Wuribware̱ mbɔɔ e̱ ba kaase̱ ba ywe̱e̱ mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, si ba kiŋŋi ba yɔ so̱so̱.”

ref: Three days later Mary, the mother of Jesus, was at a wedding feast in the village of Cana in Galilee.
text: Amo̱ ŋke ŋnyɔ kamɛɛ‑rɔ, ne̱ baa taa ko̱ko̱fɔ Kana maŋ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: Jesus and his disciples had also been invited and were there.
text: Ne̱ baa te̱e̱ Yeesuu na mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ba ko̱ko̱fɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ. Amɔ Yeesuu mò̱ nyi bo̱ mfe̱ŋ.

ref: When the wine was all gone, Mary said to Jesus, “They don't have any more wine.”
text: Saŋ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ ko̱ko̱fɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ a nta‑ɔ a lo̱we̱. Ne̱ Yeesuu mò̱ nyi a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Bamo̱ nta‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a lo̱we̱.”

ref: Jesus replied, “Mother, my time hasn't yet come! You must not tell me what to do.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “N na, m fe̱raa, mo̱ saŋ‑ɔ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ fo̱ a ŋ waa ko̱tɔko̱.”

ref: Mary then said to the servants, “Do whatever Jesus tells you to do.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu mò̱ nyi a tɔwe̱ gywii ayaafɔre̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ waa ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ɔ tɔwe̱ a o gywii mo̱ne̱‑ɔ.”

ref: At the feast there were six stone water jars that were used by the people for washing themselves in the way that their religion said they must. Each jar held about
text: Yudaa awuye de asare̱e̱ ke̱fwe̱e̱ ko̱ ne̱ baa suye le̱e̱ bamo̱ ade̱daapo̱ ase̱‑ɔ. Nkyu ne̱ bo̱ de ba fwe̱e̱ bamo̱ asare̱e̱‑ɔ bo̱ alaŋ dabe̱ dabe̱ ko̱ ne̱ baa ba afo̱re̱ bo̱ waa‑ɔ‑rɔ. Alaŋ mɔ ke̱ke̱maa nkyu e̱ fo̱ aŋko̱re̱ kitiŋ.

ref: Jesus told the servants to fill them to the top with water. Then after the jars had been filled,
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii ayaafɔre̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ sɛɛ nkyu bo̱ bo̱rɔ alaŋ‑ɔ. Ne̱ baa sɛɛ nkyu bo̱ bo̱rɔ amo̱ pe̱e̱pe̱e̱pe̱e̱.

ref: and the man in charge drank some of the water that had now turned into wine. He did not know where the wine had come from, but the servants did. He called the bridegroom over
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mbe̱yɔmɔ mo̱nꞌ saa nkyu‑o ŋko̱ le̱e̱ alaŋ‑ɔ ko̱ko̱ŋko̱‑rɔ yaa bo̱ sa ko̱ko̱fɔ‑ɔ ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱.” Ne̱ nkyu‑o a buruwi nta. Ne̱ ŋkee baa taa nta‑ɔ yaa mò̱, ne̱ ɔɔ da ke̱e̱. Mò̱ a da ke̱e̱‑ɔ, ɔ mo̱ŋ pini mfe̱ŋ ne̱ nta amo̱ a le̱e̱‑ɔ, amaa ayaafɔre̱ ne̱ baa saa nkyu‑o baa‑ɔ fe̱raa nyi. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ ɔko̱fɔ kuri,

ref: and said, “The best wine is always served first. Then after the guests have had plenty, the other wine is served. But you have kept the best until last!”
text: ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔke̱maa i ke nta timaa‑o pwɛɛ ne̱e̱, ne̱ afɔ pɛɛɛ ya nya nuu ane̱ŋ ne̱ a baare bamo̱‑ɔ pwɛɛ na ɔ dɛɛ baa nta ke̱maa ne̱ ɔ kpa‑ɔ. Amaa fo̱ fe̱raa, fo̱ a taa nta timaa bo̱ sii kamɛɛ bo̱ fo̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ ne̱e̱, ne̱ fo̱ a dɛɛ lee baa.”

ref: Not long before the Jewish festival of Passover, Jesus went to Jerusalem.
text: Kasu kanɔ ateese kigyibe̱e̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ Wuribware̱-a‑kya-ane̱-yɔwe̱‑ɔ a kpa a ko̱ fo̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu a yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ.

ref: So he took some rope and made a whip. Then he chased everyone out of the temple, together with their sheep and cattle. He turned over the tables of the moneychangers and scattered their coins.
text: Yeesuu a ŋu amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ taa ife bo̱ waa paara, ne̱ ɔɔ gya e̱sanne̱‑ɔ na e̱naate̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa a kabuno‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ gye̱ra gye̱ra atanne̱ akyeeripo̱ a iteeburi‑o bo̱ da, ne̱ ɔɔ ŋo̱ra bamo̱ atanne̱ agyi‑o‑ro.

ref: The disciples then remembered that the Scriptures say, “My love for your house burns in me like a fire.”
text: Ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a nyiŋŋi ane̱ŋ ne̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ se̱ fe̱yɛ, “Wuribware̱, ke̱kpa de̱maŋte̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa fo̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ a kɔɔre̱ mo̱ kakpo̱nɔ.”

ref: “Destroy this temple,” Jesus answered, “and in three days I will build it again!”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ya bwee Wuribware̱ suŋkpa mɔ, ŋke nsa bo̱ fo̱‑ɔ, mo̱‑ɔ mo̱ a be̱e̱ pwɛɛ mò̱ bo̱ ye̱ra.”

ref: There he found people selling cattle, sheep, and doves in the temple. He also saw moneychangers sitting at their tables.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa kabuno ya to̱ anyare̱ na ba fe e̱naate̱ na e̱sanne̱ na awurele̱pɔ, ne̱ bamo̱ bo̱ko̱ mɔ te bamo̱ iteeburi ase̱ ba kyeeri Roma swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ atanne̱ ba yaa Yude̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ atanne̱‑rɔ.

ref: Jesus said to the people who had been selling doves, “Get those doves out of here! Don't make my Father's house a marketplace.”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ ba fe awurele̱pɔ‑ɔ a taa amo̱ le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ. Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ma taa n se̱ aye̱ bo̱ kii ke̱gya gyikpa.”

ref: The Jewish leaders asked Jesus, “What miracle will you work to show us why you have done this?”
text: Ne̱ Yudaa awuye agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ a ba mò̱ ase̱ bo̱ bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ntɔ atɔ dabe̱ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ taare̱ a fo̱ waa bo̱ kaapo̱ ane̱, na a kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ de kpa fe̱yɛ fo̱ waa amo̱‑ɔ?”

ref: And when he was raised from death, his disciples remembered what he had told them. Then they believed the Scriptures and the words of Jesus. Jesus Knows What People Are Like
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ mò̱ a wu ne̱ baa pure mò̱‑ɔ, mò̱ kikyiŋŋi‑ro‑o mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a nyiŋŋi si fe̱yɛ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ na ke̱tɔ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱‑ɔ gyi.

ref: In Jerusalem during Passover many people put their faith in Jesus, because they saw him work miracles.
text: Yeesuu a bo̱ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ saŋ ne̱ ba gyi Wuribware̱-a‑kya-ane̱-yɔwe̱ a ateese‑o, ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ a kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi, ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa ŋu atɔ dabe̱ dabe̱ ne̱ ɔɔ waa‑ɔ se̱.

ref: But Jesus knew what was in their hearts, and he would not let them have power over him.
text: Amaa Yeesuu mo̱ŋ taa mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ bamo̱ se̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ o nyi ane̱ŋ ne̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ du‑o.

ref: No one had to tell him what people were like. He already knew.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ a mo̱ŋ tiri fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ bo̱ du‑o gywii mò̱, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ o nyi ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ bo̱ bamo̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: There was a man named Nicodemus who was a Pharisee and a Jewish leader.
text: Ɔnyare̱ ko̱ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Nikodeemus. Ɔ gye̱ Yudaa awuye a maŋ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ ɔko̱, ne̱ ɔ be̱e̱ ɔ gye̱ Farisii awuye‑o ɔko̱.

ref: One night he went to Jesus and said, “Rabbi, we know that God has sent you to teach us. You could not work these miracles, unless God were with you.”
text: Kanye ko̱, ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ Yeesuu ase̱ ya tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔkaapo̱po̱, ane̱ nyi fe̱yɛ fo̱ gye̱ ɔkaapo̱po̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a suŋ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ maa taare̱ a ɔ waa akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ mɔ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ waa‑ɔ, amɔ Wuribware̱ ye̱re̱ mò̱‑rɔ.”

ref: Jesus replied, “I tell you for certain that you must be born from above before you can see God's kingdom!”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ i gyi fo̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ maa taare̱ a o tii Wuribware̱ a kuwure‑o si, amɔ baa be̱e̱ ko̱we̱ mò̱ pwɛɛ.”

ref: Jesus answered: I tell you for certain that before you can get into God's kingdom, you must be born not only by water, but by the Spirit.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ i gyi fo̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ, ɔko̱ maa taare̱ a o tii Wuribware̱ a kuwure‑o si, amɔ baa ba nkyu bo̱ bɔ mò̱ asuu, ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a ko̱we̱ mò̱.

ref: Humans give life to their children. Yet only God's Spirit can change you into a child of God.
text: Mò̱ ne̱ dimaadi a ko̱we̱ mò̱‑ɔ de dimaadi ko̱yo̱wɔre̱gyi, ne̱ mò̱‑ɔ mɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a ko̱we̱ mò̱‑ɔ fe̱raa mò̱ kra a nya ŋkpa po̱pwɛɛ.

ref: Don't be surprised when I say that you must be born from above.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ fo̱ ma sa a a kpe̱ŋ fo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii fo̱ fe̱yɛ a tiri fe̱yɛ bo̱ be̱e̱ ko̱we̱ mo̱ne̱ ɔke̱maa.

ref: Only God's Spirit gives new life. The Spirit is like the wind that blows wherever it wants to. You can hear the wind, but you don't know where it comes from or where it is going.
text: To, afwii e̱ da a yɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ a kpa‑ɔ ne̱e̱. Fo̱ i nu amo̱ se̱, amaa fo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi mfe̱ŋ ne̱ aa le̱e̱‑ɔ na mfe̱ŋ ne̱ a yɔ‑ɔ. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ane̱ maa nu kaase̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o e̱ ko̱we̱ ɔko̱‑ɔ.”

ref: Jesus replied: How can you be a teacher of Israel and not know these things?
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ gye̱ Isireelii awuye‑ro ɔkaapo̱po̱ dabe̱‑ɔ, na fo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi amo̱‑ɔ aaa?

ref: I tell you for certain we know what we are talking about because we have seen it ourselves. But none of you will accept what we say.
text: Mo̱ i gyi fo̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ane̱ nyi‑o ase̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ tɔwe̱, ne̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ane̱ a ŋu‑o ase̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ tɔwe̱. Amaa amo̱ pɛɛɛ gbaa mo̱ne̱ ɔke̱maa maa kɔɔre̱ ane̱ ase̱ŋ o gyi.

ref: If you don't believe when I talk to you about things on earth, how can you possibly believe if I talk to you about things in heaven?
text: Kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ atɔ ase̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ maa kɔɔre̱ mo̱ mo̱ne̱ i gyi‑o, saŋbo̱to̱ Wuribware̱ se̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ ya tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ne̱, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ a mo̱nꞌ gyi aaa?

ref: No one has gone up to heaven except the Son of Man, who came down from there.
text: Na ɔko̱ mɔ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ yɔ Wuribware̱ se̱ ya ŋu. Amaa mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, a le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ se̱ ba. Amo̱se̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ taare̱ a n tɔwe̱ Wuribware̱ se̱ atɔ ase̱ŋ.

ref: Then everyone who has faith in the Son of Man will have eternal life.
text: na ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ o gyi‑o maa wu, amaa na ɔ nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: God loved the people of this world so much that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who has faith in him will have eternal life and never really die.
text: A kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kpa kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ ke̱baŋse̱‑ɔ se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ taa mò̱ gyi ko̱ŋko̱ wo̱re̱ kpeŋ ne̱ o de‑o sa ane̱ fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ kɔɔre̱ mò̱ o gyi‑o maa wu, amaa ɔ nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: God did not send his Son into the world to condemn its people. He sent him to save them!
text: A mo̱ŋ gye̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a suŋ mò̱ gyi bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ bo̱ kii kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ ɔse̱ŋgyipo̱, amaa ɔɔ ba ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ ba a ɔ bo̱ kii kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ ɔmo̱rɔwe̱po̱.

ref: No one who has faith in God's Son will be condemned. But everyone who doesn't have faith in him has already been condemned for not having faith in God's only Son.
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ kɔɔre̱ mò̱ o gyi‑o, Wuribware̱ maa bu mò̱ ke̱pɔ, amaa ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ maa kɔɔre̱ mò̱ o gyi‑o, Wuribware̱ a kyɔ bu mò̱ ke̱pɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi ko̱ŋko̱ kpeŋ‑o gyi.

ref: The light has come into the world, and people who do evil things are judged guilty because they love the dark more than the light.
text: Mfaanɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ ba a mo̱nꞌ bo̱ ŋu bamo̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ i bu ke̱pɔ‑ɔ, amo̱ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ a ba kaye̱‑rɔ, amaa kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ e̱ kpa kibugyii‑o bo̱ kyo̱ŋ ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ba waa atɔ ne̱ a gye̱ bɔye̱‑ɔ.

ref: People who do evil hate the light and won't come to the light, because it clearly shows what they have done.
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ waa e̱bɔye̱‑ɔ se̱re̱ ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ‑ɔ. Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, ɔ maa kpa a ɔ ba ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ‑rɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ maa kpa a mò̱ e̱bɔye̱‑ɔ le̱e̱ kawu.

ref: But everyone who lives by the truth will come to the light, because they want others to know that God is really the one doing what they do. Jesus and John the Baptist
text: Amaa ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o e̱ ba ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ‑rɔ, na ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ kya mò̱‑rɔ a ɔ waa ke̱tɔ ke̱maa.” Yeesuu na Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱‑ɔ

ref: Later, Jesus and his disciples went to Judea, where he stayed with them for a while and was baptizing people.
text: Amo̱‑ɔ kamɛɛ‑rɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu na mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a yɔ Yude̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱. Ne̱ mò̱ aa bamo̱ a kye̱na mfe̱ŋ ŋke mfe̱ne̱ ko̱, ne̱ baa bɔ ase̱sɛ asuu.

ref: John's followers got into an argument with a Jewish man about a ceremony of washing.
text: Ne̱ Yohanee agyase̱po̱‑ɔ na Yudaanyi ko̱ a le̱e̱ ba gyiiri ikii bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ba bɔ asuu‑o si.

ref: They went to John and said, “Rabbi, you spoke about a man when you were with him east of the Jordan. He is now baptizing people, and everyone is going to him.”
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa yɔ Yohanee ase̱, ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔkaapo̱po̱, fo̱ a nyiŋŋi ɔnyare̱ ne̱ ɔɔ ba fo̱ ase̱ nno̱ŋ a bo̱ŋbe̱‑ɔ se̱ aaa? Ɔko̱ ne̱ fo̱ a tɔwe̱ mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ, Yeesuu ne̱ ane̱ de. Mbe̱yɔmɔ ɔ maa se̱ ɔ bɔ asuu, amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ɔke̱maa naa mò̱ ase̱ ɔ bɔ asuu! A bware fe̱yɛ ɔ waa ane̱ŋ aaa?”

ref: John replied: No one can do anything unless God in heaven allows it.
text: Ne̱ Yohanee a be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “A bware. Ɔke̱maa na aketɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a sa mò̱‑ɔ.

ref: You surely remember how I told you that I am not the Messiah. I am only the one sent ahead of him.
text: Mo̱ne̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ye̱re̱ mo̱‑rɔ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ n gye̱ mo̱ e̱ gye̱ Kristoo‑o, amaa Wuribware̱ a suŋ mo̱ bo̱ gye̱ mò̱ ŋkpɛɛ ne̱e̱.

ref: At a wedding the groom is the one who gets married. The best man is glad just to be there and to hear the groom's voice. That's why I am so glad.
text: Kristoo du fe̱yɛ ɔko̱fɔ kuri‑o, ne̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ naa mò̱ ase̱‑ɔ du fe̱yɛ mò̱ ɔko̱fɔ‑ɔ. M fe̱raa, n du fe̱yɛ ɔko̱fɔ kuri mò̱ nyare̱ ne̱ ɔ de̱e̱re̱ mò̱ kpa‑ɔ. To, mbe̱yɔmɔ mo̱ a ŋu mò̱ a ba, ne̱ mò̱ a nu mò̱ se̱ŋsa‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ akatɔ a gyi bwe̱e̱tɔ fe̱yɛ ŋkee ɔɔ nya mò̱ ɔko̱fɔ‑ɔ.

ref: Jesus must become more important, while I become less important. The One Who Comes from Heaven
text: A bware fe̱yɛ Yeesuu e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ waa ke̱dabe̱, na mo̱, Yohanee, mɔ ŋ kii kagyingyii.” Mò̱ ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ se̱‑ɔ

ref: God's Son comes from heaven and is above all others. Everyone who comes from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks about earthly things. The one who comes from heaven is above all others.
text: Yeesuu a le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ se̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ɔ kyɔ ɔke̱maa. Ɔko̱ ne̱ baa ko̱we̱ mò̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ mɔ gye̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ se̱sɛ, ne̱ ɔ tɔwe̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ase̱ŋ, amaa mò̱ ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ se̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ kyɔ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ.

ref: He speaks about what he has seen and heard, and yet no one believes him.
text: Mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ oo ŋu‑o na ke̱tɔ ne̱ oo nu‑o, amaa nsɛ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔɔ kɔɔre̱ mò̱ ase̱ŋ gyi?

ref: But everyone who does believe him has shown that God is truthful.
text: Amaa ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ kɔɔre̱ mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ o gyi‑o e̱ ba amo̱ a ɔ bo̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ e̱ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ wuye.

ref: The Son was sent to speak God's message, and he has been given the full power of God's Spirit.
text: Mò̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a suŋ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ tɔwe̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱ŋ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a sa mò̱ mò̱ kufwiiŋe timaa‑o ke̱yaale̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: The Father loves the Son and has given him everything.
text: Mò̱ se̱ Wuribware̱ a kpa mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ taa ke̱tɔ ke̱maa bo̱ waa mò̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ.

ref: Everyone who has faith in the Son has eternal life. But no one who rejects him will ever share in that life, and God will be angry with them forever.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑o o gyi‑o e̱ nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ, amaa ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ maa kɔɔre̱ mò̱ o gyi‑o maa nya ŋkpa pɛɛɛ, na Wuribware̱ kaduŋfwii sii mò̱ se̱.

ref: But Jesus' disciples were really the ones doing the baptizing, and not Jesus himself. Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
text: Kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱raa Yeesuu fɔŋfɔŋ mo̱ŋ bɔ asuu‑o, amaa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ ya bɔ ase̱sɛ asuu.

ref: This time he had to go through Samaria,
text: Saŋ ne̱ ɔ yɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ oo ŋu fe̱yɛ a tiri fe̱yɛ ɔ bo̱rɔ Samariya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱.

ref: “You are a Jew,” she replied, “and I am a Samaritan woman. How can you ask me for a drink of water when Jews and Samaritans won't have anything to do with each other?”
text: Ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ gye̱ Yudaanyi ne̱e̱, ne̱ mo̱‑ɔ n gye̱ Samariyanyi, ne̱ e̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ n sa fo̱ nkyu a fo̱ nuu?” Ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ Yudaa awuye na Samariya awuye mɔ maa nuu ato̱re̱nkyu ko̱ŋko̱‑rɔ. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ bo̱ maa gyi e̱gyare̱ ko̱ŋko̱‑rɔ.

ref: “Sir,” the woman said, “you don't even have a bucket, and the well is deep. Where are you going to get this life-giving water?
text: Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, fo̱ mo̱ŋ de atɔ a fo̱ bo̱ saa nkyu‑o, ne̱ kitiribu‑o mɔ bo̱ keŋ. Ne̱ mfe̱ne̱ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ nya nkyu mɔ ne̱ n sa ŋkpa ne̱ m maa lo̱we̱‑ɔ a fo̱ le̱e̱?

ref: Our ancestor Jacob dug this well for us, and his family and animals got water from it. Are you greater than Jacob?”
text: Fo̱ nyi fe̱yɛ fo̱ kyɔ ane̱ nana Yakubu aaa? Mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔɔ taa kitiribu mɔ sa ane̱. Ne̱ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ na mò̱ gyi‑ana na mò̱ mbo̱ a nuu ke̱mo̱ nkyu‑o.”

ref: But no one who drinks the water I give will ever be thirsty again. The water I give will become in that person a flowing fountain that gives eternal life.”
text: Amaa ɔke̱maa ne̱ o nuu nkyu ne̱ mo̱ e̱ sa mò̱‑ɔ fe̱raa, burufo maa lɛɛ nya mò̱ pɛɛɛ. Nkyu ne̱ mo̱ e̱ sa mò̱‑ɔ i sii mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱‑rɔ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kabo̱ŋgyi ne̱ ka sa nkyu ne̱ m maa lo̱we̱‑ɔ. Ane̱ŋ a nkyu mɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ n sa mò̱ ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ.”

ref: The woman replied, “Sir, please give me a drink of that water! Then I won't get thirsty and have to come to this well again.”
text: Ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, sa mo̱ nkyu amo̱, na burufo maa lɛɛ nya mo̱ pɛɛɛ. N gye̱ ane̱ŋ, mo̱ e̱ de̱e̱ ba mfe̱e̱ a m bo̱ saa nkyu.”

ref: The woman answered, “I don't have a husband.” “That's right,” Jesus replied, “you're telling the truth. You don't have a husband. You have already been married five times, and the man you are now living with isn't your husband.”
text: Ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ŋ de kuri.” Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ mo̱ŋ fo̱ kpa fe̱yɛ fo̱ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ mo̱ŋ de kuri, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ a waare̱e̱ anyare̱ anuu, amaa ɔnyare̱ ne̱ fo̱ te mò̱ aye̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ fo̱ kuri kase̱ŋtiŋ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱ fo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱‑ɔ.”

ref: The woman said, “Sir, I can see that you are a prophet.
text: Ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, mo̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ fo̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ a akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ ɔko̱.

ref: Jesus said to her: Believe me, the time is coming when you won't worship the Father either on this mountain or in Jerusalem.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔpe̱kye̱e̱, kɔɔre̱ mo̱ gyi fe̱yɛ saŋ ko̱ e̱ ba, ne̱ ɔko̱ maa lɛɛ suŋ Wuribware̱ ke̱be̱e̱ mɔ se̱ bɛɛɛ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ.

ref: You Samaritans don't really know the one you worship. But we Jews do know the God we worship, and by using us, God will save the world.
text: Mo̱ne̱ Samariya awuye mo̱ŋ nyi mò̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i suŋ‑o, amaa ane̱ Yudaa awuye fe̱raa nyi mò̱ ne̱ ane̱ i suŋ‑o, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Yudaa awuye‑ro ne̱ ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ mo̱rɔwe̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ e̱ le̱e̱ a ɔ ba.

ref: The woman said, “I know that the Messiah will come. He is the one we call Christ. When he comes, he will explain everything to us.”
text: Ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ Masiya ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ Kristoo‑o e̱ ba. Mò̱ ya ba, ɔ tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa a o gywii ane̱.”

ref: “I am that one,” Jesus told her, “and I am speaking to you now.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ gye̱ mò̱, mo̱ e̱ sa se̱ŋsa fo̱ ase̱‑ɔ.”

ref: The disciples returned about this time and were surprised to find Jesus talking with a woman. But none of them asked him what he wanted or why he was talking with her.
text: Saŋ ne̱ ba sa se̱ŋsa‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a ba, ne̱ aa kpe̱ŋ bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔɔ sa se̱ŋsa ɔkye̱e̱ ase̱. Amaa bamo̱ ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ bise ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ntɔ ya waa?”, ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ bise Yeesuu mɔ fe̱yɛ, “E̱me̱ne̱ ne̱e̱ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ sa se̱ŋsa fo̱ i gywii ɔkye̱e̱ amo̱?”

ref: The woman left her water jar and ran back into town, where she said to the people,
text: Ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱ amo̱ a yɔwe̱ mò̱ nkyu na kalɔ bo̱ ye̱ra, ne̱ oo kiŋŋi yɔ maŋ‑nɔ ya tɔwe̱ gywii ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ,

ref: “Come and see a man who told me everything I have ever done! Could he be the Messiah?”
text: “Mo̱nꞌ ba bo̱ ke̱e̱ ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ a dɛɛ waa‑ɔ gywii mo̱‑ɔ ooo. N gye̱ Kristoo ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱?”

ref: Everyone in town went out to see Jesus.
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ a kpɔwe̱ le̱e̱ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ a bo̱ ya de̱e̱re̱ Yeesuu.

ref: While this was happening, Jesus' disciples were saying to him, “Teacher, please eat something.”
text: Ɔkye̱e̱ amo̱ a yii maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a ko̱re̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔkaapo̱po̱, kɔɔre̱ ateese gyi.”

ref: But Jesus told them, “I have food you don't know anything about.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “N de ateese ne̱ mo̱ i gyi, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi amo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ.”

ref: His disciples started asking each other, “Has someone brought him something to eat?”
text: Ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a kii ba bise abɛɛ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔko̱ a baa mò̱ ateese ne̱e̱ bɛɛɛ?”

ref: Jesus said: My food is to do what God wants! He is the one who sent me, and I must finish the work that he gave me to do.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ ateese e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ mò̱ ne̱ oo suŋ mo̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ e̱ kpa‑ɔ, na n lo̱we̱ asuŋ ke̱maa ne̱ ɔɔ sa mo̱ a ŋ waa‑ɔ.

ref: You may say there are still four months until harvest time. But I tell you to look, and you will see that the fields are ripe and ready to harvest.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ya tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘A saŋ aferi ana pwɛɛ na yaabraa ke̱te̱ŋŋe̱be̱e̱ dɛɛ fo̱,’ mo̱‑ɔ mɔ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ yase̱ akatɔ de̱e̱re̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ adɔɔ‑rɔ a mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱. Yaabraa a fo̱ e̱te̱ŋŋe̱.

ref: Even now the harvest workers are receiving their reward by gathering a harvest that brings eternal life. Then everyone who planted the seed and everyone who harvests the crop will celebrate together.
text: Mò̱ ne̱ ɔ te̱ŋŋe̱ na o swii yaabraa mɔ bo̱ tii abɛɛ se̱ a ɔ sa mo̱‑ɔ e̱ nya kakɔka, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ yaabraa amo̱ gye̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ ba nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ odwiipo̱‑ɔ na ɔte̱ŋŋe̱po̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ akatɔ i gyi.

ref: So the saying proves true, “Some plant the seed, and others harvest the crop.”
text: Ke̱kpare̱ mɔ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ɔko̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ o dwii, ne̱ ɔko̱ mɔ e̱ kpa.’

ref: A lot of Samaritans in that town put their faith in Jesus because the woman had said, “This man told me everything I have ever done.”
text: Ne̱ Samariya awuye bwe̱e̱tɔ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ maŋ amo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ a kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱ amo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ mò̱, ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ, a dɛɛ waa‑ɔ gywii mò̱‑ɔ se̱.

ref: They came and asked him to stay in their town, and he stayed on for two days.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ Samariya awuye‑o a ba mò̱ ase̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa ko̱re̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ ba bo̱ kye̱na bamo̱ ase̱. Ne̱ Yeesuu a kye̱na bamo̱ ase̱ ŋke ŋnyɔ.

ref: Many more Samaritans put their faith in Jesus because of what they heard him say.
text: Ase̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱‑ɔ se̱, ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ a be̱e̱ kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi.

ref: They told the woman, “We no longer have faith in Jesus just because of what you told us. We have heard him ourselves, and we are certain that he is the Savior of the world!” Jesus Heals an Official's Son (Matthew
text: Ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ gywii ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mbe̱yɔmɔ fe̱raa ane̱ a kɔɔre̱ gyi. N gye̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ se̱ ne̱ ane̱ a kɔɔre̱ gyi, amaa ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ fɔŋfɔŋ a nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱‑ɔ se̱. Ane̱ nyi fe̱yɛ mò̱ e̱ gye̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ ɔmo̱rɔwe̱po̱.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya kayaagyi ko̱‑ɔ

ref: Jesus had said, “Prophets are honored everywhere, except in their own country.” Then two days later he left
text: a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Yeesuu fɔŋfɔŋ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ ke̱maa, bo̱ maa bu mò̱ mò̱ aye̱ fe̱yɛ to̱ŋ ko̱‑ɔ.”

ref: and went to Galilee. The people there welcomed him, because they had gone to the festival in Jerusalem and had seen everything he had done.
text: Mò̱ a fo̱ Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ, ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ a kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, saŋ ne̱ baa yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm ya gyi Wuribware̱-a‑kya-ane̱-yɔwe̱ a ateese‑o mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, baa ŋu ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ɔɔ waa‑ɔ.

ref: While Jesus was in Galilee, he returned to the village of Cana, where he had turned the water into wine. There was an official in Capernaum whose son was sick.
text: Ne̱ oo kiŋŋi ba Kana maŋ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ dɛɛ ba nkyu bo̱ buruwi nta‑ɔ. Saŋ amo̱‑ɔ ɔbre̱sɛ ko̱ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ Kapaaniyum, mfe̱ŋ ne̱ mò̱ gyi nyaŋsɛ e̱ lɔ‑ɔ. Ɔnyare̱ mɔ e̱ waa asuŋ ɔ sa owure ne̱e̱.

ref: And when the man heard that Jesus had come from Judea, he went and begged him to keep his son from dying.
text: Mò̱ a nu fe̱yɛ Yeesuu a le̱e̱ Yude̱ya ba Galile̱ya‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ mò̱ ase̱ ya ko̱re̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ sa a bo̱ yɔ Kapaaniyum maŋ‑nɔ na ɔ ya kya mò̱ gyi ne̱ ɔ lɔ ɔ kpa a o wu‑o sa mò̱.

ref: Jesus told the official, “You won't have faith unless you see miracles and wonders!”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ɔke̱maa maa kɔɔre̱ mo̱ o gyi, amɔ oo ŋu atɔ dabe̱ na akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa‑ɔ pwɛɛ.”

ref: The man replied, “Lord, please come before my son dies!”
text: Ne̱ ɔbre̱sɛ‑ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, ba a ane̱ yɔ pwɛɛ na mo̱ gyi‑o dɛɛ wu.”

ref: Jesus then said, “Your son will live. Go on home to him.” The man believed Jesus and started back home.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nare̱, fo̱ gyi‑o e̱ kpaare̱.” Ne̱ ɔbre̱sɛ‑ɔ a kɔɔre̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱‑ɔ gyi, ne̱ oo kiŋŋi ɔ yɔ pe̱.

ref: Some of the official's servants met him along the road and told him, “Your son is better!”
text: Mò̱ a kiŋŋi ɔ yɔ pe̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱ ayaafɔre̱ a gyaŋŋaa mò̱ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ gyi‑o maa se̱ ɔ kpaare̱.”

ref: He asked them when the boy got better, and they answered, “The fever left him yesterday at one o'clock.”
text: Ne̱ oo bise bamo̱ saŋ ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱ mò̱ gyi amo̱ a ko̱lɔ‑ɔ a bɔ‑ɔ. Ne̱ baa be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ndee mpase̱ ne̱ aŋuafwii amo̱ a yɔwe̱ mò̱.”

ref: The boy's father realized that at one o'clock the day before, Jesus had told him, “Your son will live!” So the man and everyone in his family put their faith in Jesus.
text: Ne̱ kayaagyi‑o mò̱ se̱ a nyiŋŋi si fe̱yɛ saŋ ko̱ŋko̱ amo̱ dɛɛ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ gyi‑o e̱ kpaare̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱. Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱ aa mò̱ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ a kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi.

ref: This was the second miracle that Jesus worked after he left Judea and went to Galilee.
text: Amo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ atɔ dabe̱ nyɔse̱po̱ ne̱ Yeesuu a waa le̱e̱ saŋ ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ Yude̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ ba Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ.

ref: Beside the pool was a man who had been sick for
text: Saŋ amo̱ ɔnyare̱ ko̱ mɔ da mfe̱ŋ dɛɛ, ne̱ ɔɔ lɔ nsu adusa na mburuwa.

ref: When Jesus saw the man and realized that he had been crippled for a long time, he asked him, “Do you want to be healed?”
text: Yeesuu a ŋu mò̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ kyɔ pini fe̱yɛ oo di mfe̱ŋ kyee, ne̱ oo bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ fo̱ kpaare̱ aaa?”

ref: The man answered, “Sir, I don't have anyone to put me in the pool when the water is stirred up. I try to get in, but someone else always gets there first.”
text: Ne̱ ɔlɔpo̱‑ɔ a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ‑ɔ ya bo̱ waa kyuwuri nkyu‑o‑ro lo̱we̱, mo̱ŋ de ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ taa mo̱ a ɔ bo̱ waa nkyu‑o‑ro‑o. Amaa mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ e̱ maa se̱ mo̱ e̱ yɔ, amɔ ɔko̱ a bo̱ kyo̱ŋ mo̱ se̱ gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ ya lwee nkyu‑o‑ro.”

ref: Jesus told him, “Pick up your mat and walk!”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ko̱so̱, taa fo̱ kraŋ na fo̱ nare̱.”

ref: Right then the man was healed. He picked up his mat and started walking around. The day on which this happened was a Sabbath.
text: Puri amo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ɔɔ kpaare̱, ne̱ ɔɔ taa mò̱ kraŋ, ne̱ ɔɔ nare̱ o yii. Kake nsi amo̱ mɔ gye̱ kukyure kake.

ref: When the Jewish leaders saw the man carrying his mat, they said to him, “This is the Sabbath! No one is allowed to carry a mat on the Sabbath.”
text: Ne̱ Yudaa awuye abre̱sɛ ko̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii ɔnyare̱ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya mò̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ndɔɔ gye̱ kukyure kake, amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ fo̱ mo̱ŋ de kpa fe̱yɛ fo̱ so̱rɔ kraŋ kake mɔ.”

ref: But he replied, “The man who healed me told me to pick up my mat and walk.”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔnyare̱ ne̱ ɔɔ kya mo̱‑ɔ ya tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Taa fo̱ kraŋ na fo̱ nare̱.’ ”

ref: They asked him, “Who is this man that told you to pick up your mat and walk?”
text: Ne̱ baa bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nsɛ ya tɔwe̱ gywii fo̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ko̱so̱ taa fo̱ kraŋ na fo̱ nare̱’?”

ref: But he did not know who Jesus was, and Jesus had left because of the crowd.
text: Amaa ɔnyare̱ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya mò̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ nyi mò̱ ne̱ ɔɔ kya mò̱‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, ase̱sɛ a kyɔ mfe̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ se̱‑ɔ, Yeesuu a le̱e̱ o yii.

ref: Later, Jesus met the man in the temple and told him, “You are now well. But don't sin anymore or something worse might happen to you.”
text: Amo̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu a ŋu mò̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mbe̱yɔmɔ fo̱ a kpaare̱, amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ yɔwe̱ e̱bɔye̱ ko̱waa. N gye̱ ane̱ŋ, ko̱tɔko̱ e̱ waa fo̱ na ko̱ kyɔ ke̱mo̱‑ɔ.”

ref: The man left and told the leaders that Jesus was the one who had healed him.
text: Ne̱ ɔnyare̱ amo̱ a le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ ya tɔwe̱ gywii Yudaa awuye amo̱ fe̱yɛ Yeesuu ya kya mò̱.

ref: They started making a lot of trouble for Jesus because he did things like this on the Sabbath.
text: Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya ɔko̱ kukyure kake‑o si, ne̱ baa le̱e̱ ba tɔɔraa mò̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: But Jesus said, “My Father has never stopped working, and this is why I keep on working.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “N se̱ i suŋ asuŋ saŋ ke̱maa, amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ mo̱‑ɔ mo̱ i suŋ saŋ ke̱maa.”

ref: Now the leaders wanted to kill Jesus for two reasons. First, he had broken the law of the Sabbath. But even worse, he had said God was his Father, which made him equal with God. The Son's Authority
text: Ase̱ŋ mɔ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱‑ɔ a sa ne̱ Yudaa awuye abre̱sɛ a pee akatɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ mɔɔ mò̱. N gye̱ sa ne̱ bo̱ yɛ oo kye bamo̱ mbraa ne̱ ŋ ye̱re̱ bo̱ sa kukyure kake‑o wo̱re̱ ne̱e̱, amaa ɔɔ be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ e̱ gye̱ mò̱ se̱, amo̱‑ɔ e̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ aa Wuribware̱ ta ne̱e̱. Ke̱yaale̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑o de‑o

ref: Jesus told the people: I tell you for certain the Son cannot do anything on his own. He can do only what he sees the Father doing, and he does exactly what he sees the Father do.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya se̱ ɔ be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ mo̱, Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑o, maa taare̱ a ŋ waa ke̱tɔ ke̱maa mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ se̱. Mo̱ e̱ waa ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ i ŋu fe̱yɛ n se̱ e̱ waa‑ɔ ne̱e̱. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ n se̱ e̱ waa‑ɔ ne̱ ŋ gbaa mo̱ e̱ waa,

ref: The Father loves the Son and has shown him everything he does. The Father will show him even greater things, and you will be amazed.
text: a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ n se̱ e̱ kpa mo̱ ase̱ŋ, ne̱ ɔ kaapo̱ mo̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ e̱ waa‑ɔ. N se̱ e̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ atɔ dabe̱ dabe̱ a ŋ waa na a kyɔ amo̱‑ɔ, na a kpe̱ŋ mo̱ne̱ pɛɛɛ e̱ye̱e̱.

ref: The Father doesn't judge anyone, but he has made his Son the judge of everyone.
text: N se̱ fɔŋfɔŋ maa gyi ase̱ŋ o bu ɔko̱ ke̱pɔ, amaa ɔɔ sa mo̱ kpa na ke̱yaale̱ŋ fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ m bo̱ gyi ase̱ŋ, na m bu ke̱pɔ.

ref: The Father wants all people to honor the Son as much as they honor him. When anyone refuses to honor the Son, this is the same as refusing to honor the Father who sent him.
text: Wuribware̱ a waa amo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱ a ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ nya bu mò̱ gyi‑o fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ba bu mò̱‑ɔ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ maa bu mo̱, Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑o, maa bu Wuribware̱ ne̱ oo suŋ mo̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: I tell you for certain that everyone who hears my message and has faith in the one who sent me has eternal life and will never be condemned. They have already gone from death to life.
text: Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa ne̱ o nu mo̱ ase̱ŋ, ne̱ ɔ kɔɔre̱ mò̱ ne̱ oo suŋ mo̱‑ɔ o gyi‑o e̱ nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ. Kase̱ŋtiŋ si o de ane̱ŋ a ŋkpa‑ɔ le̱e̱ saŋ ne̱ ɔɔ kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi‑o. Wuribware̱ maa bu mò̱ ne̱ ɔɔ kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi‑o ke̱pɔ.

ref: I tell you for certain the time will come, and it is already here, when all of the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God. And those who listen to it will live!
text: Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ te mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ne̱ baa wu‑o. Amaa saŋ e̱ ba, saŋ amo̱ a kyɔ fo̱ gbaa, fe̱yɛ, bamo̱ ya nu ase̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱, Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑o, e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ, ne̱ bamo̱ ya kɔɔre̱ gyi, ba nya ŋkpa po̱pwɛɛ.

ref: The Father has the power to give life, and he has given that same power to the Son.
text: Wuribware̱ e̱ gye̱ ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ kuŋu, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ mo̱, mò̱ gyi‑o, gbaa a kii ŋkpa na kukyure amo̱ kuŋu.

ref: And he has given his Son the right to judge everyone, because he is the Son of Man.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ sa mo̱, mò̱ gyi‑o, kpa fe̱yɛ n gyi ase̱sɛ ase̱ŋ, na m bu bamo̱ ke̱pɔ bɛɛɛ ke̱be̱ŋ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ n gye̱ dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o dɛɛ ne̱e̱.

ref: and they will come out of their graves. Everyone who has done good things will rise to life, but everyone who has done evil things will rise and be condemned.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ mo̱ne̱ ma sa a amo̱‑ɔ kpe̱ŋ mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱. Saŋ ko̱ e̱ ba ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa wu‑o pɛɛɛ i nu mo̱ bo̱re̱, na bo̱ le̱e̱ bamo̱ agye̱raŋta‑rɔ ba, na bo̱ ma lɛɛ wu. Bamo̱ ne̱ baa waa itimaa‑o e̱ nya kukyure, na bamo̱ ne̱ baa waa e̱bɔye̱‑ɔ mɔ kɔɔre̱ bamo̱ ke̱pɔ kakɔka.” Yeesuu aye̱re̱rɔpo̱

ref: I cannot do anything on my own. The Father sent me, and he is the one who told me how to judge. I judge with fairness, because I obey him, and I don't just try to please myself. Witnesses to Jesus
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya se̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ma taare̱ a ŋ waa ke̱tɔ ke̱maa mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ se̱. Mo̱ i gyi ase̱ŋ ane̱ŋ ne̱ n se̱ a kaapo̱ mo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱, amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ mo̱ i bu ke̱pɔ bɛɛɛ ke̱be̱ŋ kpa se̱ ne̱e̱. Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ n gye̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ e̱ kpa‑ɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa, amaa ke̱tɔ ne̱ mò̱ ne̱ oo suŋ mo̱‑ɔ e̱ kpa‑ɔ wo̱re̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa.

ref: But there is someone else who speaks for me, and I know what he says is true.
text: Amaa ɔko̱ dɛɛ ɔ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ mo̱ ase̱ŋ gywii mo̱ne̱, mò̱ e̱ gye̱ Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱‑ɔ. Ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ mo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ kaye̱ba ne̱e̱.

ref: I don't depend on what people say about me, but I tell you these things so that you may be saved.
text: Amaa n gye̱ se̱sɛ dimaadi ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ ye̱re̱ mo̱‑rɔ na mo̱nꞌ ŋu fe̱yɛ mo̱ atɔ ke̱kaapo̱‑ɔ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱e̱. Mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱ a Wuribware̱ nya mo̱rɔwe̱ mo̱ne̱.

ref: John was a lamp that gave a lot of light, and you were glad to enjoy his light for a while.
text: Yohanee dɛɛ o du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fetiri ne̱ ɔko̱ a kyaŋŋe̱, ne̱ ɔ laŋŋe̱‑rɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ. Katiŋ ko̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ mo̱ne̱ akatɔ a gyi ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a ŋu mò̱ ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ.

ref: But something more important than John speaks for me. I mean the things that the Father has given me to do! All of these speak for me and prove that the Father sent me.
text: Amaa n de atɔ ko̱ ne̱ a ye̱re̱ mo̱‑rɔ, ne̱ a ko̱ŋ a kyo̱ŋ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yohanee ye̱re̱ mo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, atɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ atɔ ne̱ n se̱ a sa mo̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ waa‑ɔ. Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ a tɔwe̱ mo̱ ase̱ŋ, ne̱ a kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ ya suŋ mo̱.

ref: The Father who sent me also speaks for me, but you have never heard his voice or seen him face to face.
text: Ne̱ n se̱ Wuribware̱ ne̱ oo suŋ mo̱‑ɔ gbaa a tɔwe̱ mo̱ ase̱ŋ. N gye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ a nu mò̱ bo̱re̱, bɛɛɛ mo̱ne̱ a ŋu mò̱ akatɔ‑rɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: I don't care about human praise,
text: To, n gye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ kpa a mo̱nꞌ kyo̱rɔ mo̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: but I do know that none of you love God.
text: Amaa ŋ nyi ase̱sɛ suyo ne̱ mo̱ne̱ gye̱‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ maa kpa Wuribware̱ mo̱ne̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ.

ref: I have come with my Father's authority, and you have not welcomed me. But you will welcome people who come on their own.
text: N se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ‑nɔ ne̱ mo̱ a ba, amaa mo̱ne̱ maa kpa a mo̱nꞌ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ gyi. Amaa ɔko̱ ya ba mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ke̱yaale̱ŋ bo̱ ba fe̱raa, mo̱ne̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ mò̱ a mo̱nꞌ gyi.

ref: How could you possibly believe? You like to have your friends praise you, and you don't care about praise that the only God can give!
text: Mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱ kyo̱rɔ abɛɛ, amaa mo̱ne̱ maa kpa fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ ne̱ mò̱ wo̱re̱ kpeŋ e̱ bo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ kyo̱rɔ mo̱ne̱. Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ maa taare̱ a mo̱nꞌ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ gyi‑o.

ref: Don't think that I will be the one to accuse you to the Father. You have put your hope in Moses, yet he is the very one who will accuse you.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ma kaŋ fa fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ne̱ kuŋu si e̱bɔye̱ a n gywii n se̱. Wuribware̱ mbraa wo̱re̱ ne̱ ane̱ ɔde̱daapo̱ Mosis a kyo̱rɛɛ‑ɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a taa mo̱ne̱ te̱maa pɛɛɛ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ se̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ po̱rɔ mo̱ne̱.

ref: Moses wrote about me, and if you had believed Moses, you would have believed me.
text: Mo̱ne̱ a dɛɛ kɔɔre̱ Mosis gyi ne̱e̱‑ɔ, weetee mo̱ne̱ a kyɔ kɔɔre̱ ŋ gbaa mo̱ gyi, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ ya kyo̱rɛɛ mo̱ ase̱ŋ.

ref: But if you don't believe what Moses wrote, how can you believe what I say?
text: Amaa mo̱ne̱ a mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Mosis a kyo̱rɛɛ‑ɔ gyi‑o si‑o, mo̱ne̱ maa taare̱ a mo̱nꞌ kɔɔre̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ gyi.”

ref: The disciples gathered them up and filled twelve large baskets with what was left over from the five barley loaves.
text: Ne̱ baa swii bodobodoo mpupuriase̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ŋ ya bo̱rɔ ake kudu anyɔ.

ref: The disciples wanted to take him into the boat, but suddenly the boat reached the shore where they were headed. The Bread That Gives Life
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ baa taa mò̱ wɔre̱ se̱ waa ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ. Bamo̱ a taa mò̱ waa‑rɔ‑ɔ, a mo̱ŋ kyee ne̱ baa daŋ kideŋbe si mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ba yɔ‑ɔ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ ase̱sɛ a buwi kpa Yeesuu‑o

ref: They replied, “What miracle will you work, so that we can have faith in you? What will you do?
text: Ne̱ baa lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ waa na a kpe̱ŋ ane̱ e̱ye̱e̱, na ane̱ ye̱re̱ ane̱ŋ se̱ bo̱ kɔɔre̱ fo̱ gyi? Tɔwe̱ gywii ane̱.

ref: No one can come to me, unless the Father who sent me makes them want to come. But if they do come, I will raise them to life on the last day.
text: Ŋ yɛ ɛɛɛ ɔko̱ maa taare̱ a ɔ ba mo̱ ase̱, amɔ n se̱ ne̱ oo suŋ mo̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱‑ɔ a baa mò̱ mo̱ ase̱, na n kyiŋŋi mò̱ a ɔ ba ŋkpa na kukyure‑ro kake ne̱ kaye̱ mɔ e̱ gye̱ kɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: “There is a boy here who has five small loaves of barley bread and two fish. But what good is that with all these people?”
text: “Kanyaŋsɛɛ ko̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ mfe̱e̱ ne̱ o de bodobodoo agyi anuu na ŋkiŋgyi ngyingyii ŋnyɔ‑ɔ. Amo̱‑ɔ e̱ fo̱ ase̱sɛ de̱maŋte̱ mɔ aaa?”

ref: They got into a boat and started across for Capernaum. Later that evening Jesus had still not come to them,
text: Ne̱ baa lwee ko̱re̱e̱‑rɔ, ne̱ baa kiŋŋi yɔ kamɛɛ‑rɔ ya te̱ŋ ke̱pare̱ dabe̱‑ɔ‑rɔ Kapaaniyum a ke̱be̱gya‑ɔ. Kanye a fo̱ na Yeesuu mo̱ŋ tɛɛ ba mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ ase̱.

ref: Jesus crossed Lake Galilee, which was also known as Lake Tiberias.
text: Amo̱ kamɛɛ ne̱ Yeesuu a ya te̱ŋ Galile̱ya ke̱pare̱ dabe̱‑ɔ‑rɔ. Ke̱pare̱ dabe̱ mɔ dɛɛ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ Tibeeriyas.

ref: Philip answered, “Don't you know that it would take almost a year's wages just to buy only a little bread for each of these people?”
text: Ne̱ Filipo a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Hare̱e̱ ɔke̱maa e̱ nya mbe̱gyɛɛ mbe̱gyɛɛ gbaa, bamo̱ ya ba siidii ŋkpe̱ŋ ŋnyɔ (GHC

ref: Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter, was one of the disciples. He spoke up and said,
text: Ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ ɔko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Andruu, ne̱ ɔ gye̱ Simɔŋ Peetroo mò̱ tire‑o yɛ,

ref: men were in the crowd.
text: ) bo̱ sɔɔ bodobodoo, ɔ maa fo̱ bamo̱.”

ref: Jesus took the bread in his hands and gave thanks to God. Then he passed the bread to the people, and he did the same with the fish, until everyone had plenty to eat.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a taa bodobodoo‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ sa Wuribware̱ aŋsɛ we̱e̱ se̱, ne̱ oo ke‑ro sa ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ te mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ɔɔ waa ŋkiŋgyi‑o mɔ. Ne̱ bodobodoo‑o na ŋkiŋgyi‑o a waa ko̱kyɔ.

ref: The people ate all they wanted, and Jesus told his disciples to gather up the leftovers, so that nothing would be wasted.
text: Bamo̱ pɛɛɛ a gyi ane̱ŋ ne̱ ba taare̱‑ɔ. Saŋ ne̱ baa gyi kpo̱ne̱‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ swii mpupuriase̱e̱, mo̱ne̱ ma sa a n twe̱e̱.”

ref: Jesus realized that they would try to force him to be their king. So he went up on a mountain, where he could be alone. Jesus Walks on the Water (Matthew
text: Yeesuu a nyi fe̱yɛ baa waa siraa a bo̱ bo̱ kra mò̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ se̱ bo̱ gyi kuwure‑o si‑o, ne̱ ɔ be̱e̱ o yii abe̱e̱‑rɔ mò̱ wo̱re̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a nare̱ nkyu si‑o (Matiyo

ref: One of the prophets wrote, “God will teach all of them.” And so everyone who listens to the Father and learns from him will come to me.
text: Wuribware̱ akyaamɛɛ bo̱ko̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ bo̱ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Wuribware̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ kaapo̱ ɔke̱maa.’ Ɔke̱maa ne̱ o nu n se̱ ase̱ŋ, ne̱ o suye a ɔ le̱e̱ mò̱ ase̱‑ɔ e̱ ba mo̱ ase̱.

ref: That evening, Jesus' disciples went down to the lake.
text: Ke̱de̱e̱pwɛta a fo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a ba nkyu‑ro.

ref: and a strong wind was making the water rough.
text: Saŋ amo̱‑ɔ, afwii dabe̱ ko̱ mɔ e̱ waa na a da alaŋkpare̱.

ref: When the disciples had rowed for five or six kilometers, they saw Jesus walking on the water. He kept coming closer to the boat, and they were terrified.
text: Mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ mɔ a pare̱ waa e̱maye̱re̱ e̱sa bo̱ yɔ e̱na‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa ŋu Yeesuu naa nkyu si ɔ ba mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ bo̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ kufu a nya bamo̱.

ref: But he said, “I am Jesus! Don't be afraid!”
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ ne̱e̱! Mo̱ne̱ ma sa a kufu nya mo̱ne̱.”

ref: some boats from Tiberias sailed near the place where the crowd had eaten the bread for which the Lord had given thanks.
text: E̱ko̱re̱e̱ ne̱ e̱ ya le̱e̱ Tibeeriyas maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ e̱ko̱ a bo̱ daŋ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a sa ne̱ bodobodoo amo̱ a waa ko̱kyɔ‑ɔ.

ref: They saw that Jesus and his disciples had left. Then they got into the boats and went to Capernaum to look for Jesus.
text: Ase̱sɛ amo̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ Yeesuu na mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa lwee e̱ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ yɔ Kapaaniyum maŋ ase̱ ba buwi ba kpa Yeesuu. Yeesuu e̱ gye̱ bodobodoo ne̱ ɔ sa ŋkpa‑ɔ

ref: They found him on the west side of the lake and asked, “Rabbi, when did you get here?”
text: Ase̱sɛ amo̱ a te̱ŋ ke̱pare̱ dabe̱ amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa ŋu Yeesuu mfe̱ŋ, ne̱ baa bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔkaapo̱po̱, nsaŋ mo̱ ne̱ fo̱ a ba mfe̱e̱?”

ref: Jesus answered, “I tell you for certain that you are not looking for me because you saw the miracles, but because you ate all the food you wanted.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ, ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i buwi mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa mo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ a gyi bodobodoo‑o kpo̱ne̱. Amaa n gye̱ sa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a ŋu akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ a waa‑ɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ gya mo̱ se̱.

ref: “What exactly does God want us to do?” the people asked.
text: Ne̱ baa bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Kusuŋ mo̱ ne̱ ane̱ waa na ke̱ pre̱ Wuribware̱?”

ref: For example, when our ancestors were in the desert, they were given manna to eat. It happened just as the Scriptures say, ‘God gave them bread from heaven to eat.’ ”
text: Saŋ ne̱ Mosis na ane̱ nana‑ana te kimukee‑ro‑o, baa dɛɛ gyi ateese ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ manaa , fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ɔɔ sa ne̱ ateese a le̱e̱ so̱so̱ ba kaase̱ ne̱ baa gyi.’ ”

ref: Jesus then told them, “I tell you for certain that Moses wasn't the one who gave you bread from heaven. My Father is the one who gives you the true bread from heaven.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “N gye̱ Mosis fɔŋfɔŋ ya sa mo̱ne̱ ateese amo̱. Aa le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱ ne̱e̱. Na kase̱ŋtiŋ si, ateese amo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ ateese ne̱ aa le̱e̱ so̱so̱ ba kaase̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ amo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ. Amaa n se̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ sa mo̱ne̱ ateese kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱ aa le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ se̱‑ɔ.

ref: And the bread that God gives is the one who came down from heaven to give life to the world.”
text: Ateese ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ sa mo̱ne̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ mò̱ ne̱ oo suŋ le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ bo̱ sa kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ.”

ref: The people said, “Sir, give us this bread and don't ever stop!”
text: Ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ gywii Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱, amo̱ fe̱raa, sa ane̱ ateese amo̱ kake ke̱maa.”

ref: Jesus replied: I am the bread that gives life! No one who comes to me will ever be hungry. No one who has faith in me will ever be thirsty.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ gye̱ ateese ne̱ a sa ŋkpa na kukyure amo̱‑ɔ. Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ ba mo̱ ase̱‑ɔ, ako̱ŋ maa nya mò̱ pɛɛɛ. Ne̱ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ o gyi‑o, burufo maa nya mò̱ pɛɛɛ.

ref: I have told you already that you have seen me and still do not have faith in me.
text: To, mbe̱yɔmɔ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ a ŋu mo̱, amaa mo̱ne̱ maa kɔɔre̱ mo̱ a mo̱nꞌ gyi.

ref: Everything and everyone that the Father has given me will come to me, and I won't turn any of them away.
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ n se̱ a sa mo̱‑ɔ e̱ ba mo̱ ase̱. Ma gya mò̱ a m bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ daa.

ref: I didn't come from heaven to do what I want! I came to do what the Father wants me to do. He sent me,
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, mo̱ŋ le̱e̱ so̱so̱ ba ne̱e̱ a m bo̱ waa mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ke̱pre̱ se̱ ko̱tɔko̱, amaa mo̱ a ba ne̱e̱ a m bo̱ waa amo̱ ne̱ a pre̱ mò̱ ne̱ oo suŋ mo̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱‑ɔ.

ref: and he wants to make certain that none of the ones he has given me will be lost. Instead, he wants me to raise them to life on the last day.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ mò̱ ne̱ oo suŋ mo̱‑ɔ e̱ kpa‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ waa mo̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, bamo̱ ɔke̱maa maa kaŋ fo̱ mo̱ se̱, na n kyiŋŋi bamo̱ pɛɛɛ baa ŋkpa‑rɔ kake nsi ne̱ kaye̱ mɔ e̱ gye̱ kɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: My Father wants everyone who sees the Son to have faith in him and to have eternal life. Then I will raise them to life on the last day.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ n se̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ de̱e̱re̱ mo̱, Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑o, ne̱ ɔ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ o gyi‑o e̱ nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ m mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ, na n kyiŋŋi mò̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro kake nsi ne̱ kaye̱ mɔ e̱ gye̱ kɛɛ‑ɔ.”

ref: The people started grumbling because Jesus had said he was the bread that had come down from heaven.
text: Ne̱ Yudaa awuye abre̱sɛ ne̱ baa nu mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ iduŋ a fwii, ne̱ baa tɛɛ fe̱yɛ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ gye̱ ateese ne̱ aa le̱e̱ so̱so̱ ba kaase̱‑ɔ.”

ref: They were asking each other, “Isn't he Jesus, the son of Joseph? Don't we know his father and mother? How can he say that he has come down from heaven?”
text: Ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔnyare̱ mɔ, n gye̱ Yo̱sɛf mò̱ gyi ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Yeesuu‑o ne̱e̱ bɛɛɛ? Ane̱ nyi mò̱ se̱ na mò̱ nyi. Ne̱ e̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ ɔ yɛ ɔɔ le̱e̱ so̱so̱ ba ne̱e̱?”

ref: Jesus told them: Stop grumbling!
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ yɔwe̱ ke̱tɛɛ.

ref: The only one who has seen the Father is the one who has come from him. No one else has ever seen the Father.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ma fa fe̱yɛ ŋ yɛ ɔko̱ a kyɔ ŋu n se̱, amɔ mo̱ ne̱ mo̱ a le̱e̱ n se̱ ase̱ ba swe̱e̱re̱ mɔ se̱‑ɔ wo̱re̱ ya ŋu mò̱.

ref: I tell you for certain that everyone who has faith in me has eternal life.
text: Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ, ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ o gyi‑o e̱ nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: I am the bread that gives life!
text: Mo̱ e̱ gye̱ ateese ne̱ a sa ŋkpa‑ɔ.

ref: But the bread from heaven has come down, so that no one who eats it will ever die.
text: Amaa ateese kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱ aa le̱e̱ so̱so̱ ba kaase̱‑ɔ, ane̱ŋ a ateese‑o, ɔke̱maa ne̱ o gyi amo̱‑ɔ maa wu.

ref: Jesus answered: I tell you for certain that you won't live unless you eat the flesh and drink the blood of the Son of Man.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ gyi mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, ko̱yo̱wɔre̱gyi fe̱yɛ ateese‑o, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ nuu mo̱ mbo̱gya, amo̱ fe̱raa mo̱ne̱ maa nya ŋkpa.

ref: But if you do eat my flesh and drink my blood, you will have eternal life, and I will raise you to life on the last day.
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ o gyi mo̱ ko̱yo̱wɔre̱gyi fe̱yɛ ateese, ne̱ o nuu mo̱ mbo̱gya‑ɔ de ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ, na n kyiŋŋi mò̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro kake ne̱ kaye̱ mɔ e̱ gye̱ kɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: My flesh is the true food, and my blood is the true drink.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ ko̱yo̱wɔre̱gyi e̱ gye̱ ateese kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o, ne̱ mo̱ mbo̱gya mɔ e̱ gye̱ nta kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o.

ref: If you eat my flesh and drink my blood, you are one with me, and I am one with you.
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ o gyi mo̱ ko̱yo̱wɔre̱gyi fe̱yɛ ateese, ne̱ o nuu mo̱ mbo̱gya‑ɔ e̱ kye̱na mo̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ, na mo̱‑ɔ mɔ n kye̱na mò̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ.

ref: The living Father sent me, and I have life because of him. Now everyone who eats my flesh will live because of me.
text: N se̱ Wuribware̱ ya suŋ mo̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱. Ne̱ mò̱ a ma wu pɛɛɛ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ŋ gbaa n de ŋkpa amo̱ ne̱ o de‑o. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ɔke̱maa ne̱ o gyi mo̱ ko̱yo̱wɔre̱gyi fe̱yɛ ateese‑o e̱ kye̱na, na ɔ ma wu pɛɛɛ.

ref: Jesus was teaching in a synagogue in Capernaum when he said these things. The Words of Eternal Life
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a yɔ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ ne̱ ke̱ bo̱ Kapaaniyum maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ ɔ kaapo̱ ke̱mo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ. Ase̱ŋkpare̱gyi ne̱ a sa ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ

ref: Many of Jesus' disciples heard him and said, “This is too hard for anyone to understand.”
text: Ase̱sɛ biribiri ne̱ bo̱ gya Yeesuu si‑o bo̱ko̱ a nu amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Ase̱ŋ mɔ fe̱raa bo̱ le̱ŋ, ɔko̱ maa taare̱ amo̱‑ɔ a ɔ gya se̱.”

ref: Jesus knew that his disciples were grumbling. So he asked, “Does this bother you?
text: Yeesuu a ŋu fe̱yɛ baa tɛɛ ase̱ŋ amo̱ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ oo bise bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ ase̱ŋ mɔ maa nya a a nu ne̱e̱, bɛɛɛ?

ref: What if you should see the Son of Man go up to heaven where he came from?
text: Fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ a ŋu mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, a yɔ so̱so̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ a le̱e̱ ba‑ɔ ne̱e̱, weetee e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ waa?

ref: But some of you refuse to have faith in me.” Jesus said this, because from the beginning he knew who would have faith in him. He also knew which one would betray him.
text: Amo̱ pɛɛɛ gbaa mo̱ne̱ bo̱ko̱ maa kɔɔre̱ mo̱ mo̱ne̱ i gyi.” Ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ kyɔ o nyi bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ maa ba a bo̱ bo̱ kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi‑o, na mò̱ ne̱ ɔ ba a ɔ bo̱ gyi mò̱ kidiburo‑o.

ref: Then Jesus said, “You cannot come to me, unless the Father makes you want to come. That is why I have told these things to all of you.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya se̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mfaanɛɛ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ maa taare̱ a ɔ ba mo̱ ase̱, amɔ n se̱ a sa mò̱ kpa fe̱raa.”

ref: Because of what Jesus said, many of his disciples turned their backs on him and stopped following him.
text: Amo̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ gya Yeesuu si‑o bwe̱e̱tɔ a kine mò̱ se̱ ke̱gya.

ref: Jesus then asked his twelve disciples if they also were going to leave him.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ oo bise mò̱ agyase̱po̱ kudu anyɔ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Amɔ mo̱ne̱ mɔ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa ke̱le̱e̱ mo̱ ase̱ aaa?”

ref: We have faith in you, and we are sure that you are God's Holy One.”
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ saŋ ane̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ fo̱ ane̱ i gyi, ne̱ ane̱ nyi fe̱yɛ fo̱ e̱ gye̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱ timaa ne̱ Wuribware̱ a lee suŋ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱‑ɔ.”

ref: Jesus told his disciples, “I chose all twelve of you, but one of you is a demon!”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ mo̱ a lee mo̱ne̱ abɛɛ kudu anyɔ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ gya mo̱ se̱, bɛɛɛ? Amaa ɔko̱ bo̱ mo̱ne̱‑rɔ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ Ɔbɔnsam mò̱ gyi.”

ref: Jesus was talking about Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot. He would later betray Jesus, even though he was one of the twelve disciples.
text: Yudas Kariyotinyi ase̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ. Mò̱ se̱ mɔ e̱ gye̱ Simɔŋ Kariyotinyi. Amaa a bo̱‑rɔ fe̱yɛ Yudas gye̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱ kudu anyɔ‑ɔ ɔko̱, ne̱ mò̱‑ɔ mɔ i gyi mò̱ kidiburo.

ref: Jesus decided to leave Judea and to start going through Galilee because the leaders of the people wanted to kill him.
text: Amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ kamɛɛ‑ɔ, Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya se̱ ɔ naa ɔ kaapo̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, mò̱ ya nare̱ Yude̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱, mfe̱ŋ a abre̱sɛ‑ɔ i buwi a bo̱ kpa mò̱ a bo̱ mɔɔ.

ref: It was almost time for the Festival of Shelters,
text: Ne̱ saŋ a tɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ Yudaa awuye gyi Abwii‑ro ke̱kye̱na a kigyibe̱e̱‑ɔ.†

ref: and Jesus' brothers said to him, “Why don't you go to Judea? Then your disciples can see what you are doing.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu mò̱ tire‑ana a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Le̱e̱ mfe̱e̱ yɔ Yude̱ya na fo̱ agyase̱po̱ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ mɔ nya ŋu akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ waa‑ɔ.

ref: No one does anything in secret, if they want others to know about them. So let the world know what you are doing!”
text: Ɔko̱ ne̱ mò̱ ke̱nyare̱ a de̱e̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ de ke̱mo̱ ne̱ ɔ waa‑ɔ ɔ kwe̱e̱rɔ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ fo̱ e̱ taare̱ a fo̱ waa atɔ dabe̱, sa a kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ gyii fo̱.”

ref: Even Jesus' own brothers had not yet become his followers.
text: Mò̱ tire‑ana fɔŋfɔŋ gbaa mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi.

ref: The people of this world cannot hate you. They hate me, because I tell them that they do evil things.
text: Kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ maa kisi mo̱ne̱, amaa m fe̱raa, ba kisi mo̱, bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ atɔ ne̱ baa waa ne̱ a gye̱ e̱bɔye̱‑ɔ mo̱ i gywii bamo̱‑ɔ se̱.

ref: Jesus said this and stayed on in Galilee. Jesus at the Festival of Shelters
text: Mò̱ a tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ kye̱na Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ ŋke kafwe̱e̱ ko̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a ya gyi Abwii‑ro ke̱kye̱na a kigyibe̱e̱‑ɔ

ref: After Jesus' brothers had gone to the festival, he went secretly, without telling anyone.
text: Mò̱ tire‑ana a ya gyi Abwii‑ro ke̱kye̱na a kigyibe̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu mɔ a yɔ, amaa ɔ mo̱ŋ gya lamaŋ se̱ yɔ, oo ywii yɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: During the festival the leaders of the people looked for Jesus and asked, “Where is he?”
text: Ne̱ Yudaa awuye abre̱sɛ‑ɔ i buwi ba kpa mò̱, ne̱ baa bise ngyaŋŋe̱‑ɔ‑rɔ ase̱sɛ fe̱yɛ, “Ŋke̱e̱ mò̱?”

ref: The crowds even got into an argument about him. Some were saying, “Jesus is a good man,” while others were saying, “He is lying to everyone.”
text: Mò̱ ase̱ŋ sooo ne̱ ba ŋwiini lamaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ. Bamo̱ bo̱ko̱ yɛ, “Ɔ gye̱ se̱sɛ timaa.” Bo̱ko̱ mɔ yɛ, “Daabii! Ɔnyare̱ amo̱ e̱ pe̱nna ase̱sɛ ne̱e̱.”

ref: But the people were afraid of their leaders, and none of them talked in public about him.
text: Amaa ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ taare̱ tɔwe̱ mò̱ kuŋu si ase̱ŋ bo̱ le̱e̱ kawu si, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ se̱re̱ Yudaa awuye abre̱sɛ‑ɔ.

ref: When the festival was about half over, Jesus went into the temple and started teaching.
text: Amo̱ a fo̱ nse̱na na bo̱ lo̱we̱ kigyibe̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu a yɔ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ kabuno ya le̱e̱ ɔ kaapo̱ ase̱sɛ abware̱se̱ŋ.

ref: The leaders were surprised and said, “How does this man know so much? He has never been taught!”
text: Ne̱ aa kpe̱ŋ Yudaa awuye abre̱sɛ‑ɔ e̱ye̱e̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ, ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “E̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ ɔnyare̱ mɔ a waa o nyi e̱kaapo̱ faanɛɛ, na ɔ mo̱ŋ nya ke̱kaapo̱ le̱e̱ to̱ŋ ke̱maa?”

ref: Jesus replied: I am not teaching something I thought up. What I teach comes from the one who sent me.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Atɔ mɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ le̱e̱ mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ase̱, amaa aa le̱e̱ mò̱ ne̱ oo suŋ mo̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱‑ɔ ase̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: If you really want to obey God, you will know if what I teach comes from God or from me.
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ o sure si a ɔ waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ i pini fe̱yɛ ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱‑ɔ a le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱ ne̱e̱, bɛɛɛ mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ke̱yaale̱ŋ ne̱ n de mo̱ e̱ waa.

ref: If I wanted to bring honor to myself, I would speak for myself. But I want to honor the one who sent me. This is why I tell the truth and not a lie.
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ke̱yaale̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ i buwi ɔ kpa ke̱dabe̱ na wuraa a ɔ sa mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ne̱e̱. Amaa mò̱ ne̱ ɔ kpa ke̱dabe̱ na wuraa a ɔ sa mò̱ ne̱ oo suŋ mò̱‑ɔ de kase̱ŋtiŋ, ɔ maa ba aye̱ba.

ref: Didn't Moses give you the Law? Yet none of you obey it! So why do you want to kill me?
text: Mo̱ne̱ e̱ kyo̱rɔ Mosis ne̱ ɔɔ kyo̱rɛɛ Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ bo̱ sa mo̱ne̱‑ɔ, amaa mo̱ne̱ ko̱ŋko̱ gbaa mo̱ŋ gya mbraa‑ɔ se̱. Ne̱ e̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa a mo̱nꞌ mɔɔ mo̱?”

ref: The crowd replied, “You're crazy! What makes you think someone wants to kill you?”
text: Ne̱ lamaŋ‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ibrisi te fo̱ se̱. Nsɛ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ kpa a ɔ mɔɔ fo̱?”

ref: Jesus answered: I worked one miracle, and it amazed you.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ a kya ɔko̱ kukyure kake ne̱e̱, ne̱ aa kpe̱ŋ mo̱ne̱ pɛɛɛ e̱ye̱e̱.

ref: Moses commanded you to circumcise your sons. But it wasn't really Moses who gave you this command. It was your ancestors, and even on the Sabbath you circumcise your sons
text: To, Mosis a sa mo̱ne̱ Wuribware̱ mbraa ko̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ ade̱daapo̱ a kyɔ gya se̱‑ɔ, fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ya ko̱we̱ kagyinyaŋsɛɛ, kake buruwase̱po̱‑ɔ bo̱ te̱ŋ mò̱ ko̱twe̱e̱tu, na kake nsiŋ amo̱ e̱ gye̱ kukyure kake gbaa, bo̱ ma kaŋ yɔwe̱.

ref: Some of the people from Jerusalem were saying, “Isn't this the man they want to kill?
text: Ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ a nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱‑ɔ, ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “N gye̱ ɔnyare̱ mɔ ne̱ ane̱ abre̱sɛ e̱ kpa a bo̱ mɔɔ‑ɔ ɔɔɔ?

ref: Yet here he is, speaking for everyone to hear. And no one is arguing with him. Do you suppose the authorities know he is the Messiah?
text: Mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱re̱ mò̱ a ye̱re̱ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa lamaŋ‑nɔ, na bo̱ mo̱ŋ tɔwe̱ sɛye̱ bo̱ kye mò̱. Bɛɛɛ aa waa fe̱yɛ baa ŋu ane̱ŋ ne̱ o du‑o, ne̱ baa sure si fe̱yɛ ɔ gye̱ Kristoo kase̱ŋtiŋ aaa?

ref: But how could that be? No one knows where the Messiah will come from, but we know where this man comes from.”
text: Kristoo‑o ya ba, ɔko̱ maa pini mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ le̱e̱ a ɔ ba‑ɔ, amaa ɔnyare̱ mɔ fe̱raa ane̱ nyi mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱‑ɔ.”

ref: As Jesus was teaching in the temple, he shouted, “Do you really think you know me and where I came from? I didn't come on my own! The one who sent me is truthful, and you don't know him.
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yeesuu bo̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa a kabuno‑o ɔ maa se̱ ɔ kaapo̱‑ɔ, oo nu ase̱ŋ ne̱ baa tɔwe̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ keŋkeŋ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ e̱ fa fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ nyi mo̱ na mfe̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ a le̱e̱‑ɔ ɔɔɔ? N gye̱ mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ke̱yaale̱ŋ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ a ba. Mò̱ ne̱ oo suŋ mo̱‑ɔ de kase̱ŋtiŋ, amaa mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi mò̱.

ref: But I know the one who sent me, because I came from him.”
text: Amaa m fe̱raa, ŋ nyi mò̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ ase̱ ne̱ mo̱ a le̱e̱, ne̱ mò̱‑ɔ mɔ ya be̱e̱ suŋ mo̱.”

ref: Some of the people wanted to arrest Jesus right then. But no one even laid a hand on him, because his time had not yet come.
text: Ne̱ baa kpa a bo̱ kra Yeesuu, amaa bo̱ mo̱ŋ taare̱ kra mò̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ saŋ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ fo̱.

ref: A lot of people in the crowd put their faith in him and said, “When the Messiah comes, he surely won't perform more miracles than this man has done!” Officers Sent To Arrest Jesus
text: Amaa ase̱sɛ‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ a kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi, ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Kristoo‑o ya ba, ɔ waa atɔ ne̱ a kpe̱ŋ e̱ye̱e̱‑ɔ a ɔ kyo̱ŋ ɔnyare̱ mɔ amo̱‑ɔ ne̱ ɔɔ waa‑ɔ ɔɔɔ?” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa suŋ ade̱e̱re̱se̱po̱ ko̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ya kra Yeesuu‑o

text: Ɔ yɛ ane̱ i buwi a ane̱ kpa mò̱, na ane̱ ma ŋu mò̱, na ane̱ ma taare̱ a ane̱ yɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ yɔ‑ɔ. Amɔ kaase̱ e̱ gye̱ e̱me̱ne̱?” Nkyu ne̱ n sa ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ

text: Kigyibe̱e̱‑ɔ kake lalalo̱we̱ ne̱ ka gye̱ kake dabe̱‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu a kyuwi‑ro, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ keŋkeŋ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔke̱maa ne̱ burufo de mò̱‑ɔ, ɔ ba mo̱ ase̱ bo̱ nuu nkyu.

text: Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ, ‘Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ o gyi‑o, nkyu ne̱ n sa ŋkpa‑ɔ e̱ le̱e̱ mò̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ŋ nkyu‑o.’ ”

ref: Jesus was talking about the Holy Spirit, who would be given to everyone that had faith in him. The Spirit had not yet been given to anyone, since Jesus had not yet been given his full glory. The People Take Sides
text: Ase̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu e̱ tɔwe̱ mfe̱e̱‑ɔ gye̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o ase̱ŋ ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ ba kɔɔre̱ mò̱ a bo̱ gyi‑o e̱ nya‑ɔ ne̱e̱. Amaa saŋ amo̱‑ɔ bo̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ nya Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Yeesuu mo̱ŋ tɛɛ yɔ Wuribware̱ ase̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ ase̱sɛ a gyiiri Yeesuu si ikii‑o

ref: When the crowd heard Jesus say this, some of them said, “He must be the Prophet!”

text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ a ke‑ro ntuŋ ŋnyɔ ba gyiiri Yeesuu si ikii.

ref: The Pharisees said to them, “Have you also been fooled?

ref: Not one of the chief priests or the Pharisees has faith in him.
text: Mo̱ne̱ nyi Farisii awuye na maŋ abre̱sɛ ko̱ na baa kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi aaa? Mo̱ne̱ e̱ taare̱ a mo̱nꞌ kyii bamo̱ ɔko̱ ke̱nyare̱ aaa?

ref: Nicodemus was there at the time. He was a member of the council, and was the same one who had earlier come to see Jesus. He said,

text: “Ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ mbraa‑ɔ i gyi‑o, ane̱ maa taare̱ a ane̱ bu nyiŋkpasɛ ke̱pɔ na ane̱ mo̱ŋ nu mò̱ kanɔ‑rɔ ase̱ŋ, bɛɛɛ ane̱ mo̱ŋ ŋu ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ waa‑ɔ.”

ref: Then they said, “Nicodemus, you must be from Galilee! Read the Scriptures, and you will find that no prophet is to come from Galilee.” A Woman Caught in Sin
text: Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “To, fo̱ gbaa fo̱ a le̱e̱ Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱? Fo̱ ya suye abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ, fo̱ i ŋu fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ ko̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ le̱e̱ Galile̱ya.”

ref: Then early the next morning he went to the temple. The people came to him, and he sat down and started teaching them.
text: Kaye̱ ŋke‑o, ne̱ Yeesuu a kiŋŋi yɔ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa a kabuno‑o. Ne̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a kpɔwe̱ muruwaa mò̱, ne̱ ɔɔ kye̱na ɔ kaapo̱ bamo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ.

ref: The Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses brought in a woman who had been caught in bed with a man who wasn't her husband. They made her stand in the middle of the crowd.
text: Mò̱ a bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱ na Farisii awuye bo̱ko̱ a baa ɔkye̱e̱ waare̱e̱po̱ ko̱ ne̱ baa kra mò̱ kanyare̱kpa‑rɔ‑ɔ Yeesuu ase̱. Ne̱ baa sa ne̱ ɔɔ ye̱re̱ lamaŋ‑ɔ nse̱na Yeesuu ase̱.

ref: Then they said, “Teacher, this woman was caught sleeping with a man who isn't her husband.
text: Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Ɔkaapo̱po̱, ane̱ a kra ɔkye̱e̱ mɔ kanyare̱kpa‑rɔ ne̱e̱.

text: Baa tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱, a bo̱ nya nya Yeesuu ke̱bɔye̱, na bo̱ nya po̱rɔ mò̱. Amaa ne̱ oo buŋŋe o de ke̱sare̱e̱gyi ɔ kyo̱rɛɛ swe̱e̱re̱.

ref: They kept on asking Jesus about the woman. Finally, he stood up and said, “If any of you have never sinned, then go ahead and throw the first stone at her!”
