text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kyuwi‑ro, ne̱ oo bise ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ŋke̱e̱ bamo̱? Ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ saŋ a o bu fo̱ ke̱pɔ aaa?”

ref: “No sir,” the woman answered. Then Jesus told her, “I am not going to accuse you either. You may go now, but don't sin anymore.” Jesus Is the Light for the World
text: Ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ ɔ bo̱‑rɔ.” Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “To, m fe̱raa, ma bu fo̱ ke̱pɔ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ nare̱, amaa le̱e̱ ndɔɔ bo̱ yɔ, fo̱ ma lɛɛ ya waa e̱bɔye̱.”] Yeesuu e̱ gye̱ kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ

ref: Once again Jesus spoke to the people. This time he said, “I am the light for the world! Follow me, and you won't be walking in the dark. You will have the light that gives life.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ sa se̱ŋsa gywii Farisii awuye ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “N du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ laŋŋe̱‑rɔ ke̱ e̱ sa kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ. Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ gya mo̱ se̱‑ɔ maa nare̱ kibugyii‑ro. Ane̱ŋ a se̱sɛ‑ɔ de ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ sa ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ.”

ref: The Pharisees objected, “You are the only one speaking for yourself, and what you say isn't true!”
text: Ne̱ Farisii awuye‑o a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mbe̱yɔmɔ fo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ fo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ e̱ye̱e̱ se̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱e̱. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ane̱ maa taare̱ a ane̱ kɔɔre̱ fo̱ gyi.”

ref: Jesus replied: Even if I do speak for myself, what I say is true! I know where I came from and where I am going. But you don't know where I am from or where I am going.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “N gye̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱e̱. Hare̱e̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ kuŋu si ase̱ŋ gbaa, ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱e̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ nyi mfe̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ a le̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ŋ nyi mfe̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ yɔ‑ɔ. Amaa mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi mfe̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ a le̱e̱‑ɔ, na mfe̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ yɔ‑ɔ.

ref: You judge in the same way that everyone else does, but I don't judge anyone.
text: Mo̱ne̱ fe̱raa, mo̱ne̱ e̱ de̱e̱re̱ ke̱mo̱ ne̱ ki te akatɔ se̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱ a mo̱nꞌ bo̱ po̱rɔ ɔko̱. Amaa m fe̱raa n gye̱ mo̱ kusuŋ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ n gyi ɔko̱ ase̱ŋ.

ref: If I did judge, I would judge fairly, because I would not be doing it alone. The Father who sent me is here with me.
text: Amaa mo̱ a dɛɛ gyi ɔko̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱e̱, weetee mo̱ se̱ŋgyi‑o gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ n se̱ ne̱ oo suŋ mo̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ bo̱ mo̱ ase̱, ɔ kya mo̱‑rɔ.

ref: “Where is your Father?” they asked. “You don't know me or my Father!” Jesus answered. “If you knew me, you would know my Father.”
text: Ne̱ baa bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ŋke̱e̱ fo̱ se̱?” Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi mo̱ gbaa, saŋbo̱to̱ n se̱. Mo̱ne̱ i nyi mo̱, amo̱ fe̱raa mo̱ne̱ nyi n se̱.”

ref: Jesus said this while he was still teaching in the place where the temple treasures were stored. But no one arrested him, because his time had not yet come. You Cannot Go Where I Am Going
text: Mfaanɛɛ a ase̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii ase̱sɛ‑ɔ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa a kabuno mfe̱ŋ ne̱ bo̱ de adakaa ne̱ ba twe̱e̱twe̱e̱ atanne̱ ba waa‑rɔ‑ɔ ye̱re̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱. Amaa ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ kra mò̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ saŋ‑ɔ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ fo̱. Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ ɔ yɔ‑ɔ

ref: Jesus also told them, “I am going away, and you will look for me. But you cannot go where I am going, and you will die with your sins unforgiven.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ le̱e̱ mfe̱e̱ a ŋ yɔ. Mo̱ne̱ i buwi a mo̱nꞌ kpa mo̱ kpo̱ne̱, na mo̱ne̱ ma ŋu mo̱, na mo̱ne̱ e̱bɔye̱ sii mo̱ne̱ se̱ a mo̱nꞌ taa bo̱ wu. Mo̱ne̱ maa taare̱ a mo̱nꞌ yɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ yɔ‑ɔ.”

ref: The people asked, “Does he intend to kill himself? Is that what he means by saying we cannot go where he is going?”
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ Yudaa awuye abre̱sɛ‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔ yɛ, ane̱ maa taare̱ a ane̱ yɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ yɔ‑ɔ. Amo̱‑ɔ e̱ kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ mɔɔ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ aaa?”

ref: Jesus answered, “You are from below, but I am from above. You belong to this world, but I don't.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ a le̱e̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ne̱e̱. Amaa m fe̱raa, mo̱ŋ le̱e̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: This is why I said you will die with your sins unforgiven. If you don't have faith in me for who I am, you will die, and your sins will not be forgiven.”
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱bɔye̱ i sii mo̱ne̱ se̱, na mo̱nꞌ taa bo̱ wu. Mo̱ne̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ gyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ n gye̱ fe̱raa, mo̱ne̱ e̱bɔye̱ i sii mo̱ne̱ se̱, na mo̱nꞌ taa bo̱ wu.”

ref: “Who are you?” they asked Jesus. Jesus answered, “I am exactly who I told you at the beginning.
text: Ne̱ baa bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nsɛ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ fa fe̱yɛ fo̱ gye̱?” Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “N kyɔ mo̱ŋ tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ne̱ aaa?

ref: There is a lot more I could say to condemn you. But the one who sent me is truthful, and I tell the people of this world only what I have heard from him.”
text: N de ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ na n tɔwe̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱bɔye̱ se̱. Mò̱ ne̱ oo suŋ mo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ wuye, ne̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ a nu le̱e̱ mò̱ ase̱‑ɔ wo̱re̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ.”

ref: No one understood that Jesus was talking to them about the Father.
text: Ase̱sɛ ne̱ baa nu mò̱ ase̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ mò̱ se̱ Wuribware̱ ne̱ o de.

ref: Jesus went on to say, “When you have lifted up the Son of Man, you will know who I am. You will also know that I don't do anything on my own. I say only what my Father taught me.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ya yase̱ mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, so̱so̱, mo̱ne̱ i ŋu fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ n gye̱. Mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i ŋu fe̱yɛ ma waa ke̱tɔ ke̱maa mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ e̱ye̱e̱ se̱ ne̱e̱. Amaa mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ n se̱ a tɔwe̱ ɛ n tɔwe̱‑ɔ wo̱re̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: The one who sent me is with me. I always do what pleases him, and he will never leave me.”
text: Mò̱ ne̱ oo suŋ mo̱‑ɔ bo̱ mo̱ ase̱, ɔ mo̱ŋ yɔwe̱ mo̱ wo̱re̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ waa mò̱ ke̱pre̱ saŋ ke̱maa.”

ref: After Jesus said this, many of the people put their faith in him. The Truth Will Set You Free
text: Ase̱sɛ ne̱ baa nu fe̱yɛ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ a kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi. Agyi na anya

ref: Jesus told the people who had faith in him, “If you keep on obeying what I have said, you truly are my disciples.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi‑o fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ i de ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ yɔwe̱‑rɔ fe̱raa, mo̱ne̱ e̱ gye̱ mo̱ agyase̱po̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ si ne̱e̱.

ref: You will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.”
text: Na mo̱nꞌ gyii kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o, na mo̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ ke̱kaapo̱ e̱ sa a mo̱nꞌ nya mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱.”

ref: They answered, “We are Abraham's children! We have never been anyone's slaves. How can you say we will be set free?”
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ bo̱ko̱ a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ a le̱e̱ Abraham ke̱nana‑rɔ ne̱e̱. Ane̱ kyɔ ane̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ kii bo̱ko̱ anya. Ne̱ e̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ ɛ ane̱ e̱ nya ane̱ e̱ye̱e̱?”

ref: Jesus replied: I tell you for certain that anyone who sins is a slave of sin!
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱ mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ waa e̱bɔye̱‑ɔ gye̱ e̱bɔye̱ ke̱nya ne̱e̱.

ref: And slaves don't stay in the family forever, though the Son will always remain in the family.
text: Ke̱nya ya kyee mò̱ nyaŋpe̱ aye̱ kanɔ ŋke̱maa, ɔ maa kii ogyi, amaa ogyi fe̱raa gye̱ ogyi ne̱e̱ bo̱ yɔ na a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ.

ref: I know that you are from Abraham's family. Yet you want to kill me, because my message isn't really in your hearts.
text: Ŋ nyi dame̱naŋsɛ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ a le̱e̱ Abraham ke̱nana‑ɔ‑rɔ ne̱e̱. Amaa amo̱ pɛɛɛ gbaa, mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ mɔɔ mo̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ ase̱ŋkpare̱gyi mo̱ŋ bo̱ mo̱ne̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ.

ref: I am telling you what my Father has shown me, just as you are doing what your father has taught you. Your Father Is the Devil
text: Mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ n se̱ a kaapo̱ mo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱, amaa mo̱ne̱ e̱ waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ se̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ne̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱.”

ref: The people said to Jesus, “Abraham is our father!” Jesus replied, “If you were Abraham's children, you would do what Abraham did.
text: Ne̱ baa lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ se̱ e̱ gye̱ Abraham.” Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ dɛɛ mo̱ne̱ gye̱ Abraham mò̱ gyi‑ana kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱e̱, weetee mo̱ne̱ e̱ waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ waa‑ɔ dɛɛ.

ref: Instead, you want to kill me for telling you the truth that God gave me. Abraham never did anything like that.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ n tɔwe̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱ mo̱ a nu le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱‑ɔ gywii mo̱ne̱, amaa amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ gbaa, mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ mɔɔ mo̱ ne̱e̱. Abraham mɔ mo̱ŋ waa ane̱ŋ a atɔ mɔ.

ref: But you are doing exactly what your father does.” “Don't accuse us of having someone else as our father!” they said. “We just have one father, and he is God.”
text: Mo̱ne̱ e̱ waa ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ se̱ a waa‑ɔ ne̱e̱.” Ne̱ baa lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Wuribware̱ wo̱re̱ e̱ gye̱ ane̱ se̱ ne̱ ane̱ de‑o, ane̱ gye̱ mò̱ gyi‑ana kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱e̱.”

ref: Jesus answered: If God were your Father, you would love me, because I came from God and now I am here. He sent me. I did not come on my own.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Wuribware̱ dɛɛ ɔ gye̱ mo̱ne̱ se̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ si ne̱e̱, weetee mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa mo̱ ase̱ŋ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱ ne̱e̱ ba mfe̱e̱. Mo̱ŋ ba mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ke̱yaale̱ŋ se̱ ne̱e̱, amaa mò̱ ne̱ oo suŋ mo̱‑ɔ ke̱yaale̱ŋ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ a ba.

ref: Why can't you understand what I am talking about? Can't you stand to hear what I am saying?
text: Ne̱ weetee ŋkee nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ maa nu ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ kaase̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱? A du fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ maa taare̱ a mo̱nꞌ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ gyi ne̱e̱.

ref: Your father is the devil, and you do exactly what he wants. He has always been a murderer and a liar. There is nothing truthful about him. He speaks on his own, and everything he says is a lie. Not only is he a liar himself, but he is also the father of all lies.
text: Mo̱ne̱ gye̱ mo̱ne̱ se̱ Ɔbɔnsam mò̱ gyi‑ana ne̱e̱, amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa a mo̱nꞌ gya ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ pre̱ mo̱ne̱ se̱‑ɔ se̱. Le̱e̱ atɔ ke̱maa ke̱le̱e̱kaase̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ se̱ gye̱ ɔmɔɔpo̱ ne̱e̱, ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ ye̱re̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ a ke̱be̱gya‑ɔ pɛɛɛ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kase̱ŋtiŋ mo̱ŋ bo̱ mò̱ ase̱. Mò̱ ya ba aye̱ba, ɔ waa ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ kyɔ o du‑o ne̱e̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ gye̱ ɔye̱bapo̱, mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ŋye̱ba pɛɛɛ kuŋu.

ref: Everything I have told you is true, and you still refuse to have faith in me.
text: Amaa m fe̱raa, mo̱ i gyi kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱e̱, amo̱se̱ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ maa kɔɔre̱ mo̱ mo̱ne̱ i gyi.

ref: Can any of you accuse me of sin? If you cannot, why won't you have faith in me? After all, I am telling you the truth.
text: Mo̱ne̱‑rɔ nsɛ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ taare̱ a ɔ kaapo̱ mo̱‑rɔ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a gyi ke̱pɔ bo̱ le̱e̱ e̱bɔye̱ ko̱waa se̱? Mo̱ ya tɔwe̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ gywii mo̱ne̱, nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ maa kɔɔre̱ mo̱ ase̱ŋ mo̱ne̱ i gyi?

ref: Anyone who belongs to God will listen to his message. But you refuse to listen, because you don't belong to God. Jesus and Abraham
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ bo̱ mò̱‑ɔ i nu mò̱ ase̱ŋ. Mo̱ne̱ fe̱raa, n gye̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ bo̱ mo̱ne̱ kpa ke̱maa se̱, amo̱se̱ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ maa nu mo̱ ase̱ŋ.” Yeesuu na Abraham

ref: The people told Jesus, “We were right to say that you are a Samaritan and that you have a demon in you!”
text: Ne̱ baa bise Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “A mo̱ŋ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ fo̱ gye̱ Samariyanyi, ne̱ ibrisi ko̱ te fo̱ se̱ aaa?”

ref: Jesus answered, “I don't have a demon in me. I honor my Father, and you refuse to honor me.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ibrisi ke̱maa mo̱ŋ te mo̱ se̱. Ase̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ e̱ kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ i bu n se̱ ne̱e̱. Amaa mo̱ne̱ fe̱raa mo̱ne̱ e̱ saare̱ mo̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: I tell you for certain that if you obey my words, you will never die.”
text: Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ gya mo̱ ke̱kaapo̱ se̱‑ɔ maa wu daa.”

ref: Then the people said, “Now we are sure that you have a demon. Abraham is dead, and so are the prophets. How can you say that no one who obeys your words will ever die?
text: Ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ gywii Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Mbe̱yɔmɔ ane̱ nyi kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ ibrisi te fo̱ se̱. Abraham a wu, ne̱ Wuribware̱ akyaamɛɛ a wu, amaa fo̱ fe̱raa, fo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ gya fo̱ ke̱kaapo̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ maa wu daa.

ref: Are you greater than our father Abraham? He died, and so did the prophets. Who do you think you are?”
text: Ane̱ se̱ Abraham a wu, amɔ fo̱ e̱ kpa a fo̱ tɔwe̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ kyɔ Abraham aaa? Ne̱ Wuribware̱ a akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ mɔ a wu. Nsɛ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ fa fe̱yɛ fo̱ gye̱?”

ref: Jesus replied, “If I honored myself, it would mean nothing. My Father is the one who honors me. You claim that he is your God,
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “N dɛɛ ŋ yɛ m bu mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ke̱dabe̱ ne̱e̱, weetee mo̱ kibu mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ de awaasɛ ke̱maa. Ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱nyaa mo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ n se̱, mò̱ dɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ yɛ ɔ gye̱ mo̱ne̱ se̱.

ref: even though you don't really know him. If I said I didn't know him, I would be a liar, just like all of you. But I know him, and I do what he says.
text: Mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi mò̱ pɛɛɛ, amaa m fe̱raa, ŋ nyi mò̱. Mo̱ a dɛɛ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ŋ nyi Wuribware̱ ne̱e̱, amo̱ fe̱raa mo̱ i kii ɔye̱bapo̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱‑ɔ. Amaa ŋ nyi mò̱, ne̱ n gya mò̱ kanɔ se̱.

ref: Jesus answered, “I tell you for certain that even before Abraham was, I was, and I am.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ n kyɔ n gye̱ pwɛɛ ne̱ baa dɛɛ ko̱we̱ Abraham.”

ref: The people picked up stones to kill Jesus, but he hid and left the temple.
text: Ne̱ baa taa abu a bo̱ twe̱e̱ bo̱ da mò̱. Amaa Yeesuu a kwe̱e̱rɔ bamo̱ se̱ se̱re̱ le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa a kabuno‑o mfe̱ŋ.

ref: As Jesus walked along, he saw a man who had been blind since birth.
text: Yeesuu a naa ɔ yɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ oo ŋu ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ baa ko̱we̱ mò̱ te̱napo̱‑ɔ.

ref: Jesus' disciples asked, “Teacher, why was this man born blind? Was it because he or his parents sinned?”
text: Ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔkaapo̱po̱, nsɛ e̱bɔye̱ ya sa ne̱ baa ko̱we̱ ɔnyare̱ mɔ te̱napo̱? Aa le̱e̱ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ase̱ ne̱e̱, bɛɛɛ mò̱ ako̱we̱po̱ e̱bɔye̱ se̱ aaa?”

ref: As long as it is day, we must do what the one who sent me wants me to do. When night comes, no one can work.
text: Kusuŋ amo̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a suŋ mo̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ waa‑ɔ ne̱e̱. A saŋ a gye̱ mpase̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa n se̱ kusuŋ, amaa kibugyii ya ba, ɔko̱ maa lɛɛ taare̱ a o suŋ kusuŋ.

ref: While I am in the world, I am the light for the world.”
text: Mo̱ a tɛɛ m bo̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, mo̱ e̱ gye̱ kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ.”

ref: After Jesus said this, he spit on the ground. He made some mud and smeared it on the man's eyes.
text: Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ twe̱e̱ akyɔne̱ we̱e̱ swe̱e̱re̱, ne̱ ɔɔ ba akyɔne̱‑ɔ bo̱ pɔ e̱se̱ kafwe̱e̱, ne̱ ɔɔ ba e̱se̱‑ɔ bo̱ bure ɔnyare̱ amo̱ akatɔ.

ref: Then he said, “Go wash off the mud in Siloam Pool.” The man went and washed in Siloam, which means “One Who Is Sent.” When he had washed off the mud, he could see.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nare̱ yɔ Silowam ke̱pare̱‑rɔ ya fwe̱e̱ fo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ.” Ke̱nyare̱ Silowam‑o kaase̱ e̱ gye̱ Baa-suŋ-fo̱. Ne̱ ɔnyare̱ amo̱ a ya fwe̱e̱ mò̱ akatɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ oo kiŋŋi ba, na mò̱ akatɔ a buŋŋi.

ref: The man's neighbors and the people who had seen him begging wondered if he really could be the same man.
text: Ne̱ te̱napo̱‑ɔ akye̱nabɛɛpo̱ na ase̱sɛ ne̱ baa dɛɛ ŋu mò̱ pwɛɛ ne̱ amo̱‑ɔ a dɛɛ waa‑ɔ a bise abɛɛ fe̱yɛ, “N gye̱ te̱napo̱ ne̱ ɔ dɛɛ o te ɔ ko̱re̱ atɔ‑ɔ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱?”

ref: Some of them said he was the same beggar, while others said he only looked like him. But he told them, “I am that man.”
text: Bo̱ko̱ yɛ, “Mò̱ ne̱e̱.” Ne̱ bo̱ko̱ mɔ yɛ, “N gye̱ mò̱ ne̱e̱. Ɔɔ le̱e̱ mò̱ ne̱e̱.” Ne̱ ɔnyare̱‑ɔ fɔŋfɔŋ yɛ, “Mo̱ ne̱e̱.”

ref: “Then how can you see?” they asked.
text: Ne̱ baa bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ ne̱e̱ ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ fe̱raa fo̱ ke̱e̱?”

ref: He answered, “Someone named Jesus made some mud and smeared it on my eyes. He told me to go and wash it off in Siloam Pool. When I did, I could see.”
text: Ne̱ oo lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔnyare̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Yeesuu‑o ya waa e̱se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ bure mo̱ akatɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ yɔ Silowam ke̱pare̱‑rɔ ya fwe̱e̱ mo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ mo̱ a yɔ mo̱ a de̱ŋ fwe̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ ŋ ke̱e̱.”

ref: “Where is he now?” they asked. “I don't know,” he answered. The Pharisees Try To Find Out What Happened
text: Ne̱ baa bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mfe̱ne̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱?” Ne̱ oo lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ŋ nyi.” Farisii awuye e̱ de̱e̱re̱‑rɔ na bo̱ nya ŋu ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya te̱napo̱‑ɔ

ref: The day when Jesus made the mud and healed the man was a Sabbath. So the people took the man to the Pharisees.
text: Kake ne̱ Yeesuu a waa e̱se̱ bo̱ kya te̱napo̱ amo̱‑ɔ gye̱ kukyure kake ne̱e̱.

ref: They asked him how he was able to see, and he answered, “Jesus made some mud and smeared it on my eyes. Then after I washed it off, I could see.”
text: Ne̱ Farisii awuye‑o a be̱e̱ bise ɔnyare̱‑ɔ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mò̱ akatɔ a waa buŋŋi‑o. Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔɔ taa e̱se̱ ne̱e̱ bo̱ bure mo̱ akatɔ, ne̱ mo̱ a fwe̱e̱ mo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ ŋ ke̱e̱.”

ref: Some of the Pharisees said, “This man Jesus doesn't come from God. If he did, he would not break the law of the Sabbath.” Others asked, “How could someone who is a sinner work such a miracle?” Since the Pharisees could not agree among themselves,
text: Ne̱ Farisii awuye‑o bo̱ko̱ yɛ, “Ɔnyare̱ mɔ ne̱ ɔɔ waa amo̱‑ɔ maa taare̱ a ɔ le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ o suŋ kusuŋ kukyure kake‑o.” Ne̱ bo̱ko̱ mɔ yɛ, “E̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ ɔnyare̱ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ e̱bɔye̱ ɔwaapo̱‑ɔ e̱ taare̱ a ɔ waa ane̱ŋ a akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ mɔ?” Ne̱ bamo̱ wo̱re̱ wo̱re̱ i gyiiri ikii.

ref: they asked the man, “What do you say about this one who healed your eyes?” “He is a prophet!” the man told them.
text: Ne̱ Farisii awuye‑o a be̱e̱ bise ɔnyare̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ yɛ ɔɔ kya fo̱, ne̱ fo̱ akatɔ a buŋŋi, e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ fa mò̱ ase̱ŋ?” Ne̱ ɔnyare̱‑ɔ a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔ gye̱ Wuribware̱ a akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ ɔko̱ ne̱e̱.”

ref: But the Jewish leaders would not believe that the man had once been blind. They sent for his parents
text: Yudaa awuye abre̱sɛ‑ɔ mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ ɔnyare̱ amo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ gyi fe̱yɛ ɔ dɛɛ ɔ gye̱ te̱napo̱ ne̱e̱, ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ mò̱ akatɔ a buŋŋi, amɔ bamo̱ a te̱e̱ mò̱ ako̱we̱po̱ bise bamo̱‑rɔ.

ref: and asked them, “Is this the son that you said was born blind? How can he now see?”
text: Mò̱ ako̱we̱po̱‑ɔ a ba‑ɔ, ne̱ baa bise bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ gyi ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱? Mo̱ne̱ yɛ mo̱ne̱ a ko̱we̱ mò̱ te̱napo̱ ne̱e̱. To, e̱me̱ne̱ ya waa ne̱ ŋkee ɔ ke̱e̱?”

ref: The man's parents answered, “We are certain that he is our son, and we know that he was born blind.
text: Ne̱ mò̱ ako̱we̱po̱ a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ nyi fe̱yɛ ane̱ gyi ne̱e̱, ne̱ ane̱ nyi fe̱yɛ ane̱ a ko̱we̱ mò̱ te̱napo̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: But we don't know how he got his sight or who gave it to him. Ask him! He is old enough to speak for himself.”
text: Amaa ane̱ mo̱ŋ nyi ane̱ŋ ne̱ aa waa ne̱ ŋkee ɔ ke̱e̱‑ɔ, bɛɛɛ ane̱ mo̱ŋ nyi mò̱ ne̱ ɔɔ kya mò̱, ne̱ ŋkee ɔ ke̱e̱‑ɔ. Mo̱nꞌ bise mò̱. Ɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ keegyi ne̱e̱, ɔ taare̱ a o lee kanɔ sa mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱.”

ref: The leaders called the man back and said, “Swear by God to tell the truth! We know that Jesus is a sinner.”
text: Ne̱ baa te̱e̱ ɔnyare̱ ne̱ baa ko̱we̱ mò̱ te̱napo̱‑ɔ nyɔse̱po̱ baa. Ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ ka Wuribware̱ ke̱yaa fe̱yɛ ɔ tɔwe̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ. Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Ane̱ nyi fe̱yɛ ɔnyare̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Yeesuu‑o gye̱ e̱bɔye̱ ɔwaapo̱ ne̱e̱.”

ref: The man replied, “I don't know if he is a sinner or not. All I know is that I used to be blind, but now I can see!”
text: Ne̱ ɔnyare̱‑ɔ a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔ gye̱ e̱bɔye̱ ɔwaapo̱ ne̱e̱ ooo, ɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ ooo, m fe̱raa mo̱ŋ nyi. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ŋ nyi‑o e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ n dɛɛ n gye̱ te̱napo̱, amaa mbe̱yɔmɔ ŋ ke̱e̱.”

ref: “What did he do to you?” they asked. “How did he heal your eyes?”
text: Ne̱ baa bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “E̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ ɔɔ waa kya fo̱ akatɔ‑ɔ?”

ref: The man answered, “I have already told you once, and you refused to listen. Why do you want me to tell you again? Do you also want to become his disciples?”
text: Ne̱ oo lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ a kyɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ne̱, amaa mo̱ne̱ maa nu. E̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ be̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ gbaa mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa a mo̱nꞌ nu? Mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa a mo̱nꞌ kii mò̱ agyase̱po̱ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱?”

ref: The leaders insulted the man and said, “You are his follower! We are followers of Moses.
text: Ne̱ baa saare̱ mò̱, ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱‑ɔ fo̱ gye̱ se̱sɛ amo̱ ɔgyase̱po̱ ne̱e̱. Amaa ane̱ fe̱raa, ane̱ gye̱ Mosis agyase̱po̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: We are sure God spoke to Moses, but we don't even know where Jesus comes from.”
text: Ane̱ nyi fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔɔ sa se̱ŋsa gywii Mosis, amaa ane̱ŋ a se̱sɛ‑ɔ fe̱raa, ane̱ mo̱ŋ nyi mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱‑ɔ.”

ref: “How strange!” the man replied. “He healed my eyes, and yet you don't know where he comes from.
text: Ne̱ ɔnyare̱ amo̱ a be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ ase̱ŋ mɔ ne̱e̱? Mo̱ne̱ yɛ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ ba‑ɔ, amaa oo buŋŋi mo̱ akatɔ.

ref: We know that God listens only to people who love and obey him. God doesn't listen to sinners.
text: Ane̱ nyi fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ maa nu e̱bɔye̱ awaapo̱ ase̱ŋ, amɔ bamo̱ ne̱ ba bu mò̱, ne̱ ba waa ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ kpa fe̱yɛ bo̱ waa‑ɔ.

ref: And this is the first time in history anyone has ever given sight to someone born blind.
text: Le̱e̱ saŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a twe̱e̱ kaye̱‑ɔ, ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ ŋu fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ a buŋŋi mò̱ ne̱ baa ko̱we̱ mò̱ te̱napo̱‑ɔ akatɔ.

ref: Jesus could not do anything unless he came from God.”
text: Ɔnyare̱ mɔ dɛɛ ɔ mo̱ŋ le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱ ne̱e̱, weetee ɔ maa taare̱ a ɔ waa ane̱ŋ a atɔ‑ɔ.”

ref: The leaders told the man, “You have been a sinner since the day you were born! Do you think you can teach us anything?” Then they said, “You can never come back into any of our synagogues!”
text: Ne̱ baa lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Le̱e̱ saŋ ne̱ fo̱ bo̱ fo̱ nyi kame‑ro‑o fo̱ gye̱ e̱bɔye̱ ɔwaapo̱ ne̱e̱. Ne̱ fo̱‑ɔ fo̱ e̱ kpa a fo̱ kaapo̱ ane̱ atɔ aaa?” Ne̱ baa gya mò̱ le̱e̱ bamo̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ Farisii awuye du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ate̱napo̱‑ɔ

ref: When Jesus heard what had happened, he went and found the man. Then Jesus asked, “Do you have faith in the Son of Man?”
text: Yeesuu a nu fe̱yɛ baa gya ɔnyare̱ amo̱ le̱e̱ bamo̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ oo buwi kpa ɔnyare̱‑ɔ. Mò̱ a ŋu mò̱‑ɔ, ne̱ oo bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o fo̱ i gyi aaa?”

ref: He replied, “Sir, if you will tell me who he is, I will put my faith in him.”
text: Ne̱ ɔnyare̱ amo̱ a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, kaapo̱ mo̱ dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, na ŋ nya kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi.”

ref: “You have already seen him,” Jesus answered, “and right now he is talking with you.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ a kyɔ ŋu mò̱, mo̱ e̱ gye̱ mò̱ ne̱ fo̱ aa mò̱ e̱ sa se̱ŋsa mbe̱yɔmɔ.”

ref: The man said, “Lord, I put my faith in you!” Then he worshiped Jesus.
text: Ne̱ oo lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ a kɔɔre̱ fo̱ gyi, mo̱ nyaŋpe̱.” Ne̱ oo kpuni aŋurii suŋ Yeesuu.

ref: Jesus told him, “I came to judge the people of this world. I am here to give sight to the blind and to make blind everyone who can see.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Mo̱ a ba kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ne̱e̱ na m bo̱ gyi ase̱sɛ ase̱ŋ, bu ke̱pɔ, na ate̱napo̱ akatɔ buŋŋi, na bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ ke̱e̱‑ɔ mɔ kii fe̱yɛ ate̱napo̱‑ɔ.”

ref: Jesus answered, “If you were blind, you would not be guilty. But now that you claim to see, you will keep on being guilty.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ dɛɛ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ ke̱e̱ ne̱e̱‑ɔ, weetee mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ e̱bɔye̱ awaapo̱, amaa mo̱ne̱ a tɔwe̱ ɛ mo̱ne̱ ke̱e̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ e̱bɔye̱ i sii mo̱ne̱ se̱.”

ref: Jesus said: I tell you for certain only thieves and robbers climb over the fence instead of going in through the gate to the sheep pen.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ, mo̱ne̱ nyi fe̱yɛ ane̱ ase̱sɛ e̱ pwɛɛ adeŋbee kuriikuriisɛ ne̱e̱, ne̱ ba kpaa bamo̱ e̱sanne̱ ba waa‑rɔ kanye. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ bo̱rɔ kideŋbee a kibunogyi‑o‑ro lweero e̱sanne̱‑ɔ ase̱, ne̱ ɔ de̱e̱ kideŋbee‑o lweero‑o gye̱ oyu ne̱e̱.

ref: But the gatekeeper opens the gate for the shepherd, and he goes in through it. The sheep know their shepherd's voice. He calls each of them by name and leads them out.
text: Mò̱ ne̱ ɔ ke̱e̱ kibunogyi‑o si‑o i buŋŋi kpa a ɔ sa mò̱. Na mò̱ a maa se̱ ɔ te̱e̱ e̱sanne̱‑ɔ anyare̱ ako̱ ako̱‑ɔ, na bo̱ pini mò̱ bo̱re̱, na o lee bamo̱ kawu.

ref: When he has led out all of his sheep, he walks in front of them, and they follow, because they know his voice.
text: Mò̱ ya lee bamo̱ kawu‑o, na ɔ gye̱ bamo̱ ŋkpɛɛ, na bo̱ gya mò̱ se̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ nyi mò̱ bo̱re̱.

ref: The sheep will not follow strangers. They don't recognize a stranger's voice, and they run away.
text: Amaa bo̱ maa gya ɔko̱ se̱, ba se̱re̱ a bo̱ yɔwe̱ se̱sɛ amo̱ ne̱e̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi mò̱ bo̱re̱.”

ref: Jesus told the people this story. But they did not understand what he was talking about. Jesus Is the Good Shepherd
text: Amaa Yeesuu a da ke̱kpare̱ mɔ‑ɔ, bo̱ mo̱ŋ nu ke̱mo̱ kaase̱.

ref: Jesus said: I tell you for certain that I am the gate for the sheep.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ kya se̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ m be̱e̱ n du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ e̱sanne̱ a kibunogyi‑o.

ref: Everyone who came before me was a thief or a robber, and the sheep did not listen to any of them.
text: Bamo̱ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔɔ daa mo̱ e̱ba‑ɔ gye̱ oyu ne̱e̱, amaa e̱sanne̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ gya bamo̱ kanɔ se̱.

ref: I am the gate. All who come in through me will be saved. Through me they will come and go and find pasture.
text: N du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kibunogyi‑o. Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ bo̱rɔ mo̱ se̱ a o lweero‑o maa fo̱, o lweero a ɔ le̱e̱ na ɔ nya gyirokpa timaa.

ref: A thief comes only to rob, kill, and destroy. I came so everyone would have life, and have it fully.
text: Ayu fe̱raa i lweero ne̱e̱ a bo̱ bo̱ ywii, na bo̱ mɔɔ, na bo̱ nye̱ra. Amaa m fe̱raa, mo̱ a ba ne̱e̱ a bo̱ nya nya ŋkpa na kakatɔgyi na ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ki tiri bamo̱‑ɔ.

ref: I am the good shepherd, and the good shepherd gives up his life for his sheep.
text: Mo̱ dɛɛ dɛɛ mo̱ e̱ gye̱ e̱sanne̱ ɔde̱e̱re̱se̱po̱ timaa, ne̱ mo̱ a waa mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ siraa fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ ba a m bo̱ wu sa mo̱ e̱sanne̱‑ɔ.

ref: Hired workers are not like the shepherd. They don't own the sheep, and when they see a wolf coming, they run off and leave the sheep. Then the wolf attacks and scatters the flock.
text: Osuŋpo̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ, ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ e̱sanne̱ ɔde̱e̱re̱se̱po̱‑ɔ ya ŋu fe̱yɛ ɔfaasɛ e̱ ba, amɔ ɔ se̱re̱ a ɔ yɔwe̱ e̱sanne̱‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ n gye̱ mò̱ e̱ bo̱ e̱sanne̱‑ɔ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ na ɔfaasɛ‑ɔ lwee e̱sanne̱‑ɔ‑rɔ, amɔ ɔ kra bamo̱ bo̱ko̱, na bamo̱ ne̱ ba saŋ‑ɔ brawe̱‑rɔ.

ref: I have other sheep that are not in this sheep pen. I must also bring them together, when they hear my voice. Then there will be one flock of sheep and one shepherd.
text: E̱sanne̱ ko̱ mɔ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ mo̱ lee, ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ tii mfe̱e̱ a e̱sanne̱‑ɔ se̱. Mo̱ e̱ baa bamo̱ a m ba bo̱ tii bo̱mo̱‑ɔ se̱. Ba nu mo̱ kanɔ, na bo̱ kii e̱sanne̱ kape ko̱ŋko̱ na ɔde̱e̱re̱se̱po̱ ko̱ŋko̱ lee.

ref: The Father loves me, because I give up my life, so I may receive it back again.
text: N se̱ e̱ kpa mo̱ ase̱ŋ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a sure si fe̱yɛ mo̱ i wu a m bo̱ sa ase̱sɛ, na m be̱e̱ bo̱ kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro.

ref: No one takes my life from me. I give it up willingly! I have the power to give it up and the power to receive it back again, just as my Father commanded me to do.
text: Ɔko̱ maa taare̱ a ɔ mɔɔ mo̱, amɔ mo̱ a sa kpa fe̱raa. Mo̱ a lee mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ i wu a m bo̱ sa ase̱sɛ. N de kpa fe̱yɛ ŋ wu, ne̱ m be̱e̱ n de kpa fe̱yɛ n kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro, ane̱ŋ ne̱ n se̱ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ waa‑ɔ.”

ref: The people took sides because of what Jesus had told them.
text: Ase̱sɛ‑ɔ a nu amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa gyiiri ikii, ne̱ baa ke‑ro ntuŋ ŋnyɔ.

ref: Many of them said, “He has a demon in him! He is crazy! Why listen to him?”
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ ne̱e̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i nu ɔnyare̱ mɔ ase̱ŋ? Ibrisi te mò̱ se̱, ne̱ ɔ lɔ e̱bo̱ŋ.”

ref: But others said, “How could anyone with a demon in him say these things? No one like this could give sight to a blind person!” Jesus Is Rejected
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ bo̱ko̱ mɔ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔko̱ ne̱ ibrisi te mò̱ se̱‑ɔ maa taare̱ a ɔ sa se̱ŋsa mfaanɛɛ. E̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ ibrisi e̱ waa a ki buŋŋi ate̱napo̱ akatɔ?” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kine Yeesuu‑o

ref: That winter, Jesus was in Jerusalem for the Temple Festival.
text: Pwe̱e̱re̱pwe̱e̱re̱‑rɔ Yɛro̱salɛm awuye de kasu kanɔ ngyaŋŋe̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa a ke̱kpɛɛ iyisi‑o a ŋke‑o.†

ref: One day he was walking in the part of the temple known as Solomon's Porch,
text: Saŋ amo̱ ne̱ Yeesuu naa Wuribware̱ suŋkpa a abraŋdaye̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ Solomɔŋ a abraŋdaye̱‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: and the people gathered all around him. They said, “How long are you going to keep us guessing? If you are the Messiah, tell us plainly!”
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ a kpɔwe̱ muruwaa mò̱, ne̱ baa bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ŋke mfe̱ne̱ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ane̱ kye̱na, na ane̱ mo̱ŋ nyi ane̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ du‑o? Tɔwe̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ gywii ane̱. Fo̱ e̱ gye̱ Kristoo‑o ooo?”

ref: Jesus answered: I have told you, and you refused to believe me. The things I do by my Father's authority show who I am.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Mo̱ a kyɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ne̱, amaa mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ gyi. Atɔ ne̱ n de n se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ mo̱ e̱ waa‑ɔ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ ase̱ŋ.

ref: But since you are not my sheep, you don't believe me.
text: Amaa mo̱ne̱ maa kɔɔre̱ mo̱ a mo̱nꞌ gyi, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ mo̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱e̱.

ref: My sheep know my voice, and I know them. They follow me,
text: Mo̱ ase̱sɛ fe̱raa i nu mo̱ ase̱ŋ, ne̱ ŋ nyi bamo̱, ne̱ bo̱ gya mo̱ se̱.

ref: and I give them eternal life, so that they will never be lost. No one can snatch them out of my hand.
text: Mo̱ e̱ sa bamo̱ ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ, amo̱se̱‑ɔ bo̱ maa lɛɛ gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ daa. Ɔko̱ maa taare̱ a ɔ kɔɔre̱ bamo̱ le̱e̱ mo̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ.

ref: My Father gave them to me, and he is greater than all others. No one can snatch them from his hands,
text: N se̱ ya taa bamo̱ sa mo̱. Ɔ kyɔ ɔke̱maa, amo̱se̱‑ɔ ɔko̱ maa taare̱ a ɔ kɔɔre̱ bamo̱ le̱e̱ n se̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ.

ref: and I am one with the Father.
text: Mo̱ aa n se̱ gye̱ ko̱ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱e̱.”

ref: Once again the people picked up stones in order to kill Jesus.
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ amo̱ a be̱e̱ taa abu na bo̱ twe̱e̱ bo̱ da mò̱ mɔɔ.

ref: But he said, “I have shown you many good things my Father sent me to do. Which one are you going to stone me for?”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ a waa asuŋ timaa bwe̱e̱tɔ mo̱ne̱ akatɔ‑rɔ, amo̱ ne̱ n se̱ a sa mo̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ waa‑ɔ. Mo̱ asuŋ mɔ‑rɔ, ke̱mo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ki kye mo̱ne̱, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa a mo̱nꞌ twe̱e̱ abu bo̱ da mo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱?”

ref: They answered, “We are not stoning you because of any good thing you did. We are stoning you because you did a terrible thing. You are just a man, and here you are claiming to be God!”
text: Ne̱ baa lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “N gye̱ asuŋ timaa si ne̱ ane̱ e̱ kpa a ane̱ twe̱e̱ abu bo̱ da fo̱, amaa fo̱ maa bu Wuribware̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ se̱. Se̱sɛ e̱ gye̱ fo̱, ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ fo̱ waa fo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ aaa?”

ref: Jesus replied: In your Scriptures doesn't God say, “You are gods”?
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Baa kyo̱rɛɛ mo̱ne̱ fɔŋfɔŋ mbraa wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a te̱e̱ ase̱sɛ ko̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ gyi‑ana.

ref: So why do you accuse me of a terrible sin for saying that I am the Son of God? After all, it is the Father who prepared me for this work. He is also the one who sent me into the world.
text: Ane̱ nyi fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ, na ɔko̱ ma taare̱ a ɔ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ bo̱ kye wo̱re̱ amo̱. Wuribware̱ a te̱e̱ bamo̱ mò̱ gyi‑ana, ne̱ ɔɔ taa mò̱ ase̱ŋ sa bamo̱. To, n se̱ Wuribware̱ ya lee mo̱ suŋ mo̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ kaye̱‑rɔ, ne̱ e̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ n gye̱ Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑o si‑o, mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ mo̱ i kye Wuribware̱ ne̱e̱?

ref: If I don't do as my Father does, you should not believe me.
text: Mo̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ n se̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ŋ waa‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ ma kɔɔre̱ mo̱ gyi.

ref: But if I do what my Father does, you should believe because of that, even if you don't have faith in me. Then you will know for certain that the Father is one with me, and I am one with the Father.
text: Amaa mo̱ ya waa amo̱ ne̱ a pre̱ n se̱‑ɔ fe̱raa, hare̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ maa kɔɔre̱ mo̱ ase̱ŋ a mo̱nꞌ gyi gbaa, mo̱nꞌ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ gyi le̱e̱ atɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ mo̱ a waa‑ɔ se̱. Na mo̱nꞌ nya pini, na mo̱nꞌ kɔɔre̱ gyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ aa n se̱ gye̱ ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱e̱.”

ref: Again they wanted to arrest Jesus. But he escaped
text: Ne̱ baa kpa a bo̱ be̱e̱ kra Yeesuu, ne̱ ɔɔ se̱re̱ le̱e̱ bamo̱ ase̱.

ref: and crossed the Jordan to the place where John had earlier been baptizing. While Jesus was there,
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ kiŋŋi ya te̱ŋ Yo̱o̱daŋ bo̱ŋ‑nɔ yɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yohanee a dɛɛ bɔ ase̱sɛ asuu‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ kye̱na mfe̱ŋ.

ref: many people came to him. They were saying, “John didn't work any miracles, but everything he said about Jesus is true.”
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ a ba mò̱ ase̱. Ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Yohanee mo̱ŋ waa akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ ke̱maa. Amaa ke̱tɔ ne̱ Yohanee a tɔwe̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ Yeesuu si‑o gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ.”

ref: A lot of those people also put their faith in Jesus.
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ a kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi.

ref: The sisters sent a message to the Lord and told him that his good friend Lazarus was sick.
text: Ne̱ mò̱ pe̱kye̱e̱‑ana a kra bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ Yeesuu tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱, fo̱ nyare̱ timaa e̱ lɔ.”

ref: When Jesus heard this, he said, “His sickness won't end in death. It will bring glory to God and his Son.”
text: Yeesuu a nu‑o, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Ko̱lɔ mɔ maa baa Lasarus lowi, amaa kaa ba ne̱e̱ a ase̱sɛ nya ŋu fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ kyɔ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa, na Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑o bo̱rɔ ke̱mo̱ se̱ bo̱ nya ke̱dabe̱ na wuraa.”

ref: “Teacher,” they said, “the people there want to stone you to death! Why do you want to go back?”
text: Ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔkaapo̱po̱, Yudaa awuye a kpa a bo̱ twe̱e̱ abu bo̱ daye̱ fo̱ mɔɔ‑ɔ, a mo̱ŋ tɛɛ kyee, ne̱ fo̱ be̱e̱ fo̱ e̱ yɔ mfe̱ŋ mbe̱yɔmɔ aaa?”

ref: Jesus answered, “Aren't there twelve hours in each day? If you walk during the day, you will have light from the sun, and you won't stumble.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Kyɔwe̱ e̱ da e̱dɔŋhwe̱re̱e̱ kudu e̱nyɔ, bɛɛɛ n gye̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱? Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ naa mpase̱‑ɔ maa da ke̱yaa pɛɛɛ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kyɔwe̱ a sa ne̱ to̱ŋ ke̱maa ke̱e̱‑rɔ.

ref: But if you walk during the night, you will stumble, because you don't have any light.”
text: Amaa mò̱ ya nare̱ kanye, ɔ da ke̱yaa a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ.”

ref: Then he told them, “Our friend Lazarus is asleep, and I am going there to wake him up.”
text: Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ ba ako̱ bo̱ tii si fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ nyare̱ Lasarus a di idi, amaa mo̱ e̱ yɔ a n kyiŋŋi mò̱.”

text: Ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱, mò̱ ya di idi fe̱raa, ɔ ko̱so̱ ŋkpa na alaŋfiya‑ro.”

text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ Yeesuu e̱ kaapo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ Lasarus a wu, amaa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱raa nyi fe̱yɛ Yeesuu yɛ idi mɔ ne̱ ɔke̱maa i di‑o ne̱ Lasarus a di.

ref: Thomas, whose nickname was “Twin,” said to the other disciples, “Come on. Let's go, so we can die with him.” Jesus Brings Lazarus to Life
text: Tomas, ne̱ ɔ gye̱ ke̱le̱ŋba‑ɔ, a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ aa ɔkaapo̱po̱‑ɔ a yɔ, ne̱ amo̱ e̱ gye̱ lowi ne̱e̱ na ane̱ aa mò̱ a wu.” Yeesuu e̱ gye̱ ne̱ o kyiŋŋi se̱sɛ a ɔ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro na ɔ sa mò̱ ŋkpa

text: Yeesuu a kpa a ɔ fo̱ Lasarus‑ana aye̱‑ɔ, oo ŋu fe̱yɛ baa kyɔ pure Lasarus, ne̱ ŋke nna a kyo̱ŋ gbaa.

ref: Bethany was less than three kilometers from Jerusalem,

text: Ne̱ Maataa a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ i gyi fe̱yɛ fo̱ e̱ gye̱ Kristoo, Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑o, ne̱ Wuribware̱ yɛ o suŋ a ɔ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ kaye̱‑rɔ‑ɔ.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a su‑o

text: Yeesuu a ŋu ane̱ŋ ne̱ Mariya i su‑o na ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ gya mò̱ se̱‑ɔ i su‑o, ne̱ mò̱ bo̱re̱‑rɔ a duŋwi mò̱, ne̱ aa waa mò̱ e̱wɛɛ.
