text: Ne̱ baa ba Filipo ase̱, ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Ɔbre̱sɛ, ane̱ e̱ kpa a ane̱ ŋu Yeesuu ne̱e̱.”

ref: But the Pharisees said to each other, “There is nothing we can do! Everyone in the world is following Jesus.” Some Greeks Want To Meet Jesus
text: Ne̱ Farisii awuye‑o e̱ tɔwe̱ ba gywii abɛɛ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ a ŋu aaa? Ane̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ maa kpa a a kra pɛɛɛ‑ɔ. Mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ a gya mò̱ se̱!” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Griiki awuye ko̱ a buwi kpa Yeesuu‑o

ref: So Father, bring glory to yourself. A voice from heaven then said, “I have already brought glory to myself, and I will do it again!”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ fe̱yɛ, “N se̱, sa a fo̱ nya ke̱nyare̱ dabe̱ na wuraa.” Puri amo̱‑rɔ, ne̱ oo nu bo̱re̱ ko̱ le̱e̱ so̱so̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ a nya ke̱nyare̱ dabe̱ na wuraa, na m be̱e̱ sa a ŋ nya.”

ref: “The Lord has blinded the eyes of the people, and he has made the people stubborn. He did this so that they could not see or understand, and so that they would not turn to the Lord and be healed.”
text: “Wuribware̱ a buŋ bamo̱ akatɔ se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ bamo̱ aŋu‑ro a waa le̱ŋ, Le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, bamo̱ akatɔ maa buŋŋi a bo̱ ŋu atɔ, ne̱ bamo̱ mfɛɛre̱ maa sa a bo̱ nu ase̱ŋ kaase̱, ne̱ bo̱ maa nu bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱, na ɔ taa bamo̱ e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ ke bamo̱.”

ref: The next day a large crowd was in Jerusalem for Passover. When they heard that Jesus was coming for the festival,
text: Kaye̱ ŋke‑o, ne̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ baa ba Wuribware̱-a‑kya-ane̱-yɔwe̱ a ateese kigyibe̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ a nu fe̱yɛ Yeesuu e̱ ba Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ.

ref: “People of Jerusalem, don't be afraid! Your King is now coming, and he is riding on a donkey.”
text: “Mo̱ne̱ ɔke̱maa ne̱ o te Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ! Mo̱nꞌ sa a mo̱ne̱ akatɔ gyi. Mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ owure a de̱e̱ kakurumagyii ɔ ba.”

ref: At first, Jesus' disciples did not understand. But after he had been given his glory, they remembered all this. Everything had happened exactly as the Scriptures said it would.
text: Saŋ ne̱ ɔɔ waa amo̱‑ɔ, mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ nu kaase̱. Amaa saŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a wu, ne̱ oo kyiŋŋi o yii Wuribware̱ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa nyiŋŋi si fe̱yɛ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ a kyɔ tɔwe̱ mò̱ ase̱ŋ amo̱, ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ a waa‑ɔ ne̱e̱.”

ref: A crowd had come to meet Jesus because they had seen him call Lazarus out of the tomb. They kept talking about him and this miracle.
text: Amo̱ ya sa ne̱ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ a yɔ ya gyaŋŋaa Yeesuu kpa‑rɔ‑ɔ. Baa nu le̱e̱ bo̱ko̱‑ɔ ase̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ ya waa ane̱ŋ a akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ mɔ.

ref: Philip told Andrew. Then the two of them went to Jesus and told him. The Son of Man Must Be Lifted Up
text: Ne̱ Filipo a ya tɔwe̱ gywii Andruu, ne̱ bamo̱ abɛɛ anyɔ a ya tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ase̱sɛ amo̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ gywii Yeesuu.

ref: Jesus said: The time has come for the Son of Man to be given his glory.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, saŋ a fo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ sa mo̱ ke̱dabe̱ na wuraa.

ref: I tell you for certain that a grain of wheat that falls on the ground will never be more than one grain unless it dies. But if it dies, it will produce lots of wheat.
text: Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ ke̱yaabraagyi ya sii atɔ‑rɔ, ke̱ maa waa sɛye̱, amaa ke̱mo̱ ya le̱e̱ da e̱se̱‑rɔ fe̱raa, ke̱ e̱ kwɛɛ, na kigyi de̱daa‑ɔ wu na ke̱po̱pwɛɛ‑ɔ daŋ ko̱we̱ ayaabraagyi bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: If you love your life, you will lose it. If you give it up in this world, you will be given eternal life.
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ kpa mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ŋkpa bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ e̱ paŋ mmo̱ ne̱e̱. Ɔke̱maa mɔ ne̱ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ŋkpa ase̱ŋ mo̱ŋ tiri mò̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ e̱ nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: Now I am deeply troubled, and I don't know what to say. But I must not ask my Father to keep me from this time of suffering. In fact, I came into the world to suffer.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya se̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mbe̱yɔmɔ mo̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ a nye̱ra. Amɔ n tɔwe̱ gywii n se̱ a o kuŋ ase̱ŋ mɔ ne̱ a kpa a ba mo̱ se̱‑ɔ bɛɛɛ? Daabii! Amo̱ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ a ba. Mo̱ a ba ne̱e̱ a m bo̱ gyi faanɛɛ a awo̱re̱fɔɔ mɔ, na ŋ wu saŋ ko̱ŋko̱ mɔ‑rɔ.”

ref: When the crowd heard the voice, some of them thought it was thunder. Others thought an angel had spoken to Jesus.
text: Ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ ye̱re̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ a nu bo̱re̱‑ɔ, ne̱ bamo̱ bo̱ko̱ yɛ baa nu ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bware̱ a su‑ro. Ne̱ bo̱ko̱ mɔ yɛ, “Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ya sa se̱ŋsa gywii mò̱.”

ref: This world's people are now being judged, and the ruler of this world is already being thrown out!
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ e̱ gye̱ saŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ i gyi kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ ase̱ŋ, na o tu Ɔbɔnsam ne̱ bo̱ fo̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ o de kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ mfɛɛre̱ se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ‑ɔ le̱e̱ mò̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱.

ref: If I am lifted up above the earth, I will make everyone want to come to me.”
text: Bamo̱ ya yase̱ mo̱ so̱so̱, mo̱ i gyiiri ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ a m baa mo̱ ase̱.”

ref: Jesus was talking about the way he would be put to death.
text: Mò̱ a tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ, ase̱sɛ e̱ ye̱re̱ amo̱ se̱ a bo̱ bo̱ pini lowi suyo ne̱ ɔ ba a ɔ bo̱ wu‑o.

ref: The crowd said to Jesus, “The Scriptures teach that the Messiah will live forever. How can you say that the Son of Man must be lifted up? Who is this Son of Man?”
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “A bo̱ Wuribware̱ mbraa a wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ Kristoo‑o e̱ kye̱na nsu pɛɛɛ. Ne̱ e̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ a tiri fe̱yɛ bo̱ yase̱ dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o so̱so̱? Ne̱ ŋkee, nsɛ e̱ gye̱ dimaadi mò̱ gyi ne̱ fo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱e̱? Fo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ase̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱, bɛɛɛ ɔko̱‑ɔ aaa?”

ref: Jesus answered, “The light will be with you for only a little longer. Walk in the light while you can. Then you won't be caught walking blindly in the dark.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “N du fe̱yɛ ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ‑ɔ. Ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ‑ɔ e̱ laŋŋe̱ mo̱ne̱‑rɔ a ki kyee kafwe̱e̱. Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ kya mo̱ne̱ kpa se̱ nare̱ nsaŋ ne̱ ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ bo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, na kibugyii ma nya da mo̱ne̱ kpa‑rɔ. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ naa kibugyii‑ro‑o mo̱ŋ nyi mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ yɔ‑ɔ.

ref: Have faith in the light while it is with you, and you will be children of the light.” The People Refuse To Have Faith in Jesus After Jesus had said these things, he left and went into hiding.
text: Amo̱se̱, mo̱ a saŋ m bo̱ mo̱ne̱ ase̱‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ gyi, na mo̱nꞌ nya kii ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ te ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ ase̱sɛ ko̱ a kine ko̱kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi‑o Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ kamɛɛ‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ ya kwe̱e̱rɔ bamo̱ se̱.

ref: He had worked a lot of miracles among the people, but they were still not willing to have faith in him.
text: Amaa ɔɔ waa akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ, ne̱ bamo̱ akatɔ a ŋu, amo̱ pɛɛɛ gbaa bo̱ mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi.

ref: This happened so that what the prophet Isaiah had said would come true, “Lord, who has believed our message? And who has seen your mighty strength?”
text: Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ Isaya ne̱ ɔɔ dɛɛ kye̱na‑ɔ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ a ba‑rɔ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ, “Wuribware̱, ɔko̱ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ɔɔ kɔɔre̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ gyi aaa? Ne̱ ɔko̱ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ fo̱ a sa ne̱ oo ŋu ane̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ fo̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ de̱maŋte̱‑ɔ ɔɔɔ?”

ref: The people could not have faith in Jesus, because Isaiah had also said,
text: Isaya a tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ taare̱ kɔɔre̱ gyi‑o, ɔ yɛ,

ref: Isaiah said this, because he saw the glory of Jesus and spoke about him.
text: Ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ Isaya a tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ oo ŋu Yeesuu ke̱yaale̱ŋ ne̱ ko̱ kyɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ mò̱ ase̱ŋ.

ref: Even then, many of the leaders put their faith in Jesus, but they did not tell anyone about it. The Pharisees had already given orders for the people not to have anything to do with anyone who had faith in Jesus.
text: Amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ gbaa Yudaa awuye abre̱sɛ‑ɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ a kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi, amaa Farisii awuye‑o si‑o kufu de bamo̱, amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ bo̱ mo̱ŋ lee amo̱ kawu. Kufu de bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ya tɔwe̱ amo̱ ase̱ŋ kawu si, Farisii awuye‑o maa lɛɛ sa bamo̱ kpa a bo̱ lwee bamo̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: And besides, the leaders liked praise from others more than they liked praise from God. Jesus Came To Save the World
text: Ba kpa ase̱sɛ nkyo̱rɔ bo̱ kyo̱ŋ Wuribware̱ nkyo̱rɔ. Abware̱se̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu ye̱re̱ se̱ o de o bu ke̱pɔ‑ɔ

ref: In a loud voice Jesus said: Everyone who has faith in me also has faith in the one who sent me.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a yase̱ mò̱ bo̱re̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ o gyi‑o, n gye̱ mo̱ wo̱re̱ ne̱ ɔ kɔɔre̱ o gyi, amaa ɔ kɔɔre̱ n se̱ ne̱ oo suŋ mo̱‑ɔ gbaa o gyi.

ref: And everyone who has seen me has seen the one who sent me.
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ɔke̱maa ne̱ o ŋu mo̱‑ɔ i ŋu mò̱ ne̱ oo suŋ mo̱‑ɔ.

ref: I am the light that has come into the world. No one who has faith in me will stay in the dark.
text: Mo̱ a ba kaye̱‑rɔ fe̱yɛ ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ‑ɔ ne̱e̱, na ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ o gyi‑o ma nya sii kibugyii‑ro.

ref: I am not the one who will judge those who refuse to obey my teachings. I came to save the people of this world, not to be their judge.
text: Amaa ɔke̱maa ne̱ o nu mo̱ ase̱ŋ, ne̱ ɔ maa bu amo̱‑ɔ, n gye̱ mo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ mo̱ i bu mò̱ ke̱pɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ŋ ba ne̱e̱ a m bo̱ bu kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ ke̱pɔ. Mo̱ a ba ne̱e̱ a m bo̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ bamo̱.

ref: But everyone who rejects me and my teachings will be judged on the last day by what I have said.
text: To, se̱sɛ ne̱ o kine mo̱, ne̱ ɔ maa gya mo̱ kanɔ se̱‑ɔ de ɔko̱ ne̱ o bu mò̱ ke̱pɔ‑ɔ. Mò̱ e̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ. Mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ o bu mò̱ ke̱pɔ.

ref: I don't speak on my own. I say only what the Father who sent me has told me to say.
text: N gye̱ mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ke̱yaale̱ŋ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱, amaa n se̱ ne̱ oo suŋ mo̱‑ɔ ya sa mo̱ kanɔ fe̱yɛ n tɔwe̱.

ref: I know that his commands will bring eternal life. This is why I tell you exactly what the Father has told me.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa ne̱ mò̱ ya nu n se̱ kanɔ‑ɔ e̱ nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ n se̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ fe̱yɛ n tɔwe̱‑ɔ.”

ref: It was before Passover, and Jesus knew that the time had come for him to leave this world and to return to the Father. He had always loved his followers in this world, and he loved them to the very end.
text: Amo̱ a saŋ kake ko̱ŋko̱ a bo̱ gyi Wuribware̱-a‑kya-ane̱-yɔwe̱ a ateese‑o, Yeesuu a nyi fe̱yɛ mò̱ saŋ‑ɔ a fo̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ le̱e̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ yɔ mò̱ se̱ ase̱‑ɔ. Ɔɔ kpa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ ase̱ŋ saŋ ke̱maa, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ gye̱ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ase̱sɛ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ ɔ ba a ɔ bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ kpa bamo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ.

ref: Even before the evening meal started, the devil had made Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot, decide to betray Jesus.
text: Yeesuu mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a te ba gyi ke̱de̱e̱pwɛta a ateese‑o, Ɔbɔnsam a kyɔ bo̱ lwee bamo̱ ɔko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Yudas, Simɔŋ Kariyotinyi mò̱ gyi‑o, kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ o gyi Yeesuu kidiburo.

ref: Jesus knew he had come from God and would go back to God. He also knew that the Father had given him complete power.
text: Yeesuu mɔ nyi fe̱yɛ mò̱ se̱ a sa mò̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa se̱, ne̱ o nyi fe̱yɛ ɔɔ le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱ ne̱e̱, ne̱ ɔ be̱e̱ ɔ yɔ mò̱ ase̱.

ref: So during the meal Jesus got up, removed his outer garment, and wrapped a towel around his waist.
text: Amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ gbaa Yeesuu a ko̱so̱ teeburi ase̱ ye̱re̱, ne̱ oo buŋŋi mò̱ waagya na kaare̱ ne̱ o buŋ‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ mare̱ brɔde̱baa laare̱‑rɔ.

ref: He put some water into a large bowl. Then he began washing his disciples' feet and drying them with the towel he was wearing.
text: Ne̱ oo twiiri nkyu waa ke̱baa‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ ɔ fwe̱e̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ ayaa se̱, na o de brɔde̱baa ne̱ ɔɔ mare̱ laare̱‑rɔ‑ɔ o de ɔ kpɛɛ bamo̱ ayaa se̱.

ref: Jesus answered, “You don't really know what I am doing, but later you will understand.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mbe̱yɔmɔ fo̱ maa nu ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa‑ɔ kaase̱, amaa kamɛɛ‑rɔ fo̱ e̱ ba a fo̱ bo̱ nu kaase̱.”

ref: “You will never wash my feet!” Peter replied. “If I don't wash you,” Jesus told him, “you don't really belong to me.”
text: Ne̱ Peetroo a tɔwe̱ gywii Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ ma fwe̱e̱ mo̱ ayaa mɔ se̱ daa!” Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Fo̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ sa a m fwe̱e̱ fo̱ ayaa se̱, amo̱ fe̱raa fo̱ maa lɛɛ tii mo̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ se̱.”

ref: Jesus answered, “People who have bathed and are clean all over need to wash just their feet. And you, my disciples, are clean, except for one of you.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔɔ kyɔ gyee‑o mo̱ŋ de iyisi pɛɛɛ, amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, a mo̱ŋ lɛɛ a tiri fe̱yɛ o gyee, amɔ ayaa se̱ ke̱fwe̱e̱ wo̱re̱. Mo̱ne̱ pɛɛɛ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ n de iyisi, amɔ mo̱ne̱‑rɔ ɔko̱ŋko̱ wo̱re̱.”

ref: After Jesus had washed his disciples' feet and had put his outer garment back on, he sat down again. Then he said: Do you understand what I have done?
text: Yeesuu a fwe̱e̱ bamo̱ ayaa se̱ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ taa mò̱ kaare̱‑ɔ na mò̱ waagya‑ɔ be̱e̱ buŋ, ne̱ oo kiŋŋi ya kye̱na mò̱ kye̱nakpa teeburi ase̱. Ne̱ oo bise mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ŋ mɔ ne̱ mo̱ a waa mo̱ne̱‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ a nu kaase̱ aaa?” Ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ taare̱ lee kanɔ.

ref: I have set the example, and you should do for each other exactly what I have done for you.
text: Mo̱ a waa amo̱‑ɔ bo̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ ne̱e̱ a mo̱ne̱ mɔ mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱ waa ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ a waa sa mo̱ne̱‑ɔ dɛɛ.

ref: I tell you for certain that servants are not greater than their master, and messengers are not greater than the one who sent them.
text: Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ ke̱yaafɔre̱ ko̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ɔ kyɔ mò̱ nyaŋpe̱. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ɔ kyɔ mò̱ ne̱ oo suŋ mò̱‑ɔ.

ref: I am telling you this before it all happens. Then when it does happen, you will believe who I am.
text: Mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ pwɛɛ na a dɛɛ ba, na, amo̱ ya ba‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ ma yɔwe̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ n gye̱.

ref: So the disciple leaned toward Jesus and asked, “Lord, which one of us are you talking about?”
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ ane̱ŋ a ɔgyase̱po̱‑ɔ a sɔŋ me̱raa Yeesuu, ne̱ oo kuri bise fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, nsɛ ne̱ fo̱ de?”

ref: After Jesus had said these things, he was deeply troubled and told his disciples, “I tell you for certain that one of you will betray me.”
text: Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ a nye̱ra, ne̱ oo lee amo̱ kawu tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱‑rɔ ɔko̱ e̱ ba a ɔ bo̱ gyi mo̱ kidiburo.”

ref: Jesus' favorite disciple was sitting next to him at the meal,
text: Ne̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ ɔko̱ ne̱ Yeesuu e̱ kpa mò̱ ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ te ɔ gya mò̱ se̱.

ref: But because Judas was in charge of the money, some of them thought that Jesus had told him to buy something they needed for the festival. Others thought that Jesus had told him to give some money to the poor.
text: Yudas a gye̱ ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ de̱e̱re̱ bamo̱ atanne̱ ko̱re̱e̱ se̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, agyase̱po̱‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ nyi fe̱yɛ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ ya sɔɔ ateese ne̱ a tiri bamo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱, bɛɛɛ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ o lee atanne̱‑ɔ ako̱ sa atiripo̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: Judas took the piece of bread and went out. It was already night. The New Command
text: Yudas a kɔɔre̱ bodobodoo‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ kawu kaluwa ko̱ŋko̱ o yii. Saŋ amo̱‑ɔ kyɔwe̱ a lo̱we̱. Mbraa po̱pwɛɛ‑ɔ

ref: Then, after God is given glory because of him, God will bring glory to him, and God will do it very soon.
text: To, Wuribware̱ e̱ bo̱rɔ mo̱ se̱ a ɔ bo̱ kaapo̱ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ke̱dabe̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ɔ bo̱rɔ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ se̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, ke̱dabe̱. Ɔ kpa a ɔ waa amo̱‑ɔ mbe̱yɔmɔ.

ref: My children, I will be with you for only a little while longer. Then you will look for me, but you won't find me. I tell you just as I told the people, “You cannot go where I am going.”
text: Mo̱ gyi‑ana, ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii Yudaa awuye abre̱sɛ‑ɔ dɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ mo̱ aa mo̱ne̱ e̱ kye̱na saŋ katiŋ ko̱ ne̱e̱, mo̱ne̱ i buwi a mo̱nꞌ kpa mo̱, amaa mo̱ne̱ maa taare̱ a mo̱nꞌ yɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ yɔ‑ɔ.

ref: “Would you really die for me?” Jesus asked. “I tell you for certain before a rooster crows, you will say three times that you don't even know me.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱ fo̱ a waa siraa fe̱yɛ fo̱ i wu a fo̱ sa mo̱ aaa? Mo̱ i gyi fo̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ, fo̱ e̱ swe̱e̱ iluwi e̱sa fe̱yɛ fo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi mo̱ pwɛɛ na kyaŋare̱ dɛɛ fo̱re̱.”

ref: Jesus said to his disciples, “Don't be worried! Have faith in God and have faith in me.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya se̱ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ma lɛɛ sa a mo̱ne̱ iduŋ te̱ŋŋe̱ mo̱ne̱. Mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱ kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ gyi, na mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱ kɔɔre̱ mo̱‑ɔ gyi.

ref: There are many rooms in my Father's house. I wouldn't tell you this, unless it was true. I am going there to prepare a place for each of you.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ akyaŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ bo̱ n se̱ aye̱, amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ mo̱ e̱ gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ a ŋ nya lɔŋŋɔ e̱kye̱nakpa sa mo̱ne̱. Weetee a dɛɛ a mo̱ŋ gye̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱e̱, ma tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ a n gywii mo̱ne̱.

ref: After I have done this, I will come back and take you with me. Then we will be together.
text: Fe̱yɛ mo̱ ya lɔŋŋɔ e̱kye̱nakpa sa mo̱ne̱‑ɔ, mo̱ i kiŋŋi a m ba bo̱ taa mo̱ne̱ yaa mo̱ ase̱, na mo̱nꞌ nya kye̱na mfe̱ŋ ne̱ n te‑o.

ref: You know the way to where I am going.”
text: Mo̱ne̱ nyi ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ waa a mo̱nꞌ yɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ ya kye̱na‑ɔ.”

ref: Thomas said, “Lord, we don't even know where you are going! How can we know the way?”
text: Ne̱ Tomas a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, ane̱ mo̱ŋ nyi mfe̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ yɔ‑ɔ. Ne̱ e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ waa a ane̱ ŋu kpa bo̱ yɔ mfe̱ŋ?”

ref: If you had really known me, you would have known the Father. But from now on, you do know him, and you have seen him.”
text: Mo̱ne̱ nyi mo̱ ne̱e̱, weetee mbe̱yɔmɔ mo̱ne̱ nyi n se̱. Le̱e̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ bo̱ yɔ, mo̱ne̱ i gyii mò̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ a ŋu mò̱.”

ref: Philip said, “Lord, show us the Father. That is all we need.”
text: Ne̱ Filipo a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, kaapo̱ ane̱ fo̱ se̱‑ɔ, ke̱tɔ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ pɛɛɛ ne̱e̱.”

ref: Jesus replied: Philip, I have been with you for a long time. Don't you know who I am? If you have seen me, you have seen the Father. How can you ask me to show you the Father?
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Filipo, le̱e̱ ke̱de̱daade̱daa ne̱ mo̱ aa mo̱ne̱ pɛɛɛ a kye̱na‑ɔ. Fo̱ saŋ fo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi mo̱ aaa? Ɔke̱maa ne̱ oo ŋu mo̱‑ɔ, oo ŋu n se̱ ne̱e̱. Ne̱ e̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ fo̱ yɛ ŋ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ n se̱?

ref: Don't you believe that I am one with the Father and that the Father is one with me? What I say isn't said on my own. The Father who lives in me does these things.
text: Fo̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ fo̱ i gyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ aa n se̱ gye̱ ko̱ko̱ŋko̱, bɛɛɛ? Abware̱se̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ne̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ le̱e̱ mo̱ ase̱ ne̱e̱. Aa le̱e̱ n se̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ mo̱ ase̱‑ɔ ase̱ ne̱e̱. N se̱ bo̱ mo̱ ase̱ ne̱e̱ ɔ waa mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ kusuŋ.

ref: Have faith in me when I say that the Father is one with me and that I am one with the Father. Or else have faith in me simply because of the things I do.
text: Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱ kɔɔre̱ gyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ aa n se̱ gye̱ ko̱ko̱ŋko̱. Mo̱ne̱ e̱ ma taare̱ a mo̱nꞌ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ase̱ŋ gyi, amo̱ fe̱raa, asuŋ ne̱ mo̱ a waa‑ɔ se̱, mo̱nꞌ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ ase̱ŋ gyi.

ref: I tell you for certain that if you have faith in me, you will do the same things I am doing. You will do even greater things, now that I am going back to the Father.
text: Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ o gyi‑o e̱ waa asuŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa‑ɔ. Ɔ waa ako̱ na a kyɔ amo̱‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ yɔ n se̱ ase̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: Ask me, and I will do whatever you ask. This way the Son will bring honor to the Father.
text: Mo̱ ya yɔ n se̱ ase̱, ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ ke̱maa ya ko̱re̱ mo̱ ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, mo̱ e̱ waa a n sa mò̱, na n se̱ ke̱dabe̱ nya bo̱rɔ mo̱ se̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ kawu.

ref: Jesus said to his disciples: If you love me, you will do as I command.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya se̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa mo̱ ase̱ŋ fe̱raa, mo̱nꞌ gya mo̱ mbraa se̱.

ref: Then I will ask the Father to send you the Holy Spirit who will help you and always be with you.
text: Na ŋ ye̱re̱‑rɔ tɔwe̱ gywii n se̱, na ɔ ba ɔkyarɔpo̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ mo̱ne̱, na ɔ ye̱re̱ mo̱ ayaa‑rɔ, na mo̱ne̱ aa mò̱ kye̱na.

ref: The Spirit will show you what is true. The people of this world cannot accept the Spirit, because they don't see or know him. But you know the Spirit, who is with you and will keep on living in you.
text: Mò̱ e̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o, kase̱ŋtiŋ wuye‑o. Kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ maa taare̱ a bo̱ buŋŋi bamo̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ sa mò̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ mo̱ŋ ke̱e̱ mò̱, ne̱ bo̱ maa taare̱ a bo̱ gyii mò̱. Amaa mo̱ne̱ fe̱raa, mo̱ne̱ e̱ taare̱ a mo̱nꞌ gyii mò̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ o sii mo̱ne̱ ase̱, na ɔ kye̱na mo̱ne̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ.

ref: I won't leave you like orphans. I will come back to you.
text: Ma yɔwe̱ mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ aŋɔŋgyi‑o, amaa mo̱ e̱ be̱e̱ kiŋŋi a m ba mo̱ne̱ ase̱.

ref: In a little while the people of this world won't be able to see me, but you will see me. And because I live, you will live.
text: A bo̱ waa kafwe̱e̱‑ɔ, kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ maa lɛɛ ŋu mo̱, amaa mo̱ne̱ fe̱raa mo̱ne̱ i ŋu mo̱. Bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ i kyiŋŋi a m be̱e̱ nya ŋkpa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ mɔ mo̱ne̱ e̱ nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: Then you will know I am one with the Father. You will know you are one with me, and I am one with you.
text: Kake amo̱ ya fo̱, mo̱ne̱ i ŋu fe̱yɛ mo̱ aa n se̱ gye̱ ko̱ko̱ŋko̱, ne̱ mo̱ aa mo̱ne̱ mɔ gye̱ ko̱ko̱ŋko̱.

ref: If you love me, you will do what I have said, and my Father will love you. I will also love you and show you what I am like.
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ mbraa, ne̱ ɔ gya mmo̱ se̱ nɛɛnɛɛ‑ɔ, a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ kpa mo̱ ase̱ŋ. Ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ kpa mo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ, n se̱ e̱ kpa mò̱ ase̱ŋ. Na ŋ gbaa, ŋ kpa mò̱ ase̱ŋ, na n sa a o gyii mo̱.”

ref: The other Judas, not Judas Iscariot, then spoke up and asked, “Lord, what do you mean by saying that you will show us what you are like, but you will not show the people of this world?”
text: Ne̱ Yudas yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, e̱me̱ne̱ ya waa se̱ ne̱ fo̱ maa sa a kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ gyii fo̱, ne̱ ane̱ wo̱re̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ane̱ i gyii fo̱?”

ref: Jesus replied: If anyone loves me, they will obey me. Then my Father will love them, and we will come to them and live in them.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ kpa mo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ e̱ gya mo̱ kanɔ se̱. Na n se̱ kpa mò̱ ase̱ŋ, na mo̱ aa n se̱ ba mò̱ ase̱ bo̱ kye̱na.

ref: But anyone who doesn't love me, won't obey me. What they have heard me say doesn't really come from me, but from the Father who sent me.
text: Ɔke̱maa mɔ ne̱ ɔ maa kpa mo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ maa gya mo̱ kanɔ se̱. Ase̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a nu le̱e̱ mo̱ ase̱‑ɔ mɔ mo̱ŋ le̱e̱ mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ase̱ ne̱e̱, amaa aa le̱e̱ n se̱ ne̱ oo suŋ mo̱‑ɔ ase̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: I have told you these things while I am still with you.
text: Mo̱ a saŋ m bo̱ mo̱ne̱ ase̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ gywii mo̱ne̱.

ref: But the Holy Spirit will come and help you, because the Father will send the Spirit to take my place. The Spirit will teach you everything and will remind you of what I said while I was with you.
text: Amaa mo̱ne̱ ya taŋ mo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱, ɔkyarɔpo̱ amo̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o ne̱ n se̱ de mò̱ ɔ kyo̱ŋwe̱ mo̱ ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ‑ɔ e̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa, na ɔ sa a mo̱nꞌ nyiŋŋi ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ne̱‑ɔ se̱.

ref: I give you peace, the kind of peace only I can give. It isn't like the peace this world can give. So don't be worried or afraid.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ko̱ kyɔ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ sa mo̱ne̱ kaye̱e̱yuri. Mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ kaye̱e̱yuri ne̱ n de mo̱ e̱ sa mo̱ne̱, amaa n gye̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ de ba sa‑ɔ ne̱ n de mo̱ e̱ sa mo̱ne̱. Mo̱ne̱ ma sa a mo̱ne̱ iduŋ te̱ŋŋe̱ mo̱ne̱ na kufu nya mo̱ne̱.

ref: You have already heard me say I am going and I will also come back to you. If you really love me, you should be glad I am going back to the Father, because he is greater than I am.
text: Mo̱ne̱ a nu fe̱yɛ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ yɔ ne̱e̱, amaa mo̱ i kiŋŋi a m ba mo̱ne̱ ase̱, bɛɛɛ? Mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa mo̱ ase̱ŋ fe̱raa, weetee mo̱ne̱ akatɔ i gyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ a yɔ n se̱ ase̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ kyɔ mo̱ kpa ke̱maa se̱.

ref: I am telling you this before I leave, so when it does happen, you will have faith in me.
text: Mo̱ a tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ gywii mo̱ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ pwɛɛ na a dɛɛ ba, ne̱ amo̱‑ɔ ya ba‑ɔ, na mo̱nꞌ nya kɔɔre̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ gyi.

ref: I cannot speak with you much longer, because the ruler of this world is coming. But he has no power over me.
text: Ma lɛɛ taare̱ a mo̱ aa mo̱ne̱ a sa se̱ŋsa kyee, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Ɔbɔnsam ne̱ o de kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ mfɛɛre̱ se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ‑ɔ e̱ ba, amaa ɔ mo̱ŋ de ke̱yaale̱ŋ mo̱ se̱.

ref: I obey my Father, so everyone in the world might know that I love him. It is time for us to go now.
text: Amaa amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ e̱ ba na kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ nya ŋu fe̱yɛ kase̱ŋtiŋ si mo̱ e̱ kpa n se̱ ase̱ŋ, ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ n se̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ ɛ ŋ waa‑ɔ ne̱e̱. To, mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ le̱e̱ mfe̱e̱.”

ref: Jesus said to his disciples: I am the true vine, and my Father is the gardener.
text: Bamo̱ a maa se̱ ba yɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu a kya se̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “N du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bobe kiyii timaa. N se̱ mɔ e̱ bo̱ ke̱mo̱.

ref: He cuts away every branch of mine that doesn't produce fruit. But he trims clean every branch that does produce fruit, so that it will produce even more fruit.
text: Ɔ kpo̱ŋŋe̱ kayaagyii ke̱maa ne̱ ka maa swɛɛ agyi‑o a ɔ bo̱ twe̱e̱, na o ŋeri ŋyaagyii ko̱ ne̱ n swɛɛ agyi‑o si, na ŋ nya pɔwe̱ swɛɛ agyi bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: You are already clean because of what I have said to you.
text: Mo̱ne̱ fe̱raa, mo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ne̱‑ɔ a kyɔ sa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a te̱ŋ mo̱ne̱ ŋyo̱wɔre̱ le̱e̱ e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ.

ref: Stay joined to me, and I will stay joined to you. Just as a branch cannot produce fruit unless it stays joined to the vine, you cannot produce fruit unless you stay joined to me.
text: Kiyii‑o yɛ, ŋyaagyii, mo̱nꞌ kye̱na mo̱ se̱ nɛɛnɛɛ, na mo̱‑ɔ ŋ nya kra mo̱ne̱‑rɔ keŋkeŋ. Kiyii kayaagyii maa taare̱ a ka le̱ŋ kamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ se̱ swɛɛ agyi, amɔ kayaagyii‑o te kiyii‑o si. N du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kiyii‑o, mo̱ne̱ mɔ mo̱ne̱ du fe̱yɛ ŋyaagyii‑o. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ maa taare̱ a mo̱nꞌ swɛɛ agyi, amɔ mo̱ne̱ te mo̱ se̱.

ref: I am the vine, and you are the branches. If you stay joined to me, and I stay joined to you, then you will produce lots of fruit. But you cannot do anything without me.
text: Ŋ yɛ ɛɛɛ, n du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bobe kiyii‑o, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ mɔ mo̱ne̱ du fe̱yɛ ke̱mo̱ ŋyaagyii‑o. Ɔke̱maa ne̱ o te mo̱ se̱, ne̱ mo̱‑ɔ n de mò̱‑rɔ keŋkeŋ‑o e̱ swɛɛ agyi bwe̱e̱tɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ tii mo̱ se̱, mo̱ne̱ wo̱re̱ maa taare̱ a mo̱nꞌ waa sɛye̱.

ref: If you don't stay joined to me, you will be thrown away. You will be like dry branches that are gathered up and burned in a fire.
text: Amaa mò̱ ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ te mo̱ se̱‑ɔ e̱ le̱e̱ a ɔ le̱e̱ da, na bo̱ taa mò̱ twe̱e̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ fe̱yɛ kiyii kayaagyii‑o, na ka di wo̱re̱. Na bo̱ taa kamo̱ bo̱ waa de̱e̱kpa‑rɔ kure.

ref: Stay joined to me and let my teachings become part of you. Then you can pray for whatever you want, and your prayer will be answered.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ya kye̱na mo̱ se̱, ne̱ mo̱‑ɔ mɔ mo̱ a kra mo̱ne̱‑rɔ keŋkeŋ, amo̱ fe̱raa mo̱ne̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ e̱ nya.

ref: When you become fruitful disciples of mine, my Father will be honored.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ya swɛɛ agyi bwe̱e̱tɔ, n se̱ e̱ nya ke̱dabe̱, na a be̱e̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ a kii mo̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: I have loved you, just as my Father has loved me. So remain faithful to my love for you.
text: Mo̱ e̱ de̱e̱ kpa mo̱ne̱ ase̱ŋ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ n se̱ e̱ kpa mo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ. Mo̱ne̱ fe̱raa mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱ kye̱na ane̱ŋ ne̱ a sa a n de̱e̱ kpa mo̱ne̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ.

ref: If you obey me, I will keep loving you, just as my Father keeps loving me, because I have obeyed him.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ya gya mo̱ mbraa se̱, mo̱ e̱ de̱e̱ kpa mo̱ne̱ ase̱ŋ, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ a gya n se̱ mbraa se̱, ne̱ ɔ saŋ ɔ kpa mo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ.

ref: I have told you this to make you as completely happy as I am.
text: Mo̱ a tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ gywii mo̱ne̱ ne̱e̱ a mo̱ne̱ akatɔ nya gyi. Mo̱ne̱ kakatɔgyi bwe̱e̱tɔ mɔ e̱ le̱e̱ mo̱ ase̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: Now I tell you to love each other, as I have loved you.
text: Mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱ kpa abɛɛ ase̱ŋ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ŋ kine mo̱ne̱ ase̱ŋ ke̱kpa‑ɔ.

ref: The greatest way to show love for friends is to die for them.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ nyiŋkpasɛ e̱ ba a ɔ bo̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ kpa mò̱ nyare̱‑ana ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ a wu sa bamo̱.

ref: Servants don't know what their master is doing, and so I don't speak to you as my servants. I speak to you as my friends, and I have told you everything my Father has told me.
text: Ma lɛɛ te̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ ayaafɔre̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱yaafɔre̱ mo̱ŋ nyi ke̱tɔ ne̱ mò̱ nyaŋpe̱ e̱ waa‑ɔ. Amaa ŋkee, mo̱ e̱ te̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ nyare̱‑ana, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ a nu le̱e̱ n se̱ ase̱‑ɔ gywii mo̱ne̱.

ref: You did not choose me. I chose you and sent you out to produce fruit, the kind of fruit that will last. Then my Father will give you whatever you ask for in my name.
text: N gye̱ mo̱ne̱ ya lee mo̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ kii mo̱ne̱ nyaŋpe̱. Amaa mo̱ ya te̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ bo̱ kii mo̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ, na n suŋ mo̱ne̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ ya swɛɛ agyi bwe̱e̱tɔ, agyi ne̱ a maa gyo‑o. Mo̱ne̱ ya waa amo̱‑ɔ, n se̱ e̱ sa mo̱ne̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ mo̱ ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: If you belonged to the world, its people would love you. But you don't belong to the world. I have chosen you to leave the world behind, and this is why its people hate you.
text: Mo̱ne̱ dɛɛ mo̱ne̱ tii kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ se̱, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ aa bamo̱ asuŋ gye̱ ako̱ŋko̱ ne̱e̱‑ɔ, weetee ba kpa mo̱ne̱ ase̱ŋ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱‑ɔ. Amaa mo̱ a te̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ le̱e̱ kaye̱ mɔ ase̱sɛ‑rɔ. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ mo̱ne̱ bo̱ kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ a ke̱be̱gya‑ɔ, ane̱ŋ se̱ ne̱ kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ i kisi mo̱ne̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: Remember how I told you that servants are not greater than their master. So if people mistreat me, they will mistreat you. If they do what I say, they will do what you say.
text: Mo̱nꞌ nyiŋŋi ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ne̱‑ɔ se̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱yaafɔre̱ ke̱maa mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ɔ kyɔ mò̱ nyaŋpe̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ane̱ŋ ne̱ bo̱ko̱ a gya mo̱ kanɔ se̱‑ɔ dɛɛ ne̱ ba gya mo̱ne̱ lee si. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ bo̱ko̱ mɔ a waa mo̱ awo̱re̱fɔɔ‑ɔ mɔ dɛɛ ne̱ ba waa mo̱ne̱ gbaa mo̱ne̱ awo̱re̱fɔɔ.

ref: People will do to you exactly what they did to me. They will do it because you belong to me, and they don't know the one who sent me.
text: Ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ ke̱be̱gya‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ se̱ ne̱ ba waa mo̱ne̱ amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi mò̱ ne̱ oo suŋ mo̱‑ɔ.

ref: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not be guilty of sin. But now they have no excuse for their sin.
text: N dɛɛ mo̱ŋ ba kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ bo̱ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ gywii kamo̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ ne̱e̱, weetee n se̱ maa bu bamo̱ bɔye̱ ne̱ ba waa‑ɔ ke̱pɔ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ a ba‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, bo̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ bo̱ de ke̱tɔ ne̱ ba ye̱re̱ se̱ a bo̱ bo̱ waa bɔye̱‑ɔ.

ref: Everyone who hates me also hates my Father.
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ o kisi mo̱‑ɔ, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ o kisi n se̱ gbaa.

ref: I have done things no one else has ever done. If they had not seen me do these things, they would not be guilty. But they did see me do these things, and they still hate me and my Father too.
text: N dɛɛ mo̱ŋ ba kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ bo̱ waa asuŋ ne̱ ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ waa amo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱, weetee n se̱ maa bu bamo̱ bɔye̱ ne̱ ba waa‑ɔ ke̱pɔ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ a du‑o, baa ŋu ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ a waa‑ɔ, ne̱ amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ gbaa bo̱ saŋ ba kisi mo̱ aa n se̱ pɛɛɛ.

ref: This is why the Scriptures are true when they say, “People hated me for no reason.”
text: Amo̱‑ɔ kyɔ a ba ane̱ŋ, na ke̱tɔ ne̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ nya ba kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ, ‘Kikisi ne̱ baa kisi mo̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ de kaase̱.’

ref: I will send you the Spirit who comes from the Father and shows what is true. The Spirit will help you and will tell you about me.
text: Ɔkyarɔpo̱ ne̱ ɔ le̱e̱ n se̱ ase̱‑ɔ e̱ ba. Mò̱ e̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o, kase̱ŋtiŋ wuye‑o. Mò̱ ya ba, mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ kya mo̱ne̱‑rɔ. Mo̱ e̱ sa a ɔ le̱e̱ n se̱ ase̱ ba mo̱ne̱ ase̱, na ɔ tɔwe̱ mo̱ ase̱ŋ gywii ase̱sɛ.

ref: Then you will also tell others about me, because you have been with me from the beginning.
text: Na mo̱ne̱ mɔ, mo̱nꞌ tɔwe̱ mo̱ ase̱ŋ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ kyɔ mo̱ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ ase̱ le̱e̱ ke̱gye̱ŋkpɛɛ‑ɔ‑rɔ.”

ref: I am telling you these things, so that you will not turn away.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya se̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ a tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ gywii mo̱ne̱ ne̱e̱ a mo̱ne̱ ma nya kiŋŋi kamɛɛ.

ref: You will be chased out of the synagogues. And the time will come when people will kill you and think they are doing God a favor.
text: Ba gya mo̱ne̱ a bo̱ le̱e̱ bamo̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ akyaŋ‑nɔ. Saŋ ko̱ e̱ ba a ɔ bo̱ ba, ne̱ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ mɔɔ mo̱ne̱‑ɔ e̱ fa fe̱yɛ, mò̱ a waa amo̱‑ɔ, ɔɔ waa ko̱tɔko̱ sa Wuribware̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: They will do these things because they don't know either the Father or me.
text: Ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ ba waa mo̱ne̱ amo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi n se̱, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi mo̱.

ref: I am saying this to you now, so that when the time comes, you will remember what I have said. The Work of the Holy Spirit I was with you at the first, and so I didn't tell you these things.
text: Amaa mo̱ a tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ gywii mo̱ne̱ ne̱e̱ a saŋ‑ɔ ya fo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ waa atɔ mɔ, na mo̱nꞌ nya nyiŋŋi si fe̱yɛ mo̱ a kyɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ne̱.” Asuŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o e̱ waa‑ɔ Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya se̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ a saŋ m bo̱ mo̱ne̱ ase̱‑ɔ se̱, ne̱ mo̱ŋ wuree tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ mɔ gywii mo̱ne̱.

ref: But now I am going back to the Father who sent me, and none of you asks me where I am going.
text: To, mbe̱yɔmɔ mo̱ e̱ yɔ mò̱ ne̱ oo suŋ mo̱‑ɔ ase̱ ne̱e̱. Amaa mo̱ne̱ ko̱ gbaa mo̱ŋ taare̱ bise mo̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mfe̱ne̱ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ yɔ?’

ref: You are very sad from hearing all of this.
text: Ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ fe̱raa ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ne̱‑ɔ, aa ko̱ŋ waa mo̱ne̱ e̱wɛɛ.

ref: But I tell you I am going to do what is best for you. This is why I am going away. The Holy Spirit cannot come to help you until I leave. But after I am gone, I will send the Spirit to you.
text: Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ, mo̱ ya yɔ, a baare a a sa mo̱ne̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, mo̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ yɔ, ɔkyarɔpo̱‑ɔ maa ba mo̱ne̱ ase̱. Amaa mo̱ ya yɔ fe̱raa, mo̱ e̱ ba mò̱ a m bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ mo̱ne̱.

ref: The Spirit will show them that they are wrong about sin, because they didn't have faith in me.
text: Ɔ kaapo̱ bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ baa waa bɔye̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ gyi.

ref: They are wrong about God's justice, because I am going to the Father, and you won't see me again.
text: Ɔ be̱e̱ ɔ kaapo̱ bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ mpo̱rɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ ya le̱e̱ mfe̱e̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ ŋu mo̱, a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ n se̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱ ne̱ mo̱ a yɔ.

ref: I have much more to say to you, but right now it would be more than you could understand.
text: N de ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ a n tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ne̱, amaa mbe̱yɔmɔ amo̱ kunu e̱ waa mo̱ne̱ ase̱ ko̱kyɔ.

ref: The Spirit shows what is true and will come and guide you into the full truth. The Spirit doesn't speak on his own. He will tell you only what he has heard from me, and he will let you know what is going to happen.
text: Amaa Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o fe̱raa, mò̱ a gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ wuye‑o si‑o, mò̱ ya ba, ɔ gye̱ mo̱ne̱ ŋkpɛɛ a mo̱nꞌ nu ke̱tɔ ke̱maa kaase̱. N gye̱ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ke̱yaale̱ŋ se̱ ne̱ ɔ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ a o gywii mo̱ne̱. Amaa ɔ tɔwe̱ amo̱ ne̱ oo nu‑o ne̱e̱, na ɔ tɔwe̱ atɔ ne̱ a ba a a bo̱ ba‑ɔ.

ref: The Spirit will bring glory to me by taking my message and telling it to you.
text: Ɔ sa mo̱ ke̱dabe̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ o nu mo̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ n de‑o, na ɔ tɔwe̱ amo̱ gywii mo̱ne̱.

ref: Everything the Father has is mine. This is why I have said that the Spirit takes my message and tells it to you. Sorrow Will Turn into Joy
text: Ase̱ŋ ne̱ n de‑o amo̱ dɛɛ ne̱ n se̱ mɔ de. Amo̱se̱ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o i nu mo̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ n de‑o, na ɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ne̱.” E̱wɛɛ na kakatɔgyi

ref: They said to each other, “What does Jesus mean by saying that for a little while we won't see him, but after a while we will see him? What does he mean by saying he is going to the Father?
text: Ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ a bise bamo̱ wo̱re̱ wo̱re̱ fe̱yɛ, “E̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ amo̱‑ɔ kaase̱ e̱ kaapo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱? Ɔ tɔwe̱ o gywii ane̱ fe̱yɛ, amo̱ ya waa kafwe̱e̱, ane̱ maa lɛɛ ŋu mò̱, amaa a be̱e̱ bo̱ waa kafwe̱e̱‑ɔ, ane̱ e̱ be̱e̱ ŋu mò̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ kpa a ŋ yɔ n se̱ ase̱ ne̱e̱.’

ref: What is this ‘little while’ that he is talking about? We don't know what he means.”
text: E̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ ‘a bo̱ waa kafwe̱e̱’ mɔ kaase̱ e̱ kaapo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱? Ane̱ mo̱ŋ nyi ke̱tɔ ne̱ amo̱‑ɔ e̱ kaapo̱‑ɔ!”

ref: Jesus knew they had some questions, so he said: You are wondering what I meant when I said that for a little while you won't see me, but after a while you will see me.
text: Yeesuu a pini fe̱yɛ ba kpa a bo̱ bise mò̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ŋ yɛ ɛɛɛ, ‘Amo̱ ya waa kafwe̱e̱, mo̱ne̱ maa lɛɛ ŋu mo̱,’ ne̱ m be̱e̱ ŋ yɛ ɛɛɛ, ‘Amaa a be̱e̱ bo̱ waa kafwe̱e̱‑ɔ, na mo̱nꞌ be̱e̱ ŋu mo̱.’ Amo̱‑ɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i bise abɛɛ aaa?

ref: I tell you for certain that you will cry and be sad, but the world will be happy. You will be sad, but later you will be happy.
text: Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ i su akyukyu na imee, amaa kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ fe̱raa, a waa bamo̱ kakatɔgyi. A waa mo̱ne̱ e̱wɛɛ, amaa mo̱ne̱ e̱wɛɛ‑ɔ i kyurowi na i kii kakatɔgyi.

ref: When a woman is about to give birth, she is in great pain. But after it is all over, she forgets the pain and is happy, because she has brought a child into the world.
text: Ɔkye̱e̱ e̱ kpa a ɔ ko̱we̱ kayaagyi, amɔ mò̱ ase̱ŋ a waa e̱wɛɛ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ awo̱re̱fɔɔ saŋ a fo̱. Amaa mò̱ ya ko̱we̱ kayaagyi‑o, amɔ ɔ taŋ mò̱ awo̱re̱fɔɔ se̱. Na mò̱ akatɔ gyi fe̱yɛ ɔɔ ko̱we̱ kayaagyi bo̱ waa kaye̱‑rɔ.

ref: You are now very sad. But later I will see you, and you will be so happy that no one will be able to change the way you feel.
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ a du mo̱ne̱ ase̱. Mbe̱yɔmɔ a maa waa mo̱ne̱ kɔne̱, amaa m be̱e̱ mo̱ i ŋu mo̱ne̱, na mo̱ne̱ akatɔ gyi kyo̱ŋ se̱, kakatɔgyi ne̱ ɔko̱ maa taare̱ a ɔ kɔɔre̱ le̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ ase̱‑ɔ.

ref: When that time comes, you won't have to ask me about anything. I tell you for certain the Father will give you whatever you ask for in my name.
text: Kake amo̱ ya fo̱, mo̱ne̱ maa bise mo̱ sɛye̱. Amaa mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ n se̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ sa mo̱ne̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ mò̱ mo̱ ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: You have not asked for anything in this way before, but now you must ask in my name. Then it will be given to you, so you will be completely happy.
text: Hare̱e̱ le̱e̱ saŋ‑ɔ bo̱ fo̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ, mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ ko̱re̱ ko̱tɔko̱ mo̱ ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ. Le̱e̱ ndɔɔ bo̱ yɔ, mo̱nꞌ ko̱re̱ na mo̱nꞌ nya, na mo̱ne̱ akatɔ nya gyi bwe̱e̱tɔ.” Yeesuu a kɔ gyi kaye̱‑rɔ ɔyaale̱ŋpo̱ se̱

ref: I have used examples to explain to you what I have been talking about. But the time will come when I will speak to you plainly about the Father and will no longer use examples like these.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya se̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mbe̱yɔmɔ fe̱raa akpare̱‑rɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ atɔ mɔ. Amaa saŋ ko̱ e̱ ba, ne̱ ma lɛɛ ba akpare̱ a m bo̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱. Saŋ amo̱ fe̱raa, mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ n se̱ ase̱ŋ a ŋ kaapo̱‑rɔ gywii mo̱ne̱.

ref: You will ask the Father in my name, and I won't have to ask him for you.
text: Kake amo̱ ya fo̱ fe̱raa, n gye̱ mo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ n se̱ mo̱ne̱ kuŋu si ko̱tɔko̱. Mo̱ne̱ fɔŋfɔŋ e̱ ko̱re̱ mò̱ mo̱ ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ,

ref: I came from the Father into the world, but I am leaving the world and returning to the Father.
text: Mo̱ a le̱e̱ n se̱ ase̱ ne̱e̱, ne̱ mo̱ a ba kaye̱‑rɔ. Ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ mo̱ e̱ le̱e̱ kaye̱‑rɔ a ŋ yɔ n se̱ ase̱.”

ref: The disciples said, “Now you are speaking plainly to us! You are not using examples.
text: Ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɛhɛɛɛŋ, ŋkee fe̱raa fo̱ a sa se̱ŋsa, na fo̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ fo̱ de akpare̱ fo̱ e̱ waa‑rɔ.

ref: At last we know that you understand everything, and we don't have any more questions. Now we believe you truly have come from God.”
text: Ane̱ nyi mbe̱yɔmɔ fe̱yɛ fo̱ nyi ke̱tɔ ke̱maa. Fo̱ nyi ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ bo̱ ane̱ mfɛɛre̱‑rɔ‑ɔ. Amo̱se̱, a mo̱ŋ lɛɛ a tiri fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ bise fo̱ se̱ŋbise ke̱maa. Amo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ a sa a ane̱ kɔɔre̱ gyi fe̱yɛ fo̱ a le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱ ne̱e̱.”

ref: Jesus replied: Do you really believe me?
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ mo̱ne̱ i gyi aaa?

ref: The time will come and is already here when all of you will be scattered. Each of you will go back home and leave me by myself. But the Father will be with me, and I won't be alone.
text: Saŋ e̱ ba, ɔ kyɔ ɔ fo̱ mfe̱e̱ gbaa, saŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ pɛɛɛ e̱ brawe̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, na mo̱nꞌ se̱re̱ yɔwe̱ mo̱ wo̱re̱ kpeŋ bo̱ sii mfe̱e̱. Na mo̱ne̱ ɔke̱maa se̱re̱ yɔ mò̱ aye̱. Amaa kase̱ŋtiŋ si, n gye̱ sa ne̱ mo̱ a saŋ mo̱ wo̱re̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ n se̱ bo̱ mo̱ ase̱.

ref: I have told you this, so that you might have peace in your hearts because of me. While you are in the world, you will have to suffer. But cheer up! I have defeated the world.
text: Mo̱ a tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ gywii mo̱ne̱ ne̱e̱ a mo̱nꞌ nya nya kaye̱e̱yuri ne̱ ka le̱e̱ mo̱ ase̱‑ɔ. Kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ e̱ sa a mo̱nꞌ kpo̱ne̱. Amaa mo̱nꞌ nya wɔre̱ saŋ ke̱maa, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a kɔ gyi Ɔbɔnsam, kaye̱‑rɔ a ɔyaale̱ŋpo̱‑ɔ, se̱.”

ref: After Jesus had finished speaking to his disciples, he looked up toward heaven and prayed: Father, the time has come for you to bring glory to your Son, in order that he may bring glory to you.
text: Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ de̱e̱re̱ Wuribware̱ se̱, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “N se̱, mo̱ saŋ‑ɔ a fo̱. Sa mo̱ ke̱dabe̱ na wuraa, na n sa a ase̱sɛ ŋu fo̱ ke̱dabe̱ na wuraa ne̱ fo̱ de‑o,

ref: And you gave him power over all people, so he would give eternal life to everyone you give him.
text: a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ ya sa mo̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ se̱, na ŋ nya taa ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ bo̱ sa bamo̱ pɛɛɛ ne̱ fo̱ a sa mo̱‑ɔ.

ref: I have brought glory to you here on earth by doing everything you gave me to do.
text: Mo̱ a lo̱we̱ kusuŋ ne̱ fo̱ a sa mo̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ waa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ a kaapo̱ fo̱ ke̱dabe̱ na wuraa kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ.

ref: Now, Father, give me back the glory I had with you before the world was created.
text: N se̱, sa a n kye̱na fo̱ ase̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ, na ŋ nya ke̱dabe̱ na wuraa ne̱ mo̱ aa fo̱ dɛɛ ane̱ de pwɛɛ ne̱ ane̱ a dɛɛ twe̱e̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑ɔ.

ref: You have given me some followers from this world, and I have shown them what you are like. They were yours, but you gave them to me, and they have obeyed you.
text: Mo̱ a sa ne̱ mo̱ agyase̱po̱ mɔ ne̱ fo̱ a lee le̱e̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ sa mo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a gyii fo̱. Bo̱ gye̱ fo̱ lee ne̱e̱, ne̱ fo̱ a taa bamo̱ sa mo̱. Ne̱ baa nu fo̱ kanɔ.

ref: They know that you gave me everything I have.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ fe̱raa, bo̱ nyi fe̱yɛ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ fo̱ a sa mo̱‑ɔ a le̱e̱ fo̱ ase̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: I told my followers what you told me, and they accepted it. They know I came from you, and they believe you are the one who sent me.
text: Ase̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ a bo̱ sa mo̱‑ɔ ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱, ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ amo̱ gyi. Bo̱ nyi fe̱yɛ kase̱ŋtiŋ si ne̱ mo̱ a le̱e̱ fo̱ ase̱ ba. Ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ gyi fe̱yɛ fo̱ ya suŋ mo̱.

ref: I am praying for them, but not for those who belong to this world. My followers belong to you, and I am praying for them.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ mo̱ e̱ sa bamo̱ ne̱e̱. N gye̱ kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ mo̱ e̱ sa mbe̱yɔmɔ, amaa mo̱ agyase̱po̱ mɔ ne̱ fo̱ a sa mo̱‑ɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ mo̱ e̱ sa.

ref: All I have is yours, and all you have is mine, and they will bring glory to me.
text: Se̱sɛ ke̱maa ne̱ m bo̱ mò̱‑ɔ gye̱ fo̱ se̱sɛ ne̱e̱, ne̱ se̱sɛ ke̱maa ne̱ fo̱ bo̱ mò̱‑ɔ mɔ gye̱ mo̱ se̱sɛ. Ne̱ mo̱ ke̱dabe̱ a bo̱rɔ bamo̱ se̱ le̱e̱ kawu.

ref: Holy Father, I am no longer in the world. I am coming to you, but my followers are still in the world. So keep them safe by the power of the name you have given me. Then they will be one with each other, just as you and I are one.
text: Ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ mo̱ e̱ ba fo̱ ase̱. Ma lɛɛ kye̱na kaye̱‑rɔ. Amaa bamo̱ fe̱raa, bo̱ te kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ne̱e̱. N se̱ kpe̱yaa, mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ fo̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ ba fo̱ ke̱nyare̱ ne̱ fo̱ a be̱e̱ sa mo̱‑ɔ ke̱yaale̱ŋ bo̱ kuŋ bamo̱, na bo̱ nya waa ko̱ko̱ŋko̱, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ aa fo̱ e̱ gye̱ ko̱ko̱ŋko̱‑ɔ.

ref: While I was with them, I kept them safe by the power you have given me. I guarded them, and not one of them was lost, except the one who had to be lost. This happened so that what the Scriptures say would come true.
text: Mo̱ a bo̱ bamo̱ ase̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ a ba ke̱nyare̱ amo̱ ne̱ fo̱ a sa mo̱‑ɔ ke̱yaale̱ŋ bo̱ kuŋ bamo̱. Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, bamo̱ ɔko̱ŋko̱ mo̱ŋ fo̱, amɔ odiburopo̱ ne̱ fo̱ a kyɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ fo̱‑ɔ, na ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ nya waa kase̱ŋtiŋ.

ref: I am on my way to you. But I say these things while I am still in the world, so my followers will have the same complete joy that I do.
text: Ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ mo̱ e̱ ba fo̱ ase̱ ne̱e̱. Mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ mɔ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ne̱e̱ na bo̱ nya nya mo̱ kakatɔgyi ne̱ n de‑o bamo̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: I have told them your message. But the people of this world hate them, because they don't belong to this world, just as I don't.
text: Mo̱ a tɔwe̱ fo̱ ase̱ŋ gywii bamo̱, ne̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ase̱sɛ a kisi bamo̱, bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ tii kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ŋ gbaa mo̱ŋ tii kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ.

ref: Father, I don't ask you to take my followers out of the world, but keep them safe from the evil one.
text: Ma ko̱re̱ fo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ lee bamo̱ le̱e̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ. Amaa ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ fo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ kuŋ bamo̱ na bo̱ ma lwee Ɔbɔnsam ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ.

ref: I am sending them into the world, just as you sent me.
text: Mo̱ a suŋ bamo̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ kaye̱‑rɔ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ fo̱ a suŋ mo̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ kaye̱‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: I have given myself completely for their sake, so they may belong completely to the truth.
text: Mo̱ a kperi bamo̱ se̱ taa mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ sa fo̱, na bamo̱‑ɔ bo̱ nya nya taa bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ sa fo̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ‑no saŋ ke̱maa.

ref: I am not praying just for these followers. I am also praying for everyone else who will have faith because of what my followers will say about me.
text: Ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ mo̱ e̱ sa bamo̱ ne̱ ba kɔɔre̱ mo̱ a bo̱ gyi bo̱ le̱e̱ mo̱ agyase̱po̱ mɔ abware̱se̱ŋ ko̱tɔwe̱‑ɔ se̱.

ref: I want all of them to be one with each other, just as I am one with you and you are one with me. I also want them to be one with us. Then the people of this world will believe that you sent me.
text: Mo̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ waa ko̱ko̱ŋko̱. Sa a mo̱ aa bamo̱ aa fo̱ waa ko̱ko̱ŋko̱. N se̱, sa a bo̱ waa ko̱ko̱ŋko̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ aa fo̱ gye̱ ko̱ko̱ŋko̱‑ɔ, na kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ nya kɔɔre̱ gyi fe̱yɛ fo̱ ya suŋ mo̱.

ref: I have honored my followers in the same way you honored me, in order that they may be one with each other, just as we are one.
text: Mo̱ a sa bamo̱ ke̱dabe̱ na wuraa ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱ fo̱ a sa mo̱‑ɔ dɛɛ, na bo̱ nya waa ko̱ko̱ŋko̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ aa fo̱ gye̱ ko̱ko̱ŋko̱‑ɔ.

ref: I am one with them, and you are one with me, so they may become completely one. Then this world's people will know that you sent me. They will know that you love my followers as much as you love me.
text: Na fo̱ sa a mo̱ aa bamo̱ a tii fo̱ se̱ waa ko̱ko̱ŋko̱ kpeŋ, na kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ nya ŋu fe̱yɛ fo̱ ya suŋ mo̱, ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kpa bamo̱ ase̱ŋ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kpa mo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ.

ref: Father, I want everyone you have given me to be with me, wherever I am. Then they will see the glory you have given me, because you loved me before the world was created.
text: N se̱, agyase̱po̱ ne̱ fo̱ a sa mo̱‑ɔ fe̱raa, ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ bo̱ ba mo̱ ase̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ yɔ‑ɔ, na bo̱ nya ŋu mo̱ ke̱dabe̱ na wuraa, ke̱dabe̱ na wuraa ne̱ fo̱ a sa mo̱‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ a kpa mo̱ ase̱ŋ pwɛɛ ne̱ ane̱ a dɛɛ twe̱e̱ kaye̱ mɔ.

ref: Good Father, the people of this world don't know you. But I know you, and my followers know that you sent me.
text: N se̱ timaa, kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ mo̱ŋ nyi fo̱, amaa ŋ nyi fo̱. Ne̱ mo̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ mɔ bo̱ nyi fe̱yɛ fo̱ ya suŋ mo̱.

ref: I told them what you are like, and I will tell them even more. Then the love you have for me will become part of them, and I will be one with them.
text: Mo̱ a sa ne̱ baa gyii fo̱. Amaa mo̱ e̱ kya se̱ a n sa a bo̱ gyii fo̱, na ke̱kpa ne̱ fo̱ de sa mo̱‑ɔ nya sii bamo̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ, na mo̱ aa bamo̱ waa ko̱ko̱ŋko̱.”

ref: One of the high priest's servants was there. He was a relative of the servant whose ear Peter had cut off, and he asked, “Didn't I see you in the garden with that man?”
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ a anya‑ɔ ɔko̱ ne̱ Peetroo a ba ke̱paŋ bo̱ ŋe mò̱ ke̱se̱bɔ te̱ŋ‑ɔ mò̱ nyipo̱ ko̱ a bise Peetroo fe̱yɛ, “Ne̱e̱ mo̱ a ŋu fo̱ bo̱ mò̱ ase̱ bane̱‑ɔ‑rɔ ɛ?”

ref: Jesus again asked, “Who are you looking for?” “We are looking for Jesus from Nazareth,” they answered.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ bise bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nsɛ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i buwi mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa?” Ne̱ bo̱ be̱e̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Yeesuu Nasarɛte̱nyi‑o.”

ref: This time Jesus replied, “I have already told you that I am Jesus. If I am the one you are looking for, let these others go.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ a kyɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ m bo̱ mfe̱e̱. Mo̱ne̱ i buwi mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa mo̱ fe̱raa, mo̱nꞌ sa a bo̱mo̱‑ɔ ne̱ bo̱ tii mo̱ se̱‑ɔ nare̱.”

ref: Then everything will happen, just as I said, ‘I did not lose anyone you gave me.’ ”
text: Ɔɔ tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱ a ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ kyɔ tɔwe̱‑ɔ nya waa kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ, “N se̱, mo̱ŋ sa ne̱ fo̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ fo̱ a sa mo̱‑ɔ ɔko̱ŋko̱ gbaa a fo̱.”

ref: Simon Peter had brought along a sword. He pulled it out and struck at Malchus, the servant of the high priest, cutting off his right ear.
text: Saŋ amo̱‑ɔ Simɔŋ Peetroo de ke̱paŋ, ne̱ oo gyiiri ke̱mo̱ le̱e̱ ke̱mo̱ ke̱gyafo̱‑rɔ bo̱ ŋe Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ a anya‑ɔ ɔko̱ kigyisese̱bɔ te̱ŋ. Ke̱nya‑ɔ ke̱nyare̱ e̱ gye̱ Malakus.

ref: The Roman officer and his men, together with the temple police, arrested Jesus and tied him up.
text: Ne̱ asoogyaa awuye‑o na bamo̱ ɔbre̱sɛ na Wuribware̱ suŋkpa a ade̱e̱re̱se̱po̱‑ɔ a kra Yeesuu, ne̱ baa ŋure mò̱.

ref: Peter stayed outside near the gate. But the other disciple came back out and spoke to the girl at the gate. She let Peter go in,
text: Peetroo a sii ɔ bo̱ lɔŋ‑ɔ kibunogyi‑ro‑o si‑o, mò̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ ɔgyase̱po̱ ne̱ o nyi ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ‑ɔ dame̱naŋsɛ‑ɔ a kiŋŋi yɔ kibunogyi‑o‑ro ya sa se̱ŋsa ko̱ gywii kakye̱e̱sɛɛ ne̱ ɔ ye̱re̱ kibunogyi‑o‑ro‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ Peetroo a lweero.

ref: but asked him, “Aren't you one of that man's followers?” “No, I am not!” Peter answered.
text: Kakye̱e̱sɛɛ ne̱ ɔ ye̱re̱ kibunogyi‑o‑ro‑o a bise Peetroo fe̱yɛ, “Ne̱e̱ fo̱ gye̱ Yeesuu mɔ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ ɔko̱ ne̱e̱ ɛ?” Ne̱ Peetroo a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Daabii! N gye̱ bamo̱ ɔko̱ e̱ gye̱ mo̱!”

ref: It was cold, and the servants and temple police had made a charcoal fire. They were warming themselves around it, when Peter went over and stood near the fire to warm himself. Jesus Is Questioned by the High Priest (Matthew
text: Saŋ amo̱‑ɔ afwii bo̱‑rɔ. Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ ayaafɔre̱ na ade̱e̱re̱se̱po̱‑ɔ a ba aduŋdurii bo̱ kure de̱e̱kpa, ne̱ baa kya muruwaa ba wo̱re̱, ne̱ Peetroo a yɔ bamo̱ ase̱ mfe̱ŋ ɔ wo̱re̱ de̱e̱kpa‑ɔ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Hanaa a bise Yeesuu ase̱ŋ‑ɔ

ref: The high priest questioned Jesus about his followers and his teaching.
text: Ne̱ Hanaa a bise Yeesuu mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ kuŋu si ase̱ŋ na mò̱ ke̱kaapo̱‑ɔ ase̱ŋ.

ref: Why are you questioning me? Why don't you ask the people who heard me? They know what I have said.”
text: Ne̱ e̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ fo̱ i bise mo̱ ane̱ŋ a se̱ŋbise mɔ? Bise ase̱sɛ ne̱ mo̱ a kaapo̱ bamo̱‑ɔ. Bise bamo̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱‑ɔ. Bo̱ nyi ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ.”

ref: Jesus was still tied up, and Annas sent him to Caiaphas the high priest. Peter Again Denies that He Knows Jesus (Matthew
text: Amo̱‑ɔ kamɛɛ‑rɔ, ne̱ Hanaa a sa ne̱ baa yaa Yeesuu ya bo̱ sa Kayafas, Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ‑ɔ, na agbarabi saŋ a ŋure mò̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Peetroo a be̱e̱ swe̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ mo̱ŋ nyi Yeesuu‑o (Matiyo

ref: It was early in the morning when Jesus was taken from Caiaphas to the building where the Roman governor stayed. But the crowd waited outside. Any of them who had gone inside would have become unclean and would not be allowed to eat the Passover meal.
text: Ne̱ baa lee Yeesuu le̱e̱ Kayafas ase̱ yaa Romanyi gominaa ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Pilat‑o leŋbu‑ro. Saŋ amo̱‑ɔ gye̱gyaye̱ kese‑ro ne̱e̱. Yudaa awuye abre̱sɛ mo̱ŋ kpa kulwee Romanyi leŋbu‑o‑ro a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ yɛ bamo̱ ya waa ane̱ŋ, iyisi e̱ kye̱na bamo̱ se̱, na bo̱ ma taare̱ nya kpa gyi Wuribware̱-a‑kya-ane̱-yɔwe̱ a ateese‑o.

ref: They answered, “He is a criminal! That's why we brought him to you.”
text: Ne̱ baa lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔ dɛɛ ɔ mo̱ŋ waa bɔye̱ ne̱e̱, weetee ane̱ maa baa mò̱ fo̱ ase̱.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Are you asking this on your own or did someone tell you about me?”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Se̱ŋbise mɔ a le̱e̱ fo̱ ase̱ ne̱e̱, bɛɛɛ bo̱ko̱ ya tɔwe̱ mo̱ ase̱ŋ gywii fo̱ aaa?”

ref: “You know I'm not a Jew!” Pilate said. “Your own people and the chief priests brought you to me. What have you done?”
text: Ne̱ gominaa‑o a be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ sa se̱ŋsa fo̱ i gywii mo̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a gye̱ Yudaanyi‑o? Fo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ase̱sɛ na fo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ ya taa fo̱ bo̱ waa mo̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ. Nte̱tɔ ne̱ fo̱ a waa?”

ref: Jesus answered, “My kingdom doesn't belong to this world. If it did, my followers would have fought to keep me from being handed over to our leaders. No, my kingdom doesn't belong to this world.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ gye̱ owure‑o, n gye̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ne̱ mo̱ i gyi kuwure. N dɛɛ mo̱ i gyi kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ kuwure ne̱e̱, weetee mo̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ awuye a kɔ bo̱ kuŋ mo̱ na m ma lwee Yudaa awuye abre̱sɛ‑ɔ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ. Daabii, kuwure ne̱ mo̱ i gyi‑o mo̱ŋ bo̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ mfe̱e̱.”

ref: “So you are a king,” Pilate replied. “You are saying that I am a king,” Jesus told him. “I was born into this world to tell about the truth. And everyone who belongs to the truth knows my voice.”
text: Ne̱ gominaa‑o a bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “To, ne̱e̱ fo̱ gye̱ owure ne̱e̱, bɛɛɛ?” Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ kuwure mo̱ŋ du fe̱yɛ kuwure ne̱ fo̱ i bise mo̱ ke̱mo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ. Ase̱ŋ ko̱ŋko̱ mɔ se̱ dɛɛ ne̱ baa ko̱we̱ mo̱ waa kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ na m bo̱ tɔwe̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o. Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ bo̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ a ke̱be̱gya‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ e̱ gya mo̱ kanɔ se̱.”

ref: Pilate asked Jesus, “What is truth?” Jesus Is Sentenced to Death (Matthew
text: Ne̱ gominaa‑o a bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ e̱ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ?” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa sure si fe̱yɛ bo̱ mɔɔ Yeesuu‑o (Matiyo

ref: And since I usually set a prisoner free for you at Passover, would you like for me to set free the king of the Jews?”
text: To, ane̱ de ananatɔ ko̱ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ waa ane̱ e̱ sa mo̱ne̱ Wuribware̱-a‑kya-ane̱-yɔwe̱ ateese kigyibe̱e̱ ke̱maa‑rɔ‑ɔ. Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ lee ase̱sɛ ne̱ ane̱ a tii bamo̱ tiikpa‑ɔ ɔko̱ bo̱ sa mo̱ne̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mbe̱yɔmɔ, mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ n lee mo̱ne̱ Yudaa awuye owure bo̱ sa mo̱ne̱ aaa?”

ref: Pilate gave orders for Jesus to be beaten with a whip.
text: Ne̱ Gominaa Pilat a sa ne̱ mò̱ asoogyaa awuye a da Yeesuu paara.

ref: The soldiers made a crown out of thorn branches and put it on Jesus. Then they put a purple robe on him.
text: Ne̱ asoogyaa awuye‑o a ba iwu bo̱ lo̱ kuwurepa bo̱ buŋ mò̱, ne̱ baa ba waagya pipee bo̱ buŋ mò̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ a gye̱ owure‑o.

ref: They came up to him and said, “Hey, you king of the Jews!” They also hit him with their fists.
text: Ne̱ ba ba mò̱ ase̱ ba tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Aŋsuma, nana, Yudaa awuye owure!” Ne̱ baa daye̱ mò̱ akatɔ‑rɔ abaa kpo̱ra‑rɔ.

ref: Once again Pilate went out. This time he said, “I will have Jesus brought out to you again. Then you can see for yourselves that I have not found him guilty.”
text: Ne̱ gominaa‑o a be̱e̱ le̱e̱ ya tɔwe̱ gywii lamaŋ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ laatɔ a mo̱nꞌ nu. Mo̱ i lee mò̱ a m baa mo̱ne̱ ase̱ kawu mfe̱e̱, na mo̱nꞌ nya ŋu fe̱yɛ ma taare̱ a ŋ nya ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ ye̱re̱ ke̱mo̱ se̱ a m bo̱ bu mò̱ ke̱pɔ‑ɔ.”

ref: Jesus came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe. Pilate said, “Here is the man!”
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ Yeesuu a le̱e̱ kawu na o buŋ iwu kuwurepa‑ɔ na waagya pipee‑o. Ne̱ gominaa‑o a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱ ɔnyare̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱!”

ref: When the chief priests and the temple police saw him, they yelled, “Nail him to a cross! Nail him to a cross!” Pilate told them, “You take him and nail him to a cross! I don't find him guilty of anything.”
text: Saŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ na Wuribware̱ suŋkpa ade̱e̱re̱se̱po̱‑ɔ a ŋu mò̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa fɛɛ‑rɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Da mò̱ bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱! Da mò̱ bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱!” Ne̱ gominaa‑o a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Amo̱ fe̱raa mo̱ne̱ fɔŋfɔŋ taa mò̱ ya da mò̱ bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱. M fe̱raa, ma taare̱ a ŋ ŋu ke̱tɔ bɔye̱ ne̱ ɔɔ waa, ne̱ mo̱ e̱ ye̱re̱ se̱ a m bo̱ bu mò̱ ke̱pɔ‑ɔ.”

ref: When Pilate heard this, he was terrified.
text: Gominaa‑o a nu baa tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ kufu ne̱ o de‑o a tii si.

ref: He went back inside and asked Jesus, “Where are you from?” But Jesus did not answer.
text: Ne̱ oo kiŋŋi yɔ leŋbu‑o‑ro ya bise Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Mfe̱ne̱ ne̱ fo̱ a le̱e̱?” Amaa Yeesuu mo̱ŋ lee kanɔ.

ref: “Why won't you answer my question?” Pilate asked. “Don't you know I have the power to let you go free or to nail you to a cross?”
text: Ne̱ gominaa‑o a be̱e̱ bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ maa be̱ŋŋaa mo̱ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱? Nyiŋŋi si fe̱yɛ n de ke̱yaale̱ŋ fe̱yɛ ŋ yɔwe̱ fo̱ a fo̱ ya kyure, ne̱ n de ke̱yaale̱ŋ fe̱yɛ n da fo̱ bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱.”

ref: Jesus replied, “If God had not given you the power, you couldn't do anything at all to me. But the one who handed me over to you did something even worse.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ de mo̱ se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ ya taa bo̱ sa fo̱. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, ɔnyare̱ ne̱ ɔɔ taa mo̱ bo̱ sa fo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔɔ waa bɔye̱ kyo̱ŋ fo̱.”

ref: Then Pilate wanted to set Jesus free. But the crowd again yelled, “If you set this man free, you are no friend of the Emperor! Anyone who claims to be a king is an enemy of the Emperor.”
text: Gominaa‑o a nu amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ le̱ŋ e̱ye̱e̱ ɔ kpa kpa ne̱ ɔ ye̱re̱ se̱ a ɔ bo̱ yɔwe̱ Yeesuu‑o. Amaa lamaŋ‑ɔ a fɛɛ‑rɔ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ ya yɔwe̱ ɔnyare̱ mɔ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ kwaa, amo̱ fe̱raa fo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ fo̱ owure dabe̱‑ɔ mò̱ nyare̱ ne̱e̱! Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ ba mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a ɔ bo̱ kii owure‑o gye̱ owure dabe̱‑ɔ do̱ŋ ne̱e̱!”

ref: When Pilate heard this, he brought Jesus out. Then he sat down on the judge's bench at the place known as “The Stone Pavement.” In Aramaic this pavement is called “Gabbatha.”
text: Gominaa‑o a nu amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ oo lee Yeesuu kawu, ne̱ ɔɔ kye̱na ɔse̱ŋgyipo̱ a kagya‑ɔ‑rɔ, mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Heebrii se̱ŋsa‑rɔ ba te̱e̱ ɛ Gabataa‑ɔ .

ref: It was about noon on the day before Passover, and Pilate said to the crowd, “Look at your king!”
text: Ifiyara amo̱, na kyɔwe̱ a yase̱ kafwe̱e̱. Ne̱ gominaa‑o a tɔwe̱ gywii lamaŋ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ owure!”

ref: Then Pilate handed Jesus over to be nailed to a cross. Jesus Is Nailed to a Cross (Matthew
text: Ne̱ ŋkee gominaa‑o a ba Yeesuu bo̱ sa mò̱ asoogyaa awuye fe̱yɛ bo̱ ya da mò̱ bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa da Yeesuu bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: The soldiers first broke the legs of the other two men who were nailed there.
text: Ne̱ asoogyaa awuye a ya gyɛɛ ɔnyare̱ gye̱ŋkpɛɛsɛ na ɔnyɔse̱po̱ ne̱ baa da bamo̱ aa Yeesuu bo̱ me̱ra ayii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ ayaa.

ref: So the chief priests went to Pilate and said, “Why did you write that he is King of the Jews? You should have written, ‘He claimed to be King of the Jews.’ ”
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ a ya tɔwe̱ gywii gominaa‑o fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ ma kyo̱rɛɛ fe̱yɛ, ‘Yudaa awuye owure‑o,’ amaa kyo̱rɛɛ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ɔnyare̱ mɔ yɛ, Mo̱ e̱ gye̱ Yudaa awuye owure‑o.’ ”

ref: When Jesus saw his mother and his favorite disciple with her, he said to his mother, “This man is now your son.”
text: Yeesuu a ŋu mò̱ nyi‑o na ɔgyase̱po̱ ne̱ Yeesuu e̱ kpa mò̱ ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ ye̱re̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ nyi‑o fe̱yɛ, “Ke̱e̱, fo̱ gyi‑o ne̱e̱.”

ref: and he carried his cross to a place known as “The Skull.” In Aramaic this place is called “Golgotha.”
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa taa Yeesuu nare̱, ne̱ ɔɔ ya so̱rɔ mò̱ kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ baa mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Heebrii se̱ŋsa‑rɔ ba te̱e̱ ɛ Gologotaa‑o .

ref: There Jesus was nailed to the cross, and on each side of him a man was also nailed to a cross.
text: Ne̱ baa da mò̱ bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱. Ne̱ baa da anyare̱ anyɔ ko̱ mɔ bo̱ me̱ra ayii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱, ɔko̱ŋko̱ bo̱ Yeesuu kigyise si, ne̱ ɔko̱ŋko̱ mɔ bo̱ mò̱ ke̱be̱na se̱.

ref: Pilate ordered the charge against Jesus to be written on a board and put above the cross. It read, “Jesus of Nazareth, King of the Jews.”
text: Ne̱ Gominaa Pilat a kyo̱rɛɛ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ ko̱wo̱re̱taa ko̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ, “Yeesuu Nasarɛte̱nyi, Yudaa awuye owure‑o,” ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ baa taa ke̱mo̱ bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱.

ref: The words were written in Hebrew, Latin, and Greek. The place where Jesus was taken wasn't far from the city, and many of the people read the charge against him.
text: Ane̱ŋ a kawo̱re̱taa‑ɔ, baa kyo̱rɛɛ ke̱mo̱ se̱ Heebrii na Roma na Griiki e̱se̱ŋsa‑rɔ ne̱e̱. Ne̱ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ a kare̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ baa da Yeesuu bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱‑ɔ na maŋ‑nɔ mo̱ŋ bo̱ e̱fɔ.

ref: But Pilate told them, “What is written will not be changed!”
text: Ne̱ gominaa a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ‑ɔ, ma lɛɛ kyurowi.”

ref: The soldiers said to each other, “Let's not rip it apart. We will gamble to see who gets it.” This happened so the Scriptures would come true, which say, “They divided up my clothes and gambled for my garments.” The soldiers then did what they had decided.
text: Ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ gywii abɛɛ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ma sa a ane̱ kyaŋŋe̱ kaare̱‑ɔ‑rɔ, mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ waa mpini mpini na mò̱ ne̱ o gyi si‑o na ka kii mò̱ lee.” Ane̱ŋ a ase̱ŋ mɔ a ba ne̱e̱ na ke̱tɔ ne̱ baa kyɔ kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ nya waa kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ, “Ne̱ baa waa mpini mpini bo̱ ke mo̱ atɔ buŋsɛ‑rɔ sa abɛɛ.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ asoogyaa awuye‑o a waa‑ɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: Jesus' mother stood beside his cross with her sister and Mary the wife of Clopas. Mary Magdalene was standing there too.
text: Yeesuu mò̱ nyi na mò̱ nyikaasɛ na Mariya ne̱ ɔ gye̱ Kilopas mò̱ ka‑ɔ na Mariya Magadalanyi ye̱re̱ bo̱ me̱raa mfe̱ŋ ne̱ baa da mò̱ bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ.

ref: But when they came to Jesus, they saw he was already dead, and they did not break his legs.
text: Amaa bamo̱ a ba Yeesuu ase̱‑ɔ, baa ŋu fe̱yɛ ɔɔ kyɔ wu, amo̱se̱‑ɔ bo̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ gyɛɛ mò̱ ayaa.

ref: In the place where Jesus had been nailed to a cross, there was a garden with a tomb that had never been used.
text: Baa gyi bane̱ bo̱ kuŋ ayii ko̱ me̱raa mfe̱ŋ ne̱ baa mɔɔ Yeesuu‑o. Bane̱ amo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ɔko̱ a ŋeri ke̱fo̱re̱ lee bɔ fe̱yɛ ke̱kyaŋ‑ɔ bo̱ waa ke̱gye̱raŋta po̱pwɛɛ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ pure ɔko̱ ke̱mo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: Jesus knew that he had now finished his work. And in order to make the Scriptures come true, he said, “I am thirsty!”
text: Yeesuu a ŋu fe̱yɛ mbe̱yɔmɔ fe̱raa ke̱tɔ ke̱maa a lo̱we̱‑ɔ, na ase̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyɔ kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ nya waa kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Burufo de mo̱.”

ref: A jar of cheap wine was there. Someone then soaked a sponge with the wine and held it up to Jesus' mouth on the stem of a hyssop plant.
text: Kalɔ na nta ko̱ ye̱re̱ mfe̱ŋ, ne̱ baa taa ikyikyee bo̱ da‑rɔ lee bo̱ kye̱na kayii si, yase̱ yaa mò̱ kanɔ ase̱.

ref: kilograms of spices made from myrrh and aloes. This was the same Nicodemus who had visited Jesus one night.
text: Yeesuu a kyo̱kywe̱e̱ nta‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Aa lo̱we̱.” Ne̱ ɔɔ gyaa kuŋu bo̱ twe̱e̱‑rɔ, ne̱ oo kii E̱bware̱ lee. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa yii Yeesuu ke̱kpa keri‑ro‑o

ref: The next day would be both a Sabbath and the Passover. It was a special day for the Jewish people, and they did not want the bodies to stay on the crosses during this day. So they asked Pilate to break the men's legs and take their bodies down.
text: Ne̱ Yudaa awuye abre̱sɛ‑ɔ a bo̱ ko̱re̱ Gominaa Pilat fe̱yɛ ɔ sa bamo̱ kpa a bo̱ ya gyɛɛ anyare̱ ne̱ baa da bamo̱ bo̱ me̱ra ayii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱‑ɔ ayaa, na bo̱ maye̱ bamo̱ afuniŋ‑o le̱e̱ ayii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱. Ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ baa ya ko̱re̱ kpa‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ kake nsi amo̱ gye̱ Ifiyara, ne̱ bo̱ maa kpa fe̱yɛ afuniŋ‑o kye̱na ayii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱ Me̱me̱ne̱da, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Me̱me̱ne̱da ne̱ ɔ ba‑ɔ gye̱ Me̱me̱ne̱da kukyure kake dabe̱.

ref: One of the soldiers stuck his spear into Jesus' side, and blood and water came out.
text: Amaa asoogyaa awuye‑o ɔko̱ a yii Yeesuu ke̱kpa keri‑ro. Puri amo̱‑rɔ, ne̱ mbo̱gya na nkyu a le̱e̱.

ref: We know this is true, because it was told by someone who saw it happen. Now you can have faith too.
text: Se̱sɛ ne̱ oo ŋu ase̱ŋ mɔ a waa‑ɔ e̱ tɔwe̱ amo̱ ase̱ŋ o gywii mo̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kare̱ wo̱re̱ mɔ‑ɔ, na mo̱ne̱ gbaa na mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱ kɔɔre̱ gyi. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱‑ɔ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱e̱.

ref: All this happened so that the Scriptures would come true, which say, “No bone of his body will be broken”
text: Amo̱‑ɔ a waa ne̱e̱ na ke̱tɔ ne̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ nya waa kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ, “Mò̱ kibowii ko̱ŋko̱ gbaa maa gyaa‑rɔ.”

ref: and “They will see the one in whose side they stuck a spear.” Jesus Is Buried (Matthew
text: Abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ a be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ to̱ŋ ko̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ase̱sɛ e̱ de̱e̱re̱ mo̱ ne̱ baa yii ke̱kpa‑ɔ se̱.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa pure Yeesuu‑o (Matiyo

ref: The tomb was nearby, and since it was the time to prepare for the Sabbath, they were in a hurry to put Jesus' body there.
text: Amo̱ a gye̱ fe̱yɛ kibugyii ya da, na bamo̱ kukyure kake dabe̱ fo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ane̱ŋ a ke̱gye̱raŋta‑ɔ maa mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa pure Yeesuu mfe̱ŋ.

ref: Joseph from Arimathea was one of Jesus' disciples. He had kept it secret though, because he was afraid of the Jewish leaders. But now he asked Pilate to let him have Jesus' body. Pilate gave him permission, and Joseph took it down from the cross.
text: Amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ kamɛɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Yo̱sɛf ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ Arimate̱ya maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ a ya ko̱re̱ Gominaa Pilat fe̱yɛ ɔ taa Yeesuu kifuniŋ sa mò̱. Ane̱ŋ a Yo̱sɛf mɔ gya Yeesuu si ɔ kwe̱e̱rɔ ne̱e̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ se̱re̱ Yudaa awuye abre̱sɛ‑ɔ. Ne̱ gominaa‑o a sa mò̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ ya taa Yeesuu kifuniŋ‑o nare̱. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ Yo̱sɛf a ya taa kifuniŋ‑o o yii.

ref: The two men wrapped the body in a linen cloth, together with the spices, which was how the Jewish people buried their dead.
text: Anyare̱ anyɔ‑ɔ a taa Yeesuu kifuniŋ‑o, ne̱ baa ba kyefuri bo̱ miri mò̱, ne̱ baa ba ndwii‑o na kakyanne̱‑ɔ bo̱ waa‑rɔ, ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yudaa awuye ananatɔ‑rɔ ba lɔŋŋɔ kifuniŋ pwɛɛ na bo̱ dɛɛ pure‑o.

ref: Thomas replied, “You are my Lord and my God!”
text: Ne̱ Tomas a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱ na Wuribware̱ e̱ gye̱ fo̱!”

ref: On Sunday morning while it was still dark, Mary Magdalene went to the tomb and saw that the stone had been rolled away from the entrance.
text: Kosiyara gye̱gyaye̱ kese‑ro‑o, kaye̱ a mo̱ŋ tɛɛ ke nɛɛnɛɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ Mariya Magadalanyi a yɔ ke̱gye̱raŋta‑ɔ se̱. Mò̱ a fo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ oo ŋu fe̱yɛ ke̱fo̱re̱ ne̱ baa kuroŋ bo̱ kpuse ke̱gye̱raŋta‑ɔ kanɔ‑ɔ se̱ fe̱yɛ po̱ne̱‑ɔ, baa kyɔ kuroŋ le̱e̱ ke̱gye̱raŋta‑ɔ kanɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: So the two of them went back to the other disciples. Jesus Appears to Mary Magdalene (Mark
text: Ne̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a kiŋŋi bo̱ yii pe̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a lee mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ Mariya Magadalanyi‑o (Maak

ref: The disciple who got there first then went into the tomb, and when he saw it, he believed.
text: Ne̱ ɔgyase̱po̱ ne̱ ɔɔ gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ fo̱ ke̱gye̱raŋta‑ɔ ase̱‑ɔ mɔ a lweero, ne̱ oo ŋu ke̱tɔ ke̱maa. Amo̱se̱ ne̱ ɔɔ kɔɔre̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Mariya a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱‑ɔ gyi.

ref: Although Thomas the Twin was one of the twelve disciples, he wasn't with the others when Jesus appeared to them.
text: Agyase̱po̱ kudu anyɔ‑ɔ ɔko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Tomas ne̱ ɔ gye̱ ke̱le̱ŋba‑ɔ mo̱ŋ bo̱ bamo̱ ase̱ nsaŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a ba‑ɔ.

ref: and saw two angels inside. They were dressed in white and were sitting where Jesus' body had been. One was at the head and the other was at the foot.
text: Ne̱ oo ŋu Wuribware̱ mbɔɔ ŋnyɔ ko̱ ne̱ bo̱ buŋ atɔ fufuri‑o te mfe̱ŋ ne̱ baa pure Yeesuu‑o, ɔko̱ŋko̱ bo̱ ayaa se̱, ne̱ ɔko̱ mɔ bo̱ aŋu si. Amaa saŋ amo̱, Yeesuu kifuniŋ‑o mo̱ŋ lɛɛ ɔ bo̱ ke̱gye̱raŋta‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: She ran to Simon Peter and to Jesus' favorite disciple and said, “They have taken the Lord from the tomb! We don't know where they have put him.”
text: Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ oo kiŋŋi ɔ se̱re̱ ɔ yɔ Simɔŋ Peetroo na ɔgyase̱po̱ ne̱ Yeesuu a kpa mò̱ ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ ase̱, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Baa taa ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ le̱e̱ ke̱gye̱raŋta‑ɔ‑rɔ, ane̱ mɔ ane̱ mo̱ŋ nyi mfe̱ŋ ne̱ baa taa mò̱ yaa bo̱ be̱ya‑ɔ.”

ref: Peter and the other disciple started for the tomb.
text: Amo̱se̱ ne̱ Peetroo na ɔgyase̱po̱ ko̱ amo̱‑ɔ a yɔ ke̱gye̱raŋta‑ɔ ase̱.

ref: They ran side by side, until the other disciple ran faster than Peter and got there first.
text: Agyase̱po̱ anyɔ mɔ se̱re̱ ne̱e̱, amaa ɔko̱ŋko̱‑ɔ se̱re̱ keŋkeŋ ɔ kyo̱ŋ Peetroo. Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ ɔɔ fo̱ ke̱gye̱raŋta‑ɔ ase̱ pwɛɛ ne̱ Peetroo a dɛɛ fo̱.

ref: He bent over and saw the strips of linen cloth lying inside the tomb, but he did not go in.
text: Mò̱ a fo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ oo buŋŋe de̱e̱re̱ ke̱gye̱raŋta‑ɔ‑rɔ ke̱e̱, ne̱ oo ŋu kyefuri ne̱ baa bo̱ miri Yeesuu kifuniŋ‑o da mfe̱ŋ, amaa ɔ mo̱ŋ lwee ke̱gye̱raŋta‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: When Simon Peter got there, he went into the tomb and saw the strips of cloth.
text: Ne̱ Simɔŋ Peetroo mɔ a sii kamɛɛ ba, ne̱ oo lwee ke̱gye̱raŋta‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ oo ŋu kyefuri‑o da mfe̱ŋ,

ref: He also saw the piece of cloth that had been used to cover Jesus' face. It was rolled up and in a place by itself.
text: na kaprakɛɛ ne̱ baa bo̱ ŋure Yeesuu kuŋu‑o. Kaprakɛɛ ne̱ baa bo̱ ŋure Yeesuu kuŋu‑o na kyefuri‑o mo̱ŋ da to̱ŋ ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱e̱. Kaprakɛɛ‑ɔ a bɔɔ ka da kamo̱ ko̱kwe̱e̱.

ref: Mary Magdalene stood crying outside the tomb. She was still weeping, when she stooped down
text: Mariya mɔ ye̱re̱ ke̱gye̱raŋta‑ɔ kawu si o su. Mò̱ a saŋ ɔ maa se̱ o su‑o, ne̱ oo buŋŋe de̱e̱re̱ ke̱gye̱raŋta‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: The angels asked Mary, “Why are you crying?” She answered, “They have taken away my Lord's body! I don't know where they have put him.”
text: Ne̱ baa bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔpe̱kye̱e̱, nte̱tɔ ne̱e̱ ne̱ fo̱ i su?” Ne̱ oo lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Baa taa mo̱ nyaŋpe̱ bo̱ de bo̱ yii, ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi mfe̱ŋ ne̱ baa taa mò̱ yaa bo̱ be̱ya‑ɔ.”

ref: Jesus asked her, “Why are you crying? Who are you looking for?” She thought he was the gardener and said, “Sir, if you have taken his body away, please tell me, so I can go and get him.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔpe̱kye̱e̱, nte̱tɔ ne̱e̱ ne̱ fo̱ i su? Nsɛ ne̱ fo̱ i buwi fo̱ e̱ kpa?” Ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ e̱ fa fe̱yɛ bane̱ amo̱ ɔde̱e̱re̱se̱po̱ ne̱e̱, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Bre̱sɛ, mfe̱ne̱ ne̱ fo̱ a taa mò̱ bo̱ be̱ya? Mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ fo̱ tɔwe̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ a taa mò̱ yaa bo̱ be̱ya‑ɔ, na ŋ nya ya taa mò̱.”

ref: Jesus told her, “Don't hold on to me! I have not yet gone to the Father. But tell my disciples I am going to the one who is my Father and my God, as well as your Father and your God.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ ma kaŋ kra mo̱‑rɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ yɔ so̱so̱ n se̱ ase̱. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, nare̱ mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana ase̱ ya tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ i kiŋŋi mo̱ e̱ yɔ mò̱ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ n se̱ na mo̱ nyaŋpe̱ Wuribware̱‑ɔ ase̱. Na fo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ ŋkee fe̱raa ɔ gye̱ bamo̱ se̱ na bamo̱ nyaŋpe̱ Wuribware̱ bo̱ tii si.”

ref: and showed them his hands and his side. When the disciples saw the Lord, they became very happy.
text: Mò̱ a tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ kyo̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ ba mò̱ asare̱e̱ na mò̱ keri‑ro bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱. Ne̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ akatɔ a gyi bwe̱e̱tɔ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ a ŋu Yeesuu.

ref: The disciples were afraid of the Jewish leaders, and on the evening of that same Sunday they locked themselves in a room. Suddenly, Jesus appeared in the middle of the group. He greeted them
text: Kake nsi amo̱ gye̱ Kosiyara. Amo̱ ke̱de̱e̱pwɛta‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a gyaŋŋe̱ twe̱e̱ ke̱kyaŋ‑nɔ bo̱ bo̱‑rɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kufu de bamo̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ Yudaa awuye abre̱sɛ‑ɔ se̱. Ne̱ Yeesuu a bo̱ ye̱re̱ bamo̱ nse̱na‑rɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ ka bamo̱ kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “E̱ye̱e̱ yure mo̱ne̱.”

ref: After Jesus had greeted them again, he said, “I am sending you, just as the Father has sent me.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “E̱ye̱e̱ yure mo̱ne̱. To, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ n se̱ a suŋ mo̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱‑ɔ, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ i suŋ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱.”

ref: Then he breathed on them and said, “Receive the Holy Spirit.
text: Ne̱ oo fwii ŋe waa bamo̱‑rɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o.

ref: So they told him, “We have seen the Lord!” But Thomas said, “First, I must see the nail scars in his hands and touch them with my finger. I must put my hand where the spear went into his side. I won't believe unless I do this!”
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ agyase̱po̱ ko̱‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ a ŋu ane̱ nyaŋpe̱.” Amaa Tomas a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Amɔ mo̱ a ŋu mfe̱ŋ ne̱ baa da mò̱ ndangyii asare̱e̱‑rɔ na n taa mo̱ asare̱e̱gyi bo̱ dabo̱rɔ mfe̱ŋ, na n neŋ mo̱ ke̱sare̱e̱ bo̱ waa mò̱ keri‑ro pwɛɛ na n dɛɛ kɔɔre̱ gyi.”

ref: A week later the disciples were together again. This time, Thomas was with them. Jesus came in while the doors were still locked and stood in the middle of the group. He greeted his disciples
text: Kadaawɔkywe̱e̱ be̱e̱ bo̱ fo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a be̱e̱ bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱ ke̱kyaŋ‑nɔ twe̱e̱ po̱ne̱‑rɔ. Ŋkee fe̱raa Tomas bo̱ bamo̱ ase̱. Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ bo̱ ye̱re̱ bamo̱ nse̱na‑rɔ ka bamo̱ kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “E̱ye̱e̱ yure mo̱ne̱.”

ref: and said to Thomas, “Put your finger here and look at my hands! Put your hand into my side. Stop doubting and have faith!”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii Tomas fe̱yɛ, “Taa fo̱ ke̱sare̱e̱gyi bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ se̱ mfe̱e̱, na fo̱ de̱e̱re̱ mo̱ asare̱e̱‑rɔ. Na fo̱ neŋ fo̱ ke̱sare̱e̱ bo̱ waa mo̱ keri‑ro, na fo̱ yɔwe̱ fo̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi ke̱‑mo̱ŋ‑de‑o, na fo̱ kɔɔre̱ gyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ a kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro.”

ref: Jesus said, “Thomas, do you have faith because you have seen me? The people who have faith in me without seeing me are the ones who are really blessed!” Why John Wrote His Book
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ a ŋu mo̱‑ɔ se̱ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ fo̱ i gyi aaa? Bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ ke̱e̱ mo̱, ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ gyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ a kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro‑o de ŋyure.” Ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ Yohanee a kyo̱rɛɛ ane̱ŋ a wo̱re̱ mɔ‑ɔ

ref: Jesus worked many other miracles for his disciples, and not all of them are written in this book.
text: Yeesuu a waa akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ ko̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ akatɔ‑rɔ ne̱ mo̱ŋ kyo̱rɛɛ waa wo̱re̱ mɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: But these are written so that you will put your faith in Jesus as the Messiah and the Son of God. If you have faith in him, you will have true life.
text: Amaa mo̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ amo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱ a mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱ kɔɔre̱ gyi fe̱yɛ Yeesuu e̱ gye̱ Kristoo‑o, Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑o, na bo̱ le̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi si na mò̱ ke̱nyare̱ na ke̱yaale̱ŋ se̱, mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱ nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: Simon Peter, Thomas the Twin, Nathanael from Cana in Galilee, and the brothers James and John, were there, together with two other disciples.
text: Bamo̱ ne̱ baa gyaŋŋe̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ Simɔŋ Peetroo, na Tomas ne̱ ɔ gye̱ ke̱le̱ŋba‑ɔ, na Nataniyɛl ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ Kana maŋ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, na Sibidii mò̱ gyi‑ana anyɔ‑ɔ, na Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ‑rɔ anyɔ ko̱.

ref: Simon Peter said, “I'm going fishing!” The others said, “We will go with you.” They went out in their boat. But they didn't catch a thing that night.
text: Ne̱ Simɔŋ Peetroo a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ ya kra ŋkiŋgyi ne̱e̱.” Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Ane̱ aa fo̱ e̱ yɔ.” Ne̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ a kye̱na ko̱re̱e̱‑rɔ yɔ. Ne̱ kibugyii a da, ne̱ baa twe̱e̱ kisawu ane̱ŋ‑aaa. Ne̱ kaye̱ a bo̱ ke na bo̱ mo̱ŋ kra sɛye̱.

ref: Early the next morning Jesus stood on the shore, but the disciples did not realize who he was.
text: Saŋ ne̱ kyɔwe̱ e̱ le̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu ye̱re̱ kyukɛɛ, amaa agyase̱po̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ mò̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: So he told them, “Let your net down on the right side of your boat, and you will catch some fish.” They did, and the net was so full of fish that they could not drag it up into the boat.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ya twe̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ kisawu‑o yaa ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ kigyise si a keri‑o, mo̱ne̱ e̱ kra ŋkiŋgyi.” Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa twe̱e̱ kisawu‑o bo̱ waa‑rɔ, ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ taare̱ gyiiri lee, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ baa kra ŋkiŋgyi bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: Jesus' favorite disciple told Peter, “It's the Lord!” When Simon heard it was the Lord, he put on the clothes he had taken off while he was working. Then he jumped into the water.
text: Ne̱ ɔgyase̱po̱ ne̱ Yeesuu e̱ kpa mò̱ ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ gywii Peetroo fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ ne̱e̱!” Peetroo a nu fe̱yɛ bamo̱ nyaŋpe̱ ne̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ oo kpuriti waagya laare̱‑rɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔɔ kyɔ lee mò̱ atɔ buŋsɛ le̱e̱ mò̱ kayo̱wɔre̱ se̱ ne̱e̱, ne̱ oo fuwi lwee nkyu‑o‑ro le̱e̱ ɔ twɛɛ ɔ yɔ kideŋbe si.

ref: When the disciples got out of the boat, they saw some bread and a charcoal fire with fish on it.
text: Bamo̱ a fo̱ kideŋbe si‑o, ne̱ baa ŋu aduŋdurii de̱e̱kpa na ŋkiŋgyi tɔ‑rɔ, na bodobodoo.

ref: Jesus told his disciples, “Bring some of the fish you just caught.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ taa ŋkiŋgyi ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a de̱ŋ kra‑ɔ ŋko̱ baa.”

ref: Jesus said, “Come and eat!” But none of the disciples dared ask who he was. They knew he was the Lord.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ bo̱ gyi.” Amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ gbaa agyase̱po̱‑ɔ ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ nsɛ ne̱e̱?”, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ nyi fe̱yɛ bamo̱ nyaŋpe̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: Jesus took the bread in his hands and gave some of it to his disciples. He did the same with the fish.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a taa bodobodoo‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ sa bamo̱, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ɔɔ waa ŋkiŋgyi‑o gbaa.

ref: This was the third time Jesus appeared to his disciples after he was raised from death. Jesus and Peter
text: Mò̱ a kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro‑o kamɛɛ‑rɔ, ke̱mo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ sase̱po̱ fe̱yɛ Yeesuu a lee mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii Peetroo‑o

ref: When Jesus and his disciples had finished eating, he asked, “Simon son of John, do you love me more than the others do?” Simon Peter answered, “Yes, Lord, you know I do!” “Then feed my lambs,” Jesus said.
text: Bamo̱ a gyi lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii Simɔŋ Peetroo fe̱yɛ, “Yohanee mò̱ gyi Simɔŋ, fo̱ e̱ kpa mo̱ ase̱ŋ fo̱ kyɔ ane̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana mɔ e̱ kpa mo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ ɔɔɔ?” Ne̱ oo lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, fo̱ nyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ kpa fo̱ ase̱ŋ ɛ.” Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “De̱e̱re̱ mo̱ nsanne̱gyii‑o si.”

ref: Jesus asked a second time, “Simon son of John, do you love me?” Peter answered, “Yes, Lord, you know I love you!” “Then take care of my sheep,” Jesus told him.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ nyɔse̱po̱ fe̱yɛ, “Yohanee mò̱ gyi Simɔŋ, fo̱ e̱ kpa mo̱ ase̱ŋ aaa?” Ne̱ Peetroo a be̱e̱ lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, fo̱ nyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ kpa fo̱ ase̱ŋ ɛ.” Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “De̱e̱re̱ mo̱ e̱sanne̱‑ɔ se̱.”

ref: Jesus asked a third time, “Simon son of John, do you love me?” Peter was hurt because Jesus had asked him three times if he loved him. So he told Jesus, “Lord, you know everything. You know I love you.” Jesus replied, “Feed my sheep.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ sase̱po̱ fe̱yɛ, “Yohanee mò̱ gyi Simɔŋ, kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kpa mo̱ ase̱ŋ aaa?” A mo̱ŋ waa Peetroo kɔne̱ fe̱yɛ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ bise mò̱ sase̱po̱. Ane̱ŋ se̱ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, fo̱ kyɔ fo̱ nyi ke̱tɔ ke̱maa. Fo̱ nyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ kpa fo̱ ase̱ŋ.” Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “De̱e̱re̱ mo̱ e̱sanne̱‑ɔ se̱.
