text: Mo̱ i gyi fo̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ, saŋ ne̱ fo̱ gye̱ kayaafɔre̱gyii‑o fo̱ a nyaa fo̱ ŋkpaŋkaare̱e̱ ba fe bo̱ te̱ŋ se̱ yɔ to̱ŋ ke̱maa ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ. Amaa fo̱ ya kii ke̱nyaŋbre̱sɛ, fo̱ i teyi asare̱e̱‑rɔ, na ɔko̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ ba fe a ɔ bo̱ ŋure fo̱, na ɔ yaa fo̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ maa kpa‑ɔ.”

ref: Jesus said this to tell how Peter would die and bring honor to God. Then he said to Peter, “Follow me!” Jesus and His Favorite Disciple
text: Ane̱ŋ a ase̱ŋ mɔ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱‑ɔ, Yeesuu e̱ kpa a ɔ kaapo̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Peetroo e̱ ba a ɔ bo̱ wu, na a sa a Wuribware̱ nya ke̱dabe̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱. Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Gya mo̱ se̱.” Ke̱tɔ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱ e̱ waa Yohanee‑o

text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ kye̱na bo̱ fo̱ saŋ ne̱ mo̱ i kiŋŋi a m ba‑ɔ gbaa, a mo̱ŋ gye̱ fo̱ ase̱ŋ. Fo̱ fe̱raa, gya mo̱ se̱ de.”

ref: So the rumor spread among the other disciples that this disciple would not die. But Jesus did not say he would not die. He simply said, “What is it to you, if I want him to live until I return?”
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ase̱ŋ amo̱ a saŋŋe̱‑rɔ yɔ Kristoo awuye ase̱ fe̱yɛ ɔgyase̱po̱ amo̱ maa wu. Amaa Yeesuu mo̱ŋ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ɔgyase̱po̱‑ɔ maa wu. Ɔɔ tɔwe̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ kye̱na bo̱ fo̱ saŋ ne̱ mo̱ i kiŋŋi a m ba‑ɔ gbaa, a mo̱ŋ gye̱ fo̱ ase̱ŋ.”

ref: This disciple is the one who told all of this. He wrote it, and we know he is telling the truth.
text: Ɔgyase̱po̱ amo̱ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ dɛɛ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ oo ŋu amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ ne̱ ɔɔ kyo̱rɛɛ bo̱ be̱ya‑ɔ. Ne̱ ane̱ nyi fe̱yɛ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱‑ɔ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ. Ase̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a waa bo̱ gye̱ kɛɛ‑ɔ

ref: Jesus did many other things. If they were all written in books, I don't suppose there would be room enough in the whole world for all the books.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ, ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ Yeesuu a waa bo̱ tii si. Baa dɛɛ kyo̱rɛɛ amo̱ pɛɛɛ bo̱ be̱ya ako̱ ako̱ ne̱e̱‑ɔ, weetee mo̱ e̱ fa fe̱yɛ amo̱ e̱wo̱re̱ ne̱ ba kyo̱rɛɛ‑ɔ maa lɔɔ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ gbaa.

ref: While he was still with them, he said: Don't leave Jerusalem yet. Wait here for the Father to give you the Holy Spirit, just as I told you he has promised to do.
text: Saŋ amo̱‑rɔ ne̱ mò̱ aa bamo̱ a be̱e̱ ba gyi ateese saŋ ko̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ma kaŋ le̱e̱ Yɛro̱salɛm mfe̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ bo̱‑ɔ. Amaa mo̱nꞌ gywii Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa ne̱ n se̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ ɔ sa mo̱ne̱‑ɔ.

text: Nkyu ne̱ Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱‑ɔ a bo̱ bɔ ase̱sɛ asuu. Amaa ŋke mfe̱ne̱ mɔ‑rɔ Wuribware̱ e̱ sa a mò̱ kufwiiŋe timaa‑o ywe̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ se̱, na ɔ ba kufwiiŋe timaa amo̱ bo̱ gyee mo̱ne̱.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu yii so̱so̱‑ɔ

text: Yeesuu a de̱ŋ tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ o yii Wuribware̱ se̱, na mò̱ ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ ye̱re̱ ba de̱e̱re̱, ne̱ ko̱wo̱re̱ ko̱ a bo̱ mwiire mò̱ se̱, ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ bo̱ ke̱e̱ mò̱.

ref: but as he went up, they kept looking up into the sky. Suddenly two men dressed in white clothes were standing there beside them.
text: Bamo̱ a saŋ bo̱ ye̱re̱ ba de̱e̱re̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ, na Yeesuu mɔ e̱ yɔ, ne̱ puri amo̱‑rɔ anyare̱ anyɔ ko̱ mɔ ne̱ bo̱ buŋ atɔ fufuri‑o a le̱e̱ bamo̱ se̱.

ref: They said, “Why are you men from Galilee standing here and looking up into the sky? Jesus has been taken to heaven. But he will come back in the same way you have seen him go.” Someone To Take the Place of Judas
text: Ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ gywii ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ awuye, nte̱tɔ ne̱e̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ ye̱re̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ de̱e̱re̱ awo̱re̱‑rɔ? Yeesuu mɔ ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ ase̱ o yii Wuribware̱ ase̱‑ɔ e̱ be̱e̱ kiŋŋi a ɔ ba fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ mɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a ŋu mò̱ na ɔ yɔ Wuribware̱ se̱‑ɔ mɔ dɛɛ.” Ɔko̱ ne̱ oo teere Yudas ayaa‑rɔ‑ɔ

ref: The Mount of Olives was about a kilometer from Jerusalem. The apostles who had gone there were Peter, John, James, Andrew, Philip, Thomas, Bartholomew, Matthew, James the son of Alphaeus, Simon, known as the Eager One, and Judas the son of James. After the apostles returned to the city, they went upstairs to the room where they had been staying.
text: Bamo̱ pɛɛɛ, Peetroo, na Yohanee, na Yakubu, na Andruu, na Filipo, na Tomas, na Bar‑Tolomiyu, na Matiyo, na Alafiyus mò̱ gyi Yakubu, na Simɔŋ Sɛlɔt, na Yakubu mò̱ gyi Yudas, a fo̱ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ baa ya lwee bamo̱ abaŋso̱ro̱o̱‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: The apostles often met together and prayed with a single purpose in mind. The women and Mary the mother of Jesus would meet with them, and so would his brothers.
text: Ke̱kyaŋ mɔ‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ bamo̱ aa akye̱e̱ ne̱ bo̱ dɛɛ bo̱ gya Yeesuu si‑o na Yeesuu mò̱ nyi Mariya na mò̱ tire‑ana e̱ gyaŋŋe̱ saŋ ke̱maa ba ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱.

ref: Then Judas bought some land with the money he was given for doing that evil thing. He fell headfirst into the field. His body burst open, and all his insides came out.
text: (Fo̱ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kare̱ wo̱re̱ mɔ‑ɔ, nyiŋŋi si fe̱yɛ Yudas a ba atanne̱ ne̱ oo gyi Yeesuu kidiburo‑o nya‑ɔ bo̱ sɔɔ swe̱e̱re̱ sa mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱. Swe̱e̱re̱ amo̱ se̱ dɛɛ ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ da, ne̱ mò̱ amerotɔ pɛɛɛ a le̱e̱.

ref: When the people of Jerusalem found out about this, they called the place Akeldama, which in the local language means “Field of Blood.”
text: Ne̱ Yɛro̱salɛm awuye a nu ase̱ŋ amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa sa swe̱e̱re̱ amo̱ mfe̱ŋ ke̱nyare̱ bamo̱ aye̱‑rɔ fe̱yɛ Akɛlɛdama, kaase̱ e̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ Mbo̱gya swe̱e̱re̱.)

ref: In the book of Psalms it says, “Leave his house empty, and don't let anyone live there.” It also says, “Let someone else have his job.”
text: To, ne̱ Peetroo a kya se̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “A bo̱ Deefid iliŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱nꞌ sa a mò̱ aye̱ kii ke̱taakpaŋ, na se̱sɛ ma kye̱na mfe̱ŋ.’ Ne̱ a be̱e̱ a bo̱‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱nꞌ sa a ɔko̱ ye̱re̱ mò̱ ayaa‑rɔ, na se̱sɛ‑ɔ tii ane̱ se̱ waa ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ kusuŋ.’

ref: So we need someone else to help us tell others that Jesus has been raised from death. He must also be one of the men who was with us from the very beginning. He must have been with us from the time the Lord Jesus was baptized by John until the day he was taken to heaven.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ lee ɔko̱ na ɔ bo̱ tii ane̱ se̱, na mò̱ gbaa tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro‑o ase̱ŋ. Se̱sɛ amo̱ waa ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ gya ane̱ aa ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu si saŋ ne̱ ɔ naa ɔ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ kaye̱‑rɔ, le̱e̱ hare̱e̱ saŋ ne̱ Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱‑ɔ a bɔ ase̱sɛ asuu‑o bo̱ fo̱ kake ne̱ Yeesuu yii Wuribware̱ se̱‑ɔ.”

ref: Two men were suggested: One of them was Joseph Barsabbas, known as Justus, and the other was Matthias.
text: Peetroo a sa se̱ŋsa lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa lee abɛɛ anyɔ. Bamo̱ e̱ gye̱ Yo̱sɛf ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Bar‑Sabas , na ɔko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Matiyas‑ɔ.

ref: They drew names, and Matthias was chosen to join the group of the eleven apostles.
text: Ne̱ baa lee ŋyii na bo̱ lee abɛɛ anyɔ amo̱ ɔko̱. Mò̱ ne̱ baa lee‑o e̱ gye̱ Matiyas. Ne̱ oo tii ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱ kudu kako̱‑ɔ se̱.

ref: On the day of Pentecost all the Lord's followers were together in one place.
text: Saŋ ko̱, ne̱ kasu kanɔ kake ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ Pentikos‑o† a fo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi‑o a gyaŋŋe̱ to̱ŋ ko̱ŋko̱.

ref: Suddenly there was a noise from heaven like the sound of a mighty wind! It filled the house where they were meeting.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ baa nu e̱lawo̱ ko̱ a le̱e̱ so̱so̱ na i su fe̱yɛ afwii dabe̱‑ɔ, ne̱ e̱ ya bo̱rɔ lɔŋ ne̱ baa gyaŋŋe̱ bo̱ te mò̱‑rɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: The Holy Spirit took control of everyone, and they began speaking whatever languages the Spirit let them speak.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a ywe̱e̱ ɔke̱maa se̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ baa le̱e̱ ba sa e̱se̱ŋsa baŋbaŋ ne̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ bamo̱ aye̱‑rɔ se̱ŋsa‑ɔ.

ref: Many religious Jews from every country in the world were living in Jerusalem.
text: Saŋ‑ɔ mɔ, Yudaa awuye bwe̱e̱tɔ ko̱ mɔ ne̱ baa tu yɔ bo̱ te kaye̱‑rɔ swe̱e̱re̱ pɛɛɛ se̱‑ɔ a be̱e̱ ba bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ.

ref: They were excited and amazed, and said: Don't all these who are speaking come from Galilee?
text: Bo̱ mo̱ŋ nu ke̱tɔ ne̱ kaa ba‑ɔ kaase̱, ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Ne̱e̱ ase̱sɛ mɔ ne̱ ba sa se̱ŋsa mfaanɛɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ gye̱ Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ awuye ne̱e̱ ɛ?

ref: Some of us are from Parthia, Media, and Elam. Others are from Mesopotamia, Judea, Cappadocia, Pontus, Asia,
text: Ane̱ bo̱ko̱ a le̱e̱ Paatiya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱, bo̱ko̱ mɔ a le̱e̱ Mɛde̱ya, bo̱ko̱ mɔ Ilam. Ne̱ bo̱ko̱ mɔ a le̱e̱ Mesopotamiya, na Yude̱ya, na Kapadokiya, na Pontus, na Asiya e̱swe̱e̱re̱ se̱.

ref: Crete, and Arabia. Some of us were born Jews, and others of us have chosen to be Jews. Yet we all hear them using our own languages to tell the wonderful things God has done.
text: Ane̱ pɛɛɛ gye̱ Yudaa awuye bɛɛɛ ase̱sɛ laŋgyaŋ ne̱ ane̱ gya Yudaa awuye ke̱bware̱suŋ a e̱kpa‑ɔ se̱. Ne̱ bo̱ko̱ mɔ a le̱e̱ Kriita kwii si na Arabiya e̱swe̱e̱re̱ se̱. Amaa ane̱ mɔ i nu bamo̱ a de ane̱ ɔke̱maa swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ a se̱ŋsa‑ɔ ba tɔwe̱ atɔ dabe̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a waa‑ɔ!”

ref: Everyone was excited and confused. Some of them even kept asking each other, “What does all this mean?”
text: Ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ bamo̱, ne̱ bamo̱ mfɛɛre̱ a waa ko̱kyɔ, ne̱ baa bise abɛɛ fe̱yɛ, “Amo̱‑ɔ kaase̱ e̱ gye̱ e̱me̱ne̱?”

ref: Others made fun of the Lord's followers and said, “They are drunk.” Peter Speaks to the Crowd
text: Amaa bo̱ko̱ fe̱raa e̱ mɔse̱ bamo̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o te bamo̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱, na ba tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ase̱sɛ mɔ pɛɛɛ a bwe̱e̱ ne̱e̱!” Peetroo abware̱se̱ŋ ko̱tɔwe̱

ref: Peter stood with the eleven apostles and spoke in a loud and clear voice to the crowd: Friends and everyone else living in Jerusalem, listen carefully to what I have to say!
text: Ne̱ ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱ kudu anyɔ‑ɔ a ko̱so̱ ye̱re̱. Ne̱ Peetroo a le̱e̱ bamo̱ ya bo̱ ye̱re̱ akatɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ sa se̱ŋsa gywii ase̱sɛ ne̱ baa gyaŋŋe̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ yase̱ mò̱ bo̱re̱‑ɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “M bɛɛko̱‑ana Yudaa awuye na ɔke̱maa ne̱ o te Yɛro̱salɛm mfe̱e̱‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ lɔŋ e̱se̱bɔ na mo̱nꞌ nu ke̱tɔ ne̱ amo̱‑ɔ ne̱ aa ba‑ɔ e̱ kaapo̱‑ɔ.

ref: You are wrong to think that these people are drunk. After all, it is only nine o'clock in the morning.
text: Ase̱sɛ mɔ, n gye̱ sa ne̱ baa bwe̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ fa‑ɔ. Mbe̱yɔmɔ saŋ a gye̱ gye̱gyaye̱ ne̱e̱, kyɔwe̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ daŋ.

ref: But this is what God told the prophet Joel to say,
text: Na amo̱‑ɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ Yo̱wɛl a tɔwe̱ amo̱ ase̱ŋ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: “When the last days come, I will give my Spirit to everyone. Your sons and daughters will prophesy. Your young men will see visions, and your old men will have dreams.
text: Ɔ yɛ, ‘Wuribware̱ yɛ, Mfaanɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa ŋke lalalo̱we̱‑ɔ‑rɔ. Mo̱ e̱ sa a mo̱ kufwiiŋe‑o bo̱ ywe̱e̱ ɔke̱maa se̱. Mo̱ne̱ gyi‑ana, akye̱e̱ na anyare̱ pɛɛɛ, e̱ tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ i lee a m bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱‑ɔ, na mo̱ne̱ ayaafɔre̱ ŋu mo̱ atɔ idee‑ro, na mo̱ne̱ anyaŋbre̱sɛ ku mo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ idee.

ref: In those days I will give my Spirit to my servants, both men and women, and they will prophesy.
text: Ŋ yɛ, ŋke lalalo̱we̱ amo̱‑rɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ sa a mo̱ kufwiiŋe‑o bo̱ ywe̱e̱ anya gbaa se̱, akye̱e̱ na anyare̱ pɛɛɛ, na bo̱ tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ i lee a m bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱‑ɔ.

ref: “I will work miracles in the sky above and wonders on the earth below. There will be blood and fire and clouds of smoke.
text: Mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ awo̱re̱‑rɔ na swe̱e̱re̱ se̱. Mo̱ne̱ i ŋu mbo̱gya na de̱e̱kpa na e̱bɔɔre̱e̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: The sun will turn dark, and the moon will be as red as blood before the great and wonderful day of the Lord appears.
text: Kibugyii e̱ da mpase̱, na kiferi pee fe̱yɛ mbo̱gya‑ɔ, pwɛɛ na kake dabe̱ dabe̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ ba‑ɔ dɛɛ fo̱.

ref: Then the Lord will save everyone who asks for his help.”
text: Na saŋ amo̱, m mo̱rɔwe̱ ɔke̱maa ne̱ o su a ɔ te̱e̱ mo̱‑ɔ.’ ”

ref: God had already planned and decided that Jesus would be handed over to you. So you took him and had evil men put him to death on a cross.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kyɔ kra bo̱ ye̱re̱ kyee fe̱yɛ ba taa Yeesuu a bo̱ sa mo̱ne̱, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a sa ne̱ abo̱ro̱kɔɔne̱po̱ a da mò̱ bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱ mɔɔ.

ref: But God set him free from death and raised him to life. Death could not hold him in its power.
text: Amaa Wuribware̱ a kyiŋŋi mò̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro, ne̱ ɔɔ kɔɔre̱ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ le̱e̱ lowi a e̱le̱ŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ mo̱ŋ sa lowi kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ nya mò̱ se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ.

ref: What David said are really the words of Jesus, “I always see the Lord near me, and I will not be afraid with him at my right side.
text: Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ ɔde̱daapo̱ Deefid a kyo̱rɛɛ mò̱ ase̱ŋ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, mo̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ fo̱ bo̱ mo̱ ase̱ saŋ ke̱maa. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ bo̱ mo̱ kigyise si‑o si, sɛye̱ maa tɔɔraa mo̱.

ref: Because of this, my heart will be glad, my words will be joyful, and I will live in hope.
text: Amo̱se̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a yuri mo̱, ne̱ mo̱ akatɔ a gyi mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ fo̱ kuŋu si ase̱ŋ. ne̱ n de te̱maa, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ,

ref: The Lord won't leave me in the grave. I am his holy one, and he won't let my body decay.
text: mo̱ a gye̱ fo̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱ timaa‑o si‑o, fo̱ maa yɔwe̱ mo̱ kra a o sii alowipo̱‑rɔ, fo̱ maa sa a mo̱ kayo̱wɔre̱ gyo ke̱gye̱raŋta‑rɔ.

ref: He has shown me the path to life, and he makes me glad by being near me.”
text: Fo̱ a kaapo̱ mo̱ kpa ne̱ ɔ yaa mo̱ ŋkpa‑rɔ‑ɔ, na, mo̱ ya yɔ fo̱ ase̱, mo̱ akatɔ gyi bwe̱e̱tɔ.’

ref: My friends, it is right for me to speak to you about our ancestor David. He died and was buried, and his tomb is still here.
text: Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, mo̱nꞌ nu nɛɛnɛɛ fe̱yɛ Owure Deefid, ane̱ ɔde̱daapo̱ dabe̱, mɔ dɛɛ a wu, ne̱ baa pure mò̱‑ɔ, mò̱ ke̱gye̱raŋta saŋ ke̱ bo̱ mfe̱e̱ bo̱ fo̱ ndɔɔ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ n gye̱ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ dɛɛ ɔ tɔwe̱‑ɔ.

ref: But David was a prophet, and he knew that God had made a promise he would not break. He had told David someone from his own family would someday be king.
text: Deefid amo̱ dɛɛ ɔ gye̱ Wuribware̱ a akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ ɔko̱ ne̱e̱, ne̱ o nyi dame̱naŋsɛ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a waa e̱taŋ fe̱yɛ ɔ sa a mò̱ ananagyi‑o ɔko̱ gyi kuwure fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ a gyi‑o dɛɛ.

ref: David knew this would happen, and so he told us Christ would be raised to life. He said God would not leave him in the grave or let his body decay.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ kyɔ ŋu fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ e̱ ba a ɔ bo̱ waa, ne̱ ɔɔ kyo̱rɛɛ amo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ i kyiŋŋi Kristoo ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ o suŋ a ɔ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱‑ɔ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro. Kristoo amo̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ mo̱ŋ sa a mò̱ kra sii alowipo̱‑rɔ. Ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ sa a mò̱ kayo̱wɔre̱ gyo ke̱gye̱raŋta‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: All of us can tell you that God has raised Jesus to life!
text: Ane̱ pɛɛɛ a ŋu Yeesuu amo̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ mò̱ kikyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro kamɛɛ‑rɔ.

ref: Jesus was taken up to sit at the right side of God, and he was given the Holy Spirit, just as the Father had promised. Jesus is also the one who has given the Spirit to us, and this is what you are now seeing and hearing.
text: Mò̱ a kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ so̱so̱ ya kye̱na Wuribware̱ kigyise si, na mò̱ aa mò̱ gyi kuwure. Ne̱ Wuribware̱ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ mò̱ se̱‑ɔ a sa mò̱ mò̱ kufwiiŋe timaa‑o ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ ɔ sa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a ŋu, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a nu ndɔɔ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o ne̱ Yeesuu amo̱ a sa ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ ywe̱e̱ ane̱ se̱‑ɔ.

ref: Everyone in Israel should then know for certain that God has made Jesus both Lord and Christ, even though you put him to death on a cross.
text: Ne̱ Peetroo a tɔwe̱ bo̱ gye̱ kɛɛ fe̱yɛ, “Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ Isireelii awuye nu fe̱yɛ Yeesuu mɔ ne̱ baa da mò̱ bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ mò̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a lee fe̱yɛ mò̱ e̱ gye̱ Kristoo, ne̱ o kii ɔke̱maa mò̱ nyaŋpe̱‑ɔ.”

ref: Peter said to them, “Turn to God and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins, and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
text: Ne̱ Peetroo a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ɔke̱maa nu mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ na o kiŋŋi le̱e̱ mò̱ e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ, na ɔ bɔ asuu Yeesuu Kristoo ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ, na Wuribware̱ taa mo̱ne̱ e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ ke mo̱ne̱, na o ke mo̱ne̱ mò̱ kufwiiŋe timaa‑o.

ref: Peter told them many other things as well. Then he said, “I beg you to save yourselves from what will happen to all these evil people.”
text: Ne̱ Peetroo a tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ gywii bamo̱ ane̱ŋ‑aaa. Ne̱ ŋkee ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ lee mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ ke̱se̱bɔgyiiri ne̱ ke̱ e̱ ba ndɔɔ a kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ a abo̱ro̱kɔɔne̱po̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ‑rɔ.”

ref: They spent their time learning from the apostles, and they were like family to each other. They also broke bread and prayed together. Life among the Lord's Followers
text: Le̱e̱ ŋke nsi amo̱, ne̱ baa le̱e̱ ba suye abware̱se̱ŋ le̱e̱ ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ ase̱. Ne̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ a waa kanɔ ko̱ŋko̱ tii abɛɛ se̱. Ne̱ ŋkee ba gyaŋŋe̱ ba gyi ateese, na ba ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ saŋ ke̱maa.

ref: All the Lord's followers often met together, and they shared everything they had.
text: Ne̱ akɔɔre̱gyipo̱ amo̱ pɛɛɛ a kya se̱ tii abɛɛ se̱, ne̱ ŋkee bamo̱ ɔko̱ ne̱ mò̱ i de ko̱tɔko̱, mò̱ bɛɛko̱ e̱ taare̱ kra‑rɔ.

ref: They would sell their property and possessions and give the money to whoever was in need.
text: Saŋ ko̱ na bo̱ fe bamo̱ mpo̱tɛɛ pɛɛɛ, na bo̱ taa atanne̱‑ɔ bo̱ tii abɛɛ se̱. Na bo̱ sa ɔke̱maa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ki tiri mò̱ ne̱ ɔ kpa‑ɔ.

ref: Day after day they met together in the temple. They broke bread together in different homes and shared their food happily and freely,
text: Na ba gyaŋŋe̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ kabuno kake ke̱maa, na ba gyaŋŋe̱ ba gyi abɛɛ aye̱ kakatɔgyi‑ro na kakpo̱nɔyuri‑ro.

ref: while praising God. Everyone liked them, and each day the Lord added to their group others who were being saved.
text: Na ba kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱. Ne̱ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ mɔ akatɔ a gyi bamo̱ se̱. Na Wuribware̱ e̱ sa ne̱ kake ke̱maa ba nya ase̱sɛ ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ mo̱rɔwe̱‑ɔ ba tii bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ se̱.

ref: The time of prayer was about three o'clock in the afternoon, and Peter and John were going into the temple.
text: Kake ŋko̱ kyɔwe̱ kikpeerebe̱e̱, ne̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ saŋ a fo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ Peetroo na Yohanee e̱ yɔ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa kabuno na bo̱ ya ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱.

ref: A man who had been born lame was being carried to the temple door. Each day he was placed beside this door, known as the Beautiful Gate. He sat there and begged from the people who were going in.
text: Saŋ amo̱ ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ hare̱e̱ le̱e̱ mò̱ ŋyaagyi‑ro oo kii ke̱gyabɔɔ‑ɔ mɔ te Wuribware̱ suŋkpa kibunogyi ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ke̱mo̱ ɛ “Kibunogyi dame̱naŋsɛ”‑ɔ kanɔ. Kake ke̱maa bo̱ so̱ mò̱ ba ba mfe̱ŋ, na o te ɔ ko̱re̱ bamo̱ ne̱ ba lwee suŋkpa kabuno a bo̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱‑ɔ atanne̱.

ref: The man saw Peter and John entering the temple, and he asked them for money.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, saŋ ne̱ oo ŋu Peetroo na Yohanee i lweero‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ ko̱re̱ bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ sa mò̱ ko̱tɔko̱.

ref: But they looked straight at him and said, “Look up at us!”
text: Ne̱ baa de̱e̱re̱ mò̱ akatɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ Peetroo a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “De̱e̱re̱ ane̱!”

ref: The man stared at them and thought he was going to get something.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ de̱e̱re̱ bamo̱, na ɔ fa fe̱yɛ ɔ nya ko̱tɔko̱ a ɔ le̱e̱ bamo̱ ase̱.

ref: But Peter said, “I don't have any silver or gold! But I will give you what I do have. In the name of Jesus Christ from Nazareth, get up and start walking.”
text: Amaa Peetroo a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ŋ de atanne̱, amaa mo̱ e̱ sa fo̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ n de‑o. Yeesuu Kristoo Nasarɛte̱nyi‑o ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ, ŋ yɛ fo̱ ko̱so̱ nare̱!”

ref: Peter then took him by the right hand and helped him up. At once the man's feet and ankles became strong,
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ kra mò̱ kigyisesare̱e̱‑rɔ kya mò̱‑rɔ bo̱ kyuwi‑ro. Puri amo̱‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ke̱gyabɔɔ amo̱ ayaa a waa le̱ŋ.

ref: and he jumped up and started walking. He went with Peter and John into the temple, walking and jumping and praising God.
text: Ne̱ oo fuwi ywe̱e̱, ne̱ ɔɔ ye̱re̱ mò̱ ayaa se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ ɔ naa. Ne̱ mò̱ aa bamo̱ a lwee Wuribware̱ suŋkpa kabuno, na ɔ naa na o fuwi ɔ ywe̱e̱, na ɔ kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱.

ref: Everyone saw him walking around and praising God.
text: Ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a ŋu mò̱ a naa na ɔ kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱.

ref: They knew he was the beggar who had been lying beside the Beautiful Gate, and they were completely surprised. They could not imagine what had happened to the man. Peter Speaks in the Temple
text: Saŋ ne̱ baa pini fe̱yɛ ke̱gyabɔɔ ne̱ o te Kibunogyi dame̱naŋsɛ‑ɔ kanɔ ɔ ko̱re̱ atɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ bamo̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ kaa waa‑ɔ se̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Peetroo a tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa kabuno‑o

ref: While the man kept holding on to Peter and John, the whole crowd ran to them in amazement at the place known as Solomon's Porch.
text: Amɔ ke̱gyabɔɔ ne̱ Peetroo a kya‑ɔ maa lɛɛ kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ maye̱ Peetroo na Yohanee. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ase̱sɛ a ŋu fe̱yɛ ŋkee ɔɔ kpaare̱, ne̱ mò̱ aa Peetroo na Yohanee bo̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ Solomɔŋ abraŋdaye̱‑ɔ, ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ bamo̱, ne̱ baa ŋwɛɛnaŋ yɔ bamo̱ ase̱.

ref: Peter saw that a crowd had gathered, and he said: Friends, why are you surprised at what has happened? Why are you staring at us? Do you think we have some power of our own? Do you think we were able to make this man walk because we are so religious?
text: Saŋ ne̱ Peetroo a ŋu ase̱sɛ amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “M bɛɛko̱‑ana Isireelii awuye, nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ mo̱ne̱, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ de̱e̱re̱ ane̱ ane̱ŋ? Mo̱ne̱ e̱ fa fe̱yɛ ane̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ke̱yaale̱ŋ bɛɛɛ itimaa ne̱ ane̱ a bo̱ sa ne̱ ke̱gyabɔɔ mɔ a ko̱so̱ nare̱ bɛɛɛ? Yeesuu ke̱yaale̱ŋ‑nɔ ne̱ ane̱ a taare̱ sa ne̱ ɔɔ ko̱so̱ nare̱.

ref: The God that Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and our other ancestors worshiped has brought honor to his Servant Jesus. He is the one you betrayed. You turned against him when he was being tried by Pilate, even though Pilate wanted to set him free.
text: Wuribware̱ ne̱ ane̱ i suŋ, ne̱ ane̱ ade̱daapo̱ Abraham na Isak na Yakubu a suŋ‑o dɛɛ ya taa mò̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ bo̱ sa Yeesuu. Yeesuu mɔ dɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a taa mò̱ bo̱ waa Gominaa Pilat ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ fe̱yɛ ɔ mɔɔ‑ɔ. Gominaa mɔ gbaa a kpa a ɔ yɔwe̱ mò̱ a ɔ nare̱ ya kyure.

ref: and you killed the one who leads people to life. But God raised him from death, and all of us can tell you what he has done.
text: Ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a mɔɔ mò̱ ne̱ ɔɔ kaapo̱ ane̱ kpa ne̱ ane̱ e̱ gya se̱, na ane̱ nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ. Amaa Wuribware̱ a kyiŋŋi mò̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro, ne̱ ane̱ pɛɛɛ a ŋu.

ref: You see this man, and you know him. He put his faith in the name of Jesus and was made strong. Faith in Jesus made this man completely well while everyone was watching.
text: Ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ a kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi‑o si, ne̱ ke̱gyabɔɔ mɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ ke̱e̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ e̱ taare̱ a ɔ nare̱. Yeesuu ke̱nyare̱ ne̱ ane̱ a bo̱ kya mò̱. Yeesuu ne̱ ane̱ a kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi‑o ke̱nyare̱ ne̱ ane̱ a bo̱ kya mò̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a ŋu mò̱ a kpaare̱ lo̱we̱‑ɔ.

ref: My friends, I am sure you and your leaders didn't know what you were doing.
text: Mo̱ nyare̱‑ana, mbe̱yɔmɔ ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ aa mo̱ne̱ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ pɛɛɛ mo̱ŋ nyi ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ waa‑ɔ ne̱e̱ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a waa Yeesuu ane̱ŋ.

ref: So turn to God! Give up your sins, and you will be forgiven.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱nꞌ nu mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱, na mo̱nꞌ kiŋŋi le̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ, na mo̱nꞌ ba Wuribware̱ ase̱, na ɔ nya taa mo̱ne̱ e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ ke mo̱ne̱.

ref: Then the time will come when the Lord will give you fresh strength. He will send you Jesus, his chosen Messiah.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ya waa amo̱‑ɔ, ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ sa a mo̱ne̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ yuri, na ɔ be̱e̱ suŋ Kristoo ne̱ oo lee fe̱yɛ ɔ ba mo̱ne̱ ase̱‑ɔ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱. Kristoo mɔ e̱ gye̱ Yeesuu.

ref: But Jesus must stay in heaven until God makes all things new, just as his holy prophets promised long ago.
text: O sii so̱so̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ bo̱‑ɔ bo̱ fo̱ saŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ waa ke̱tɔ ke̱maa nɛɛnɛɛ, ane̱ŋ ne̱ mò̱ akyaamɛɛ timaa ne̱ baa dɛɛ kye̱na‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ hare̱e̱ le̱e̱ de̱daa‑ɔ.

ref: Moses said, “The Lord your God will choose one of your own people to be a prophet, just as he chose me. Listen to everything he tells you.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ ɔde̱daapo̱ Mosis a kyo̱rɛɛ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ne̱ nyaŋpe̱ Wuribware̱ i lee se̱sɛ ko̱ a ɔ le̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ ako̱we̱bɛɛ‑rɔ, se̱sɛ ne̱ o du fe̱yɛ mo̱, Mosis‑o, fe̱yɛ ɔ waa mò̱ kyaamɛɛ sa mo̱ne̱, A bware fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ nu mò̱ ase̱ŋ tɔwe̱sɛ.

ref: No one who disobeys this prophet will be one of God's people any longer.”
text: Amaa Wuribware̱ i lee ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ maa nu ane̱ŋ a kyaamɛɛ a kanɔ‑ɔ a ɔ le̱e̱ mò̱ ase̱sɛ‑rɔ, na ɔ gya mò̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱.’

ref: You are really the ones God told his prophets to speak to. And you were given the promise God made to your ancestors. He said to Abraham, “All nations on earth will be blessed because of someone from your family.”
text: Ase̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ bo̱ bo̱rɔ mò̱ akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ se̱ na amo̱ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii ane̱ ɔde̱daapo̱ Abraham‑ɔ gye̱ ko̱ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱e̱. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii Abraham‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ e̱ bo̱rɔ fo̱ ananagyi‑o si a ŋ yure ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ kaye̱‑rɔ‑ɔ.’ Ase̱ŋ mɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya‑ɔ pɛɛɛ gye̱ mo̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ te ndɔɔ‑ɔ lee ne̱e̱.

ref: God sent his chosen Son to you first, because God wanted to bless you and make each one of you turn away from your sins.
text: Ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ Wuribware̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱ Yeesuu mɔ ne̱ ɔɔ gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ suŋ mò̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ mo̱ne̱ Isireelii awuye ase̱, na ɔ bo̱ yure mo̱ne̱ ɔke̱maa, na mo̱nꞌ nya yɔwe̱ bo̱ro̱kɔɔne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ de‑o.”

ref: The apostles were still talking to the people, when some priests, the captain of the temple guard, and some Sadducees arrived.
text: Peetroo na Yohanee saŋ bo̱ ye̱re̱ ba tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ ba gywii ase̱sɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ na Wuribware̱ suŋkpa ade̱e̱re̱se̱po̱‑ɔ ɔbre̱sɛ‑ɔ na Sadukii awuye ko̱ a ba mfe̱ŋ.

ref: These men were angry because the apostles were teaching the people that the dead would be raised from death, just as Jesus had been raised from death.
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ iduŋ a fwii, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ abɛɛ anyɔ‑ɔ e̱ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Yeesuu a kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro, ne̱ ke̱tɔ e̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ saŋ ko̱ e̱ ba ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa wu‑o i kyiŋŋi a bo̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro.

ref: It was already late in the afternoon, and they arrested Peter and John and put them in jail for the night.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa kra bamo̱ ya tii, na kaye̱ ya ke‑o na bo̱ ŋu ane̱ŋ ne̱ ba waa bamo̱‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ saŋ ne̱ baa kra bamo̱‑ɔ, kyɔwe̱ a kyɔ lo̱we̱.

ref: The next morning the leaders, the elders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses met in Jerusalem.
text: Kaye̱ ŋke‑o, Yudaa awuye agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ na abre̱sɛ na Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a gyaŋŋe̱ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ mfe̱ŋ.

ref: The high priest Annas was there, as well as Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and other members of the high priest's family.
text: Bamo̱ aa Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ Hanaa, na mò̱ ako̱we̱bɛɛ Kayafas, na Yohanee na Alɛsanda na bamo̱ bo̱ko̱ ne̱ baa saŋ‑ɔ i tii bamo̱ ne̱ baa gyaŋŋe̱‑ɔ se̱.

ref: They brought in Peter and John and made them stand in the middle while they questioned them. They asked, “By what power and in whose name have you done this?”
text: Ne̱ baa sa ne̱ Peetroo na Yohanee a bo̱ ye̱re̱ bamo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ baa bise bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “E̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a waa kya ke̱gyabɔɔ‑ɔ? Nte̱tɔ e̱le̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ de, bɛɛɛ nsɛ e̱le̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a bo̱ kya ɔnyare̱ amo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱?”

ref: Peter was filled with the Holy Spirit and told the nation's leaders and the elders:
text: Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a sa Peetroo ke̱yaale̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Maŋ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ na abre̱sɛ,

ref: You are questioning us today about a kind deed in which a man who could not walk was healed.
text: ndɔɔ fe̱raa, mo̱ne̱ i bise ane̱ ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ ane̱ a waa ke̱gyabɔɔ ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ, na ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ a waa kya mò̱‑ɔ bɛɛɛ?

ref: But there is something we must tell you and everyone else in Israel. This man is standing here completely well because of the power of Jesus Christ from Nazareth. You put Jesus to death on a cross, but God raised him to life.
text: To, mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ Isireelii awuye fe̱yɛ Yeesuu Kristoo Nasarɛte̱nyi e̱le̱ŋ‑nɔ na ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ ne̱ ane̱ a bo̱ kya ɔnyare̱ mɔ, ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ ɔɔ kpaare̱ pɛɛɛ, ɔ ye̱re̱ mo̱ne̱ akatɔ‑rɔ mfe̱e̱‑ɔ. Ŋ yɛ ɛɛɛ, Yeesuu mɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a da mò̱ bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱, ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kyiŋŋi mò̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro‑o e̱le̱ŋ‑nɔ na ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ ne̱ ane̱ a bo̱ kya ɔnyare̱ mɔ.

ref: He is the stone you builders thought was worthless, and now he is the most important stone of all.
text: Yeesuu amo̱ kuŋu si ase̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ke̱fo̱re̱ serisɛ ko̱ ne̱ apwɛɛpo̱ de bo̱ pwɛɛ ne̱ baa kine ke̱mo̱ bo̱ twe̱e̱‑ɔ, ya ko̱ŋ bo̱ kii ke̱mo̱ ne̱ kaa le̱ŋ lɔŋ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ bo̱ ye̱ra‑ɔ.’

ref: Only Jesus has the power to save! His name is the only one in all the world that can save anyone.
text: Ɔke̱maa mo̱ŋ bo̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a sa e̱le̱ŋ a ɔ taare̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ se̱sɛ, amɔ Yeesuu wo̱re̱ kpeŋ. Mò̱ wo̱re̱ e̱ gye̱ ane̱ pɛɛɛ mɔ ɔmo̱rɔwe̱po̱.”

ref: The officials were amazed to see how brave Peter and John were, and they knew that these two apostles were only ordinary men and not well educated. The officials were certain these men had been with Jesus.
text: Ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a ko̱ŋ kpe̱ŋ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ na abre̱sɛ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ Peetroo na Yohanee a nya kakpo̱nɔ be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ ane̱ŋ, na bamo̱ mɔ bo̱ mo̱ŋ suye wo̱re̱ akatɔ‑rɔ. Ŋkee ne̱ baa nyiŋŋi si fe̱yɛ abɛɛ anyɔ‑ɔ dɛɛ bo̱ gya Yeesuu si ne̱e̱.

ref: But they could not deny what had happened. The man who had been healed was standing there with the apostles.
text: Amaa bo̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ taare̱ bise bamo̱ sɛye̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ baa ŋu Peetroo na Yohanee na ɔnyare̱ ne̱ baa kya mò̱‑ɔ a ye̱re̱ mfe̱ŋ.

ref: The officials commanded them to leave the council room. Then the officials said to each other,
text: Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ Peetroo na Yohanee na ɔnyare̱‑ɔ le̱e̱ se̱ŋgyikpa a ke̱kyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ. Bamo̱ a le̱e̱ kawu‑o, ne̱ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ na abre̱sɛ‑ɔ wo̱re̱ wo̱re̱ a le̱e̱ ba tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ,

ref: “What can we do with these men? Everyone in Jerusalem knows about this miracle, and we cannot say it didn't happen.
text: “E̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ waa ase̱sɛ mɔ‑ɔ ne̱e̱? Yɛro̱salɛm awuye pɛɛɛ kyɔ bo̱ nyi fe̱yɛ bamo̱ a waa akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ mɔ, ne̱ ane̱ gbaa ane̱ maa taare̱ a ane̱ gyiiri amo̱ ikii.

ref: But to keep this thing from spreading, we will warn them never again to speak to anyone about the name of Jesus.”
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ yii bamo̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ma lɛɛ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ Yeesuu ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ gywii ɔko̱. N gye̱ ane̱ŋ, ane̱ maa taare̱ a ane̱ kuŋ ɔke̱maa kunu ase̱ŋ‑ɔ.”

ref: So they called the two apostles back in and told them they must never, for any reason, teach anything about the name of Jesus.
text: Ne̱ baa te̱e̱ bamo̱ lweero, ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ma lɛɛ kaapo̱ ase̱sɛ atɔ bɛɛɛ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ Yeesuu ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ ŋke ŋko̱ se̱.

ref: Peter and John answered, “Do you think God wants us to obey you or to obey him?
text: Amaa Peetroo na Yohanee a be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “To, mbe̱yɔmɔ mo̱ne̱ fɔŋfɔŋ lee ke̱mo̱ ne̱ ki bware Wuribware̱ ase̱‑ɔ, ane̱ bu mo̱ne̱, bɛɛɛ ane̱ bu Wuribware̱?

ref: We cannot keep quiet about what we have seen and heard.”
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ fe̱raa, ane̱ maa taare̱ a ane̱ yɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ a nu, ne̱ ane̱ a ŋu‑o ko̱tɔwe̱.”

ref: The officials could not find any reason to punish Peter and John. So they threatened them and let them go. The man who was healed by this miracle was more than
text: Ke̱gyabɔɔ ne̱ baa kya mò̱ mɔ‑ɔ a gyi kyo̱ŋ nsu aduna. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Kristoo akɔɔre̱gyipo̱‑ɔ a ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ na bo̱ nya nya kakpo̱nɔ‑ɔ

ref: As soon as Peter and John had been set free, they went back and told the others everything the chief priests and the leaders had said to them.
text: Saŋ ne̱ baa de̱ŋ yɔwe̱ bamo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ Peetroo na Yohanee a kiŋŋi yɔ bamo̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana Yeesuu akɔɔre̱gyipo̱ ase̱ ya tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ na maŋ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ.

ref: When the rest of the Lord's followers heard this, they prayed together and said: Master, you created heaven and earth, the sea, and everything in them.
text: Bamo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana‑ɔ a nu‑o, ne̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ a gyaŋŋe̱ kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱ fe̱yɛ, “O Nyaŋpe̱ Wuribware̱, fo̱ ya twe̱e̱ so̱so̱ na kaase̱ na ɔpo̱o̱‑ɔ na ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ke̱ bo̱‑rɔ pɛɛɛ‑ɔ,

ref: And by the Holy Spirit you spoke to our ancestor David. He was your servant, and you told him to say: “Why are all the Gentiles so furious? Why do people make foolish plans?
text: ane̱ nyi fe̱yɛ fo̱ a bo̱rɔ fo̱ kufwiiŋe timaa‑o si bo̱ sa ne̱ ane̱ ɔde̱daapo̱ Owure Deefid, fo̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱, a tɔwe̱ ɛ, ‘Nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ nde̱ se̱ awuye iduŋ a fwii, ne̱ ba da Wuribware̱ na owure ne̱ oo lee‑o kuŋu si kikpuni, na bamo̱ mɔ bo̱ maa taare̱ a bo̱ waa sɛye̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱?

ref: The kings of earth prepare for war, and the rulers join together against the Lord and his Messiah.”
text: Kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ awure na agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ a ke̱re̱ ke̱naa, na bo̱ waa kanɔ kɔ Wuribware̱ na mò̱ ne̱ oo lee‑o. Amaa bamo̱ mɔ, ba waa kwaa.’

ref: Here in Jerusalem, Herod and Pontius Pilate got together with the Gentiles and the people of Israel. Then they turned against your holy Servant Jesus, your chosen Messiah.
text: Ne̱ amo̱‑ɔ a ba kase̱ŋtiŋ ane̱ŋ, fe̱yɛ Owure Hɛrɔd na Gominaa Pontiyus Pilat na Roma awuye na Yudaa awuye a gyaŋŋe̱ da fo̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱ timaa Yeesuu kuŋu si kikpuni bɔye̱. Baa tii abɛɛ se̱, ne̱ baa kra mò̱ ne̱ fo̱ a suŋ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ bo̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ ane̱‑ɔ.

ref: They did what you in your power and wisdom had already decided would happen.
text: Baa gyaŋŋe̱ waa ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ fo̱ e̱le̱ŋ‑nɔ fo̱ a kyɔ kra fo̱ mfɛɛre̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱ e̱ waa‑ɔ.

ref: Lord, listen to their threats! We are your servants. So make us brave enough to speak your message.
text: Wuribware̱, mbe̱yɔmɔ, ke̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ba fuŋte ane̱‑ɔ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, sa ane̱ ne̱ ane̱ gye̱ fo̱ ayaafɔre̱‑ɔ kakpo̱nɔ, na ane̱ taare̱ bo̱ tɔwe̱ fo̱ ase̱ŋ timaa‑o na kufu mo̱ŋ de ane̱.

ref: Show your mighty power, as we heal people and work miracles and wonders in the name of your holy Servant Jesus.
text: Teyi fo̱ ke̱sare̱e̱ bo̱ kya alɔpo̱, na fo̱ sa a ane̱ waa atɔ ne̱ a kpe̱ŋ ase̱sɛ e̱ye̱e̱‑ɔ fo̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱ timaa Yeesuu ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ.”

ref: After they had prayed, the meeting place shook. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and bravely spoke God's message. Sharing Possessions
text: Bamo̱ a ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, mfe̱ŋ ne̱ baa gyaŋŋe̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a le̱ŋkpaŋ ne̱e̱. Ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a ywe̱e̱ bamo̱ se̱, ne̱ baa nya ŋkpo̱nɔ ba tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu akɔɔre̱gyipo̱‑ɔ a ke bamo̱ mpo̱tɛɛ‑rɔ sa abɛɛ‑ɔ

ref: The group of followers all felt the same way about everything. None of them claimed that their possessions were their own, and they shared everything they had with each other.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu akɔɔre̱gyipo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a waa ko̱ŋko̱. Bamo̱ mfɛɛre̱ na nnɔ pɛɛɛ a waa ŋko̱ŋko̱. Ɔko̱ maa tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ atɔ ne̱ o de‑o gye̱ mò̱ wo̱re̱ mò̱ lee ne̱e̱. Amaa ɔko̱ ne̱ mò̱ i de ko̱tɔko̱‑ɔ, bamo̱ pɛɛɛ mɔ i ke‑ro ne̱e̱.

ref: In a powerful way the apostles told everyone that the Lord Jesus was now alive. God greatly blessed his followers,
text: Ne̱ ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱ kudu anyɔ‑ɔ a nya Wuribware̱ e̱le̱ŋ bo̱ tɔwe̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu kikyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro ase̱ŋ gywii ase̱sɛ, ne̱ Wuribware̱ a sa bamo̱ pɛɛɛ ŋyure bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: and no one went in need of anything. Everyone who owned land or houses would sell them and bring the money
text: Ko̱tɔko̱ mo̱ŋ tiri ɔko̱ bamo̱‑rɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ de adɔɔ bɛɛɛ e̱lɔŋ‑ɔ i fe na bo̱ taa atanne̱ ne̱ baa fe nya‑ɔ baa.

ref: to the apostles. Then they would give the money to anyone in need.
text: Na bo̱ taa sa ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ na bamo̱‑ɔ bo̱ sa ɔke̱maa ane̱ŋ ne̱ o nyi fe̱yɛ ɔ kpa‑ɔ.

ref: Joseph was one of the followers who had sold a piece of property and brought the money to the apostles. He was a Levite from Cyprus, and the apostles called him Barnabas, which means “one who encourages others.”
text: Ne̱ oo fe mò̱ ko̱dɔɔ ne̱ o de‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ baa atanne̱ ne̱ ɔɔ nya‑ɔ baa bo̱ sa ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ke‑ro sa atiripo̱‑ɔ.

ref: Ananias and his wife Sapphira also sold a piece of property.
text: Amaa saŋ ko̱, ne̱ ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Ananiyas‑o na mò̱ ka Safiraa a fe bamo̱ ko̱dɔɔ ko̱.

ref: But they agreed to cheat and keep some of the money for themselves. So when Ananias took the rest of the money to the apostles,
text: Ne̱ mò̱ aa mò̱ ka a waa kanɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ te̱ŋ atanne̱‑ɔ kitiŋ lee, ne̱ ɔɔ taa amo̱ ne̱ aa saŋ‑ɔ yaa bo̱ sa ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ke‑ro sa atiripo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa waa fe̱yɛ atanne̱‑ɔ gye̱ amo̱ ne̱ baa fe bamo̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ nya‑ɔ pɛɛɛ ne̱e̱.

ref: Peter said, “Why has Satan made you keep back some of the money from the sale of the property? Why have you lied to the Holy Spirit?
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Peetroo a bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ananiyas, nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ fo̱ a sa ne̱ Ɔbɔnsam a nya fo̱ hare̱e̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ fo̱ a pe̱nna Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ a te̱ŋ atanne̱ ne̱ fo̱ a fe fo̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ nya‑ɔ kitiŋ taa‑ɔ ne̱e̱?

ref: The property was yours before you sold it, and even after you sold it, the money was still yours. What made you do such a thing? You didn't lie to people. You lied to God!”
text: Fo̱ e̱ bo̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ pwɛɛ ne̱ fo̱ a dɛɛ fe, ne̱ fo̱ a be̱e̱ fe‑o atanne̱‑ɔ fo̱ dɛɛ fo̱ e̱ bo̱. Ne̱ nte̱tɔ ne̱e̱ ne̱ fo̱ a waa ane̱ŋ a ase̱ŋ‑ɔ? N gye̱ se̱sɛ ne̱ fo̱ a pe̱nna‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ ne̱ fo̱ a pe̱nna!”

ref: As soon as Ananias heard this, he dropped dead, and everyone who heard about it was frightened.
text: Ananiyas a de̱ŋ nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ Peetroo a tɔwe̱‑ɔ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, puri amo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ da wu. Ne̱ kufu a nya ɔke̱maa ne̱ oo nu ase̱ŋ mɔ‑ɔ.

ref: Some young men came in and wrapped up his body. Then they took it out and buried it.
text: Ne̱ ayaafɔre̱ ko̱ a lweero bo̱ ba atɔ bo̱ buŋ mò̱ se̱, ne̱ baa taa mò̱ yaa pure.

ref: Three hours later Sapphira came in, but she did not know what had happened to her husband.
text: Bo̱ fo̱ dɔŋhwe̱re̱e̱ e̱sa‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱ ka Safiraa mɔ a bo̱ lweero, na ɔ mo̱ŋ nyi ke̱tɔ ne̱ kaa waa mò̱ kuri‑o.

ref: Peter asked her, “Tell me, did you sell the property for this amount?” “Yes,” she answered, “that's the amount.”
text: Ne̱ Peetroo a bise Safiraa fe̱yɛ, “Gyi kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ a fe mo̱ne̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ, amɔ atanne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a nya pɛɛɛ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ amo̱‑ɔ aaa?” Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱ŋŋaa ɛ, “Ɔɔŋ, amo̱ pɛɛɛ ne̱e̱!”

ref: Then Peter said, “Why did the two of you agree to test the Lord's Spirit? The men who buried Ananias are by the door, and they will carry you out!”
text: Ne̱ Peetroo a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ ne̱e̱ ne̱ fo̱ aa fo̱ kuri a waa kanɔ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kyɔ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o‑ro mo̱ne̱ e̱ ke̱e̱? Ase̱sɛ ne̱ baa ya pure fo̱ kuri‑o i kiŋŋi ba ba bo̱ bo̱‑rɔ. Amaa bamo̱ dɛɛ bamo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ba so̱rɔ fo̱ a bo̱ be̱e̱ bo̱ le̱e̱!”

ref: At once she fell at Peter's feet and died. When the young men came back in, they found Sapphira lying there dead. So they carried her out and buried her beside her husband.
text: Puri amo̱‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ mò̱ gbaa a le̱e̱ da Peetroo ayaa‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ wu. Ayaafɔre̱ amo̱ a bo̱ lweero‑o, baa ŋu mò̱ a wu ɔ da, ne̱ baa so̱rɔ mò̱ le̱e̱, ne̱ baa ya pure mò̱ me̱raa mò̱ kuri.

ref: The church members were afraid, and so was everyone else who heard what had happened. Peter's Unusual Power
text: Ne̱ kufu a nya asɔre̱e̱ awuye na ɔke̱maa ne̱ oo nu bamo̱ ilowi‑o si‑o. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ a waa akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ

ref: No one outside their group dared join them, even though everyone liked them very much.
text: Hare̱e̱ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ ya bu bamo̱ gbaa ooo, ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ tii bamo̱ se̱‑ɔ, kufu de mò̱ kulwee bamo̱‑rɔ gyaŋŋe̱kpa mfe̱ŋ.

ref: Many men and women started having faith in the Lord.
text: Amaa ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ, akye̱e̱ na anyare̱ pɛɛɛ ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi‑o tii akɔɔre̱gyipo̱‑ɔ se̱ saŋ ke̱maa.

ref: Then sick people were brought out to the road and placed on mats. It was hoped that Peter would walk by, and his shadow would fall on them and heal them.
text: Bo̱ le̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ e̱ waa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ase̱sɛ a so̱rɔ bamo̱ alɔpo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ ya bo̱ be̱ya e̱kraŋ na e̱kyaakyaa na e̱bɛɛre̱ se̱ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ mbo̱re̱ se̱, na Peetroo ya kyo̱ŋ mfe̱ŋ a mò̱ kuyayu dɔŋŋɔ bamo̱ se̱ a bo̱ nya kpaare̱.

ref: A lot of people living in the towns near Jerusalem brought those who were sick or troubled by evil spirits, and they were all healed. Trouble for the Apostles
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ a le̱e̱ e̱maŋ ne̱ e̱ maa Yɛro̱salɛm‑ɔ se̱ so̱rɔ alɔpo̱ na bamo̱ ne̱ ibrisi te bamo̱ se̱‑ɔ baa ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ ase̱. Ne̱ baa kya bamo̱ pɛɛɛ. Ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ ase̱ŋ kuŋu

ref: They arrested the apostles and put them in the city jail.
text: Ne̱ baa kra ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ yaa tii mfe̱ŋ ne̱ baa tii abo̱ro̱kɔɔne̱po̱‑ɔ.

ref: But that night an angel from the Lord opened the doors of the jail and led the apostles out. The angel said,
text: Amaa ke̱mo̱ kanye ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ a le̱e̱ so̱so̱ bo̱ taye̱ ke̱kyaŋ ne̱ baa tii bamo̱ ke̱mo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ a po̱ne̱‑ɔ, ne̱ oo lee ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ,

ref: “Go to the temple and tell the people everything about this new life.”
text: “Mo̱nꞌ nare̱ ya ye̱re̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa kabuno, na mo̱nꞌ tɔwe̱ ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ ase̱ŋ mfe̱ŋ.”

ref: So they went into the temple before sunrise and started teaching. The high priest and his men called together their council, which included all of Israel's leaders. Then they ordered the apostles to be brought to them from the jail.
text: Ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ kine, ne̱ baa fo̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa kabuno gye̱gyaye̱ kese‑ro, ne̱ baa le̱e̱ abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ ke̱kaapo̱. Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ‑ɔ na mò̱ ase̱sɛ mo̱ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ a le̱e̱. Ne̱ baa te̱e̱ Yudaa awuye abre̱sɛ‑ɔ bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱. Ne̱ baa suŋ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ lee ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ baa se̱ŋgyikpa mfe̱ŋ.

ref: The temple police who were sent to the jail did not find the apostles. They returned and said,
text: Amaa bamo̱ a yɔ‑ɔ, bo̱ mo̱ŋ ŋu bamo̱ tiikpa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa kiŋŋi bo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii abre̱sɛ ne̱ baa gyaŋŋe̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ,

ref: “We found the jail locked tight and the guards standing at the doors. But when we opened the doors and went in, we didn't find anyone there.”
text: “Saŋ ne̱ ane̱ a yɔ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ ke̱kyaŋ‑ɔ a tii hare̱e̱ baa twe̱e̱‑rɔ, ne̱ asoogyaa awuye ye̱re̱ ba ke̱e̱ se̱. Amaa ane̱ a taye̱ lweero‑o, ane̱ mo̱ŋ ŋu ɔko̱ ke̱mo̱‑rɔ!”

ref: The captain of the temple police and the chief priests listened to their report, but they did not know what to think about it.
text: Wuribware̱ suŋkpa ade̱e̱re̱se̱po̱ ɔbre̱sɛ‑ɔ na Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ a nu amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ aa waa bamo̱ mfɛɛre̱, ne̱ kufu de bamo̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱ŋ e̱ ba.

ref: Just then someone came in and said, “Now those men you put in jail are in the temple, teaching the people!”
text: Ne̱ ɔnyare̱ ko̱ a lweero bamo̱ ase̱ mfe̱ŋ, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ i nu aaa, ase̱sɛ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a tii tiikpa‑ɔ ye̱re̱ ba kaapo̱ ase̱sɛ atɔ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa kabuno!”

ref: The captain went with some of the temple police and brought the apostles back. But they did not use force. They were afraid that the people might start throwing stones at them.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa ade̱e̱re̱se̱po̱ ko̱ na bamo̱ ɔbre̱sɛ a yɔ mfe̱ŋ ya baa ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ. Amaa ade̱e̱re̱se̱po̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ ye̱re̱ bamo̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kufu de bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ya ye̱re̱ bamo̱, ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ ye̱re̱ ba nu ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱ amo̱ ase̱ŋ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ e̱ twe̱e̱ bamo̱ abu.

ref: When the apostles were brought before the council, the high priest said to them,
text: Ne̱ baa baa ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ baa bo̱ ye̱ra maŋ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ na abre̱sɛ‑ɔ akatɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ,

ref: “We told you plainly not to teach in the name of Jesus. But look what you have done! You have been teaching all over Jerusalem, and you are trying to blame us for his death.”
text: “Ne̱e̱ ane̱ a sa mo̱ne̱ mbraa fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ ma lɛɛ kaapo̱ ase̱sɛ atɔ Yeesuu ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ, amaa mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱re̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a waa‑ɔ! Mo̱ne̱ ke̱kaapo̱‑ɔ a saŋŋe̱‑rɔ lwee Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ pɛɛɛ‑rɔ, ne̱ ŋkee mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa a mo̱nꞌ taa Yeesuu lowi bo̱ so̱rɔ ane̱.”

ref: Peter and the apostles replied: We don't obey people. We obey God.
text: Ne̱ Peetroo na ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱ ne̱ bo̱ tii si‑o pɛɛɛ a be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ, “Wuribware̱ ne̱ ane̱ i bu, n gye̱ se̱sɛ.

ref: You killed Jesus by nailing him to a cross. But the God our ancestors worshiped raised him to life
text: Wuribware̱ ne̱ ane̱ Isireelii awuye a suŋ hare̱e̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ ade̱daapo̱ mbe̱e̱ se̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ oo kyiŋŋi Yeesuu le̱e̱ lowi‑ro saŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a da mò̱ bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱ mɔɔ‑ɔ kamɛɛ‑rɔ.

ref: and made him our Leader and Savior. Then God gave him a place at his right side, so that the people of Israel would turn back to him and be forgiven.
text: Wuribware̱ a kyiŋŋi mò̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro yaa mò̱ ya bo̱ kye̱na mò̱ kigyise si fe̱yɛ mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ane̱ owure na ane̱ ɔmo̱rɔwe̱po̱, na Isireelii awuye nya nu bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱, na ɔ taa bamo̱ e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ ke bamo̱.

ref: We are here to tell you about all this, and so is the Holy Spirit, who is God's gift to everyone who obeys God.
text: Amɔ ase̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ a ye̱re̱ ayaa‑rɔ ane̱ e̱ tɔwe̱. Ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa ne̱ Wuribware̱ i ke bamo̱ ne̱ ba gya mò̱ kanɔ se̱‑ɔ, e̱ de̱ŋ ɔ be̱e̱ ɔ ye̱re̱ ase̱ŋ amo̱ ayaa‑rɔ.”

ref: When the council members heard this, they became so angry they wanted to kill the apostles.
text: Ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ‑ɔ na mò̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ iduŋ a fwii bamo̱ se̱, hare̱e̱ baa kpa fe̱yɛ bo̱ mɔɔ bamo̱.

ref: But one of the members was the Pharisee Gamaliel, a highly respected teacher. He ordered the apostles to be taken out of the room for a little while.
text: Amaa bamo̱‑rɔ Farisiinyi ko̱ ne̱ ɔke̱maa i bu, ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Gamaliilii‑o a ko̱so̱ ye̱re̱ ne̱e̱, ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ baa lee ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ le̱e̱ ngyaŋŋe̱‑ɔ‑rɔ kafwe̱e̱.

ref: Then he said to the council: Men of Israel, be careful what you do with these men.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ sa se̱ŋsa gywii bamo̱ ne̱ baa gyaŋŋe̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “M bɛɛko̱‑ana Isireelii awuye, mo̱nꞌ sa ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa a mo̱nꞌ bo̱ waa ase̱sɛ mɔ‑ɔ se̱.

ref: Later, when the people of our nation were being counted, Judas from Galilee showed up. A lot of people followed him, but he was killed, and all his followers were scattered.
text: Amo̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ ne̱ Yudas Galile̱yanyi‑o a ba kasu ne̱ baa kare̱ ase̱sɛ kamo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ a gya mò̱ se̱, ne̱ baa kɔ Roma awuye‑o, ne̱ Roma awuye amo̱ a mɔɔ mò̱ gbaa mò̱, ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ gya mò̱ se̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a brawe̱‑rɔ.

ref: So I advise you to stay away from these men. Leave them alone. If what they are planning is something of their own doing, it will fail.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mbe̱yɔmɔ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ ma waa ase̱sɛ mɔ sɛye̱. Mo̱nꞌ yɔwe̱ bamo̱! Ne̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ baa waa mɔ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ bamo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ e̱le̱ŋ ne̱ baa bo̱ waa, amo̱ fe̱raa a gye̱ kɛɛ.

ref: But if God is behind it, you cannot stop it anyway, unless you want to fight against God. The council members agreed with what he said,
text: Amaa ne̱ amo̱ ya le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱ fe̱raa, ane̱ maa taare̱ a ane̱ tii bamo̱ kpa, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ aa Wuribware̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kɔ.” Ne̱ baa gya Gamaliilii a ase̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱.

ref: and they called the apostles back in. They had them beaten with a whip and warned them not to speak in the name of Jesus. Then they let them go.
text: Ne̱ baa te̱e̱ ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ kiŋŋi baa ngyaŋŋe̱‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ baa sa ne̱ baa daye̱ bamo̱ ko̱kyo̱kywe̱e̱, ne̱ baa be̱e̱ yii bamo̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ma lɛɛ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ gywii ase̱sɛ Yeesuu ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ. Ne̱ ŋkee baa yɔwe̱ bamo̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱.

ref: The apostles left the council and were happy, because God had considered them worthy to suffer for the sake of Jesus.
text: Ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ a le̱e̱ ngyaŋŋe̱‑ɔ‑rɔ na bamo̱ akatɔ a gyi, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a lee bamo̱ sa ne̱ baa kperi Yeesuu si gyi ipeere.

ref: Every day they spent time in the temple and in one home after another. They never stopped teaching and telling the good news that Jesus is the Messiah.
text: Ne̱ ŋkee kake ke̱maa bo̱ naa Wuribware̱ suŋkpa kabuno na ase̱sɛ e̱lɔŋ‑nɔ ba kya se̱ ba kaapo̱, na ba tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ timaa fe̱yɛ Yeesuu e̱ gye̱ Kristoo ne̱ Wuribware̱ a suŋ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱‑ɔ ase̱ŋ ba gywii ase̱sɛ.

ref: A lot of people were now becoming followers of the Lord. But some of the ones who spoke Greek started complaining about the ones who spoke Aramaic. They complained that the Greek-speaking widows were not given their share when the food supplies were handed out each day.
text: Saŋ ko̱ bo̱ fo̱‑ɔ, ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ dɛɛ bo̱ gya Kristoo ase̱ŋ ke̱kaapo̱ se̱‑ɔ a waa ko̱kyɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Ne̱ e̱kɔ a lwee Yudaa awuye ne̱ baa ko̱we̱ bamo̱ e̱maŋ ne̱ ba sa Griiki se̱ŋsa e̱mo̱‑ɔ‑rɔ na Yudaa awuye ne̱ baa ko̱we̱ bamo̱ bamo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ aye̱‑ɔ mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ. Bamo̱ ne̱ ba sa Griiki‑ro‑o yɛ akɔɔre̱gyipo̱‑ɔ i ke ateese‑ro kake ke̱maa, bamo̱ akpenlekye̱e̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ nya.

ref: The twelve apostles called the whole group of followers together and said, “We should not give up preaching God's message in order to serve at tables.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱ kudu anyɔ‑ɔ a te̱e̱ Kristoo ase̱ŋ ke̱kaapo̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱, ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “A mo̱ŋ bware fe̱yɛ ane̱ a yɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ ko̱tɔwe̱, na ane̱ i gyi ateese na atanne̱ ase̱ŋ.

ref: My friends, choose seven men who are respected and wise and filled with God's Spirit. We will put them in charge of these things.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, ane̱ ako̱so̱bɛɛ, mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱re̱ mo̱ne̱‑rɔ, na mo̱nꞌ lee abɛɛ asunoo ne̱ mo̱ne̱ nyi fe̱yɛ bo̱ de Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o na kanyiase̱ŋ‑ɔ, na ane̱ sa a bamo̱ e̱ de̱ŋ bo̱ de̱e̱re̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: We can spend our time praying and serving God by preaching.”
text: Na ane̱ fe̱raa ane̱ kusuŋ kii fe̱yɛ ane̱ de̱e̱ kya se̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱, ne̱ ane̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ.”

ref: This suggestion pleased everyone, and they began by choosing Stephen. He had great faith and was filled with the Holy Spirit. Then they chose Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and also Nicolaus, who worshiped with the Jewish people in Antioch.
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ akatɔ a gyi mfɛɛre̱ timaa ne̱ ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ a baa‑ɔ se̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa lee Stifaŋ ne̱ mò̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi‑ro bo̱ le̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ o de Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ na Filipo na Prokorus na Nikanɔɔ na Timɔŋ na Paame̱nas na Nikolas ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaanyi, ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ Ante̱yɔke̱ya maŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ saŋ ko̱ ɔɔ ba o suŋ Wuribware̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yudaa awuye i suŋ‑o.

ref: These men were brought to the apostles. Then the apostles prayed and placed their hands on the men to show they had been chosen to do this work.
text: Ne̱ akɔɔre̱gyipo̱‑ɔ a taa bamo̱ ya bo̱ kaapo̱ ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ bamo̱‑ɔ baa ba asare̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ bamo̱ aŋu si, ne̱ baa ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ sa bamo̱.

ref: God's message spread, and many more people in Jerusalem became followers. Even a large number of priests put their faith in the Lord. Stephen Is Arrested
text: Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ a kya se̱ yɔ to̱ŋ ke̱maa. Ne̱ akɔɔre̱gyipo̱ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ a waa ko̱kyɔ. Ne̱ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ a bo̱ kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi bo̱ tii bamo̱ se̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kra Stifaŋ‑o

ref: God gave Stephen the power to work great miracles and wonders among the people.
text: Ne̱ Stifaŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a sa ŋyure na e̱le̱ŋ‑ɔ a kya alɔpo̱, ne̱ ɔɔ waa atɔ dabe̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ, ne̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ baa ŋu‑o e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ bamo̱.

ref: But some men from Cyrene and Alexandria were members of a group who called themselves “Free Men.” They started arguing with Stephen. Some others from Cilicia and Asia also argued with him.
text: Amɔ Yudaa awuye ko̱ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ bo̱ dɛɛ bo̱ gye̱ anya, ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ bo̱ gye̱ agyi. Ne̱ bo̱ de bamo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ ba suŋ Wuribware̱ ke̱mo̱‑rɔ. Bamo̱‑ɔ‑rɔ bo̱ko̱ mo̱ŋ sure si, ne̱ baa gyiiri Stifaŋ ikii. Bamo̱ bo̱ko̱ gye̱ Yudaa awuye ne̱ baa le̱e̱ Kireenee na Alɛsandriya e̱maŋ‑nɔ na Kilikiya na Asiya e̱swe̱e̱re̱ se̱.

ref: But they were no match for Stephen, who spoke with the great wisdom that the Spirit gave him.
text: Amaa Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a sa Stifaŋ kanyiase̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, mò̱ a sa se̱ŋsa‑ɔ, bo̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ taare̱ tɔwe̱ sɛye̱ bo̱ gyiiri mò̱ ikii.

ref: So they talked some men into saying, “We heard Stephen say terrible things against Moses and God!”
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa sa ase̱sɛ ko̱ atanne̱ na bo̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ a nu mò̱ a tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ o kye Wuribware̱ na ane̱ mbraa ne̱ Wuribware̱ a bo̱rɔ Mosis si bo̱ sa ane̱‑ɔ.”

ref: They turned the people and their leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses against Stephen. Then they all grabbed him and dragged him in front of the council.
text: Mfaanɛɛ ne̱ baa waa bo̱ fwii ase̱sɛ na abre̱sɛ na Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ iduŋ. Ne̱ baa kra Stifaŋ yaa maŋ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ na abre̱sɛ‑ɔ akatɔ‑rɔ.

ref: Some men agreed to tell lies about Stephen, and they said, “This man keeps on saying terrible things about this holy temple and the Law of Moses.
text: Ne̱ baa ba ase̱sɛ ko̱ ne̱ baa bo̱ ba aye̱ba bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ mò̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ, “Saŋ ke̱maa ɔnyare̱ mɔ e̱ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ bɔye̱ o kye Wuribware̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ na Wuribware̱ mbraa ne̱ ane̱ a nya le̱e̱ Mosis ase̱‑ɔ.

ref: We have heard him claim that Jesus from Nazareth will destroy this place and change the customs Moses gave us.”
text: Ane̱ a nu mò̱ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ Yeesuu Nasarɛte̱nyi‑o i bwee Wuribware̱ suŋkpa, na o kyurowi mbraa ne̱ Mosis a sa ane̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ!”

ref: Then all the council members stared at Stephen. They saw that his face looked like the face of an angel.
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ te se̱ŋgyikpa mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a kii bamo̱ akatɔ bo̱ ye̱ra Stifaŋ si ba de̱e̱re̱ mò̱. Ne̱ baa ŋu fe̱yɛ mò̱ akatɔ‑rɔ du fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ lee‑o.

ref: The high priest asked Stephen, “Are they telling the truth about you?”
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ‑ɔ a bise Stifaŋ ɛ, “Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ba po̱rɔ fo̱ ke̱mo̱ se̱‑ɔ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ aaa?”

ref: Stephen answered: Friends, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he had moved to Haran.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ, “N se̱‑ana na n daa‑ana na n tire‑ana, mo̱nꞌ nu mo̱ ase̱! Pwɛɛ ne̱ ane̱ nana Abraham a dɛɛ tu ya kye̱na Haraŋ maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, Wuribware̱, owure dabe̱‑ɔ, a lee mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ mò̱ Mesopotamiya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Kalade̱ya awuye te‑o.

ref: God told him, “Leave your country and your relatives and go to a land that I will show you.”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Le̱e̱ swe̱e̱re̱ mɔ se̱ na fo̱ yɔwe̱ fo̱ ako̱we̱bɛɛ na fo̱ yɔ swe̱e̱re̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ fo̱‑ɔ se̱.’

ref: Then Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After his father died, Abraham came and settled in this land where you now live.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ mò̱ swe̱e̱re̱‑ɔ se̱ ya kye̱na Haraŋ. Abraham mò̱ se̱ lowi kamɛɛ, ne̱ Wuribware̱ a sa ne̱ Abraham a be̱e̱ tu bo̱ kye̱na swe̱e̱re̱ mɔ mfe̱e̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ te mò̱ se̱‑ɔ.

ref: God didn't give him any part of it, not even a square meter. But God did promise to give it to him and his family forever, even though Abraham didn't have any children.
text: Saŋ‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ taa hare̱e̱ swe̱e̱re̱‑ɔ kabe̱gyɛɛ gbaa sa Abraham, amaa ɔɔ tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ ɔ saŋ ɔ taa a ɔ bo̱ sa mò̱ aa mò̱ ananagyi. Saŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ mɔ‑ɔ, na Abraham mo̱ŋ de agyi.

ref: God said Abraham's descendants would live for a while in a foreign land. There they would be slaves and would be mistreated
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Fo̱ ananagyi e̱ ba a bo̱ bo̱ kii afɔ swe̱e̱re̱ baŋbaŋ ko̱ se̱, na bo̱ waa mfe̱ŋ awuye‑o anya kusuŋ, na bo̱ waa bamo̱ bo̱rɔkraa nsu alafa ana.

ref: But he also said, “I will punish the nation that makes them slaves. Then later they will come and worship me in this place.”
text: Amaa mo̱ i gyiiri bamo̱ anyaŋpe̱‑ɔ ke̱se̱bɔ, na ke̱mo̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ na n lee bamo̱ le̱e̱ swe̱e̱re̱ amo̱ se̱, na bo̱ bo̱ suŋ mo̱ mfe̱e̱.’

ref: These men were our ancestors. Joseph was also one of our famous ancestors. His brothers were jealous of him and sold him as a slave to be taken to Egypt. But God was with him
text: Yakubu mò̱ gyi‑ana a bo̱ waa ayaafɔre̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa kisi bamo̱ tire Yo̱sɛf, ne̱ baa fe mò̱ sa akpakyo̱ŋpo̱ ko̱, ne̱ bamo̱‑ɔ a fe mò̱ sa Igyipiti awuye ko̱, ne̱ bamo̱‑ɔ baa ba mò̱ bo̱ kii ke̱nya. Amaa Wuribware̱ a gya mò̱ kamɛɛ.

ref: and rescued him from all his troubles. God made him so wise that the Egyptian king Pharaoh thought highly of him. The king even made Joseph governor over Egypt and put him in charge of everything he owned.
text: Ne̱ oo lee mò̱ le̱e̱ ase̱ŋ‑nɔ. Saŋ ne̱ baa te̱e̱ mò̱ Igyipiti owure akatɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ a sa ne̱ owure‑o akatɔ a gyi mò̱ se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ owure‑o a pini Yo̱sɛf kanyiase̱ŋ, ne̱ ɔɔ waa Yo̱sɛf mò̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ ayaafɔre̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ bre̱sɛ na Igyipiti swe̱e̱re̱ pɛɛɛ odikuroo.

ref: Everywhere in Egypt and Canaan the grain crops failed. There was terrible suffering, and our ancestors could not find enough to eat.
text: Ne̱ ako̱ŋ a ba Igyipiti na Kanaŋ e̱swe̱e̱re̱ se̱, ne̱ baa ŋu ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Ne̱ ane̱ ade̱daapo̱‑ɔ a kpa ateese kpo̱ne̱.

ref: But when Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time.
text: Saŋ ne̱ bamo̱ se̱ Yakubu a nu fe̱yɛ abwaye bo̱ Igyipiti‑o, ne̱ oo suŋ mò̱ gyi‑ana, ane̱ ade̱daapo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa yɔ mfe̱ŋ ke̱gye̱ŋkpɛɛsɛ, ne̱ baa ya nya abwaye.

ref: It was on their second trip that Joseph told his brothers who he was, and the king learned about Joseph's family.
text: Bamo̱ a yɔ mfe̱ŋ nyɔse̱po̱‑ɔ, ne̱ bamo̱ tire Yo̱sɛf a lee mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ tire e̱ gye̱ mò̱, ne̱ Igyipiti owure mɔ a pini saŋ amo̱ fe̱yɛ Yo̱sɛf ako̱we̱bɛɛ ne̱e̱.

ref: His father went to Egypt and died there, just as our ancestors did.
text: Ne̱ Yakubu‑ana a ya kye̱na Igyipiti mfe̱ŋ ane̱ŋ‑aaa, ne̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ a bo̱ wu.

ref: Later their bodies were taken back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had bought from the sons of Hamor.
text: Amo̱‑ɔ kamɛɛ‑rɔ, ne̱ baa taa bamo̱ abowii yaa Yakubu aye̱ Sikim mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ dɛɛ o te‑o, ne̱ baa taa yaa bo̱ pure purekpa ne̱ Abraham a sɔɔ le̱e̱ Hamɔɔ mò̱ gyi‑ana ase̱‑ɔ.

ref: Finally, the time came for God to do what he had promised Abraham. By then the number of our people in Egypt had greatly increased.
text: Ane̱ ade̱daapo̱ a kye̱na Igyipiti swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ mfe̱ŋ ane̱ŋ‑aaa, ne̱ baa waa ko̱kyɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Ne̱ ŋkee saŋ a fo̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ lee mò̱ ase̱sɛ le̱e̱ swe̱e̱re̱ amo̱ se̱, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ kyɔ tɔwe̱ gywii Abraham bo̱ be̱ya‑ɔ.

ref: Another king was ruling Egypt, and he didn't know anything about Joseph.
text: Amo̱ lalalo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱ ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ nyi Yo̱sɛf ase̱ŋ‑ɔ a bo̱ gyi kuwure.

ref: During this time Moses was born. He was a very beautiful child, and for three months his parents took care of him in their home.
text: Saŋ mɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ baa ko̱we̱ Mosis, kayaagyi po̱pwɛɛ dame̱naŋsɛ. Aferi asa ne̱ baa be̱ra mò̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ.

ref: Then when they were forced to leave him outside, the king's daughter found him and raised him as her own son.
text: Bamo̱ a lee mò̱ le̱e̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ taa yaa bo̱ kwe̱e̱rɔ owure‑o si‑o, ne̱ owure kagyikye̱e̱sɛɛ a ya ŋu mò̱ taa mò̱ yaa be̱ra fe̱yɛ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ mò̱ gyi‑o.

ref: Moses was given the best education in Egypt. He was a strong man and a powerful speaker.
text: Ne̱ baa kaapo̱ mò̱ bamo̱ Igyipiti awuye kanyiase̱ŋ, ne̱ oo kii se̱sɛ dabe̱ mò̱ se̱ŋsa na asuŋ waasɛ‑rɔ.

ref: One day he saw an Egyptian mistreating one of them. So he rescued the man and killed the Egyptian.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ ya to̱ fe̱yɛ Igyipitinyi ko̱ e̱ waa Isireeliinyi ko̱ bo̱rɔkraa. Mò̱ a yɔ mfe̱ŋ a ɔ ya kya mò̱ aye̱po̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ mɔɔ Igyipitinyi‑o bo̱ gyiiri mò̱ ke̱se̱bɔ.

ref: Moses thought the rest of his people would realize God was going to use him to set them free. But they didn't understand.
text: Ɔɔ fa ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ aye̱ awuye‑o i nu kaase̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ e̱ ba mò̱ ne̱e̱ a ɔ bo̱ kɔɔre̱ bamo̱ na bo̱ nya bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱, amaa bo̱ mo̱ŋ nu kaase̱ ane̱ŋ.

ref: The next day Moses saw two of his own people fighting, and he tried to make them stop. He said, “Men, you are both Israelites. Why are you so cruel to each other?”
text: Ke̱mo̱ kaye̱ ŋke‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ ya ŋu fe̱yɛ Isireelii awuye anyɔ wo̱re̱ wo̱re̱ e̱ kɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ ya to̱rɔwe̱ bamo̱ a ɔ lɔŋŋɔ bamo̱ mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ. Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ yɛ, ‘E̱me̱ne̱ ne̱e̱ ne̱ fo̱ aa fo̱ ko̱we̱bɛɛ Heebriinyi e̱ kɔ?’

ref: But the man who had started the fight pushed Moses aside and asked, “Who made you our ruler and judge?
text: Amaa mò̱ ne̱ ɔ waa mò̱ bɛɛko̱ bo̱rɔkraa‑ɔ a neŋ Mosis ne̱e̱ le̱e̱ keri si, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, ‘Nsɛ ya ba fo̱ bo̱ waa ane̱ ɔde̱e̱re̱se̱po̱ na ɔse̱ŋgyipo̱?

ref: Are you going to kill me, just as you killed that Egyptian yesterday?”
text: Fo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ fo̱ mɔɔ mo̱ fe̱yɛ ndee ane̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ a mɔɔ Igyipitinyi‑o ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱?’

ref: When Moses heard this, he ran away to live in the country of Midian. His two sons were born there.
text: Mosis a de̱ŋ nu ane̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ se̱re̱ le̱e̱ Igyipiti swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ ya kye̱na Midiyaŋ swe̱e̱re̱ se̱. Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ ko̱we̱ agyi anyɔ.

ref: Forty years later, an angel appeared to Moses from a burning bush in the desert near Mount Sinai.
text: Nsu aduna kamɛɛ‑rɔ, ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ a lee mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ kiyii ko̱ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ kywɛɛ kimukee‑ro‑o‑ro, me̱raa Sinaa ke̱be̱e̱‑ɔ bo̱ kaapo̱ Mosis.

ref: Moses was surprised by what he saw. He went closer to get a better look, and the Lord said,
text: Mosis a ŋu ane̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ mò̱, ne̱ ɔɔ sɔŋ tɔ kiyii‑o‑ro, na ɔ nya ŋu ane̱ŋ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ kywɛɛ‑ɔ nɛɛnɛɛ. Ne̱ oo nu Wuribware̱ bo̱re̱ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ,

ref: “I am the God who was worshiped by your ancestors, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” Moses started shaking all over and didn't dare to look at the bush.
text: ‘Mo̱ e̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱, nyaŋpe̱ ne̱ fo̱ nana‑ana Abraham na Isak na Yakubu i suŋ‑o.’ Ne̱ kufu a nya Mosis, ne̱ ɔɔ yo̱ŋ, ŋkee ɔ maa lɛɛ taare̱ a ɔ de̱e̱re̱ kiyii ne̱ ke̱ e̱ kywɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: The Lord said to him, “Take off your sandals, because the place where you are standing is holy.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ye̱re̱ mfe̱ŋ. Lee fo̱ ase̱be̱ta‑ɔ. Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ ye̱re̱‑ɔ gye̱ swe̱e̱re̱ kpe̱yaa ne̱e̱.

ref: With my own eyes I have seen the suffering of my people in Egypt. I have heard their groans and have come down to rescue them. Now I am sending you back to Egypt.”
text: Kase̱ŋtiŋ, mo̱ a ŋu awo̱re̱fɔɔ ne̱ mo̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ i gyi Igyipiti swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ. Mo̱ a nu bamo̱ kusu bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ bamo̱ anya ade̱e̱re̱se̱po̱‑ɔ e̱ waa bamo̱‑ɔ se̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱ a ba a m bo̱ kɔɔre̱ bamo̱ le̱e̱ Igyipiti awuye ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ, Amo̱se̱‑ɔ waa me̱naŋ, nare̱, mo̱ i suŋ fo̱ a m be̱e̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ Igyipiti swe̱e̱re̱ se̱.’

ref: This was the same Moses that the people rejected by saying, “Who made you our leader and judge?” God's angel had spoken to Moses from the bush. And God had even sent the angel to help Moses rescue the people and be their leader.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ Mosis mɔ ne̱ Isireelii awuye‑o a kine, ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Nsɛ ya waa fo̱ ane̱ ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ na ɔse̱ŋgyipo̱?’ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a lee fe̱yɛ mò̱ e̱ gye̱ bamo̱ ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ na ɔmo̱rɔwe̱po̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ Wuribware̱ a suŋ mò̱ mbɔɔ‑ɔ ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ mò̱ se̱ kiyii ne̱ ke̱ e̱ kywɛɛ‑ɔ se̱.

ref: In Egypt and at the Red Sea and in the desert, Moses rescued the people by working miracles and wonders for
text: Mosis mɔ dɛɛ ya waa afutɔ na akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ Igyipiti swe̱e̱re̱ se̱, ne̱ oo lee Isireelii awuye‑o le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ yaa kimukee‑ro. Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ waa afutɔ na akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ Ɔpo̱o̱ Pipee‑o‑ro na kimukee‑ro mfe̱ŋ nsu aduna.

ref: Moses is the one who told the people of Israel, “God will choose one of your people to be a prophet, just as he chose me.”
text: Mosis mɔ dɛɛ ya tɔwe̱ gywii Isireelii awuye fe̱yɛ, ‘Wuribware̱ i lee se̱sɛ ko̱ a ɔ le̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ ako̱we̱bɛɛ‑rɔ, se̱sɛ ne̱ o du fe̱yɛ mo̱ Mosis‑o, fe̱yɛ ɔ waa mò̱ kyaamɛɛ sa mo̱ne̱.’

ref: Moses brought our people together in the desert, and the angel spoke to him on Mount Sinai. There he was given these life-giving words to pass on to us.
text: Mosis dɛɛ na Isireelii awuye ya kpɔwe̱ gyaŋŋe̱ kimukee‑o‑ro. Mosis wo̱re̱ ne̱ ane̱ ade̱daapo̱ a taare̱ bo̱rɔ se̱ sa se̱ŋsa gywii Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ mò̱ se̱ Sinaa ke̱be̱e̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ. Mosis mɔ dɛɛ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a be̱e̱ bo̱rɔ se̱ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ a sa ŋkpa‑ɔ gywii ane̱ ade̱daapo̱ na ane̱.

ref: But our ancestors refused to obey Moses. They rejected him and wanted to go back to Egypt.
text: Amaa ane̱ ade̱daapo̱‑ɔ a kine, bo̱ mo̱ŋ bu mò̱, ne̱ baa lee mò̱ keri si, ne̱ baa yo̱rɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ kiŋŋi yɔ Igyipiti.

ref: Then they made an idol in the shape of a calf. They offered sacrifices to the idol and were pleased with what they had done.
text: Saŋ amo̱ ne̱ baa pwɛɛ bamo̱ kisi ko̱ fe̱yɛ kakpe̱ŋnyare̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa mɔɔ mbo̱ we̱e̱ se̱, ne̱ baa gyi ateese nuu nta, ne̱ baa kyaa bo̱ gyi akatɔ sa bamo̱ kisi amo̱ ne̱ baa waa‑ɔ.

ref: God turned his back on his people and left them. Then they worshiped the stars in the sky, just as it says in the Book of the Prophets, “People of Israel, you didn't offer sacrifices and offerings to me during those
text: Amo̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a yɔwe̱ bamo̱ ase̱ŋ sa bamo̱. Ne̱ baa suŋ akye̱e̱‑e̱‑kpa‑agyi na kiferi na kyɔwe̱ kyo̱ŋ Wuribware̱. Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ ɔko̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ‑ɔ, ‘Isireelii awuye, saŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a kaa kimukee‑o‑ro nsu aduna‑ɔ, n gye̱ mo̱, Wuribware̱, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a mɔɔ mbo̱ bo̱ lɔŋŋɔ.

ref: Instead, you carried the tent where the god Molech is worshiped, and you took along the star of your god Rephan. You made those idols and worshiped them. So now I will have you carried off beyond Babylonia.”
text: Kisi Mɔlɔk suŋkpa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a so̱rɔ, na kisi Rifaŋ ne̱ ku du fe̱yɛ ke̱kye̱e̱‑e̱‑kpa‑agyi ne̱ mo̱ne̱ fɔŋfɔŋ a ba mo̱ne̱ asare̱e̱ bo̱ waa‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a suŋ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱ e̱ gya mo̱ne̱ a ŋ yaa anya kusuŋ‑no swe̱e̱re̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ Babilɔŋ maŋ kamɛɛ ke̱fɔ ke̱fɔ‑ɔ se̱.’

ref: The tent where our ancestors worshiped God was with them in the desert. This was the same tent that God had commanded Moses to make. And it was made like the model that Moses had seen.
text: Wuribware̱ ya kaapo̱ Mosis ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ waa Wuribware̱ kye̱nakpa, mfe̱ŋ ne̱ e̱taŋ a ke̱dakaa‑ɔ ye̱re̱‑rɔ‑ɔ. Ɔ yɛ ɔ waa mò̱ fe̱yɛ taŋte̱e̱ ke̱kyaŋ na bo̱ taare̱ so̱rɔ mò̱ to̱ŋ ke̱maa na ɔ gye̱ bamo̱ ke̱gye̱ŋkpɛɛ kimukee‑o‑ro‑o.

ref: Later it was given to our ancestors, and they took it with them when they went with Joshua. They carried the tent along as they took over the land from those people that God had chased out for them. Our ancestors used this tent until the time of King David.
text: Saŋ ne̱ Mosis a wu‑o, ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ ne̱ ɔ gya se̱ ɔnyɔse̱po̱‑ɔ, mò̱ e̱ gye̱ Yosuwa. Yosuwa mɔ a gye̱ bamo̱ ke̱gye̱ŋkpɛɛ yaa bamo̱ swe̱e̱re̱ po̱pwɛɛ se̱, ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kɔ gyi ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ kyɔ bo̱ te mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱, mɔɔ bamo̱ kpuri. Saŋ ke̱maa mɔ ne̱ ane̱ ade̱daapo̱ naa‑ɔ, bo̱ so̱ taŋte̱e̱ ke̱kyaŋ amo̱. Ne̱ kaa sii swe̱e̱re̱ amo̱ se̱ ane̱ŋ‑aaa bo̱ fo̱ Owure Deefid a mbe̱e̱‑ɔ se̱.

ref: He pleased God and asked him if he could build a house of worship for the people of Israel.
text: Wuribware̱ a kpa Deefid ase̱ŋ, ne̱ oo yure mò̱. Ne̱ Deefid a ko̱re̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ sa a ɔ pwɛɛ kye̱nakpa ne̱ ba ba a bo̱ bo̱ te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ ‘Suŋkpa ne̱ Yakubu nyaŋpe̱ Wuribware̱ te mò̱‑rɔ‑ɔ.’

ref: And it was finally King Solomon who built a house for God.
text: Amaa Wuribware̱ mo̱ŋ sure si ane̱ŋ. Amɔ Solomɔŋ, Deefid mò̱ gyi‑o, ya bo̱ pwɛɛ suŋkpa sa Wuribware̱.

ref: But the Most High God doesn't live in houses made by humans. It is just as the prophet said, when he spoke for the Lord,
text: Amaa Nyiŋkpe̱ŋ-kyo̱ŋ-atɔ‑pɛɛɛ-Bware̱ mo̱ŋ te lɔŋ ne̱ se̱sɛ a pwɛɛ‑ɔ‑rɔ. Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ ɔko̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ,

ref: “Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me? In what place will I rest?
text: ‘Wuribware̱ yɛ, So̱so̱ e̱ gye̱ mo̱ kuwuregya, ne̱ swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ gye̱ mo̱ ayaa ye̱re̱se̱kpa. Ne̱ nte̱tɔ lɔŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ pwɛɛ a mo̱nꞌ sa mo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱? Mfe̱ne̱ e̱ gye̱ mo̱ kyurekpa?

ref: Is there one prophet that your ancestors didn't mistreat? They killed the prophets who told about the coming of the One Who Obeys God. And now you have turned against him and killed him.
text: Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ ko̱ bo̱‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ ba, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ waa mò̱ awo̱re̱fɔɔ aaa? Bo̱ mo̱ŋ yɔwe̱ ke̱mɔɔ bamo̱ ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ i suŋ mò̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱ timaa Kristoo‑o gbaa. Ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ mo̱ne̱ a gyi mò̱ ne̱ oo suŋ mò̱‑ɔ kidiburo, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a mɔɔ mò̱.

ref: When the council members heard Stephen's speech, they were angry and furious.
text: Amɔ bamo̱ ne̱ baa gyaŋŋe̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ a nu ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱‑ɔ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ bamo̱ iduŋ a fwii, ne̱ baa duŋwi bamo̱ nnɔ.

ref: But Stephen was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked toward heaven, where he saw our glorious God and Jesus standing at his right side.
text: Amaa Stifaŋ de Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o bwe̱e̱tɔ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mò̱ a de̱e̱re̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ, ne̱ oo ŋu Wuribware̱ se̱ a buŋŋi‑ro, ne̱ oo ŋu Wuribware̱ a ke̱dabe̱ na wuraa‑o, ne̱ oo ŋu fe̱yɛ Yeesuu a ye̱re̱ Wuribware̱ kigyise si.

ref: Then Stephen said, “I see heaven open and the Son of Man standing at the right side of God!”
text: Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱re̱! Ŋ ke̱e̱ so̱so̱ a buŋŋi‑ro, ne̱ ŋ ke̱e̱ dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o na ɔ ye̱re̱ Wuribware̱ kigyise si!”

ref: The council members shouted and covered their ears. At once they all attacked Stephen
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa gyaŋŋe̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ ɔke̱maa a ba mò̱ asare̱e̱gyi bo̱ tii mò̱ e̱se̱bɔ, ne̱ baa faa‑rɔ keŋkeŋ, ne̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ a se̱re̱ ya buŋ Stifaŋ si.

ref: and dragged him out of the city. Then they started throwing stones at him. The men who had brought charges against him put their coats at the feet of a young man named Saul.
text: Ne̱ baa lee mò̱ le̱e̱ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ yaa mò̱ nlaŋbɛɛ, ne̱ baa twe̱e̱ mò̱ abu. Ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa twe̱e̱ Stifaŋ abu‑o a taa bamo̱ awaagya sa ke̱yaafɔre̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Sɔɔl‑ɔ a ɔ kra.

ref: As Stephen was being stoned to death, he called out, “Lord Jesus, please welcome me!”
text: Ne̱ baa saŋ me̱raa se̱ ba twe̱e̱ Stifaŋ abu na ɔ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ ɔ te̱e̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu, kɔɔre̱ mo̱ kra!”

ref: He knelt down and shouted, “Lord, don't blame them for what they have done.” Then he died.
text: Ne̱ oo kpuni aŋurii, ne̱ ɔɔ faa‑rɔ keŋkeŋ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, fo̱ ma sa a ase̱sɛ mɔ a bɔye̱ mɔ sii bamo̱ se̱!” Mò̱ a tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ oo wu mfe̱ŋ.

ref: Saul started making a lot of trouble for the church. He went from house to house, arresting men and women and putting them in jail. The Good News Is Preached in Samaria
text: Amaa Sɔɔl a pee ke̱katɔ we̱e̱ asɔre̱e̱ awuye‑o si ko̱tɔɔraa. Ne̱ ŋkee ɔɔ nare̱ bamo̱ e̱lɔŋ‑nɔ a ɔ bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱ se̱ o gyiiri bamo̱, hare̱e̱ ɔkye̱e̱ bɛɛɛ ɔnyare̱ ooo, o tii. Abware̱se̱ŋ ne̱ Filipo a tɔwe̱ Samariya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ

ref: The Lord's followers who had been scattered went from place to place, telling the good news.
text: Ne̱ asɔre̱e̱ awuye ne̱ baa brawe̱‑rɔ‑ɔ a lweero yɔ to̱ŋ ke̱maa ba tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ.

ref: They crowded around Philip because they were eager to hear what he was saying and to see him work miracles.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ waa akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱. Amo̱se̱ se̱ ne̱ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ a gyaŋŋe̱ a bo̱ nu ase̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa lɔŋ e̱se̱bɔ nu mò̱ ase̱ nɛɛnɛɛ.

ref: Many people with evil spirits were healed, and the spirits went out of them with a shout. A lot of paralyzed and lame people were also healed.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ gya ibrisi bo̱ ko̱so̱ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ se̱ na e̱ fɛɛ‑rɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ kya agyabɔɔ na bamo̱ ne̱ bamo̱ ke̱sare̱e̱ bɛɛɛ ke̱yaa a wu‑o bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: Everyone in that city was very glad because of what was happening.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ kakatɔgyi a ba maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: For some time a man named Simon had lived there and had amazed the people of Samaria. He practiced witchcraft and claimed to be somebody great.
text: Saŋ‑ɔ mɔ, ɔnyare̱ ko̱ mɔ dɛɛ ɔ bo̱ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ, ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Simɔŋ, na o de kisibeŋ ɔ waa atɔ ne̱ a kpe̱ŋ e̱ye̱e̱‑ɔ ɔ kaapo̱ maŋ awuye amo̱. Ɔ yɛ ɔ gye̱ se̱sɛ dabe̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: For a long time, Simon had used witchcraft to amaze the people, and they kept crowding around him.
text: Ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ baa nu mò̱ ase̱ŋ nɛɛnɛɛ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ɔɔ ba mò̱ kisibeŋ‑o bo̱ waa akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ kyee.

ref: But when they believed what Philip was saying about God's kingdom and about the name of Jesus Christ, they were all baptized.
text: Amaa bamo̱ a nu Filipo a tɔwe̱ Wuribware̱ a kuwure‑o‑ro ase̱ŋ timaa na ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu Kristoo ke̱nyare̱ de ke̱yaale̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ gyi, ne̱ bamo̱ akye̱e̱ na anyare̱ pɛɛɛ a bɔ asuu.

ref: Even Simon believed and was baptized. He stayed close to Philip, because he marveled at all the miracles and wonders.
text: Ne̱ Simɔŋ fɔŋfɔŋ‑ɔ gbaa a kɔɔre̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ gyi. Ne̱ ɔɔ bɔ asuu, ne̱ ŋkee ɔ naa ɔ gya Filipo si, ne̱ akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ ne̱ Filipo a waa‑ɔ a kpe̱ŋ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱.

ref: The apostles in Jerusalem heard that some people in Samaria had accepted God's message, and they sent Peter and John.
text: Ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ a nu fe̱yɛ Samariya awuye‑o a kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ a ase̱ŋ‑ɔ gyi‑o, ne̱ baa suŋ Peetroo na Yohanee bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ bamo̱ ase̱.

ref: When the two apostles arrived, they prayed that these people would be given the Holy Spirit.
text: Bamo̱ a fo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ sa bamo̱, na Wuribware̱ nya sa bamo̱ mò̱ kufwiiŋe timaa‑o.

ref: Before this, the Holy Spirit had not been given to any of them, though some of them had been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ hare̱e̱ Filipo a bɔ bamo̱ asuu Yeesuu ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ‑ɔ gbaa ooo, Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o mo̱ŋ tɛɛ ywe̱e̱ bamo̱ ɔko̱ se̱ daa.

ref: Peter and John then placed their hands on everyone who had faith in the Lord, and they were given the Holy Spirit.
text: Ne̱ Peetroo na Yohanee a teyi bamo̱ asare̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ bamo̱ se̱, ne̱ baa nya Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o.

ref: Simon noticed that the Spirit was given only when the apostles placed their hands on the people. So he brought money
text: Simɔŋ a ŋu fe̱yɛ Peetroo na Yohanee a ba bamo̱ asare̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ akɔɔre̱gyipo̱ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱, ne̱ baa nya Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o si‑o, ne̱ oo ke Peetroo na Yohanee atanne̱.

ref: and said to Peter and John, “Let me have this power too! Then anyone I place my hands on will also be given the Holy Spirit.”
text: Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ sa ŋ gbaa mo̱ ane̱ŋ a ke̱yaale̱ŋ‑ɔ na ŋ gbaa, mo̱ ya ba ke̱sare̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ ɔko̱ se̱, na se̱sɛ‑ɔ nya Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o.”

ref: Peter said to him, “You and your money will both end up in hell if you think you can buy God's gift!
text: Amaa Peetroo a be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “Wuribware̱ sa a fo̱ aa fo̱ atanne̱ lwee de̱e̱kpa ne̱ oo kure bo̱ be̱ya na ɔ bo̱ kywɛɛ e̱bɔye̱ awaapo̱‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ a fa fe̱yɛ fo̱ e̱ taare̱ a fo̱ sɔɔ Wuribware̱ aketɔ‑ɔ.

ref: I can see that you are jealous and bound by your evil ways.”
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ fo̱ ko̱kwe̱e̱ ne̱ fo̱ de‑o kyɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ, ne̱ fo̱ e̱bɔye̱ ne̱ fo̱ de‑o mɔ kyɔ fo̱ se̱.”

ref: After Peter and John had preached about the Lord, they returned to Jerusalem. On their way they told the good news in many villages of Samaria. Philip and an Ethiopian Official
text: Peetroo na Yohanee a tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Yeesuu a waa bamo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ akatɔ‑rɔ na abware̱se̱ŋ gywii ase̱sɛ‑ɔ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa kiŋŋi yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ. Bamo̱ a kiŋŋi ba yɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ gywii Samariya e̱maŋ e̱maŋ‑nɔ awuye. Filipo na Itiyopiya krakye̱e̱ bre̱sɛ ko̱ ase̱ŋ

ref: The Lord's angel said to Philip, “Go south along the desert road that leads from Jerusalem to Gaza.”
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ a le̱e̱ so̱so̱ bo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii Filipo fe̱yɛ, “Waa siraa yɔ kyɔwe̱ twɛɛkpa ke̱be̱nasare̱e̱ se̱ ya bo̱rɔ kpa ne̱ ɔ le̱e̱ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ ɔ yɔ Gasa maŋ‑nɔ.”

ref: So Philip left. An important Ethiopian official happened to be going along that road in his chariot. He was the chief treasurer for Candace, the Queen of Ethiopia. The official had gone to Jerusalem to worship
text: Ne̱ Filipo a waa siraa, ne̱ ɔɔ ywe̱e̱ kpa‑ɔ se̱. Amɔ swe̱e̱re̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Itiyopiya‑o krakye̱e̱ bre̱sɛ ko̱ ne̱ ɔ de̱e̱re̱ bamo̱ wurekye̱e̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Kaŋdakee‑o atanne̱ se̱‑ɔ te mò̱ kpaŋa tireelaa ko̱‑rɔ. Mò̱ a kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ gyi‑o si‑o ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ a ɔ ya suŋ Wuribware̱ mò̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ mfe̱ŋ. Ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ o kiŋŋi ɔ yɔ pe̱.

ref: and was now on his way home. He was sitting in his chariot, reading the book of the prophet Isaiah.
text: Mò̱ a te‑ro‑o, na ɔ kare̱ Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ Isaya a ase̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ keŋkeŋ ɔ yɔ.

ref: Philip ran up close and heard the man reading aloud from the book of Isaiah. Philip asked him, “Do you understand what you are reading?”
text: Ne̱ Filipo a yɔ mò̱ ase̱ ya nu fe̱yɛ ɔnyare̱ amo̱ e̱ kare̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ ke̱be̱gya ne̱ Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ Isaya a kyo̱rɛɛ‑ɔ. Ne̱ oo bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ i nu ke̱tɔ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kare̱‑ɔ kaase̱ aaa?”

ref: The official answered, “How can I understand unless someone helps me?” He then invited Philip to come up and sit beside him.
text: Ne̱ Itiyopiya krakye̱e̱ bre̱sɛ amo̱ a be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “E̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa a n nu kaase̱ na ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ kaapo̱ mo̱?” Ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ Filipo a de̱e̱ lweero kye̱na mò̱ ase̱, ne̱ baa kya se̱ ba yɔ.

ref: The man was reading the passage that said, “He was led like a sheep on its way to be killed. He was silent as a lamb whose wool is being cut off, and he did not say a word.
text: Wo̱re̱ ne̱ ɔ kare̱‑ɔ‑rɔ, Isaya a kyo̱rɛɛ ne̱e̱ ɛ, “Ɔɔ waa diŋŋ fe̱yɛ kasanne̱gyii ne̱ bo̱ de a bo̱ ya mɔɔ‑ɔ, bɛɛɛ ŋkɔ sanne̱ ne̱ ba te̱ŋŋe̱ mò̱ ifwii si, na ɔ maa su‑o, ɔ mo̱ŋ tɔwe̱ sɛye̱.

ref: He was treated like a nobody and did not receive a fair trial. How can he have children, if his life is snatched away?”
text: Ne̱ baa waa mò̱ awo̱re̱fɔɔ na mò̱ ase̱ŋ de kpa ne̱ bamo̱ mɔ baa kine bo̱ mo̱ŋ taa kase̱ŋtiŋ sa mò̱. Ɔko̱ maa taare̱ a ɔ kaapo̱ mò̱ ananagyi, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ baa te̱ŋ mò̱ ŋkpa se̱, lee mò̱ le̱e̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ.”

ref: The official said to Philip, “Tell me, was the prophet talking about himself or about someone else?”
text: To, ne̱ oo bise Filipo fe̱yɛ, “Tɔwe̱ se̱sɛ ne̱ kyaamɛɛ mɔ e̱ tɔwe̱ mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ gywii mo̱, mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ne̱ o de bɛɛɛ ɔko̱ aaa?”

ref: So Philip began at this place in the Scriptures and explained the good news about Jesus.
text: Ne̱ Filipo a kaapo̱ ɔnyare̱‑ɔ abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ kare̱‑ɔ tɔwe̱ Yeesuu kayo̱wɔre̱ se̱ ase̱ŋ gywii mò̱.

ref: As they were going along the road, they came to a place where there was some water. The official said, “Look! Here is some water. Why can't I be baptized?”
text: Ne̱ Filipo a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ ya kɔɔre̱ gyi fo̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ fe̱raa, fo̱ e̱ taare̱ a fo̱ bɔ asuu.” Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ a kɔɔre̱ gyi. Mo̱ a kɔɔre̱ gyi fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑o e̱ gye̱ Yeesuu Kristoo.”]

ref: He ordered the chariot to stop. Then they both went down into the water, and Philip baptized him.
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa ne̱ krakye̱e̱‑ɔ a sa ne̱ tireelaa ne̱ bo̱ te‑ro‑o a ye̱re̱, ne̱ mò̱ aa Filipo a le̱e̱ yɔ nkyu‑o‑ro, ne̱ Filipo a bɔ mò̱ asuu.

ref: After they had come out of the water, the Lord's Spirit took Philip away. The official never saw him again, but he was very happy as he went on his way.
text: Bamo̱ ke̱de̱ŋ le̱e̱ nkyu‑o‑ro, ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o a taa Filipo o de o yii. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ krakye̱e̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ ŋu mò̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ kya mò̱ kpa se̱ yɔ pe̱ na mò̱ akatɔ a gyi.

ref: Philip later appeared in Azotus. He went from town to town, all the way to Caesarea, telling people about Jesus.
text: Amaa Filipo bo̱ sa se̱ ɔ bo̱ Ase̱dɔd maŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ kya se̱ yɔ Kaye̱sare̱ya maŋ‑nɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ timaa‑o kpa‑rɔ gywii maŋ ke̱maa se̱ awuye.

ref: Saul preached with such power that he completely confused the Jewish people in Damascus, as he tried to show them that Jesus is the Messiah.
