text: Amaa kake ke̱maa mɔ na Sɔɔl a ke̱kaapo̱‑ɔ‑rɔ e̱ waa le̱ŋ ki tii si. Mò̱ a kaapo̱‑rɔ nɛɛnɛɛ ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ Yeesuu e̱ gye̱ Kristoo ne̱ Wuribware̱ a suŋ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ kaye̱‑rɔ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ ɔ mo̱rɔwe̱ bamo̱‑ɔ, Yudaa awuye ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Damasikus mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ mo̱ŋ taare̱ lee kanɔ ko̱.

ref: Now get up and go into the city, where you will be told what to do.”
text: Amaa, ko̱so̱ yɔ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ na ɔko̱ kaapo̱ fo̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ fo̱ waa mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ.”

ref: I will show him how much he must suffer for worshiping in my name.”
text: Na mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ kaapo̱ mò̱ awo̱re̱fɔɔ ne̱ o kperi kusuŋ ne̱ ɔ waa a ɔ sa mo̱‑ɔ se̱ na o gyi‑o.”

ref: When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the followers. But they were all afraid of him, because they did not believe he was a true follower.
text: Sɔɔl a fo̱ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔ kpa a o tii bamo̱ ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi‑o si. Amaa bo̱ mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi fe̱yɛ ŋkee o tii bamo̱ se̱, ne̱ kufu a nya bamo̱ pɛɛɛ.

ref: and he has seen a vision. He saw a man named Ananias coming to him and putting his hands on him, so he could see again.”
text: Ne̱ oo ŋu dee‑ro fe̱yɛ ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ Ananiyas‑o a lweero bo̱ ba mò̱ ke̱sare̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ mò̱ se̱ a mò̱ akatɔ nya be̱e̱ buŋŋi.”

ref: Suddenly something like fish scales fell from Saul's eyes, and he could see. He got up and was baptized.
text: Puri amo̱‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ atɔ ko̱ fe̱yɛ ŋkiŋgyi agyafo̱‑ɔ a le̱e̱ Sɔɔl akatɔgyi‑ro le̱e̱ da, ne̱ mò̱ akatɔ a buŋŋi waa fe̱yɛ saŋ‑ɔ ane̱ŋ ne̱ a ke̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ ko̱so̱ ye̱re̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ bɔ asuu.

ref: When Saul had almost reached Damascus, a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around him.
text: Sɔɔl a yɔ ɔ tɔ Damasikus maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, kaluwa ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱ ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ ko̱ fe̱yɛ e̱lɛɛlɛɛ‑ɔ a le̱e̱ awo̱re̱‑rɔ bo̱ we̱e̱ mò̱ se̱.

ref: He fell to the ground and heard a voice saying, “Saul! Saul! Why are you so cruel to me?”
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ da swe̱e̱re̱, ne̱ oo nu bo̱re̱ ko̱ a tɔwe̱ o gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Sɔɔl, nte̱tɔ ne̱e̱ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ tɔɔraa mo̱ ane̱ŋ?”

ref: “Who are you?” Saul asked. “I am Jesus,” the Lord answered. “I am the one you are so cruel to.
text: Ne̱ Sɔɔl a bise fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, fo̱ nsɛ ne̱e̱?” Ne̱ bo̱re̱ amo̱ a be̱ŋŋaa ɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ gye̱ Yeesuu ne̱ fo̱ e̱ tɔɔraa mo̱‑ɔ.

ref: The men with Saul stood there speechless. They had heard the voice, but they had not seen anyone.
text: Amaa, ase̱sɛ ne̱ bamo̱ aa mò̱ naa kpa‑ɔ‑rɔ mo̱ŋ buŋŋi kanɔ, ne̱ baa sii ye̱re̱. Baa nu bo̱re̱‑ɔ, bamo̱ mɔ bo̱ mo̱ŋ ŋu ɔko̱.

ref: Saul got up from the ground, and when he opened his eyes, he could not see a thing. Someone then led him by the hand to Damascus,
text: Ne̱ Sɔɔl a ko̱so̱ kyuwi‑ro, amaa mò̱ a buŋŋi akatɔ‑ɔ, ɔ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ ɔ ke̱e̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ mò̱ aa bamo̱ naa‑ɔ a kra mò̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ yaa bo̱ lwee Damasikus maŋ‑nɔ.

ref: and for three days he was blind and did not eat or drink.
text: Amo̱ ŋke nsa na ɔ mo̱ŋ ke̱e̱, na ɔ mo̱ŋ gyi, ne̱ mò̱‑ɔ mò̱ mo̱ŋ nuu.

ref: A follower named Ananias lived in Damascus, and the Lord spoke to him in a vision. Ananias answered, “Lord, here I am.”
text: Saŋ amo̱, bamo̱ ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ gya Yeesuu a e̱kpa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ mɔ bo̱ Damasikus mfe̱ŋ, ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Ananiyas. Ne̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu a te̱e̱ mò̱ dee‑ro‑o fe̱yɛ, “Ananiyas!” Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱ŋŋaa ɛ “Ke̱e̱ mo̱, mo̱ nyaŋpe̱.”

ref: The Lord said to him, “Get up and go to the house of Judas on Straight Street. When you get there, you will find a man named Saul from the city of Tarsus. Saul is praying,
text: Ne̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Waa siraa na fo̱ yɔ kabo̱re̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ ‘Kabo̱re̱ kye̱rarasɛ’‑ɔ se̱ na fo̱ lwee ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Yudas a lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ bise ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ Taasus maŋ‑nɔ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Sɔɔl‑ɔ se̱. Ɔ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ ɔ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱.

ref: Ananias replied, “Lord, a lot of people have told me about the terrible things this man has done to your followers in Jerusalem.
text: Ne̱ Ananiyas a be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ a tɔwe̱ ɔnyare̱ mɔ ase̱ŋ gywii mo̱, ne̱ mo̱ a nu awo̱re̱fɔɔ ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ waa fo̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ ase̱ŋ.

ref: Now the chief priests have given him the power to come here and arrest anyone who worships in your name.”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ nya ke̱yaale̱ŋ bo̱ le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ ase̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ ba Damasikus mfe̱e̱ bo̱ kra ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ ne̱ ba suŋ fo̱‑ɔ.”

ref: Ananias left and went into the house where Saul was staying. Ananias placed his hands on him and said, “Saul, the Lord Jesus has sent me. He is the same one who appeared to you along the road. He wants you to be able to see and to be filled with the Holy Spirit.”
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ Ananiyas a yɔ ya lwee lɔŋ ne̱ Sɔɔl bo̱ mò̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ taa mò̱ asare̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ Sɔɔl se̱, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ Sɔɔl, ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu fɔŋfɔŋ ne̱ oo lee mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ fo̱ saŋ ne̱ fo̱ bo̱ kpa‑rɔ fo̱ e̱ ba mfe̱e̱‑ɔ ya suŋ mo̱ fo̱ ase̱. Oo suŋ mo̱ fo̱ ase̱ ne̱e̱ a fo̱ akatɔ be̱e̱ nya buŋŋi fe̱yɛ saŋ‑ɔ ane̱ŋ ne̱ a du‑o, na Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o ywe̱e̱ fo̱ se̱.”

ref: Then he ate and felt much better. Saul Preaches in Damascus For several days Saul stayed with the Lord's followers in Damascus.
text: Mò̱ a gyi ateese‑o kamɛɛ‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ waa ke̱yaale̱ŋ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Sɔɔl a tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ Damasikus maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ Ne̱ Sɔɔl a gyi ŋke mfe̱ne̱ ko̱ Kristoo akɔɔre̱gyipo̱ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Damasikus maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ ase̱.

ref: Soon he went to the synagogues and started telling people that Jesus is the Son of God.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ mfe̱ŋ a ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ akyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ ya le̱e̱ ɔ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ fe̱yɛ Yeesuu gye̱ Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑o ne̱e̱.

ref: Everyone who heard Saul was amazed and said, “Isn't this the man who caused so much trouble for those people in Jerusalem who worship in the name of Jesus? Didn't he come here to arrest them and take them to the chief priests?”
text: Ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ bamo̱ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ gywii bamo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ. Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “N gye̱ ɔnyare̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ ɔ mɔɔ ase̱sɛ ne̱ ba suŋ Yeesuu‑o ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱? Ɔ mo̱ŋ ba mfe̱e̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ ba ɔ bo̱ kra Yeesuu asuŋpo̱ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱e̱‑ɔ na o kiŋŋaa bamo̱ yaa Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ ase̱ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱?”

ref: Later some of them made plans to kill Saul,
text: Amo̱ a kyee, ne̱ Yudaa awuye‑o a gyaŋŋe̱ kra ane̱ŋ ne̱ ba waa a bo̱ mɔɔ Sɔɔl‑ɔ.

ref: but he found out about it. He learned that they were guarding the gates of the city day and night in order to kill him.
text: Amaa oo nu ke̱tɔ ne̱ Yudaa awuye‑o a kra‑ɔ. Ne̱ ŋkee mpase̱ na kanye bo̱ ye̱re̱ maŋ kideŋbee a abunogyi‑o‑ro ba kpa Sɔɔl a bo̱ mɔɔ.

ref: Then Barnabas helped him by taking him to the apostles. He explained how Saul had seen the Lord and how the Lord had spoken to him. Barnabas also said when Saul was in Damascus, he had spoken bravely in the name of Jesus.
text: Ne̱ Bar‑Nabas a ba bo̱ yaa mò̱ Yeesuu ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ ase̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ kaapo̱ bamo̱‑rɔ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Sɔɔl a ŋu ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu kpa‑rɔ‑ɔ na ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a sa se̱ŋsa gywii mò̱‑ɔ, na ane̱ŋ ne̱ Sɔɔl a nya kakpo̱nɔ kaapo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ Yeesuu ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ Damasikus maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ.

ref: Saul moved about freely with the followers in Jerusalem and told everyone about the Lord.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ Sɔɔl a sii bamo̱ ase̱, ne̱ ɔɔ nya kakpo̱nɔ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ Yeesuu ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ pɛɛɛ.

ref: But the followers found out about this and took Saul to Caesarea. From there they sent him to the city of Tarsus.
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yeesuu akɔɔre̱gyipo̱‑ɔ a ŋu ane̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa yaa Sɔɔl Kaye̱sare̱ya maŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ baa ya ba mò̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ mò̱ aye̱ Taasus ne̱ ɔ bo̱ e̱fɔ‑ɔ.

ref: While Peter was traveling from place to place, he visited the Lord's followers who lived in the town of Lydda.
text: Ne̱ Peetroo a nare̱ maŋ ke̱maa ne̱ Yeesuu akɔɔre̱gyipo̱‑ɔ bo̱‑ɔ se̱ ɔ laa bamo̱. Ne̱ saŋ ko̱ ɔɔ yɔ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Lidaa maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ ase̱ a ɔ ya laa bamo̱.

ref: There he met a man named Aeneas, who for eight years had been sick in bed and could not move.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ ŋu ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Iniya, ne̱ mò̱ kayo̱wɔre̱ pɛɛɛ mɔ a wu, ne̱ ɔ maa taare̱ a ɔ ko̱so̱ le̱e̱ kraŋ se̱ nsu mburuwa‑o.

ref: Peter said to Aeneas, “Jesus Christ has healed you! Get up and make up your bed.” At once he stood up.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Peetroo a te̱e̱ mò̱ ke̱nyare̱ ɛ, “Iniya, Yeesuu Kristoo e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ sa a fo̱ kpaare̱. Ko̱so̱ bɔɔ fo̱ kraŋ.” Puri‑ro mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Iniya a ko̱so̱ kyuwi‑ro.

ref: Many people in the towns of Lydda and Sharon saw Aeneas and became followers of the Lord. Peter Brings Dorcas Back to Life
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ te Lidaa maŋ‑nɔ na Sarɔŋ ifuri si‑o pɛɛɛ a ŋu ɔnyare̱ amo̱ a kpaare̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi, ne̱ baa bo̱ gya mò̱ e̱kpa‑ɔ se̱.

ref: In Joppa there was a follower named Tabitha. Her Greek name was Dorcas, which means “deer.” She was always doing good things for people and had given much to the poor.
text: Amɔ ɔkye̱e̱ ko̱ mɔ bo̱ Yopaa maŋ ne̱ mò̱ aa Lidaa mo̱ŋ bo̱ e̱fɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi bwe̱e̱tɔ. Mò̱ ke̱nyare̱ e̱ gye̱ Tabitaa. Ɔkye̱e̱ mɔ fe̱raa atiripo̱‑rɔ ke̱kya na ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ wo̱re̱ dooo ne̱ ɔ naa ɔ waa.

ref: But she got sick and died, and her body was washed and placed in an upstairs room.
text: Saŋ amo̱ mɔ, ɔɔ lɔ, ne̱ oo wu. Ne̱ baa gyee mò̱ taa mò̱ kifuniŋ‑o bo̱ be̱ya ke̱baŋso̱ro̱o̱ ke̱kyaŋ ko̱‑rɔ so̱so̱ na ba su kale.

ref: Joppa wasn't far from Lydda, and the followers heard that Peter was there. They sent two men to say to him, “Please come with us as quickly as you can!”
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ Yopaa awuye ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi‑o a nu Peetroo si fe̱yɛ ɔ bo̱ Lidaa maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa suŋ abɛɛ anyɔ ko̱ mò̱ ase̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ya tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ ɛ, “Ane̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ fo̱, waa me̱naŋ ba ane̱ ase̱.”

ref: At once, Peter went with them. The men took Peter upstairs into the room. Many widows were there crying. They showed him the coats and clothes that Dorcas had made while she was still alive.
text: Ne̱ Peetroo a waa siraa, ne̱ mò̱ aa abɛɛ anyɔ amo̱ a yɔ. Bamo̱ a fo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa yaa Peetroo ke̱baŋso̱ro̱o̱ ke̱kyaŋ ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ da ke̱mo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ. Ne̱ akpenlekye̱e̱ ye̱re̱ muruwaa kifuniŋ‑o na ba su. Ne̱ baa ba e̱kaare̱ ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ a baara sa atiripo̱ saŋ ne̱ o te‑o bo̱ kaapo̱ Peetroo ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ bware‑o.

ref: After Peter had sent everyone out of the room, he knelt down and prayed. Then he turned to the body of Dorcas and said, “Tabitha, get up!” The woman opened her eyes, and when she saw Peter, she sat up.
text: Ne̱ Peetroo a sa ne̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ a le̱e̱ ke̱kyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ oo kpuni aŋurii, ne̱ ɔɔ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱, ne̱ oo kii ba kifuniŋ‑o ase̱, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Tabitaa, kyiŋŋi kye̱na.” Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ oo buŋŋi akatɔ. Mò̱ a ŋu Peetroo‑o, ne̱ oo kyiŋŋi kye̱na.

ref: He took her by the hand and helped her to her feet. Peter called in the widows and the other followers and showed them that Dorcas had been raised from death.
text: Ne̱ Peetroo a tɔ mò̱‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ kra mò̱‑rɔ kya mò̱‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ ko̱so̱ ye̱re̱. Ne̱ Peetroo a te̱e̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi‑o pɛɛɛ na akpenlekye̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ ba ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱.

ref: Everyone in Joppa heard what had happened, and many of them put their faith in the Lord.
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ bo̱ Yopaa maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ a nu ase̱ŋ mɔ. Ne̱ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ a kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi.

ref: Peter stayed on for a while in Joppa in the house of a man named Simon, who made leather.
text: Ne̱ Peetroo a gyi ŋke kafwe̱e̱ ko̱ gbaŋse̱ba ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ Simɔŋ‑ɔ ase̱ Yopaa mfe̱ŋ.

ref: In Caesarea there was a man named Cornelius, who was the captain of a group of soldiers called “The Italian Unit.”
text: Saŋ amo̱ mɔ, ɔnyare̱ ko̱ mɔ bo̱ Kaye̱sare̱ya maŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Kooniiliyus. Mò̱ e̱ gye̱ Roma awuye asoogyaa alafa asiye ne̱ ba te̱e̱ bamo̱ ɛ Italiya asoogyaa‑o‑ro ke̱lafa ɔbre̱sɛ.

ref: Cornelius was a very religious man. He worshiped God, and so did everyone else who lived in his house. He had given a lot of money to the poor and was always praying to God.
text: Ɔ gye̱ se̱sɛ timaa ne̱e̱ fɛɛ. Ne̱ mò̱ aa mò̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ awuye pɛɛɛ mɔ a mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye gbaa ooo, ne̱ ba suŋ Wuribware̱. Ne̱ ɔ be̱e̱ ɔ kya Yudaa awuye atiripo̱‑rɔ, na ɔ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ saŋ ke̱maa.

ref: One afternoon at about three o'clock, Cornelius had a vision. He saw an angel from God coming to him and calling him by name.
text: Kake ŋko̱, kyɔwe̱ kikpeerebe̱e̱, ne̱ ɔnyare̱ mɔ a yɔ ya ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱, ne̱ oo ŋu ko̱tɔko̱ fe̱yɛ dee‑ro‑o. Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ ne̱ oo ŋu nɛɛnɛɛ‑ɔ na oo lweero mò̱ ase̱ te̱e̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ “Kooniiliyus!”

ref: Cornelius was surprised and stared at the angel. Then he asked, “What is this all about?” The angel answered, “God has heard your prayers and knows about your gifts to the poor.
text: Na kufu de mò̱, ne̱ ɔɔ de̱e̱re̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ oo bise fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ ne̱e̱, mo̱ nyaŋpe̱?” Ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ amo̱ a be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ ɛ, “Wuribware̱ akatɔ a gyi fo̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ na ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ waa atiripo̱‑ɔ se̱, ne̱ ɔ kpa a ɔ be̱ŋŋaa fo̱.

ref: Now send some men to Joppa for a man named Simon Peter.
text: Na mbe̱yɔmɔ suŋ ase̱sɛ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ Yopaa maŋ‑nɔ, na bo̱ ya te̱e̱ ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ Simɔŋ Peetroo‑o.

ref: He is staying with Simon the leather maker, who lives in a house near the sea.”
text: Ɔ gye̱ gbaŋse̱ba ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Simɔŋ, ne̱ o te ɔpo̱o̱ kɛɛ‑ɔ ɔfɔ ne̱e̱.”

ref: After saying this, the angel left. Cornelius called in two of his servants and one of his soldiers who worshiped God.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ‑ɔ a le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ o yii, ne̱ Kooniiliyus a te̱e̱ mò̱ anya anyɔ na soogyaa ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱ ɔ waa kusuŋ ɔ sa mò̱ mò̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ o suŋ Wuribware̱‑ɔ.

ref: He explained everything to them and sent them off to Joppa.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ kaa waa‑ɔ gywii bamo̱, ne̱ oo suŋ bamo̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ Yopaa maŋ‑nɔ.

ref: The next day about noon these men were coming near Joppa. Peter went up on the roof of the house to pray
text: Ke̱mo̱ kaye̱ ŋke‑o ne̱ baa yɔ ba tɔ‑rɔ Yopaa‑o, ne̱ Peetroo a de̱e̱ bamo̱ akyaŋ tɛɛse̱sɛ amo̱ ko̱ko̱ mpase̱ amo̱ a ɔ ya ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱.

ref: and became very hungry. While the food was being prepared, he fell sound asleep and had a vision.
text: Saŋ‑ɔ ako̱ŋ de mò̱, ɔ kpa ateese a o gyi. Bamo̱ a maa se̱ ba waa ateese‑o, ne̱ oo ŋu ke̱tɔ ko̱ fe̱yɛ dee‑ro‑o.

ref: He saw heaven open, and something came down like a huge sheet held up by its four corners.
text: Oo ŋu ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ awo̱re̱‑rɔ a buŋŋi‑ro, ne̱ waagya dabe̱ ko̱ a le̱e̱ so̱so̱ ɔ ba‑rɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ko̱ a de mò̱ aŋu ana‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ le̱e̱ da‑rɔ mò̱ ase̱.

ref: In it were all kinds of animals, reptiles, and birds.
text: Mbo̱ na awɔ na akeri na mbo̱gyii e̱wo̱re̱se̱ e̱wo̱re̱se̱ mɔ bo̱ waagya amo̱‑rɔ.

ref: A voice said to him, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
text: Ne̱ oo nu bo̱re̱ ko̱ a le̱e̱ so̱so̱ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Peetroo, ko̱so̱ na fo̱ mɔɔ wo̱!”

ref: But Peter said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never eaten anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
text: Amaa Peetroo a be̱ŋŋaa ne̱e̱ ɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, ma gyi mmo̱ daa, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a ko̱so̱‑ɔ, mo̱ŋ tɛɛ gyi ke̱tɔ ne̱ ane̱ Yudaa awuye mbraa‑ɔ yɛ a gye̱ iyisi ne̱ ba kisi‑o.”

ref: The voice spoke to him again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
text: Ne̱ bo̱re̱‑ɔ a be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ nyɔse̱po̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ke̱tɔ ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ Wuribware̱ yɛ ki bware kigyi‑o, fo̱‑ɔ fo̱ ma kisi kigyi!”

ref: This happened three times before the sheet was suddenly taken back to heaven.
text: Ne̱ bo̱re̱ amo̱ a tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ iluwi e̱sa gywii mò̱, ne̱ oo kine kigyi ane̱ŋ dɛɛ, ne̱ atɔ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a be̱e̱ kiŋŋi yɔ so̱so̱.

ref: Peter was still wondering what all this meant, when the men sent by Cornelius came and stood at the gate. They had found their way to Simon's house
text: Peetroo a saŋ ɔ fa ke̱tɔ ne̱ oo ŋu‑o mfɛɛre̱‑ɔ, na ŋkee ne̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ Kooniiliyus a suŋ bamo̱‑ɔ a fo̱ Simɔŋ lɔŋ ba bo̱ ye̱re̱ mò̱ kabuno.

ref: While Peter was still thinking about the vision, the Holy Spirit said to him, “Three men are here looking for you.
text: Peetroo a saŋ ɔ bo̱ ke̱kyaŋ‑ɔ so̱so̱ mfe̱ŋ ɔ fa ke̱tɔ ne̱ oo ŋu‑o mfɛɛre̱‑ɔ, ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nu! Ase̱sɛ asa ko̱ bo̱ mfe̱e̱ ba kpa fo̱.

ref: Peter went down and said to the men, “I am the one you are looking for. Why have you come?”
text: Ne̱ Peetroo a kpo̱ro̱we̱ ya tɔwe̱ gywii ase̱sɛ asa amo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i buwi mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa ne̱e̱. Mo̱ne̱ kpa se̱?”

ref: They answered, “Captain Cornelius sent us. He is a good man who worships God and is liked by the Jewish people. One of God's holy angels told Cornelius to send for you, so he could hear what you have to say.”
text: Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “To, ane̱ mo̱ŋ de bɔye̱ ko̱. Roma soogyaa bre̱sɛ Kooniiliyus ya suŋ ane̱ fo̱ ase̱. Ɔ gye̱ se̱sɛ timaa, ne̱ o suŋ Wuribware̱, ne̱ Yudaa awuye mɔ pɛɛɛ i bu mò̱. Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ a ba mò̱ ase̱ bo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ te̱e̱ fo̱ mò̱ aye̱, na o nu ke̱tɔ ne̱ fo̱ de a fo̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ.”

ref: Peter invited them to spend the night. The next morning, Peter and some of the Lord's followers in Joppa left with the men who had come from Cornelius.
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa, ne̱ Peetroo a sa ne̱ baa lwee lɔŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ po̱pɔre̱ bamo̱ na bo̱ di a kaye̱ ke. Kaye̱ a ke‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ waa siraa, ne̱ mò̱ aa bamo̱ na Yopaa awuye bamo̱ ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi‑o bo̱ko̱ a yɔ.

ref: The next day they arrived in Caesarea where Cornelius was waiting for them. He had also invited his relatives and close friends.
text: Ke̱mo̱ kaye̱ ŋke‑o ne̱ baa fo̱ Kaye̱sare̱ya maŋ‑nɔ kyɔ ya to̱ fe̱yɛ Kooniiliyus na mò̱ ko̱we̱bɛɛ‑ana na mò̱ nyare̱‑ana ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱‑ɔ a waa siraa bo̱ te bo̱ gywii mò̱.

ref: When Peter arrived, Cornelius greeted him. Then he knelt at Peter's feet and started worshiping him.
text: Peetroo a de̱ŋ o lwee lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ Kooniiliyus a le̱e̱ bo̱ gyaŋŋaa mò̱ buŋŋe kpuni aŋurii mò̱ akatɔ‑rɔ suŋ mò̱.

ref: But Peter took hold of him and said, “Stand up! I am nothing more than a human.”
text: Ne̱ Peetroo a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ ɛ, “O daabii, ko̱so̱ ye̱re̱, mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ gye̱ se̱sɛ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱‑ɔ dɛɛ.”

ref: As Peter entered the house, he was still talking with Cornelius. Many people were there,
text: Bamo̱ a lwee lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ na ɔ sa se̱ŋsa Kooniiliyus ase̱, ne̱ ɔɔ ya to̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ a gyaŋŋe̱ mfe̱ŋ.

ref: and Peter said to them, “You know that we Jews are not allowed to have anything to do with other people. But God has shown me that he doesn't think anyone is unclean or unfit.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ fɔŋfɔŋ nyi fe̱yɛ ane̱ Yudaa awuye a mbraa‑ɔ mo̱ŋ sa kpa fe̱yɛ Yudaanyi na ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaanyi nare̱ bɛɛɛ o lwee mò̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ. Amaa mbe̱yɔmɔ Wuribware̱ a kaapo̱ mo̱ fe̱yɛ m ma lɛɛ teŋŋi‑ro na m ma lɛɛ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ de iyisi na ŋ kperi ane̱ŋ se̱ na mo̱ aa mò̱ ma nare̱.

ref: I agreed to come here, but I want to know why you sent for me.”
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a suŋ te̱e̱ mo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ŋ kine, ne̱ mo̱ a ba‑ɔ ne̱e̱. To, mo̱ i bise mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱mo̱ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a te̱e̱ mo̱.”

ref: Cornelius answered: Four days ago at about three o'clock in the afternoon I was praying at home. Suddenly a man in bright clothes stood in front of me.
text: Ne̱ Kooniiliyus yɛ, “To, mo̱ŋ de bɔye̱ ko̱. Ndɔɔ ŋke nna ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a yɔ a ŋ ya ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ mo̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ mpase̱, ne̱ mo̱ a ŋu ɔnyare̱ ko̱ fe̱yɛ dee‑ro‑o na ɔ ye̱re̱ mo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ o buŋ kaare̱ to̱re̱se̱sɛ.

ref: He said, “Cornelius, God has heard your prayers, and he knows about your gifts to the poor.
text: Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, ‘Kooniiliyus, Wuribware̱ a nu fo̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱, ne̱ oo ŋu ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ waa atiripo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ.’

ref: Now send to Joppa for Simon Peter. He is visiting in the home of Simon the leather maker, who lives near the sea.”
text: Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, ‘Mbe̱yɔmɔ suŋ ɔko̱ Yopaa maŋ‑nɔ na ɔ ya te̱e̱ ɔnyare̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ Simɔŋ Peetroo‑o. Ɔ gye̱ ɔfɔ ɔ bo̱ ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Simɔŋ Gbaŋse̱ba ne̱ o te ɔpo̱o̱ kɛɛ‑ɔ aye̱!’

ref: I sent for you at once, and you have been good enough to come. All of us are here in the presence of the Lord God, so that we can hear what he has to say.
text: Amo̱se̱ ne̱ mo̱ a suŋ te̱e̱ fo̱. Fo̱ a ba‑ɔ, aa waa mo̱ kɔne̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Mbe̱yɔmɔ ane̱ pɛɛɛ a gyaŋŋe̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Wuribware̱ akatɔ‑rɔ ane̱ e̱ kpa a ane̱ nu ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔ yɛ fo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii ane̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱.” Ke̱tɔ ne̱ Peetroo a tɔwe̱ gywii Kooniiliyus‑ana‑ɔ

ref: God is pleased with everyone who worships him and does right, no matter what nation they come from.
text: Na Wuribware̱ fe̱raa, kasuro ke̱maa ne̱ fo̱ a le̱e̱ kamo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ fo̱ e̱ tɛɛ fo̱ i suŋ mò̱, ne̱ fo̱ e̱ waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔ kpa‑ɔ, ɔ kpa fo̱ ase̱ŋ.

ref: This is the same message that God gave to the people of Israel, when he sent Jesus Christ, the Lord of all, to offer peace to them.
text: Amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ gbaa Isireelii awuye ne̱ Wuribware̱ a taa mò̱ ase̱ŋ amo̱ bo̱ sa. Ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱rɔ Yeesuu Kristoo si tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ e̱ kpa a o yuri ane̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ. Amaa kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱raa Yeesuu mo̱ŋ gye̱ ane̱ Yudaa awuye wo̱re̱ nyaŋpe̱ ne̱e̱. Ɔ gye̱ to̱ŋ ke̱maa awuye nyaŋpe̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: We all saw what Jesus did both in Israel and in the city of Jerusalem. Jesus was put to death on a cross.
text: Ne̱ ane̱ pɛɛɛ a ŋu ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ɔɔ waa Yude̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ pɛɛɛ se̱ na Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ. Ne̱ baa ba ndangyii bo̱ da mò̱ bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱ mɔɔ.

ref: But three days later, God raised him to life and let him be seen.
text: Ne̱ ke̱mo̱ kake sase̱po̱‑ɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kyiŋŋi mò̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro, ne̱ oo lee mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ifuri.

ref: Not everyone saw him. He was seen only by us, who ate and drank with him after he was raised from death. We were the ones God chose to tell others about him.
text: Amaa n gye̱ ɔke̱maa ya ŋu mò̱. Ane̱ ne̱ ane̱ gye̱ bamo̱ ne̱ ɔɔ kyɔ lee‑o ya ŋu mò̱, ane̱ ne̱ ane̱ a ŋu ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ Yeesuu a waa‑ɔ, ne̱ ane̱ aa mò̱ a gyi ateese, ne̱ ane̱ a nuu nta saŋ ne̱ oo kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro‑o kamɛɛ‑rɔ.

ref: God told us to announce clearly to the people that Jesus is the one he has chosen to judge the living and the dead.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii ane̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ tɔwe̱ mò̱ ase̱ŋ gywii ase̱sɛ bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a lee fe̱yɛ ɔ waa bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ wu‑o na bamo̱ ne̱ baa wu‑o pɛɛɛ ɔse̱ŋgyipo̱.

ref: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit took control of everyone who was listening.
text: Na Peetroo saŋ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa, ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a bo̱ ywe̱e̱ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ o nu mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱.

ref: Some Jewish followers of the Lord had come with Peter, and they were surprised that the Holy Spirit had been given to Gentiles.
text: Ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ Yudaa awuye ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi, ne̱ bamo̱ aa Peetroo pɛɛɛ a le̱e̱ Yopaa, ne̱ bo̱ ye̱re̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a sa ne̱ mò̱ kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a ywe̱e̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o gbaa se̱‑ɔ.

ref: Now they were hearing Gentiles speaking unknown languages and praising God. Peter said,
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ baa nu bamo̱ a sa e̱se̱ŋsa ko̱ ne̱ bamo̱ aa ase̱sɛ‑ɔ fɔŋfɔŋ gbaa maa nu e̱mo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, na ba kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱ a ke̱dabe̱‑ɔ.

ref: “These Gentiles have been given the Holy Spirit, just as we have! I am certain that no one would dare stop us from baptizing them.”
text: Ne̱ Peetroo yɛ, “Ase̱sɛ mɔ a nya Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱‑ɔ dɛɛ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ɔko̱ e̱ taare̱ a o kuŋ bamo̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ ma ba nkyu bo̱ bɔ bamo̱ asuu aaa?”

ref: Peter ordered them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ, and they asked him to stay on for a few days.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ baa bɔ bamo̱ asuu Yeesuu Kristoo ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ. Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ Peetroo kye̱na bamo̱ ase̱ ŋke ŋnyɔ ko̱.

ref: So when Peter came to Jerusalem, some of the Jewish followers started arguing with him. They wanted Gentile followers to be circumcised, and
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ saŋ ne̱ Peetroo a yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ ye̱re̱‑rɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ te̱ŋŋe̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o atwe̱e̱tu‑o pwɛɛ na bamo̱ aa bamo̱ dɛɛ taare̱ nare̱ na bo̱ gyi abɛɛ ase̱‑ɔ a po̱rɔ mò̱ fe̱yɛ,

ref: they said, “You stayed in the homes of Gentiles, and you even ate with them!”
text: “Fo̱ a waa ɔfɔ ya so̱we̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ te̱ŋŋe̱ atwe̱e̱tu‑o aye̱.”

ref: Then Peter told them exactly what had happened:
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ Peetroo a tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ kaa waa‑ɔ pɛɛɛ ke̱gyase̱ ke̱gyase̱ gywii bamo̱.

ref: I was in the town of Joppa and was praying when I fell sound asleep and had a vision. I saw heaven open, and something like a huge sheet held by its four corners came down to me.
text: Ɔɔ tɔwe̱ ne̱e̱ ɛ, “Saŋ ne̱ m bo̱ Yopaa maŋ‑nɔ mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ a ŋu atɔ ko̱ fe̱yɛ dee‑ro‑o a le̱e̱ so̱so̱ a ba fe̱yɛ bamo̱ a kra waagya aŋu ana‑rɔ, ne̱ ba yɔwe̱‑rɔ ba le̱e̱ so̱so̱ ba‑rɔ‑ɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ le̱e̱ da mo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ.

ref: When I looked in it, I saw animals, wild beasts, reptiles, and birds.
text: Mo̱ a de̱e̱re̱ waagya‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ a ŋu mbo̱ na awɔ na akeri na mbo̱gyii.

ref: I heard a voice saying to me, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a nu bo̱re̱ ko̱ a le̱e̱ so̱so̱ tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Peetroo, ko̱so̱ ye̱re̱ na fo̱ mɔɔ wo̱.’

ref: But I said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never taken a bite of anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
text: Amaa mo̱ a be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, ma gyi mmo̱ daa, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a ko̱so̱‑ɔ, mo̱ŋ tɛɛ gyi ke̱tɔ ne̱ ane̱ Yudaa awuye mbraa‑ɔ yɛ a gye̱ iyisi ne̱ ba kisi‑o.’

ref: The voice from heaven spoke to me again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
text: Ne̱ bo̱re̱ amo̱ a be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ ɛ, ‘Ke̱tɔ ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ Wuribware̱ yɛ ki bware kigyi‑o, fo̱‑ɔ fo̱ ma kisi kigyi.’

ref: This happened three times before it was all taken back into heaven.
text: Amo̱‑ɔ a waa iluwi e̱sa, ne̱ ŋkee amo̱ pɛɛɛ a be̱e̱ kiŋŋi yɔ so̱so̱.

ref: Suddenly three men from Caesarea stood in front of the house where I was staying.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ abɛɛ asa ko̱ ne̱ baa suŋ bamo̱ le̱e̱ Kaye̱sare̱ya maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ a fo̱ lɔŋ ne̱ n te mò̱‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: The Holy Spirit told me to go with them and not to worry. Then six of the Lord's followers went with me to the home of a man
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o a tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ aa bamo̱ a yɔ na m ma kine. Abɛɛ asiye mɔ ne̱ baa le̱e̱ Yopaa maŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi‑o ya yaa mo̱ Kaye̱sare̱ya mfe̱ŋ, ne̱ ane̱ pɛɛɛ a lwee ɔnyare̱ ne̱ oo suŋ te̱e̱ mo̱‑ɔ a lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: who told us that an angel had appeared to him. The angel had ordered him to send to Joppa for someone named Simon Peter.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii ane̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ oo ŋu Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ a bo̱ ye̱re̱ mò̱ lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ. Na ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ amo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ ɛ, ‘Suŋ ɔko̱ Yopaa maŋ‑nɔ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Simɔŋ Peetroo‑o ase̱.

ref: Then Peter would tell him how he and everyone in his house could be saved.
text: Mò̱‑ɔ mɔ e̱ tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ fo̱ aa fo̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ awuye‑o a o gywii fo̱.’

ref: After I started speaking, the Holy Spirit was given to them, just as the Spirit had been given to us at the beginning.
text: Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a le̱e̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ mo̱ i gywii bamo̱‑ɔ, a mo̱ŋ kyee ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a bo̱ ywe̱e̱ bamo̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱gye̱ŋkpɛɛ‑ɔ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ ywe̱e̱ ane̱ se̱‑ɔ dɛɛ.

ref: God gave those Gentiles the same gift that he gave us when we put our faith in the Lord Jesus Christ. So how could I have gone against God?
text: A bo̱‑rɔ ane̱ŋ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a sa bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o mò̱ kufwiiŋe timaa‑o ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ sa ane̱ ne̱ ane̱ a kɔɔre̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo gyi‑o dɛɛ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ nsɛ e̱ gye̱ mo̱‑ɔ mo̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ taare̱ a n tii Wuribware̱ kusuŋ kpa?”

ref: When they heard Peter say this, they stopped arguing and started praising God. They said, “God has now let Gentiles turn to him, and he has given life to them!” The Church in Antioch
text: Bamo̱ a nu ase̱ŋ mɔ pɛɛɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa yɔwe̱ Peetroo ke̱po̱rɔ, ne̱ baa kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱. Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Amo̱ fe̱raa Wuribware̱ a sa bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o gbaa kpa fe̱yɛ bo̱ nu bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱, na bo̱ le̱e̱ bamo̱ e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ, na bo̱ nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ.” Asɔre̱e̱ ne̱ a bo̱ Ante̱yɔke̱ya maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ

ref: Some of the Lord's followers had been scattered because of the terrible trouble that started when Stephen was killed. They went as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch, but they told the message only to the Jews.
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yudaa awuye‑o a mɔɔ Stifaŋ‑o, bamo̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana Yudaa awuye ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi‑o a brawe̱‑rɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ baa le̱e̱ ba waa bamo̱ awo̱re̱fɔɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Bamo̱ bo̱ko̱ a yɔ hare̱e̱ Fonikiya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ na Kiprus kwii si na Ante̱yɔke̱ya maŋ‑nɔ ba tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ timaa‑o ba gywii Yudaa awuye‑o wo̱re̱.

ref: Some of the followers from Cyprus and Cyrene went to Antioch and started telling Gentiles the good news about the Lord Jesus.
text: Amaa bamo̱ bo̱ko̱ ne̱ baa ko̱we̱ bamo̱ Kiprus kwii si na Kireenee maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ a yɔ Ante̱yɔke̱ya, ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ timaa bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu kuŋu si gywii bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o.

ref: The Lord's power was with them, and many people turned to the Lord and put their faith in him.
text: Bamo̱ a de ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ a ke̱yaale̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o bwe̱e̱tɔ a kɔɔre̱ ase̱ŋ timaa‑o gyi, ne̱ baa gya Yeesuu a ke̱bware̱suŋ‑o si.

ref: News of what was happening reached the church in Jerusalem. Then they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
text: Ne̱ asɔre̱e̱ awuye ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ a nu amo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa suŋ Bar‑Nabas bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ Ante̱yɔke̱ya mfe̱ŋ.

ref: Barnabas went to Tarsus to look for Saul.
text: Ne̱ Bar‑Nabas a kya se̱ yɔ Taasus maŋ‑nɔ a ɔ ya buwi kpa Sɔɔl.

ref: He found Saul and brought him to Antioch, where they met with the church for a whole year and taught many of its people. There in Antioch the Lord's followers were first called Christians.
text: Mò̱ a ya kpa Sɔɔl ŋu‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ yaa mò̱ Ante̱yɔke̱ya. Abɛɛ anyɔ amo̱ a gyi kasu mfe̱ŋ, na bamo̱ aa mfe̱ŋ a asɔre̱e̱ awuye‑o e̱ gyaŋŋe̱, ne̱ baa kaapo̱ ase̱sɛ biribiri abware̱se̱ŋ. Ante̱yɔke̱ya mfe̱ŋ ne̱ baa gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ te̱e̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi‑o ke̱nyare̱ fe̱yɛ Kristoo awuye.

ref: During this time some prophets from Jerusalem came to Antioch.
text: Amɔ saŋ amo̱ Wuribware̱ akyaamɛɛ ko̱ mɔ a le̱e̱ Yɛro̱salɛm yɔ Ante̱yɔke̱ya mfe̱ŋ.

ref: One of them was Agabus. Then with the help of the Spirit, he told that there would be a terrible famine everywhere in the world. And it happened when Claudius was Emperor.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o a sa ne̱ bamo̱‑rɔ ɔko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Agabus‑o a ko̱so̱ ye̱re̱, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ako̱ŋ dabe̱ ko̱ e̱ kpa a a ba swe̱e̱re̱ pɛɛɛ se̱.

ref: The followers in Antioch decided to send whatever help they could to the followers in Judea.
text: Ne̱ Kristoo awuye ne̱ bo̱ te Ante̱yɔke̱ya‑ɔ a kra fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ɔke̱maa e̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ bamo̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi, ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Yude̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ ke̱tɔ ne̱ baa taare̱‑ɔ.

ref: So they appointed Barnabas and Saul to take their gifts to the church leaders in Jerusalem.
text: Kase̱ŋtiŋ mɔ, ne̱ baa kpa atanne̱ bo̱ suŋ Bar‑Nabas na Sɔɔl, ne̱ baa taa yaa bo̱ sa mfe̱ŋ a asɔre̱e̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ.

ref: At that time King Herod caused terrible suffering for some members of the church.
text: Amɔ saŋ mɔ Owure Hɛrɔd a le̱e̱ ɔ waa asɔre̱e̱ awuye ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ bo̱rɔkraa.

ref: He ordered soldiers to cut off the head of James, the brother of John.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ mò̱ asoogyaa awuye a ba ke̱paŋ bo̱ mɔɔ Yohanee mò̱ daa Yakubu.

ref: When Herod saw that this pleased the Jewish people, he had Peter arrested during the Festival of Thin Bread.
text: Mò̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ Yakubu ke̱mɔɔ‑ɔ a waa Yudaa awuye kɔne̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ kra Peetroo. Ɔɔ waa amo̱ ŋke ne̱ Yudaa awuye e̱ waa kasu kanɔ ngyaŋŋe̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba gyi Bodobodoo-ne̱-yiisi-mo̱ŋ‑te‑ro‑o.

ref: While Peter was being kept in jail, the church never stopped praying to God for him. Peter Is Rescued
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa tii mò̱. Amaa asɔre̱e̱ awuye‑o a ye̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ se̱ kake ke̱maa fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ lee mò̱ le̱e̱ owure‑o ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ.

ref: The night before Peter was to be put on trial, he was asleep and bound by two chains. A soldier was guarding him on each side, and two other soldiers were guarding the entrance to the jail.
text: Fe̱yɛ nyaŋe̱ ne̱ Owure Hɛrɔd yɛ ɔ gyaŋŋe̱ ase̱sɛ na o gyi Peetroo ase̱ŋ‑ɔ, ke̱mo̱ ndɔɔ kanye na Peetroo da asoogyaa awuye anyɔ mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ, na baa ŋure mò̱ agbarabi anyɔ, na asoogyaa awuye ko̱ mɔ ye̱re̱ ke̱kyaŋ ne̱ baa tii Peetroo ke̱mo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ kibunogyi‑ro.

ref: Suddenly an angel from the Lord appeared, and light flashed around in the cell. The angel poked Peter in the side and woke him up. Then he said, “Quick! Get up!” The chains fell off his hands,
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ a bo̱ ye̱re̱ Peetroo ase̱ ke̱kyaŋ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ ke̱mo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ. Amo̱ a gye̱ kanye gbaa ooo, ne̱ ke̱kyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ a laŋŋe̱‑rɔ. Ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ amo̱ a le̱ŋkpaŋ Peetroo ke̱baakpa, ne̱ oo kyiŋŋi. Ne̱ kabɔɔ‑ɔ yɛ, “Waa me̱naŋ ko̱so̱!” Puri amo̱‑rɔ ne̱ agbarabi ne̱ baa bo̱ da mò̱ asare̱e̱‑rɔ‑ɔ a saŋŋe̱ le̱e̱ da.

ref: and the angel said, “Get dressed and put on your sandals.” Peter did what he was told. Then the angel said, “Now put on your coat and follow me.”
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ‑ɔ yɛ, “Ke̱re̱ se̱ na fo̱ waa‑rɔ fo̱ ase̱be̱ta na fo̱ buŋ fo̱ waagya.” Ne̱ Peetroo a waa ane̱ŋ.

ref: They went past the two groups of soldiers, and when they came to the iron gate to the city, it opened by itself. They went out and were going along the street, when all at once the angel disappeared.
text: Ne̱ baa kyo̱ŋ asoogyaa awuye katuŋ gye̱ŋkpɛɛsɛ‑ɔ na kanyɔse̱po̱‑ɔ se̱. Ne̱ ŋkee lalalo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa bo̱ fo̱ po̱ne̱ dabe̱ ne̱ baa ba ke̱gye̱re̱tɔ bo̱ waa, ne̱ o tii kabo̱re̱ ne̱ ka lwee maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ. Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ po̱ne̱ amo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ a buŋŋi, ne̱ baa kyo̱ŋ le̱e̱, ne̱ baa bo̱rɔ kabo̱re̱ ko̱, ne̱ puri‑ro mfe̱ŋ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ amo̱ a ŋaa yɔwe̱ Peetroo.

ref: Peter now realized what had happened, and he said, “I am certain that the Lord sent his angel to rescue me from Herod and from everything the Jewish leaders planned to do to me.”
text: Ŋkee fe̱raa, ne̱ Peetroo a pini ke̱tɔ ne̱ kaa waa‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mbe̱yɔmɔ fe̱raa, mo̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ a gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ. Wuribware̱ ya suŋ mò̱ kabɔɔ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ ɔ bo̱ lee mo̱ le̱e̱ Owure Hɛrɔd ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ, na o lee mo̱ le̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ Yudaa awuye e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ko̱ waa mo̱‑ɔ‑rɔ.”

ref: Then Peter went to the house of Mary the mother of John whose other name was Mark. Many of the Lord's followers had come together there and were praying.
text: Mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ a ŋu ane̱ŋ ne̱ a du bo̱ sa mò̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ Yohanee Maak mò̱ nyi Mariya aye̱, na ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ a gyaŋŋe̱ mfe̱ŋ kanye amo̱ ba ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ ba sa Peetroo.

ref: Peter knocked on the gate, and a servant named Rhoda came to answer.
text: Ne̱ Peetroo a da lɔŋ a kanɔ a po̱ne̱‑ɔ se̱, ne̱ bamo̱ kabre ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Rosa‑o a le̱e̱ ya ke̱e̱.

ref: When she heard Peter's voice, she was too excited to open the gate. She ran back into the house and said Peter was standing there.
text: Ne̱ oo pini Peetroo bo̱re̱, ne̱ mò̱ akatɔ a gyi bwe̱e̱tɔ hare̱e̱ ɔ taŋ se̱ a o buŋŋi po̱ne̱‑ɔ sa Peetroo, ne̱ oo kiŋŋi lweero ya tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ Peetroo ye̱re̱ kawu.

ref: “You are crazy!” everyone told her. But she kept saying it was Peter. Then they said, “It must be his angel.”
text: Ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ e̱ lɔ e̱bo̱ŋ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱?” Ne̱ ɔɔ ye̱re̱‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, “A gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ.” Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Wuribware̱ a kabɔɔ ne̱ Peetroo de, ne̱ ka kuŋ mò̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱.”

ref: But Peter kept on knocking, until finally they opened the gate. They saw him and were completely amazed.
text: Saŋ mɔ pɛɛɛ na Peetroo saŋ ɔ da po̱ne̱‑ɔ se̱. Ne̱ ŋkee fe̱raa baa bo̱ buŋŋi, ne̱ bamo̱ a ŋu mò̱‑ɔ, ne̱ aa kpe̱ŋ bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱.

ref: Peter motioned for them to keep quiet. Then he told how the Lord had led him out of jail. He also said, “Tell James and the others what has happened.” After that, he left and went somewhere else.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ ya mò̱ ke̱sare̱e̱ bo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ laatɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a lee mò̱ le̱e̱ tiikpa‑ɔ pɛɛɛ gywii bamo̱. Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ ya tɔwe̱ amo̱ gywii Yakubu na Kristoo awuye ne̱ baa saŋ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ.” Ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ bamo̱ ase̱ mfe̱ŋ be̱e̱ yɔ to̱ŋ ko̱.

ref: The next morning the soldiers who had been on guard were terribly worried and wondered what had happened to Peter.
text: Kaye̱ a ke‑o, ne̱ ase̱ŋ a to̱ asoogyaa awuye ne̱ ba de̱e̱re̱ tiikpa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ. Nte̱tɔ ya waa Peetroo?

ref: Herod ordered his own soldiers to search for him, but they could not find him. Then he questioned the guards and had them put to death. After this, Herod left Judea to stay in Caesarea for a while. Herod Dies
text: Ne̱ Owure Hɛrɔd yɛ bo̱ buwi kpa mò̱. Amaa baa kpa mò̱ kpo̱ne̱. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ oo bise asoogyaa awuye‑o amo̱ ase̱ŋ, ne̱ ɔɔ sa kanɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ mɔɔ bamo̱. Amo̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ ne̱ owure‑o a le̱e̱ Yude̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ ya kye̱na Kaye̱sare̱ya maŋ‑nɔ kafwe̱e̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Hɛrɔd a wu‑o

ref: Herod and the people of Tyre and Sidon were very angry with each other. But their country got its food supply from the region that he ruled. So a group of them went to see Blastus, who was one of Herod's high officials. They convinced Blastus that they wanted to make peace between their cities and Herod,
text: Ne̱ Owure Hɛrɔd duŋ a fwii Tiro na Sidɔŋ e̱maŋ awuye si. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa kpɔwe̱ abɛɛ yɔ a bo̱ ya ŋu mò̱, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ateese gyisɛ pɛɛɛ a le̱e̱ owure a swe̱e̱re̱‑ɔ se̱ ne̱e̱. Pwɛɛ ne̱ baa yɔ‑ɔ, baa tɔwe̱ gywii ɔnyare̱ ne̱ ɔ de̱e̱re̱ owure a lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ a ayaafɔre̱‑ɔ se̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Balasitus‑o ne̱e̱, ne̱ oo sure si fe̱yɛ ɔ kya bamo̱‑rɔ. Ne̱ baa yɔ owure‑o ase̱ ya tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ sa a mò̱ duŋ yuri.

ref: and a day was set for them to meet with him. Herod came dressed in his royal robes. He sat down on his throne and made a speech.
text: Ne̱ owure‑o a sa kake, ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ baa mò̱ akatɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ oo buŋ mò̱ awuretɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ kye̱na mò̱ kawuregya se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ sa se̱ŋsa gywii ase̱sɛ‑ɔ.

ref: The people shouted, “You speak more like a god than a man!”
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ a kyo̱rɔ mò̱ se̱ŋsa sasɛ fɛɛ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, “N gye̱ se̱sɛ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa‑ɔ, kisi ko̱ ne̱e̱.”

ref: At once an angel from the Lord struck him down because he took the honor that belonged to God. Later, Herod was eaten by worms and died.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ a ba kame‑ro ko̱lɔ bo̱ da mò̱, ne̱ aso̱ŋsɔɔ a gyi mò̱ e̱kyo̱rɔwe̱, ne̱ oo wu, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ mo̱ŋ kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ a sa mò̱ kuwure na ke̱dabe̱.

ref: And after Barnabas and Saul had done the work they were sent to do, they went back to Jerusalem with John, whose other name was Mark.
text: Ne̱ Bar‑Nabas na Sɔɔl a lo̱we̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ baa yɔ ke̱mo̱ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa taa Yohanee Maak, ne̱ bamo̱ aa mò̱ a kiŋŋi yɔ Ante̱yɔke̱ya maŋ‑nɔ.

ref: The church at Antioch had several prophets and teachers. They were Barnabas, Simeon, also called Niger, Lucius from Cyrene, Manaen, who was Herod's close friend, and Saul.
text: Saŋ amo̱ Ante̱yɔke̱ya asɔre̱e̱ awuye‑o bo̱ko̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ akyaamɛɛ na abware̱se̱ŋ akaapo̱po̱. Bamo̱ anyare̱ e̱ gye̱ Bar‑Nabas, na Simeyɔŋ na Lukiyus na Manaye̱ŋ na Sɔɔl.

ref: While they were worshiping the Lord and going without eating, the Holy Spirit told them, “Appoint Barnabas and Saul to do the work for which I have chosen them.”
text: Saŋ ko̱, ne̱ ba suŋ Wuribware̱ na ba kra ako̱ŋ, ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ lee Bar‑Nabas na Sɔɔl sa mo̱, na bo̱ ya suŋ mo̱ kusuŋ ne̱ mo̱ a te̱e̱ bamo̱ ke̱mo̱ se̱‑ɔ.”

ref: Everyone prayed and went without eating for a while longer. Next, they placed their hands on Barnabas and Saul to show that they had been appointed to do this work. Then everyone sent them on their way. Barnabas and Saul in Cyprus
text: Ne̱ baa me̱raa se̱ ba kra ako̱ŋ na ba ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱, ne̱ baa ba asare̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ bamo̱ aŋu si yure bamo̱, ne̱ baa suŋ bamo̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Sɔɔl a yɔ mò̱ kpa gye̱ŋkpɛɛsɛ‑ɔ na ɔ ya tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ

ref: After Barnabas and Saul had been sent by the Holy Spirit, they went to Seleucia. From there they sailed to the island of Cyprus.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a kaapo̱ Bar‑Nabas na Sɔɔl, ne̱ baa yɔ Silukiya maŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ baa bo̱rɔ nkyu si yɔ Kiprus kwii si.

ref: They arrived at Salamis and began to preach God's message in the synagogues. They also had John as a helper.
text: Bamo̱ a fo̱ Salamis maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ Yudaa awuye ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ a ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ akyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ gywii ase̱sɛ. Amɔ Yohanee Maak gbaa tii bamo̱ se̱ ɔ kya bamo̱‑rɔ.

ref: Barnabas and Saul went all the way to the city of Paphos on the other end of the island, where they met a Jewish man named Bar-Jesus. He practiced witchcraft and was a false prophet.
text: Ne̱ baa ya fo̱ hare̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ baa te̱e̱ ɛ Pafos maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ. Ne̱ baa to̱ kisibeŋ wuye ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Bar‑Yeesuu‑o mfe̱ŋ. Griiki se̱ŋsa‑rɔ mò̱ ke̱nyare̱ e̱ gye̱ Ilimas. Ɔ gye̱ Yudaanyi ne̱e̱, ne̱ ɔ yɛ mò̱ fe̱raa gye̱ Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ ne̱e̱.

ref: He also worked for Sergius Paulus, who was very smart and was the governor of the island. Sergius Paulus wanted to hear God's message, and he sent for Barnabas and Saul.
text: Kisibeŋ wuye amo̱ mò̱ nyare̱ e̱ gye̱ kwii amo̱ se̱ gominaa ne̱ ɔɔ ye̱re̱ Roma owure dabe̱‑ɔ ayaa‑rɔ, ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Sɛrɛgiyus Pɔɔl. Ne̱ gominaa‑o de kanyiase̱ŋ, ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ Bar‑Nabas na Sɔɔl bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ kpa a o nu abware̱se̱ŋ.

ref: But Bar-Jesus, whose other name was Elymas, was against them. He even tried to keep the governor from having faith in the Lord.
text: Amaa kisibeŋ wuye‑o mo̱ŋ sure si sa bamo̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ maa kpa fe̱yɛ gominaa‑o kɔɔre̱ abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ gyi.

ref: Then Saul, better known as Paul, was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked straight at Elymas
text: Ne̱ Sɔɔl a de̱e̱re̱ kisibeŋ wuye amo̱, na Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o ke̱yaale̱ŋ bo̱ mò̱ ase̱.

ref: and said, “You son of the devil! You are a liar, a crook, and an enemy of everything that is right. When will you stop speaking against the true ways of the Lord?
text: Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Fo̱, Ɔbɔnsam mò̱ gyi! Fo̱ fe̱raa, ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ki bware‑o gye̱ fo̱ do̱ŋ ne̱e̱. Fo̱ e̱ pe̱nna ase̱sɛ ne̱e̱ saŋ ke̱maa, ne̱ ŋkee fo̱ i kyurowi Wuribware̱ a kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o fo̱ i kii kaye̱ba saŋ ke̱maa!

ref: The Lord is going to punish you by making you completely blind for a while.” Suddenly the man's eyes were covered by a dark mist, and he went around trying to get someone to lead him by the hand.
text: Wuribware̱ i gyiiri fo̱ ke̱se̱bɔ. Fo̱ akatɔ i bwee na fo̱ ma lɛɛ ŋu atɔ bo̱ fo̱ saŋ ko̱ pwɛɛ.” Puri‑ro mfe̱ŋ ne̱ aa waa Ilimas fe̱yɛ kibugyii a da mò̱ akatɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱ akatɔ a bwee. Ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ teyi asare̱e̱ o buwi ɔ kpa ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ kra mò̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ na ɔ kaapo̱ mò̱ kpa‑ɔ.

ref: When the governor saw what had happened, he was amazed at this teaching about the Lord. So he put his faith in the Lord. Paul and Barnabas in Antioch of Pisidia
text: Gominaa amo̱ a ŋu ke̱tɔ ne̱ kaa waa‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ kɔɔre̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu gyi, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Pɔɔl a abware̱se̱ŋ ke̱kaapo̱‑ɔ a tɔɔraa mò̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ. Pɔɔl ko̱yɔ Ante̱yɔke̱ya ne̱ a bo̱ Pisidiya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ

ref: Paul and the others left Paphos and sailed to Perga in Pamphylia. But John left them and went back to Jerusalem.
text: Ne̱ Pɔɔl mò̱ aa mò̱ anare̱bɛɛpo̱ a bo̱rɔ ɔpo̱o̱ se̱ le̱e̱ Pafos yɔ Pɛɛga maŋ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ Pamfiliya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ‑rɔ. Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yohanee Maak a yɔwe̱ bamo̱ kiŋŋi yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ.

ref: The rest of them went on from Perga to Antioch in Pisidia. Then on the Sabbath they went to the synagogue and sat down.
text: Ne̱ baa kya se̱ le̱e̱ Pɛɛga mfe̱ŋ ya fo̱ Ante̱yɔke̱ya ne̱ ɔ bo̱ Pisidiya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ. Kukyure kake a fo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa yɔ Yudaa awuye ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ a ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ ya kye̱na.

ref: The God of Israel chose our ancestors, and he let our people prosper while they were living in Egypt. Then with his mighty power he led them out,
text: Wuribware̱ ne̱ Isireelii awuye i suŋ‑o a le̱e̱ ane̱ nana‑ana, ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ baa waa ko̱kyɔ saŋ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ anya, ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Igyipiti swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ Wuribware̱ a ba mò̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ bo̱ lee bamo̱ le̱e̱ Igyipiti awuye a asare̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: Make sure what the prophets have said doesn't happen to you. They said,
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱nꞌ sa se̱, na ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a akyaamɛɛ ne̱ baa dɛɛ kye̱na‑ɔ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ maa nya ba mo̱ne̱ se̱.

ref: He destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan and gave their land to our people.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ gye̱ bamo̱ ke̱gye̱ŋkpɛɛ yaa Kanaŋ swe̱e̱re̱ se̱, ne̱ oo kpuri nsuro nsunoo ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ te mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ taa bamo̱ swe̱e̱re̱ bo̱ kii ane̱ ade̱daapo̱‑ɔ lee.

ref: Later, God removed Saul and let David rule in his place. God said about him, “David the son of Jesse is the kind of person who pleases me most! He does everything I want him to do.”
text: ne̱ Wuribware̱ a lee Sɔɔl le̱e̱ kuwure‑o si, ne̱ ɔɔ taa Deefid bo̱ kye̱na se̱. Mfaanɛɛ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ Deefid ase̱ŋ, ‘Mo̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ Yɛse̱e̱ mò̱ gyi Deefid e̱ gye̱ se̱sɛ ne̱ ɔ pre̱ mo̱‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ waa ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ waa‑ɔ.’

ref: God promised that someone from David's family would come to save the people of Israel, and this one is Jesus.
text: Deefid a ke̱nana‑ɔ‑rɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a lee ɔnyare̱ ko̱ fe̱yɛ o kii Isireelii ɔmo̱rɔwe̱po̱, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ kyɔ tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ ɔ waa a ɔ sa bamo̱‑ɔ. Ɔmo̱rɔwe̱po̱ amo̱ e̱ gye̱ Yeesuu.

ref: But before Jesus came, John was telling everyone in Israel to turn back to God and be baptized.
text: Pwɛɛ ne̱ Yeesuu a le̱e̱ mò̱ kusuŋ‑o kaase̱, Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ gywii Isireelii awuye pɛɛɛ fe̱yɛ bo̱ nu bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ na bo̱ kiŋŋi le̱e̱ bamo̱ e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ na bo̱ bɔ asuu.

ref: Then, when John's work was almost done, he said, “Who do you people think I am? Do you think I am the Promised One? He will come later, and I am not good enough to untie his sandals.”
text: Na saŋ ne̱ Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱‑ɔ e̱ kpa a ɔ lo̱we̱ mò̱ kusuŋ‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii ase̱sɛ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ne̱ ya fa fe̱yɛ mo̱‑ɔ mo̱ e̱ gye̱ ɔmo̱rɔwe̱po̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ yɛ o suŋ a ɔ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ mo̱ne̱‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ e̱ fo̱ kpa. N gye̱ mo̱ e̱ gye̱ ɔnyare̱ amo̱. Amaa mo̱nꞌ nu fe̱yɛ ɔ bo̱ kamɛɛ ɔ ba na mo̱ŋ fo̱ fe̱yɛ m buŋŋe lee mò̱ ase̱be̱ta na m fwe̱e̱ mò̱ ayaa se̱ gbaa!’

ref: Now listen, you descendants of Abraham! Pay attention, all of you Gentiles who are here to worship God! Listen to this message about how to be saved, because it is for everyone.
text: Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana Isireelii awuye, Abraham ananagyi, na mo̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Isireelii awuye‑o pɛɛɛ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i suŋ Wuribware̱‑ɔ, ane̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a suŋ bo̱ tɔwe̱ ɔmo̱rɔwe̱po̱‑ɔ kuŋu si ase̱ŋ gywii!

ref: They did exactly what the Scriptures said they would. Even though they couldn't find any reason to put Jesus to death, they still asked Pilate to have him killed. After Jesus had been put to death, he was taken down from the cross and placed in a tomb.
text: Bamo̱ a waa mò̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ yɛ ba waa mò̱‑ɔ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa lee mò̱ le̱e̱ kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱, ne̱ baa pure mò̱ ke̱gye̱raŋta‑rɔ.

ref: But God raised him from death!
text: Amaa Wuribware̱ a kyiŋŋi mò̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro.

ref: And in another psalm it says, “God will never let the body of his Holy One decay.”
text: Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ a be̱e̱ a bo̱ Deefid a iliŋ‑o‑ro to̱ŋ ko̱ ɛ, ‘Mo̱ a gye̱ fo̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱ timaa‑o si‑o, fo̱ ma sa a mo̱ kayo̱wɔre̱ gyo ke̱gye̱raŋta‑rɔ.’

ref: When David was alive, he obeyed God. Then after he died, he was buried in the family grave, and his body decayed.
text: Deefid a mbe̱e̱‑ɔ se̱, ɔɔ waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ ne̱e̱, ne̱ ŋkee ɔɔ bo̱ wu, ne̱ baa pure mò̱ ade̱daapo̱‑ɔ ase̱, ne̱ oo gyo ke̱gye̱raŋta‑rɔ.

ref: But God raised Jesus from death, and his body did not decay.
text: Amaa amo̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ waa Yeesuu ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kyiŋŋi mò̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro‑o.

ref: As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people begged them to say more about these same things on the next Sabbath.
text: Amɔ saŋ ne̱ Pɔɔl na Bar‑Nabas e̱ le̱e̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ase̱sɛ‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kukyure kake ne̱ ka ba‑ɔ, bo̱ be̱e̱ bo̱ tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ baa tɔwe̱‑ɔ ase̱ŋ be̱e̱ gywii bamo̱.

ref: After the service, many Jews and a lot of Gentiles who worshiped God went with them. Paul and Barnabas begged them all to remain faithful to God, who had treated them with undeserved grace.
text: Ase̱sɛ amo̱ a le̱e̱ ngyaŋŋe̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ kamɛɛ‑rɔ, ne̱ Yudaa awuye bwe̱e̱tɔ na bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye, ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ ba suŋ Wuribware̱‑ɔ, bwe̱e̱tɔ a yɔ bo̱ naa bo̱ gya Pɔɔl na Bar‑Nabas se̱. Ne̱ Pɔɔl na Bar‑Nabas a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ kya se̱ gya Wuribware̱ se̱ na bo̱ ma kine ŋyure ne̱ Wuribware̱ a sa bamo̱‑ɔ.

ref: The next Sabbath almost everyone in town came to hear the message about the Lord.
text: Kukyure kake ne̱ kaa gya se̱‑ɔ, ase̱sɛ kafwe̱e̱ ko̱ ya saŋ, weetee maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ a yɔ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ ya nu abware̱se̱ŋ.

ref: But Paul and Barnabas bravely said: We had to tell God's message to you before we told it to anyone else. But you rejected the message! This proves that you don't deserve eternal life. Now we are going to the Gentiles.
text: Amaa ŋkee gbaa ne̱ Pɔɔl na Bar‑Nabas a nya ŋkpo̱nɔ ba tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱e̱. Ne̱ Pɔɔl yɛ, “Weetee a tiri fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ Yudaa awuye ne̱ ane̱ a gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ gywii. Amaa mo̱ne̱ a tɛɛ kine ase̱ŋ mɔ‑ɔ fe̱raa, mo̱ne̱ fɔŋfɔŋ a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ fo̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ a nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ane̱ e̱ yɔwe̱ mo̱ne̱ na ane̱ yɔ nde̱ se̱ awuye‑o ase̱.

ref: The Lord has given us this command, “I have placed you here as a light for the Gentiles. You are to take the saving power of God to people everywhere on earth.”
text: Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ ane̱ waa‑ɔ ne̱e̱‑ɔ yɛ, ‘Mo̱ e̱ sa a fo̱ laŋŋe̱‑rɔ fe̱yɛ fetiri bo̱ sa nde̱ se̱ awuye, na fo̱ nya mo̱rɔwe̱ bamo̱.’ ”

ref: This message made the Gentiles glad, and they praised what they had heard about the Lord. Everyone who had been chosen for eternal life then put their faith in the Lord.
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa, bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o a nu ane̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa kyo̱rɔ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kyɔ lee fe̱yɛ bo̱ nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ a kii Yeesuu akɔɔre̱gyipo̱.

ref: The message about the Lord spread all over this region.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu ase̱ŋ a saŋŋe̱‑rɔ swe̱e̱re̱ amo̱ pɛɛɛ se̱.

ref: But the Jewish leaders went to some of the important men in the town and to some respected women who were religious. They turned them against Paul and Barnabas and started making trouble for them. They even chased them out of this part of the country.
text: Ne̱ Yudaa awuye‑o a sa ne̱ akye̱e̱ abre̱sɛ ne̱ ba suŋ Wuribware̱‑ɔ na maŋ‑ɔ abre̱sɛ iduŋ a fwii Pɔɔl na Bar‑Nabas se̱, ne̱ baa le̱e̱ ba tɔɔraa bamo̱, ne̱ baa gya bamo̱ le̱e̱ swe̱e̱re̱ amo̱ se̱.

ref: Paul and Barnabas shook the dust from that place off their feet and went on to the city of Iconium.
text: Saŋ ne̱ Pɔɔl na Bar‑Nabas e̱ le̱e̱ maŋ amo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa kpo̱ŋkpaŋ bamo̱ ase̱be̱ta‑rɔ e̱se̱ bo̱ sii mfe̱ŋ bo̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ baa yɔwe̱ maŋ amo̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ ase̱ŋ sa bamo̱. Ne̱ baa kyo̱ŋ yɔ Ikoniyum maŋ‑nɔ.

ref: But the Lord's followers in Antioch were very happy and were filled with the Holy Spirit.
text: Amaa bamo̱ ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi, ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Ante̱yɔke̱ya‑ɔ fe̱raa akatɔ a gyi ase̱ŋ timaa‑o si, ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a kye̱na bamo̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ.

ref: Paul and Barnabas spoke in the synagogue in Iconium, just as they had done at Antioch, and many Jews and Gentiles put their faith in the Lord.
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ya waa Ikoniyum maŋ‑nɔ. Pɔɔl na Bar‑Nabas a yɔ Yudaa awuye ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ ya tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ. Bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa sa se̱ŋsa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ Yudaa awuye na bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o bwe̱e̱tɔ a kii Yeesuu akɔɔre̱gyipo̱.

ref: But the Jews who did not have faith in him made the other Gentiles angry and turned them against the Lord's followers.
text: Amaa Yudaa awuye ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ ase̱ŋ timaa‑o gyi‑o a sa ne̱ maŋ awuye amo̱ a kisi bamo̱ ne̱ baa kii Yeesuu akɔɔre̱gyipo̱‑ɔ.

ref: Paul and Barnabas stayed there for a while, having faith in the Lord and bravely speaking his message. The Lord gave them the power to work miracles and wonders, and he showed that their message about his gift of undeserved grace was true.
text: Amaa hare̱e̱ amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ ooo, Pɔɔl na Bar‑Nabas a kye̱na mfe̱ŋ kyee, ne̱ baa waa ŋkpo̱nɔ ba tɔwe̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu ase̱ŋ. Ne̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ a sa bamo̱ e̱le̱ŋ ne̱ baa bo̱ waa akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ bo̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ ɛ Yeesuu e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ mo̱rɔwe̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ.

ref: The people of Iconium did not know what to think. Some of them believed the Jewish group, and others believed the apostles.
text: Ne̱ maŋ‑ɔ a ke‑ro. Bo̱ko̱ gya Yudaa awuye ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi‑o si, ne̱ bo̱ko̱ mɔ gya Pɔɔl na Bar‑Nabas se̱.

ref: Finally, some Gentiles and Jews, together with their leaders, decided to make trouble for Paul and Barnabas and to stone them to death.
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o bo̱ko̱ na Yudaa awuye‑o bo̱ko̱ na bamo̱ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ a waa bamo̱ mfɛɛre̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ waa Pɔɔl na Bar‑Nabas bo̱rɔkraa, na bo̱ twe̱e̱ bamo̱ abu.

ref: In Lystra there was a man who had been born with paralyzed feet and had never been able to walk.
text: Saŋ amo̱, ɔnyare̱ ko̱ mɔ bo̱ Lisitra mfe̱ŋ, ne̱ baa ko̱we̱ mò̱ ke̱gyabɔɔ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ɔ mo̱ŋ nare̱ mò̱ ayaa se̱ ŋke ŋko̱ se̱.

ref: The man was listening to Paul speak, when Paul saw that he had faith in Jesus and could be healed. So he looked straight at the man
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ kye̱na swe̱e̱re̱ mfe̱ŋ nu ase̱ŋ ne̱ Pɔɔl a tɔwe̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ. Ne̱ Pɔɔl a ŋu fe̱yɛ ɔɔ kɔɔre̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ gyi, ne̱ ɔ kɔɔre̱ o gyi fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ e̱ taare̱ a ɔ kya mò̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ ɔɔ de̱e̱re̱ ke̱gyabɔɔ‑ɔ akatɔ‑rɔ diŋŋ.

ref: When the crowd saw what Paul had done, they yelled out in the language of Lycaonia, “The gods have turned into humans and have come down to us!”
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ a ŋu ke̱tɔ ne̱ Pɔɔl a waa‑ɔ, ne̱ baa le̱e̱ ba fɛɛ‑rɔ bamo̱ Likoniya se̱ŋsa‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ ikisi‑o a buruwi ase̱sɛ ba ane̱ ase̱!”

ref: The people then gave Barnabas the name Zeus, and they gave Paul the name Hermes, because he did the talking.
text: Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ Bar‑Nabas e̱ gye̱ kisi bre̱sɛ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ke̱mo̱ ɛ Siyus, ne̱ bo̱ yɛ Pɔɔl mɔ e̱ gye̱ bamo̱ kisi ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ke̱mo̱ ɛ Hɛɛmɛs, Siyus kyaamɛɛ, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ ɔtɔwe̱po̱ ɔbre̱sɛ.

ref: The temple of Zeus was near the entrance to the city. Its priest and the crowds wanted to offer a sacrifice to Barnabas and Paul. So the priest brought some bulls and flowers to the city gates.
text: Ne̱ Siyus okisipo̱ ne̱ mò̱ kisi ke̱kyaŋ ye̱re̱ maŋ‑ɔ nlaŋbɛɛ‑ɔ a ya kpa mò̱ akpe̱ŋnyare̱ na afe̱raagyi ne̱ a de ato̱to̱o̱ dame̱naŋsɛ‑ɔ baa maŋ‑ɔ kibunogyi‑ro fe̱yɛ mò̱ aa mò̱ ase̱sɛ e̱ kpa a bo̱ ba a bo̱ lɔŋŋɔ Pɔɔl na Bar‑Nabas ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ikisi‑o.

ref: In times past, God let each nation go its own way.
text: De̱daa‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ a yɔwe̱ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ ne̱ baa bo̱rɔ e̱kpa ke̱maa ne̱ ba kpa‑ɔ se̱.

ref: But he showed he was there by the good things he did. God sends rain from heaven and makes your crops grow. He gives food to you and makes your hearts glad.
text: Amaa saŋ ke̱maa mɔ‑ɔ, mò̱ e̱ de̱ŋ ɔ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ bo̱‑rɔ. Mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ o bware‑o, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ sa mo̱ne̱ bware̱ ɔ le̱e̱ so̱so̱, ne̱ ɔ sa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ adɔɔteese i baare a daŋ a waa ko̱kyɔ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ akatɔ i gyi.”

ref: Even after Paul and Barnabas had said all this, they could hardly keep the people from offering a sacrifice to them.
text: Hare̱e̱ Pɔɔl na Bar‑Nabas ase̱ŋ de̱maŋte̱ mɔ ne̱ baa tɔwe̱‑ɔ, aa waa le̱ŋ pwɛɛ ne̱ baa dɛɛ taare̱ kuŋ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ma mɔɔ akpe̱ŋnyare̱‑ɔ bo̱ lɔŋŋɔ bamo̱.

ref: Some Jewish leaders from Antioch and Iconium came and turned the crowds against Paul. They hit him with stones and dragged him out of the city, thinking he was dead.
text: Ne̱ Yudaa awuye bo̱ko̱ a le̱e̱ Ante̱yɔke̱ya na Ikoniyum ba Lisitra mfe̱ŋ bo̱ sa ne̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ a kine Pɔɔl na Bar‑Nabas ase̱ŋ ne̱ baa gya bamo̱ lee‑o si. Ne̱ bamo̱ aa ase̱sɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a daye̱ Pɔɔl abu, ne̱ baa gyiiri mò̱ yaa bo̱ be̱ya maŋ‑ɔ kawu si. Ba fa fe̱yɛ oo wu ne̱e̱, ne̱ baa yɔwe̱ mò̱.

ref: But when the Lord's followers gathered around Paul, he stood up and went back into the city. The next day he and Barnabas went to Derbe. Paul and Barnabas Return to Antioch in Syria
text: Amaa saŋ ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa bo̱ kii Yeesuu akɔɔre̱gyipo̱ maŋ amo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ a ya ye̱re̱ muruwaa mò̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ ko̱so̱ be̱e̱ kiŋŋi yɔ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ. Ke̱mo̱ kaye̱ ŋke‑o, ne̱ mò̱ aa Bar‑Nabas a yɔ De̱re̱be̱e̱ maŋ‑nɔ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Pɔɔl na Bar‑Nabas a kiŋŋi yɔ bamo̱ aye̱ asɔre̱e̱ awuye ase̱‑ɔ

ref: Paul and Barnabas preached the good news in Derbe and won some people to the Lord. Then they went back to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch in Pisidia.
text: Ne̱ Pɔɔl na Bar‑Nabas a tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ timaa bo̱ le̱e̱ Yeesuu kuŋu si De̱re̱be̱e̱ maŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ baa nya agyase̱po̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ mfe̱ŋ. Ne̱ baa kiŋŋi yɔ Lisitra na Ikoniyum na Ante̱yɔke̱ya ne̱ ɔ bo̱ Pisidiya‑o.

ref: They encouraged the followers and begged them to remain faithful. They told them, “We have to suffer a lot before we can get into God's kingdom.”
text: Ne̱ baa sa ne̱ Yeesuu akɔɔre̱gyipo̱‑ɔ a nya bamo̱ ke̱bware̱suŋ‑o ke̱yaale̱ŋ, ne̱ baa sa bamo̱ wɔre̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ kra bamo̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi a kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o‑ro keŋkeŋ. Ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “A tiri fe̱yɛ ane̱ ŋu ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ pwɛɛ na ane̱ yɔ so̱so̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ i gyi kuwure‑o.”

ref: Paul and Barnabas chose some leaders for each of the churches. Then they went without eating and prayed that the Lord would take good care of these leaders who had trusted in the Lord.
text: Ne̱ baa kra ako̱ŋ, ne̱ baa ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱. Ne̱ baa taa asɔre̱e̱ awuye‑o bo̱ waa ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Wuribware̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ fe̱yɛ ɔ de̱e̱re̱ bamo̱ se̱. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ ne̱ baa ba bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ se̱.

ref: Paul and Barnabas went on through Pisidia to Pamphylia,
text: Bamo̱ a muruwi Pisidiya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ kamɛɛ‑rɔ, ne̱ baa yɔ Pamfiliya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱.

ref: where they preached in the town of Perga. Then they went down to Attalia
text: Ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ Pɛɛga maŋ‑nɔ bo̱ fo̱ Ataliya maŋ‑nɔ.

ref: After arriving in Antioch, they called the church together. They told the people what God had helped them do and how he had made it possible for the Gentiles to believe.
text: Saŋ ne̱ baa fo̱ Ante̱yɔke̱ya mfe̱ŋ, ne̱ baa gyaŋŋe̱ asɔre̱e̱ awuye‑o pɛɛɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a bo̱rɔ bamo̱ se̱ waa‑ɔ na ane̱ŋ ne̱ oo buŋŋi kpa sa nde̱ se̱ awuye‑o na bo̱ kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi‑o gywii bamo̱.

ref: Then they stayed there with the followers for a long time.
text: Ne̱ baa kye̱na mfe̱ŋ a Yeesuu akɔɔre̱gyipo̱‑ɔ ase̱ kyee.

ref: This caused trouble, and Paul and Barnabas argued with them about this teaching. So it was decided to send Paul and Barnabas and a few others to Jerusalem to discuss this problem with the apostles and the church leaders. The Church Leaders Meet in Jerusalem
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ aa Pɔɔl na Bar‑Nabas a nya amo̱ ikii na a bo̱ le̱ŋ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa waa ke̱mo̱ fe̱yɛ Pɔɔl na Bar‑Nabas na Yeesuu akɔɔre̱gyipo̱ ko̱ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Ante̱yɔke̱ya‑ɔ yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ na bo̱ ya ŋu Yeesuu a ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ na asɔre̱e̱ abre̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ amo̱ ase̱ŋ.

ref: The men who were sent by the church went through Phoenicia and Samaria, telling how the Gentiles had turned to God. This news made the Lord's followers very happy.
text: Ne̱ asɔre̱e̱ awuye‑o a suŋ bamo̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱. Bamo̱ a yɔ bo̱ bo̱ kpa‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ nde̱ se̱ awuye bwe̱e̱tɔ a ba bo̱ gya Yeesuu a e̱kpa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ gywii Fonikiya na Samariya e̱swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ awuye. Ne̱ aa baa Kristoo akɔɔre̱gyipo̱ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ kakatɔgyi.

ref: When the men arrived in Jerusalem, they were welcomed by the church, including the apostles and the leaders. They told them everything God had helped them do.
text: Bamo̱ a fo̱ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ asɔre̱e̱ awuye na ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ na asɔre̱e̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ a kra bamo̱ nɛɛnɛɛ. Ne̱ Pɔɔl na Bar‑Nabas a tɔwe̱ asuŋ dabe̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a bo̱rɔ bamo̱ se̱ waa bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o gywii bamo̱.

ref: The apostles and church leaders met to discuss this problem about Gentiles.
text: Ne̱ ŋkee ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ na asɔre̱e̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ a gyaŋŋe̱ na bo̱ fa ase̱ŋ amo̱ mfɛɛre̱.

ref: They had talked it over for a long time, when Peter got up and said: My friends, you know that God decided long ago to let me be the one from your group to preach the good news to the Gentiles. God did this so they would hear and obey him.
text: Ne̱ baa gyiiri ase̱ŋ‑ɔ ikii kyee. Ne̱ ŋkee Peetroo a ko̱so̱ ye̱re̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ “Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, mo̱nꞌ nyiŋŋi si fe̱yɛ saŋ‑ɔ Wuribware̱ a lee mo̱ le̱e̱ mo̱ne̱‑rɔ fe̱yɛ mo̱ ya gye̱ mo̱ne̱ ŋkpɛɛ ya tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ timaa bo̱ le̱e̱ Yeesuu kuŋu si gywii ase̱sɛ ko̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o, ne̱ baa bo̱ kii Yeesuu akɔɔre̱gyipo̱.

ref: He knows what is in everyone's heart. And he showed he had chosen the Gentiles, when he gave them the Holy Spirit, just as he had given his Spirit to us.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ ne̱ o nyi ɔke̱maa mfɛɛre̱‑ɔ a sa bamo̱ mò̱ kufwiiŋe timaa‑o fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ sa ane̱‑ɔ dɛɛ bo̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ kpa bamo̱ ase̱ŋ.

ref: Now why are you trying to make God angry by placing a heavy burden on these followers? This burden was too heavy for us or our ancestors.
text: Ne̱ ŋkee nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa a mo̱nꞌ kyɔ Wuribware̱‑rɔ ke̱e̱, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ de tɔso̱rɔ ne̱ ane̱ ade̱daapo̱ na ane̱ fɔŋfɔŋ mo̱ŋ taare̱ so̱rɔ‑ɔ mo̱ne̱ e̱ gya bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye, ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi‑o aŋu si?

ref: Everyone kept quiet and listened as Barnabas and Paul told how God had given them the power to work a lot of miracles and wonders for the Gentiles.
text: Ne̱ ngyaŋŋe̱‑rɔ pɛɛɛ a waa diŋŋ ba nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ Bar‑Nabas na Pɔɔl e̱ tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a ba bamo̱ bo̱ waa akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o.

ref: After they had finished speaking, James said: My friends, listen to me!
text: Bamo̱ a sa bamo̱ se̱ŋsa lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ Yakubu mɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, mo̱nꞌ nu mo̱ ase̱!

ref: “I, the Lord, will return and rebuild David's fallen house. I will build it from its ruins and set it up again.
text: ‘Wuribware̱ yɛ, Amo̱‑ɔ kamɛɛ‑rɔ mo̱ i kiŋŋi a m ba na m bo̱ lɔŋŋɔ Deefid a kuwure ne̱ kaa ke‑ro‑o, na m be̱e̱ pwɛɛ mò̱ maŋ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ oo bwee kii ke̱taakpaŋ‑ɔ bo̱ ye̱ra fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ dɛɛ o du‑o,

ref: Then other nations will turn to me and be my chosen ones. I, the Lord, say this.
text: na se̱sɛ dimaadi pɛɛɛ ne̱ baa saŋ‑ɔ na nde̱ se̱ awuye ne̱ mo̱ a te̱e̱ bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ bo̱ kii mo̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ nya buwi kpa mo̱.

ref: And so, my friends, I don't think we should place burdens on the Gentiles who are turning to God.
text: Ne̱ Yakubu a kya se̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ fa ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ ma lɛɛ tɔɔraa ase̱sɛ mɔ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye, ne̱ ba ba Wuribware̱ ase̱‑ɔ.

ref: We should simply write and tell them not to eat anything that has been offered to idols. They should be told not to eat the meat of any animal that has been strangled or that still has blood in it. They must also not commit any terrible sexual sins.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ki tiri‑o e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ kyo̱rɛɛ wo̱re̱ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ma gyi ateese bɛɛɛ wo̱ kiyee ne̱ ikisi asuŋpo̱ a bo̱ lɔŋŋɔ bamo̱ ikisi‑o, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ a ateese na kiyee‑o a waa iyisi. Na bo̱ ma waa kakye̱e̱kpa na kanyare̱kpa. Na bo̱ ma wo̱ kabo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ te̱ŋ kamo̱ ke̱bɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, na bo̱ ma gyi mbo̱gya.

ref: We must remember that the Law of Moses has been preached in city after city for many years, and every Sabbath it is read in our synagogues. A Letter to Gentiles Who Had Faith in the Lord
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ hare̱e̱ de̱daa‑ɔ swe̱e̱re̱ pɛɛɛ se̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ akyaŋ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ane̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana Yudaa awuye i suŋ Wuribware̱, ne̱ ba kaapo̱ mbraa ne̱ Mosis a sa ane̱‑ɔ kukyure kake ke̱maa amo̱‑rɔ. Ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o a nu bamo̱ a kaapo̱ ane̱ŋ a mbraa‑ɔ.”

ref: The apostles, the leaders, and all the church members decided to send some men to Antioch along with Paul and Barnabas. They chose Silas and Judas Barsabbas, who were two leaders of the Lord's followers.
text: Ne̱ ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ na asɔre̱e̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ na asɔre̱e̱ awuye‑o pɛɛɛ a sure si, ne̱ baa lee bamo̱‑rɔ anyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ asɔre̱e̱ awuye‑o i bu‑o bo̱ tii Pɔɔl na Bar‑Nabas se̱ a bo̱ nya suŋ bamo̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ Ante̱yɔke̱ya. Bamo̱ anyare̱ e̱ gye̱ Yudas ne̱ bo̱ be̱e̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Bar‑Sabas‑ɔ na Silas.

ref: We have heard that some people from here have terribly upset you by what they said. But we did not send them!
text: To, ane̱ a nu fe̱yɛ ane̱ ase̱sɛ ko̱ ne̱ baa le̱e̱ ane̱ ase̱ mfe̱e̱‑ɔ a ba mo̱ne̱ ase̱ a bo̱ ba ase̱ŋ ko̱ bo̱ nye̱ra mo̱ne̱ mfɛɛre̱ bo̱ tɔɔraa mo̱ne̱. Amaa n gye̱ ane̱ aa bamo̱ kanɔ.

ref: We are also sending Judas and Silas, who will tell you in person the same things that we are writing.
text: Yudas na Silas ne̱ bo̱ tii si‑o e̱ tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ane̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ waa wo̱re̱ mɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ kanɔ‑rɔ a bo̱ be̱e̱ gywii mo̱ne̱.

ref: The Holy Spirit has shown us that we should not place any extra burden on you.
text: Ane̱ aa Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a sure si fe̱yɛ ane̱ ma lɛɛ baa ase̱ŋ tɔɔraasɛ ko̱ a ane̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ mo̱ne̱ se̱, amɔ mbraa mɔ wo̱re̱ kpeŋ.

ref: But you should not eat anything offered to idols. You should not eat any meat that still has the blood in it or any meat of any animal that has been strangled. You must also not commit any terrible sexual sins. If you follow these instructions, you will do well. We send our best wishes.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ma kaŋ gyi ateese bɛɛɛ wo̱ kabo̱ ne̱ baa mɔɔ we̱e̱ ikisi si‑o, mo̱ne̱ ma kaŋ gyi mbo̱gya, mo̱ne̱ ma kaŋ wo̱ kabo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ te̱ŋ kamo̱ ke̱bɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ. Na mo̱ne̱ ma waa kakye̱e̱kpa na kanyare̱kpa. Mo̱ne̱ ya taare̱ gya mbraa mɔ se̱ fe̱raa, mo̱ne̱ a waa atɔ. Wuribware̱ ye̱re̱ mo̱ne̱ kamɛɛ.”

ref: The four men left Jerusalem and went to Antioch. Then they called the church members together and gave them the letter.
text: Ne̱ baa suŋ mbɔɔ‑ɔ bo̱ yɔ Ante̱yɔke̱ya. Bamo̱ a fo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱ baa gyaŋŋe̱ Kristoo awuye‑o pɛɛɛ, ne̱ baa taa wo̱re̱‑ɔ sa bamo̱.

ref: When the letter was read, everyone was pleased and greatly encouraged.
text: Kristoo awuye‑o a kare̱ wo̱re̱‑ɔ, ne̱ bamo̱ akatɔ a gyi bo̱ le̱e̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱.

ref: Judas and Silas were prophets, and they spoke a long time, encouraging and helping the Lord's followers.
text: Ne̱ Yudas na Silas ne̱ bo̱ be̱e̱ bo̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ a sa se̱ŋsa ne̱ ɔ sa kakpo̱nɔ na wɔre̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ ke̱bware̱suŋ si‑o bamo̱ ase̱ kyee.

ref: The men from Jerusalem stayed on in Antioch for a while. And when they left to return to the ones who had sent them, the followers wished them well.
text: Bamo̱ a kye̱na bamo̱ ase̱ mfe̱ŋ bo̱ fo̱ saŋ ko̱‑ɔ, ne̱ asɔre̱e̱ awuye a sa bamo̱ kpa fe̱yɛ bo̱ yɔ pe̱. Ne̱ Yudas a kiŋŋi yɔ bamo̱ ne̱ baa suŋ bamo̱‑ɔ ase̱. [

ref: But Paul and Barnabas stayed on in Antioch, where they and many others taught and preached about the Lord. Paul and Barnabas Go Their Separate Ways
text: Ne̱ Pɔɔl na Bar‑Nabas a kye̱na Ante̱yɔke̱ya kafwe̱e̱, ne̱ bamo̱ aa ase̱sɛ‑ɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ a nare̱ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ timaa bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo si. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Pɔɔl na Bar‑Nabas a brawe̱ abɛɛ se̱‑ɔ

ref: Sometime later Paul said to Barnabas, “Let's go back and visit the Lord's followers in the cities where we preached his message. Then we will know how they are doing.”
text: Amɔ bo̱ fo̱ saŋ ko̱‑ɔ, ne̱ Pɔɔl a tɔwe̱ gywii Bar‑Nabas fe̱yɛ, “Sa a ane̱ kiŋŋi ya ka ane̱ ako̱so̱bɛɛ ne̱ ane̱ a tɔwe̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo ase̱ŋ gywii bamo̱‑ɔ kanɔ, na ane̱ nya ŋu ane̱ŋ ne̱ bo̱ te bo̱ du‑o.”

ref: Barnabas wanted to take along John, whose other name was Mark.
text: Ne̱ Bar‑Nabas yɛ, “Yooo,” ne̱ ɔ yɛ ɔ yo̱rɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ aa Yohanee Maak yɔ.

ref: But Paul did not want to, because Mark had left them in Pamphylia and had stopped working with them.
text: Amaa Pɔɔl mo̱ŋ fa fe̱yɛ a de kpa ane̱ŋ, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ mo̱ŋ kye̱na bamo̱ ase̱ bo̱ fo̱ bamo̱ kusuŋ‑o kɛɛ, ne̱ oo kiŋŋi yɔwe̱ bamo̱ bo̱ sii Pamfiliya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱.

ref: Paul and Barnabas argued, then each of them went his own way. Barnabas took Mark and sailed to Cyprus,
text: Ne̱ baa gyiiri ikii, ne̱ baa brawe̱ abɛɛ se̱, ne̱ Bar‑Nabas a sa ne̱ mò̱ aa Maak a taa ko̱re̱e̱ kye̱na‑rɔ yɔ Kiprus kwii si.

ref: but Paul took Silas and left after the followers had placed them in God's care.
text: Ne̱ Pɔɔl a lee Silas, ne̱ mò̱ aa mò̱ a yɔ. Ne̱ Kristoo awuye‑o a ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ na Wuribware̱ gya bamo̱ kamɛɛ yaa bamo̱.

ref: Paul and Silas went back to Derbe and Lystra, where there was a follower named Timothy. His mother was also a follower. She was Jewish, and his father was Greek.
text: Ne̱ Pɔɔl a yɔ De̱re̱be̱e̱ maŋ‑nɔ kyo̱ŋ yɔ Lisitra maŋ‑nɔ, mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Kristoonyi ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Timotii‑o bo̱‑ɔ. Mò̱ nyi gbaa gye̱ Kristoonyi ne̱e̱, ne̱ ɔ be̱e̱ ɔ gye̱ Yudaanyi. Amaa Timotii mò̱ se̱ fe̱raa gye̱ Griikinyi ne̱e̱.

ref: The Lord's followers in Lystra and Iconium said good things about Timothy,
text: Timotii bware Kristoo akɔɔre̱gyipo̱ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Lisitra na Ikoniyum‑o pɛɛɛ ase̱.

ref: and Paul wanted him to go with them. But Paul first had him circumcised, because all the Jewish people around there knew that Timothy's father was Greek.
text: Pɔɔl a kpa fe̱yɛ mò̱ aa Timotii yɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ te̱ŋ Timotii ko̱twe̱e̱tu. Ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ ɔɔ waa ane̱ŋ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ Yudaa awuye ne̱ bo̱ te mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ nyi fe̱yɛ Timotii mò̱ se̱ gye̱ Griikinyi. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ bamo̱ ase̱ Timotii gbaa gye̱ Griikinyi ne̱e̱.

ref: As Paul and the others went from city to city, they told the followers what the apostles and leaders in Jerusalem had decided, and they urged them to follow these instructions.
text: Bamo̱ a naa e̱maŋ amo̱ se̱‑ɔ na ba kaapo̱ Kristoo awuye‑o mbraa ne̱ ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ na asɔre̱e̱ abre̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ a kyo̱rɛɛ sa bamo̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ gya ane̱ŋ a mbraa‑ɔ se̱.

ref: The churches became stronger in their faith, and each day more people put their faith in the Lord. Paul's Vision in Troas
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ asɔre̱e̱ awuye‑o pɛɛɛ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi a waa ko̱kyɔ, ne̱ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ a bo̱ tii bamo̱ se̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kaapo̱ Pɔɔl dee‑ro ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ yɔ Makedoniya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ

ref: Paul and his friends went through Phrygia and Galatia, but the Holy Spirit would not let them preach in Asia.
text: Ne̱ baa bo̱rɔ kyo̱ŋ Firigiya na Galatiya e̱swe̱e̱re̱ se̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o mo̱ŋ sa bamo̱ kpa fe̱yɛ bo̱ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ swe̱e̱re̱ amo̱ pɛɛɛ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ Asiya‑o si.

ref: After they arrived in Mysia, they tried to go into Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus would not let them.
text: Saŋ ne̱ baa fo̱ Misiya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa kpa fe̱yɛ bo̱ kii bo̱rɔ kigyise si yɔ Bitiniya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱, amaa Yeesuu a kufwiiŋe‑o mo̱ŋ sa bamo̱ kpa.

ref: So they went on through Mysia until they came to Troas.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa kya se̱ bo̱rɔ Misiya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ ane̱ŋ‑aaa, ne̱ baa bo̱ fo̱ ɔpo̱o̱ kɛɛ Trowas maŋ‑nɔ.

ref: During the night, Paul had a vision of someone from Macedonia who was standing there and begging him, “Come over to Macedonia and help us!”
text: Ke̱mo̱ kanye, ne̱ Pɔɔl a ku dee fe̱yɛ ɔ de̱e̱re̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ Makedoniya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ ɔpo̱o̱ nno̱ŋ a bo̱ŋbe̱‑ɔ se̱, na ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ye̱re̱ ɔ te̱e̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ fo̱, ba mfe̱e̱ bo̱ kya ane̱‑rɔ.”

ref: After Paul had seen the vision, we began looking for a way to go to Macedonia. We were sure that God had called us to preach the good news there. Lydia Becomes a Follower of the Lord
text: Pɔɔl a de̱ŋ ku dee amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ane̱ a waa siraa na ane̱ yɔ Makedoniya, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ a fa mfɛɛre̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ ane̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ yɔ mfe̱ŋ ya tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ timaa bo̱ le̱e̱ Yeesuu kuŋu si gywii ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Lidiya a kii Kristoonyi Filipii‑o

ref: We sailed straight from Troas to Samothrace, and the next day we arrived in Neapolis.
text: Ne̱ ane̱ a taa ko̱re̱e̱ le̱e̱ Trowas yɔ Samotrakee kwii si. Ke̱mo̱ kaye̱ ŋke‑o, ne̱ ane̱ a yɔ Niyapolis maŋ‑nɔ.

ref: From there we went to Philippi, which is a Roman colony in the first district of Macedonia. We spent several days in Philippi.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ a le̱e̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ ne̱ ane̱ a nare̱ ayaa‑rɔ yɔ Filipii ne̱ ɔ gye̱ Makedoniya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ maŋ dabe̱‑ɔ se̱. Roma awuye ya pwɛɛ maŋ amo̱. Ne̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ gya Roma mbraa se̱. Ane̱ a gyi ŋke kafwe̱e̱ ko̱ mfe̱ŋ.

ref: Then on the Sabbath we went outside the city gate to a place by the river, where we thought there would be a Jewish meeting place for prayer. We sat down and talked with the women who came.
text: Ane̱ a fo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, kukyure kake ne̱ ke̱ gya se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ane̱ a yɔ bo̱ŋ kɛɛ ya buwi kpa Yudaa awuye ne̱ bo̱ te maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ a mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ba gyaŋŋe̱ ba suŋ Wuribware̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ ane̱ a to̱ bamo̱ a ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ bo̱ŋ kɛɛ. Akye̱e̱ dooo e̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ. Ne̱ ane̱ a kye̱na, ne̱ ane̱ aa akye̱e̱‑ɔ a sa se̱ŋsa.

ref: One of them was Lydia, who was from the city of Thyatira and sold expensive purple cloth. She was a worshiper of the Lord God, and he made her willing to accept what Paul was saying.
text: Akye̱e̱ amo̱ ne̱ ane̱ a sa se̱ŋsa gywii‑o ɔko̱ ke̱nyare̱ e̱ gye̱ Lidiya. Ɔ gye̱ awaagya timaa ofepo̱ ne̱e̱, ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ Tiyatira maŋ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ Asiya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ. Ɔkye̱e̱ amo̱ i suŋ Wuribware̱ nɛɛnɛɛ. Ne̱ Wuribware̱ a buŋŋi mò̱ mfɛɛre̱ na ɔ kɔɔre̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ Pɔɔl e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ gyi.

ref: Then after she and her family were baptized, she kept on begging us, “If you think I really do have faith in the Lord, come stay in my home.” Finally, we accepted her invitation. Paul and Silas Are Put in Jail
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ Lidiya na mò̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ awuye pɛɛɛ a bɔ asuu. Amo̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii ane̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ya fa fe̱yɛ mo̱ a kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱raa, mo̱nꞌ bo̱ so̱we̱ mo̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ.” Ne̱ ɔɔ po̱pɔre̱ ane̱, ne̱ ane̱ a yɔ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa tii Pɔɔl na Silas tiikpa Filipii‑o

ref: One day on our way to the place of prayer, we were met by a slave girl. She had a spirit in her that gave her the power to tell the future. By doing this she made a lot of money for her owners.
text: Ŋke ŋko̱ ane̱ a yɔ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kpa‑ɔ, ne̱ ke̱nya kakye̱e̱sɛɛ ko̱ ne̱ kagyaa te mò̱ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ amo̱se̱‑ɔ ɔ taare̱ ɔ tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ waa nyaŋe̱‑ɔ a gyaŋŋaa ane̱. Ɔɔ nya atanne̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ bo̱ le̱e̱ mò̱ ŋkperi amo̱‑rɔ sa mò̱ anyaŋpe̱.

ref: The girl followed Paul and the rest of us, and she kept yelling, “These men are servants of the Most High God! They are telling you how to be saved.”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ gya ane̱ aa Pɔɔl se̱ ɔ fɛɛ‑rɔ ɔ gya se̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ase̱sɛ mɔ gye̱ Nyiŋkpe̱ŋ-kyo̱ŋ-atɔ‑pɛɛɛ-Bware̱ mò̱ ayaafɔre̱ ne̱e̱! Ba tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ taare̱ a ɔ mo̱rɔwe̱ mo̱ne̱‑ɔ ba gywii mo̱ne̱!”

ref: This went on for several days. Finally, Paul got so upset that he turned and said to the spirit, “In the name of Jesus Christ, I order you to leave this girl alone!” At once the evil spirit left her.
text: Ɔɔ waa ane̱ŋ bo̱ gya ane̱ se̱ kyee. Ne̱ ŋkee Pɔɔl a nyite kpo̱ne̱, ne̱ oo buruwaa tɔwe̱ gywii kagyaa ne̱ ka te kakye̱e̱sɛɛ amo̱ se̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Yeesuu Kristoo ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ, ŋ yɛ fo̱ ko̱so̱ le̱e̱ kakye̱e̱sɛɛ amo̱ se̱!” Puri amo̱‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ kagyaa amo̱ a ko̱so̱ le̱e̱ kakye̱e̱sɛɛ amo̱ se̱.

ref: When the girl's owners realized they had lost all chances for making more money, they grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them into court.
text: Amɔ kakye̱e̱sɛɛ‑ɔ mò̱ anyaŋpe̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ kpa ne̱ bo̱ ye̱re̱ se̱ ba nya atanne̱‑ɔ a lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa kra Pɔɔl na Silas, ne̱ baa gyiiri bamo̱ yaa iwurekpa.

ref: The crowd joined in the attack on Paul and Silas. Then the officials tore the clothes off the two men and ordered them to be beaten with a whip.
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa ne̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ ye̱re̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a tii bamo̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana se̱, ne̱ baa waa Pɔɔl na Silas awo̱re̱fɔɔ. Ne̱ ase̱ŋgyipo̱‑ɔ a lee Pɔɔl na Silas awaagya na e̱kaare̱, ne̱ baa sa kpa fe̱yɛ bo̱ da bamo̱ ko̱kyo̱kywe̱e̱.

ref: After they had been badly beaten, they were put in jail, and the jailer was told to guard them carefully.
text: Bamo̱ a da bamo̱ ko̱kyo̱kywe̱e̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa taa bamo̱ yaa tii, ne̱ bo̱ yɛ mò̱ ne̱ ɔ de̱e̱re̱ tiikpa amo̱ se̱‑ɔ twe̱e̱ ke̱kyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ nɛɛnɛɛ.

ref: The jailer did as he was told. He put them deep inside the jail and chained their feet to heavy blocks of wood.
text: Bamo̱ a sa mò̱ kanɔ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ taa Pɔɔl na Silas waa ke̱kyaŋ amo̱ ke̱mo̱ ne̱ ke̱ bo̱ do̱dɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ da bamo̱ paŋ na e̱kpe̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ. Ne̱ ŋkee ɔɔ twe̱e̱‑rɔ nɛɛnɛɛ.

ref: About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing praises to God, while the other prisoners listened.
text: Amɔ waa kaye̱ nse̱na‑ɔ, ne̱ Pɔɔl na Silas a kyɔ le̱e̱ ba ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ na ba waa asɔre̱e̱ iliŋ ba sa Wuribware̱. Na ase̱sɛ ne̱ bamo̱ gbaa baa tii bamo̱‑ɔ i nu bamo̱ ase̱.

ref: When the jailer woke up and saw that the doors were open, he thought that the prisoners had escaped. He pulled out his sword and was about to kill himself.
text: Ne̱ ɔde̱e̱re̱se̱po̱‑ɔ a kyiŋŋi, ne̱ oo ŋu fe̱yɛ akyaŋ a e̱po̱ne̱‑ɔ a taye̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ ɔ fa ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a se̱re̱ le̱e̱ ne̱e̱. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ oo gyiiri mò̱ ke̱paŋ lee a ɔ bo̱ mɔɔ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱.

ref: But Paul shouted, “Don't harm yourself! No one has escaped.”
text: Amaa Pɔɔl a faa‑rɔ keŋkeŋ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ ma mɔɔ fo̱ e̱ye̱e̱! Ane̱ pɛɛɛ bo̱ mfe̱e̱!”

ref: The jailer asked for a torch and went into the jail. He was shaking all over as he knelt down in front of Paul and Silas.
text: Ne̱ ɔde̱e̱re̱se̱po̱ amo̱ a sa ne̱ baa baa mò̱ fetiri, ne̱ ɔɔ waa me̱naŋ lwee ke̱kyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ ya kpuni aŋurii Pɔɔl na Silas akatɔ‑rɔ na ɔ yo̱ŋ.

ref: After he had led them out of the jail, he asked, “What must I do to be saved?”
text: Ne̱ oo lee bamo̱ baa kawu, ne̱ oo bise bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Abre̱sɛ, e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa a Wuribware̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ mo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱?”

ref: They replied, “Have faith in the Lord Jesus and you will be saved! This is also true for everyone who lives in your home.”
text: Ne̱ baa be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ aa fo̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ awuye ya kɔɔre̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo gyi, Wuribware̱ e̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ mo̱ne̱.”

ref: Then Paul and Silas told him and everyone else in his house about the Lord.
text: Ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ gywii mò̱ aa mò̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ awuye pɛɛɛ.

ref: While it was still night, the jailer took them to a place where he could wash their cuts and bruises. Then he and everyone in his home were baptized.
text: Ke̱mo̱ kanye amo̱ dɛɛ ne̱ ɔde̱e̱re̱se̱po̱ amo̱ a ya ba nkyu bo̱ kpɛɛ bamo̱ e̱lɔ ne̱ baa nya bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ maŋ ase̱ŋgyipo̱‑ɔ a da bamo̱ ko̱kyo̱kywe̱e̱‑ɔ se̱. Ne̱ baa bɔ mò̱ aa mò̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ awuye asuu puri amo̱‑rɔ.

ref: They were very glad they had put their faith in God. After this, the jailer took Paul and Silas to his home and gave them something to eat.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ yaa Pɔɔl na Silas mò̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ ya sa bamo̱ ateese, ne̱ baa gyi. Ne̱ mò̱ aa mò̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ awuye akatɔ a gyi, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ŋkee fe̱raa baa kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ gyi.

ref: The next morning the officials sent some police with orders for the jailer to let Paul and Silas go.
text: Ke̱mo̱ kaye̱ ŋke‑o, ne̱ Roma awuye ase̱ŋgyipo̱‑ɔ a suŋ purisii awuye ya tɔwe̱ gywii ɔde̱e̱re̱se̱po̱‑ɔ ɛ, “Sa a anyare̱ amo̱ kpa a bo̱ nare̱.”

ref: The jailer told Paul, “The officials have ordered me to set you free. Now you can leave in peace.”
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔde̱e̱re̱se̱po̱ amo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii Pɔɔl fe̱yɛ, “Ase̱ŋgyipo̱‑ɔ a suŋ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ fe̱yɛ n taye̱ mo̱ne̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱nꞌ nare̱, Wuribware̱ yaa mo̱ne̱ dame̱naŋsɛ.”

ref: But Paul told the police, “We are Roman citizens, and the Roman officials had us beaten in public without giving us a trial. They threw us into jail. Now do they think they can secretly send us away? No, they cannot! They will have to come here themselves and let us out.”
