text: Ne̱ Pɔɔl a tɔwe̱ gywii purisii awuye‑o fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ mo̱ŋ waa ke̱se̱ŋ bɔye̱ ko̱ hare̱e̱ baa bu ane̱ ke̱pɔ, amaa bamo̱ mɔ baa da ane̱ ko̱kyo̱kywe̱e̱ lamaŋ‑nɔ, na ane̱ mɔ ane̱ gbaa ane̱ gye̱ Roma maŋ agyi ne̱e̱. Ne̱ baa tii ane̱ tiikpa. Ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ ba kpa a bo̱ kwe̱e̱rɔ taye̱ ane̱. Ane̱ maa sure! Roma awuye ase̱ŋgyipo̱‑ɔ fɔŋfɔŋ ba mfe̱e̱ na bo̱ bo̱ lee ane̱.”

ref: When the police told the officials that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens, the officials were afraid.
text: Ne̱ purisii awuye‑o a ya tɔwe̱ gywii Roma awuye ase̱ŋgyipo̱‑ɔ. Bamo̱ a nu fe̱yɛ Pɔɔl na Silas gye̱ Roma maŋ agyi ne̱e̱‑ɔ,† ne̱ kufu a nya bamo̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ baa waa bamo̱ bo̱rɔkraa.

ref: So they came and apologized. They led them out of the jail and asked them to please leave town.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa ya waa bamo̱ kisiri, ne̱ baa taye̱ bamo̱, ne̱ baa ko̱re̱ bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ le̱e̱ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: But Paul and Silas went straight to the home of Lydia, where they saw the Lord's followers and encouraged them. Then they left.
text: Ne̱ Pɔɔl na Silas a le̱e̱ tiikpa‑ɔ yɔ Lidiya lɔŋ‑nɔ. Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ bamo̱ aa bamo̱ ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi‑o a gyaŋŋe̱, ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ a sa wɔre̱‑ɔ gywii bamo̱, ne̱ baa nare̱ bo̱ yii.

ref: After Paul and his friends had traveled through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they went on to Thessalonica. A synagogue was in that city.
text: Ne̱ Pɔɔl na Silas a bo̱rɔ Amfipolis na Apoloniya e̱maŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ baa ba Tisalonikaa maŋ‑nɔ.

ref: Some of them believed what Paul had said, and they became followers with Paul and Silas. Some Gentiles and many important women also believed the message.
text: Ne̱ Yudaa awuye bo̱ko̱ a kɔɔre̱ gyi, ne̱ baa tii Pɔɔl na Silas si. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ akye̱e̱ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ bo̱ko̱ na Griiki awuye ne̱ ba suŋ Wuribware̱‑ɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ a tii Pɔɔl na Silas si.

ref: The Jewish leaders were jealous and got some troublemakers who hung around the marketplace to start a riot in the city. They wanted to drag Paul and Silas out to the mob, and so they went straight to Jason's home.
text: Ne̱ Yudaa awuye‑o bo̱ko̱ mɔ a le̱e̱ ba ka Pɔɔl na Silas ko̱kwe̱e̱, ne̱ baa kpɔwe̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ naa mbo̱re̱ se̱, ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱ ko̱ko̱rɔ‑ɔ bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱. Ne̱ baa le̱e̱ ba waa e̱lawo̱ na kusuŋ bɔye̱ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ. Bamo̱ a nyi fe̱yɛ Pɔɔl na Silas a bo̱ so̱we̱ ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Yasɔŋ a lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa tii lɔŋ amo̱ ba kpa bamo̱, na bo̱ lee bamo̱ baa lamaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: But when they did not find them there, they dragged out Jason and some of the Lord's followers. They took them to the city authorities and shouted, “Paul and Silas have been upsetting things everywhere. Now they have come here,
text: Amaa bamo̱ a mo̱ŋ ŋu bamo̱ lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ baa gyiiri Yasɔŋ na bamo̱ ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi‑o bo̱ko̱ yaa maŋ‑ɔ abre̱sɛ akatɔ‑rɔ, na ba fɛɛ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, “Anyare̱ ko̱ a ba ane̱ maŋ mɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ baa waa ase̱ŋ bɔye̱ to̱ŋ ke̱maa ne̱ baa bo̱rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: and Jason has welcomed them into his home. All of them break the laws of the Roman Emperor by claiming that someone named Jesus is king.”
text: Ne̱ Yasɔŋ a kɔɔre̱ bamo̱ bo̱ waa mò̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ. Ba nye̱ra ane̱ mbraa ne̱ Roma owure dabe̱‑ɔ a sa ane̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ yɛ owure ko̱ be̱e̱ ɔ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Yeesuu.”

ref: That same night the Lord's followers sent Paul and Silas on to Berea, and after they arrived, they went to the synagogue.
text: Kanye a de̱ŋ fo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi‑o a ba Pɔɔl na Silas bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ Beroya maŋ‑nɔ. Bamo̱ a fo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa yɔ Yudaa awuye a ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: The people in Berea were much nicer than those in Thessalonica, and they gladly accepted the message. Day after day they studied the Scriptures to see if these things were true.
text: Mfe̱ŋ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ gye̱ ase̱sɛ timaa bo̱ kyɔ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Tisalonikaa maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ. Bamo̱ a nu abware̱se̱ŋ bo̱ le̱e̱ Yeesuu kuŋu si‑o, ne̱ baa kpa ane̱ ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ, ne̱ baa pee akatɔ suye abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ ase̱ŋ kake ke̱maa na bo̱ ke̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱ŋ ne̱ Pɔɔl e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ aaa!

ref: Many of them put their faith in the Lord, including some important Greek women and several men.
text: Ne̱ Yudaa awuye bwe̱e̱tɔ na Griiki akye̱e̱ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ ko̱ na Griiki anyare̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ a kɔɔre̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ gyi.

ref: When the Jewish leaders in Thessalonica heard that Paul had been preaching God's message in Berea, they went there and caused trouble by turning the crowds against Paul.
text: Amaa saŋ ne̱ Yudaa awuye ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Tisalonikaa‑o a nu fe̱yɛ Pɔɔl a kaapo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ Beroya gbaa‑ɔ, ne̱ baa sa ne̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱ ko̱ko̱rɔ‑ɔ a baa ase̱ŋ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ bo̱ kye Pɔɔl.

ref: At once the followers sent Paul down to the coast, but Silas and Timothy stayed in Berea.
text: Puri amo̱‑rɔ, ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi‑o a yaa Pɔɔl ɔpo̱o̱ kɛɛ, amaa Silas na Timotii fe̱raa a sii Beroya mfe̱ŋ.

ref: Some men went with Paul as far as Athens, and then returned with instructions for Silas and Timothy to join him as soon as possible. Paul in Athens
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa yaa Pɔɔl ɔpo̱o̱ kɛɛ‑ɔ a yaa mò̱ ya bo̱ fo̱ hare̱e̱ Atɛnis maŋ‑nɔ,† ne̱ baa kiŋŋi ba Beroya, ne̱ baa taa ŋkra le̱e̱ Pɔɔl ase̱ baa Silas na Timotii fe̱yɛ bo̱ waa me̱naŋ ba mò̱ ase̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ bo̱‑ɔ. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ Pɔɔl a waa Atɛnis‑o

ref: While Paul was waiting in Athens, he was upset to see all the idols in the city.
text: Pɔɔl saŋ o gywii Silas na Timotii Atɛnis maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, mò̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ a nye̱ra bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ oo ŋu ikisi bwe̱e̱tɔ maŋ amo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱.

ref: Some of them were Epicureans and some were Stoics, and they started arguing with him. People were asking, “What is this know-it-all trying to say?” Some even said, “Paul must be preaching about foreign gods! That's what he means when he talks about Jesus and about people rising from death.”
text: To, akaapo̱po̱ ko̱ mɔ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ. Bamo̱ bo̱ko̱ gye̱ Ipikurus‑o† awuye . Bo̱ko̱ mɔ gye̱ Stowaa awuye . Ne̱ baa le̱e̱ ba gyiiri Pɔɔl ikii, ne̱ bo̱ko̱ yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ ne̱ adabe̱dabe̱se̱ŋ wuye mɔ e̱ tɔwe̱ ne̱e̱?” Ne̱ bo̱ko̱ mɔ yɛ, “A waa to̱ŋ ko̱ ikisi po̱pwɛɛ ko̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ tɔwe̱‑ɔ.” Ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ Pɔɔl e̱ kaapo̱ Yeesuu ase̱ŋ, na ɔ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ i kyiŋŋi a bo̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro.

ref: They brought Paul before a council called the Areopagus, and said, “Tell us what your new teaching is all about.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa yaa Pɔɔl bamo̱ se̱ŋgyikpa ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Ariyopagus‑o. Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Ane̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ fo̱ kaapo̱ ane̱‑rɔ ke̱kaapo̱ po̱pwɛɛ mɔ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kaapo̱‑ɔ.

ref: We have heard you say some strange things, and we want to know what you mean.”
text: Ase̱ŋ amo̱ ako̱ ne̱ ane̱ i nu fe̱yɛ fo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ gye̱ ase̱ŋ po̱pwɛɛ ane̱ ase̱. Ane̱ mɔ ane̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ane̱ nu amo̱ kaase̱.”

ref: More than anything else the people of Athens and the foreigners living there loved to hear and to talk about anything new.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ Atɛnis maŋ agyi‑o na afɔ ne̱ bo̱ te mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, a mo̱ŋ tiri bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ba bamo̱ saŋ pɛɛɛ ba tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ, na ba nu ase̱ŋ po̱pwɛɛ ne̱ aa ba‑ɔ.

ref: So Paul stood up in front of the council and said: People of Athens, I see that you are very religious.
text: Ne̱ Pɔɔl a ko̱so̱ ye̱re̱ se̱ŋgyikpa‑ɔ mfe̱ŋ, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Abre̱sɛ, mo̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ kpa ke̱maa se̱ mo̱ne̱ Atɛnis awuye mo̱ne̱ akatɔ i gyi ikisi bwe̱e̱tɔ kusuŋ si.

ref: This God made the world and everything in it. He is Lord of heaven and earth, and he doesn't live in temples built by human hands.
text: Mò̱ ke̱nyare̱ e̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱. Mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔɔ twe̱e̱ kaye̱ na ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ke̱ bo̱ kamo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ. Mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa nyaŋpe̱, so̱so̱ na kaase̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ɔ mo̱ŋ te isuŋkpa akyaŋ ne̱ ase̱sɛ a pwɛɛ‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: He doesn't need help from anyone. He gives life, breath, and everything else to all people.
text: Mò̱‑ɔ mò̱ maa kpa ko̱tɔko̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ kusuŋ ne̱ ane̱ a waa‑ɔ‑rɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ sa ɔke̱maa mɔ ŋkpa na ŋe na ke̱tɔ ke̱maa.

ref: From one person God made all nations who live on earth, and he decided when and where every nation would be.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ twe̱e̱ se̱sɛ dimaadi, ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱rɔ se̱sɛ ko̱ŋko̱ amo̱ se̱ bo̱ waa nsuro ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ, ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ baa kye̱na kaye̱‑rɔ pɛɛɛ. Na pwɛɛ ne̱ ɔɔ dɛɛ twe̱e̱ se̱sɛ‑ɔ, ɔɔ kyɔ tɔwe̱ saŋ na mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔke̱maa e̱ kye̱na‑ɔ.

ref: God has done all this, so that we will look for him and reach out and find him. He isn't far from any of us,
text: Wuribware̱ a waa faanɛɛ ne̱e̱ a bo̱ nya buwi kpa mò̱, na saŋse̱ bo̱ ŋu mò̱ saŋ ne̱ ba buwi ba kpa mò̱‑ɔ. Kase̱ŋtiŋ mò̱ aa ɔke̱maa ase̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱ e̱fɔ.

ref: and he gives us the power to live, to move, and to be who we are. “We are his children,” just as some of your poets have said.
text: Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ aliŋwaapo̱‑ɔ ɔko̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ‑ɔ, ‘Mò̱ ya sa ne̱ ane̱ te, ne̱ ane̱ naa, ne̱ ɔɔ waa ane̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ane̱ gye̱‑ɔ.’ Ne̱ mo̱ne̱ aliŋwaapo̱‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ mɔ a kyo̱rɛɛ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ane̱ gbaa ane̱ gye̱ mò̱ gyi‑ana‑ɔ ne̱e̱.’

ref: Since we are God's children, we must not think that he is like an idol made out of gold or silver or stone. He isn't like anything that humans have thought up and made.
text: Ane̱ a tɛɛ ane̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑ana‑ɔ fe̱raa, ane̱ ma fa fe̱yɛ mò̱ kudugyi du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kisi ne̱ nyiŋkpasɛ ne̱ o nyi‑ro‑o a ba atanne̱ pipee bɛɛɛ afufuri bɛɛɛ ke̱fo̱re̱ bo̱ waa‑ɔ.

ref: In the past, God forgave all this because people did not know what they were doing. But now he says that everyone everywhere must turn to him.
text: Wuribware̱ a nyite ke̱tɔ ne̱ baa waa saŋ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi Wuribware̱ na mò̱ e̱kpa‑ɔ. Amaa mbe̱yɔmɔ saŋ a fo̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ sa to̱ŋ ke̱maa ase̱sɛ kanɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ nu bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱, na bo̱ kiŋŋi le̱e̱ bamo̱ e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ.

ref: He has set a day when he will judge the world's people with fairness. And he has chosen the man Jesus to do the judging for him. God has given proof of this to all of us by raising Jesus from death.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔɔ sa kake na ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ oo lee‑o bo̱ gyi kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ ase̱ŋ nɛɛnɛɛ‑ɔ. Ɔɔ ba ɔnyare̱ amo̱ a kikyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro‑o bo̱ kaapo̱ ɔke̱maa amo̱‑ɔ.”

ref: As soon as the people heard Paul say a man had been raised from death, some of them started laughing. Others said, “We will hear you talk about this some other time.”
text: Saŋ ne̱ baa nu Pɔɔl a tɔwe̱ kikyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro ase̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ bamo̱ bo̱ko̱ a mɔse̱ mò̱, ne̱ bamo̱ bo̱ko̱ mɔ yɛ, “Ane̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ fo̱ be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ amo̱ gywii ane̱ saŋ ko̱.”

ref: When Paul left the council meeting,
text: Ne̱ Pɔɔl a le̱e̱ ngyaŋŋe̱ amo̱‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ.

ref: some of the men put their faith in the Lord and went with Paul. One of them was a council member named Dionysius. A woman named Damaris and several others also put their faith in the Lord.
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ bo̱ko̱ a bo̱ tii Pɔɔl si, ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ gyi. Bamo̱‑rɔ bo̱ko̱ e̱ gye̱ Diyonisiyus ne̱ ɔ gye̱ Ariyopagus ase̱ŋgyipo̱‑ɔ ɔko̱, ne̱ oo nu Pɔɔl ase̱ŋ‑ɔ, na ɔkye̱e̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Damaris‑o, na ase̱sɛ ko̱ be̱e̱.

ref: Paul left Athens and went to Corinth,
text: Amo̱‑ɔ kamɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ Pɔɔl a le̱e̱ Atɛnis yɔ Korintoo maŋ‑nɔ.

ref: Paul then moved into the house of a man named Titius Justus, who worshiped God and lived next door to the synagogue.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ ya kye̱na ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaanyi ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Tiitus Yusutus, ne̱ o suŋ Wuribware̱‑ɔ a lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ. Mò̱ lɔŋ‑ɔ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ maa ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ‑ɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: Crispus was the leader of the synagogue. He and everyone in his family put their faith in the Lord. Many others in Corinth also heard the message, and all the people who had faith in the Lord were baptized.
text: Amaa ase̱sɛ ko̱ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu gyi. Bamo̱ ɔko̱ e̱ gye̱ Krisipus, ne̱ saŋ amo̱ ɔ dɛɛ ɔ gye̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ‑ɔ ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ, na mò̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ awuye‑o. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ Korintoo awuye bwe̱e̱tɔ ne̱ baa nu Pɔɔl ase̱ŋ‑ɔ a kɔɔre̱ gyi, ne̱ baa bɔ asuu.

ref: One night, Paul had a vision, and in it the Lord said, “Don't be afraid to keep on preaching. Don't stop!
text: Ŋke ŋko̱ kanye, ne̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ dee‑ro gywii Pɔɔl fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ ma se̱re̱ kufu, amaa kya se̱ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ, na fo̱ ma yɔwe̱.

ref: I am with you, and you won't be harmed. Many people in this city belong to me.”
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ n gya fo̱ kamɛɛ. Ɔko̱ maa taare̱ a ɔ waa fo̱ ke̱bɔye̱ ke̱maa, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ maŋ mɔ‑rɔ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ gye̱ mo̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱e̱.”

ref: Paul stayed on in Corinth for a year and a half, teaching God's message to the people.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ Pɔɔl a kye̱na mfe̱ŋ kaapo̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ abware̱se̱ŋ kasu na ke̱be̱gya.

ref: While Gallio was governor of Achaia, some of the Jewish leaders got together and grabbed Paul. They brought him into court
text: Saŋ ne̱ Galiyo a gyi Akaya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ mfe̱ŋ a Roma kigominaa‑o, ne̱ Yudaa awuye‑o pɛɛɛ a gyaŋŋe̱, ne̱ baa kra Pɔɔl, ne̱ baa yaa mò̱ iwurekpa.

ref: and said, “This man is trying to make our people worship God in a way that is against our Law!”
text: Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Ɔnyare̱ mɔ e̱ kpa a ɔ po̱pɔre̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱e̱ a bo̱ suŋ Wuribware̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ a kye ane̱ mbraa‑ɔ ne̱e̱!”

ref: Even before Paul could speak, Gallio said, “If you were charging this man with a crime or some other wrong, I would have to listen to you.
text: Mfe̱ŋ, ne̱ Pɔɔl e̱ kpa a o lee kanɔ, ne̱ Gominaa Galiyo a tɔwe̱ gywii Yudaa awuye‑o fe̱yɛ, “Amo̱‑ɔ dɛɛ a gye̱ kusuŋ bɔye̱ bɛɛɛ ase̱ŋ bɔye̱ ko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ waa ne̱e̱, weetee mo̱ i gyi kanyite na n nu mo̱ne̱ ase̱.

ref: But since this concerns only words, names, and your own law, you will have to take care of it yourselves. I refuse to judge such matters.”
text: Amaa amo̱ a gye̱ ase̱ŋkpare̱gyi ko̱ na anyare̱ ko̱ kaase̱ na mo̱ne̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ke̱bware̱suŋ mbraa‑ɔ fe̱raa, mo̱ne̱ fɔŋfɔŋ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ mo̱nꞌ lɔŋŋɔ amo̱. Ma waa ane̱ŋ a ase̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱ŋgyipo̱!”

ref: Then he sent them out of the court.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ gya bamo̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ iwurekpa mfe̱ŋ.

ref: The crowd grabbed Sosthenes, the Jewish leader, and beat him up in front of the court. But none of this mattered to Gallio. Paul Returns to Antioch in Syria
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ a kra Sɔste̱nɛs ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ ɔ gye̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ‑ɔ ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa da mò̱ se̱ŋgyikpa akatɔ‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ. Amaa a mo̱ŋ tɔɔraa Galiyo kafo̱ŋfwe̱e̱ gbaa. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Pɔɔl a kiŋŋi yɔ asɔre̱e̱ awuye ase̱ Ante̱yɔke̱ya‑ɔ

ref: After Paul had stayed for a while with the Lord's followers in Corinth, he told them goodbye and sailed on to Syria with Aquila and Priscilla. But before he left, he had his head shaved at Cenchreae because he had made a promise to God.
text: Ne̱ Pɔɔl a kye̱na Korintoo awuye ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi‑o ase̱ ŋke bwe̱e̱tɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ, ne̱ mò̱ aa Prisikila na Akwila a bo̱rɔ nkyu si ba yɔ mò̱ aye̱ Siriya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱. Pwɛɛ ne̱ baa dɛɛ le̱e̱ Ke̱ŋkre̱ya maŋ‑nɔ lwee ko̱re̱e̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, ɔɔ kpɛɛ mò̱ kuŋu bo̱ le̱e̱ e̱taŋ ko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ waa‑ɔ se̱.

ref: The three of them arrived in Ephesus, where Paul left Priscilla and Aquila. He then went into the synagogue to talk with the people there.
text: Ne̱ baa te̱ŋ ɔpo̱o̱‑rɔ, ne̱ baa fo̱ Ifisus maŋ‑nɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ Yudaa awuye ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ ya kaapo̱ bamo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ na ɔ po̱pɔre̱ bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ kɔɔre̱ mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ gyi.

ref: After staying there for a while, he left and visited several places in Galatia and Phrygia. He helped the followers there to become stronger in their faith. Apollos in Ephesus
text: Mò̱ a kyee Ante̱yɔke̱ya maŋ‑nɔ kafwe̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ bo̱rɔ Galatiya na Firigiya e̱swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ yɔ ɔ sa mfe̱ŋ a Kristoo awuye‑o wɔre̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Prisikila na Akwila a kaapo̱ Apolos Wuribware̱ a e̱kpa‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ

ref: A Jewish man named Apollos came to Ephesus. Apollos had been born in the city of Alexandria. He was a very good speaker and knew a lot about the Scriptures.
text: Saŋ amo̱ Yudaanyi ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Apolos, ne̱ baa ko̱we̱ mò̱ Alɛsandriya maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ a ba Ifisus mfe̱ŋ. Ɔ taare̱ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa nɛɛnɛɛ, ne̱ o nyi abware̱se̱ŋ nɛɛnɛɛ.

ref: He also knew much about the Lord's Way, and he spoke about it with great excitement. What he taught about Jesus was right, but all he knew was John's message about baptism.
text: Oo suye ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu a ase̱ŋ‑ɔ e̱kpa‑rɔ dame̱naŋsɛ, ne̱ ɔɔ le̱ŋ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ keŋkeŋ ɔ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ, na ɔ kaapo̱ Yeesuu ase̱ŋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ. Amaa ase̱ŋ‑ɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ oo nyi‑o e̱ gye̱ asuu suyo ne̱ Yohanee a bɔ‑ɔ.

ref: Apollos started speaking bravely in the synagogue. But when Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they took him to their home and helped him understand God's Way even better.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ waa kakpo̱nɔ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ. Saŋ ne̱ Prisikila na Akwila a nu mò̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ tɔwe̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa yaa mò̱ bamo̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ ya kaapo̱ mò̱ Wuribware̱ a e̱kpa‑ɔ‑rɔ kyɛɛkyɛɛ.

ref: Apollos decided to travel through Achaia. So the Lord's followers wrote letters, encouraging the followers there to welcome him. After Apollos arrived in Achaia, he was a great help to everyone who had put their faith in the Lord Jesus because of God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: Ne̱ Apolos a waa mò̱ mfɛɛre̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ yɔ Akaya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi, ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Ifisus‑o a sure mò̱ ko̱yɔ‑ɔ se̱, ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi, ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Akaya‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, mò̱ ya ba, bo̱ kra mò̱ nɛɛnɛɛ. Saŋ ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ɔɔ kya bamo̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a ŋu bamo̱ e̱wɛɛ‑ɔ ne̱ ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ baa bo̱ kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi‑o‑ro bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: He got into fierce arguments with the Jewish people, and in public he used the Scriptures to prove that Jesus is the Messiah.
text: Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔɔ ba ikii le̱ŋle̱ŋsɛ bo̱ gyi Yudaa awuye a e̱mo̱‑ɔ se̱ lamaŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ ba abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ bo̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ Kristoo ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ Yeesuu.

ref: While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul traveled across the hill country to Ephesus, where he met some of the Lord's followers.
text: Saŋ ne̱ Apolos a le̱e̱ Ifisus maŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ Akaya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱, ɔ bo̱ Korintoo maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ Pɔɔl a kya se̱ bo̱rɔ Asiya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ ya fo̱ Ifisus. Ne̱ ɔɔ ya ŋu Yeesuu ase̱ŋ agyase̱po̱ bo̱ko̱.

ref: He asked them, “When you put your faith in Jesus, were you given the Holy Spirit?” “No!” they answered. “We have never even heard of the Holy Spirit.”
text: Ne̱ oo bise bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Saŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi‑o, Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a ywe̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ se̱ aaa?” Ne̱ baa be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ nu gbaa fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o bo̱‑rɔ.”

ref: “Then why were you baptized?” Paul asked. They answered, “Because of what John taught.”
text: Ne̱ Pɔɔl a be̱e̱ bise fe̱yɛ, “To, ne̱ ŋkee nte̱tɔ asuu ke̱bɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a bɔ‑ɔ ne̱e̱.” Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Asuu suyo ne̱ Yohanee a bɔ‑ɔ.”

ref: Paul replied, “John baptized people so they would turn to God. But he also told them someone else was coming, and they should put their faith in him. Jesus is the one that John was talking about.”
text: Ne̱ Pɔɔl yɛ, “Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ gywii ase̱sɛ fe̱yɛ bo̱ bɔ asuu bo̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ baa nu bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱, ne̱ baa kiŋŋi le̱e̱ bamo̱ e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii Yudaa awuye‑o fe̱yɛ bo̱ kɔɔre̱ mò̱ ne̱ ɔ ba kamɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ gyi. Mò̱ e̱ gye̱ Yeesuu‑o.”

ref: After the people heard Paul say this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
text: Saŋ ne̱ baa nu amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa bɔ asuu ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ.

ref: Then Paul placed his hands on them. The Holy Spirit was given to them, and they spoke unknown languages and prophesied.
text: Ne̱ Pɔɔl a ba mò̱ asare̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ bamo̱ se̱, ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a ywe̱e̱ bamo̱ se̱, ne̱ baa sa e̱se̱ŋsa ne̱ bamo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ maa nu e̱mo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a lee bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱‑ɔ.

ref: There were about twelve men in this group.
text: Anyare̱ amo̱ e̱ waa fe̱yɛ kudu anyɔ.

ref: but some of them were stubborn and refused to believe. In front of everyone they said terrible things about God's Way. Paul left and took the followers with him to the lecture hall of Tyrannus. He spoke there every day
text: Amaa bamo̱ bo̱ko̱ a waa ke̱se̱bɔrɔle̱ŋ, ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ sure si na bo̱ kɔɔre̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ gyi, ne̱ baa ye̱re̱ ngyaŋŋe̱ amo̱‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ bɔye̱ bo̱ kye ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu a e̱kpa‑ɔ. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ Pɔɔl a le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ taa bamo̱ ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi‑o yaa mò̱ ase̱. Na kake ke̱maa mò̱ aa bamo̱ e̱ gyaŋŋe̱ ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Tiranus‑o sukuu‑ro, ne̱ ɔ kaapo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔ po̱pɔre̱ ase̱sɛ fe̱yɛ bo̱ kɔɔre̱ mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ gyi.

ref: God gave Paul the power to work great miracles.
text: Na Wuribware̱ bɔ Pɔɔl se̱ ɔ waa akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ dabe̱.

ref: People even took handkerchiefs and aprons that had touched Paul's body, and they carried them to everyone who was sick. All of the sick people were healed, and the evil spirits went out.
text: Hare̱e̱ mò̱ aŋke̱te̱e̱ na mprakɛɛ ne̱ ɔ dɛɛ o de‑o gbaa, baa taa mmo̱ yaa bamo̱ ne̱ ba lɔ‑ɔ ase̱, ne̱ bamo̱ alɔ a lo̱we̱. Ne̱ ibrisi a ko̱so̱ bamo̱ ne̱ i te bamo̱ se̱‑ɔ se̱.

ref: when an evil spirit said to them, “I know Jesus! And I have heard about Paul. But who are you?”
text: Amaa ibrisi‑o a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ŋ nyi Yeesuu, ne̱ ŋ nyi Pɔɔl ase̱ŋ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ nsɛ‑ana e̱ gye̱ mo̱ne̱?”

ref: Then the man with the evil spirit jumped on them and beat them up. They ran out of the house, naked and bruised.
text: Ne̱ ɔnyare̱ ne̱ ibrisi te mò̱ se̱‑ɔ a lwee bamo̱‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ da bamo̱ keŋkeŋ kyaŋŋe̱ bamo̱ atɔ buŋsɛ‑rɔ. Ne̱ baa se̱re̱ le̱e̱ mò̱ lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ, na bamo̱ ŋyo̱wɔre̱ se̱ a swɛɛ swɛɛ na n le̱e̱ mbo̱gya na bo̱ko̱ mɔ a le̱e̱ ke̱te̱bɔŋburoŋ.

ref: When the Jews and Gentiles in Ephesus heard about this, they were so frightened that they praised the name of the Lord Jesus.
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Ifisus maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ a nu amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ kufu a nya bamo̱ pɛɛɛ, ne̱ baa bu ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu ke̱nyare̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: Many who were followers now started telling everyone about the evil things they had been doing.
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi‑o bwe̱e̱tɔ a ba lamaŋ‑nɔ bo̱ tɔwe̱ e̱bɔye̱ ne̱ baa waa bo̱ fo̱ kake amo̱‑ɔ.

ref: So the Lord's message spread and became even more powerful. The Riot in Ephesus
text: Bo̱ le̱e̱ mfaanɛɛ a atɔ dabe̱ mɔ se̱‑ɔ, ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu ase̱ŋ a ye̱re̱ keŋkeŋ, ne̱ aa saŋŋe̱‑rɔ yɔ to̱ŋ ke̱maa. Ase̱ŋ ne̱ aa ba Ifisus maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ

ref: After all this had happened, Paul decided to visit Macedonia and Achaia on his way to Jerusalem. Paul had said, “From there I will go on to Rome.”
text: Amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ kamɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ Pɔɔl a waa mò̱ mfɛɛre̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ bo̱rɔ Makedoniya na Akaya e̱swe̱e̱re̱ se̱, na ɔ kyo̱ŋ yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ. Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ ya yɔ mfe̱ŋ, ke̱mo̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ na ŋ yɔ Roma maŋ‑nɔ.”

ref: So he sent his two helpers, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia. But he stayed on in Asia for a while.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ oo suŋ mò̱ akyarɔpo̱ anyɔ Timotii na Irasitus bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ Makedoniya, ne̱ mò̱‑ɔ mò̱ a sii Asiya swe̱e̱re̱‑ɔ se̱ kafwe̱e̱.

ref: At that time there was serious trouble because of the Lord's Way.
text: Saŋ mɔ dɛɛ ne̱ ase̱ŋ dabe̱ a ba Ifisus bo̱ le̱e̱ Yeesuu a e̱kpa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ se̱.

ref: Demetrius brought together everyone who was in the same business and said: Friends, you know we make a good living at this.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ mò̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana abwaye̱po̱ na bamo̱ ne̱ bamo̱ kusuŋ du fe̱yɛ mò̱ lee‑o bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana, mo̱ne̱ nyi fe̱yɛ ane̱ kibaare a le̱e̱ kusuŋ mɔ‑rɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: But you have surely seen and heard how this man Paul is upsetting a lot of people, not only in Ephesus, but almost everywhere in Asia. He claims that the gods we humans make are not really gods at all.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ, mo̱ne̱ fɔŋfɔŋ a ŋu, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a nu ke̱tɔ ne̱ Pɔɔl amo̱ e̱ waa‑ɔ. Ɔ yɛ ikisi ne̱ se̱sɛ fɔŋfɔŋ a waa‑ɔ mo̱ŋ de ke̱yaale̱ŋ pɛɛɛ. Ne̱ ɔɔ taare̱ kyurowi ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ mfɛɛre̱ Ifisus maŋ‑nɔ mfe̱e̱ na Asiya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ pɛɛɛ.

ref: Everyone will start saying terrible things about our business. They will stop respecting the temple of the goddess Artemis, who is worshiped in Asia and all over the world. Our great goddess will be forgotten!
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ kufu bo̱‑rɔ fe̱yɛ ane̱ kusuŋ mɔ e̱ nya ke̱nyare̱ bɔye̱. N gye̱ ane̱ŋ wo̱re̱, kufu be̱e̱ ko̱ bo̱‑rɔ fe̱yɛ ane̱ kisi dabe̱ Aatimis a suŋkpa dabe̱‑ɔ maa lɛɛ ba a ɔ bo̱ kii sɛye̱, na ane̱ kisi dabe̱ mɔ ne̱ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ bo̱ Asiya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ na kaye̱‑rɔ pɛɛɛ i suŋ‑o ma lɛɛ nya ke̱nyare̱!”

ref: When the workers heard this, they got angry and started shouting, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
text: Ase̱sɛ amo̱ a nu ase̱ŋkpare̱gyi mɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ bamo̱ iduŋ a fwii, ne̱ baa le̱e̱ ba fɛɛ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ Ifisus kisi Aatimis gye̱ kisi dabe̱.”

ref: Soon the whole city was in a riot, and some men grabbed Gaius and Aristarchus, who had come from Macedonia with Paul. Then everyone in the crowd rushed to the place where the town meetings were held.
text: Ne̱ e̱lawo̱ amo̱ a kɔɔre̱ se̱ maŋ amo̱ pɛɛɛ‑rɔ. Ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ naa ba waa ase̱ŋ amo̱‑ɔ a gyiiri Makedoniya awuye anyɔ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ bamo̱ ɛ Gayus na Aristaakus, ne̱ bamo̱ aa Pɔɔl naa‑ɔ, ne̱ baa yaa bamo̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ e̱ gyaŋŋe̱‑ɔ.

ref: Paul wanted to go out and speak to the people, but the Lord's followers would not let him.
text: Ne̱ Pɔɔl fɔŋfɔŋ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ yɔ lamaŋ amo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ ya sa se̱ŋsa gywii ase̱sɛ‑ɔ, amaa bamo̱ ne̱ ba kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu ba gyi‑o mo̱ŋ sure.

ref: A few of the local officials were friendly to Paul, and they sent someone to warn him not to go.
text: Ne̱ swe̱e̱re̱ amo̱ se̱ a ayaale̱ŋpo̱‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ mò̱ nyare̱‑ana‑ɔ a ba ŋkra bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ mò̱, ko̱re̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ ma kaŋ yɔ lamaŋ e̱gyaŋŋe̱kpa amo̱ mfe̱ŋ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kufu de bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ko̱ e̱ mɔɔ mò̱.

ref: Some of the people in the meeting were shouting one thing, and others were shouting something else. Everyone was completely confused, and most of them did not even know why they were there.
text: Na ngyaŋŋe̱‑ɔ‑rɔ pɛɛɛ de e̱lawo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Bamo̱ bo̱ko̱ e̱ fɛɛ‑rɔ ba tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ ko̱, ne̱ bo̱ko̱ mɔ e̱ fɛɛ‑rɔ ba tɔwe̱ bamo̱ lee, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ bo̱ko̱ gbaa mo̱ŋ nyi ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ baa gyaŋŋe̱‑ɔ.

ref: Several of the Jewish leaders pushed a man named Alexander to the front of the crowd and started telling him what to say. He motioned with his hand and tried to explain what was going on.
text: Yudaa awuye ne̱ bo̱ te maŋ amo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ a lee bamo̱‑rɔ ɔko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ Alɛsanda‑ɔ fe̱yɛ ɔ ye̱re̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ akatɔ‑rɔ sa se̱ŋsa. Ase̱sɛ amo̱ bo̱ko̱ a te̱e̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ ba‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ ya bamo̱ ke̱sare̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ laatɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa o gywii lamaŋ‑ɔ na bo̱ nya ŋu fe̱yɛ mò̱ aa mò̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana Yudaa awuye mo̱ŋ gyi bɔye̱.

ref: But when the crowd saw that he was Jewish, they all shouted for two hours, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
text: Amaa bamo̱ a pini fe̱yɛ ɔ gye̱ Yudaanyi‑o, ne̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ mɔ a kpo̱ra faa‑rɔ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ ko̱ŋko̱ dɔŋhwe̱re̱e̱ e̱nyɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ Ifisus kisi Aatimis gye̱ kisi dabe̱!”

ref: Finally, a town official made the crowd be quiet. Then he said: People of Ephesus, who in the world doesn't know that our city is the center for worshiping the great goddess Artemis? Who doesn't know that her image which fell from heaven is right here?
text: Ne̱ ŋkee maŋ‑ɔ krakye̱e̱ bre̱sɛ a bo̱ yuri lamaŋ‑ɔ. Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana Ifisus awuye, ɔke̱maa nyi fe̱yɛ ane̱ maŋ mɔ e̱ gye̱ kisi dabe̱ Aatimis na mò̱ suŋkpa dabe̱ a ɔde̱e̱re̱se̱po̱‑ɔ, mò̱ aa mò̱ ke̱fo̱re̱ ne̱ kaa le̱e̱ so̱so̱ le̱e̱ da‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: No one can deny this, and so you should calm down and not do anything foolish.
text: Ɔke̱maa maa taare̱ a o gyiiri amo̱‑ɔ ikii. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ mo̱nꞌ yuri e̱ye̱e̱, na mo̱ne̱ ma waa ke̱bɔye̱ ko̱.

ref: You have brought men in here who have not robbed temples or spoken against our goddess.
text: Mo̱ne̱ a baa anyare̱ mɔ mfe̱e̱, a mo̱ŋ gye̱ fe̱yɛ baa ywii le̱e̱ ane̱ suŋkpa dabe̱‑ɔ‑rɔ ne̱e̱ bɛɛɛ baa tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ bɔye̱ ko̱ bo̱ kye ane̱ kisi‑o ne̱e̱.

ref: If Demetrius and his workers have a case against these men, we have courts and judges. Let them take their complaints there.
text: Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ Demeetriyus na mò̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana abwaye̱po̱ i de ɔko̱ ase̱ŋ, ane̱ de igominaa na ŋke ne̱ ane̱ de bo̱ gyi ase̱ŋ‑ɔ, amo̱se̱‑ɔ ase̱ŋ ke̱maa ne̱ bo̱ de‑o, bo̱ taa baa mfe̱ŋ.

ref: But if you want to do more than that, the matter will have to be brought before the city council.
text: Amaa ke̱tɔ ko̱ e̱ bo̱‑rɔ na ko̱ kyɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa fe̱raa, amɔ mo̱nꞌ lɔŋŋɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ maŋ agyi e̱ gyaŋŋe̱ ba waa mbraa ba sa maŋ‑ɔ.

ref: After saying this, he told the people to leave.
text: Mò̱ a tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ mɔ a brawe̱‑rɔ.

ref: When the riot was over, Paul sent for the followers and encouraged them. He then told them goodbye and left for Macedonia.
text: To, e̱lawo̱ amo̱ a yɔwe̱‑ɔ kamɛɛ‑rɔ, ne̱ Pɔɔl a te̱e̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi‑o pɛɛɛ bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ bo̱ sa bamo̱ wɔre̱, ne̱ ɔɔ kra bamo̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ yɔ ne̱e̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ yɔ Makedoniya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱.

ref: As he traveled from place to place, he encouraged the followers with many messages. Finally, he went to Greece
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ nare̱ swe̱e̱re̱ amo̱ se̱ e̱maŋ se̱ waa bamo̱ wɔre̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ fo̱ Akaya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱.

ref: and stayed there for three months. Paul was about to sail to Syria. But some of the Jewish leaders plotted against him, so he decided to return by way of Macedonia.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ kye̱na mfe̱ŋ aferi asa. Ɔɔ waa siraa fe̱yɛ ɔ taa ko̱re̱e̱ na o kiŋŋi yɔ Siriya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱, ne̱ oo ŋu fe̱yɛ Yudaa awuye‑o e̱ kpa a bo̱ baa mò̱ ase̱ŋ, amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ ɔɔ waa mò̱ mfɛɛre̱ fe̱yɛ o kiŋŋi a ɔ bo̱rɔ Makedoniya be̱e̱.

ref: With him were Sopater, son of Pyrrhus from Berea, and Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica. Gaius from Derbe was also with him, and so were Timothy and the two Asians, Tychicus and Trophimus.
text: Mò̱ aa Pirus mò̱ gyi Sopata ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ Beroya maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, na Aristaakus na Sikundus ne̱ baa le̱e̱ Tisalonikaa maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, na Gayus ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ De̱re̱be̱e̱ maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, na Tikikus na Trofimus ne̱ baa le̱e̱ Asiya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ, na Timotii ya yɔ.

ref: They went on ahead to Troas and waited for us there.
text: Ne̱ baa gye̱ ane̱ ŋkpɛɛ ɔpo̱o̱ se̱ ya gywii ane̱ Trowas maŋ‑nɔ.

ref: After the Festival of Thin Bread, we sailed from Philippi. Five days later we met them in Troas and stayed there for a week. Paul's Last Visit to Troas
text: Wuribware̱-a‑kya-ane̱-yɔwe̱ a ateese‑o kigyi a kyo̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ ane̱ a le̱e̱ Filipii maŋ‑nɔ bo̱rɔ nkyu si. Bo̱ fo̱ ŋke nnuu‑o, ne̱ ane̱ a to̱ bamo̱ Trowas, ne̱ ane̱ a gyi kadaawɔkywe̱e̱ ko̱ŋko̱ mfe̱ŋ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Pɔɔl a yɔ Trowas lalalo̱we̱‑ɔ

ref: In the upstairs room where we were meeting, there were a lot of lamps.
text: Na ifetiri bwe̱e̱tɔ e̱ laŋŋe̱ ke̱baŋso̱ro̱o̱ ke̱kyaŋ ne̱ ane̱ te ke̱mo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: A young man by the name of Eutychus was sitting on a window sill. While Paul was speaking, the young man got very sleepy. Finally, he went to sleep and fell three floors all the way down to the ground. When they picked him up, he was dead.
text: Ne̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Yutikus‑o te mfaŋse̱re̱e̱ se̱ saŋ ne̱ Pɔɔl e̱ sa se̱ŋsa‑ɔ, na idi de mò̱ kafwe̱e̱, ne̱ lalalo̱we̱‑ɔ ɔɔ bo̱ di idi. Ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ akyaŋ asa ke̱baŋso̱ro̱o̱ lalalo̱we̱ a ke̱kyaŋ ne̱ ane̱ te ke̱mo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ a mfaŋse̱re̱e̱‑ɔ se̱ le̱e̱ da‑rɔ swe̱e̱re̱. Bamo̱ a ya yase̱ mò̱‑ɔ, ɔɔ kyɔ wu.

ref: Paul went down and bent over Eutychus. He took him in his arms and said, “Don't worry! He's alive.”
text: Amaa Pɔɔl a yɔ swe̱e̱re̱ mfe̱ŋ, ne̱ ɔɔ ya di ke̱yaafɔre̱ amo̱ se̱, ne̱ oo powi mò̱, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ma sa a a tiri mo̱ne̱ na ɔ ke̱e̱!”

ref: After Paul had gone back upstairs, he broke bread, and ate with us. He then spoke until dawn and left.
text: Ne̱ Pɔɔl a kiŋŋi yɔ so̱so̱ a ke̱kyaŋ ne̱ ane̱ te ke̱mo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ be̱e̱ ya gyi bodobodoo. Pɔɔl a sa se̱ŋsa gywii bamo̱ kyee bo̱ fo̱ kyɔwe̱ ke̱le̱e̱be̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ yɔwe̱ bamo̱ o yii.

ref: Then the followers took the young man home alive and were very happy. The Voyage from Troas to Miletus
text: Ne̱ baa taa ke̱yaafɔre̱ amo̱ yaa pe̱ na ɔ ke̱e̱, ne̱ bamo̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ a yuri bamo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Pɔɔl a le̱e̱ Trowas yɔ Mileetus‑o

ref: Paul decided to travel by land to Assos. The rest of us went on ahead by ship, and we were to take him aboard there.
text: Ne̱ ane̱ a yɔ akatɔ‑rɔ ya taa ko̱re̱e̱ bo̱rɔ nkyu si yɔ Asos maŋ‑nɔ, mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Pɔɔl e̱ ya lwee ane̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ. Mò̱ ya tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ waa ane̱ŋ, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ fe̱raa naa swe̱e̱re̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: When he met us in Assos, he came aboard, and we sailed on to Mitylene.
text: Saŋ ne̱ ane̱ a fo̱ Asos‑o, ne̱ ane̱ a taa Pɔɔl ane̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ ane̱ a yɔ Mitileenee maŋ‑nɔ.

ref: The next day we came to a place near Chios, and the following day we reached Samos. The day after that we sailed to Miletus.
text: Ke̱mo̱ kaye̱ ŋke‑o, ne̱ ane̱ a be̱e̱ bo̱rɔ nkyu si le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ ya kyo̱ŋ Kiyos kwii si, ne̱ ane̱ a be̱e̱ bo̱rɔ nkyu si, ne̱ ane̱ a ya fo̱ Samus kwii si. Ne̱ ane̱ a bo̱ fo̱ Mileetus maŋ‑nɔ.

ref: Paul had decided to sail on past Ephesus, because he did not want to spend too much time in Asia. He was in a hurry and wanted to be in Jerusalem in time for Pentecost. Paul Says Goodbye to the Church Leaders of Ephesus
text: Ne̱ Pɔɔl a waa mò̱ mfɛɛre̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ bo̱rɔ nkyu si na ɔ kyo̱ŋ Ifisus si ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ maa nya a ɔ nye̱ra saŋ Asiya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ. Ɔɔ kpa me̱naŋ a ɔ fo̱ Yɛro̱salɛm, mò̱ e̱ taare̱ de, na ɔ fo̱ Yɛro̱salɛm pwɛɛ na Pentikos kake‑o dɛɛ fo̱. Ase̱ŋ ne̱ Pɔɔl a tɔwe̱ bo̱ kra Ifisus asɔre̱e̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ

ref: From Miletus, Paul sent a message for the church leaders at Ephesus to come and meet with him.
text: Ne̱ Pɔɔl a sa ŋkra le̱e̱ Mileetus bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ Ifisus asɔre̱e̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ba a bo̱ gyaŋŋaa mò̱.

ref: When they got there, he said: You know everything I did during the time I was with you when I first came to Asia.
text: Saŋ ne̱ baa fo̱ Mileetus‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ nyi ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ a waa ke̱mo̱ saŋ ne̱ m bo̱ mo̱ne̱ ase̱‑ɔ, hare̱e̱ le̱e̱ mo̱ kake gye̱ŋkpɛɛsɛ ne̱ mo̱ a ba Asiya swe̱e̱re̱ mɔ se̱‑ɔ.

ref: When I preached in public or taught in your homes, I didn't hold back from telling anything that would help you.
text: Mo̱ne̱ nyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ŋ ba ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ke̱ e̱ kya mo̱ne̱‑rɔ bo̱ kwe̱e̱rɔ mo̱ne̱ se̱ saŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ na mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ lamaŋ‑nɔ na mo̱ne̱ e̱lɔŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ.

ref: I don't know what will happen to me in Jerusalem, but I must obey God's Spirit and go there.
text: Amaa mbe̱yɔmɔ, Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o e̱ tɔwe̱ o gywii mo̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm, amaa mo̱ŋ nyi ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ ya waa mo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ.

ref: In every city I visit, I am told by the Holy Spirit that I will be put in jail and will be in trouble in Jerusalem.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ŋ nyi‑o e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ to̱ŋ ke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ e̱ yɔ‑ɔ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a yii mo̱ se̱ saŋ ke̱maa fe̱yɛ ba tii mo̱ Yɛro̱salɛm, na bo̱ sa a n gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ.

ref: But I don't care what happens to me, as long as I finish the work the Lord Jesus gave me to do. And this work is to tell the good news about God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: Amaa ŋkpa bɛɛɛ lowi‑o, ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ lo̱we̱ mo̱ kusuŋ ne̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu a sa mo̱ fe̱yɛ n tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ timaa‑o bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a ŋu ane̱ e̱wɛɛ se̱‑ɔ.

ref: I have gone from place to place, preaching to you about God's kingdom, but now I know that none of you will ever see me again.
text: Mo̱ a nare̱ laa mo̱ne̱ pɛɛɛ mɔ, na mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ Wuribware̱ a kuwure‑o‑ro ase̱ŋ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱. Na mbe̱yɔmɔ ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ ɔko̱ maa lɛɛ ŋu mo̱.

ref: I have told you everything God wants you to know.
text: Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ŋ ba ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ɔɔ waa‑ɔ bo̱ kwe̱e̱rɔ mo̱ne̱ se̱.

ref: Look after yourselves and everyone the Holy Spirit has placed in your care. Be like shepherds to God's church. It is the flock he bought with the blood of his own Son.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱re̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ se̱ na ase̱sɛ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a taa bo̱ waa mo̱ne̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ se̱ nɛɛnɛɛ. Na mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱re̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu asɔre̱e̱ ase̱sɛ se̱, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ e̱sanne̱ ɔde̱e̱re̱se̱po̱ e̱ de̱e̱re̱ mò̱ e̱sanne̱ se̱ nɛɛnɛɛ‑ɔ. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Yeesuu a ba mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ lowi bo̱ lee bamo̱ le̱e̱ Ɔbɔnsam ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ sa ne̱ baa kii mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ase̱sɛ.

ref: I know that after I am gone, others will come like fierce wolves to attack you.
text: Ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ a yɔwe̱ mo̱ne̱ mɔ‑ɔ, ase̱sɛ i lwee mo̱ne̱‑rɔ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ kwaare̱ i lwee e̱sanne̱‑rɔ, ne̱ mò̱ ya nya bamo̱, ne̱ ɔ mɔɔ a ɔ wo̱‑ɔ.

ref: Some of your own people will tell lies to win over the Lord's followers.
text: Saŋ ko̱ e̱ ba na mo̱ne̱‑rɔ bo̱ko̱ ba aye̱ba na a sa a bamo̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi‑o kii bo̱ gya bamo̱ se̱.

ref: Be on your guard! Remember how day and night for three years I kept warning you with tears in my eyes.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱nꞌ sa se̱ na mo̱nꞌ nyiŋŋi si ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ a kye̱na mo̱ne̱ ase̱ nsu nsa‑ɔ. Na mo̱nꞌ nyiŋŋi si fe̱yɛ kanye na mpase̱, mo̱ a ba akyukyu bo̱ nyiŋŋi mo̱ne̱ Wuribware̱ a e̱kpa‑ɔ se̱.

ref: I now place you in God's care. Remember the message about his gift of undeserved grace! This message can help you and give you what belongs to you as God's people.
text: Na mbe̱yɔmɔ, n de mo̱ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa Wuribware̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ. Ne̱ mo̱ne̱ ya kya se̱ gya Wuribware̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱, ase̱ŋ amo̱ ne̱ a kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ kpa mo̱ne̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ e̱ taare̱ a a mo̱rɔwe̱ mo̱ne̱. Na a sa a mo̱nꞌ nya ke̱yaale̱ŋ na Wuribware̱ a ŋyure ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ ɔ sa mò̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ.

ref: I have never wanted anyone's money or clothes.
text: Saŋ ne̱ m bo̱ mo̱ne̱ ase̱‑ɔ, mo̱ŋ yo̱rɔwe̱ ko̱tɔko̱, hare̱e̱ atanne̱ pipee bɛɛɛ afufuri bɛɛɛ awaagya.

ref: You know how I have worked with my own hands to make a living for myself and my friends.
text: Mo̱ne̱ fɔŋfɔŋ nyi fe̱yɛ asare̱e̱ mɔ ne̱ mo̱ a bo̱ waa kusuŋ bo̱ nya ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ aa mo̱ anare̱bɛɛpo̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ.

ref: By everything I did, I showed how you should work to help everyone who is weak. Remember that our Lord Jesus said, “More blessings come from giving than from receiving.”
text: Mo̱ a waa kusuŋ mɔ keŋkeŋ bo̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ a tiri fe̱yɛ ane̱ kya bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱ le̱ŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ, na ane̱ nyiŋŋi ase̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu fɔŋfɔŋ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ se̱, ɔ yɛ, ‘Ɔko̱ ne̱ o ke atɔ‑ɔ de ŋyure ɔ kyɔ ɔko̱ ne̱ ba ke mò̱‑ɔ.’ ”

ref: After Paul had finished speaking, he knelt down with all of them and prayed.
text: Pɔɔl a sa se̱ŋsa gywii bamo̱ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱ aa bamo̱ a kpuni aŋurii, ne̱ baa ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱.

ref: They were especially sad because Paul had told them, “You will never see me again.” Then they went with him to the ship.
text: Ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ a mo̱ŋ waa bamo̱ kɔne̱ pɛɛɛ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ maa lɛɛ ŋu mò̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ baa yaa mò̱ ya bo̱ fo̱ mò̱ ko̱re̱e̱ ase̱.

ref: We found a ship going to Phoenicia, so we got on board and sailed off.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ a nya ko̱re̱e̱ ne̱ ɔ yɔ Fonikiya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ , ne̱ ane̱ a lwee mò̱‑rɔ.

ref: We came within sight of Cyprus and then sailed south of it on to the port of Tyre in Syria, where the ship was going to unload its cargo.
text: Ne̱ ane̱ a fo̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ ke̱e̱ Kiprus kwii‑o, ne̱ ane̱ a bo̱rɔ kigyise si yɔ Siriya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱. Ne̱ ane̱ a ya daŋ Tiro maŋ‑nɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ko̱re̱e̱ amo̱ i lee mò̱ tɔso̱rɔ.

ref: But when the week was over, we started on our way again. All the men, together with their wives and children, walked with us from the town to the seashore. We knelt on the beach and prayed.
text: Amaa ane̱ saŋ ne̱ ane̱ i gyi bamo̱ ase̱‑ɔ a fo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ane̱ a be̱e̱ me̱raa ane̱ kpa se̱. Ne̱ bamo̱ aa bamo̱ aka na bamo̱ gyi‑ana pɛɛɛ mɔ a yaa ane̱ kpa‑rɔ ya bo̱ fo̱ nkyu‑o kanɔ, ne̱ ane̱ pɛɛɛ mɔ a kpuni aŋurii mfe̱ŋ, ne̱ ane̱ a ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱.

ref: Then after saying goodbye to each other, we got into the ship, and they went back home.
text: Ne̱ ane̱ aa bamo̱ a gyi ŋkra, ne̱ ane̱ a be̱e̱ ya lwee ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ bamo̱ mɔ baa kiŋŋi yɔ pe̱.

ref: We had been in Caesarea for several days, when the prophet Agabus came to us from Judea.
text: Ane̱ a gyi mfe̱ŋ ŋke bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Agabus‑o a le̱e̱ Yude̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ ba.

ref: He took Paul's belt, and with it he tied up his own hands and feet. Then he told us, “The Holy Spirit says that some of the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem will tie up the man who owns this belt. They will also hand him over to the Gentiles.”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ ba ane̱ ase̱, ne̱ ɔɔ taa Pɔɔl a laare̱‑rɔ ke̱re̱se̱sɛ‑ɔ bo̱ ŋure mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ayaa na asare̱e̱ bo̱ kya abɛɛ, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a tɔwe̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mfaanɛɛ dɛɛ ne̱ Yudaa awuye i ŋure ke̱re̱se̱sɛ mɔ wuye Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ, na bo̱ taa mò̱ bo̱ waa Roma awuye‑o ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ.”

ref: After Agabus said this, we and the followers living there begged Paul not to go to Jerusalem.
text: Saŋ ne̱ ane̱ a nu amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ane̱ aa bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ a ko̱re̱ Pɔɔl fe̱yɛ ɔ ma yɔ mfe̱ŋ.

ref: But Paul answered, “Why are you crying and breaking my heart? I am not only willing to be put in jail for the Lord Jesus, but I am even willing to die for him in Jerusalem!”
text: Amaa ɔɔ be̱ŋŋaa ane̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ waa‑ɔ ne̱e̱, mo̱ne̱ i su mfaanɛɛ na mo̱ne̱ e̱ nye̱ra mo̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱? N gye̱ bamo̱ kuŋure mo̱ wo̱re̱ siraa ne̱ mo̱ a waa a ŋ yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm, amo̱ aa lowi, na ŋ kperi ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu si, na ŋ wu mfe̱ŋ.”

ref: Since we could not get Paul to change his mind, we gave up and prayed, “Lord, please make us willing to do what you want.”
text: Ane̱ mo̱ŋ taare̱ tɔwe̱ a o sure si fe̱yɛ ɔ ma yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ ane̱ a yɔwe̱ mò̱, ne̱ ane̱ yɛ, “Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ a ke̱pre̱‑ɔ waa.”

ref: Some of the followers from Caesarea went with us and took us to stay in the home of Mnason. He was from Cyprus and had been a follower from the beginning. Paul Visits James
text: Ane̱ aa bamo̱ ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi, ne̱ baa le̱e̱ Kaye̱sare̱ya mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ ya yɔ, ne̱ baa yaa ane̱ ɔnyare̱ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ ya so̱we̱ mò̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ aye̱. Ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Menasɔŋ. Ɔɔ le̱e̱ Kiprus kwii si ne̱e̱, ne̱ ɔ gye̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa wuree kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi‑o ɔko̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Pɔɔl a ka Yɛro̱salɛm asɔre̱e̱ awuye kanɔ‑ɔ

ref: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the Lord's followers gladly welcomed us.
text: Saŋ ne̱ ane̱ a fo̱ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, bamo̱ ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi‑o a sa ane̱ aŋsɛ dame̱naŋsɛ.

ref: Paul went with us to see James the next day, and all the church leaders were present.
text: Ke̱mo̱ kaye̱ ŋke‑o, ne̱ ane̱ aa Pɔɔl a yɔ a ɔ ya ka Yakubu kanɔ. Mò̱ a yɔ‑ɔ, asɔre̱e̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ.

ref: Paul greeted them and told how God had used him to help the Gentiles.
text: Ne̱ Pɔɔl a ka bamo̱ kanɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ Wuribware̱ a bo̱rɔ mò̱ se̱ waa sa nde̱ se̱ awuye bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ gywii bamo̱.

ref: Everyone who heard this praised God and said to Paul: My friend, you can see how many tens of thousands of our people have become followers! And all of them are eager to obey the Law of Moses.
text: Bamo̱ a nu mò̱ ase̱ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱. Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Ane̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ Pɔɔl, fo̱ a ŋu ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ Yudaa awuye ŋkpe̱ŋ ŋkpe̱ŋ mfe̱e̱ a kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi‑o, na ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa le̱ŋ bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ gya ane̱ mbraa ne̱ ane̱ ɔde̱daapo̱ Mosis a sa ane̱‑ɔ se̱ nɛɛnɛɛ‑ɔ ɔɔɔ?

ref: But they have been told that you are teaching those who live among the Gentiles to disobey this Law. They claim that you are telling them not to circumcise their sons or to follow our customs.
text: Baa nu fe̱yɛ fo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ Yudaa awuye ne̱ bo̱ te nde̱ se̱ awuye‑o‑ro ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ yɔwe̱ mbraa ne̱ Mosis a sa ane̱‑ɔ dɛɛ ke̱gya se̱, na fo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ fo̱ i gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ma lɛɛ te̱ŋŋe̱ bamo̱ gyi‑ana atwe̱e̱tu, na bo̱ ma lɛɛ gya ane̱ ade̱daase̱ŋ se̱.

ref: Some while ago we told the Gentile followers what we think they should do. We instructed them not to eat anything offered to idols. They were told not to eat any meat with blood still in it or the meat of an animal that has been strangled. They were also told not to commit any terrible sexual sins.
text: Amaa bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye, ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi‑o fe̱raa, ane̱ a kyɔ kyo̱rɛɛ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ bamo̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ma kaŋ gyi ateese bɛɛɛ wo̱ kabo̱ ne̱ baa mɔɔ we̱e̱ ikisi si‑o, na bo̱ ma kaŋ gyi mbo̱gya, na bo̱ ma kaŋ wo̱ kabo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ te̱ŋ kamo̱ ke̱bɔ‑rɔ, na bo̱ ma waa kakye̱e̱kpa na kanyare̱kpa. Ane̱ŋ a mbraa mɔ baare sa bamo̱.”

ref: The next day Paul took the four men with him and got himself ready at the same time they did. Then he went into the temple and told when the final ceremony would take place and when an offering would be made for each of them. Paul Is Arrested
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ kaye̱ ŋke‑o, ne̱ Pɔɔl a yaa anyare̱‑ɔ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ ase̱, ne̱ bamo̱ aa Pɔɔl a ya kpɛɛ bamo̱ iyisi, na bo̱ nya fo̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa kulweero. Ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa ya tɔwe̱ ŋke ne̱ e̱taŋ i gyi‑o, na saŋ ne̱ ba ba a bo̱ lɔŋŋɔ Wuribware̱‑ɔ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kra Pɔɔl Wuribware̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ

ref: When the period of seven days for the ceremony was almost over, some of the Jewish people from Asia saw Paul in the temple. They got a large crowd together and started attacking him.
text: Amaa ŋke nsunoo amo̱ a kpa a m fo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ Yudaa awuye ko̱ ne̱ baa le̱e̱ Asiya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ a ŋu Pɔɔl Wuribware̱ suŋkpa kabuno Yɛro̱salɛm mfe̱ŋ. Ne̱ baa wure ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ baa kra Pɔɔl.

ref: They were shouting, “Friends, help us! This man goes around everywhere, saying bad things about our nation and about the Law of Moses and about this temple. He has even brought shame to this holy temple by bringing in Gentiles.”
text: Ne̱ baa faa‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, “Isireelii awuye, mo̱nꞌ kya ane̱‑rɔ! Ɔnyare̱ mɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ naa to̱ŋ ke̱maa ɔ kaapo̱ ase̱ŋ o kye ane̱ Isireelii awuye na ane̱ mbraa‑ɔ, na ɔ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ bɔye̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa mɔ se̱. Ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ ɔɔ baa bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o baa Wuribware̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ‑rɔ mfe̱e̱ baa bo̱ waa mò̱ iyisi!”

ref: Some of them thought that Paul had brought Trophimus from Ephesus into the temple, because they had seen them together in the city.
text: Baa tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ baa ŋu Trofimus ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ Ifisus maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ na Pɔɔl a nare̱ maŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ baa fa fe̱yɛ Pɔɔl a yaa mò̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa mɔ‑rɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: The whole city was in an uproar, and the people turned into a mob. They grabbed Paul and dragged him out of the temple. Then suddenly the doors were shut.
text: Ne̱ ase̱ŋ dabe̱ a ba maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ a ŋwɛɛnaŋ le̱e̱ to̱ŋ ke̱maa bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱, ne̱ baa ya kra Pɔɔl, ne̱ baa gyiiri mò̱ le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa a kabuno‑o. Puri amo̱‑rɔ, ne̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa ade̱e̱re̱se̱po̱‑ɔ a tii e̱po̱ne̱‑ɔ na ase̱sɛ‑ɔ ma be̱e̱ taa ase̱ŋ‑ɔ lweero mfe̱ŋ.

ref: The people were about to kill Paul when the Roman army commander heard that all Jerusalem was starting to riot.
text: Ase̱sɛ‑ɔ a kpa a bo̱ mɔɔ Pɔɔl‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔko̱ a ya tɔwe̱ gywii Roma asoogyaa ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ Yɛro̱salɛm awuye pɛɛɛ a baa ase̱ŋ dabe̱ maŋ‑nɔ.

ref: So he quickly took some soldiers and officers and ran to where the crowd had gathered. As soon as the mob saw the commander and soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
text: Puri amo̱‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ amo̱ a taa asoogyaa awuye ko̱ na bamo̱ abre̱sɛ, ne̱ baa waa me̱naŋ yɔ ase̱sɛ kukwii‑o amo̱ ase̱. Saŋ ne̱ baa ŋu asoogyaa ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ na asoogyaa awuye‑o, ne̱ baa yɔwe̱ Pɔɔl ke̱da.

ref: The army commander went over and arrested him and had him bound with two chains. Then he tried to find out who Paul was and what he had done.
text: Ne̱ asoogyaa bre̱sɛ‑ɔ a ya kra Pɔɔl, ne̱ ɔɔ sa kanɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ba agbarabi anyɔ bo̱ ŋure mò̱. Ne̱ ŋkee oo bise fe̱yɛ, “Nsɛ e̱ gye̱ ɔnyare̱ mɔ, ne̱ nte̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ waa?”

ref: Part of the crowd shouted one thing, and part of them shouted something else. But they were making so much noise that the commander could not find out a thing. Then he ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress.
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ a faa‑rɔ ba be̱ŋŋaa ase̱ŋ ko̱, na bo̱ko̱ mɔ e̱ be̱ŋŋaa ako̱, na a kwe̱e̱. Mfaanɛɛ se̱‑ɔ, asoogyaa ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ taare̱ ŋu ke̱tɔ ne̱ kaa baa ase̱ŋ‑ɔ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ sa kanɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ taa Pɔɔl de̱e̱ yaa bo̱ be̱ya asoogyaa awuye kwatase̱‑rɔ.

ref: As they reached the steps, the crowd became so wild that the soldiers had to lift Paul up and carry him.
text: Ne̱ lamaŋ‑ɔ a gya asoogyaa awuye‑o si ya fo̱ abe̱ŋbe̱re̱e̱ ne̱ ba kyikye si baa lwee asoogyaa awuye kwatase̱‑ɔ‑rɔ ase̱, ne̱ asoogyaa awuye‑o a yase̱ Pɔɔl so̱rɔ so̱so̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ iduŋ a fwii mò̱ se̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: The crowd followed and kept shouting, “Kill him! Kill him!” Paul Speaks to the Crowd
text: Saŋ ne̱ bo̱ gya se̱‑ɔ, na ba fɛɛ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ mɔɔ mò̱.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Pɔɔl a tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ na ɔ bo̱ lee mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱‑ɔ

ref: When Paul was about to be taken into the fortress, he asked the commander, “Can I say something to you?” “How do you know Greek?” the commander asked.
text: Saŋ ne̱ asoogyaa awuye‑o e̱ kpa a bo̱ taa Pɔɔl waa kwatase̱‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ oo bise asoogyaa ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ Griiki‑ro fe̱yɛ, “Ɔbre̱sɛ, mo̱ e̱ taare̱ a n sa se̱ŋsa gywii fo̱ aaa?” Ne̱ asoogyaa ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ amo̱ yɛ, “Fo̱ kyɔ fo̱ i nu Griiki‑ro aaa?

ref: “Aren't you that Egyptian who started a riot not long ago and led
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa n gye̱ fo̱ e̱ gye̱ Igyipitinyi ne̱ saŋ ko̱ ɔɔ dɛɛ baa ase̱ŋ maŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ gye̱ anaapo̱ ŋkpe̱ŋ nna ke̱gye̱ŋkpɛɛ yɔ kimukee‑ro‑o bɛɛɛ?”

ref: “No!” Paul replied. “I am a Jew from Tarsus, an important city in Cilicia. Please let me speak to the crowd.”
text: Ne̱ Pɔɔl a be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ, “N gye̱ Yudaanyi ne̱e̱. Baa ko̱we̱ mo̱ Taasus maŋ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ Kilikiya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ‑rɔ ne̱e̱, n gye̱ maŋ dabe̱ kigyi ne̱e̱. Mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ fo̱ ne̱e̱ a fo̱ sa a n sa se̱ŋsa gywii ase̱sɛ‑ɔ.”

ref: The commander told him he could speak, so Paul stood on the steps and motioned to the people. When they were quiet, he spoke to them in Aramaic:
text: Ne̱ asoogyaa ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ a sa mò̱ kpa. Ne̱ Pɔɔl a ye̱re̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ba kyikye ba lwee kwatase̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ ya mò̱ ke̱sare̱e̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ laatɔ. Saŋ ne̱ baa laatɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ Pɔɔl a sa bamo̱ aye̱‑rɔ Heebrii se̱ŋsa gywii bamo̱.

ref: When the crowd heard Paul speak to them in Aramaic, they became even quieter. Then Paul said:
text: Bamo̱ a nu fe̱yɛ ɔ sa bamo̱ Heebrii se̱ŋsa‑rɔ bamo̱ ase̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa waa diŋŋ diŋŋ diŋŋ, ne̱ Pɔɔl a kya se̱ fe̱yɛ,

ref: I am a Jew, born in the city of Tarsus in Cilicia. But I grew up here in Jerusalem where I was a student of Gamaliel and was taught to follow every single law of our ancestors. In fact, I was just as eager to obey God as any of you are today.
text: “Yudaanyi e̱ gye̱ mo̱. Taasus maŋ‑nɔ Kilikiya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ ne̱ baa ko̱we̱ mo̱. Amaa Yɛro̱salɛm mfe̱e̱ ne̱ baa be̱ra mo̱, ne̱ mo̱ a kye̱na ane̱ ɔkaapo̱po̱ dabe̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Gamaliilii‑o ayaa‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ kaapo̱ mo̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ n gya ane̱ ade̱daapo̱ a mbraa‑ɔ se̱ nɛɛnɛɛ‑ɔ. Ne̱ mo̱ a le̱ŋ mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ suŋ Wuribware̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ bo̱ mfe̱e̱ ndɔɔ‑ɔ i suŋ‑o.

ref: I made trouble for everyone who followed the Lord's Way, and I even had some of them killed. I had others arrested and put in jail. I didn't care if they were men or women.
text: N gye̱ amo̱ wo̱re̱, mo̱ a be̱e̱ waa bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ gya Yeesuu a e̱kpa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ awo̱re̱fɔɔ, ne̱ mo̱ a sa ne̱ baa mɔɔ bamo̱ bo̱ko̱. Na mo̱ e̱ kra anyare̱ na akye̱e̱, ne̱ mo̱ a sa ne̱ baa tii bamo̱.

ref: One day about noon I was getting close to Damascus, when a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around me.
text: Ne̱ Pɔɔl a kya se̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ŋke ŋko̱ mpase̱, mo̱ a yɔ mo̱ e̱ kpa a m fo̱ Damasikus‑o, ne̱ ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ dabe̱ ko̱ ne̱ ku du fe̱yɛ e̱lɛɛlɛɛ‑ɔ a le̱e̱ so̱so̱ kaluwa ko̱ŋko̱ bo̱ muruwaa we̱e̱ mo̱ se̱.

ref: I fell to the ground and heard a voice asking, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me?”
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a le̱e̱ da swe̱e̱re̱, ne̱ mo̱ a nu bo̱re̱ ko̱ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Sɔɔl, Sɔɔl! Nte̱tɔ ne̱e̱, ne̱ fo̱ e̱ tɔɔraa mo̱ ane̱ŋ?’

ref: “Who are you?” I answered. The Lord replied, “I am Jesus from Nazareth! I am the one you are so cruel to.”
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a bise fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, fo̱ nsɛ ne̱e̱?’ Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱, Yeesuu Nasarɛte̱nyi‑o ne̱ fo̱ e̱ tɔɔraa‑ɔ ne̱e̱.’

ref: Those who were traveling with me saw the light, but did not hear the voice.
text: Ase̱sɛ ne̱ mo̱ aa bamo̱ naa‑ɔ a ŋu ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ‑ɔ, amaa bo̱ mo̱ŋ nu bo̱re̱ ne̱ ɔɔ sa se̱ŋsa mo̱ ase̱‑ɔ.

ref: I asked, “Lord, what do you want me to do?” Then he told me, “Get up and go to Damascus. When you get there, you will be told what to do.”
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, nte̱tɔ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ŋ waa?’ Ne̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ko̱so̱ na fo̱ yɔ Damasikus. Ɔko̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ fo̱ waa‑ɔ a o gywii fo̱ mfe̱ŋ.’

ref: The light had been so bright that I couldn't see. And the others had to lead me by the hand to Damascus.
text: Ne̱ ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ amo̱ a sa ne̱ mo̱ akatɔ a bwee, amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ mo̱ anare̱bɛɛpo̱‑ɔ a kra mo̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ yaa Damasikus maŋ‑nɔ.

ref: In that city there was a man named Ananias, who faithfully obeyed the Law of Moses and was well liked by all the Jewish people living there.
text: Amɔ ane̱ bɛɛko̱ Yudaanyi ko̱ mɔ bo̱ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Ananiyas. Ɔ gye̱ se̱sɛ timaa, ne̱ ɔ gya ane̱ mbraa‑ɔ se̱ nɛɛnɛɛ, ne̱ Yudaa awuye ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ i bu mò̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: He came to me and said, “Saul, my friend, you can now see again!” At once I could see.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ ba mo̱ ase̱, ne̱ ɔɔ ye̱re̱ me̱raa mo̱, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, ‘Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ Sɔɔl, be̱e̱ ŋu atɔ.’ Puri amo̱‑rɔ ne̱ mo̱ akatɔ a buŋŋi, ne̱ mo̱ a de̱e̱re̱ mò̱.

ref: Then Ananias told me, “The God that our ancestors worshiped has chosen you to know what he wants done. He has chosen you to see the One Who Obeys God and to hear his voice.
text: Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, ‘Wuribware̱ ne̱ ane̱ a suŋ le̱e̱ ane̱ ade̱daapo̱ a mbe̱e̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ a lee fo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ pini mò̱ ke̱pre̱, na fo̱ gyii mò̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ mò̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱ timaa, na fo̱ nu mò̱ bo̱re̱.

ref: What are you waiting for? Get up! Be baptized, and wash away your sins by praying to the Lord.”
text: Ne̱ nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ fo̱ be̱e̱ fo̱ gywii? Ko̱so̱ na fo̱ bɔ asuu, na fo̱ ko̱re̱ Wuribware̱ fe̱yɛ Yeesuu ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ ɔ bo̱ kpɛɛ fo̱ e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ twe̱e̱.’

ref: After this I returned to Jerusalem and went to the temple to pray. There I had a vision
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a kiŋŋi ba Yɛro̱salɛm mfe̱e̱. Saŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ, ne̱ aa waa fe̱yɛ mo̱ a ku dee‑o.

ref: of the Lord who said to me, “Hurry and leave Jerusalem! The people won't listen to what you say about me.”
text: Dee amo̱‑rɔ mo̱ a ŋu ane̱ nyaŋpe̱, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Waa me̱naŋ le̱e̱ Yɛro̱salɛm mfe̱e̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mfe̱e̱ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ maa sure ase̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ a fo̱ gywii bamo̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ mo̱ kuŋu si‑o si.’

ref: I replied, “Lord, they know that in many of our synagogues I arrested and beat people who had faith in you.
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, bo̱ nyi dame̱naŋsɛ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a dɛɛ nare̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ akyaŋ‑nɔ kra ase̱sɛ ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ fo̱ gyi‑o. Ne̱ mo̱ a tii bamo̱ tiikpa, ne̱ mo̱ a da bamo̱.

ref: Stephen was killed because he spoke for you, and I stood there and cheered them on. I even guarded the clothes of the men who murdered him.”
text: Na saŋ ne̱ baa mɔɔ Stifaŋ ne̱ kufu mo̱ŋ de mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ tɔwe̱ lamaŋ‑nɔ fe̱yɛ ɔ gya fo̱ e̱kpa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, na m bo̱ mfe̱ŋ, ne̱ mo̱ a sure si fe̱yɛ bo̱ mɔɔ mò̱, ne̱ mo̱ a be̱e̱ de̱e̱re̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa ya mɔɔ mò̱‑ɔ awaagya se̱ sa bamo̱.’

ref: But the Lord told me to go, and he promised to send me far away to the Gentiles.
text: Ne̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ yɛ, ‘Nare̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ i suŋ fo̱ ke̱fɔ a m bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ nde̱ se̱ awuye‑o ase̱.’ ” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Pɔɔl a lee bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ gye̱ Roma maŋ kigyi‑o

ref: They kept shouting. They waved their clothes around and threw dust into the air. Paul and the Roman Army Commander
text: Na ba fɛɛ‑rɔ, na ba le̱ŋkpaŋ bamo̱ awaagya‑rɔ, na ba twe̱e̱ e̱se̱ ba kyo̱ŋwe̱ so̱so̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ iduŋ a fwii Pɔɔl se̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: The Roman commander ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress and beaten with a whip. He did this to find out why the people were screaming at Paul.
text: Ne̱ Roma soogyaa ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ a sa kanɔ fe̱yɛ asoogyaa awuye‑o taa Pɔɔl yaa bamo̱ kwatase̱‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ da mò̱ ko̱kyo̱kywe̱e̱, na bo̱ nya ŋu ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ Yudaa awuye‑o e̱ fɛɛ‑rɔ ba kye mò̱‑ɔ.

ref: While the soldiers were tying Paul up to be beaten, he asked the officer standing there, “Is it legal to beat a Roman citizen before he has been tried in court?”
text: Amaa bamo̱ a ŋure mò̱ bo̱ be̱ya swe̱e̱re̱, na bo̱ da mò̱‑ɔ, ne̱ Pɔɔl a tɔwe̱ gywii soogyaa bre̱sɛ ne̱ ɔ ye̱re̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “N gye̱ Roma maŋ kigyi ne̱e̱. Ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ gyi mo̱ ase̱ŋ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a waa bɔye̱ ko̱. A mo̱ŋ da mo̱ne̱ mbraa‑rɔ fe̱yɛ a gye̱ bɔye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa a mo̱nꞌ da mo̱ ko̱kyo̱kywe̱e̱ aaa?”

ref: When the officer heard this, he went to the commander and said, “What are you doing? This man is a Roman citizen!”
text: Soogyaa bre̱sɛ amo̱ a nu ane̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ asoogyaa ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ ase̱ ya bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ke̱tɔ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kpa a fo̱ waa ɔnyare̱ amo̱‑ɔ bware aaa? A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ gye̱ Roma maŋ kigyi ne̱e̱!”

ref: The commander went to Paul and asked, “Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?” “Yes,” Paul answered.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ soogyaa ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ amo̱ a yɔ Pɔɔl ase̱ ya bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱, a gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ Roma maŋ kigyi e̱ gye̱ fo̱ aaa?” Ne̱ Pɔɔl yɛ, “Ɔɔŋ.”

ref: The commander then said, “I paid a lot of money to become a Roman citizen.” But Paul replied, “I was born a Roman citizen.”
text: Ne̱ soogyaa ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ a ka atanne̱ dabe̱ pwɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ a dɛɛ kii Roma maŋ kigyi.” Ne̱ Pɔɔl a be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ, “Amaa m fe̱raa Roma maŋ agyi a de̱ŋ ko̱we̱ mo̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, n gye̱ Roma maŋ kigyi ne̱e̱.”

ref: The men who were about to beat and question Paul quickly backed off. And the commander himself was frightened when he realized that he had put a Roman citizen in chains. Paul Is Tried by the Council
text: Puri amo̱‑rɔ, ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ ba kpa kibise mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a lwii kamɛɛ le̱e̱ Pɔɔl ase̱. Saŋ ne̱ soogyaa ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ a pini fe̱yɛ Roma maŋ kigyi ne̱ oo ŋure agbarabi‑o, ne̱ kufu a nya mò̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa yaa Pɔɔl maŋ abre̱sɛ akatɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ

ref: The next day the commander wanted to know the real reason why the Jewish leaders had brought charges against Paul. So he had Paul's chains removed, and he ordered the chief priests and the whole council to meet. Then he had Paul led in and made him stand in front of them.
text: Amaa soogyaa ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ o ŋu ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ Yudaa awuye‑o e̱ po̱rɔ Pɔɔl‑ɔ. Amo̱se̱ ke̱mo̱ kaye̱ ŋke‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ baa saŋŋe̱ agbarabi ne̱ baa bo̱ ŋure Pɔɔl‑ɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱‑ɔ na maŋ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ fe̱yɛ bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ baa Pɔɔl baa bo̱ ye̱ra bamo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ.

ref: Paul looked straight at the council members and said, “My friends, to this day I have served God with a clear conscience!”
text: Ne̱ Pɔɔl a de̱e̱re̱ maŋ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ akatɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana Isireelii awuye! Wuribware̱ ase̱ fe̱raa, ŋ nyi mo̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ mo̱ŋ de ke̱bɔye̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ mo̱ kakye̱na‑rɔ hare̱e̱ bo̱ fo̱ ndɔɔ!”

ref: Then Ananias the high priest ordered the men standing beside Paul to hit him on the mouth.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Ananiyas‑o a sa bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ ye̱re̱ bo̱ me̱raa Pɔɔl‑ɔ kanɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ daye̱ mò̱ kanɔ se̱ abaa.

ref: Paul turned to the high priest and said, “You whitewashed wall! God will hit you. You sit there to judge me by the Law of Moses. But at the same time you order men to break the Law by hitting me.”
text: Ne̱ Pɔɔl a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “A waa dɛɛ Wuribware̱ mɔ e̱ da fo̱, nnɔ ŋnyɔ ŋnyɔ wuye. Fo̱ te mfe̱ŋ fo̱ yɛ fo̱ de mbraa‑ɔ, ne̱ fo̱ i gyi mo̱ ase̱ŋ, na fo̱‑ɔ fo̱ be̱e̱ fo̱ e̱ waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ki kye mbraa‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ yɛ bo̱ da mo̱!”

ref: The men standing beside Paul asked, “Don't you know you are insulting God's high priest?”
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ ye̱re̱ me̱raa Pɔɔl‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ e̱ saare̱ Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ‑ɔ ne̱e̱!”

ref: Paul replied, “Oh! I didn't know he was the high priest. The Scriptures do tell us not to speak evil about a leader of our people.”
text: Ne̱ Pɔɔl a be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana Isireelii awuye, mo̱ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ ɔ gye̱ Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ‑ɔ ne̱e̱. A gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ yɛ, ‘Fo̱ ma tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ bɔye̱ bo̱ kye fo̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱.’ ”

ref: When Paul saw that some of the council members were Sadducees and others were Pharisees, he shouted, “My friends, I am a Pharisee and the son of a Pharisee. I am on trial simply because I believe that the dead will be raised to life.”
text: Saŋ ne̱ Pɔɔl a ŋu fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ ngyaŋŋe̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ gye̱ Sadukii awuye, ne̱ bo̱ko̱ mɔ gye̱ Farisii awuye‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana Isireelii awuye! N gye̱ Farisiinyi ne̱e̱, ne̱ n se̱ na n na gye̱ Farisii awuye ne̱e̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ a kɔɔre̱ gyi fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ne̱ baa wu‑o i kyiŋŋi a bo̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro‑o si ne̱ ba gyi mo̱ ase̱ŋ mfe̱e̱‑ɔ!”

ref: As soon as Paul said this, the Pharisees and the Sadducees got into a big argument, and the council members started taking sides.
text: Mò̱ ke̱de̱ŋ tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ Farisii awuye‑o na Sadukii awuye‑o a le̱e̱ ba kɔ, ne̱ baa ke‑ro,

ref: The Sadducees do not believe in angels or spirits or that the dead will rise to life. But the Pharisees believe in all of these,
text: a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Sadukii awuye‑o yɛ ase̱sɛ maa kyiŋŋi a bo̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro. Ne̱ bo̱ be̱e̱ bo̱ yɛ Wuribware̱ mbɔɔ mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ, ne̱ se̱sɛ ya wu, mò̱ kayo̱wɔre̱ na mò̱ kra i wu pɛɛɛ. Amaa ne̱ Farisii awuye‑o mɔ e̱ kɔɔre̱ amo̱ asa pɛɛɛ ba gyi.

ref: and so there was a lot of shouting. Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees. Finally, they became angry and said, “We don't find anything wrong with this man. Maybe a spirit or an angel really did speak to him.”
text: Ne̱ e̱lawo̱‑ɔ a be̱e̱ tii si, ne̱ Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Farisii awuye a ke̱be̱gya‑ɔ a ko̱so̱ ye̱re̱‑rɔ keŋkeŋ fe̱yɛ bo̱ mo̱ŋ ŋu Pɔɔl ke̱bɔye̱ ko̱. Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Ane̱ mo̱ŋ ŋu ɔnyare̱ mɔ ke̱bɔye̱ ko̱! Nsaŋse̱ a gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kye̱ŋaŋpo̱ bɛɛɛ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ a sa se̱ŋsa gywii mò̱!”

ref: The argument became fierce, and the commander was afraid that Paul would be pulled apart. So he ordered the soldiers to go in and rescue Paul. Then they took him back into the fortress.
text: Ane̱ŋ ne̱ ikii‑o‑ro a waa le̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ kufu a nya soogyaa ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ baa kyaye̱ Pɔɔlo̱‑rɔ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ ɔɔ sa kanɔ fe̱yɛ soogyaa awuye‑o yɔ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ‑rɔ ya lee Pɔɔl le̱e̱ bamo̱ ase̱, na bo̱ be̱e̱ taa mò̱ yaa tii bamo̱ kwatase̱‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: That night the Lord stood beside Paul and said, “Don't worry! Just as you have told others about me in Jerusalem, you must also tell about me in Rome.” A Plot To Kill Paul
text: Kanye amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu a lee mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ Pɔɔl dee‑ro, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ ma sa a kufu nya fo̱! Fo̱ a ye̱re̱ bo̱ sa mo̱ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ ye̱re̱ a fo̱ bo̱ sa mo̱ Roma maŋ‑nɔ.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa da Pɔɔl kuŋu si kikpuni‑o

ref: The next morning more than
text: Bamo̱ ne̱ baa kra ase̱ŋ mɔ‑ɔ kyɔ ase̱sɛ aduna.

ref: Then some of them went to the chief priests and the nation's leaders and said, “We have promised God that we would not eat a thing until we have killed Paul.
text: Ne̱ baa yɔ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱‑ɔ na maŋ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ ase̱ ya tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ a gyaŋŋe̱ waa e̱taŋ fe̱yɛ ane̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ mɔɔ Pɔɔl, ane̱ maa gyi ateese.

ref: You and everyone in the council must go to the commander and pretend that you want to find out more about the charges against Paul. Ask for him to be brought before your court. Meanwhile, we will be waiting to kill him before he gets there.”
text: Na mbe̱yɔmɔ mo̱nꞌ suŋ a bo̱ ya ko̱re̱ Roma soogyaa ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ ɔ be̱e̱ baa Pɔɔl mo̱ne̱ akatɔ‑rɔ, na mo̱nꞌ waa fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ a kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ be̱e̱ nu ase̱ŋ bo̱ le̱e̱ mò̱ kuŋu si nɛɛnɛɛ‑ɔ. Amaa ane̱ e̱ waa siraa, na ane̱ mɔɔ mò̱ pwɛɛ na ɔ dɛɛ fo̱ mfe̱e̱.”

ref: So Paul said to one of the army officers, “Take this young man to the commander. He has something to tell him.”
text: Ne̱ Pɔɔl a te̱e̱ soogyaa ɔbre̱sɛ ko̱, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Yaa ke̱yaafɔre̱ mɔ soogyaa ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ ase̱. O de ase̱ŋ ko̱ a ɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱.”

ref: The officer took him to the commander and said, “The prisoner named Paul asked me to bring this young man to you, because he has something to tell you.”
text: Ne̱ soogyaa bre̱sɛ‑ɔ a gye̱ mò̱ ke̱gye̱ŋkpɛɛ yaa mò̱ soogyaa ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ ase̱ ya tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Pɔɔl ne̱ baa tii‑o ya te̱e̱ mo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ fe̱yɛ m baa ke̱yaafɔre̱ mɔ fo̱ ase̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ o de ase̱ŋ ko̱ a ɔ tɔwe̱ gywii fo̱.”

ref: The commander took the young man aside and asked him in private, “What do you want to tell me?”
text: Ne̱ soogyaa ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ a kra ke̱yaafɔre̱‑ɔ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ taa mò̱ yaa keri si ya bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ ne̱ fo̱ de a fo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱?”

ref: He answered, “Some men are planning to ask you to bring Paul down to the Jewish council tomorrow. They will claim they want to find out more about him.
text: Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Yudaa awuye maŋ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ a waa kanɔ fe̱yɛ ba tɔwe̱ a bo̱ gywii fo̱ nyaŋe̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ baa Pɔɔl bamo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ, na bo̱ waa fe̱yɛ bamo̱ a kpa a bo̱ nu ase̱ŋ nɛɛnɛɛ be̱e̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ Pɔɔl kuŋu si‑o.

ref: The commander sent the young man away after saying to him, “Don't let anyone know you told me this.” Paul Is Sent to Felix the Governor
text: Ne̱ soogyaa ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ a nu. Mbe̱yɔmɔ fo̱ de kpa fe̱yɛ fo̱ nare̱, amaa fo̱ ma kaŋ tɔwe̱ gywii ɔko̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ a tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ gywii mo̱.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa yaa Pɔɔl Gominaa Feelis ase̱‑ɔ

ref: Get a horse ready for Paul and make sure he gets safely through to Felix the governor.”
text: Mo̱nꞌ kpa kpaŋa ko̱ sa Pɔɔl a ɔ de̱e̱, na mo̱nꞌ kuŋ mò̱ nɛɛnɛɛ yaa bo̱ fo̱ Gominaa Feelis ase̱.”

ref: The commander wrote a letter that said:
text: Ne̱ soogyaa ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ a kyo̱rɛɛ wo̱re̱ sa bamo̱ a bo̱ yaa fe̱yɛ,

ref: Some Jews grabbed this man and were about to kill him. But when I found out that he was a Roman citizen, I took some soldiers and rescued him.
text: Yudaa awuye ya kra ɔnyare̱ mɔ ne̱ n de mo̱ e̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ fo̱ ase̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ ba kpa a bo̱ mɔɔ mò̱. Ne̱ mo̱ a nu fe̱yɛ ɔ gye̱ Roma maŋ kigyi ne̱e̱, amo̱se̱ ne̱ mo̱ aa mo̱ asoogyaa awuye a ya kɔɔre̱ mò̱.

ref: I wanted to find out what they had against him. So I brought him before their council
text: Mo̱ a kpa fe̱yɛ n nu ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ ba po̱rɔ mò̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ a taa mò̱ yaa bamo̱ maŋ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ ase̱.

ref: and learned that the charges concern only their religious laws. This man isn't guilty of anything for which he should die or even be put in jail.
text: Amaa mo̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ ɔ mo̱ŋ waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ mɔɔ mò̱ bɛɛɛ bo̱ tii mò̱‑ɔ. Bamo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ mbraa‑ɔ ase̱ŋ ne̱ bo̱ de ba po̱rɔ mò̱.

ref: As soon as I learned that there was a plot against him, I sent him to you and told their leaders to bring charges against him in your court.
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ baa da mò̱ kuŋu si kikpuni. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ puri amo̱‑rɔ ne̱ mo̱ a fa mfɛɛre̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ baa mò̱ a m bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ fo̱ ase̱. Mo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ ne̱ ba po̱rɔ mò̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ baa mò̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ bo̱ de‑o fo̱ ase̱.”

ref: The soldiers obeyed the commander's orders, and that same night they took Paul to the city of Antipatris.
text: Ne̱ asoogyaa awuye‑o a waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ bamo̱ ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ baa me̱raa se̱ kanye amo̱, ne̱ baa taa Pɔɔl yaa fo̱ hare̱e̱ Antipatris maŋ‑nɔ.

ref: The next day the foot soldiers returned to the fortress and let the soldiers on horseback take him the rest of the way.
text: Ke̱mo̱ kaye̱ ŋke‑o ne̱ asoogyaa awuye ne̱ bo̱ naa swe̱e̱re̱‑ɔ a kiŋŋi yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm yɔwe̱ bamo̱ ne̱ ba de̱e̱ e̱kpaŋa‑ɔ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ aa Pɔɔl a yɔ.

ref: When they came to Caesarea, they gave the letter to the governor and handed Paul over to him.
text: Ne̱ baa taa ane̱ŋ‑aaa ya lweero Kaye̱sare̱ya, ne̱ baa taa wo̱re̱‑ɔ sa gominaa‑o, ne̱ baa taa Pɔɔl bo̱ waa mò̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ.

ref: The governor said, “I will listen to your case as soon as the people come to bring their charges against you.” After saying this, he gave orders for Paul to be kept as a prisoner in Herod's palace.
text: Ne̱ gominaa‑o a kare̱ wo̱re̱‑ɔ, ne̱ oo bise Pɔɔl swe̱e̱re̱ ke̱be̱gya ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ ke̱mo̱ se̱‑ɔ. Mò̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ ɔɔ le̱e̱ Kilikiya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Bamo̱ ne̱ fo̱ aa bamo̱ de ase̱ŋ‑ɔ ya ba, mo̱ i nu fo̱ ase̱.” Ne̱ ɔɔ sa kanɔ fe̱yɛ asoogyaa awuye taa Pɔɔl yaa gominaa a ko̱lɔŋ dabe̱ ne̱ Owure Hɛrɔd ko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ dɛɛ kye̱na‑ɔ† a pwɛɛ‑ɔ‑rɔ ya ye̱re̱ de̱e̱re̱ mò̱ se̱.

ref: Five days later Ananias the high priest, together with some of their leaders and a lawyer named Tertullus, went to the governor to present their case against Paul.
text: To, ke̱mo̱ ŋke nnuu kamɛɛ, ne̱ Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ Ananiyas na maŋ abre̱sɛ ko̱ na lɔɔyaa ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Tɛrɛtulus‑o a le̱e̱ Yɛro̱salɛm ba Kaye̱sare̱ya mfe̱ŋ. Ne̱ baa yɔ gominaa‑o akatɔ‑rɔ ya tɔwe̱ bamo̱ kanɔ se̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ bo̱ de bo̱ kye Pɔɔl‑ɔ.

ref: So Paul was called in, and Tertullus stated the case against him: Honorable Felix, you have brought our people a long period of peace, and because of your concern our nation is much better off.
text: Ne̱ gominaa‑o a te̱e̱ Pɔɔl mɔ baa, ne̱ Tɛrɛtulus a le̱e̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ bamo̱ aa Pɔɔl de‑o ko̱tɔwe̱ kaase̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Bre̱sɛ Feelis, fo̱ kanyiase̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ de fo̱ e̱ de̱e̱re̱ ane̱ se̱‑ɔ se̱, ane̱ a nya kaye̱e̱yuri kyee, ne̱ atɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ i kyurowi a baa atɔ timaa ane̱ swe̱e̱re̱ mɔ se̱.

ref: All of us are always grateful for what you have done.
text: Ane̱ a sure nkyurowi amo̱ se̱ to̱ŋ ke̱maa na saŋ ke̱maa, ne̱ aa waa ane̱ kɔne̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: I don't want to bother you, but please be patient with us and listen to me for just a few minutes.
text: To, ma kpa a ŋ kra mo̱ne̱ bo̱ kye̱na mfe̱e̱ kyee, amaa mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ mo̱ne̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ nu ane̱ ase̱ŋ katiŋ mɔ.

ref: This man has been found to be a real pest and troublemaker for our people all over the world. He is also a leader of a group called Nazarenes.
text: Ane̱ a ŋu ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔnyare̱ mɔ gye̱ se̱sɛ bɔye̱, ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ aa fii ane̱‑ɔ. Ɔ naa ɔ baa Yudaa awuye wo̱re̱ wo̱re̱ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ ase̱ŋ. Ne̱ ɔ be̱e̱ ɔ gye̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ gya Yeesuu Nasarɛte̱nyi a e̱kpa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ ɔko̱.

ref: When he tried to disgrace the temple, we arrested him. If you question him, you will find out for yourself that our charges are true.
text: Fo̱ ya bise ɔnyare̱ mɔ, fo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ i nu a fo̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ mò̱ ase̱ ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ ane̱ aa mò̱ de ase̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ po̱rɔ mò̱‑ɔ.”

ref: The Jewish crowd spoke up and agreed with what Tertullus had said. Paul Defends Himself
text: Ne̱ Yudaa awuye ne̱ bamo̱ aa Tɛrɛtulus a ba‑ɔ a ba bamo̱ nnɔ bo̱ waa‑rɔ fe̱yɛ mpo̱rɔ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱e̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Pɔɔl a kya kanɔ gominaa‑o akatɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ

ref: The governor motioned for Paul to speak, and he began: I know that you have judged the people of our nation for many years, and I am glad to defend myself in your court.
text: Ne̱ ŋkee gominaa‑o a sa Pɔɔl kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ tɔwe̱ mò̱ ase̱ŋ. Ne̱ Pɔɔl yɛ, “Ɔbre̱sɛ, ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ fo̱ a gyi ase̱ŋ swe̱e̱re̱ mɔ se̱ kyee. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱ akatɔ a gyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ a ye̱re̱ fo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ mfe̱e̱ mo̱ e̱ kya kanɔ.

ref: It was no more than twelve days ago that I went to worship in Jerusalem. You can find this out easily enough.
text: Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ a ŋu‑o, a mo̱ŋ kyɔ ŋke kudu ŋnyɔ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm a ŋ ya suŋ Wuribware̱ mò̱ suŋkpa mfe̱ŋ.

ref: Never once did the Jews find me arguing with anyone in the temple. I didn't cause trouble in the synagogues or in the city itself.
text: Amaa mo̱ apo̱rɔpo̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ ŋu mo̱ mfe̱ŋ na mo̱ aa ɔko̱ i gyiiri ikii, ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ ŋu mo̱ a sa ne̱ ase̱sɛ a baa ase̱ŋ ko̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ akyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ bɛɛɛ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ to̱ŋ ko̱.

ref: There is no way that they can prove these charges they are now bringing against me.
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ mɔ bo̱ maa taare̱ a bo̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱ŋ ne̱ bo̱ yɛ mo̱ a waa‑ɔ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ.

ref: I admit that their leaders think the Lord's Way which I follow is based on wrong beliefs. But I still worship the same God that my ancestors worshiped. And I believe everything written in the Law of Moses and in the Prophets.
text: Amaa mo̱ a sure si fo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ mfe̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ gya Yeesuu Nasarɛte̱nyi a e̱kpa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ ɔko̱ e̱ gye̱ mo̱. Hare̱e̱ gbaa bamo̱ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ a e̱kpa‑ɔ gye̱ kaye̱ba ne̱e̱ ooo, mo̱‑ɔ mɔ, e̱kpa amo̱ dɛɛ se̱ ne̱ m bɔ mo̱ i suŋ Wuribware̱ ne̱ ane̱ Yudaa awuye a suŋ hare̱e̱ le̱e̱ de̱daa‑ɔ. Amaa ŋ gbaa mo̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ mbraa ne̱ ane̱ ɔde̱daapo̱ Mosis a kyo̱rɛɛ na ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ Wuribware̱ akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ a kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ mo̱ i gyi.

ref: I am just as sure as these people are that God will raise from death everyone, good or evil.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ a kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ gyi, ne̱ n gywii ke̱mo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ bo̱mo̱‑ɔ gbaa bo̱ gywii. Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ, atimaa na abɔye̱‑ɔ, i kyiŋŋi a bo̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro.

ref: And because I am sure, I try my best to have a clear conscience in whatever I do for God or for people.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ saŋ ke̱maa ŋ nyi mo̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ mo̱ŋ waa bɔye̱ ko̱ bo̱ kye Wuribware̱ bɛɛɛ se̱sɛ.

ref: After being away for several years, I returned here to bring gifts for the poor people of my nation and to offer sacrifices.
text: To, mo̱ a le̱e̱ Yɛro̱salɛm‑ɔ nsu kanɔ ŋko̱ ne̱e̱, ne̱ mo̱ a kiŋŋi yɔ mfe̱ŋ a ŋ ya taa atanne̱ sa mo̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ ba wu kitiri‑o, na n lɔŋŋɔ Wuribware̱ mò̱ suŋkpa mfe̱ŋ.

ref: Some Jews from Asia were there at that time, and if they have anything to say against me, they should be here now.
text: Mo̱ a bo̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ Yudaa awuye ko̱ ne̱ baa le̱e̱ Asiya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ a ba. Bamo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ weetee bo̱ ba fo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ mfe̱e̱, ne̱ mo̱ aa bamo̱ i de ase̱ŋ na bo̱ tɔwe̱.

ref: Or ask the ones who are here. They can tell you that they didn't find me guilty of anything when I was tried by their own council.
text: Bɛɛɛ fo̱ sa a ase̱sɛ mɔ ne̱ bo̱ ye̱re̱ mfe̱e̱‑ɔ tɔwe̱ ke̱bɔye̱ ne̱ mo̱ a gyi ke̱mo̱ ke̱pɔ saŋ ne̱ baa yaa mo̱ maŋ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ akatɔ‑rɔ.

ref: The only charge they can bring against me is what I shouted out in court, when I said, “I am on trial today because I believe that the dead will be raised to life!”
text: Amɔ ke̱se̱ŋ ko̱ŋko̱ kpeŋ ne̱ mo̱ a sure fe̱yɛ mo̱ a ye̱re̱ bamo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ a kɔɔre̱ gyi fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ ne̱ baa wu‑o i kyiŋŋi a bo̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro si, ne̱ ba gyi mo̱ ase̱ŋ mfe̱e̱ ndɔɔ‑ɔ.’ ”

ref: Felix knew a lot about the Lord's Way. But he brought the trial to an end and said, “I will make my decision after Lysias the commander arrives.”
text: Ne̱ gominaa‑o ne̱ ɔɔ kyɔ nu Yeesuu a e̱kpa‑ɔ ke̱kaapo̱‑ɔ kaase̱ nɛɛnɛɛ‑ɔ a sa ne̱ baa yɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ kigyi. Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Lisiyas, soogyaa ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ, ya ba, mo̱ e̱ fa mo̱ne̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ mfɛɛre̱.”

ref: He then ordered the army officer to keep Paul under guard, but not to lock him up or to stop his friends from helping him. Paul Is Kept under Guard
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ sa soogyaa bre̱sɛ ne̱ ɔ de̱e̱re̱ Pɔɔl se̱‑ɔ kanɔ fe̱yɛ ɔ taa mò̱ yaa de̱e̱re̱ mò̱ se̱ nɛɛnɛɛ, amaa ɔ sa a ɔ nya mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ nare̱ kafwe̱e̱ fe̱yɛ se̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ tii mò̱‑ɔ, na ɔ sa a mò̱ nyare̱‑ana sa mò̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ki tiri mò̱‑ɔ. Pɔɔl na Feelis na Durusiilaa ase̱ŋ

ref: Several days later Felix and his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, went to the place where Paul was kept under guard. They sent for Paul and listened while he spoke to them about having faith in Christ Jesus.
text: Ŋke mfe̱ne̱ ko̱ kamɛɛ, ne̱ Gominaa Feelis a te̱e̱ mò̱ ka ne̱ ɔ gye̱ Yudaanyi, ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Durusiilaa‑o fe̱yɛ ɔ bo̱ nu Pɔɔl ase̱ŋ. Ne̱ oo suŋ te̱e̱ Pɔɔl baa, ne̱ ɔɔ sa mò̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa gywii bamo̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Kristoo Yeesuu ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi gye̱‑ɔ.

ref: But Felix was frightened when Paul started talking to them about doing right, about self-control, and about the coming judgment. So he said to Paul, “That's enough for now. You may go. But when I have time I will send for you.”
text: Amaa Pɔɔl a maa se̱ ɔ tɔwe̱ ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ, na ane̱ŋ ne̱ se̱sɛ kra mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱, na ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ ba a ɔ bo̱ gyi ase̱sɛ ase̱ŋ ŋke ŋko̱‑ɔ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ kufu a nya gominaa‑o, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mbe̱yɔmɔ fo̱ e̱ taare̱ a fo̱ yɔ. Mo̱ ya nya mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱, m be̱e̱ mo̱ i suŋ a n te̱e̱ fo̱.”

ref: After this, Felix often sent for Paul and talked with him, because he hoped that Paul would offer him a bribe.
text: Amaa ɔ fa fe̱yɛ Pɔɔl e̱ sa mò̱ atanne̱ na ɔ nya mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ le̱e̱ tiikpa. Bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, saŋ ke̱maa o suŋ ɔ te̱e̱ Pɔɔl na mò̱ aa mò̱ sa se̱ŋsa.

ref: Two years later Porcius Festus became governor in place of Felix. But since Felix wanted to do the Jewish leaders a favor, he kept Paul in jail.
text: Amo̱ a fo̱ nsu ŋnyɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Prakiyus Feesitus‑o a bo̱ teere Feelis gyi kigominaa. Feelis a yɔ‑ɔ, bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ kpa fe̱yɛ Yudaa awuye‑o kpa mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ɔ mo̱ŋ sa a bo̱ lee Pɔɔl le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ baa tii mò̱‑ɔ.

ref: if he would be willing to bring Paul to Jerusalem. They begged him to do this because they were planning to attack and kill Paul on the way.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ na Yudaa awuye agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ a baa bamo̱ aa Pɔɔl a ase̱ŋ ne̱ bo̱ de‑o Feesitus ase̱, bo̱ ko̱re̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ o lee Pɔɔl baa Yɛro̱salɛm, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ baa kra fe̱yɛ bo̱ mɔɔ Pɔɔl kpa‑rɔ bo̱ kwe̱e̱rɔ.

ref: But Festus told them, “Paul will be kept in Caesarea, and I am soon going there myself.
text: Ne̱ gominaa‑o a be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Baa tii Pɔɔl Kaye̱sare̱ya na mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ i kiŋŋi a ŋ yɔ mfe̱ŋ mbe̱yɔmɔ.

ref: If he has done anything wrong, let your leaders go with me and bring charges against him there.”
text: Mo̱nꞌ sa a mo̱ aa mo̱ne̱ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ yɔ Kaye̱sare̱ya, ne̱ ɔnyare̱ amo̱ ya waa mo̱ne̱ ke̱bɔye̱ ko̱, na mo̱nꞌ tɔwe̱.”

ref: Festus stayed in Jerusalem for eight or ten more days before going to Caesarea. Then the next day he took his place as judge and had Paul brought into court.
text: Ne̱ gominaa‑o a be̱e̱ gyi ŋke mburuwa bɛɛɛ kudu bamo̱ ase̱, ne̱ ɔɔ dɛɛ yɔ Kaye̱sare̱ya. Ke̱mo̱ kaye̱ ŋke‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ kye̱na mò̱ se̱ŋgyikpa a kye̱nakpa‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ sa kanɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ baa Pɔɔl.

ref: As soon as Paul came in, the leaders from Jerusalem crowded around him and said he was guilty of many serious crimes. But they could not prove anything.
text: Saŋ ne̱ Pɔɔl a ba‑ɔ, ne̱ Yudaa awuye ne̱ baa le̱e̱ Yɛro̱salɛm ba‑ɔ a ye̱re̱ muruwaa mò̱, ne̱ baa le̱e̱ ba po̱rɔ mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔɔ waa ase̱ŋ bɔye̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Amaa bo̱ mo̱ŋ taare̱ nya ke̱se̱ŋ ko̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ a gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ.

ref: Then Paul spoke in his own defense, “I have not broken the Law of my people. And I have not done anything against either the temple or the Emperor.”
text: Amaa Pɔɔl a kya kanɔ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ŋ waa ase̱ŋ bɔye̱ ko̱ bo̱ kye ane̱ Yudaa awuye a mbraa‑ɔ. Ne̱ mo̱ŋ sa ne̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ a waa iyisi, ne̱ mo̱ŋ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ bɔye̱ ko̱ mɔ bo̱ kye Roma awuye owure dabe̱‑ɔ na mò̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ ne̱ o de‑o.”

ref: Festus wanted to please the leaders. So he asked Paul, “Are you willing to go to Jerusalem and be tried by me on these charges?”
text: Amaa gominaa‑o a kpa fe̱yɛ Yudaa awuye kpa mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ oo bise Pɔɔl fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ fo̱ yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm, na ase̱sɛ ya gyi fo̱ ase̱ŋ mo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ aaa?”

ref: Paul replied, “I am on trial in the Emperor's court, and that's where I should be tried. You know very well I have not done anything to harm the Jewish nation.
text: Ne̱ Pɔɔl yɛ, “Roma ɔse̱ŋgyipo̱ ne̱ ɔ ye̱re̱ Roma owure dabe̱‑ɔ ayaa‑rɔ e̱ gye̱ fo̱, ne̱ fo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ ne̱ ŋ ye̱re̱. Mo̱ a gye̱ Roma maŋ kigyi‑o si‑o, kpa da fe̱yɛ fo̱ gyi mo̱ ase̱ŋ mfe̱e̱. Fo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ a ŋu fe̱yɛ mo̱ŋ waa Yudaa awuye‑o bɔye̱ ko̱.
