ref: If I had done something deserving death, I would not ask to escape the death penalty. But I am not guilty of any of these crimes, and no one has the right to hand me over to these people. I now ask to be tried by the Emperor himself.”
text: Ne̱ mo̱ ya kye mbraa‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ ya waa ko̱tɔko̱ ne̱ a kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ mɔɔ mo̱‑ɔ, ma tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ yɔwe̱ mo̱. Amaa ase̱ŋ ne̱ bo̱ yɛ mo̱ a waa bo̱ kye bamo̱‑ɔ a mo̱ŋ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o si‑o, fo̱ mo̱ŋ de kpa fe̱yɛ fo̱ taa mo̱ sa bamo̱, na bo̱ nya kpa fo̱ ase̱ŋ. Mo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ Roma owure dabe̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ o gyi mo̱ ase̱ŋ.”

ref: After Festus had talked this over with members of his council, he told Paul, “You have asked to be tried by the Emperor, and to the Emperor you will go!” Paul Speaks to Agrippa and Bernice
text: Ne̱ gominaa‑o na mò̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ awuye a ya ba aŋu bo̱ suŋ abɛɛ, ne̱ baa sa mò̱ mfɛɛre̱‑ɔ pwɛɛ ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ be̱ŋŋaa Pɔɔl fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ yɛ ane̱ owure dabe̱‑ɔ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ o gyi fo̱ ase̱ŋ. Amo̱se̱, mò̱ ase̱ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ yɔ.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Gominaa Feesitus a baa Pɔɔl Agripa na Beronikee ase̱‑ɔ

ref: A few days later King Agrippa and Bernice came to Caesarea to visit Festus.
text: Ke̱tɔ mo̱ŋ kyee, ne̱ Yudaa awuye owure ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Agripa‑o na mò̱ pe̱kye̱e̱ Beronikee a ba Kaye̱sare̱ya maŋ‑nɔ a bo̱ bo̱ ka gominaa‑o kanɔ.

ref: They had been there for several days, when Festus told the king about the charges against Paul. He said: Felix left a man here in jail,
text: Bamo̱ a kyee mfe̱ŋ kafwe̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ gominaa‑o a kaapo̱ owure‑o Pɔɔl ase̱ŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ. Ɔ yɛ, “Ɔnyare̱ ko̱ bo̱ mfe̱e̱, ne̱ Feelis a tii mò̱ yɔwe̱ mò̱ bo̱ be̱ya‑rɔ bo̱ fo̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ.

ref: and when I went to Jerusalem, the chief priests and the Jewish leaders came and asked me to find him guilty.
text: Saŋ ne̱ mo̱ a yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm‑ɔ, Yudaa awuye a Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱‑ɔ na maŋ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ a baa mò̱ ase̱ŋ mo̱ ase̱, ne̱ bo̱ yɛ m bu mò̱ ke̱pɔ.

ref: I told them it isn't the Roman custom to hand a man over to people who are bringing charges against him. He must first have the chance to meet them face to face and to defend himself against their charges.
text: Amaa mo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ Roma awuye fe̱raa ane̱ mo̱ŋ de se̱sɛ ne̱ baa baa mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ ane̱ e̱ sa bo̱ko̱ pwɛɛ na bamo̱ ne̱ mò̱ aa bamo̱ de ase̱ŋ‑ɔ dɛɛ gyaŋŋe̱ na ɔ kya kanɔ.

ref: So when they came here with me, I wasted no time. On the very next day I took my place on the judge's bench and ordered him to be brought in.
text: Saŋ ne̱ baa ba mfe̱e̱‑ɔ, mo̱ŋ lɛɛ gywii, amaa ke̱mo̱ kaye̱ ŋke‑o, ne̱ mo̱ a kye̱na mo̱ se̱ŋgyikpa, ne̱ mo̱ a sa kanɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ baa ɔnyare̱‑ɔ.

ref: But when the men stood up to make their charges against him, they did not accuse him of any of the crimes that I thought they would.
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ mò̱ aa bamo̱ de ase̱ŋ‑ɔ a ko̱so̱ ye̱re̱, amaa bo̱ mo̱ŋ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ɔɔ waa ase̱ŋ bɔye̱ ne̱ weetee mo̱ a fa fe̱yɛ ba tɔwe̱‑ɔ.

ref: Since I did not know how to find out the truth about all this, I asked Paul if he would be willing to go to Jerusalem and be put on trial there.
text: N de̱ŋ mo̱ŋ nyi ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa, na ŋ nya ase̱ŋ amo̱‑rɔ nu‑o. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ mo̱ a bise Pɔɔl fe̱yɛ ɔ kpa a ɔ yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm, na bo̱ ya gyi ase̱ŋ aaa.

ref: But Paul asked to be kept in jail until the Emperor could decide his case. So I ordered him to be kept here until I could send him to the Emperor.
text: Amaa Pɔɔl mo̱ŋ sure. Ɔ yɛ mò̱ a gye̱ Roma maŋ kigyi‑o si‑o, o de kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ yaa mò̱ ane̱ owure dabe̱‑ɔ ase̱, na ɔ ya gyi mò̱ ase̱ŋ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ mo̱ a sa kanɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ de̱e̱re̱ mò̱ se̱ bo̱ fo̱ saŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ nya a ŋ yaa mò̱ ane̱ owure dabe̱‑ɔ ase̱‑ɔ.”

ref: Then Agrippa said to Festus, “I would also like to hear what this man has to say.” Festus answered, “You can hear him tomorrow.”
text: Ne̱ Agripa a tɔwe̱ gywii gominaa‑o fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ nu ɔnyare̱ amo̱ kanɔ‑rɔ ase̱ŋ.” Ne̱ gominaa‑o a be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ, “Nyaŋe̱ fo̱ i nu mò̱ kanɔ‑rɔ ase̱ŋ.”

ref: The next day Agrippa and Bernice made a big show as they came into the meeting room. High ranking army officers and leading citizens of the town were also there. Festus then ordered Paul to be brought in
text: Ke̱mo̱ kaye̱ ŋke‑o, ne̱ gominaa‑o na Owure Agripa na mò̱ pe̱kye̱e̱‑ɔ na bamo̱ ne̱ ba da nkyirele bo̱ gya bamo̱ se̱‑ɔ na asoogyaa agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ na maŋ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ a bo̱ lwee lamaŋ a ke̱kyaŋ dabe̱ ne̱ ba gyaŋŋe̱ ke̱mo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ gominaa‑o a sa kanɔ, ne̱ soogyaa awuye a baa Pɔɔl mfe̱ŋ.

ref: and said: King Agrippa and other guests, look at this man! Every Jew from Jerusalem and Caesarea has come to me, demanding for him to be put to death.
text: Ne̱ gominaa‑o yɛ, “Nana Agripa na bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱e̱‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ a ŋu ɔnyare̱ ne̱ Yudaa awuye yɛ bamo̱ aa mò̱ de ase̱ŋ, ne̱ baa baa mò̱ ase̱ŋ mo̱ ase̱‑ɔ. Mfe̱e̱ na Yɛro̱salɛm ne̱ baa ko̱re̱ mo̱ fe̱yɛ n de̱e̱re̱ bamo̱ ase̱ŋ‑nɔ, na ba fɛɛ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ m mɔɔ mò̱.

ref: I have not found him guilty of any crime deserving death. But because he has asked to be judged by the Emperor, I have decided to send him to Rome.
text: Amaa mo̱ŋ taare̱ ŋu ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ waa ne̱ ke̱ e̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ n sa a bo̱ mɔɔ mò̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ mò̱ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ o de kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ yaa mò̱ ane̱ owure dabe̱‑ɔ ase̱, na o gyi mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ a fa fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ yaa mò̱ mò̱ ase̱.

ref: I have to write some facts about this man to the Emperor. So I have brought him before all of you, but especially before you, King Agrippa. After we have talked about his case, I will then have something to write.
text: Amaa mo̱ŋ de ke̱se̱ŋ ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ ye̱re̱ se̱ a m bo̱ kyo̱rɛɛ mò̱ ase̱ŋ yaa ane̱ owure dabe̱‑ɔ. Amo̱se̱ ne̱ mo̱ a baa mò̱ mo̱ne̱ ase̱, fo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ Nana Agripa ase̱, na ane̱ ya bise mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, na ŋ nya nya ko̱tɔko̱ kyo̱rɛɛ.

ref: Agrippa told Paul, “You may now speak for yourself.” Paul stretched out his hand and said:
text: Ne̱ Owure Agripa a tɔwe̱ gywii Pɔɔl fe̱yɛ, “Mbe̱yɔmɔ fe̱raa, ane̱ a sa fo̱ kpa, tɔwe̱ fo̱ ase̱ŋ.” Ne̱ Pɔɔl a teyi ke̱sare̱e̱, ne̱ ɔ yɛ,

ref: King Agrippa, I am glad for this chance to defend myself before you today on all these charges my own people have brought against me.
text: “Nana Agripa, mo̱ kuŋu bo̱ daŋ fe̱yɛ ndɔɔ mo̱ a ye̱re̱ fo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ mo̱ e̱ kya kanɔ bo̱ le̱e̱ ase̱ŋ pɛɛɛ ne̱ Yudaa awuye yɛ mo̱ a waa‑ɔ se̱.

ref: You know a lot about our religious customs and the beliefs that divide us. So I ask you to listen patiently to me.
text: Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ nyi Yudaa awuye ade̱daase̱ŋ‑ɔ na bamo̱ ikii‑ro dame̱naŋsɛ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ fo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ gyi kanyite, na fo̱ nu mo̱ ase̱.

ref: All the Jews have known me since I was a child. They know what kind of life I have lived in my own country and in Jerusalem. And if they were willing, they could tell you I was a Pharisee, a member of a group that is stricter than any other.
text: Bamo̱ e̱ kaa bo̱ gyi kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱raa de, bo̱ nyi hare̱e̱ le̱e̱ saŋ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a gye̱ se̱sɛ ne̱ n dɛɛ n tii Farisii awuye‑o si. Ne̱ mo̱ne̱ nyi fe̱yɛ Farisii awuye‑o gye̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ gya ane̱ mbraa‑ɔ na ane̱ ananatɔ ke̱maa se̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: Day and night our twelve tribes have earnestly served God, waiting for his promised blessings. King Agrippa, because of this hope, some of our leaders have brought charges against me.
text: Ke̱tɔ ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱ ane̱ ase̱sɛ a nsuro kudu ŋnyɔ‑ɔ a kɔɔre̱ gyi fe̱yɛ ane̱ e̱ nya ne̱ a kperi amo̱ se̱ ane̱ i suŋ Wuribware̱ mpase̱ na kanye‑o. Na bo̱ le̱e̱ mo̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi mɔ se̱ ne̱ Yudaa awuye e̱ po̱rɔ mo̱‑ɔ.

ref: I once thought that I should do everything I could to oppose Jesus from Nazareth.
text: Ŋ gbaa, mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ a dɛɛ fa fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ gya e̱kpa ke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ e̱ taare̱‑ɔ se̱, na ŋ waa ase̱ŋ bo̱ nye̱ra Yeesuu Nasarɛte̱nyi a ke̱nyare̱‑ɔ.

ref: I did this first in Jerusalem, and with the authority of the chief priests I put many of God's people in jail. I even voted for them to be killed.
text: Ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ a waa Yɛro̱salɛm‑ɔ ne̱e̱. Mo̱ a nya ke̱yaale̱ŋ bo̱ le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱‑ɔ ase̱, ne̱ mo̱ a tii Wuribware̱ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ, na saŋ ne̱ baa bu bamo̱ ke̱pɔ‑ɔ, mo̱ a sure si fe̱yɛ bo̱ mɔɔ bamo̱.

ref: I often had them punished in our synagogues, and I tried to make them give up their faith. In fact, I was so angry with them, that I went looking for them in foreign cities.
text: Iluwi bwe̱e̱tɔ mo̱ a nare̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ akyaŋ‑nɔ gyaŋŋe̱ se̱ mo̱ e̱ sa ne̱ amo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ i gyiiri bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ gya Yeesuu a e̱kpa‑ɔ se̱ ke̱se̱bɔ. Ne̱ mo̱ a le̱ŋ bamo̱, na bo̱ taa Yeesuu ke̱nyare̱ bo̱ da yii. Mo̱ duŋ a fwii bamo̱ se̱, hare̱e̱ gbaa mo̱ a yɔ ke̱fɔ ke̱fɔ e̱maŋ ko̱ se̱ ya waa bamo̱ awo̱re̱fɔɔ.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Pɔɔl a tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ oo kii Kristoonyi‑o

ref: About noon I saw a light brighter than the sun. It flashed from heaven on me and on everyone traveling with me.
text: Nana, mpase̱ ko̱ ne̱ mo̱ a ŋu ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ ko̱ ne̱ ko̱ kyɔ kyɔwe̱ ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ‑ɔ a le̱e̱ awo̱re̱‑rɔ bo̱ we̱e̱ muruwaa mo̱ aa ase̱sɛ ne̱ mo̱ aa bamo̱ naa‑ɔ se̱.

ref: We all fell to the ground. Then I heard a voice say to me in Aramaic, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me? It's foolish to fight against me!”
text: Ne̱ ane̱ pɛɛɛ a le̱e̱ da swe̱e̱re̱, ne̱ mo̱ a nu bo̱re̱ ko̱ ane̱ Heebrii se̱ŋsa‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Sɔɔl, Sɔɔl! Nte̱tɔ ne̱e̱ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ tɔɔraa mo̱ ane̱ŋ? Kayaagyi ne̱ ɔ yɛ ɔ maa nu mò̱ se̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ mo̱ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ ɔ waa mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ne̱e̱.’

ref: “Who are you?” I asked. Then the Lord answered, “I am Jesus! I am the one you are so cruel to.
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a bise fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, fo̱ nsɛ ne̱e̱?’ Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ e̱ gye̱ Yeesuu ne̱ fo̱ e̱ tɔɔraa‑ɔ.

ref: Now stand up. I have appeared to you, because I have chosen you to be my servant. You are to tell others what you have learned about me and what I will show you later.”
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ ko̱so̱ ye̱re̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a le̱e̱ fo̱ se̱ ne̱e̱ a n lee fo̱ a fo̱ suŋ kusuŋ sa mo̱. Na fo̱ ya tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ a ŋu mo̱ ndɔɔ‑ɔ ase̱ŋ na ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ ba a m bo̱ kaapo̱ fo̱ nyaŋe̱ na kraye̱‑ɔ gywii ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ.

ref: First I preached to the people in Damascus, and then I went to Jerusalem and all over Judea. Finally, I went to the Gentiles and said, “Stop sinning and turn to God! Then prove what you have done by the way you live.”
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ yɔ Damasikus, ne̱ mo̱ a yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ na Yude̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ pɛɛɛ, na ŋkee nde̱ se̱ awuye‑o ase̱. Ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ɔke̱maa nu mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱, na bo̱ kiŋŋi le̱e̱ bamo̱ e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ, na bo̱ ba Wuribware̱ ase̱, na bo̱ waa ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ baa nu bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱, ne̱ baa kiŋŋi le̱e̱ e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: But all this time God has helped me, and I have preached both to the rich and to the poor. I have told them only what the prophets and Moses said would happen.
text: Amaa Wuribware̱ a kya mo̱‑rɔ, ne̱ mo̱ a ye̱re̱ keŋkeŋ bo̱ fo̱ ndɔɔ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ mo̱ i gywii ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ hare̱e̱ awure na ase̱sɛ laŋgyaŋ. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱ e̱ ba a ke̱ bo̱ waa‑ɔ dɛɛ ne̱ Wuribware̱ akyaamɛɛ ne̱ baa dɛɛ kye̱na‑ɔ na ane̱ mbraa ɔsapo̱ Mosis a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱ e̱ waa‑ɔ.

ref: I told them how the Messiah would suffer and be the first to be raised from death, so he could bring light to his own people and to the Gentiles.
text: Bo̱ yɛ, Kristoo ne̱ Wuribware̱ yɛ o suŋ‑o e̱ ba na mò̱ ase̱sɛ sa a o gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ, na bo̱ mɔɔ mò̱, na mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ a o kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro. Na mò̱‑ɔ mɔ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ timaa a ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ mɔ gywii mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ase̱sɛ Yudaa awuye na nde̱ se̱ awuye‑o.”

ref: Before Paul finished defending himself, Festus shouted, “Paul, you're crazy! Too much learning has driven you out of your mind.”
text: Pɔɔl a maa se̱ ɔ kya kanɔ mfaanɛɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ gominaa‑o a faa mò̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ, “Pɔɔl, fo̱ e̱ lɔ e̱bo̱ŋ ne̱e̱. Fo̱ atɔ ne̱ fo̱ nyi bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ a waa fo̱ e̱bo̱ŋ!”

ref: But Paul replied, “Honorable Festus, I am not crazy. What I am saying is true, and it makes sense.
text: Ne̱ Pɔɔl a be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ, “Ɔbre̱sɛ! Ma lɔ e̱bo̱ŋ ne̱e̱. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱e̱.

ref: None of these things happened off in a corner somewhere. I am sure that King Agrippa knows what I am talking about. That's why I can speak so plainly to him.”
text: Nana Agripa, mo̱ e̱ taare̱ a n sa se̱ŋsa fo̱ ase̱, na kufu mo̱ŋ de mo̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ nyi amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ ase̱ŋ. Mo̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ a n gyi fe̱yɛ fo̱ nyi ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ yɔ se̱ ane̱ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ dame̱naŋsɛ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa a ŋu ase̱ŋ mɔ pɛɛɛ ne̱ aa ba‑ɔ.

ref: Then Paul said to Agrippa, “Do you believe what the prophets said? I know you do.”
text: Nana, fo̱ a kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ akyaamɛɛ ne̱ baa dɛɛ kye̱na‑ɔ gyi bɛɛɛ? Ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ fo̱ a kɔɔre̱ bamo̱ gyi!”

ref: Agrippa asked Paul, “In such a short time do you think you can talk me into being a Christian?”
text: Ne̱ Agripa a tɔwe̱ gywii Pɔɔl fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ e̱ fa fe̱yɛ saŋ kafwe̱e̱ mɔ ne̱ ane̱ a gyi mfe̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ, fo̱ e̱ taare̱ a fo̱ sa a ŋ kii Kristoonyi aaa?” Ne̱ Pɔɔl a be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ,

ref: Paul answered, “Whether it takes a short time or a long time, I wish you and everyone else who hears me today would become just like me! Except, of course, for these chains.”
text: “Hare̱e̱ ke̱tɔ a kyee, bɛɛɛ ke̱tɔ mo̱ŋ kyee ooo, mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ aa bamo̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i nu mo̱ ase̱ ndɔɔ‑ɔ kii ke̱tɔ ne̱ n gye̱‑ɔ, amɔ e̱kpe̱e̱ mɔ ne̱ baa da mo̱‑ɔ.”

ref: Then King Agrippa, Governor Festus, Bernice, and everyone who was with them got up.
text: Ne̱ owure‑o na gominaa‑o na owure mò̱ pe̱kye̱e̱‑ɔ na bamo̱ ne̱ baa saŋ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a ko̱so̱ le̱e̱ ke̱kyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: But before they left, they said, “This man isn't guilty of anything. He doesn't deserve to die or to be put in jail.”
text: Bamo̱ a le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ gywii abɛɛ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔnyare̱ mɔ mo̱ŋ waa ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ke̱ e̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ mɔɔ mò̱ bɛɛɛ bo̱ tii mò̱‑ɔ.”

ref: Agrippa told Festus, “Paul could have been set free, if he had not asked to be tried by the Roman Emperor.”
text: Ne̱ owure‑o a tɔwe̱ gywii gominaa‑o fe̱yɛ, “Ɔnyare̱ mɔ dɛɛ ɔ mo̱ŋ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ kpa fe̱yɛ owure dabe̱‑ɔ gyi mò̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱e̱, weetee ane̱ dee a ane̱ yɔwe̱ mò̱ a ɔ nare̱.”

ref: When it was time for us to sail to Rome, Captain Julius from the Emperor's special troops was put in charge of Paul and the other prisoners.
text: Saŋ ne̱ baa fa mfɛɛre̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ taa ko̱re̱e̱ bo̱rɔ nkyu si yɔ Italiya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa baa Pɔɔl na ase̱sɛ ko̱ ne̱ bamo̱ gbaa baa tii‑o bo̱ waa soogyaa bre̱sɛ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Yuliyus ne̱ ɔ bo̱ asoogyaa katuŋ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ “owure asoogyaa katuŋ”‑o ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ.

ref: We went aboard a ship from Adramyttium that was about to sail to some ports along the coast of Asia. Aristarchus from Thessalonica in Macedonia sailed on the ship with us.
text: Ne̱ ane̱ a lwee ko̱re̱e̱ ko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ Adramitiyɔŋ maŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ waa siraa ɔ yɔ e̱daŋkpa ne̱ e̱ bo̱ Asiya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ ane̱ a me̱raa se̱ ane̱ e̱ yɔ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ Tisalonikaa maŋ‑nɔ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ Makedoniya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Aristaakus tii ane̱ se̱.

ref: The next day we came to shore at Sidon. Captain Julius was very kind to Paul. He even let him visit his friends, so they could give him whatever he needed.
text: Kaye̱ ŋke‑o, ne̱ ane̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ a ya daŋ Sidɔŋ maŋ se̱. Yuliyus a bware‑o si‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ sa Pɔɔl kpa mfe̱ŋ fe̱yɛ ɔ le̱e̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ ya ŋu mò̱ nyare̱‑ana, na bo̱ sa mò̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ki tiri mò̱‑ɔ.

ref: When we left Sidon, the winds were blowing against us, and we sailed close to the island of Cyprus to be safe from the wind.
text: Ne̱ ane̱ a be̱e̱ kya se̱ le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ. Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ e̱ gyaŋŋaa afwii se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ane̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ a kwaye̱ bo̱rɔ Kiprus kwii a keri‑o, na afwii maa nya da gyaŋŋaa ane̱‑ɔ.

ref: There the army captain found a ship from Alexandria that was going to Italy. So he ordered us to board that ship.
text: Ane̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ a fo̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ yɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ soogyaa bre̱sɛ‑ɔ a taa ane̱ waa ko̱re̱e̱ baŋbaŋ‑nɔ. Ko̱re̱e̱ amo̱ a le̱e̱ Alɛsandriya maŋ‑nɔ ne̱e̱, ne̱ ɔɔ waa siraa ɔ kpa a ɔ yɔ Italiya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱.

ref: We sailed along slowly for several days and had a hard time reaching Cnidus. The wind would not let us go any farther in that direction, so we sailed past Cape Salmone, where the island of Crete would protect us from the wind.
text: Ne̱ ane̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ a nare̱ bɔye̱ bɔye̱ ŋke bwe̱e̱tɔ, ne̱ ane̱ a ba ke̱kpo̱ne̱ bo̱ fo̱ Kanidus maŋ se̱. Ne̱ ane̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ e̱ kpa a ɔ yɔ kyɔwe̱ twɛɛkpa a keri‑o. Amaa afwii a gyaŋŋaa ane̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ane̱ a kii bo̱rɔ ke̱be̱na se̱ a keri‑o, ne̱ ane̱ a te̱ŋ ɔpo̱o̱‑rɔ yɔ ya fo̱ Kriita kwii. Ne̱ ane̱ a kyo̱ŋ Salamonee maŋ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ yɔ kyɔwe̱ twɛɛkpa a keri‑o na swe̱e̱re̱ bo̱ ane̱ kigyise si, na afwii‑o ma nya da ane̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: We went slowly along the coast and finally reached a place called Fair Havens, not far from the town of Lasea.
text: Amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ gbaa ooo, ane̱ a nare̱ bɔye̱, ne̱ aa waa le̱ŋ sa ane̱ pwɛɛ ne̱ ŋkee ane̱ dɛɛ ya daŋ daŋkpa ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ “Mfe̱ŋ a e̱ko̱re̱e̱ daŋkpa timaa‑o” ne̱ ɔ maa maŋ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ Lase̱ya‑ɔ.

ref: Then Paul spoke to the crew of the ship, “Men, listen to me! If we sail now, our ship and its cargo will be badly damaged, and many lives will be lost.”
text: “Mo̱ nyare̱‑ana, mo̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ mbe̱yɔmɔ ane̱ ya kya ane̱ kpa‑ɔ se̱, ase̱ŋ bɔye̱ e̱ ba. Ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ i buŋ na e̱tɔso̱rɔ ne̱ e̱ bo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ fo̱, na ase̱sɛ be̱e̱ wu amo̱ se̱.”

ref: But Julius listened to the captain of the ship and its owner, rather than to Paul.
text: Amaa ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ de̱e̱re̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ na ko̱re̱e̱ wuye yɛ bo̱ kya bamo̱ kpa‑ɔ se̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ soogyaa bre̱sɛ‑ɔ a nu ke̱tɔ ne̱ baa tɔwe̱‑ɔ yɔwe̱ Pɔɔl lee‑o.

ref: The harbor at Fair Havens wasn't a good place to spend the winter. Because of this, almost everyone agreed that we should at least try to sail along the coast of Crete as far as Phoenix. It had a harbor that opened toward the southwest and northwest, and we could spend the winter there. The Storm at Sea
text: Daŋkpa amo̱ mfe̱ŋ mo̱ŋ bo̱ daŋ fe̱yɛ bo̱ kye̱na, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ saŋ amo̱ afwii e̱ da mfe̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ, a bware bamo̱ ase̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ kya bamo̱ kpa‑ɔ se̱ nkyu‑o si, na bamo̱ e̱ taare̱ a bo̱ fo̱ daŋkpa ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ Fonikii ne̱ ɔ bo̱ daŋ sa bamo̱ a bo̱ kye̱na aferi asa bɛɛɛ ana ne̱ afwii e̱ da bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ. Fonikii gye̱ e̱ko̱re̱e̱ daŋkpa Kriita kwii si ne̱e̱, ne̱ a bo̱ kyɔwe̱ twɛɛkpa kigyise si a keri‑o na kyɔwe̱ twɛɛkpa ke̱be̱na se̱ a keri‑o. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ afwii a to̱ ane̱ ɔpo̱o̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ

ref: When a gentle wind from the south started blowing, the men thought it was a good time to do what they had planned. So they pulled up the anchor, and we sailed along the coast of Crete.
text: Ne̱ afwii kafwe̱e̱ ko̱ a le̱e̱ kyɔwe̱ le̱e̱kpa kigyise si a keri‑o ba a kyaare̱, ne̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ a fa fe̱yɛ ba taare̱ a bo̱ waa ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kra‑ɔ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa saŋŋe̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ be̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ ɔpo̱o̱ se̱, ne̱ baa kya se̱ ba yɔ kyɔwe̱ twɛɛkpa a keri‑o, ne̱ Kriita kideŋbe si bo̱ bamo̱ kigyise si fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ bo̱ kyɔ bo̱ naa‑ɔ.

ref: days you have been so worried that you haven't eaten a thing.
text: Amaa ke̱tɔ mo̱ŋ kyee, ne̱ afwii dabe̱ ko̱ ne̱ aa le̱e̱ kyɔwe̱ le̱e̱kpa ke̱be̱na‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ a da le̱e̱ kideŋbe si.

ref: We went along the island of Cauda on the side that was protected from the wind. We had a hard time holding the lifeboat in place,
text: Ne̱ ane̱ a bo̱rɔ Kawuda kwii kamɛɛ, ne̱ ntɛɛrɛɛ a yase̱ kako̱re̱e̱gyii ne̱ ba gyiiri saŋ ke̱maa‑ɔ le̱e̱ nkyu‑ro bo̱ waa ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ alaŋkpare̱‑ɔ e̱ da kamo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Baa kpo̱ne̱ pwɛɛ ne̱ baa dɛɛ waa amo̱.

ref: but finally we got it where it belonged. Then the sailors wrapped ropes around the ship to hold it together. They lowered the sail and let the ship drift along, because they were afraid it might hit the sandbanks in the gulf of Syrtis.
text: Ne̱ baa ba ife dabe̱ so̱swe̱e̱ bo̱ ŋure muruwaa ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ fɔŋfɔŋ na ɔ ma nya bwee‑ro. Ne̱ kufu de bamo̱ fe̱yɛ ba lwee apo̱rɔ ko̱ ne̱ a bo̱ nkyu‑o kaase̱‑ɔ‑rɔ, na bamo̱ ko̱re̱e̱ sii‑ro. Apo̱rɔ mɔ maa Libiya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ a kideŋbe‑o ne̱e̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa lee waagya ne̱ ɔ kya ko̱re̱e̱ so̱so̱ ne̱ mò̱ afwii‑o a sa ne̱ ko̱re̱e̱ naa‑ɔ, na afwii‑o nya taa ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ yaa to̱ŋ ko̱.

ref: The storm was so fierce that the next day they threw some of the ship's cargo overboard.
text: Afwii dabe̱ amo̱ mo̱ŋ yɔwe̱, amo̱se̱‑ɔ ke̱mo̱ kaye̱ ŋke‑o, ne̱ baa le̱e̱ ba lee e̱tɔso̱rɔ ne̱ e̱ bo̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ e̱ko̱ bo̱ twe̱e̱ nkyu‑o‑ro.

ref: Then on the third day, with their bare hands they threw overboard some of the ship's gear.
text: Ne̱ kake ne̱ ka be̱e̱ ka gya se̱‑ɔ, baa lee ko̱re̱e̱‑rɔ ate̱tɔmo̱ŋta‑ɔ ako̱ bo̱ twe̱e̱.

ref: For several days we could not see either the sun or the stars. A strong wind kept blowing, and we finally gave up all hope of being saved.
text: Ŋkee, waa ŋke kanɔ ŋko̱ na ane̱ maa ŋu kyɔwe̱ bɛɛɛ ke̱kye̱e̱‑e̱‑kpa‑agyi, na afwii maa se̱ a kya bwe̱e̱tɔ. Ŋkee fe̱raa, ne̱ ane̱ abaa‑rɔ a yɔre̱. Ane̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ kɔɔre̱ gyi fe̱yɛ sɛye̱ e̱ lɛɛ taare̱ a a mo̱rɔwe̱ ane̱.

ref: Since none of us had eaten anything for a long time, Paul stood up and told the men: You should have listened to me! If you had stayed on in Crete, you would not have had this damage and loss.
text: Ke̱tɔ a kyee na ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ taare̱ gyi ateese, ne̱ Pɔɔl a ko̱so̱ ye̱re̱, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ nyare̱‑ana, mo̱ne̱ a dɛɛ nu mo̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱e̱, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ baa ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ le̱e̱ Kriita kwii ne̱e̱, weetee ase̱ŋ dabe̱ na ke̱nye̱ra mɔ be̱e̱ a mo̱ŋ to̱ ane̱.

ref: But now I beg you to cheer up, because you will be safe. Only the ship will be lost.
text: Amaa mbe̱yɔmɔ fe̱raa mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ mo̱ne̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ waa kakpo̱nɔ! Mo̱ne̱ ɔko̱ maa wu daa, amɔ ko̱re̱e̱ wo̱re̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ fo̱.

ref: I belong to God, and I worship him. Last night he sent an angel
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ndee kanye Wuribware̱, nyaŋpe̱ ne̱ mo̱ i suŋ‑o, a suŋ mò̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ mo̱ ase̱.

ref: to tell me, “Paul, don't be afraid! You will stand trial before the Emperor. And because of you, God will save the lives of everyone on the ship.”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Pɔɔl! Fo̱ ma sa a kufu nya fo̱. Kanɔ ŋke̱maa, fo̱ e̱ fo̱ Roma awuye owure dabe̱‑ɔ akatɔ‑rɔ. Wuribware̱ bware, ne̱ ɔ kpa fo̱ ase̱ŋ, ne̱ oo nu fo̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱‑ɔ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ bamo̱ ne̱ fo̱ aa bamo̱ bo̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ ɔko̱ maa wu.’

ref: Cheer up! I am sure that God will do exactly what he promised.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ nyare̱‑ana, mo̱nꞌ waa kakpo̱nɔ! A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ gyi fe̱yɛ ɔ waa ane̱ŋ mɔ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱‑ɔ.

ref: But we will first be shipwrecked on some island.
text: Amaa ane̱ e̱ daŋ kwii si pwɛɛ.”

ref: They cut the anchors loose and let them sink into the sea. At the same time they untied the ropes that were holding the rudders. Next, they raised the sail at the front of the ship and let the wind carry the ship toward the beach.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa te̱ŋ ife ne̱ ii ŋure adebu dabe̱ ne̱ a sa ne̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ e̱ ye̱re̱‑ɔ, ne̱ aa lwee nkyu‑o kaase̱, ne̱ baa saŋŋe̱ ife ne̱ ii ŋure abaŋ dabe̱ ne̱ bo̱ de bo̱ le̱ŋ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ baa yase̱ waagya kagyingyii ne̱ ɔ bo̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ akatɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ na afwii‑o nya neŋ ane̱ yaa akatɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ ane̱ a waa fe̱yɛ ane̱ e̱ daŋ kideŋbe si.

ref: The sailors wanted to escape from the ship. So they lowered the lifeboat into the water, pretending that they were letting down some anchors from the front of the ship.
text: Ne̱ ntɛɛrɛɛ‑ɔ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ bo̱ se̱re̱ le̱e̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa ba ife bo̱ kya kako̱re̱e̱gyii‑o, ne̱ baa yɔwe̱‑rɔ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ nkyu si, ne̱ baa waa fe̱yɛ bamo̱ a kpa a bo̱ lweero ya waa akatɔ‑rɔ a adebu dabe̱ ne̱ a sa ne̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ e̱ ye̱re̱‑ɔ.

ref: The sailors were afraid that we might hit some rocks, and they let down four anchors from the back of the ship. Then they prayed for daylight.
text: Ne̱ kufu de bamo̱ fe̱yɛ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ e̱ nare̱ afo̱re̱ se̱ na o bwee‑ro, amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa ba ko̱re̱e̱ amo̱ kamɛɛ adebu dabe̱ ana ne̱ baa ba agye̱re̱tɔ bo̱ waa, ne̱ a sa ne̱ ɔ ye̱re̱‑ɔ bo̱ waa mò̱ kaase̱, na ɔ nya ye̱re̱. Ne̱ baa ko̱re̱ bamo̱ ikisi fe̱yɛ kaye̱ ke me̱naŋ.

ref: But Paul said to Captain Julius and the soldiers, “If the sailors don't stay on the ship, you won't have any chance to save your lives.”
text: Amaa Pɔɔl a tɔwe̱ gywii asoogyaa bre̱sɛ na asoogyaa‑o ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ntɛɛrɛɛ‑ɔ mɔ e̱ mo̱ŋ sii kye̱na ko̱re̱e̱ mɔ‑rɔ fe̱raa, ane̱ pɛɛɛ i wu.”

ref: The soldiers then cut the ropes that held the lifeboat and let it fall into the sea.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ asoogyaa‑o a te̱ŋŋe̱ ife ne̱ kako̱re̱e̱gyii de‑ro‑o, ne̱ kako̱re̱e̱gyii‑o a se̱re̱ ka yii.

ref: I beg you to eat something. Your lives depend on it. Do this and not one of you will be hurt.”
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ mo̱ne̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ gyi ateese. N gye̱ ane̱ŋ, mo̱ne̱ i wu. Mo̱ne̱ ɔko̱ kipwiigyi gbaa maa fo̱, saŋbo̱to̱ lowi ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i wu.”

ref: After Paul had said this, he took a piece of bread and gave thanks to God. Then in front of everyone, he broke the bread and ate some.
text: Pɔɔl a tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ taa bodobodoo, ne̱ ɔɔ sa Wuribware̱ aŋsɛ bamo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ we̱e̱ se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ te̱ŋŋe̱‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ o gyi.

ref: They all felt encouraged, and each of them ate something.
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ a waa kakpo̱nɔ, ne̱ bamo̱ ɔke̱maa gbaa a gyi ateese.

ref: and after everyone had eaten, they threw the cargo of wheat into the sea to make the ship lighter. The Shipwreck
text: Ɔke̱maa mɔ a gyi kpo̱ne̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa lee e̱tɔso̱rɔ na ayu ne̱ a bo̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ bo̱ twe̱e̱ nkyu‑o‑ro na ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ ma lɛɛ nya waa dwii bwe̱e̱tɔ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ a bwee‑ro, ne̱ oo mwiire‑o

ref: Morning came, and the ship's crew saw a coast they did not recognize. But they did see a cove with a beach. So they decided to try to run the ship aground on the beach.
text: Kaye̱ ŋke‑o, ntɛɛrɛɛ‑ɔ a pini fe̱yɛ swe̱e̱re̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱ e̱fɔ, amaa bo̱ mo̱ŋ taare̱ pini mò̱ ke̱nyare̱. Baa ŋu fe̱yɛ ɔpo̱o e̱kɛɛke̱e̱ se̱ kisipu bo̱ mfe̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ, amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa fa fe̱yɛ bamo̱ e̱ taare̱ ba ya daŋ mfe̱ŋ.

ref: But it ran aground on a sandbank. The front of the ship stuck firmly in the sand, and the rear was being smashed by the force of the waves.
text: Ne̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ a ya daŋ e̱se̱ kipireŋ ko̱ ne̱ ke̱ bo̱ nkyu‑o‑ro‑o si, ne̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ akatɔ‑rɔ a sii e̱se̱‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ ŋkee ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ maa lɛɛ yɔ akatɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ alaŋkpare̱ dabe̱ a da ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ kamɛɛ ane̱ŋ‑aaa, ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ bwee‑ro kyikpuri kyikpuri.

ref: But Captain Julius wanted to save Paul's life, and he did not let the soldiers do what they had planned. Instead, he ordered everyone who could swim to jump into the water and head for shore.
text: Amaa soogyaa bre̱sɛ‑ɔ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ mo̱rɔwe̱ Pɔɔl. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ ɔbre̱sɛ‑ɔ yɛ bo̱ yɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ ko̱waa. Ne̱ ɔ yɛ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ nyi e̱twɛɛ‑ɔ gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ fuwi ywe̱e̱ nkyu‑o‑ro, na bo̱ twɛɛ yɔ kideŋbe si.

ref: Then he told the others to hold on to planks of wood or parts of the ship. At last, everyone safely reached shore.
text: Na bamo̱ ne̱ baa saŋ‑ɔ mɔ kra iyii baŋŋe̱sɛ na ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ abweesɛ‑ɔ‑rɔ, na e̱ nya taa bamo̱ yaa kideŋbe si. Mfaanɛɛ ne̱ ane̱ pɛɛɛ a bo̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ ane̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ya fo̱ kideŋbe si.

ref: The local people were very friendly, and they welcomed us by building a fire, because it was rainy and cold.
text: Mfe̱ŋ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ gye̱ ase̱sɛ timaa, ne̱ baa waa ane̱ ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Bware̱ a le̱e̱ ke̱ba‑ɔ, ne̱ afwii a kra ane̱, amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa kure de̱e̱kpa, ne̱ bo̱ yɛ ane̱ bo̱ wo̱re̱.

ref: After Paul had gathered some wood and had put it on the fire, the heat caused a snake to crawl out, and it bit him on the hand.
text: Ne̱ Pɔɔl a kpa gyake̱e̱, ne̱ oo lee ngya‑ɔ ŋko̱ ako̱ ɔ waa de̱e̱kpa‑ɔ‑rɔ. Mò̱ a de ɔ waa‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ de̱e̱kpa‑ɔ kipeŋpeŋ a lee ko̱wɔ kagyo̱saŋ‑ɔ kako̱‑rɔ, ne̱ kaa ba ke̱mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ ke̱e̱te̱ Pɔɔl ke̱sare̱e̱ duŋ.

ref: When the local people saw the snake hanging from Paul's hand, they said to each other, “This man must be a murderer! He didn't drown in the sea, but the goddess of justice will kill him anyway.”
text: Kwii amo̱ se̱ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ a ŋu ko̱wɔ amo̱ Pɔɔl ke̱sare̱e̱ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa le̱e̱ ba tɔwe̱ ba gywii abɛɛ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔnyare̱ mɔ a mɔɔ ase̱sɛ. Hare̱e̱ mò̱ a mo̱ŋ wu nkyu‑o‑ro gbaa ooo, mò̱ e̱bɔye̱ ne̱ ɔɔ waa‑ɔ e̱ mɔɔ mò̱ a e̱ bo̱ ka ko̱kɔ.”

ref: Paul shook the snake off into the fire and wasn't harmed.
text: Amaa Pɔɔl a kpo̱kpaa ke̱mo̱ ne̱e̱ bo̱ waa de̱e̱kpa‑ɔ‑rɔ, na ke̱ mo̱ŋ waa mò̱ sɛye̱.

ref: The people kept thinking that Paul would either swell up or suddenly drop dead. They watched him for a long time, and when nothing happened to him, they changed their minds and said, “This man is a god.”
text: Ŋkee bo̱ gywii ne̱e̱ a Pɔɔl puŋŋe bɛɛɛ ɔ te̱ŋ le̱e̱ da wu. Amaa baa gywii kpo̱ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ ŋu sɛye̱ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ se̱, ne̱ baa kyurowi bamo̱ mfɛɛre̱, ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Ɔ gye̱ kisi ne̱e̱!”

ref: The governor of the island was named Publius, and he owned some of the land around there. Publius was very friendly and welcomed us into his home for three days.
text: Amɔ kwii amo̱ se̱ owure ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ Pubiliyus‑o de mò̱ swe̱e̱re̱ ko̱ me̱raa mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ wo̱re̱ de̱e̱kpa‑ɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ yaa ane̱ mò̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ. Ŋke nsa ne̱ ane̱ a gyi mò̱ aye̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ɔɔ kra ane̱ dame̱naŋsɛ.

ref: His father was in bed, sick with fever and stomach trouble, and Paul went to visit him. Paul healed the man by praying and placing his hands on him.
text: Saŋ amo̱ Pubiliyus mò̱ se̱ e̱ lɔ fekyu, na ɔ kɔɔ mò̱ kame‑ro. Ne̱ Pɔɔl a yɔ ɔnyare̱ amo̱ ase̱ mò̱ ke̱kyaŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱, ne̱ ɔɔ ba mò̱ asare̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ mò̱ se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ kpaare̱.

ref: After this happened, everyone on the island brought their sick people to Paul, and they were all healed.
text: Amo̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ alɔpo̱ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a ba mò̱ ase̱, ne̱ ɔɔ kya bamo̱.

ref: There we found some of the Lord's followers, who begged us to stay with them. A week later we left for the city of Rome.
text: Ne̱ mfe̱ŋ a ase̱sɛ ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu gyi‑o yɛ ane̱ kye̱na bamo̱ ase̱ kadaawɔkywe̱e̱ ko̱ŋko̱. Le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ a kya ane̱ kpa‑ɔ se̱ bo̱rɔ ayaa‑rɔ yɔ Roma a keri‑o.

ref: Some of the followers in Rome heard about us and came to meet us at the Market of Appius and at the Three Inns. When Paul saw them, he thanked God and was encouraged. Paul in Rome
text: Ne̱ Kristoo awuye ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Roma maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ a nu fe̱yɛ ane̱ e̱ ba‑ɔ, ne̱ bamo̱ bo̱ko̱ a gyaŋŋaa ane̱ maŋ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ Apiyus ke̱gya‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ bo̱ko̱ mɔ a gyaŋŋaa ane̱ maŋ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ Afɔ-e̱so̱we̱kpa-e̱sa se̱. Saŋ ne̱ Pɔɔl a ŋu bamo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ sa Wuribware̱ aŋsɛ, ne̱ ŋkee wɔre̱ a nya mò̱. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ Pɔɔl a waa Roma maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ

ref: We arrived in Rome, and Paul was allowed to live in a house by himself with a soldier to guard him.
text: Saŋ ne̱ ane̱ a fo̱ Roma maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa sa Pɔɔl kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ ya kye̱na mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ kpa‑ɔ, amaa baa lee soogyaanyi ko̱ ne̱ ɔ de̱e̱re̱ mò̱ se̱, na ɔ ma nya se̱re̱‑ɔ.

ref: Three days after we got there, Paul called together some of the Jewish leaders and said: My friends, I have never done anything to hurt our people, and I have never gone against the customs of our ancestors. But in Jerusalem I was handed over as a prisoner to the Romans.
text: Amo̱ ŋke nsa kamɛɛ, ne̱ Pɔɔl a gyaŋŋe̱ Yudaa awuye abre̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Roma‑o, na ɔ nya tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ ɔɔ ba‑ɔ gywii bamo̱. Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana Isireelii awuye, mo̱ne̱ a nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa taa mo̱ le̱e̱ Yɛro̱salɛm bo̱ baa mfe̱e̱, na bo̱ gyi mo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ. Mfe̱ŋ a awuye‑o a po̱rɔ mo̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a waa bɔye̱ bo̱ kye ane̱ Isireelii awuye na e̱kpa ne̱ ane̱ ade̱daapo̱ a kaapo̱ ane̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ baa tii mo̱, ne̱ baa taa mo̱ sa Roma agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Yɛro̱salɛm mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ. Amaa mo̱ŋ waa bɔye̱ ko̱ daa.

ref: They looked into the charges against me and wanted to release me. They found that I had not done anything deserving death.
text: Bamo̱ a bise mo̱ ase̱ŋ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ba kpa a bo̱ yɔwe̱ mo̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ mo̱ŋ ŋu bɔye̱ ko̱ ne̱ mo̱ a waa, ne̱ a kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ mɔɔ mo̱‑ɔ. Amaa Yudaa awuye mo̱ŋ sure fe̱yɛ bo̱ yɔwe̱ mo̱ a n nare̱.

ref: I am bound by these chains because of what we people of Israel hope for. This is why I have called you here to talk about this hope of ours.
text: Amo̱se̱ ne̱ ŋ yɛ ŋ ŋu mo̱ne̱, na mo̱ aa mo̱ne̱ a tɔwe̱ ke̱mo̱ ase̱ŋ. Kase̱ŋtiŋ si fe̱raa, ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ baa da mo̱ e̱kpe̱e̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a kɔɔre̱ Kristoo ne̱ ane̱ Isireelii awuye de mò̱ te̱maa fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ i suŋ na ɔ bo̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ ane̱‑ɔ gyi ne̱e̱.”

ref: The leaders replied, “No one from Judea has written us a letter about you. And not one of them has come here to report on you or to say anything against you.
text: Ne̱ baa be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “To, ane̱ mo̱ŋ nya e̱wo̱re̱ ko̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ Yude̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ fo̱ kuŋu si, ne̱ ane̱ se̱sɛ ko̱ mɔ mo̱ŋ le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ bo̱ tɔwe̱ fo̱ kuŋu si ase̱ŋ bɛɛɛ ase̱ŋ bɔye̱ ko̱ gywii ane̱.

ref: But we would like to hear what you have to say. We understand that people everywhere are against this new group.”
text: Amaa ane̱ e̱ kpa a ane̱ ŋu fo̱ mfɛɛre̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ nyi fe̱yɛ to̱ŋ ke̱maa ane̱ ase̱sɛ e̱ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ ba kye Yeesuu a e̱kpa mɔ ne̱ fo̱ gya se̱‑ɔ.”

ref: They agreed on a time to meet with Paul, and many of them came to his house. From early morning until late in the afternoon, Paul talked to them about God's kingdom. He used the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets to try to win them over to Jesus.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ bamo̱ aa Pɔɔl a sa kake. Kake amo̱ a fo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ a ba mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Pɔɔl bo̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ kaapo̱ bamo̱ Wuribware̱ a kuwure‑o si ase̱ŋ le̱e̱ gye̱gyaye̱ ya bo̱ fo̱ kanye. Ne̱ ɔɔ kare̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ to̱ŋ ko̱ gywii bamo̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Mosis mbraa ɔsapo̱‑ɔ na Wuribware̱ akyaamɛɛ ne̱ baa dɛɛ kye̱na‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ Kristoo, ɔmo̱rɔwe̱po̱ ne̱ ɔ ba‑ɔ, ase̱ŋ‑ɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ kaapo̱ bamo̱‑rɔ, na bo̱ nya nu kaase̱ fe̱yɛ Yeesuu amo̱ dɛɛ e̱ gye̱ Kristoo‑o.

ref: Some of the leaders agreed with what Paul said, but others did not.
text: Ɔɔ taare̱ kyurowi bamo̱ bo̱ko̱ mfɛɛre̱, amaa bamo̱ bo̱ko̱ mɔ mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ gyi.

ref: Since they could not agree among themselves, they started leaving. But Paul said, “The Holy Spirit said the right thing when he sent Isaiah the prophet
text: Amaa saŋ ne̱ baa brawe̱‑rɔ ba yɔ, na bamo̱ wo̱re̱ wo̱re̱ i gyiiri ase̱ŋ ne̱ baa nu‑o ikii‑o, ne̱ Pɔɔl a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a sa ne̱ Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ Isaya a tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ne̱ ade̱daapo̱‑ɔ.

ref: to tell our ancestors, ‘Go to these people and tell them: You will listen and listen, but never understand. You will look and look, but never see.
text: Ɔ yɛ, ‘Yɔ ya tɔwe̱ gywii ase̱sɛ amo̱ fe̱yɛ, Mo̱ne̱ ya nu mo̱ ase̱ŋ kpo̱ra‑rɔ iluwi bwe̱e̱tɔ gbaa, mo̱ne̱ maa nu amo̱ kaase̱. Mo̱ne̱ be̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ de̱e̱re̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ na mo̱nꞌ fa amo̱ mfɛɛre̱ kpo̱ne̱, mo̱ne̱ maa nu kaase̱.’

ref: All of you have stubborn hearts. Your ears are stopped up, and your eyes are covered. You cannot see or hear or understand. If you could, you would turn to me, and I would heal you.’ ”
text: Ne̱ Isaya a kya se̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ yɛ amo̱‑ɔ a waa ane̱ŋ, ‘A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ aŋu‑ro bo̱ le̱ŋ, ne̱ baa tii bamo̱ e̱se̱bɔ, ne̱ baa buŋ bamo̱ akatɔ se̱. N gye̱ ane̱ŋ, bamo̱ akatɔ i buŋŋi a bo̱ ŋu atɔ, na bamo̱ e̱se̱bɔ mɔ taye̱ a bo̱ nu ase̱ŋ, na bo̱ nu amo̱ kaase̱, na bo̱ nu bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱, na ŋkee mo̱, Wuribware̱, nya taa bamo̱ e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ ke bamo̱.’ ”

ref: Paul said, “You may be sure that God wants to save the Gentiles! And they will listen.”
text: Mò̱ a sa se̱ŋsa lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ Yudaa awuye‑o a brawe̱‑rɔ, na bamo̱ wo̱re̱ wo̱re̱ i gyiiri ase̱ŋ ne̱ baa nu‑o ikii, ne̱ aa waa le̱ŋ.]

ref: For two years Paul stayed in a rented house and welcomed everyone who came to see him.
text: Ne̱ Pɔɔl a kpa ke̱kyaŋ mfe̱ŋ kye̱na‑rɔ nsu ŋnyɔ, na ɔ ka ke̱mo̱ ko̱kɔ. Ne̱ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ naa mò̱ ase̱ ba ka mò̱ kanɔ mfe̱ŋ, ne̱ mò̱ akatɔ a gyi bamo̱ se̱.

ref: This good news is about his Son, our Lord Jesus Christ! As a human, he was from the family of David. But the Holy Spirit proved that Jesus is the powerful Son of God, because he was raised from death.
text: Amaa kpa ko̱ mɔ se̱‑ɔ, ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ gye̱ Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑o e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a ba Wuribware̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ bo̱ kyiŋŋi mò̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro.

ref: This letter is to all of you in Rome. God loves you and has chosen you to be his very own people. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! A Prayer of Thanks
text: Wuribware̱ e̱ kpa mo̱ne̱ ase̱ŋ, ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ na mo̱nꞌ nya kii mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ase̱sɛ. Mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ se̱ Wuribware̱ na ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo yure mo̱ne̱ na bo̱ sa mo̱ne̱ kaye̱e̱yuri. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Pɔɔl a ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ sa mò̱ kpa na ɔ yɔ Roma nya ya tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ timaa‑o

ref: First, I thank God in the name of Jesus Christ for all of you. I do this because people everywhere in the world are talking about your faith.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ, mo̱ne̱ Roma awuye si, mo̱ e̱ sa Wuribware̱ aŋsɛ Yeesuu Kristoo ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ e̱ tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a kɔɔre̱ Kristoo gyi‑o ase̱ŋ.

ref: God has seen how I never stop praying for you, while I serve him with all my heart and tell the good news about his Son.
text: N de mo̱ kakpo̱nɔ pɛɛɛ mo̱ i suŋ mo̱ e̱ sa Wuribware̱, na mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑o kuŋu si ase̱ŋ timaa. Wuribware̱ gbaa nyi fe̱yɛ a gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ saŋ ke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱‑ɔ, mo̱ i nyiŋŋi mo̱ne̱ se̱.

ref: In all my prayers, I ask God to make it possible for me to visit you.
text: Mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ Wuribware̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ ya sure si, ŋkee fe̱raa ɔ sa mo̱ kpa a ŋ nya taare̱ bo̱ ka mo̱ne̱ kanɔ.

ref: I want to see you and share with you the same blessings that God's Spirit has given me. Then you will grow stronger in your faith.
text: Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ yo̱rɔwe̱ ke̱bo̱ŋu mo̱ne̱, na ŋ nya sa a mo̱nꞌ nya ŋyure ne̱ ŋ ya le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o ase̱‑ɔ ŋko̱. Na a nya sa a mo̱nꞌ nya ke̱yaale̱ŋ mo̱ne̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi‑o‑ro.

ref: What I am saying is that we can encourage each other by the faith that is ours.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi‑o e̱ waa mo̱ne̱ wɔre̱, na mo̱ne̱ mɔ mo̱ne̱ ke̱mo̱‑ɔ mɔ waa mo̱ wɔre̱ ane̱ŋ dɛɛ.

ref: My friends, I want you to know that I have often planned to come for a visit. But something has always kept me from doing it. I want to win followers to Christ in Rome, as I have done in many other places.
text: Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, mo̱ e̱ kpa a mo̱nꞌ ŋu fe̱yɛ mo̱ a kra ke̱bo̱ka mo̱ne̱ kanɔ kyee. Amaa bo̱ fo̱ hare̱e̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ gbaa ko̱tɔko̱ saŋ ki tii mo̱ kpa. Mo̱ e̱ kpa ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ n sa a mo̱ne̱ bo̱ko̱ gbaa kii Kristoo awuye kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ a sa ne̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ gbaa a kii‑o.

ref: It doesn't matter if people are civilized and educated, or if they are uncivilized and uneducated. I must tell the good news to everyone. That's why I am eager to visit all of you in Rome. The Power of the Good News
text: M fe̱raa mo̱ e̱ yo̱rɔwe̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ fe̱yɛ m bo̱ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ timaa‑o gywii mo̱ne̱ gbaa mo̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ te Roma maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ.

ref: I am proud of the good news! It is God's powerful way of saving all people who have faith, whether they are Jews or Gentiles.
text: A mo̱ŋ de mo̱ ipeere fe̱yɛ ŋ ye̱re̱ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ timaa bo̱ le̱e̱ Kristoo kuŋu si, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, mo̱ ya tɔwe̱, ne̱ ase̱sɛ ya kɔɔre̱ Kristoo gyi, Wuribware̱ e̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ bamo̱. Yudaa awuye‑o ya gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ nu amo̱. Ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o a gya se̱.

ref: From heaven God shows how angry he is with all the wicked and evil things that sinful people do to crush the truth.
text: Abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ dɛɛ be̱e̱ a kaapo̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱, mò̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ, duŋ a fwii e̱bɔye̱ awaapo̱ pɛɛɛ na abo̱ro̱kɔɔne̱po̱ pɛɛɛ ne̱ bo̱ de e̱kpa bɔye̱ ba tii bo̱ko̱ ke̱bo̱ŋu kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o kpa‑ɔ se̱.

ref: They know everything that can be known about God, because God has shown it all to them.
text: A mo̱ŋ gye̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi Wuribware̱ a kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o ne̱e̱. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kyɔ lee kamo̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱.

ref: God's eternal power and character cannot be seen. But from the beginning of creation, God has shown what these are like by all he has made. That's why those people don't have any excuse.
text: Ne̱ bo̱ be̱e̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ de ke̱tɔ ne̱ ba ye̱re̱ se̱ na bo̱ bo̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi ane̱ŋ ne̱ o du‑o. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, bamo̱ a mo̱ŋ taare̱ ŋu Wuribware̱ fɔŋfɔŋ gbaa ooo, hare̱e̱ le̱e̱ saŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a twe̱e̱ kaye̱‑ɔ, baa ŋu atɔ pɛɛɛ ne̱ ɔɔ waa, ne̱ a kaapo̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ o du‑o, na mò̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ dabe̱ ne̱ ke̱ bo̱‑rɔ bo̱ yɔ nsu pɛɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: They know about God, but they don't honor him or even thank him. Their thoughts are useless, and their stupid minds are in the dark.
text: Le̱e̱ saŋ‑ɔ, bamo̱ a nyi Wuribware̱‑ɔ gbaa ooo, bo̱ maa bu mò̱ ke̱dabe̱. Ne̱ bo̱ be̱e̱ bo̱ maa sa mò̱ aŋsɛ. Ne̱ ŋkee bamo̱ mfɛɛre̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ waa kyɛɛkyɛɛ, ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ ba taare̱ nu kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o kaase̱.

ref: They claim to be wise, but they are fools.
text: Bo̱ yɛ bo̱ nyi ase̱ŋ ne̱e̱, amaa bamo̱ mɔ bo̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ bo̱ de mfɛɛre̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: They don't worship the glorious and eternal God. Instead, they worship idols that are made to look like humans who cannot live forever, and like birds, animals, and reptiles.
text: Ne̱ baa yɔwe̱ kusuŋ Wuribware̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱‑rɔ nsu pɛɛɛ‑ɔ na mò̱ ke̱dabe̱. Ne̱ ŋkee ba suŋ ate̱tɔmo̱ŋta ne̱ bamo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ a waa, ne̱ a du fe̱yɛ se̱sɛ bɛɛɛ kabo̱gyii bɛɛɛ kabo̱‑ɔ. Amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ gye̱ atɔ wusɛ ne̱e̱.

ref: So God let these people go their own way. They did what they wanted to do, and their filthy thoughts made them do shameful things with their bodies.
text: Bamo̱ a du ane̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ a yɔwe̱ bamo̱ ase̱ŋ sa bamo̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mbe̱yɔmɔ bo̱ gya bamo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ŋyo̱rɔwe̱ bɔye̱ se̱ ne̱e̱, ne̱ ba waa abɛɛ apeerese̱ŋ.

ref: They gave up the truth about God for a lie, and they worshiped God's creation instead of God, who will be praised forever. Amen.
text: Ne̱ baa yɔwe̱ kusuŋ Wuribware̱ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ wuye‑o, ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ kaye̱ba gyi. Kaye̱ba amo̱ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ suŋ atɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a twe̱e̱‑ɔ yɔwe̱ Wuribware̱ ne̱ ɔɔ twe̱e̱ amo̱‑ɔ. Weetee a tiri fe̱yɛ ɔtwe̱e̱po̱‑ɔ nya nkyo̱rɔ ndɔɔ na nsu pɛɛɛ. Amɛye̱.

ref: They are evil, wicked, and greedy, as well as mean in every possible way. They want what others have, and they murder, argue, cheat, and are hard to get along with. They gossip,
text: Ne̱ ba waa bo̱ro̱kɔɔne̱ na e̱bɔye̱ e̱wo̱re̱se̱ e̱wo̱re̱se̱, ne̱ bo̱ de nyase̱pee. Ne̱ ba ka abɛɛ ko̱kwe̱e̱. Ne̱ ba mɔɔ abɛɛ. Ne̱ ba kpa e̱kɔ. Ne̱ bo̱ de aye̱ba na kikisi.

ref: They know God has said that anyone who acts this way deserves to die. But they keep on doing evil things, and they even encourage others to do them.
text: Hare̱e̱ bamo̱ a nyi fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ yɛ bamo̱ ne̱ ba waa ane̱ŋ‑ɔ bware fe̱yɛ bo̱ mɔɔ bamo̱‑ɔ gbaa ooo, bo̱ saŋ bo̱ maa se̱ ba waa e̱bɔye̱ amo̱, na bo̱ ye̱re̱ bo̱ko̱ ne̱ bamo̱ gbaa ba waa ane̱ŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: Some of you accuse others of doing wrong. But there is no excuse for what you do. When you judge others, you condemn yourselves, because you are guilty of doing the very same things.
text: Amo̱ a gye̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, e̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ fo̱ i bu fo̱ bɛɛko̱ ke̱pɔ? Fo̱ ne̱ fo̱ a ba fo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ waa ɔse̱ŋgyipo̱ fo̱ i bu fo̱ bɛɛko̱ ke̱pɔ‑ɔ gbaa a gyi ke̱pɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ atɔ ne̱ fo̱ bɛɛko̱‑ɔ e̱ waa‑ɔ dɛɛ ne̱ fo̱ gbaa fo̱ e̱ waa.

ref: We know that God is right to judge everyone who behaves in this way.
text: Ane̱ nyi fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ fe̱raa, kase̱ŋtiŋ a e̱kpa‑ɔ se̱ ne̱ ɔ gya o gyi bamo̱ ne̱ ba waa ane̱ŋ a atɔ‑ɔ ase̱ŋ nɛɛnɛɛ.

ref: You surely don't think much of God's wonderful goodness or of his patience and willingness to put up with you. Don't you know that the reason God is good to you is because he wants you to turn to him?
text: Bɛɛɛ ŋkɔ fo̱ e̱ fa fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ‑ɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ sɛye̱ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱? Amɔ fo̱ maa bu ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a nyite fo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱? Ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ waa ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ kpa a fo̱ nu fo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ne̱e̱, na fo̱ le̱e̱ fo̱ e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ.

ref: But you are stubborn and refuse to turn to God. So you are making things even worse for yourselves on that day when he will show how angry he is and will judge the world with fairness.
text: Amaa fo̱ i de ke̱se̱bɔrɔle̱ŋ, ne̱ fo̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ kyurowi fo̱ mfɛɛre̱, amo̱ fe̱raa fo̱ nyi fe̱yɛ fo̱ a waa fo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ siraa fo̱ gywii kake ne̱ mò̱ kaduŋfwii‑o e̱ le̱e̱ kawu, na ɔ bo̱ gyi ase̱sɛ ase̱ŋ kase̱ŋtiŋ si, na o gyiiri bamo̱ ke̱se̱bɔ‑ɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: God will reward each of us for what we have done.
text: Kake nsi amo̱‑ɔ, “Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔke̱maa e̱ waa‑ɔ, ke̱mo̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ ba a ɔ bo̱ ka mò̱ ko̱kɔ.”

ref: But he will show how angry and furious he can be with every selfish person who rejects the truth and wants to do evil.
text: Amaa bo̱ko̱ fe̱raa de nyase̱pe̱e̱, ne̱ ba kine ko̱waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ki bware‑o, ne̱ ba waa e̱bɔye̱. Wuribware̱ duŋ i fwii bamo̱ se̱.

ref: All who are wicked will be punished with trouble and suffering. It doesn't matter if they are Jews or Gentiles.
text: Ɔ baa bamo̱ ne̱ ba waa e̱bɔye̱‑ɔ ase̱ŋ na bamo̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ nye̱ra bamo̱. Mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ase̱sɛ Yudaa awuye‑o ne̱ o gyiiri ke̱se̱bɔ pwɛɛ na ɔ dɛɛ gyiiri bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o lee.

ref: But all who do right will be rewarded with glory, honor, and peace, whether they are Jews or Gentiles.
text: Amaa ɔ sa ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ waa itimaa‑o ke̱dabe̱ na ke̱nyare̱ na kaye̱e̱yuri. Yudaa awuye‑o ne̱ ɔ sa pwɛɛ na ɔ dɛɛ sa bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o.

ref: By reading the Scriptures you learn how God wants you to behave, and you discover what is right.
text: Fo̱ nyi ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ pre̱ Wuribware̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱ mbraa‑ɔ e̱ kaapo̱ fo̱ ne̱ fo̱ i lee ke̱tɔ ne̱ ki bware‑o.

ref: You are sure that you are a guide for the blind and a light for all who are in the dark.
text: Fo̱ yɛ fo̱ gye̱ te̱napo̱ kayii‑ro krapo̱ ne̱e̱, ne̱ fo̱ gye̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ te kibugyii‑ro‑o fetiri.

ref: And since there is knowledge and truth in God's Law, you think you can instruct fools and teach young people.
text: Ne̱ fo̱ yɛ fo̱ gye̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi sɛye̱‑ɔ ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱, ne̱ fo̱ gye̱ ŋyaagyi ɔkaapo̱po̱, ne̱ fo̱ yɛ kanyiase̱ŋ na kase̱ŋtiŋ bo̱ mo̱ne̱ mbraa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a nya le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: But how can you teach others when you refuse to learn? You preach it is wrong to steal. But do you steal?
text: To, fo̱ a yɛ fo̱ nyi ke̱mo̱ ne̱ ki bware, ne̱ fo̱ gye̱ bo̱ko̱ ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ na ɔkaapo̱po̱‑ɔ, ntɔ se̱ ne̱ fo̱ maa kaapo̱ fo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ atɔ timaa? Fo̱ a yɛ bo̱ko̱ ma kaŋ ywii‑o, fo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ‑ɔ fe̱raa maa ywii aaa?

ref: You say people should be faithful in marriage. But are you faithful? You hate idols, yet you rob their temples.
text: Fo̱ a yɛ ɔnyare̱ ma waa kakye̱e̱kpa, amaa fo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ‑ɔ fe̱raa maa waa kakye̱e̱kpa aaa? Fo̱ i kisi ikisi kusuŋ, amaa fo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ mo̱ŋ naa fo̱ i ywii fo̱ e̱ le̱e̱ ikisi‑ro aaa?

ref: You take pride in the Law, but you disobey the Law and bring shame to God.
text: Fo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ fo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mbraa‑ɔ gye̱ fo̱ lee ne̱e̱. Amaa fo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ fo̱ ya fo̱ mbraa‑ɔ, a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ maa bu Wuribware̱ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱?

ref: It is just as the Scriptures tell us, “You have made foreigners say insulting things about God.”
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, “Bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ gya Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, nde̱ se̱ awuye e̱ tɔwe̱ abuse̱ŋse̱ŋ ba kye mo̱ ke̱nyare̱‑ɔ.”

ref: Being circumcised is worthwhile, if you obey the Law. But if you don't obey the Law, you are no better off than people who are not circumcised.
text: Fo̱ gye̱ Yudaanyi, fo̱ ya gya Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ se̱, fo̱ ko̱twe̱e̱tu ke̱te̱ŋ de fo̱ to̱nɔ. Amaa bamo̱ ya te̱ŋ fo̱ ko̱twe̱e̱tu, ne̱ fo̱ ya fo̱ mbraa‑ɔ, amo̱ fe̱raa fo̱ ko̱twe̱e̱tu ke̱te̱ŋ‑ɔ mo̱ŋ de to̱nɔ bo̱ sa fo̱.

ref: So everyone who obeys the Law, but has never been circumcised, will condemn you. Even though you are circumcised and have the Law, you still don't obey its teachings.
text: Na bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ te̱ŋŋe̱ bamo̱ atwe̱e̱tu, ne̱ bo̱ gya mò̱ mbraa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ taare̱ ko̱so̱ ye̱re̱ na bo̱ po̱rɔ fo̱ Yudaanyi ne̱ Wuribware̱ a sa mò̱ mbraa‑ɔ, ne̱ baa te̱ŋ fo̱ ko̱twe̱e̱tu‑o ne̱ fo̱ mo̱ŋ gya mbraa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ.

ref: What good is it to be a Jew? What good is it to be circumcised?
text: Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ ane̱ŋ fe̱raa, nte̱tɔ ke̱tɔ timaa ne̱ Yudaa awuye de bo̱ kyo̱ŋ ase̱sɛ laŋgyaŋ? Bamo̱ atwe̱e̱tu ke̱te̱ŋŋe̱‑ɔ de bamo̱ to̱nɔ aaa?

ref: It is good in a lot of ways! First of all, God's messages were spoken to the Jews.
text: Kase̱ŋtiŋ, Yudaa awuye‑o a nya ŋyure kyo̱ŋ se̱sɛ ke̱maa. Ke̱tɔ gye̱ŋkpɛɛsɛ ne̱ baa nya‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a sa ne̱ baa ŋu mò̱ mfɛɛre̱.

ref: It is true that some of them did not believe the message. But does this mean that God cannot be trusted, just because they did not have faith?
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, amɔ Yudaa awuye‑o bo̱ko̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gya mò̱ kanɔ‑ɔ se̱ aaa? Kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱raa, bo̱ko̱ bo̱‑rɔ. Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, amo̱‑ɔ a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ maa lɛɛ waa ke̱tɔ timaa ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ ɔ waa a ɔ sa bamo̱‑ɔ ɔɔɔ?

ref: No, indeed! God tells the truth, even if everyone else is a liar. The Scriptures say about God, “Your words will be proven true, and in court you will win your case.”
text: Daabii daa! Hare̱e̱ se̱sɛ ke̱maa e̱ gye̱ ɔye̱bapo̱ gbaa, Wuribware̱ fe̱raa de kase̱ŋtiŋ saŋ ke̱maa. Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Deefid a kyo̱rɛɛ Wuribware̱ kuŋu si ase̱ŋ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, “Amo̱se̱‑ɔ fo̱ ya tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a waa bɔye̱, fo̱ de kase̱ŋtiŋ. Ne̱ fo̱ ya tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a gyi ke̱pɔ, mo̱ a nu kaase̱.”

ref: If our evil deeds show how right God is, then what can we say? Is it wrong for God to become angry and punish us? What a foolish thing to ask.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ, nsaŋse̱ ɔko̱ i gyiiri Wuribware̱ ikii fe̱yɛ ane̱ dimaadi bo̱ro̱kɔɔne̱ e̱ kaapo̱ dame̱naŋsɛ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ kpeŋ e̱ gye̱ otimaa, ne̱ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ gye̱ e̱bɔye̱ awaapo̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱. Amo̱ fe̱raa, ɔ yɛ, Wuribware̱ ya kperi bamo̱ e̱bɔye̱ se̱ gyiiri bamo̱ ke̱se̱bɔ, ɔ mo̱ŋ waa nɛɛnɛɛ.

ref: You might as well say, “Let's do something evil, so that something good will come of it!” Some people even claim that we are saying this. But God is fair and will judge them as well. No One Is Good
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ e̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ m ma waa bɔye̱, na ase̱sɛ nya ŋu fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ gye̱ otimaa?” Ase̱sɛ ko̱ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ baa saare̱ mo̱ po̱rɔ mo̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ a ase̱ŋ mɔ. Ba ba aye̱ba ne̱e̱. A bware fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ gyiiri ane̱ŋ a ase̱sɛ amo̱ ke̱se̱bɔ. Se̱sɛ ke̱maa mo̱ŋ gye̱ otimaa

ref: What does all this mean? Does it mean that we Jews are better off than the Gentiles? No, it doesn't! Jews, as well as Gentiles, are ruled by sin, just as I have said.
text: To, amɔ ŋ yɛ Yudaa awuye‑o ase̱ŋ bɔ bo̱ko̱ lee ne̱e̱ bɛɛɛ? Daabii! N gye̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱e̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a kyɔ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ e̱bɔye̱ de Yudaa awuye‑o fɔŋfɔŋ na bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o si ke̱yaale̱ŋ‑ɔ.

ref: The Scriptures tell us, “No one is acceptable to God!
text: Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, “Ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ɔ waa e̱kpe̱yaa, se̱sɛ ko̱ŋko̱ gbaa mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ.

ref: Not one of them understands or even searches for God.
text: Ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ o nu ase̱ŋ kaase̱. Ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ o buwi ɔ kpa Wuribware̱.

ref: They have all turned away and are worthless. There isn't one person who does right.
text: Bamo̱ pɛɛɛ mo̱ŋ de kase̱ŋtiŋ, bo̱ro̱kɔɔne̱ na ase̱ŋ bɔye̱ dooo ne̱ bo̱ gya se̱. Ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ɔ waa e̱kpe̱yaa, se̱sɛ ko̱ŋko̱ gbaa mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ.”

ref: Their words are like an open pit, and their tongues are good only for telling lies. Each word is as deadly as the fangs of a snake,
text: “Bamo̱ ase̱ŋ tɔwe̱sɛ ke̱maa gye̱ kanɔte̱ŋ ne̱ ka mɔɔ‑ɔ. Bamo̱ nnɔ‑rɔ ase̱ŋ ne̱ bo̱ de ba pe̱nna bo̱ko̱.” “Bamo̱ nnɔ bo̱ ya ne̱e̱ fɛɛ, n du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ko̱wɔ abwanɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ mmo̱ ase̱ŋ tɔwe̱sɛ e̱ mɔɔ, fe̱yɛ ko̱wɔ ke̱bwɛɛ‑ɔ.”

ref: and they say nothing but bitter curses.
text: “Bamo̱ nnɔ e̱ lo̱ŋ ase̱sɛ, ne̱ bo̱ maa sa se̱ŋsa kpe̱yaa, amɔ e̱bɔye̱.”

ref: These people quickly become violent.
text: “Bo̱ bo̱ saa na ase̱sɛ ne̱ baa gyi ke̱be̱ŋ‑ɔ ke̱mɔɔ.

ref: Wherever they go, they leave ruin and destruction.
text: Bamo̱ e̱nare̱kpa pɛɛɛ‑rɔ tɔnye̱ra dooo ne̱e̱.

ref: They don't know how to live in peace.
text: Bo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi kaye̱e̱yuri a e̱kpa‑ɔ.”

ref: We know that everything in the Law was written for those who are under its power. The Law says these things to stop anyone from making excuses and to let God show that the whole world is guilty.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ, ane̱ a ŋu ase̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ. M po̱rɔ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ yɛ bo̱ gya mmo̱ se̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ baa waa e̱bɔye̱, ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ de kanɔ yaye̱sɛ. Kase̱ŋtiŋ si, kaye̱‑rɔ pɛɛɛ ɔko̱ maa taare̱ a o lee Wuribware̱ kanɔ fe̱yɛ ɔ gya mò̱ mbraa‑ɔ se̱ nɛɛnɛɛ.

ref: God doesn't accept people simply because they obey the Law. No, indeed! All the Law does is to point out our sin. God's Way of Accepting People
text: Ɔko̱ ya tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ gya Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ ɔ gye̱ se̱sɛ timaa, amɔ ɔ ba aye̱ba ne̱e̱. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mbraa‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ n sa ne̱ se̱sɛ dimaadi i ŋu fe̱yɛ ɔ waa bɔye̱ saŋ ke̱maa. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ sa a Wuribware̱ te̱e̱ ane̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ timaa‑o

ref: Now we see how God does make us acceptable to him. The Law and the Prophets tell how we become acceptable, and it isn't by obeying the Law of Moses.
text: Amaa ŋkee fe̱raa Wuribware̱ a lee ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ waa ase̱sɛ na bo̱ kii ase̱sɛ timaa mò̱ ase̱‑ɔ bo̱ kaapo̱ ane̱. Hare̱e̱ abware̱se̱ŋ a wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ, mò̱ mbraa‑ɔ na akyaamɛɛ ne̱ baa dɛɛ kye̱na‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ amo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ gbaa ooo, n gye̱ mò̱ mbraa‑ɔ ne̱ o de ɔ waa amo̱‑ɔ.

ref: God sent Christ to be our sacrifice. Christ offered his life's blood, so by faith in him we could come to God. And God did this to show that in the past he was right to be patient and forgive sinners. This also shows that God is right when he accepts people who have faith in Jesus.
text: Kristoo Yeesuu mɔ dɛɛ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a lee sa ane̱, ne̱ Kristoo a ba mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ mbo̱gya bo̱ lɔŋŋɔ se̱sɛ na Wuribware̱ ke̱bo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ. Se̱sɛ ya kɔɔre̱ amo̱‑ɔ gyi, a waa kusuŋ timaa a a bo̱ sa mò̱. Wuribware̱ a waa amo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ o gyi ase̱ŋ kpa se̱ ne̱e̱. De̱daa de̱daa‑ɔ ne̱ se̱sɛ a le̱e̱ e̱bɔye̱ ko̱waa, ne̱ oo nyite mò̱, ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ fe̱raa ɔ yɛ bamo̱ ne̱ ba kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu ba gyi‑o gye̱ ase̱sɛ timaa‑o.

ref: Do we destroy the Law by our faith? Not at all! We make it even more powerful.
text: Amɔ Yeesuu ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi mɔ yɛ ane̱ ma lɛɛ gya Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ se̱ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱? Daabii! Ane̱ saŋ ane̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ mbraa‑ɔ bware.

ref: Well then, what can we say about our ancestor Abraham?
text: To, mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ de̱e̱re̱ Abraham ne̱ ɔ gye̱ Yudaa awuye ke̱nana‑rɔ ɔbre̱sɛ‑ɔ kuŋu si ase̱ŋ.

ref: If he became acceptable to God because of what he did, then he would have something to brag about. But he would never be able to brag about it to God.
text: Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ se̱sɛ e̱ taare̱ a ɔ kaapo̱ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ mò̱ asuŋ timaa si, amo̱ fe̱raa Abraham a dɛɛ nya ko̱tɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ ye̱re̱ se̱ na ɔ bo̱ kaapo̱ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱‑ɔ. Amaa Abraham mo̱ŋ nya ane̱ŋ Wuribware̱ akatɔ‑rɔ.

ref: Money paid to workers isn't a gift. It is something they earn by working.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ, se̱sɛ ya waa kusuŋ, ne̱ mò̱ ya nya atanne̱, a mo̱ŋ gye̱ fe̱yɛ baa ke mò̱ ne̱e̱. A gye̱ mò̱ kusuŋ‑o kakɔka ne̱e̱.

ref: In the Scriptures David talks about the blessings that come to people who are acceptable to God, even though they don't do anything to deserve these blessings. David says,
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ane̱ ɔde̱daapo̱ Owure Deefid gbaa a kyo̱rɛɛ fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa ne̱ mò̱ ya kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ gyi, Wuribware̱ e̱ te̱e̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ se̱sɛ timaa na O‑de‑ŋyure, na se̱sɛ‑ɔ mo̱ŋ taare̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ ɔɔ waa kusuŋ timaa ke̱maa bo̱ nya ane̱ŋ a ke̱nyare̱‑ɔ. Ɔ yɛ,

ref: “What a blessing when God forgives our sins and our evil deeds. What a blessing when the Lord erases our sins from his book.”
text: A waa kɔne̱ a bo̱ sa se̱sɛ ne̱ Wuribware̱ maa bise mò̱ e̱bɔye̱ se̱ nyaŋe̱ ko̱‑ɔ.”

ref: But when did this happen? Was it before or after Abraham was circumcised? Of course, it was before.
text: Abraham a te̱ŋ mò̱ ko̱twe̱e̱tu‑o kamɛɛ, ne̱ Wuribware̱ yɛ ɔ gye̱ se̱sɛ timaa, bɛɛɛ mò̱ a mo̱ŋ tɛɛ te̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱? Mò̱ a mo̱ŋ tɛɛ te̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱e̱. Wuribware̱ yɛ ɔ gye̱ se̱sɛ timaa pwɛɛ.

ref: Abraham let himself be circumcised to show he had been accepted because of his faith even before he was circumcised. This makes Abraham the father of all who are acceptable to God because of their faith, even though they are not circumcised.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ te̱ŋ mò̱ ko̱twe̱e̱tu bo̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ ɔɔ kyɔ kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ gyi. Wuribware̱ a waa amo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱ na Abraham nya kii bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ te̱ŋŋe̱ atwe̱e̱tu‑o gbaa bamo̱ ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱. Hare̱e̱ bamo̱ a mo̱ŋ te̱ŋŋe̱ bamo̱ atwe̱e̱tu‑o gbaa ooo, ba kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ ba gyi, amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ te̱e̱ bamo̱ ɛ ase̱sɛ timaa.

ref: This also makes Abraham the father of everyone who is circumcised and has faith in God, as Abraham did before he was circumcised. The Promise Is for All Who Have Faith
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ bo̱ko̱ ne̱ baa te̱ŋŋe̱ bamo̱ atwe̱e̱tu‑o ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱. Bamo̱ ne̱ ba ko̱te̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Abraham a gya Wuribware̱ se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ kɔɔre̱ gyi pwɛɛ ne̱ ɔɔ dɛɛ te̱ŋ mò̱ ko̱twe̱e̱tu‑o gbaa e̱ taare̱ a bo̱ te̱e̱ Abraham fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱.

ref: God promised Abraham and his descendants that he would give them the world. This promise wasn't made because Abraham had obeyed a law, but because his faith in God made him acceptable.
text: N gye̱ Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ ne̱ Abraham a gya se̱‑ɔ se̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ ɔ taa kaye̱ pɛɛɛ a ɔ bo̱ sa mò̱ aa mò̱ ananagyi. Amaa aa le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ gyi‑o si. Mfaanɛɛ se̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ gye̱ se̱sɛ timaa.

ref: If Abraham and his descendants were given this promise because they had obeyed a law, then faith would mean nothing, and the promise would be worthless.
text: Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ gya mò̱ mbraa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ taa kaye̱ a ɔ sa, amo̱ fe̱raa ane̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ gyi‑o mo̱ŋ de sɛye̱ bo̱ sa ane̱. Ne̱ a be̱e̱ a kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kyurowi ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔ yɛ ɔ sa Abraham‑ɔ.

ref: God becomes angry when his Law is broken. But where there isn't a law, it cannot be broken.
text: Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱tɔ ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ i gyiiri e̱bɔye̱ awaapo̱ ke̱se̱bɔ. Amaa mbraa e̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ, a kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ mɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ mbraa okyepo̱.

ref: The Scriptures say that Abraham would become the ancestor of many nations. This promise was made to Abraham because he had faith in God, who raises the dead to life and creates new things.
text: Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, “Wuribware̱ yɛ, Mo̱ e̱ sa na fo̱ kii e̱swe̱e̱re̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ se̱ awuye bamo̱ se̱.” Wuribware̱ ase̱ fe̱raa, Abraham e̱ gye̱ ane̱ se̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔɔ kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ gyi, Wuribware̱ ne̱ ɔ sa bamo̱ ne̱ ba wu‑o ŋkpa‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔ tɔwe̱ atɔ ne̱ a kaa a waa‑ɔ fe̱yɛ amo̱ a kyɔ waa‑ɔ.

ref: God gave Jesus to die for our sins, and he raised him to life, so that we would be made acceptable to God.
text: Wuribware̱ a sa kpa fe̱yɛ bo̱ mɔɔ mò̱ na ɔ kpɛɛ ane̱ e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ twe̱e̱. Ne̱ oo kyiŋŋi mò̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro na ɔ nya te̱e̱ ane̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ timaa.

ref: By faith we have been made acceptable to God. And now, thanks to our Lord Jesus Christ, we have peace with God.
text: Ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Kristoo ane̱ i gyi‑o si ne̱ Wuribware̱ a te̱e̱ ane̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ timaa. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ a baa kaye̱e̱yuri ane̱ aa Wuribware̱ ke̱bo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ.

ref: Christ has also introduced us to God's gift of undeserved grace on which we now take our stand. So we are happy, as we look forward to sharing in the glory of God.
text: Ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ a kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu Kristoo gyi‑o si, ne̱ ane̱ a bo̱ ŋu ŋyure mɔ ne̱ ɔɔ sa ane̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ ane̱ akatɔ a gyi ane̱ e̱ de̱e̱re̱ akatɔ‑rɔ ane̱ gywii saŋ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ nya ke̱dabe̱ na wuraa mò̱ ase̱‑ɔ.

ref: that will never disappoint us. All of this happens because God has given us the Holy Spirit, who fills our hearts with his love.
text: Ane̱ i de te̱maa fe̱raa, ipeere maa gyi ane̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ a kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa ne̱ ɔɔ sa ane̱‑ɔ a sa ne̱ ane̱ a ŋu ke̱kpa de̱maŋte̱ ne̱ ɔ kpa ane̱‑ɔ.

ref: No one is really willing to die for an honest person, though someone might be willing to die for a truly good person.
text: Ɔko̱ kuwu sa se̱sɛ timaa bo̱ le̱ŋ fɛɛ. Amaa nsaŋse̱ ɔko̱ fe̱raa e̱ taare̱ a o wu sa ane̱ŋ a se̱sɛ‑ɔ.

ref: But God showed how much he loved us by having Christ die for us, even though we were sinful.
text: Amaa Wuribware̱ a kaapo̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ kpa ane̱ ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔɔ sa ne̱ Yeesuu a bo̱ wu sa ane̱ saŋ ne̱ ane̱ gye̱ abɔye̱waapo̱‑ɔ.

ref: But there is more! Now that God has accepted us because Christ sacrificed his life's blood, we will also be kept safe from God's anger.
text: Yeesuu a wu, ne̱ mò̱ mbo̱gya a we̱e̱ swe̱e̱re̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ Wuribware̱ a te̱e̱ ane̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ timaa. Yeesuu a waa amo̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, amɔ kukuŋ fe̱raa ne̱ ɔ maa taare̱ a o kuŋ ane̱ na Wuribware̱ duŋ maa fwii ane̱ se̱ aaa?

ref: Even when we were God's enemies, he made peace with us, because his Son died for us. Yet something even greater than friendship is ours. Now that we are at peace with God, we will be saved by the life of his Son.
text: Saŋ‑ɔ ane̱ aa Wuribware̱ ke̱bo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ a te̱ŋ‑nɔ. Amaa Wuribware̱ fɔŋfɔŋ a sa ne̱ mò̱ gyi Kristoo lowi ne̱ oo wu‑o a lɔŋŋɔ ane̱ aa Wuribware̱ ke̱bo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ. Mbe̱yɔmɔ ane̱ aa Wuribware̱ a be̱e̱ waa kanɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, Kristoo kakye̱na ne̱ o te‑o e̱ sa ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ ane̱ ɔ lo̱we̱.

ref: And in addition to everything else, we are happy because God sent our Lord Jesus Christ to make peace with us. Adam and Christ
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ be̱e̱ ki tii si‑o e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo a sa ne̱ ane̱ akatɔ a gyi Wuribware̱ ase̱, bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ lɔŋŋɔ ane̱ aa Wuribware̱ ke̱bo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Adam kwe̱e̱, ne̱ Yeesuu Kristoo mɔ kwe̱e̱‑ɔ

ref: Sin was in the world before the Law came. But no record of sin was kept, because there was no Law.
text: Ase̱sɛ e̱ waa e̱bɔye̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ pwɛɛ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a dɛɛ sa Yudaa awuye mò̱ mbraa‑ɔ. Amaa pwɛɛ ne̱ baa dɛɛ nya mbraa amo̱‑ɔ, ɔko̱ maa taare̱ a ɔ tɔwe̱ saŋ amo̱ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ fe̱raa gye̱ mbraa okyepo̱.

ref: But the gift of God's undeserved grace was very different from Adam's sin. That one sin brought death to many others. Yet in an even greater way, Jesus Christ alone brought God's gift of undeserved grace to many people.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ, Wuribware̱ ke̱te̱e̱ ane̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ timaa‑o e̱ waa atɔ dabe̱ a kyɔ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Adam a bɔye̱‑ɔ a sa ne̱ kaa ba‑ɔ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ se̱sɛ ko̱ŋko̱ a waa bɔye̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ a wu. Amaa ane̱ŋ ne̱ se̱sɛ ko̱ŋko̱, Yeesuu Kristoo, a wu‑o si‑o, ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ a nya ŋyure. Wuribware̱ a ŋu ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ e̱wɛɛ, ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ bamo̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ timaa.

ref: There is a lot of difference between Adam's sin and God's gift. That one sin led to punishment. But God's gift made it possible for us to be acceptable to him, even though we have sinned many times.
text: Mo̱nꞌ be̱e̱ ŋu fe̱yɛ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Adam a waa‑ɔ na ke̱tɔ ne̱ Kristoo a waa‑ɔ kwe̱e̱. Se̱sɛ ko̱ŋko̱ a waa bɔye̱ luwi ko̱ŋko̱, ne̱ Wuribware̱ a bu mò̱ ke̱pɔ. Amaa mbe̱yɔmɔ fe̱raa, hare̱e̱ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ a waa bɔye̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ gbaa ooo, Wuribware̱ a sa bamo̱ ke̱be̱ŋ.

ref: Death ruled like a king because Adam had sinned. But that cannot compare with what Jesus Christ has done. God has treated us with undeserved grace, and he has accepted us because of Jesus. And so we will live and rule like kings.
text: Bo̱ le̱e̱ Adam a bɔye̱ ko̱ŋko̱ mɔ se̱ be̱e̱, lowi a nya se̱sɛ se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ. Amaa bo̱ le̱e̱ lowi ne̱ se̱sɛ ko̱ŋko̱ amo̱ Kristoo a wu‑o si, bamo̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a ŋu bamo̱ e̱wɛɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ, ne̱ ɔ yɛ bo̱ gye̱ ase̱sɛ timaa‑o, pɛɛɛ i gyi kuwure ke̱baŋsɛ bamo̱ kakye̱na po̱pwɛɛ‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: Adam disobeyed God and caused many others to be sinners. But Jesus obeyed him and will make many people acceptable to God.
text: Se̱sɛ ko̱ŋko̱ ke̱‑mo̱ŋ‑bu Wuribware̱ a sa ne̱ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ a kii abɔye̱waapo̱‑ɔ, amaa se̱sɛ ko̱ŋko̱ mɔ kibu Wuribware̱ a sa ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ a kii ase̱sɛ timaa Wuribware̱ ase̱.

ref: The Law came, so that the full power of sin could be seen. Yet where sin was powerful, God's gift of undeserved grace was even more powerful.
text: Ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a sa mò̱ ase̱sɛ mò̱ mbraa‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ kpa ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ nya ŋu ane̱ŋ ne̱ bamo̱ e̱bɔye̱ kyɔ‑ɔ. Amaa bamo̱ ne̱ baa ŋu ane̱ŋ ne̱ bamo̱ e̱bɔye̱ kyɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ, ne̱ baa nu bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ i ŋu bamo̱ e̱wɛɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: Sin ruled by means of death. But God's gift of grace now rules, and God has accepted us because of Jesus Christ our Lord. This means that we will have eternal life.
text: To, ŋkee fe̱raa ane̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ e̱bɔye̱ a nya ke̱yaale̱ŋ ase̱sɛ se̱‑ɔ se̱ ne̱ ba wu. Amaa bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a ŋu ase̱sɛ e̱wɛɛ‑ɔ se̱ mɔ‑ɔ, ɔ te̱e̱ bamo̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ timaa. Ne̱ ɔ bɔ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo si ɔ sa ane̱ ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: What should we say? Should we keep on sinning, so that God's gift of undeserved grace will show up even better?
text: To, amɔ ŋ yɛ ane̱ be̱e̱ kya se̱ waa bɔye̱ ne̱e̱, na Wuribware̱ de̱e̱ ŋu ane̱ e̱wɛɛ ane̱ŋ bo̱ yɔ aaa?

ref: Don't you know that all who share in Christ Jesus by being baptized also share in his death?
text: Mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ya bɔ ane̱ asuu, ne̱ ane̱ ya tii Kristoo a ko̱ko̱we̱bɛɛ‑ɔ se̱, amɔ ane̱ aa mò̱ a kii ase̱sɛ ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱?

ref: When we were baptized, we died and were buried with Christ. We were baptized, so we would live a new life, as Christ was raised to life by the glory of God the Father.
text: Bamo̱ ya bɔ ane̱ asuu, a du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ a wu, ne̱ baa pure ane̱‑ɔ, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Kristoo a wu, ne̱ baa pure mò̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ a be̱e̱ a du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ a kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro, ne̱ ane̱ te kakye̱na po̱pwɛɛ‑ɔ, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ se̱ Wuribware̱ a ba mò̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ na ke̱dabe̱ bo̱ kyiŋŋi Kristoo le̱e̱ lowi‑ro‑o.

ref: If we shared in Jesus' death by being baptized, we will be raised to life with him.
text: Ane̱ aa mò̱ a tɛɛ kii ase̱sɛ ko̱ŋko̱ mò̱ lowi‑ro‑o si‑o, ane̱ aa mò̱ i kii ase̱sɛ ko̱ŋko̱ mò̱ kuwu kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro‑o‑ro.

ref: We know that the persons we used to be were nailed to the cross with Jesus. This was done, so our sinful bodies would no longer be the slaves of sin.
text: Ane̱ be̱e̱ ane̱ nyi fe̱yɛ a du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ a da ane̱ bo̱ me̱ra Kristoo a kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱‑ɔ na bo̱ nya kpɛɛ ane̱ kuŋu si e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ twe̱e̱, na ane̱ nya lee ane̱ e̱ye̱e̱ le̱e̱ e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ.

ref: We know that death no longer has any power over Christ. He died and was raised to life, never again to die.
text: Ane̱ nyi fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a tɛɛ kyiŋŋi Kristoo le̱e̱ lowi‑ro‑o si‑o, Kristoo maa lɛɛ wu. Lowi mo̱ŋ lɛɛ o de mò̱ se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ.

ref: When Christ died, he died for sin once and for all. But now he is alive, and he lives only for God.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, Kristoo a wu‑o si‑o, ɔɔ kɔ bɔye̱ gyi si lo̱we̱. Amaa mbe̱yɔmɔ mò̱ a kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro‑o, ɔɔ nya mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ na o suŋ sa Wuribware̱ so̱so̱.

ref: Don't let sin rule your body. After all, your body is bound to die, so don't obey its desires
text: Mo̱ne̱ ma sa a e̱bɔye̱ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ne̱ aŋu, na a sa a mo̱nꞌ gya atɔ bɔye̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ se̱.

ref: or let any part of it become a slave of evil. Give yourselves to God, as people who have been raised from death to life. Make every part of your body a slave that pleases God.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ma ba mo̱ne̱ ŋyo̱wɔre̱ to̱ŋ ke̱maa bo̱ waa e̱bɔye̱. Mo̱ne̱ a tɛɛ mo̱ne̱ du fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro ba ŋkpa‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ taa mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ sa Wuribware̱, na mo̱nꞌ ba mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ suŋ asuŋ timaa sa mò̱.

ref: Don't let sin keep ruling your lives. You are ruled by God's undeserved grace and not by the Law. Slaves Who Do What Pleases God
text: Bɔye̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ ɔ gye̱ mo̱ne̱ nyaŋpe̱, mbraa‑ɔ mɔ mo̱ŋ de mo̱ne̱ e̱le̱ŋ. Amɔ Wuribware̱ a ŋyure‑o e̱ gye̱ ne̱ n de mo̱ne̱ se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ. Mbe̱yɔmɔ ane̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ anya ne̱e̱

ref: What does all this mean? Does it mean we are free to sin, because we are ruled by God's gift of undeserved grace and not by the Law? Certainly not!
text: To, amɔ mo̱ne̱ e̱ fa fe̱yɛ ŋ yɛ ane̱ de kpa fe̱yɛ ane̱ waa e̱bɔye̱, bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ mo̱ŋ de ane̱ se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ, ne̱ Wuribware̱ a ŋyure‑o e̱ gye̱ ne̱ n de ane̱ se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ‑ɔ ɔɔɔ? Daabii daa!

ref: You used to be slaves of sin. But I thank God that with all your heart you followed the example set forth in the teaching you received.
text: Amaa mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ sa Wuribware̱ aŋsɛ fe̱yɛ, hare̱e̱ saŋ ko̱ mo̱ne̱ a dɛɛ mo̱ne̱ du fe̱yɛ bɔye̱ anya a gye̱ mo̱ne̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a suŋ sa bɔye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ nyaŋpe̱‑ɔ gbaa ooo, mbe̱yɔmɔ fe̱raa mo̱ne̱ a nu abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ le̱e̱ bo̱ko̱ ase̱, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a lee mo̱ne̱ mme‑ro gya amo̱ se̱.

ref: Now you are set free from sin and are slaves who please God.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ a kɔɔre̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ le̱e̱ e̱bɔye̱ ase̱. Ne̱ ŋkee fe̱raa mo̱ne̱ e̱ waa itimaa, ba e̱mo̱ bo̱ kii mo̱ne̱ nyaŋpe̱ po̱pwɛɛ.

ref: I am using these everyday examples, because in some ways you are still weak. You used to let the different parts of your body be slaves of your evil thoughts. But now you must make every part of your body serve God, so that you will belong completely to him.
text: Ane̱ kake ke̱maa kakye̱na‑rɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ de̱e̱re̱ n de mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, mo̱ ya tɔwe̱ amo̱ abre̱sɛ‑rɔ, mo̱ne̱ maa taare̱ a mo̱nꞌ nu kaase̱. Saŋ‑ɔ mo̱ne̱ a ba mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ waa atɔ ne̱ aa sa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ a waa iyisi‑o, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ bo̱ro̱kɔɔne̱ a waa ko̱kyɔ. Amaa ŋkee fe̱raa mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱ waa itimaa na mo̱ne̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ nya fwiiri waa pare̱pare̱.

ref: When you were slaves of sin, you didn't have to please God.
text: Saŋ ne̱ bɔye̱ gye̱ mo̱ne̱ nyaŋpe̱‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ gya e̱kpa timaa si.

ref: But what good did you receive from the things you did? All you have to show for them is your shame, and they lead to death.
text: Nte̱tɔ to̱nɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a nya le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ a atɔ amo̱, ne̱ ŋkee fe̱raa amo̱ ko̱waa de mo̱ne̱ ipeere‑o ne̱e̱? Weetee ane̱ŋ a atɔ mɔ e̱ sa a Wuribware̱ gya mo̱ne̱ le̱e̱ mò̱ ase̱ nsu pɛɛɛ, na mo̱nꞌ yɔ lowi‑ro.

ref: Now you have been set free from sin, and you are God's slaves. This will make you holy and will lead you to eternal life.
text: Amaa mbe̱yɔmɔ mo̱ne̱ a tɛɛ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ le̱e̱ bɔye̱ ase̱, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a kii Wuribware̱ anya‑ɔ fe̱raa, ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ nya‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ e̱ sa a mo̱ne̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ fwiiri na mo̱nꞌ nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: My friends, you surely understand enough about law to know that laws only have power over people who are alive.
text: Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ nyi mbraa‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ i nyiŋŋi si fe̱yɛ se̱sɛ e̱ saŋ o te ŋkpa‑rɔ wo̱re̱ ne̱ mbraa‑ɔ de mò̱ se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ aaa?

ref: For example, the Law says that a man's wife must remain his wife as long as he lives. But once her husband is dead, she is free
text: Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ taa ɔkye̱e̱ waare̱e̱po̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ ase̱ŋ mɔ‑rɔ. Mbraa‑ɔ yɛ ɔkye̱e̱ mò̱ kuri e̱ tɛɛ ɔ bo̱‑rɔ fe̱raa, mò̱ kuri ke̱sare̱e̱ dɔŋ mò̱ se̱. Amaa ɔkye̱e̱ mò̱ kuri ya wu fe̱raa, awaare̱yɛ mbraa mo̱ŋ lɛɛ n dɔŋ mò̱ se̱.

ref: to marry someone else. However, if she goes off with another man while her husband is still alive, she is said to be unfaithful.
text: Amaa mò̱ kuri e̱ saŋ o te, ne̱ mò̱ ya kine mò̱ ya waare̱e̱ ɔnyare̱ baŋbaŋ, amo̱ fe̱raa ɔɔ waa kanyare̱kpa ne̱e̱. Amaa mò̱ kuri ya wu fe̱raa, o de kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ ya waare̱e̱ ɔnyare̱ baŋbaŋ, ɔ mo̱ŋ waa kanyare̱kpa.

ref: That is how it is with you, my friends. You are now part of the body of Christ and are dead to the power of the Law. You are free to belong to Christ, who was raised to life so we could serve God.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔnyare̱ ne̱ oo wu‑o mo̱ŋ lɛɛ o de mò̱ ka se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ‑ɔ, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ mbraa‑ɔ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ n de mo̱ne̱ se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ mo̱ne̱ de kuri po̱pwɛɛ, ɔko̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kyiŋŋi mò̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro‑o. Mo̱ne̱ bo̱ Kristoo ke̱yaale̱ŋ‑ɔ kaase̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱ na mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱ waa atɔ timaa bo̱ sa Wuribware̱.

ref: When we thought only of ourselves, the Law made us have sinful desires. It made every part of our bodies into slaves who are doomed to die.
text: Saŋ‑ɔ e̱bɔye̱ ke̱maa ne̱ ane̱ fɔŋfɔŋ e̱ kpa‑ɔ ne̱ ane̱ a waa. Mbraa‑ɔ ya tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱mo̱‑ɔ na ke̱mo̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ bware, amɔ ke̱mo̱ dɛɛ ke̱mo̱ ne̱ ane̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ mɔ e̱ kpa ko̱waa. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ane̱ dɛɛ ane̱ e̱ waa e̱bɔye̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ, ne̱ e̱ ya sa ne̱ ane̱ e̱ yɔ lowi ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: But the Law no longer rules over us. We are like dead people, and it cannot have any power over us. Now we can serve God in a new way by obeying his Spirit, and not in the old way by obeying the written Law. The Battle with Sin
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ ane̱ a nya ane̱ e̱ye̱e̱ le̱e̱ mbraa amo̱ se̱. M mo̱ŋ lɛɛ n de ane̱‑rɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ ke̱bo̱rɔgyi de̱daa‑ɔ a gye̱ kɛɛ. Ane̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ ane̱ gya de̱daa a mbraa ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱. Mbe̱yɔmɔ ane̱ te kakye̱na po̱pwɛɛ‑rɔ ane̱ gya Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa a e̱kpa‑ɔ se̱. Ane̱ nyi mbraa‑ɔ, ŋkee ne̱ ane̱ i ŋu fe̱yɛ ane̱ e̱ waa e̱bɔye̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ

ref: Does this mean that the Law is sinful? Certainly not! But if it had not been for the Law, I would not have known what sin is really like. For example, I would not have known what it means to want something that belongs to someone else, unless the Law had told me not to do this.
text: Amɔ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ, ŋ yɛ Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ fɔŋfɔŋ mo̱ŋ bo̱ daŋ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱? Daabii daa! Amaa ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mbraa‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ŋ ya kaapo̱ mo̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ e̱bɔye̱ gye̱‑ɔ. Mbraa‑ɔ dɛɛ m mo̱ŋ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ ma sa a fo̱ akatɔ pee fo̱ bɛɛko̱ atɔ se̱,” ne̱e̱ weetee mo̱ŋ ŋu fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ a tɔyo̱rɔwe̱‑ɔ gye̱ bɔye̱.

ref: It was sin that used this command as a way of making me have all kinds of desires. But without the Law, sin is dead.
text: Amaa mo̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ nyase̱pee gye̱ bɔye̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, bɔye̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ mo̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ a sa ne̱ mo̱ tɔyo̱rɔwe̱ a tii si. Amaa ane̱ŋ a mbraa‑ɔ dɛɛ m mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱e̱ weetee bɔye̱ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ atɔ ne̱ aa wu, ne̱ a mo̱ŋ lɛɛ a de ke̱yaale̱ŋ‑ɔ.

ref: Sin used this command to trick me, and because of it I died.
text: Aa waa ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ŋyo̱rɔwe̱ bɔye̱‑ɔ a ba Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ bo̱ pe̱nna mo̱ mɔɔ mo̱‑ɔ.

ref: Still, the Law and its commands are holy and correct and good.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ mbraa‑ɔ to̱ŋ ke̱maa gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱ m bware kpa ke̱maa se̱.

ref: We know that the Law is spiritual. But I am merely a human, and I have been sold as a slave to sin.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ ane̱ nyi fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o ya sa Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ. Amaa m fe̱raa, mo̱ a gye̱ se̱sɛ dimaadi‑o si‑o, ne̱ bɔye̱ a waa mo̱ mò̱ ke̱nya.

ref: In fact, I don't understand why I act the way I do. I don't do what I know is right. I do the things I hate.
text: Mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ maa nu ke̱tɔ ne̱ ki de mo̱‑ɔ kaase̱. Mo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ŋ waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ki bware‑o, mo̱‑ɔ maa taare̱ a ŋ waa. Ŋkee ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ i kisi‑o gbaa ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa.

ref: Although I don't do what I know is right, I agree that the Law is good.
text: Mo̱ a waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ i kisi‑o si‑o, a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a sure si fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ bware.

ref: So I am not the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
text: N gye̱ mo̱ ke̱pre̱ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa bɔye̱. Amaa bɔye̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ mo̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ sa ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa bɔye̱.

ref: I know that my selfish desires won't let me do anything that is good. Even when I want to do right, I cannot.
text: Ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ ke̱tɔ timaa ko̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱ mo̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ se̱sɛ dimaadi e̱ gye̱ mo̱. Hare̱e̱ mo̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ mo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ŋ waa itimaa gbaa ooo, ma taare̱ a ŋ waa.

ref: Instead of doing what I know is right, I do wrong.
text: Ma taare̱ a ŋ waa ke̱tɔ timaa ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa ko̱waa‑ɔ. Amaa bɔye̱ ne̱ ma kpa‑ɔ fe̱raa ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa.

ref: And so, if I don't do what I know is right, I am no longer the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
text: Amo̱ a tɛɛ a gye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ weetee mo̱ŋ lɛɛ mo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ŋ waa‑ɔ, amo̱ fe̱raa amo̱‑ɔ a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ n gye̱ mo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ n lɛɛ mo̱ e̱ waa amo̱. Amaa bɔye̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ mo̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ sa ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa bɔye̱.

ref: The Law has shown me that something in me keeps me from doing what I know is right.
text: To, ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ a ŋu‑o e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ saŋ ke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa a ŋ waa itimaa‑o, amɔ e̱bɔye̱ i lwii mo̱ kamɛɛ.

ref: With my whole heart I agree with the Law of God.
text: A bo̱ mo̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ kɔne̱ fe̱yɛ n nu Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ.

ref: But in every part of me I discover something fighting against my mind, and it makes me a prisoner of sin that controls everything I do.
text: Amaa mo̱ a be̱e̱ ŋu fe̱yɛ mbraa ko̱ be̱e̱ ŋ waa kusuŋ mo̱ mfɛɛre̱‑rɔ ne̱ n kye mbraa ne̱ mo̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ m bware‑o. Mo̱ ŋyo̱rɔwe̱ bɔye̱‑ɔ e̱ kɔ n gyi mo̱ ŋyo̱rɔwe̱ timaa‑o si. Ne̱ ŋ ya tii mo̱ waa‑rɔ.

ref: If you belong to Christ Jesus, you won't be punished.
text: Kase̱ŋtiŋ, Wuribware̱ maa po̱rɔ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Kristoo Yeesuu a ko̱ko̱we̱bɛɛ‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: The Law of Moses cannot do this, because our selfish desires make the Law weak. But God set you free when he sent his own Son to be like us sinners and to be a sacrifice for our sin. God used Christ's body to condemn sin.
text: Bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ ŋyo̱rɔwe̱ bɔye̱ se̱‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ maa taare̱ a n sa ne̱ ane̱ i kii ase̱sɛ timaa. Amaa Wuribware̱ fe̱raa e̱ taare̱. Oo suŋ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ mò̱ gyi‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ kye̱na fe̱yɛ ane̱ se̱sɛ dimaadi‑o dɛɛ. Hare̱e̱ Kristoo a mo̱ŋ waa bɔye̱‑ɔ gbaa ooo, ɔɔ taa mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ lɔŋŋɔ Wuribware̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ e̱bɔye̱ se̱, na Wuribware̱ nya bu dimaadi mò̱ aa mò̱ e̱bɔye̱ pɛɛɛ ke̱pɔ.

ref: He did this, so that we would do what the Law commands by obeying the Spirit instead of our own desires.
text: Wuribware̱ a waa ane̱ŋ ne̱e̱, na ane̱ ne̱ ane̱ gya Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe a e̱kpa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ane̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ ane̱ gya ŋyo̱rɔwe̱ bɔye̱ se̱‑ɔ, nya gya atɔ pɛɛɛ ne̱ mò̱ mbraa‑ɔ yɛ a bware‑o si.

ref: People who are ruled by their desires think only of themselves. Everyone who is ruled by the Holy Spirit thinks about spiritual things.
text: Bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ gya bamo̱ ŋyo̱rɔwe̱ bɔye̱ se̱‑ɔ, de bamo̱ mfɛɛre̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ bamo̱ ayo̱rɔwe̱tɔ bɔye̱ amo̱ se̱ ne̱e̱. Amaa bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ gya Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe a e̱kpa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ fe̱raa mfɛɛre̱ dɔŋ atɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ bo̱ waa‑ɔ se̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: If our minds are ruled by our desires, we will die. But if our minds are ruled by the Spirit, we will have life and peace.
text: Ɔko̱ ne̱ o de mò̱ mfɛɛre̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ mò̱ ayo̱rɔwe̱tɔ bɔye̱ se̱‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ e̱ gya mò̱ le̱e̱ mò̱ ase̱ yaa mò̱ bo̱ waa lowi‑ro nsu pɛɛɛ. Amaa ɔko̱ ne̱ o de mò̱ mfɛɛre̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o na mò̱ e̱kpa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ e̱ nya ŋkpa na kaye̱e̱yuri.

ref: Our desires fight against God, because they do not and cannot obey God's laws.
text: Ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ taa mò̱ mfɛɛre̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ ayo̱rɔwe̱tɔ bɔye̱ se̱‑ɔ ye̱re̱ bo̱ kye Wuribware̱ ne̱e̱. Se̱sɛ‑ɔ maa bu Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ. Kase̱ŋtiŋ, ɔ maa taare̱.

ref: You are no longer ruled by your desires, but by God's Spirit, who lives in you. People who don't have the Spirit of Christ in them don't belong to him.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o e̱ bo̱ se̱sɛ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ, se̱sɛ‑ɔ mo̱ŋ gya mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ŋyo̱rɔwe̱ bɔye̱ se̱. Ɔ gya Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe a e̱kpa‑ɔ se̱ ne̱e̱. Amaa Kristoo a kufwiiŋe‑o e̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱ se̱sɛ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ, se̱sɛ‑ɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Wuribware̱ se̱sɛ ne̱e̱.

ref: Yet God raised Jesus to life! God's Spirit now lives in you, and he will raise you to life by his Spirit.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ko̱ kyɔ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ amo̱ a gye̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ ya kyiŋŋi Kristoo le̱e̱ lowi‑ro, ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o te mo̱ne̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe amo̱ dɛɛ i kyiŋŋi mo̱ne̱ ŋyo̱wɔre̱ a ɔ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro.
