ref: Only those people who are led by God's Spirit are his children.
text: Bamo̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o e̱ gye̱ bamo̱ ŋkpɛɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ gye̱ Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑ana ne̱e̱.

ref: God's Spirit makes us sure that we are his children.
text: Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o na ane̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ a waa kanɔ ne̱e̱ sure si fe̱yɛ ane̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑ana.

ref: His Spirit lets us know that together with Christ we will be given what God has promised. We will also share in the glory of Christ, because we have suffered with him. A Wonderful Future for God's People
text: Ane̱ a tɛɛ ane̱ gye̱ mò̱ gyi‑ana‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ane̱ kanɔ tii kapo̱tɛɛ ne̱ oo lee bo̱ ye̱ra keri si so̱so̱‑ɔ se̱, kapo̱tɛɛ amo̱ dɛɛ ne̱ Kristoo i gyi kamo̱ kɔne̱‑ɔ. Amaa pwɛɛ na ane̱ kanɔ dɛɛ tii si‑o, a tiri fe̱yɛ ane̱ kanɔ gbaa tii Kristoo a awo̱re̱fɔɔ ne̱ oo gyi amo̱‑ɔ se̱. Na ane̱ nya nya mò̱ ke̱dabe̱ na wuraa ne̱ a bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ ako̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o e̱ kya ane̱‑rɔ‑ɔ

ref: I am sure what we are suffering now cannot compare with the glory that will be shown to us.
text: Awo̱re̱fɔɔ mɔ ne̱ ane̱ i gyi bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ gya Kristoo a e̱kpa‑ɔ se̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ fe̱raa, fo̱ ya de̱e̱re̱ ke̱dabe̱ na wuraa ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ ba a ɔ bo̱ sa ane̱‑ɔ, awo̱re̱fɔɔ amo̱ gye̱ kafwe̱e̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: In fact, all creation is eagerly waiting for God to show who his children are.
text: Ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ Wuribware̱ a twe̱e̱‑ɔ e̱ ko̱kɔse̱ ke̱ e̱ kpa a ku ŋu bamo̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ i lee fe̱yɛ bo̱ gye̱ mò̱ gyi‑ana‑ɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: We know that all creation is still groaning and is in pain, like a woman about to give birth.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ ane̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ Wuribware̱ a twe̱e̱‑ɔ e̱ye̱e̱ mo̱ŋ twɛɛ ke̱mo̱, ne̱ ki i keŋŋi fe̱yɛ ɔkye̱e̱ a kee ɔ kpa a ɔ ko̱we̱‑ɔ.

ref: The Spirit makes us sure about what we will be in the future. But now we groan silently, while we wait for God to show that we are his children. This means that our bodies will also be set free.
text: N gye̱ atɔ amo̱ wo̱re̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ a keŋŋi. Amaa ane̱ ne̱ ane̱ a nya Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o, ne̱ a kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ e̱ sa ane̱ ke̱tɔ dabe̱ ko̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ane̱ gbaa ane̱ i gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ ane̱ gywii saŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ ba na ɔ bo̱ kɔɔre̱ ane̱ le̱e̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ taa ane̱ yaa mò̱ ase̱, na ane̱ kii mò̱ gyi‑ana kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o ne̱e̱.

ref: However, we hope for something we have not yet seen, and we patiently wait for it.
text: Amaa amo̱ a gye̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ saŋ ane̱ gywii kake amo̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ e̱ de̱ŋ ɔ kaapo̱ ane̱ kanyite kigyi na ane̱ te̱maa nya waa ko̱kyɔ.

ref: In certain ways we are weak, but the Spirit is here to help us. For example, when we don't know what to pray for, the Spirit prays for us in ways that cannot be put into words.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o be̱e̱ ɔ waa ɔ sa ane̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ kya ane̱‑rɔ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱‑rɔ bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱ le̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱. Ane̱ a mo̱ŋ nyi ane̱ŋ ne̱ weetee ane̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o e̱ ko̱re̱ Wuribware̱ ɔ sa ane̱. O de ase̱ŋkpare̱gyi ne̱ ane̱ maa taare̱ a ane̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱ o de o su ɔ ko̱re̱ Wuribware̱ ɔ sa ane̱.

ref: All of our thoughts are known to God. He can understand what is in the mind of the Spirit, as the Spirit prays for God's people.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ ne̱ ɔ ke̱e̱ ane̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ nyi mò̱ kufwiiŋe‑o mfɛɛre̱. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ kufwiiŋe‑o e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ sa mò̱ ase̱sɛ ke̱tɔ ne̱ mò̱ Wuribware̱ fɔŋfɔŋ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ bo̱ nya‑ɔ.

ref: We know that God is always at work for the good of everyone who loves him. They are the ones God has chosen for his purpose,
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, ane̱ nyi fe̱yɛ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa‑rɔ Wuribware̱ gya mò̱ ase̱sɛ kamɛɛ, ne̱ ɔ sa ne̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa i buruwi kitimaa ke̱ e̱ sa bamo̱ ne̱ ba kpa mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ. Bamo̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a lee fe̱yɛ bo̱ waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ kra fe̱yɛ bo̱ waa‑ɔ ne̱ n de.

ref: and he has always known who his chosen ones would be. He had decided to let them become like his own Son, so his Son would be the first of many children.
text: Bamo̱ dɛɛ bamo̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kyɔ o nyi fe̱yɛ ba kii mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ɔɔ sa ne̱ bamo̱ kakra e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ kii mò̱ gyi‑ana, na bo̱ kii fe̱yɛ mò̱ gyi Yeesuu‑o, na mò̱‑ɔ mɔ nya kii bamo̱ daa bre̱sɛ.

ref: What can we say about all this? If God is on our side, can anyone be against us?
text: Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ ane̱ŋ fe̱raa, Wuribware̱ a tɛɛ ɔ gya ane̱ kamɛɛ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, nsɛ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ taare̱ a ɔ waa ko̱tɔko̱ bo̱ kye ane̱?

ref: It is exactly as the Scriptures say, “For you we face death all day long. We are like sheep on their way to be butchered.”
text: Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ e̱ sa se̱ŋsa o gywii Wuribware̱ ɔ yɛ, “Bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ gya fo̱ se̱‑ɔ se̱, ba kpa a bo̱ mɔɔ ane̱ saŋ ke̱maa. Ba waa ane̱ fe̱yɛ e̱sanne̱ ne̱ ba mɔɔ ba wo̱‑ɔ.”

ref: I would gladly be placed under God's curse and be separated from Christ for the good of my own people.
text: Kase̱ŋtiŋ, a dɛɛ a taare̱ na a waa ne̱e̱ weetee bamo̱ se̱ Wuribware̱ da mo̱ yii na o lee mo̱ le̱e̱ Kristoo a ko̱ko̱we̱bɛɛ‑ɔ‑rɔ nsu pɛɛɛ, na ɔ nya bo̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ bamo̱.

ref: They have those famous ancestors, who were also the ancestors of the Christ. I pray that God, who rules over all, will be praised forever! Amen.
text: Bamo̱ ade̱daapo̱ Abraham na Isak na Isireelii dɛɛ bo̱ gye̱ ase̱sɛ dabe̱ ne̱e̱. Bamo̱ kasuro amo̱ dɛɛ ne̱ baa ko̱we̱ Kristoo. Wuribware̱, owure dabe̱‑ɔ, nya ke̱dabe̱ na nkyo̱rɔ nsu pɛɛɛ. Amɛye̱.

ref: In fact, when God made the promise to Abraham, he meant only Abraham's descendants by his son Isaac. God was talking only about Isaac when he promised
text: Amo̱‑ɔ e̱ kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ n gye̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa ko̱we̱ bamo̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ba ko̱we̱ dimaadi pɛɛɛ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑ana. Ananagyi ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ ɔ sa Abraham‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ Abraham ananagyi kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o.

ref: Sarah, “At this time next year I will return, and you will already have a son.”
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya gywii Abraham‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ, “Su-a-ba saŋ mɔ se̱ mo̱ i kiŋŋi a m ba fo̱ ase̱, na fo̱ ka Sara ko̱we̱ ɔnyare̱.”

ref: Are we saying God is unfair? Certainly not!
text: Amo̱‑ɔ e̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ waa‑ɔ mo̱ŋ bware ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱? Daabii daa!

ref: The Lord told Moses that he has pity and mercy on anyone he wants to.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii ane̱ ɔde̱daapo̱ Mosis fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ i su ɔke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ se̱, na ŋ ŋu mò̱ e̱wɛɛ.”

ref: In the Scriptures the Lord says to the king of Egypt, “I let you become king, so that I could show you my power and be praised by all people on earth.”
text: Abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ baa be̱e̱ kyo̱rɛɛ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii Igyipiti owure fe̱yɛ, “Ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ a sa ne̱ fo̱ a kii owure‑o e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ŋ kaapo̱ mo̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ ne̱e̱ na ke̱ sa mo̱ ke̱nyare̱ dabe̱ kaye̱ pɛɛɛ‑rɔ.”

ref: Everything depends on what God decides to do, and he can either have pity on people or make them stubborn. God's Anger and Mercy
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ amo̱‑ɔ e̱ kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ i ŋu ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ kpa‑ɔ e̱wɛɛ, ne̱ ɔ waa bamo̱ ne̱ ɔ maa kpa‑ɔ kuŋurole̱ŋ. Wuribware̱ de kpa fe̱yɛ o ŋu ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ kpa‑ɔ e̱wɛɛ

ref: Someone may ask, “How can God blame us, if he makes us behave in the way he wants us to?”
text: Amɔ mo̱ne̱ ɔko̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ maa taare̱ a o kyurowi ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kra fe̱yɛ ɔ waa‑ɔ, amo̱ fe̱raa, ne̱ e̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ po̱rɔ ɔko̱?”

ref: But, my friend, I ask, “Who do you think you are to question God? Does the clay have the right to ask the potter why he shaped it the way he did?
text: Amaa, mo̱ nyare̱, nsɛ e̱ gye̱ fo̱‑ɔ fo̱ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ be̱ŋŋaa Wuribware̱ ane̱ŋ? E̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ gyare̱ e̱ taare̱ a ɔ tɔwe̱ gywii ɔpwɛɛpo̱ fe̱yɛ, “E̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ fo̱ a pwɛɛ mo̱ ane̱ŋ?”

ref: Doesn't a potter have the right to make a fancy bowl and a plain bowl out of the same lump of clay?”
text: Ɔpwɛɛpo̱ de kpa a ɔ waa kabori ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ kpa‑ɔ. Ɔ taare̱ a ɔ ba kabori ko̱ŋko̱‑ɔ bo̱ pwɛɛ taŋŋa bɛɛɛ gyare̱.

ref: He did this by showing how glorious he is when he has pity on the people he has chosen to share in his glory.
text: Oo nyite bamo̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ kpa fe̱yɛ o ŋu ase̱sɛ ko̱ e̱wɛɛ‑ɔ se̱. Ane̱ŋ a ase̱sɛ amo̱ ne̱ oo lee kyee fe̱yɛ ɔ kaapo̱ bamo̱ mò̱ ke̱dabe̱‑ɔ.

ref: just as the Lord says in the book of Hosea, “Although they are not my people, I will make them my people. I will treat with love those nations that have never been loved.
text: Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a bo̱rɔ mò̱ kyaamɛɛ Hoseya si tɔwe̱‑ɔ, “Mo̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ a dɛɛ te̱e̱ ɛ ‘Mo̱ne̱‑mo̱ŋ‑gye̱-mo̱‑ase̱sɛ’‑ɔ, mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ ‘Mo̱ne̱‑e̱‑gye̱-mo̱‑ase̱sɛ.’ Mo̱ i lee mo̱ ke̱kpa a m bo̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ a dɛɛ te̱e̱ ɛ ‘Ma‑lɛɛ‑kpa-mo̱ne̱‑ase̱ŋ’‑ɔ.”

ref: “Once they were told, ‘You are not my people.’ But in that very place they will be called children of the living God.”
text: “To̱ŋ ke̱maa ne̱ baa dɛɛ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ, saŋ ko̱ e̱ ba na ba te̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ ne̱ mò̱ wo̱re̱ dooo i de ŋkpa‑ɔ mò̱ gyi‑ana.”

ref: Isaiah also said, “If the Lord All-Powerful had not spared some of our descendants, we would have been destroyed like the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah.” Israel and the Good News
text: Ne̱ Isaya a be̱e̱ kyo̱rɛɛ to̱ŋ ko̱ fe̱yɛ, “N gye̱ fe̱yɛ Bo̱-e̱le̱ŋ‑ɔ‑kyo̱ŋ‑ɔke̱maa-Bware̱ a mo̱rɔwe̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ se̱ sa ane̱, weetee Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ pɛɛɛ i kpuri, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Sɔdɔm na Gomora e̱maŋ a kpuri‑o.”

ref: What does all of this mean? It means that the Gentiles were not trying to be acceptable to God, but they found that he would accept them if they had faith.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ke̱tɔ ne̱ kaa ba‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ nde̱ se̱ awuye‑o a nya ke̱tɔ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ buwi kpa‑ɔ, amaa Yudaa awuye‑o fe̱raa mo̱ŋ nya ke̱tɔ ne̱ baa buwi kpa‑ɔ. Nde̱ se̱ awuye‑o ne̱ Wuribware̱ a te̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ timaa, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ baa kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu Kristoo gyi.

ref: It also means that the people of Israel were not acceptable to God. And why not? It was because they were trying to be acceptable by obeying the Law instead of by having faith in God. The people of Israel fell over the stone that makes people stumble,
text: Nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ taare̱ pre̱ Wuribware̱? A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ baa kpa ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ba mò̱ mbraa‑ɔ ke̱gyase̱ kyɛɛkyɛɛ bo̱ waa bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ase̱sɛ timaa mò̱ ase̱. Ke̱fo̱re̱ ko̱ a di bamo̱ kpa‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa daye̱ ayaa ke̱mo̱ se̱ le̱e̱ da. Ke̱mo̱ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu Kristoo gyi.

ref: just as God says in the Scriptures, “Look! I am placing in Zion a stone to make people stumble and fall. But those who have faith in that one will never be disappointed.”
text: Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ mò̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ to̱ŋ ko̱‑ɔ, “Mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱, n de ke̱fo̱re̱ mo̱ e̱ be̱ya Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ, na ase̱sɛ daye̱ ayaa ke̱mo̱ se̱ na bo̱ le̱e̱ da. Ɔke̱maa mɔ ne̱ ɔ kɔɔre̱ ke̱mo̱ a o gyi‑o ipeere maa gyi mò̱, daa.”

ref: Dear friends, my greatest wish and my prayer to God is for the people of Israel to be saved.
text: Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, mo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ ŋu fe̱yɛ mo̱ kɔne̱ se̱ na mo̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ mo̱ ase̱sɛ Yudaa awuye‑o.

ref: what makes people acceptable to him. So they refuse to trust God, and they try to be acceptable by obeying the Law.
text: Baa kine kuŋu ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ waa ne̱ ɔ sa ne̱ nyiŋkpasɛ i kii se̱sɛ timaa‑o. Bo̱ mo̱ŋ sure fe̱yɛ ba gya Wuribware̱ a kpa ne̱ ɔ ba a ɔ bo̱ te̱e̱ bamo̱ ase̱sɛ timaa‑o si.

ref: But Christ makes the Law no longer necessary for those who become acceptable to God by faith. Anyone Can Be Saved
text: Ne̱ ŋkee Wuribware̱ a bo̱rɔ Kristoo si kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ mbraa‑ɔ a fo̱ mmo̱ kɛɛ, ne̱ m mo̱ŋ lɛɛ de ane̱ se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ, na ɔke̱maa ne̱ mò̱ ya kɔɔre̱ Kristoo gyi, Wuribware̱ e̱ te̱e̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ se̱sɛ timaa.

ref: Moses said a person could become acceptable to God by obeying the Law. He did this when he wrote, “If you want to live, you must do all the Law commands.”
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ, ane̱ Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ ke̱gyase̱ na ane̱ nya kii ase̱sɛ timaa‑o ase̱ŋ, ane̱ mbraa ɔsapo̱ Mosis a kyo̱rɛɛ fe̱yɛ, “Se̱sɛ ke̱maa ne̱ ɔ gya Wuribware̱ mbraa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ e̱ nya ŋkpa, ɔ maa wu.”

ref: Neither will they ask, “Who will go down into the world of the dead to raise him to life?”
text: N gye̱ fo̱ ke̱be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nsɛ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ o lwee swe̱e̱re̱‑rɔ?” fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ to̱ŋ ko̱ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ‑ɔ, na fo̱ fa fe̱yɛ fo̱ fe̱raa fo̱ e̱ ya kyiŋŋi Kristoo le̱e̱ lowi‑ro‑o.

ref: All who are acceptable because of their faith simply say, “The message is as near as your mouth or your heart.” And this is the same message we preach about faith.
text: Daabii! Ke̱tɔ ne̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ, “Wuribware̱ ase̱ŋ dɔŋ mo̱ne̱ nnɔ se̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ tɔwe̱, ne̱ a bo̱ mo̱ne̱ mfɛɛre̱‑rɔ.” Abware̱se̱ŋ amo̱ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ amo̱ ase̱ŋ saŋ ke̱maa fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ kɔɔre̱ gyi‑o.

ref: So you will be saved, if you honestly say, “Jesus is Lord,” and if you believe with all your heart that God raised him from death.
text: Amo̱ katiŋ‑o e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ yɛ, fo̱ ya lee fo̱ kanɔ‑rɔ tɔwe̱ lamaŋ‑nɔ fe̱yɛ, “Kristoo gye̱ mo̱ nyaŋpe̱,” ne̱ fo̱ a kɔɔre̱ gyi fo̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kyiŋŋi Kristoo le̱e̱ lowi‑ro, amo̱ fe̱raa Wuribware̱ e̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ fo̱.

ref: God will accept you and save you, if you truly believe this and tell it to others.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ fo̱ ya kɔɔre̱ amo̱ gyi, Wuribware̱ e̱ te̱e̱ fo̱ fe̱yɛ se̱sɛ timaa. Fo̱ ya lee amo̱ tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ fo̱ i gyi‑o lamaŋ‑nɔ, Wuribware̱ e̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ fo̱.

ref: no matter if that person is a Jew or a Gentile. There is only one Lord, and he is generous to everyone who asks for his help.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mbe̱yɔmɔ Yudaa awuye‑o mo̱ŋ lɛɛ bo̱ kwe̱e̱, ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o mɔ kwe̱e̱, Wuribware̱ e̱ gye̱ bamo̱ ɔke̱maa mɔ nyaŋpe̱, ne̱ o yure ɔke̱maa ne̱ o su a ɔ te̱e̱ mò̱‑ɔ.

ref: All who call out to the Lord will be saved.
text: Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa be̱e̱ kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, “Wuribware̱ e̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ ɔke̱maa ne̱ o su a ɔ te̱e̱ mo̱‑ɔ.”

ref: How can people have faith in the Lord and ask him to save them, if they have never heard about him? And how can they hear, unless someone tells them?
text: Ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ, e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ ɔko̱ e̱ waa a ɔ te̱e̱ Wuribware̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ mo̱rɔwe̱ mò̱, amɔ ɔɔ kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi pwɛɛ? Ne̱ e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ ɔ waa a ɔ kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi, amɔ oo nu abware̱se̱ŋ pwɛɛ? Ne̱ e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ ɔ waa a o nu abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ, amɔ abware̱se̱ŋ ɔkaapo̱po̱ a yɔ mò̱ ase̱ pwɛɛ?

ref: And how can anyone tell them without being sent by the Lord? The Scriptures say it is a beautiful sight to see even the feet of someone coming to preach the good news.
text: Ne̱ e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ abware̱se̱ŋ ɔkaapo̱po̱‑ɔ e̱ waa a ɔ yɔ mò̱ ase̱, amɔ baa suŋ mò̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ pwɛɛ? Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, “Mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ de ase̱ŋ timaa ba baa‑ɔ, na mo̱nꞌ sa a mo̱ne̱ akatɔ gyi.”

ref: Yet not everyone has believed the message. For example, the prophet Isaiah asked, “Lord, has anyone believed what we said?”
text: Amaa n gye̱ ɔke̱maa ya kɔɔre̱ ase̱ŋ timaa‑o kra‑rɔ. Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ Isaya a kyo̱rɛɛ‑ɔ. Ɔ yɛ, “Wuribware̱, ɔko̱ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ɔɔ kɔɔre̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ gyi aaa?”

ref: But am I saying that the people of Israel did not hear? No, I am not! The Scriptures say, “The message was told everywhere on earth. It was announced all over the world.”
text: Amɔ ase̱sɛ i gyiiri ikii fe̱yɛ bo̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ nu ase̱ŋ timaa‑o ooo? Daabii! Baa nu. Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, “E̱se̱ ke̱maa se̱ awuye a nu amo̱ bo̱re̱, ne̱ amo̱ ase̱ŋ a fo̱ kaye̱‑rɔ to̱ŋ ke̱maa.”

ref: Did the people of Israel understand or not? Moses answered this question when he told that the Lord had said, “I will make Israel jealous of people who are a nation of nobodies. I will make them angry with people who don't understand a thing.”
text: Amɔ Yudaa awuye‑o e̱ taare̱ a bo̱ gyiiri ikii fe̱yɛ bamo̱ fe̱raa bo̱ mo̱ŋ nu ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ kaase̱ aaa? Daabii! A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ de̱daa‑ɔ bamo̱ mbraa ɔsapo̱ Mosis a waa kiliŋ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ke̱se̱bɔrɔle̱ŋ se̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ yɛ, “Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱ e̱ ba kasuro ko̱ ne̱ ka mo̱ŋ bo̱ ko̱ko̱rɔ‑ɔ a m bo̱ sa a bo̱ ka ko̱kwe̱e̱. Mo̱ e̱ ba kasuro awuye ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi ase̱ŋ‑ɔ a m bo̱ fwii bamo̱ duŋ.”

ref: Isaiah was fearless enough to tell that the Lord had said, “I was found by people who were not looking for me. I appeared to the ones who were not asking about me.”
text: Ne̱ Isaya a be̱e̱ kyo̱rɛɛ kaapo̱‑rɔ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ yɛ, “Mo̱ a lee mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ kyɔ bo̱ maa bise mo̱ se̱‑ɔ. Kade̱ ne̱ kamo̱ se̱ awuye kyɔ bo̱ maa su ba te̱e̱ mo̱‑ɔ ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱ mo̱, mo̱ e̱ bo̱ mfe̱e̱‑ɔ.’ ”

ref: And Isaiah said about the people of Israel, “All day long the Lord has reached out to people who are stubborn and refuse to obey.”
text: Amaa Yudaa awuye‑o ase̱ŋ‑ɔ Isaya a kya se̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ yɛ, “Mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ase̱sɛ fe̱raa, mo̱ a teyi asare̱e̱ saŋ ke̱maa bo̱ te̱e̱ bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ba mo̱ ase̱ a ŋ kɔɔre̱ bamo̱. Amaa bamo̱ e̱se̱bɔ‑rɔ a waa le̱ŋ, ne̱ bo̱ de katɔmaabu.”

ref: Am I saying that God has turned his back on his people? Certainly not! I am one of the people of Israel, and I myself am a descendant of Abraham from the tribe of Benjamin.
text: Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, amɔ ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ e̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kine mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ase̱sɛ Yudaa awuye‑o ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱? A mo̱ŋ gye̱ ane̱ŋ pɛɛɛ. Ke̱e̱ ɛ, ŋ gbaa, Yudaanyi e̱ gye̱ mo̱. Mo̱ nana e̱ gye̱ Abraham, ne̱ mo̱ a le̱e̱ Bɛnyamiŋ a kasuro‑o‑ro‑o.

ref: God did not turn his back on his chosen people. Don't you remember reading in the Scriptures how Elijah complained to God about the people of Israel?
text: Daabii, Wuribware̱ mo̱ŋ kine mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ase̱sɛ Yudaa awuye‑o, bamo̱ ne̱ oo lee bamo̱ bo̱ kii mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ase̱sɛ le̱e̱ hare̱e̱ de̱daa‑ɔ. Mo̱nꞌ nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ Iliya ase̱ŋ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ. Iliya e̱ po̱rɔ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ase̱sɛ Yudaa awuye‑o o gywii Wuribware̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ,

ref: He said, “Lord, they killed your prophets and destroyed your altars. I am the only one left, and now they want to kill me.”
text: “O Wuribware̱, fo̱ ase̱sɛ Isireelii awuye a ba apaŋ bo̱ mɔɔ fo̱ akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa bwee fo̱ e̱lɔŋŋɔkpa‑ɔ. Mo̱ wo̱re̱ ya saŋ ne̱ n gye̱ fo̱ kyaamɛɛ, ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ baa ye̱re̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ba kpa a bo̱ mɔɔ ŋ gbaa mo̱.”

ref: This means that only a chosen few of the people of Israel found what all of them were searching for. And the rest of them were stubborn,
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ kaa waa‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ Yudaa awuye‑o a de̱e̱re̱ de̱e̱re̱ kpa ane̱ŋ ne̱ ba bo̱rɔ se̱ na bo̱ waa Wuribware̱ ke̱pre̱‑ɔ kpo̱ne̱. Amaa bo̱mo̱‑rɔ bamo̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a lee sa mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱‑ɔ wo̱re̱ ya pre̱ mò̱. Ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa saŋ‑ɔ fe̱raa a kii ke̱se̱bɔrɔle̱ŋ awuye.

ref: It is the same way now. God treated the people of Israel with undeserved grace, and so a few of them are still his followers.
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ Wuribware̱ ase̱sɛ ko̱ a saŋ, ne̱ oo ŋu bamo̱ e̱wɛɛ, ne̱ oo lee bamo̱ sa mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱.

ref: This happened because of God's undeserved kindness and not because of anything they have done. It could not have happened except for God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: Oo su bamo̱ se̱ ne̱e̱ se̱ ne̱ oo lee bamo̱. N gye̱ fe̱yɛ baa waa asuŋ timaa ko̱ ne̱e̱. A dɛɛ a gye̱ bamo̱ asuŋ timaa si ne̱e̱, weetee mò̱ kusu bamo̱ se̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ de kaase̱.

ref: just as the Scriptures say, “God made them so stupid that their eyes are blind, and their ears are still deaf.”
text: Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, “Amaa, bo̱ fo̱ ndɔɔ, ane̱ŋ a atɔ mɔ ne̱ ɔɔ waa ne̱ baa ŋu‑o, ɔ mo̱ŋ sa a bo̱ nu amo̱ kaase̱ a bo̱ taa bo̱ waa bamo̱ mfɛɛre̱‑rɔ.”

ref: Then David said, “Turn their meals into bait for a trap, so that they will stumble and be given what they deserve.
text: Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Owure Deefid a kyo̱rɛɛ waa mò̱ iliŋ a wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, “O Wuribware̱, mo̱ ado̱ŋ‑ɔ ya gyaŋŋe̱ a bo̱ gyi kasu kanɔ ateese, na fo̱ sa a a kpare̱ bamo̱ e̱bo̱re̱‑rɔ. na a da bamo̱ kigyi fe̱yɛ katɛɛ a da kabo̱‑ɔ.

ref: Blindfold their eyes! Don't let them see. Bend their backs beneath a burden that will never be lifted.” Gentiles Will Be Saved
text: Buŋ bamo̱ akatɔ se̱ na bo̱ ma nya taare̱ ŋu, na fo̱ sa a bamo̱ e̱laare̱ nya bamo̱ gya, na bo̱ ma taare̱ ko̱so̱ nare̱ ntiŋtiŋ.”

ref: Do I mean that the people of Israel fell, never to get up again? Certainly not! Their failure made it possible for the Gentiles to be saved, and this will make the people of Israel jealous.
text: Amɔ ke̱tɔ e̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ a le̱e̱ da‑ɔ, bo̱ maa lɛɛ taare̱ a bo̱ ko̱so̱ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱? Daabii daa! Bamo̱ a waa e̱bɔye̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ a kyo̱ŋ bamo̱ se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ ya sa nde̱ se̱ awuye‑o kpa na bo̱ bo̱rɔ se̱ ba mò̱ ase̱ na ɔ mo̱rɔwe̱ bamo̱. Mfaanɛɛ ne̱ ɔɔ waa ne̱ Yudaa awuye‑o ko̱kwe̱e̱ a ko̱so̱.

ref: When Israel rejected God, the rest of the people in the world were able to turn to him. So when God makes friends with Israel, it will be like bringing the dead back to life.
text: Wuribware̱ kikine Yudaa awuye‑o a sa ne̱ mò̱ aa kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ ne̱ baa saŋ‑ɔ a waa kanɔ. Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ ya be̱e̱ kɔɔre̱ Yudaa awuye‑o, a waa ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ a kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro‑o.

ref: If part of a batch of dough is made holy by being offered to God, then all of the dough is holy. If the roots of a tree are holy, the rest of the tree is holy too.
text: Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ taa fe̱yɛ se̱sɛ ya taa bodobodoo ke̱be̱gya sa Wuribware̱, amɔ ke̱be̱gya ne̱ kaa saŋ‑ɔ saŋ ke̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ lee ne̱e̱. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ kiyii ileŋ e̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ lee, ne̱ ke̱mo̱ ŋyaagyii gbaa gye̱ Wuribware̱ lee.

ref: You Gentiles are like branches of a wild olive tree made to be part of a cultivated olive tree. You have taken the place of some branches that were cut away from it. And because of this, you enjoy the blessings that come from being part of that cultivated tree.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ, Yudaa awuye‑o du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ko̱dɔɔ‑rɔ kiyii ne̱ Wuribware̱ a ŋeri ke̱mo̱ ŋyaagyii‑o ŋko̱ bo̱ twe̱e̱, bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ŋ ya wu‑o si‑o. Mo̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o mɔ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kiyii suyo amo̱ dɛɛ ne̱ ke̱mo̱ fe̱raa ke̱ ye̱re̱ e̱fa‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ Wuribware̱ a ŋe ke̱mo̱ ŋyaagyii kako̱ŋko̱ yaa bo̱ kye̱na ke̱mo̱ ne̱ ke̱ ye̱re̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱, ne̱ kaa kyiŋŋi daŋ‑ɔ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mbe̱yɔmɔ mo̱ne̱ e̱ nya mo̱ne̱ ateese mo̱ne̱ e̱ le̱e̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑rɔ a kiyii a ileŋ amo̱ ase̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: But don't think you are better than the branches that were cut away. Just remember you are not supporting the roots of this tree. Its roots are supporting you.
text: Amo̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ ma kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱. Mo̱nꞌ nyiŋŋi si fe̱yɛ n gye̱ mo̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ gye̱ ŋyaagyii‑o e̱ gye̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ de kiyii‑o ileŋ‑no bo̱ ye̱ra. Amaa ileŋ‑o e̱ gye̱ ne̱ i de mo̱ne̱‑rɔ bo̱ ye̱ra.

ref: Maybe you think those branches were cut away, so you could be put in their place.
text: Amaa mo̱ne̱ ɔko̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Wuribware̱ a ŋeri ŋyaagyii‑o ne̱e̱ na ɔ nya taa mo̱ bo̱ kye̱na se̱.”

ref: Now you see both how kind and how hard God can be. He was hard on those who fell, but he was kind to you. And he will keep on being kind to you, if you keep on trusting in his kindness. Otherwise, you will be cut away too.
text: De̱e̱re̱ nɛɛnɛɛ na fo̱ ŋu ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a ŋu fo̱ e̱wɛɛ‑ɔ, na ane̱ŋ ne̱ mò̱ akatɔ a pee‑o. Mò̱ akatɔ a pee we̱e̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa le̱e̱ da‑ɔ se̱. Amaa fo̱ fe̱raa, oo ŋu fo̱ e̱wɛɛ. Fo̱ ya kya se̱ sure si fe̱yɛ o ŋu fo̱ e̱wɛɛ fo̱ kakye̱na‑rɔ, Wuribware̱ maa ŋe fo̱ a ɔ bo̱ twe̱e̱.

ref: If those other branches will start having faith, they will be made a part of that tree again. God has the power to put them back.
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ Yudaa awuye‑o ya kɔɔre̱ gyi, ne̱ Wuribware̱ be̱e̱ ɔ taa bamo̱ a ɔ bo̱ kye̱na mò̱ kiyii‑o si. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ o de ke̱yaale̱ŋ a ɔ waa ane̱ŋ.

ref: After all, it wasn't natural for branches to be cut from a wild olive tree and to be made part of a cultivated olive tree. So it is much more likely that God will join the natural branches back to the cultivated olive tree. The People of Israel Will Be Brought Back
text: Wuribware̱ a taa fo̱ le̱e̱ e̱fa‑rɔ a kiyii‑o si baa bo̱ kye̱na ndɔɔ‑rɔ a kiyii‑o si. Ne̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑rɔ a kiyii‑o ŋyaagyii fe̱raa ne̱ ɔ maa taare̱ a ɔ taa bo̱ kye̱na ko̱dɔɔ‑rɔ a kiyii‑o si aaa? Saŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ Yudaa awuye‑o ase̱ŋ

ref: My friends, I don't want you Gentiles to be too proud of yourselves. So I will explain the mystery of what has happened to the people of Israel. Some of them have become stubborn, and they will stay like that until the complete number of you Gentiles has come in.
text: Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, mo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ nu ase̱ŋ tirisɛ mɔ kaase̱, na mo̱ne̱ ma nya kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱. Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ Yudaa awuye‑o aŋu‑ro e̱ waa le̱ŋ ne̱e̱, na a bo̱ fo̱ saŋ ne̱ nde̱ se̱ awuye, ne̱ Wuribware̱ nyi fe̱yɛ ba ba a bo̱ kii Kristoo awuye‑o pɛɛɛ a fo̱ kanɔ.

ref: In this way all of Israel will be saved, as the Scriptures say, “From Zion someone will come to rescue us. Then Jacob's descendants will stop being evil.
text: Amo̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ na Wuribware̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ Yudaa awuye‑o pɛɛɛ. Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, “Ɔmo̱rɔwe̱po̱ e̱ le̱e̱ Siyɔŋ maŋ‑nɔ a ɔ ba, na ɔ bo̱ lee bo̱ro̱kɔɔne̱ pɛɛɛ le̱e̱ Yakubu a ananagyi‑o‑ro.

ref: This is what the Lord has promised to do when he forgives their sins.”
text: Mo̱ ya lee bamo̱ e̱bɔye̱‑ɔ, amo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ e̱taŋ ne̱ mo̱ aa bamo̱ e̱ waa‑ɔ ne̱e̱.”

ref: The people of Israel are treated as God's enemies, so the good news can come to you Gentiles. But they are still the chosen ones, and God loves them because of their famous ancestors.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ, ane̱ ya be̱e̱ de̱e̱re̱, ane̱ i ŋu fe̱yɛ bamo̱ kikine ase̱ŋ timaa‑o ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi a sa ne̱ baa kii Wuribware̱ ado̱ŋ, ne̱ kaa baa mo̱ne̱ kitimaa. Amaa kpa ko̱ se̱ mɔ‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ saŋ ɔ kpa bamo̱ ase̱ŋ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ ya lee bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ kii mò̱ ase̱sɛ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ ɔ sa bamo̱ ade̱daapo̱ atɔ dabe̱.

ref: All people have disobeyed God, and that's why he treats them as prisoners. But he does this, so that he can have mercy on all of them.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ gye̱ e̱bɔye̱ awaapo̱ ne̱e̱, na ɔ nya ŋu ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ e̱wɛɛ.

ref: Who can measure the wealth and wisdom and knowledge of God? Who can understand his decisions or explain what he does?
text: Oo! Wuribware̱ nyi atɔ, ne̱ o nyi ase̱ŋ fɛɛ! Nsɛ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ taare̱ a o ŋu mò̱ mfɛɛre̱? Nsɛ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ taare̱ a o ŋu mò̱ e̱kpa‑rɔ?

ref: “Has anyone known the thoughts of the Lord or given him advice?
text: Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, “Nsɛ gbaa i nyi Wuribware̱ mfɛɛre̱? Ɔsɛ mo̱ ya dɛɛ ka mò̱ ase̱ŋ?”

ref: Has anyone loaned something to the Lord that must be repaid?”
text: “Nsɛ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ taare̱ a o ke mò̱ atɔ, na mò̱‑ɔ mò̱ ke se̱sɛ‑ɔ ke̱tɔ bo̱ teere?”

ref: Everything comes from the Lord. All things were made because of him and will return to him. Praise the Lord forever! Amen.
text: Ke̱tɔ ke̱maa a le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱ ne̱e̱. Mò̱ se̱ ne̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa bo̱‑rɔ. Na lalalo̱we̱‑ɔ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa be̱e̱ kiŋŋi yɔ mò̱ ase̱. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ Wuribware̱ nya ke̱dabe̱ na wuraa nsu pɛɛɛ. Amɛye̱.

ref: I realize God has treated me with undeserved grace, and so I tell each of you not to think you are better than you really are. Use good sense and measure yourself by the amount of faith that God has given you.
text: N de Wuribware̱ ne̱ oo ŋu mo̱ e̱wɛɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ mo̱ fe̱yɛ m bo̱ suŋ mò̱‑ɔ, mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ pɛɛɛ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ ɔko̱ ma kaŋ fa fe̱yɛ ɔ kyɔ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔ gye̱‑ɔ. Amaa ke̱mo̱ ne̱ ki tiri‑o e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ, mo̱nꞌ sa a mo̱ne̱ ɔke̱maa ŋu fe̱yɛ mò̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi na ke̱yaale̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a sa mò̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ mò̱ lee.

ref: A body is made up of many parts, and each of them has its own use.
text: Ane̱ ŋyo̱wɔre̱ de abe̱gya bwe̱e̱tɔ, amaa ke̱be̱gya ke̱maa na ke̱mo̱ kusuŋ waasɛ.

ref: That's how it is with us. There are many of us, but we each are part of the body of Christ, as well as part of one another.
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ane̱ asɔre̱e̱ awuye gye̱ Kristoo a ko̱ko̱we̱bɛɛ‑ɔ‑rɔ ase̱sɛ, ne̱ ane̱ pɛɛɛ gye̱ ase̱sɛ ko̱ŋko̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Kristoo a kayo̱wɔre̱‑ɔ ke̱be̱gya ne̱e̱.

ref: If we can encourage others, we should encourage them. If we can give, we should be generous. If we are leaders, we should do our best. If we are good to others, we should do it cheerfully. Rules for Christian Living
text: Na ɔko̱ ne̱ mò̱ gbaa e̱ waa ase̱sɛ wɔre̱‑ɔ waa ane̱ŋ dɛɛ. Ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ taare̱ na mò̱ aa mò̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana ke atɔ timaa ne̱ o de‑o‑ro‑o waa ane̱ŋ, na ɔ ma kpa ke̱mo̱ kakɔka. Ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ asɔre̱e̱ a agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ ɔko̱‑ɔ ba mò̱ e̱le̱ŋ pɛɛɛ bo̱ waa kusuŋ keŋkeŋ. Ɔko̱ ne̱ mò̱ e̱ taare̱ a ɔ kya mò̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana ne̱ ase̱ŋ a to̱ bamo̱‑ɔ‑rɔ kya‑rɔ na ɔ ma tɛɛ.

ref: Be sincere in your love for others. Hate everything that is evil and hold tight to everything that is good.
text: Mo̱nꞌ lee mo̱ne̱ mme‑ro kpa abɛɛ ase̱ŋ. Mo̱nꞌ kine ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ke̱ gye̱ bɔye̱‑ɔ. Na mo̱nꞌ kra ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ki bware‑o‑ro keŋkeŋ.

ref: Love each other as brothers and sisters and honor others more than you do yourself.
text: Mo̱nꞌ kpa abɛɛ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ako̱we̱bɛɛ e̱ kpa abɛɛ‑ɔ. Ɔke̱maa bu mò̱ bɛɛko̱ ke̱dabe̱ kyo̱ŋ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱.

ref: Never give up. Eagerly follow the Holy Spirit and serve the Lord.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ma waa wo̱rɔgya. Mo̱nꞌ le̱ŋ e̱ye̱e̱ waa kusuŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ waa mo̱ne̱ e̱ sa ane̱ nyaŋpe̱‑ɔ.

ref: Let your hope make you glad. Be patient in time of trouble and never stop praying.
text: Mo̱nꞌ sa a te̱maa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ de fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱ yɔ so̱so̱‑ɔ sa mo̱ne̱ kakatɔgyi. Ase̱ŋ ya to̱ mo̱ne̱, na mo̱nꞌ waa kanyite. Mo̱ne̱ ma yɔwe̱ saŋ ke̱maa ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱.

ref: Take care of God's needy people and welcome strangers into your home.
text: Asɔre̱e̱ agyi‑o ɔko̱ i wu kitiri, mo̱nꞌ kya mò̱‑rɔ. Na mo̱nꞌ kra afɔ dame̱naŋsɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱lɔŋ‑nɔ.

ref: Ask God to bless everyone who mistreats you. Ask him to bless them and not to curse them.
text: Ɔko̱ ya waa mo̱ne̱ awo̱re̱fɔɔ, mo̱nꞌ tɔwe̱ gywii se̱sɛ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Wuribware̱ yure fo̱.” Mo̱ne̱ ma kaŋ da mò̱ yii.

ref: When others are happy, be happy with them, and when they are sad, be sad.
text: Ɔko̱ ne̱ mò̱ akatɔ ya gyi, mo̱nꞌ sa a a waa mo̱ne̱ gbaa mo̱ne̱ kɔne̱. Ne̱ ɔko̱ mɔ ne̱ mò̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ ya nye̱ra, mo̱nꞌ sa mò̱ kakpo̱nɔkya.

ref: Be friendly with everyone. Don't be proud and feel that you know more than others. Make friends with ordinary people.
text: Mo̱nꞌ kye̱na kaye̱e̱yuri‑ro, na mo̱nꞌ waa kanɔ ko̱ŋko̱. Mo̱ne̱ ma de̱e̱ bu mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ase̱sɛ dabe̱. Amaa mo̱nꞌ baa mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ kaase̱, na mo̱ne̱ aa awo̱rɔba de̱e̱ nare̱. Mo̱ne̱ ma kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱.

ref: Don't mistreat someone who has mistreated you. But try to earn the respect of others,
text: Ɔko̱ ya waa mo̱ne̱ bɔye̱, mo̱ne̱ ma ba bɔye̱ bo̱ ka mò̱ ko̱kɔ. Mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱ waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔke̱maa ke̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ki bware‑o.

ref: and do your best to live at peace with everyone.
text: Mo̱ne̱ e̱ taare̱ de, mo̱nꞌ waa ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ke̱ e̱ sa a mo̱nꞌ taare̱ kye̱na abɛɛ ase̱ kaye̱e̱yuri‑ro‑o.

ref: The Scriptures also say, “If your enemies are hungry, give them something to eat. And if they are thirsty, give them something to drink. This will be the same as piling burning coals on their heads.”
text: Amaa ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱nꞌ waa‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ baa be̱e̱ kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ako̱ŋ i de fo̱ do̱ŋ, sa mò̱ ateese a o gyi. Burufo i de mò̱, sa mò̱ nkyu a o nuu. Fo̱ ya waa mò̱ ane̱ŋ, fo̱ e̱ ba ipeere a fo̱ bo̱ mɔɔ mò̱.”

ref: Don't let evil defeat you, but defeat evil with good.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ ma sa a e̱bɔye̱ nya mo̱ne̱ se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ. Amaa mo̱nꞌ ba ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ bo̱ gyi e̱bɔye̱ se̱.

ref: Obey the rulers who have authority over you. Only God can give authority to anyone, and he puts these rulers in their places of power.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ɔke̱maa bu bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ de mo̱ne̱ se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ‑ɔ. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱yaale̱ŋ ke̱maa ne̱ se̱sɛ de ɔ de̱e̱re̱ maŋ se̱‑ɔ a le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱ ne̱e̱. Wuribware̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔɔ sa maŋ ayaale̱ŋpo̱‑ɔ ke̱yaale̱ŋ ne̱ bo̱ de‑o.

ref: You must also pay your taxes. The authorities are God's servants, and it is their duty to take care of these matters.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱ ne̱ a be̱e̱ a bware fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱ ka e̱twe̱e̱twe̱e̱ na ileŋpoo. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ de maŋ ke̱yaale̱ŋ‑ɔ akatɔ a pee bamo̱ kusuŋ‑o si. Kusuŋ amo̱ ne̱ ba waa‑ɔ gye̱ Wuribware̱ lee ne̱e̱.

ref: In the Law there are many commands, such as, “Be faithful in marriage. Do not murder. Do not steal. Do not want what belongs to others.” But all of these are summed up in the command that says, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”
text: Mo̱nꞌ nyiŋŋi si fe̱yɛ mbraa‑ɔ yɛ, “Fo̱ ma kaŋ waa kakye̱e̱kpa bɛɛɛ kanyare̱kpa a fo̱ nye̱ra awaare̱yɛ, fo̱ ma kaŋ mɔɔ se̱sɛ, fo̱ ma kaŋ ywii, fo̱ maa kaŋ sa a fo̱ akatɔ pee ɔko̱ atɔ se̱.” Mbraa mɔ na mmo̱ ne̱ ŋ ya saŋ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ katiŋ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ, “Kpa fo̱ bɛɛko̱ ase̱ŋ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kpa fo̱ e̱ye̱e̱‑ɔ.”

ref: No one who loves others will harm them. So love is all that the Law demands. The Day When Christ Returns
text: Mo̱ne̱ i de ke̱kpa mo̱ne̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ, mo̱ne̱ maa waa mo̱ne̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana bɔye̱. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa mo̱ne̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana, a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ gya Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ pɛɛɛ se̱.

ref: You know what sort of times we live in, and so you should live properly. It is time to wake up. You know that the day when we will be saved is nearer now than when we first put our faith in the Lord.
text: Ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ kpa a mo̱nꞌ pini ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ saŋ a fo̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ idi‑ro. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kake ne̱ Kristoo e̱ ba na ɔ bo̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ ane̱‑ɔ a tɔ‑rɔ kyo̱ŋ saŋ ne̱ ane̱ a le̱e̱ bo̱ kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi‑o.

ref: So behave properly, as people do in the day. Don't go to wild parties or get drunk or be vulgar or indecent. Don't quarrel or be jealous.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ane̱ ma de̱e̱ gyi ateese bwe̱e̱tɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ bɛɛɛ ane̱ bwe̱e̱. Ane̱ ma waa kakye̱e̱kpa na kanyare̱kpa bɛɛɛ kipurima. Ane̱ ma kpa e̱kɔ bɛɛɛ ane̱ ka ane̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana ko̱kwe̱e̱.

ref: Let the Lord Jesus Christ be as near to you as the clothes you wear. Then you won't try to satisfy your selfish desires.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱nꞌ waa‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ sa a Kristoo nya mo̱ne̱ se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ, na mo̱ne̱ ma lɛɛ nya fa ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ waa a mo̱nꞌ nya atɔ bɔye̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ mfɛɛre̱.

ref: Welcome all the Lord's followers, even those whose faith is weak. Don't criticize them for having beliefs that are different from yours.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ, mo̱ne̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ ne̱ mò̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi mo̱ŋ bo̱ le̱ŋ‑ɔ ase̱ŋ, ane̱ŋ a se̱sɛ‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ kra mò̱ nɛɛnɛɛ, na mo̱ne̱ aa mò̱ ma kaŋ gyiiri ke̱tɔ ne̱ mò̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi mo̱ŋ bo̱ le̱ŋ ke̱mo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ ase̱ŋ ikii.

ref: Some think it is all right to eat anything, while those whose faith is weak will eat only vegetables.
text: Ɔko̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ o de kpa fe̱yɛ o gyi ke̱tɔ ke̱maa. Ɔko̱ ne̱ mò̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi mo̱ŋ bo̱ le̱ŋ‑ɔ i kisi kiyee ke̱maa.

ref: What right do you have to criticize someone else's servants? Only their Lord can decide if they are doing right, and the Lord will make sure that they do right.
text: Ɔko̱ de kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ po̱rɔ ɔko̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱ aaa? Daabii! Ke̱yaafɔre̱‑ɔ mò̱ nyaŋpe̱ dooo e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ taare̱ a ɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ waa atɔ bɛɛɛ ɔ mo̱ŋ waa atɔ. Kase̱ŋtiŋ si ɔ nya nkyo̱rɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ o de kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ɔɔ waa atɔ.

ref: Some of the Lord's followers think one day is more important than another. Others think all days are the same. But each of you should make up your own mind.
text: Se̱sɛ ko̱ e̱ fa fe̱yɛ kake ko̱ gye̱ kake dabe̱ ka kyɔ kako̱. Ne̱ ɔko̱ mɔ e̱ fa fe̱yɛ ŋke‑o pɛɛɛ ta abɛɛ se̱. Mo̱nꞌ sa a bamo̱ ɔke̱maa taa ke̱mo̱ ne̱ mò̱ mfɛɛre̱ a sa mò̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱ gye̱‑ɔ.

ref: Whether we live or die, it must be for God, rather than for ourselves.
text: N gye̱ ane̱ wo̱re̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ne̱ ane̱ te bo̱ sa. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ane̱ lowi mo̱ŋ gye̱ ane̱ wo̱re̱ lee.

ref: Whether we live or die, it must be for the Lord. Alive or dead, we still belong to the Lord.
text: Ane̱ i te, ane̱ te bo̱ sa ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu ne̱e̱. Ne̱ ane̱ ya wu mɔ, ane̱ a wu bo̱ sa ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ ne̱e̱. Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ hare̱e̱ ane̱ te bɛɛɛ ane̱ a wu ooo, ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ e̱ bo̱ ane̱.

ref: This is because Christ died and rose to life, so that he would be the Lord of the dead and of the living.
text: Ane̱ŋ se̱ ne̱ Kristoo a wu. Oo wu ne̱e̱, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro, na ɔ nya kii bamo̱ ne̱ baa wu‑o na bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ saŋ bo̱ te‑o bamo̱ nyaŋpe̱.

ref: Why do you criticize other followers of the Lord? Why do you look down on them? The day is coming when God will judge all of us.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ŋkee nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ po̱rɔ bɛɛɛ fo̱ i trasii fo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ɔ ne̱e̱? Ŋu fe̱yɛ ane̱ pɛɛɛ e̱ ye̱re̱ Wuribware̱ akatɔ‑rɔ mò̱ se̱ŋgyikpa‑ɔ.

ref: In the Scriptures God says, “I swear by my very life that everyone will kneel down and praise my name!”
text: Ke̱e̱ ɛ, baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, “Wuribware̱ yɛ, Mo̱ a bo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ŋ yɛ, ɔke̱maa i kpuni aŋurii mo̱ ayaa‑rɔ na o sure si fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ gye̱ nyaŋpe̱.”

ref: And so, each of us must give an account to God for what we do. Don't Cause Problems for Others
text: Amo̱‑ɔ e̱ kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ i bise ane̱ ɔke̱maa ase̱ŋ, na ɔke̱maa lee kanɔ sa mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ gywii Wuribware̱. Mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱ nyite abɛɛ

ref: We must stop judging others. We must also make up our minds not to upset anyone's faith.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ de̱e̱ yɔwe̱ abɛɛ ke̱po̱rɔ. Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ ma lɛɛ waa ane̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ Kristoonyi ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ sa a ɔ waa bɔye̱‑ɔ.

ref: The Lord Jesus has made it clear to me that God considers all foods fit to eat. But if you think some foods are unfit to eat, then for you they are not fit.
text: Mo̱ a gye̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ ɔko̱‑ɔ, mo̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ a n gyi fe̱yɛ ko̱tɔko̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ke̱ gye̱ iyisi ke̱mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ se̱. Amaa ne̱ ɔko̱ e̱ fa fe̱yɛ ko̱tɔko̱ gye̱ iyisi atɔ fe̱raa, ke̱ gye̱ bo̱ sa se̱sɛ amo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ne̱e̱.

ref: If you are hurting others by the foods you eat, you are not guided by love. Don't let your appetite destroy someone Christ died for.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ke̱tɔ ne̱ fo̱ i gyi‑o e̱ sa ne̱ fo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ duŋ i fwii fo̱ se̱ fe̱raa, amo̱ fe̱raa a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ fo̱ e̱ kpa mò̱ ase̱ŋ. Fo̱ ma sa a ke̱tɔ ne̱ fo̱ i gyi‑o nye̱ra ɔko̱ ne̱ Kristoo a kperi mò̱ se̱ wu‑o.

ref: God's kingdom isn't about eating and drinking. It is about pleasing God, about living in peace, and about true happiness. All this comes from the Holy Spirit.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ ane̱ kitii Wuribware̱ a kuwure‑o si ase̱ŋ, ke̱tɔ ne̱ ane̱ i gyi bɛɛɛ ane̱ i nuu‑o ase̱ŋ mo̱ŋ tiri. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ki tiri‑o e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ a de ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ na kaye̱e̱yuri na kakatɔgyi ne̱ amo̱ pɛɛɛ a le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o ase̱‑ɔ.

ref: If you serve Christ in this way, you will please God and be respected by people.
text: Ɔko̱ ne̱ o suŋ Yeesuu kpa mɔ se̱‑ɔ ase̱ŋ e̱ pre̱ Wuribware̱, na ase̱sɛ gbaa kyo̱rɔ mò̱.

ref: We should try to live at peace and help each other have a strong faith.
text: Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ le̱ŋ e̱ye̱e̱ waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ baa kaye̱e̱yuri ase̱sɛ kakye̱na‑rɔ‑ɔ na ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ke̱ e̱ sa ne̱ ase̱sɛ e̱ kya abɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: Don't let your appetite destroy what God has done. All foods are fit to eat, but it is wrong to cause problems for others by what you eat.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ma sa a Wuribware̱ kusuŋ nye̱ra bo̱ le̱e̱ ateese ko̱ se̱. A gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ ateese pɛɛɛ mɔ bware. Amaa a mo̱ŋ bware fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ gyi ateese ko̱ ne̱ a sa a mo̱ne̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ Kristoonyi lwee e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: It is best not to eat meat or drink wine or do anything else that causes problems for other followers of the Lord.
text: A mo̱ŋ bware fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ wo̱ kiyee ke̱maa bɛɛɛ mo̱nꞌ nuu nta ke̱maa ne̱ n sa a mo̱ne̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ lwee e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: What you believe about these things should be kept between you and God. You are fortunate, if your actions don't make you have doubts.
text: Ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ amo̱‑ɔ ase̱ŋ mo̱ne̱ i gyi‑o gye̱ mo̱ne̱ aa Wuribware̱ wo̱re̱ kpeŋ mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ ase̱ŋ ne̱e̱. Ɔko̱ ne̱ o gyi ko̱tɔko̱ ne̱ ke̱ maa waa mò̱ mfɛɛre̱‑rɔ fe̱yɛ ke̱ mo̱ŋ bware‑o de ŋyure.

ref: But if you do have doubts about what you eat, you are going against your beliefs. And you know that is wrong, because anything you do against your beliefs is sin.
text: Amaa ke̱tɔ ne̱ fo̱ i gyi‑o, ne̱ fo̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ ki bware bɛɛɛ ke̱ mo̱ŋ bware, amo̱ fe̱raa Wuribware̱ i bu fo̱ ke̱pɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ n gye̱ fo̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi‑o e̱ gye̱ ne̱ kaa sa ne̱ fo̱ i gyi. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ se̱sɛ e̱ ma kɔɔre̱ a o gyi fe̱yɛ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔ waa‑ɔ bware, amo̱ fe̱raa ɔ waa bɔye̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: If our faith is strong, we should be patient with the Lord's followers whose faith is weak. We should try to please them instead of ourselves.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ane̱ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ Kristoo ane̱ i gyi keŋkeŋ‑o de̱e̱ nyite bamo̱ ne̱ bamo̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi mo̱ŋ kyɔ bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ba kisi ko̱tɔko̱ se̱. Na ane̱ de̱e̱ waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ki i gyi bamo̱ akatɔ‑ɔ. Ane̱ ma waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ki i gyi ane̱ wo̱re̱ akatɔ‑ɔ.

ref: God is the one who makes us patient and cheerful. I pray that he will help you live at peace with each other, as you follow Christ.
text: Amo̱ a tɛɛ a gye̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ sa mo̱ne̱ kanyite na wɔre̱‑ɔ, amo̱ fe̱raa ane̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ mò̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ sa a mo̱nꞌ nyite abɛɛ, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Kristoo Yeesuu a nyite bo̱ko̱‑ɔ.

ref: Then all of you together will praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. The Good News Is for Jews and Gentiles
text: Ane̱ŋ‑ɔ na mo̱ne̱ nnɔ nya twɛɛ abɛɛ se̱, na mo̱nꞌ waa kanɔ kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo mò̱ se̱‑ɔ.

ref: I tell you that Christ came as a servant of the Jews to show that God has kept the promises he made to their famous ancestors. Christ also came,
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ Kristoo a ba fe̱yɛ ɔ bo̱ waa kusuŋ bo̱ sa Yudaa awuye‑o ne̱e̱, na ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ ɔ waa a ɔ sa bamo̱ ade̱daapo̱‑ɔ nya waa kase̱ŋtiŋ.

ref: so that the Gentiles would praise God for being kind to them. It is just as the Scriptures say, “I will tell the nations about you, and I will sing praises to your name.”
text: Mfaanɛɛ se̱‑ɔ, na nde̱ se̱ awuye taare̱ kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ oo ŋu bamo̱ e̱wɛɛ‑ɔ se̱. Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱ mò̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, “Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ e̱ kyo̱rɔ fo̱ a n gywii nde̱ se̱ awuye, na ŋ waa iliŋ bo̱ daa fo̱ bamo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ.”

ref: The Scriptures also say to the Gentiles, “Come and celebrate with God's people.”
text: Ne̱ baa be̱e̱ kyo̱rɛɛ fe̱yɛ, “Nde̱ se̱ awuye, mo̱nꞌ bo̱ tii Wuribware̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ se̱ kyo̱rɔ mò̱.”

ref: Again the Scriptures say, “Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles. All you nations, come and worship him.”
text: Ne̱ baa be̱e̱ kyo̱rɛɛ fe̱yɛ, “Nde̱ se̱ awuye eee, mo̱nꞌ ba a ane̱ kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱. Ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ mɔ, mo̱nꞌ waa iliŋ bo̱ daa mò̱.”

ref: Isaiah says, “Someone from David's family will come to power. He will rule the nations, and they will put their hope in him.”
text: Mo̱nꞌ be̱e̱ ke̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Isaya a kyo̱rɛɛ‑ɔ, “Yɛse̱e̱ mò̱ ke̱nanagyi ɔko̱ e̱ ba a ɔ bo̱ ba. Mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ ko̱so̱ na ɔ de̱e̱re̱ nde̱ se̱ awuye pɛɛɛ se̱ na bo̱ bo̱ kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi.”

ref: I pray that God, who gives hope, will bless you with complete happiness and peace because of your faith. And may the power of the Holy Spirit fill you with hope. Paul's Work as a Missionary
text: Mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ mo̱ne̱ i gyi‑o si‑o, Wuribware̱ ne̱ ɔ sa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ne̱ i gyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱ yɔ so̱so̱‑ɔ sa mo̱ne̱ kakatɔgyi bwe̱e̱tɔ na kaye̱e̱yuri, na Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa a ke̱yaale̱ŋ‑ɔ nya sa a mo̱ne̱ te̱maa‑ɔ de̱e̱ tii si bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: Because of Christ Jesus, I can take pride in my service for God.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ n de kpa fe̱yɛ, mo̱ a gye̱ Kristoo se̱sɛ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ŋ kaapo̱ mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ a waa sa Wuribware̱‑ɔ se̱.

ref: But I have always tried to preach where people have never heard about Christ. I am like a builder who doesn't build on someone else's foundation.
text: Mo̱ a kpa ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ saŋ ke̱maa n tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ timaa‑o to̱ŋ ko̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ nu Kristoo ke̱nyare̱ se̱‑ɔ. Mo̱ ya waa ane̱ŋ, saŋ‑ɔ a mo̱ŋ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ a talii ne̱ ɔɔ da‑ɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ pwɛɛ mo̱ e̱ gya se̱.

ref: It is just as the Scriptures say, “All who haven't been told about him will see him, and those who haven't heard about him will understand.” Paul's Plan To Visit Rome
text: Mo̱ e̱ waa amo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, “Na bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ ya tɔwe̱ mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ gywii bamo̱‑ɔ nya ŋu mò̱, na bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ nu‑o mɔ nu amo̱ kaase̱.” Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Pɔɔl a kra fe̱yɛ ɔ be̱e̱ ɔ yɔ‑ɔ

ref: I am now on my way to Jerusalem to deliver the money that the Lord's followers in Macedonia and Achaia collected for God's needy people.
text: Ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ yɔ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ asɔre̱e̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Makedoniya na Akaya e̱swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ a da bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ ba kyo̱ŋwe̱ Kristoo awuye ne̱ ba wu kitiri, ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ atanne̱.

ref: This is something they really wanted to do. But sharing their money with the Jews was also like paying back a debt, because the Jews had already shared their spiritual blessings with the Gentiles.
text: Bamo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ya da bamo̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ se̱ kra ane̱ŋ a ase̱ŋ‑ɔ. Amaa kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱raa, a kyɔ a tiri fe̱yɛ bo̱ sa Yudaa awuye atiripo̱ amo̱ ko̱tɔko̱. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ nde̱ se̱ awuye‑o a bo̱rɔ Yudaa awuye si nya ŋyure ne̱ ŋ ya le̱e̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ neŋfo a bware fe̱yɛ bo̱ be̱e̱ sa bamo̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ atɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a bo̱ yure bamo̱‑ɔ ako̱.

ref: After I have safely delivered this money, I will visit you and then go on to Spain.
text: Mo̱ ya ŋu fe̱yɛ atanne̱‑ɔ a lwee bamo̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ, na ŋkee, n kya se̱ yɔ Spaniya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱. Amaa mo̱ e̱ yɔ, mo̱ e̱ bo̱rɔ a ŋ ka mo̱ne̱ kanɔ.

ref: And when I do arrive in Rome, I know it will be with the full blessings of Christ.
text: Ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ ya bo̱ ka mo̱ne̱ kanɔ, a waa dɛɛ n de Kristoo ŋyure pɛɛɛ a m baa mo̱ne̱.

ref: My friends, by the power of the Lord Jesus Christ and by the love that comes from the Holy Spirit, I beg you to pray sincerely with me and for me.
text: Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, mo̱ne̱ a gya ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a sa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa mo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ mo̱ne̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱ kya mo̱‑rɔ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ keŋkeŋ sa mo̱.

ref: Pray that God will protect me from the unbelievers in Judea, and that his people in Jerusalem will be pleased with what I am doing.
text: Mo̱nꞌ gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ lee mo̱ le̱e̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Yude̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱, ne̱ bo̱ maa kɔɔre̱ Kristoo ba gyi‑o ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ. Na mo̱nꞌ ko̱re̱ nyɔse̱po̱ fe̱yɛ Yɛro̱salɛm asɔre̱e̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ kɔɔre̱ atanne̱ ne̱ n de mo̱ e̱ yaa bamo̱‑ɔ.

ref: Ask God to let me come to you and have a pleasant and refreshing visit.
text: Mo̱ ya waa amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ lo̱we̱, mo̱ mfɛɛre̱ i di mo̱. Na Wuribware̱ ya sure, m ba mo̱ne̱ ase̱ bo̱ ka mo̱ne̱ kanɔ kyɛɛkyɛɛ, na ane̱ waa abɛɛ wɔre̱.

ref: I pray that God, who gives peace, will be with all of you. Amen.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ, mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ kaye̱e̱yuri sapo̱‑ɔ Wuribware̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ gya mo̱ne̱ pɛɛɛ kamɛɛ. Amɛye̱.

ref: Welcome her in a way that is proper for someone who has faith in the Lord and is one of God's own people. Help her in any way you can. After all, she has proved to be a respected leader for many others, including me.
text: Mo̱nꞌ kra mò̱ nɛɛnɛɛ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ a bware bo̱ sa Wuribware̱ ase̱sɛ fe̱yɛ bo̱ waa‑ɔ. Na, mò̱ e̱ kpa ke̱tɔ ke̱maa bo̱ le̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ ase̱, mo̱nꞌ kya mò̱‑rɔ na ɔ nya ke̱mo̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ gye̱ nyare̱ timaa ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ ase̱ na mo̱ ase̱.

ref: Give my greetings to Priscilla and Aquila. They have not only served Christ Jesus together with me,
text: Mo̱ e̱ ka Prisikila na Akwila ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ mo̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana, ne̱ ane̱ e̱ waa Kristoo Yeesuu kusuŋ‑o kanɔ.

ref: but they have even risked their lives for me. I am grateful for them and so are all the Gentile churches.
text: Baa kperi mo̱ se̱ bo̱ dee a bo̱ mɔɔ bamo̱. N gye̱ mo̱ wo̱re̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ sa bamo̱ aŋsɛ. Nde̱ se̱ awuye‑o‑ro asɔre̱e̱ awuye pɛɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ i suŋ mo̱ e̱ sa‑ɔ gbaa e̱ sa bamo̱ aŋsɛ na ko̱waa.

ref: Greet the church that meets in their home. Greet my dear friend Epaenetus, who was the first person in Asia to have faith in Christ.
text: Mo̱ e̱ ka ase̱sɛ ne̱ ba gyaŋŋe̱ ba waa asɔre̱e̱ bamo̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ kanɔ. Mo̱ e̱ ka mo̱ nyare̱ timaa Ipeenitus kanɔ. Mò̱ ya gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ kii Kristoonyi Asiya swe̱e̱re̱ pɛɛɛ se̱.

ref: Greet Mary, who has worked so hard for you.
text: Mo̱ e̱ ka Mariya ne̱ oo suŋ keŋkeŋ sa mo̱ne̱‑ɔ kanɔ.

ref: Greet my relatives Andronicus and Junia, who were in jail with me. They are highly respected by the apostles and were followers of Christ before I was.
text: Mo̱ e̱ ka mo̱ ko̱we̱bɛɛ‑ana Andrunikus na ɔkye̱e̱ Yuniya kanɔ. Mo̱ aa bamo̱ ne̱ baa dɛɛ tii saŋ ko̱‑ɔ. Bo̱ gye̱ Kristoo ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ bo̱ko̱, ne̱ bo̱ de ke̱nyare̱ timaa. Baa daa mo̱ gbaa ikii Kristoo awuye.

ref: Greet Ampliatus, my dear friend whose faith is in the Lord.
text: Mo̱ e̱ ka Ampriyas kanɔ. Ɔ gye̱ mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ Kristoonyi, ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa mò̱ ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: Greet Urbanus, who serves Christ along with us. Greet my dear friend Stachys.
text: Mo̱ e̱ ka Orabanus, mo̱ bɛɛko̱ ne̱ mo̱ aa mò̱ mpe̱pe̱yɔ a waa Kristoo kusuŋ‑o kanɔ. Mo̱ e̱ ka mo̱ nyare̱ timaa Staakis kanɔ.

ref: Greet Herodion, who is a relative of mine. Greet Narcissus and the others in his family, who have faith in the Lord.
text: Mo̱ e̱ ka mo̱ ko̱we̱bɛɛ Hɛrɔde̱yɔŋ kanɔ. Mo̱ e̱ ka Naakisus lɔŋ‑nɔ awuye ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo ase̱sɛ‑ɔ kanɔ.

ref: Greet Rufus, that special servant of the Lord, and greet his mother, who has been like a mother to me.
text: Mo̱ e̱ ka Rufus kanɔ dame̱naŋsɛ. Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ a lee mò̱ kpa mò̱ ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Mo̱ e̱ ka mò̱ nyi gbaa kanɔ. Ɔɔ kra mo̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ n na e̱ kra mo̱‑ɔ.

ref: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, and Hermas, as well as our friends who are with them.
text: Mo̱ e̱ ka ase̱sɛ mɔ kanɔ, Asiŋkritus na Fe̱lɛɛgɔŋ na Hɛɛmɛs na Patrobas na Hɛɛmas na ane̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana pɛɛɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ bamo̱ ase̱‑ɔ.

ref: Greet Philologus, Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all of God's people who are with them.
text: M be̱e̱ mo̱ e̱ ka ase̱sɛ mɔ gbaa kanɔ, Filologus na Yuliya na Niriyus na mò̱ pe̱kye̱e̱ na Olimpas na Wuribware̱ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ bamo̱ ase̱‑ɔ.

ref: Be sure to give each other a warm greeting. All of Christ's churches greet you.
text: Ŋkee, mo̱nꞌ ka abɛɛ kanɔ dame̱naŋsɛ sa mo̱. Kristoo asɔre̱e̱ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ e̱ ka mo̱ne̱ kanɔ. Pɔɔl a ase̱ŋ‑ɔ lalalo̱we̱

ref: My friends, I beg you to watch out for anyone who causes trouble and divides the church by refusing to do what all of you were taught. Stay away from them!
text: Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ mo̱ne̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ sa ase̱ŋ awuye‑o si. Mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱ kwaye̱ bamo̱ ne̱ ba nye̱ra ase̱sɛ mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ ba sa a mo̱nꞌ turi ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ne̱ i gyi‑o‑ro‑o, na bamo̱ ne̱ ba kaapo̱ atɔ ne̱ a kye amo̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a kyɔ suye‑o.

ref: They want to serve themselves and not Christ the Lord. Their flattery and fancy talk fool people who don't know any better.
text: N gye̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ ne̱ ane̱ŋ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ i suŋ. Bo̱ gya ke̱tɔ ne̱ bamo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ e̱ kpa‑ɔ se̱ ne̱e̱. Bo̱ de bamo̱ nnɔrɔkɔne̱‑ɔ ba pe̱nna ase̱sɛ ne̱ bamo̱ mfɛɛre̱ maa krɛɛ ikyurowi‑o ne̱e̱.

ref: I am glad that everyone knows how well you obey the Lord. But still, I want you to understand what is good and not have anything to do with evil.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ a bo̱ mo̱ kɔne̱ fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa a kyɔ nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ fe̱raa mo̱ne̱ ye̱re̱‑rɔ keŋkeŋ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ gya abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ. Amaa mo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ kanyiase̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ de‑o sa a mo̱nꞌ gya ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ gye̱ kitimaa‑o si, na mo̱nꞌ kwaye̱ ko̱waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ gye̱ bɔye̱‑ɔ.

ref: Then God, who gives peace, will soon crush Satan under your feet. I pray that our Lord Jesus will be kind to you.
text: A saŋ kafwe̱e̱, kaye̱e̱yuri sapo̱‑ɔ Wuribware̱ e̱ sa a mo̱nꞌ kyikye Ɔbɔnsam se̱. Mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu yure mo̱ne̱.

ref: Timothy, who works with me, sends his greetings, and so do my relatives, Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater.
text: Mo̱ bɛɛko̱ Timotii ne̱ mo̱ aa mò̱ mpe̱pe̱yɔ e̱ waa Kristoo kusuŋ‑o e̱ ka mo̱ne̱ kanɔ. Mo̱ ko̱we̱bɛɛ‑ana Lukiyus na Yasɔŋ na Sosipata gbaa e̱ ka mo̱ne̱ kanɔ.

ref: I, Tertius, also send my greetings. I am a follower of the Lord, and I wrote this letter.
text: Mo̱, Tɛrɛtiyus, ne̱ mo̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ wo̱re̱ mɔ sa Pɔɔl‑ɔ e̱ ka mo̱ne̱ kanɔ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ.

ref: Praise God! He can make you strong by means of my good news, which is the message about Jesus Christ. For ages and ages this message was kept secret,
text: Ŋkee mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ taare̱ na ɔ sa mo̱ne̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ na mo̱nꞌ bo̱ kya se̱ kɔɔre̱ ase̱ŋ timaa bo̱ le̱e̱ Yeesuu Kristoo kuŋu si ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ gyi. Ase̱ŋ timaa mɔ, Wuribware̱ a taa amo̱ bo̱ kwe̱e̱rɔ hare̱e̱ pwɛɛ ne̱ ɔɔ dɛɛ twe̱e̱ kaye̱.

ref: but now at last it has been told. The eternal God commanded his prophets to write about the good news, so that all nations would obey and have faith.
text: Amaa mbe̱yɔmɔ Wuribware̱, mò̱ ne̱ o te nsu pɛɛɛ‑ɔ, a sa kanɔ fe̱yɛ ase̱ŋ timaa mɔ ne̱ mò̱ akyaamɛɛ ne̱ baa dɛɛ kye̱na‑ɔ a be̱e̱ kyo̱rɛɛ amo̱ ase̱ŋ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ le̱e̱ ifuri, na kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ nya nu amo̱ ase̱ŋ, na bo̱ kɔɔre̱ gyi, na bo̱ gya amo̱ se̱.

ref: And now, because of Jesus Christ, we can praise the only wise God forever! Amen.
text: Wuribware̱ gye̱ ɔko̱ŋko̱ ne̱e̱ kpeŋ. Mò̱ dooo e̱ gye̱ Kanyiase̱ŋ-pɛɛɛ-kuŋu-Bware̱. Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ bo̱rɔ Yeesuu Kristoo si bu Wuribware̱ ke̱dabe̱ nsu pɛɛɛ. Amɛye̱.

ref: My prayer is that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
text: Ane̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ se̱ Wuribware̱ na ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo yure mo̱ne̱, na bo̱ sa mo̱ne̱ kaye̱e̱yuri. Ŋyure ne̱ Korintoo awuye a kyɔ nya‑ɔ

ref: I never stop thanking my God for treating you with undeserved grace by giving you Christ Jesus,
text: Mo̱ne̱ Korintoo awuye si, mo̱ e̱ sa Wuribware̱ aŋsɛ saŋ ke̱maa, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a bo̱rɔ Kristoo Yeesuu si bo̱ yure mo̱ne̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: God can be trusted, and he chose you to be partners with his Son, our Lord Jesus Christ. Taking Sides
text: To, Wuribware̱ a de̱ŋ te̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ waa mò̱ gyi, ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo, a ko̱ko̱we̱bɛɛ‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ane̱ e̱ taare̱ a ane̱ kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi fe̱yɛ ɔ maa yɔwe̱ mo̱ne̱ a ɔ nare̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ asɔre̱e̱ agyi a keke‑ro‑o

ref: My dear friends, as a follower of our Lord Jesus Christ, I beg you to get along with each other. Don't take sides. Always try to agree in what you think.
text: To, mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, n de ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo ke̱nyare̱ mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ maa keke‑ro, amaa mo̱nꞌ waa ko̱ko̱ŋko̱, na mo̱nꞌ waa kanɔ ko̱ŋko̱ mo̱ne̱ mfɛɛre̱ na ase̱ŋ ke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ fa amo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: Several people from Chloe's family have already reported to me that you keep arguing with each other.
text: Ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ɔkye̱e̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Kilowi a ako̱we̱bɛɛ‑ɔ a bo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ wo̱re̱ wo̱re̱ e̱ kɔ.

ref: Has Christ been divided up? Was I nailed to a cross for you? Were you baptized in my name?
text: Abaa! E̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ ba waa a bo̱ ke Kristoo‑ro kyikpuri kyikpuri? Mo̱, Pɔɔl, ne̱ baa da bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱ sa mo̱ne̱ aaa? Bɛɛɛ mo̱, Pɔɔl, ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ ne̱ baa bɔ mo̱ne̱ asuu aaa? Daabii!

ref: I thank God that I didn't baptize any of you except Crispus and Gaius.
text: Mo̱ e̱ sa Wuribware̱ aŋsɛ fe̱yɛ mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ a mo̱ŋ bɔ mo̱ne̱‑rɔ ɔko̱ asuu, amɔ Krisipus na Gayus.

ref: Not one of you can say that you were baptized in my name.
text: Fe̱yɛ mo̱ a dɛɛ bɔ mo̱ne̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ asuu ne̱e̱, weetee mo̱ne̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ de̱e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ke̱nyare̱ ne̱ mo̱ a bo̱ bɔ mo̱ne̱ asuu.

ref: I did baptize the family of Stephanas, but I don't remember if I baptized anyone else.
text: O, mo̱ a nyiŋŋi si fe̱yɛ mo̱ a bɔ Stifaŋ na mò̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ awuye asuu, amaa ma taare̱ a ŋ nyiŋŋi si fe̱yɛ mo̱ a bɔ bo̱ko̱ mɔ asuu bɛɛɛ mo̱ŋ bɔ.

ref: As God says in the Scriptures, “I will destroy the wisdom of all who claim to be wise. I will confuse those who think they know so much.”
text: Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ, Wuribware̱ yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ nye̱ra kanyiase̱ŋ ne̱ anyiase̱ŋpo̱‑ɔ de‑o, mo̱ e̱ sa a bamo̱ atɔ kinyi pɛɛɛ waa kwaa.”

ref: What happened to those wise people? What happened to those experts in the Scriptures? What happened to the ones who think they have all the answers? Didn't God show that the wisdom of this world is foolish?
text: Amo̱ a gye̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ, kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ a anyiase̱ŋpo̱ na ikii agyiiripo̱ timaa‑o na mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ a kii fe̱yɛ bamo̱ a mo̱ŋ nyi ase̱ŋ‑ɔ.

ref: God was wise and decided not to let the people of this world use their wisdom to learn about him. Instead, God chose to save only those who believe the foolish message we preach.
text: Aa pre̱ Wuribware̱ ne̱ o nyi ase̱ŋ ɔ kyɔ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ ma ba bamo̱ kanyiase̱ŋ a bo̱ bo̱ gyii mò̱. Amaa fe̱raa aa gyi Wuribware̱ akatɔ fe̱yɛ mò̱ a ba abware̱se̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ ne̱ anyiase̱ŋpo̱‑ɔ yɛ a mo̱ŋ de kaase̱‑ɔ bo̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa bo̱ kɔɔre̱ Kristoo gyi‑o.

ref: Even when God is foolish, he is wiser than everyone else, and even when God is weak, he is stronger than everyone else.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, ɔko̱ ya fa fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ mo̱ŋ nyi ase̱ŋ ooo, mò̱‑ɔ mò̱ kanyiase̱ŋ kyɔ nyiŋkpasɛ lee‑o. Ne̱ ɔko̱ ya fa fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱ le̱ŋ ooo, mò̱‑ɔ mò̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ kyɔ nyiŋkpasɛ lee.

ref: My dear friends, remember what you were when God chose you. The people of this world didn't think that many of you were wise. Only a few of you were in places of power, and not many of you came from important families.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ, mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, mo̱nꞌ nyiŋŋi ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ dɛɛ mo̱ne̱ du saŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a te̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ bo̱ kii mò̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ se̱. Mo̱ne̱‑rɔ kafwe̱e̱ ko̱ ne̱ ase̱sɛ e̱ te̱e̱ fe̱yɛ anyiase̱ŋpo̱. Mo̱ne̱‑rɔ kafwe̱e̱ ko̱ ne̱ baa sa ke̱yaale̱ŋ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ. Mo̱ne̱‑rɔ kafwe̱e̱ ko̱ e̱ gye̱ awure ako̱we̱bɛɛ.

ref: But God chose the foolish things of this world to put the wise to shame. He chose the weak things of this world to put the powerful to shame.
text: Amaa Wuribware̱ a ba atɔ ne̱ a bo̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ ase̱sɛ e̱ fa amo̱ fe̱yɛ a mo̱ŋ de kaase̱‑ɔ bo̱ sa ne̱ ipeere a nya anyiase̱ŋpo̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ ba atɔ ne̱ a bo̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ ase̱sɛ yɛ a mo̱ŋ de e̱le̱ŋ‑ɔ bo̱ sa ne̱ ipeere a nya ayaale̱ŋpo̱‑ɔ.

ref: God did all this to keep anyone from bragging to him.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ɔko̱ maa taare̱ a ɔ kaapo̱ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ Wuribware̱ akatɔ‑rɔ.

ref: So if you want to brag, do what the Scriptures say and brag about the Lord.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, “Sa a ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ kaapo̱ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱‑ɔ kaapo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ fe̱yɛ o nyi mo̱, Wuribware̱.”

ref: Friends, when I came and told you the mystery that God had shared with us, I didn't use big words or try to sound wise.
text: Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, m fe̱raa, mo̱ a bo̱ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ gywii mo̱ne̱‑ɔ, mo̱ŋ ba ase̱ŋkpare̱gyi dabe̱ dabe̱ na kanyiase̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ a bo̱ tɔwe̱.

ref: In fact, while I was with you, I made up my mind to speak only about Jesus Christ, who had been nailed to a cross.
text: Amaa mo̱ a fa mfɛɛre̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a bo̱ mo̱ne̱ ase̱‑ɔ, mo̱ e̱ ba mo̱ mfɛɛre̱ a m bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ Yeesuu Kristoo dooo si na ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ oo wu kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱‑ɔ se̱.

ref: At first, I was weak and trembling with fear.
text: Mo̱ a ba mo̱ne̱ ase̱‑ɔ na mo̱ kayo̱wɔre̱‑rɔ du mo̱ yɔre̱yɔre̱, na mo̱ e̱ fa mfɛɛre̱ kufu si.

ref: When I talked with you or preached, I didn't try to prove anything by sounding wise. I simply let God's Spirit show his power.
text: Mo̱ a kaapo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ, mo̱ŋ ba nyiŋkpasɛ kanyiase̱ŋ na ase̱ŋkpare̱gyi ne̱ a po̱pɔre̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱. Amaa mo̱ se̱ŋsa sasɛ‑rɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a lee mò̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ‑ɔ bo̱ po̱pɔre̱ mo̱ne̱.

ref: That way you would have faith because of God's power and not because of human wisdom.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ a bo̱ kɔɔre̱ Kristoo gyi‑o, mo̱ne̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi‑o mo̱ŋ ye̱re̱ nyiŋkpasɛ kanyiase̱ŋ se̱ ne̱e̱, amaa Wuribware̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ se̱ ne̱ ke̱ ye̱re̱.

ref: We do use wisdom when speaking to people who are mature in their faith. But it isn't the wisdom of this world or of its rulers, who will soon disappear.
text: Amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ gbaa ooo, ase̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ ane̱ i gywii bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ akɔɔre̱gyipo̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o gye̱ kanyiase̱ŋse̱ŋ kase̱ŋtiŋ. Amaa a mo̱ŋ gye̱ kanyiase̱ŋ ne̱ ka gye̱ ndɔɔ a kaye̱ mɔ lee‑o ne̱e̱, bɛɛɛ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ ndɔɔ‑ɔ lee‑o ne̱e̱. Ane̱ŋ a kanyiase̱ŋ ne̱ ka du fe̱yɛ bamo̱ lee amo̱‑ɔ maa lɔŋŋɔ sɛye̱.

ref: The rulers of this world didn't know anything about this wisdom. If they had known about it, they would not have nailed the glorious Lord to a cross.
text: Ndɔɔ a kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ a ayaale̱ŋpo̱‑ɔ ɔko̱ŋko̱ gbaa mo̱ŋ nyi ane̱ŋ a kanyiase̱ŋ‑ɔ. Bo̱ dɛɛ bo̱ nyi Wuribware̱ a kanyiase̱ŋ amo̱ ne̱e̱‑ɔ, weetee ba pini Yeesuu fe̱yɛ mò̱ e̱ gye̱ kaye̱‑rɔ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ nyaŋpe̱ dabe̱, ne̱ bo̱ maa da mò̱ a bo̱ bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱.

ref: But it is just as the Scriptures say, “What God has planned for people who love him is more than eyes have seen or ears have heard. It has never even entered our minds!”
text: Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ ko̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ, “Ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ ŋu, ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ nu, ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ pini ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a waa siraa sa bamo̱ ne̱ ba kpa mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ.”

ref: God's Spirit has shown you everything. His Spirit finds out everything, even what is deep in the mind of God.
text: Ane̱ŋ a atɔ‑ɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a bo̱rɔ mò̱ kufwiiŋe timaa‑o si lee amo̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ ane̱. Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o e̱ de̱e̱re̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa‑rɔ. Wuribware̱ mfɛɛre̱ ne̱ ŋ kwe̱e̱rɔ‑ɔ gbaa, mò̱ kufwiiŋe‑o e̱ de̱e̱re̱‑rɔ.

ref: You are the only one who knows what is in your own mind, and God's Spirit is the only one who knows what is in God's mind.
text: Nsɛ i nyi nyiŋkpasɛ mfɛɛre̱, amɔ se̱sɛ amo̱ kufwiiŋe. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o wo̱re̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ taare̱ a o pini Wuribware̱ mfɛɛre̱.

ref: But God has given us his Spirit. This is why we don't think the same way that the people of this world think. This is also why we can recognize the blessings God has given us.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ n gye̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ a kanyiase̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱ ane̱ de ane̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ, amaa Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ ane̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ taare̱ ŋu atɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a sa ane̱ mò̱ ke̱pre̱ se̱‑ɔ.

ref: People who are guided by the Spirit can make all kinds of judgments, but they cannot be judged by others.
text: Se̱sɛ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o te mò̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ e̱ taare̱ a o nu atɔ mɔ pɛɛɛ kaase̱, amaa ɔko̱ ne̱ mò̱ kufwiiŋe‑o mo̱ŋ te mò̱‑rɔ‑ɔ maa taare̱ a o ŋu ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ bo̱ ane̱ŋ a se̱sɛ‑ɔ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: The Scriptures ask, “Has anyone ever known the thoughts of the Lord or given him advice?” But we understand what Christ is thinking.
text: Baa kyo̱rɛɛ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, “Nsɛ i nyi Wuribware̱ mfɛɛre̱, na ɔ taare̱ a ɔ ka mò̱ ase̱ŋ?” Amaa ane̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o te ane̱‑rɔ‑ɔ fe̱raa, ane̱ e̱ taare̱ a ane̱ ŋu Kristoo mfɛɛre̱.

ref: My friends, you are acting like the people of this world. That's why I could not speak to you as spiritual people. You are like babies as far as your faith in Christ is concerned.
text: Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, mo̱ a gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ ba mo̱ne̱ ase̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ, mo̱ŋ taare̱ sa se̱ŋsa gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ sa se̱ŋsa mo̱ i gywii ase̱sɛ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o bo̱ bamo̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ. Mo̱ a sa se̱ŋsa gywii mo̱ne̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ a gye̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ase̱sɛ ne̱ baa de̱ŋ ko̱we̱ bamo̱ waa Kristoo ko̱ko̱we̱bɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: because you are not yet spiritual. You are jealous and argue with each other. This proves you are not spiritual and you are acting like the people of this world.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ saŋ mo̱ne̱ gya atɔ ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Kristoo awuye‑o e̱ waa‑ɔ se̱. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ko̱kwe̱e̱ na e̱kɔ bo̱ mo̱ne̱ mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ. Amo̱‑ɔ maa kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ saŋ mo̱ne̱ bo̱ kaye̱ mɔ kaase̱, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ gya kamo̱ ase̱ŋ se̱ aaa?

ref: The one who plants is just as important as the one who waters. And each one will be paid for what they do.
text: Mò̱ ne̱ o dwii‑o na mò̱ ne̱ ɔ we̱e̱ se̱ nkyu‑o de kanɔ ko̱ŋko̱ bamo̱ asuŋ‑no ne̱e̱. Bamo̱ ɔke̱maa e̱ nya mò̱ kakɔka ane̱ŋ ne̱ oo suŋ bo̱rɔ‑ɔ se̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: Apollos and I work together for God, and you are God's garden and God's building. Only One Foundation
text: Ane̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ asuŋpo̱, ne̱ ane̱ pɛɛɛ i suŋ mò̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑rɔ. Mo̱ne̱ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ.

ref: Whatever we build on this foundation will be tested by fire on the day of judgment. Then everyone will find out if we have used gold, silver, and precious stones, or wood, hay, and straw.
text: Saŋ ko̱ e̱ ba a ɔ bo̱ ba ne̱ ane̱ pɛɛɛ i ŋu ate̱tɔmo̱ŋta ne̱ ase̱sɛ a bo̱ pwɛɛ‑ɔ. Saŋ amo̱ e̱ gye̱ kake nsi ne̱ Kristoo i kiŋŋi a ɔ ba‑ɔ. Kake nsi amo̱ ɔ ba de̱e̱kpa ne̱e̱ ɔ bo̱ kyɔ nyiŋkpasɛ ke̱maa kusuŋ‑no ke̱e̱.

ref: But if it is destroyed by the fire, we will lose everything. Yet we ourselves will be saved, like someone escaping from flames.
text: Amaa ne̱ de̱e̱kpa‑ɔ ya kywɛɛ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ pwɛɛ‑ɔ, amo̱ fe̱raa oo suŋ kwaa ne̱e̱. Amaa mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ fe̱raa, Wuribware̱ i lee mò̱. O du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔnyare̱ ne̱ mò̱ lɔŋ pɛɛɛ a kywɛɛ, ne̱ ɔɔ se̱re̱ le̱e̱ lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ ɔ kpa mò̱ kuŋu ŋkpa‑ɔ.

ref: All of you surely know you are God's temple and his Spirit lives in you.
text: Mo̱nꞌ sa a m be̱e̱ taa fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ Kristoo awuye pɛɛɛ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa ne̱ mò̱ kufwiiŋe timaa‑o te‑ro‑o.

ref: Don't fool yourselves! If any of you think you are wise in the things of this world, you will have to become foolish before you can be truly wise.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ ma sa a ɔko̱ pe̱nna mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱. Ɔko̱ e̱ bo̱ mo̱ne̱‑rɔ ne̱ ɔ fa fe̱yɛ o nyi ase̱ŋ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ, amo̱ fe̱raa, se̱sɛ‑ɔ yuri e̱ye̱e̱, na o kii fe̱yɛ kayaagyi‑o, na ɔ nya kii onyiase̱ŋpo̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ si.

ref: This is because God considers the wisdom of this world to be foolish. It is just as the Scriptures say, “God catches the wise when they try to outsmart him.”
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱tɔ ne̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ase̱sɛ yɛ ke̱ gye̱ kanyiase̱ŋ‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ fe̱raa maa te̱e̱ ke̱mo̱ ɛ kanyiase̱ŋ. Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ, “Ne̱ ɔ kra bo̱ko̱ ne̱ bo̱ yɛ bamo̱ akatɔ a te̱ŋ‑ɔ mò̱ katɛɛ‑rɔ.”

ref: The Scriptures also say, “The Lord knows that the plans made by wise people are useless.”
text: Baa be̱e̱ kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, “Wuribware̱ nyi anyiase̱ŋpo̱ mfɛɛre̱, ne̱ o nyi fe̱yɛ m maa waa ko̱tɔko̱.”

ref: So stop bragging about what anyone has done. Paul and Apollos and Peter all belong to you. In fact, everything is yours, including the world, life, death, the present, and the future. Everything belongs to you,
text: Mo̱, Pɔɔl, na Apolos, na Peetroo, ane̱ pɛɛɛ i kperi mo̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱e̱ ane̱ e̱ waa Wuribware̱ kusuŋ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ kaye̱ mɔ pɛɛɛ bo̱‑rɔ bo̱ sa mo̱ne̱ ne̱e̱. Hare̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ te bɛɛɛ mo̱ne̱ i wu ooo, hare̱e̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ bɛɛɛ kamɛɛ‑rɔ ooo, ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ki te‑o te bo̱ sa mo̱ne̱.

ref: and you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God.
text: Ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ a du ane̱ŋ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ Kristoo e̱ bo̱ mo̱ne̱, ne̱ Wuribware̱ mɔ e̱ bo̱ Kristoo.

ref: Think of us as servants of Christ who have been given the work of explaining God's mysterious ways.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, ane̱ Kristoo akɔɔre̱gyipo̱ pɛɛɛ a bo̱ Kristoo kaase̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ e̱ kpa a mo̱nꞌ fa mo̱, Pɔɔl, na Apolos mfɛɛre̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ e̱ gye̱ Kristoo ayaafɔre̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a lee mò̱ akwe̱e̱rɔtɔ bo̱ kaapo̱‑ɔ.

ref: And since our first duty is to be faithful to the one we work for,
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ko̱ kyɔ, ne̱ ki tii si‑o e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ a ayaafɔre̱‑ɔ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ e̱ taare̱ a ɔ kɔɔre̱ bamo̱ gyi.

ref: it doesn't matter to me if I am judged by you or even by a court of law. In fact, I don't judge myself.
text: Amaa m fe̱raa, kanɔ ŋke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ bɛɛɛ bo̱ko̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ kuŋu si ase̱ŋ‑ɔ a mo̱ŋ tiri mo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Kase̱ŋtiŋ si ma gyi mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ase̱ŋ.

ref: I don't know of anything against me, but this doesn't prove I am right. The Lord is my judge.
text: Mo̱ŋ nyi ke̱tɔ ne̱ ba taare̱ a bo̱ po̱rɔ mo̱ ke̱mo̱ se̱‑ɔ. Amaa amo̱‑ɔ maa kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ ne̱ o gyi mo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Kristoo.

ref: Friends, I have used Apollos and myself as examples to teach you the meaning of the saying, “Follow the rules.” I want you to stop saying one of us is better than the other.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ, mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, mo̱ aa Apolos ke̱nyare̱ ne̱ mo̱ a bo̱ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ bo̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱, na mo̱nꞌ nya ŋu fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ ma waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ kyo̱ŋ se̱. Mfaanɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ ɔko̱ maa kaapo̱ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ mò̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ ko̱ se̱, na o bu ɔko̱ mɔ tosoo.

ref: What is so special about you? What do you have that you were not given? And if it was given to you, how can you brag?
text: Ne̱ nte̱tɔ gbaa e̱ gye̱ ne̱ a sa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ bo̱ko̱ yɛ mo̱ne̱ kyɔ bo̱ko̱? N gye̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a nya ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ de‑o le̱e̱ aaa? Ɔɔŋ. Mò̱ ase̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a nya ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ de‑o. Ne̱ e̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱?

ref: Even today we go hungry and thirsty and don't have anything to wear except rags. We are mistreated and don't have a place to live.
text: Bo̱ fo̱ ndɔɔ a kake mɔ, ane̱ i wu ako̱ŋ na burufo ne̱e̱. Aprakɛɛ ne̱ ane̱ i buŋ. Ase̱sɛ e̱ da ane̱. Ane̱ mo̱ŋ de ane̱ fɔŋfɔŋ e̱lɔŋ.

ref: We work hard with our own hands, and when people abuse us, we wish them well. When we suffer, we are patient.
text: Ane̱ fɔŋfɔŋ asare̱e̱ ne̱ ane̱ de ane̱ i suŋ ane̱ e̱ kpa atanne̱ ane̱ i gyi. Ase̱sɛ ya saare̱ ane̱, amɔ ane̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ Wuribware̱ fe̱yɛ o yure bamo̱. Bamo̱ e̱ waa ane̱ awo̱re̱fɔɔ, amɔ ane̱ e̱ waa kanyite.

ref: When someone curses us, we answer with kind words. Until now we are thought of as nothing more than the trash and garbage of this world.
text: Ase̱sɛ ya sa se̱ŋsa bo̱ kye ane̱, amɔ ane̱ e̱ baa ane̱ e̱ye̱e̱ kaase̱ ane̱ i lee kanɔ. Ane̱ a kii akpafɛɛ ase̱sɛ ase̱ bo̱ fo̱ ndɔɔ a kake‑o.

ref: I am not writing to embarrass you. I want to help you, just as parents help their own dear children.
text: Mo̱ŋ kyo̱rɛɛ atɔ mɔ bo̱ waa mo̱ne̱ ipeere ne̱e̱. Amaa mo̱ a waa ne̱e̱ a m bo̱ yii mo̱ne̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ gyi‑ana ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa bamo̱ ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ.

ref: Ten thousand people may teach you about Christ, but I am your only father. You became my children when I told you about Christ Jesus,
text: Ŋ nyi dame̱naŋsɛ fe̱yɛ akaapo̱po̱ bo̱ mo̱ne̱‑rɔ, na bo̱ nyi ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Amaa hare̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ i de akaapo̱po̱ ŋkpe̱ŋ ŋkpe̱ŋ ne̱ ba kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ Kristoo ase̱ŋ‑ɔ gbaa, mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ de ase̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Kase̱ŋtiŋ si mo̱ ya kii mo̱ne̱ se̱ Kristoo ko̱ko̱we̱bɛɛ‑rɔ nsaŋ ne̱ mo̱ a bo̱ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ gywii mo̱ne̱‑ɔ.

ref: and I want you to be like me.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ mo̱ne̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ kase̱ mo̱ kakye̱na‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: This is why I sent Timothy to you. I love him like a son, and he is a faithful servant of the Lord. Timothy will tell you what I do to follow Christ and how it agrees with what I always teach about Christ in every church.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ i suŋ Timotii mo̱ e̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ mo̱ne̱. A du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ a gye̱ mo̱ gyi ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa mo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ Kristoo ko̱we̱bɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ mò̱ mo̱ i gyi. Mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ o nyiŋŋi mo̱ne̱ se̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ n te Kristoo kakye̱na‑rɔ‑ɔ. Mo̱ kakye̱na amo̱ na kamo̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ asɔre̱e̱ asɔre̱e̱‑rɔ to̱ŋ ke̱maa‑ɔ gye̱ kako̱ŋko̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: Some of you think I am not coming for a visit, and so you are bragging.
text: Mo̱ne̱ bo̱ko̱ e̱ fa fe̱yɛ ma lɛɛ kiŋŋi a m ba mo̱ne̱ ase̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱ne̱ a waa akatɔle̱ŋ.

ref: But if the Lord lets me come, I will soon be there. Then I will find out if the ones who are doing all this bragging really have any power.
text: Amaa ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ ya sure si, a maa kyee mo̱ e̱ ba mo̱ne̱ ase̱. Mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱ mo̱ i ŋu akatɔle̱ŋ awuye mɔ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ asuŋ waasɛ kyɔ bamo̱ ase̱ŋkpare̱gyi aaa.

ref: What do you want me to do when I arrive? Do you want me to be hard on you or to be kind and gentle?
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, ke̱mo̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa? N taa ko̱kyo̱kywe̱e̱ ba bo̱ da mo̱ne̱ bɛɛɛ ŋ kpa mo̱ne̱ ase̱ŋ na ŋ yuri e̱ye̱e̱ ba mo̱ne̱ ase̱ aaa?

ref: I have heard terrible things about some of you. In fact, you are behaving worse than Gentiles. A man is even sleeping with his own stepmother.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ ase̱ŋ ko̱ be̱e̱ a bo̱‑rɔ. Mo̱ a nu fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ asɔre̱e̱ agyi‑o‑ro ɔko̱ a waa abuse̱ŋtɔ ko̱ ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ maa suŋ Wuribware̱‑ɔ gbaa maa waa ane̱ŋ a atɔ‑ɔ. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ɔɔ kpa mò̱ se̱ka.

ref: Stop being proud! Don't you know how a little yeast can spread through the whole batch of dough?
text: Atɔ mɔ a gye̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, a mo̱ŋ bware fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱. Abre̱sɛ ya tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Yiisi kafo̱ŋfwe̱e̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ sa a bodobodoo pɛɛɛ tiŋ.”

ref: So don't celebrate the festival by being evil and sinful, which is like serving bread made with yeast. Be pure and truthful and celebrate by using bread made without yeast.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ Yudaa awuye i lee yiisi a bo̱ le̱e̱ bamo̱ e̱lɔŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ ba wo̱ Bodobodoo-ne̱-yiisi-mo̱ŋ‑te‑ro‑o, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ane̱ lee kikisi na e̱bɔye̱ ne̱ e̱ ya saŋ e̱ bo̱ ane̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ le̱e̱ hare̱e̱ de̱daa‑ɔ bo̱ twe̱e̱, na ane̱ waa itimaa, na ane̱ gyi kase̱ŋtiŋ.

ref: In my other letter I told you not to have anything to do with immoral people.
text: Wo̱re̱ ko̱ ne̱ mo̱ a kyɔ kyo̱rɛɛ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ mo̱ne̱‑ɔ‑rɔ, mo̱ a tɔwe̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ aa bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ de kakye̱e̱kpa bɛɛɛ kanyare̱kpa bɛɛɛ kipurima‑o ma nare̱.

ref: But I wasn't talking about the people of this world. You would have to leave this world to get away from everyone who is immoral or greedy or who cheats or worships idols.
text: Mo̱ a tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ, n gye̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ de kakye̱e̱kpa bɛɛɛ kanyare̱kpa‑ɔ, bɛɛɛ ba puri‑o, bɛɛɛ bamo̱ ne̱ bamo̱ akatɔ a pee atanne̱ se̱‑ɔ, bɛɛɛ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ de apoo‑o, bɛɛɛ ba suŋ ikisi‑o ne̱ n de. Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ ne̱ n dɛɛ n de ne̱e̱‑ɔ, amo̱ fe̱raa a tiri fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ le̱e̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ.

ref: Don't you know that God's people will judge the world? And if you are going to judge the world, can't you settle small problems?
text: Mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ saŋ ko̱ e̱ ba a ɔ bo̱ ba, na Wuribware̱ sa mo̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ gye̱ mò̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ bo̱ gyi kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ase̱sɛ ase̱ŋ aaa? Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, amɔ mo̱ne̱ fɔŋfɔŋ maa taare̱ a mo̱nꞌ gyi nse̱ŋgyii ne̱ m bo̱ mo̱ne̱ mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ ɔɔɔ?

ref: Don't you know we will judge angels? And if this is so, we can surely judge everyday matters.
text: Mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ ane̱ Kristoo awuye i gyi Wuribware̱ mbɔɔ ase̱ŋ aaa? Saŋbo̱to̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ a bo̱ ane̱ kakye̱na‑rɔ swe̱e̱re̱ mɔ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ ane̱ maa taare̱ a ane̱ gyi aaa?

ref: Why do you take everyday complaints to judges who are not respected by the church?
text: Mo̱ne̱ ya nya ane̱ŋ a nse̱ŋgyi amo̱, e̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ de mo̱ne̱ e̱ twe̱e̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ tii asɔre̱e̱ se̱‑ɔ akatɔ‑rɔ?

ref: I say this to your shame. Aren't any of you wise enough to act as a judge between one follower and another?
text: Ipeere mo̱ŋ de mo̱ne̱ aaa? Mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ de onyiase̱ŋpo̱ ko̱ŋko̱ mo̱ne̱‑rɔ ne̱ ɔ taare̱ a ɔ lɔŋŋɔ nse̱ŋgyi ne̱ m bo̱ mò̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana Kristoo awuye mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ ɔɔɔ?

ref: Some of you used to be like that. But now the name of our Lord Jesus Christ and the power of God's Spirit have washed you and made you holy and acceptable to God. Honor God with Your Body
text: Nsaŋ‑ɔ ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ bo̱ko̱ dɛɛ mo̱ne̱ du. Amaa ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ, Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o a kpɛɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ twe̱e̱, ne̱ ɔɔ taa mo̱ne̱ bo̱ sa Wuribware̱, ne̱ oo lee mo̱ne̱ le̱e̱ mpo̱rɔ‑rɔ. Mo̱ne̱ ŋyo̱wɔre̱ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ

ref: You also say, “Food is meant for our bodies, and our bodies are meant for food.” But I tell you that God will destroy them both. We are not supposed to do indecent things with our bodies. We are to use them for the Lord who is in charge of our bodies.
text: Mo̱ne̱ dɛɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ateese bo̱‑rɔ bo̱ sa kame ne̱e̱, ne̱ kame mɔ bo̱‑rɔ bo̱ sa ateese.” To, amaa saŋ ko̱ e̱ ba a ɔ bo̱ ba, ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ nye̱ra ateese‑o na kame‑o mpe̱pe̱yɔ. Ke̱be̱gya ko̱ mɔ se̱ be̱e̱, kayo̱wɔre̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ te bo̱ sa kakye̱e̱kpa na kanyare̱kpa na kipurima ne̱e̱, amaa mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ taa ane̱ ŋyo̱wɔre̱ bo̱ suŋ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱, na mò̱‑ɔ mò̱ de̱e̱re̱ ane̱ ŋyo̱wɔre̱ se̱ bo̱ teere.

ref: God will raise us from death by the same power he used when he raised our Lord to life.
text: Wuribware̱ a ba mò̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ bo̱ kyiŋŋi ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro. Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ o kyiŋŋi ane̱ a ɔ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro.

ref: Don't you know that your bodies are part of the body of Christ? Is it right for me to join part of the body of Christ to a prostitute? No, it isn't!
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ, mo̱ne̱ nyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ gye̱ Kristoo ko̱ko̱we̱bɛɛ‑rɔ ase̱sɛ ne̱e̱. A du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ ŋyo̱wɔre̱ a kii Kristoo kayo̱wɔre̱‑ɔ ke̱be̱gya dɛɛ‑ɔ. Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, amɔ a bware fe̱yɛ m ba mo̱ kayo̱wɔre̱ ne̱ ka gye̱ Kristoo a kayo̱wɔre̱ a ke̱be̱gya‑ɔ dɛɛ‑ɔ bo̱ di opurimakye̱e̱ ase̱ aaa? Daabii daa! A ma ba ane̱ŋ daa!

ref: Don't you know that a man who does that becomes part of her body? The Scriptures say, “The two of them will be like one person.”
text: Mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ ne̱ mò̱ aa opurimakye̱e̱ ya di, amɔ mò̱ aa mò̱ ŋyo̱wɔre̱ a kii ko̱ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱? Ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔnyare̱ e̱ waare̱e̱ ɔkye̱e̱, na mò̱ aa mò̱ ka a waa kanɔ kye̱na kii ko̱ko̱ŋko̱.”

ref: But anyone who is joined to the Lord is one in spirit with him.
text: Amaa ɔko̱ ne̱ o tii Kristoo si‑o, Kristoo na se̱sɛ amo̱ e̱ bo̱rɔ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o si na bo̱ bo̱ waa kanɔ ko̱ŋko̱.

ref: Don't be immoral in matters of sex. That is a sin against your own body in a way no other sin is.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ ma waa kakye̱e̱kpa na kanyare̱kpa na kipurima. E̱bɔye̱ ne̱ e̱ ko̱ŋ i tii si ne̱ se̱sɛ e̱ waa‑ɔ maa waa kayo̱wɔre̱‑ɔ iyisi. Amaa mò̱ ne̱ ɔ waa kakye̱e̱kpa na kanyare̱kpa‑ɔ e̱ sa ne̱ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ kayo̱wɔre̱ e̱ waa iyisi Wuribware̱ ase̱.

ref: You surely know that your body is a temple where the Holy Spirit lives. The Spirit is in you and is a gift from God. You are no longer your own.
text: Mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ ŋyo̱wɔre̱ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o te‑ro‑o ooo? O te mo̱ne̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ ase̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a nya mò̱ le̱e̱. Kase̱ŋtiŋ si, n gye̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ bo̱ mo̱ne̱ ŋyo̱wɔre̱, amaa Wuribware̱ e̱ bo̱.

ref: Now I will answer the questions you asked in your letter. You asked, “Is it best for people not to marry?”
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ mo̱ e̱ kpa a n lee se̱ŋbise ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a bise mo̱ mo̱ne̱ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ kanɔ. Ke̱mo̱ ne̱ ke̱ gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ‑ɔ bo̱ le̱e̱ awaare̱yɛ ase̱ŋ se̱. Mo̱ e̱ fa fe̱yɛ ɔnyare̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ waare̱e̱, ɔɔ waa atɔ.

ref: A wife belongs to her husband instead of to herself, and a husband belongs to his wife instead of to himself.
text: Ɔnyare̱ kayo̱wɔre̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ lee ne̱e̱, mò̱ ka e̱ bo̱ kamo̱. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱ kayo̱wɔre̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ lee, mò̱ kuri e̱ bo̱ kamo̱.

ref: In my opinion that is what should be done, though I don't know of anything the Lord said about this matter.
