text: Ane̱ŋ a atɔ mɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ mbraa ne̱ ŋ ya le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱‑ɔ, amaa a gye̱ atɔ ko̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ sa mo̱ne̱ kpa fe̱yɛ, amo̱ e̱ pre̱ mo̱ne̱, mo̱nꞌ waa.

ref: My advice for the unmarried and for widows is that it is acceptable for them to remain single, just as I am.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, ŋ yɛ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ waare̱e̱‑ɔ na akye̱e̱ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ akpenlekye̱e̱‑ɔ a bware fe̱yɛ bo̱ ma lɛɛ waare̱e̱.

ref: But if you don't have enough self-control, then go ahead and get married. After all, it is better to marry than to burn with desire.
text: Amaa bamo̱ e̱ ma taare̱ a bo̱ kra bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱, amo̱ fe̱raa bo̱ waare̱e̱. Mo̱ne̱ ya waare̱e̱, a bware a kyo̱ŋ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ a te, na mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ waare̱e̱, na mo̱ne̱ saŋ mo̱ne̱ gywii fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ waare̱e̱.

ref: I instruct married couples to stay together, and this is exactly what the Lord himself taught. A wife who leaves her husband
text: Awaare̱e̱po̱‑ɔ mɔ, n de mbraa ko̱ a m bo̱ sa mo̱ne̱, n gye̱ mo̱ ase̱ ne̱ ŋ ya le̱e̱, amaa ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Kristoo ase̱ ne̱ ŋ ya le̱e̱. Mbraa‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ɔkye̱e̱ ma kine mò̱ kuri.

ref: should either stay single or go back to her husband. And a husband should not leave his wife.
text: Amaa mò̱ ya kaŋ kine mò̱ kuri, ɔ ma kaŋ kpa anyare̱, amaa ɔ sa a mò̱ aa mò̱ kuri a be̱e̱ lɔŋŋɔ bamo̱ mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ɔnyare̱ mɔ ma kine mò̱ ka.

ref: I don't know of anything else the Lord said about marriage. All I can do is to give you my own advice. If your wife isn't a follower of the Lord, but is willing to stay with you, don't divorce her.
text: Mo̱‑ɔ, n de ase̱ŋ a n tɔwe̱ gywii awaare̱e̱po̱‑ɔ, a mo̱ŋ gye̱ mbraa ne̱ ŋ ya le̱e̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ ase̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱. Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ, ɔnyare̱ ya kii Kristoonyi, ne̱ mò̱ ka mo̱ŋ tɛɛ kii Kristoonyi, ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ ya sure si fe̱yɛ mò̱ aa mò̱ kye̱na a bo̱ bo̱ yɔ, ɔ ma kaŋ kine ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ.

ref: If your husband or wife isn't a follower of the Lord and decides to divorce you, then you should agree to it. You are no longer bound to that person. After all, God chose you and wants you to live at peace.
text: Amaa fe̱yɛ ɔnyare̱‑ɔ bɛɛɛ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Kristoonyi‑o, ne̱ mò̱ e̱ kpa a ɔ yɔ, ɔ sa a ɔ nare̱. Ane̱ŋ a ase̱ŋ mɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ maa ŋure ɔko̱ ne̱ oo sii o te mò̱ wo̱re̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a te̱e̱ ane̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ bo̱ kye̱na kaye̱e̱yuri‑ro.

ref: In every church I tell the people to stay as they were when the Lord Jesus chose them and God called them to be his own. Now I say the same thing to you.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, ŋ yɛ se̱sɛ kye̱na ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a sa mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ kye̱na‑ɔ. Ɔ kye̱na fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ kyɔ o te, ne̱ Wuribware̱ a te̱e̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ bo̱ gya mò̱ se̱‑ɔ. Mbraa mɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ asɔre̱e̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ.

ref: Being circumcised or uncircumcised isn't really what matters. The important thing is to obey God's commands.
text: Hare̱e̱ ba te̱ŋ fo̱ ko̱twe̱e̱tu bɛɛɛ bo̱ mo̱ŋ te̱ŋ ooo, a mo̱ŋ gye̱ atɔ ne̱ a tiri‑o. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ki tiri‑o e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ gya Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ se̱.

ref: Are you a slave? Don't let that bother you. But if you can win your freedom, you should.
text: Fo̱ e̱ dɛɛ fo̱ gye̱ ke̱nya nsaŋ amo̱, fo̱ ma sa a ane̱ŋ a atɔ‑ɔ tɔɔraa fo̱. Amaa fo̱ e̱ nya kpa fe̱yɛ fo̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ fo̱ e̱ye̱e̱, fo̱ waa ane̱ŋ.

ref: God paid a great price for you. So don't become slaves of anyone else.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Kristoo a sɔɔ mo̱ne̱ pɛɛɛ yawo̱ le̱ŋle̱ŋsɛ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a kii mò̱ anya. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱ne̱ ma lɛɛ kii se̱sɛ dimaadi anya.

ref: I don't know of anything the Lord said about people who have never been married. But I will tell you what I think. And you can trust me, because the Lord has treated me with kindness.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ mo̱ i lee mo̱ne̱ se̱ŋbise bo̱ le̱e̱ mbregyii si kanɔ. Mo̱ŋ de mbraa bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ a ase̱ŋ mɔ se̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Kristoo ase̱, amaa mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ amo̱ se̱ ase̱ŋ timaa a n gywii mo̱ne̱. Mo̱ a gye̱ ɔko̱ ne̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ a yure‑o si‑o, mo̱ne̱ e̱ taare̱ a mo̱nꞌ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ gyi, ma pe̱nna ɔko̱.

ref: We are now going through hard times, and I think it is best for you to stay as you are.
text: Mo̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ i gyi fe̱yɛ a bware sa se̱sɛ fe̱yɛ ɔ kye̱na ane̱ŋ na ɔ ma waare̱e̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mbe̱yɔmɔ ane̱ i ŋu ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ.

ref: If you are married, stay married. If you are not married, don't try to get married.
text: Ɔnyare̱ i de ka, ɔ ma kaŋ kine mò̱, amaa ɔnyare̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ waare̱e̱, ɔ ma lɛɛ buwi kpa ka.

ref: It isn't wrong to marry, even if you have never been married before. But those who marry will have a lot of trouble, and I want to protect you from this.
text: Ɔnyare̱ ya waare̱e̱, ɔ mo̱ŋ waa bɔye̱ ne̱e̱. Kabregyii mɔ ya waare̱e̱, ɔ mo̱ŋ waa bɔye̱. Amaa mo̱ e̱ kpa ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱ŋ ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ ba waare̱e̱‑ɔ i ŋu‑o, mo̱ne̱ ma ŋu amo̱.

ref: My friends, what I mean is that the Lord will soon come, and it won't matter if you are married or not.
text: Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ saŋ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ ɔ kyɔ na kaye̱ gye̱ kɛɛ. Le̱e̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ bo̱ yɔ, anyare̱ waare̱e̱po̱ kye̱na fe̱yɛ bamo̱ a mo̱ŋ de aka‑ɔ.

ref: It will be all the same if you are crying or laughing, or if you are buying or are completely broke.
text: Sunle‑ana kye̱na fe̱yɛ bamo̱ akatɔ a gyi‑o. Bamo̱ ne̱ bamo̱ akatɔ a gyi‑o kye̱na fe̱yɛ bamo̱ kakye̱na‑rɔ a mo̱ŋ bo̱ kɔne̱‑ɔ. Bamo̱ ne̱ baa sɔɔ ate̱tɔmo̱ŋta‑ɔ kye̱na fe̱yɛ n gye̱ bamo̱ e̱ bo̱ ate̱tɔmo̱ŋta ne̱ baa sɔɔ‑ɔ.

ref: It won't make any difference how much good you are getting from this world or how much you like it. This world as we know it is now passing away.
text: Bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ ayawo̱gyipo̱‑ɔ kye̱na fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ate̱tɔmo̱ŋta a mo̱ŋ tiri‑o. Ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ kaye̱ mɔ du mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ ka kyo̱ŋ ka yɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: I want all of you to be free from worry. An unmarried man worries about how to please the Lord.
text: Mo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ lee mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ le̱e̱ ase̱ŋ‑nɔ. Mbe̱yɔmɔ ɔnyare̱ ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ waare̱e̱‑ɔ, ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ a asuŋ‑o tiri mò̱, ne̱ ɔ le̱ŋ e̱ye̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ki i gyi ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ akatɔ‑ɔ.

ref: But a married man has more worries. He must worry about the things of this world, because he wants to please his wife.
text: Amaa ɔnyare̱ ne̱ ɔɔ waare̱e̱‑ɔ de mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ o biri kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ate̱tɔmo̱ŋta ne̱e̱, ne̱ ɔ le̱ŋ e̱ye̱e̱ ɔ waa mò̱ ka na mò̱ akatɔ gyi.

ref: So he is pulled in two directions. Unmarried women and women who have never been married worry only about pleasing the Lord, and they keep their bodies and minds pure. But a married woman worries about the things of this world, because she wants to please her husband.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, ɔ kpa a ɔ ba akatɔ anyɔ bo̱ de̱e̱re̱ praŋto̱wa‑rɔ. Ɔkye̱e̱ ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ waare̱e̱‑ɔ bɛɛɛ kabregyii ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ waare̱e̱‑ɔ, ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ ase̱ŋ tiri mò̱, ne̱ ɔɔ taa mò̱ kakpo̱nɔ pɛɛɛ na mò̱ kayo̱wɔre̱ pɛɛɛ sa ane̱ nyaŋpe̱. Amaa ɔkye̱e̱ waare̱e̱po̱ fe̱raa, kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ate̱tɔmo̱ŋta ase̱ŋ tiri mò̱, ne̱ ɔ le̱ŋ e̱ye̱e̱ a ɔ waa mò̱ kuri na mò̱ akatɔ gyi.

ref: What I am saying is for your own good—it isn't to limit your freedom. I want to help you to live right and to love the Lord above all else.
text: N gye̱ sa ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ na mo̱ne̱ kakye̱na‑rɔ waa le̱ŋ sa mo̱ne̱. Amaa mo̱ e̱ kpa mo̱ne̱ ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ ne̱e̱. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ pɛɛɛ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ waa atɔ ne̱ a bware‑o, na mo̱nꞌ taa mo̱ne̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ pɛɛɛ bo̱ suŋ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱.

ref: But suppose you are engaged to someone old enough to be married, and you want her so much that all you can think about is getting married. Then go ahead and marry. There is nothing wrong with that.
text: Fe̱yɛ ɔnyare̱ ne̱ baa ke̱re̱ ɔkye̱e̱ sa mò̱‑ɔ fe̱raa, mò̱ e̱ ma taare̱ a ɔ kra mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱, ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ e̱ kpa a ɔ daŋ kyo̱ŋ awaare̱yɛ se̱ fe̱raa, amo̱ fe̱raa, ɔnyare̱‑ɔ waare̱e̱ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ. Amo̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ bɔye̱.

ref: It is perfectly all right to marry, but it is better not to get married at all.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔɔ waare̱e̱‑ɔ a waa atɔ. Amaa ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ waare̱e̱‑ɔ a waa atɔ kyo̱ŋ ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ waare̱e̱‑ɔ.

ref: A wife should stay married to her husband until he dies. Then she is free to marry again, but only to a man who is a follower of the Lord.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ ɔkye̱e̱ ne̱ ɔɔ waare̱e̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ bo̱ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ na mò̱ kuri saŋ o te. Amaa mò̱ kuri ya wu fe̱raa, o de kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ be̱e̱ waare̱e̱ ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ kpa‑ɔ. Amaa a tiri fe̱yɛ Kristoonyi ne̱ ɔ waare̱e̱.

ref: In fact, people who think they know so much don't know anything at all.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, ɔko̱ e̱ fa fe̱yɛ o nyi ko̱tɔko̱, amo̱ fe̱raa ke̱tɔ ne̱ o nyi‑o wo̱re̱ mo̱ŋ kyɔ.

ref: But God has no doubts about who loves him.
text: Amaa se̱sɛ e̱ kpa Wuribware̱ ase̱ŋ, Wuribware̱ e̱ taa mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ gye̱ mò̱ se̱sɛ ne̱e̱.

ref: Even though food is offered to idols, we know that none of the idols in this world are alive. After all, there is only one God.
text: To, bo̱ le̱e̱ mbo̱ ne̱ ba mɔɔ ba sa ikisi‑o ase̱ŋ. Ane̱ pɛɛɛ nyi fe̱yɛ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ikisi‑o mo̱ŋ de ke̱yaale̱ŋ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ mo̱ŋ ke̱e̱, ne̱ ane̱ nyi fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ fe̱raa ɔ gye̱ ɔko̱ŋko̱ kpeŋ ne̱e̱.

ref: We have only one God, and he is the Father. He created everything, and we live for him. Jesus Christ is our only Lord. Everything was made by him, and by him life was given to us.
text: Amaa ane̱ Kristoo awuye fe̱raa, ane̱ ase̱, ane̱ se̱ Wuribware̱ gye̱ ɔko̱ŋko̱ kpeŋ ne̱e̱, mò̱ wo̱re̱ ne̱ ane̱ i suŋ, ane̱ maa suŋ ikisi. Wuribware̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔɔ twe̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa, ne̱ ane̱ bo̱‑rɔ sa mò̱. Ne̱ ane̱ de nyaŋpe̱ ko̱ŋko̱, mò̱ e̱ gye̱ Yeesuu Kristoo. Mò̱ se̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a bo̱rɔ bo̱ twe̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa, ne̱ ane̱ ŋkpa a le̱e̱ mò̱ ase̱.

ref: Not everyone knows these things. In fact, many people have grown up with the belief that idols have life in them. So when they eat meat offered to idols, they are bothered by a weak conscience.
text: Amaa n gye̱ ɔke̱maa e̱ gye̱ ne̱ o nyi amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ. Ase̱sɛ ko̱ a suŋ ikisi kyee, ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ dɛɛ ne̱ baa yɔwe̱ e̱mo̱ kusuŋ. Ane̱ŋ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ ya gyi ikisi si ateese, bo̱ saŋ ba fa amo̱ mfɛɛre̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ikisi si ateese ne̱e̱. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, amɔ bamo̱ iduŋ e̱ te̱ŋŋe̱ bamo̱, na bamo̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ waa iyisi.

ref: But food doesn't bring us any closer to God. We are no worse off if we don't eat, and we are no better off if we do.
text: Mbe̱mbe̱yɔmɔ Wuribware̱ mo̱ŋ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ gyi ateese ko̱ na a nya gyi mò̱ akatɔ. Ane̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ gyi, ane̱ sɛye̱ mo̱ŋ fo̱, ne̱ ane̱ ya gyi mɔ ne̱e̱ aaa, ane̱ maa nya sɛye̱ Wuribware̱ akatɔ‑rɔ.

ref: You know all this, and so it doesn't bother you to eat in the temple of an idol. But suppose a person with a weak conscience sees you and decides to eat food that has been offered to idols.
text: Fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ ne̱ mò̱ mfɛɛre̱ maa krɛɛ ikyurowi‑o ya ŋu fe̱yɛ fo̱ ne̱ fo̱ yɛ fo̱ de kanyiase̱ŋ‑ɔ te kisi ase̱ fo̱ i gyi ateese, amɔ fo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ a waa mò̱ wɔre̱ a o tii fo̱ se̱ a mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱ gyi ikisi si ateese aaa?

ref: So if I hurt one of the Lord's followers by what I eat, I will never eat meat as long as I live.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ i gyi‑o e̱ sa a mo̱ bɛɛko̱ Kristoonyi waa e̱bɔye̱, amo̱ fe̱raa, ma lɛɛ wo̱ kiyee pɛɛɛ. Mo̱ ya waa amo̱‑ɔ, mo̱ e̱ sa a mo̱ bɛɛko̱ Kristoonyi waa bɔye̱.

ref: We each have the right to marry one of the Lord's followers and to take her along with us, just as the other apostles and the Lord's brothers and Peter do.
text: Ne̱ ane̱ be̱e̱ ane̱ de kpa fe̱yɛ ane̱ ɔke̱maa waare̱e̱ ɔkye̱e̱ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ Kristoonyi‑o, na mò̱ e̱ yɔ kpa na mò̱ aa mò̱ ka yɔ, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu a ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱ ne̱ baa saŋ‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ na Yeesuu mò̱ tire‑ana na Peetroo e̱ waa‑ɔ.

ref: Are we the only ones who have to support ourselves by working at another job?
text: Bɛɛɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ aa Bar‑Nabas wo̱re̱ fe̱raa e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ane̱ waa kusuŋ bo̱ sɔɔ ane̱ ateese aaa?

ref: I am not saying this on my own authority. The Law of Moses tells us not to muzzle an ox when it is grinding grain. But was God concerned only about an ox?
text: Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii ane̱ ɔde̱daapo̱ Mosis fe̱yɛ ɔ kaapo̱ ane̱‑ɔ, ɔ yɛ, “Fo̱ i de naate̱ fo̱ i kyikye yaabraa se̱ fo̱ i lee ayaabraagyi‑o, fo̱ ma kaŋ ŋure mò̱ kanɔ se̱, na ɔ ma nya amo̱ ako̱ wo̱.” Amɔ e̱naate̱ ase̱ŋ dooo ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ ɔɔɔ?

ref: No, he wasn't! He was talking about us. This was written in the Scriptures so that all who plow and all who grind the grain will look forward to sharing in the harvest.
text: Mo̱ a tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ, n gye̱ ane̱ ne̱ o de aaa? Kase̱ŋtiŋ si ane̱ dimaadi ne̱ ɔɔ kyo̱rɛɛ amo̱‑ɔ sa, fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ baŋŋe̱ iferi‑o na ɔko̱ mɔ ne̱ ɔ fo̱rɔwe̱ abwaye‑o pɛɛɛ i suŋ ba kpa bamo̱ ateese le̱e̱ ko̱dɔɔ amo̱‑rɔ a bo̱ gyi ne̱e̱.

ref: If others have the right to do this, we have an even greater right. But we haven't used this right of ours. We are willing to put up with anything to keep from causing trouble for the message about Christ.
text: Bo̱ko̱ i de kpa fe̱yɛ bo̱ kɔɔre̱ ane̱ŋ a atɔ‑ɔ le̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ ase̱, amo̱ fe̱raa, ane̱ ne̱ ane̱ gye̱ Yeesuu a ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ de kpa bwe̱e̱tɔ ane̱ kyo̱ŋ bamo̱. Amaa ane̱ mo̱ŋ gya amo̱ se̱. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ a bware sa ane̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ, na ko̱tɔko̱ ma nya taare̱ kuŋ Kristoo kuŋu si ase̱ŋ timaa‑o ko̱tɔwe̱, na a nya yɔ akatɔ‑rɔ.

ref: Don't you know that people who work in the temple make their living from what is brought to the temple? Don't you know that a person who serves at the altar is given part of what is offered?
text: M be̱e̱ taa Wuribware̱ kusuŋ ase̱ŋ bo̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱. Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ e̱ waa bamo̱ kusuŋ lɔŋŋɔkpa ne̱ ɔ bo̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ. Ne̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, ase̱sɛ ya baa mbo̱ bɛɛɛ ateese a bo̱ bo̱ lɔŋŋɔ Wuribware̱, alɔŋŋɔpo̱ amo̱ e̱ nya ayee na ateese amo̱ ako̱ a bo̱ gyi.

ref: In the same way, the Lord wants everyone who preaches the good news to make a living from preaching this message.
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ a sa mbraa fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ne̱ ba tɔwe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo kuŋu si ase̱ŋ timaa‑o nya bamo̱ ateese gyisɛ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ a kusuŋ‑o‑ro.

ref: I don't have any reason to brag about preaching the good news. Preaching is something God told me to do, and if I don't do it, I am doomed.
text: Mo̱ ase̱ŋ timaa‑o ko̱tɔwe̱‑ɔ maa kaapo̱ mo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ kaapo̱ mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Kristoo e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ ye̱re̱ mo̱ fe̱yɛ n tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ timaa‑o. Mo̱ ya yɔwe̱, o gyiiri mo̱ ke̱se̱bɔ.

ref: If I preach because I want to, I will be paid. But even if I don't want to, it is still something God has sent me to do.
text: Mo̱ ya waa ane̱ŋ a kusuŋ‑o, na ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ ye̱re̱ mo̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ waa, amo̱ fe̱raa mo̱ e̱ nya kakɔka. Amaa Wuribware̱ ya taa kusuŋ mɔ waa mo̱ asare̱e̱‑rɔ, amo̱ fe̱raa mo̱ e̱ waa kusuŋ amo̱ ne̱ ɔɔ sa mo̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ waa‑ɔ dooo ne̱e̱.

ref: What pay am I given? It is the chance to preach the good news free of charge and not to use the privileges that are mine because I am a preacher.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, nte̱tɔ e̱ gye̱ mo̱ kakɔka kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ mfe̱e̱? Mo̱ kakɔka e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ n de kpa fe̱yɛ n de ase̱ŋ timaa‑o mo̱ e̱ sa ɔke̱maa na ma kɔɔre̱ bamo̱ sɛye̱. Amaa hare̱e̱ mo̱ a de kpa fe̱yɛ ŋ kɔɔre̱ ateese le̱e̱ ɔko̱ ase̱ gbaa ooo, mo̱‑ɔ ma kɔɔre̱.

ref: I am not anyone's slave. But I have become a slave to everyone, so I can win as many people as possible.
text: Ne̱ m be̱e̱ n de kpa fe̱yɛ ŋ kine bo̱ko̱ nnɔ ke̱gyase̱, amaa mo̱ e̱ waa mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a gye̱ ɔke̱maa ke̱nya‑ɔ, na ŋ nya taare̱ sa a ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ kii Kristoo awuye.

ref: When I am with the Jews, I live like a Jew to win Jews. They are ruled by the Law of Moses, and I am not. But I live by the Law to win them.
text: Mo̱ ya yɔ Yudaa awuye ase̱, n te fe̱yɛ Yudaanyi‑o na ŋ nya nya Yudaa awuye. Amaa fe̱raa hare̱e̱ mo̱ ne̱ n gye̱ Yudaanyi‑o, mo̱ a mo̱ŋ lɛɛ n gya bamo̱ mbraa ne̱ bo̱ gya se̱‑ɔ se̱, ne̱ hare̱e̱ mo̱ a kii Kristoonyi gbaa ooo, mo̱ ya yɔ bamo̱ ase̱, mo̱ e̱ taa mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ waa mbraa amo̱ kaase̱ ne̱e̱ a ŋ nya nya bamo̱.

ref: And when I am with people who are not ruled by the Law, I forget about the Law to win them. Of course, I never really forget about the law of God. In fact, I am ruled by the law of Christ.
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa a ŋ nya bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o, amɔ, mo̱ ya yɔ bamo̱ ase̱, mo̱ e̱ kye̱na ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱‑ɔ, na ma gya Yudaa awuye mbraa‑ɔ se̱. Amo̱‑ɔ e̱ kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a kye Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ ɔɔɔ? Daabii, n gye̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱e̱. N gya Kristoo mbraa se̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: I do all this for the good news, because I want to share in its blessings. A Race and a Fight
text: Mfaanɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa, ne̱ mo̱ e̱ nya mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ timaa‑o, na mo̱‑ɔ mo̱ e̱ nya amo̱ se̱ ŋyure.

ref: All of them also ate the same spiritual food
text: Bamo̱ pɛɛɛ a gyi ateese ne̱ aa le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ se̱ ba‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: and drank the same spiritual drink, which flowed from the spiritual rock that followed them. That rock was Christ.
text: Bamo̱ pɛɛɛ a nuu nkyu ne̱ ŋ ya le̱e̱ ke̱fo̱re̱‑rɔ to̱ŋ ke̱maa ne̱ baa yɔ‑ɔ. Kase̱ŋtiŋ si, n gye̱ ke̱fo̱re̱‑ɔ ya sa bamo̱ nkyu, amaa Kristoo fɔŋfɔŋ.

ref: But most of them did not please God. So they died, and their bodies were scattered all over the desert.
text: Amaa amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ gbaa, Wuribware̱ akatɔ mo̱ŋ gyi bamo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ se̱, amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ bamo̱ ɔke̱maa a wu kpa‑rɔ le̱e̱ kimukee kuŋu mɔ ya kya nno̱ŋ a kuŋu‑o si.

ref: What happened to them is a warning to keep us from wanting to do the same evil things.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ ke̱tɔ ne̱ kaa waa bamo̱‑ɔ i yii ane̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ ma kpa atɔ ne̱ a mo̱ŋ bo̱ daŋ‑ɔ,

ref: They worshiped idols, just as the Scriptures say, “The people sat down to eat and drink. Then they got up to dance around.” So don't worship idols.
text: na ane̱ ma suŋ ikisi fe̱yɛ bamo̱‑ɔ. Kake ŋko̱ ne̱ baa seri kisi atɔ ko̱ ne̱ a du fe̱yɛ kakpe̱ŋnyare̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa suŋ amo̱. Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ. “Ne̱ baa kye̱na gyi, ne̱ baa nuu, ne̱ ŋkee baa ko̱so̱ ba kyaa ba sa kisi‑o, na ba soori ba gyi bamo̱ akatɔ.”

ref: Some of you say, “We can do whatever we want to!” But I tell you not everything may be good or helpful.
text: Amaa mo̱ne̱ bo̱ko̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ de kpa fe̱yɛ ane̱ gyi ateese ke̱maa.” To, a gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ, amaa n gye̱ ateese ke̱maa kigyi e̱ bo̱ daŋ, n gye̱ ateese ke̱maa kigyi mɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ kya bo̱ko̱‑rɔ.

ref: And don't try to test Christ, as some of them did and were later bitten by poisonous snakes.
text: Ane̱ ma tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ mo̱ŋ bware, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ bamo̱ bo̱ko̱ a waa, ne̱ Wuribware̱ a suŋ awɔ bo̱ duŋwi duŋwi bamo̱ mɔɔ‑ɔ.

ref: Don't even grumble, as some of them did and were killed by the destroying angel.
text: Ane̱ be̱e̱ ane̱ ma kaŋ tɛɛ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ bamo̱ bo̱ko̱ a waa ne̱ Wuribware̱ a suŋ mò̱ mbɔɔ‑ɔ ɔko̱ fe̱yɛ ɔɔ bo̱ mɔɔ bamo̱‑ɔ.

ref: These things happened to them as a warning to us. All this was written in the Scriptures to teach us who live in these last days.
text: Amo̱ pɛɛɛ ne̱ aa waa bamo̱‑ɔ ne̱ Mosis a kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ na ane̱ ne̱ ane̱ te mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ ma lɛɛ waa ane̱ŋ a e̱bɔye̱‑ɔ. Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ te mbe̱yɔmɔ a kaye̱ mɔ ne̱ ka kpa a ka gye̱ kɛɛ‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: Even if you think you can stand up to temptation, be careful not to fall.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, mò̱ ne̱ ɔ fa fe̱yɛ ɔ ye̱re̱ keŋkeŋ‑o, na ɔ maa waa bɔye̱‑ɔ, sa se̱ na ɔ ma nya lwee ko̱waa e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ.

ref: My friends, you must keep away from idols.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ ako̱so̱bɛɛ timaa, mo̱nꞌ yɔwe̱ ikisi kusuŋ.

ref: I am speaking to you as people who have enough sense to know what I am talking about.
text: Ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ nyi ase̱ŋ, amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱re̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ‑rɔ sa mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱.

ref: By sharing in the same loaf of bread, we become one body, even though there are many of us.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bodobodoo ko̱ŋko̱ kpeŋ e̱ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ane̱ pɛɛɛ i ke‑ro ngyingyii ngyingyii ane̱ e̱ wo̱. Amo̱‑ɔ e̱ kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, hare̱e̱ ane̱ e̱ kyɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ ne̱e̱ ooo, ane̱ mɔ ane̱ de kanɔ ko̱ŋko̱.

ref: No, I am not! This food is really sacrificed to demons and not to God. I don't want you to have anything to do with demons.
text: Daabii! Ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ n gye̱ Wuribware̱ bɛɛɛ ikisi ne̱ ikisi asuŋpo̱ e̱ mɔɔ ane̱ŋ a mbo̱‑ɔ ba sa. Ba fa fe̱yɛ ba suŋ ikisi kase̱ŋtiŋ si ne̱e̱. Amaa Ɔbɔnsam na mò̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ awuye ne̱ ba suŋ. Mbe̱yɔmɔ ma kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ kii Ɔbɔnsam asuŋpo̱.

ref: You cannot drink from the cup of demons and still drink from the Lord's cup. You cannot eat at the table of demons and still eat at the Lord's table.
text: Mo̱ne̱ maa taare̱ a mo̱nꞌ nuu ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu a nta‑ɔ na mo̱nꞌ be̱e̱ nuu Ɔbɔnsam a nta‑ɔ. Mo̱ne̱ maa taare̱ a mo̱nꞌ gyi ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ a ateese‑o na mo̱nꞌ be̱e̱ gyi Ɔbɔnsam lee‑o.

ref: We would make the Lord jealous if we did this. And we are not stronger than the Lord. Always Honor God
text: Mo̱ne̱ ya waa ane̱ŋ, mo̱ne̱ e̱ sa a ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ duŋ fwii mo̱ne̱ se̱. Mo̱ne̱ bo̱ le̱ŋ mo̱ne̱ kyɔ mò̱ aaa?

ref: However, when you buy meat in the market, go ahead and eat it. Keep your conscience clear by not asking where the meat came from.
text: To, mo̱ne̱ de kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ sɔɔ kiyee ne̱ ba fe ke̱gya‑rɔ‑ɔ. Hare̱e̱ atɔ mɔ ase̱ŋ e̱ tɔɔraa mo̱ne̱ gbaa ooo, mo̱ne̱ ma kaŋ bise mfe̱ŋ ne̱ kiyee amo̱ a le̱e̱ ba‑ɔ.

ref: The Scriptures say, “The earth and everything in it belong to the Lord.”
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ baa kyo̱rɛɛ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, “Swe̱e̱re̱ mɔ ne̱ ɔ da‑ɔ, na mò̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa mɔ, Wuribware̱ e̱ bo̱.”

ref: If an unbeliever invites you to dinner, and you want to go, then go. Eat whatever you are served. Don't cause a problem for someone's conscience by asking where the food came from.
text: Fe̱yɛ se̱sɛ ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Kristoonyi‑o ya nyiŋŋi fo̱ ateese, ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kpa a fo̱ ya gyi, amo̱ fe̱raa gyi ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ oo nyiŋŋi fo̱ a fo̱ bo̱ gyi‑o. Hare̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ateese amo̱ a le̱e̱‑ɔ ase̱ŋ e̱ tɔɔraa fo̱ gbaa ooo, fo̱ ma kaŋ bise mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ateese amo̱ a le̱e̱‑ɔ.

ref: But if you are told it has been sacrificed to idols, don't cause a problem by eating it. I don't mean a problem for yourself, but for the one who told you. Why should my freedom be limited by someone else's conscience?
text: N gye̱ mo̱ne̱ kuŋu si ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ. Amaa se̱sɛ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ kisi si ateese ne̱e̱‑ɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ. Fo̱ ya gyi, fo̱ e̱ taare̱ a fo̱ sa a o lwee e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ. Mo̱ne̱ ɔko̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ i de kpa fe̱yɛ n gyi ke̱tɔ ke̱maa, ne̱ ŋkee nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ ɔko̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ amo̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ a tiri mo̱?”

ref: Don't cause problems for Jews or Greeks or anyone else who belongs to God's church.
text: Amaa fo̱ ma kaŋ waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ sa a Yudaa awuye bɛɛɛ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱‑ɔ bɛɛɛ Wuribware̱ asɔre̱e̱ awuye iduŋ fwii fo̱ se̱‑ɔ.

ref: I always try to please others instead of myself, in the hope that many of them will be saved.
text: Mo̱nꞌ kase̱ mo̱ kakye̱na‑ɔ‑rɔ. Ma kpa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ kya mo̱ wo̱re̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, amaa mo̱ e̱ le̱ŋ e̱ye̱e̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa a ase̱sɛ akatɔ gyi ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa‑ɔ se̱ ne̱e̱, na Wuribware̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ bamo̱.

ref: You must follow my example, as I follow the example of Christ. Rules for Worship
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ mo̱nꞌ kase̱ mo̱‑rɔ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kase̱ Kristoo‑ro‑o. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ akye̱e̱ waa saŋ ne̱ ba waa asɔre̱e̱‑ɔ

ref: I am proud of you, because you always remember me and obey the teachings I gave you.
text: Mo̱ne̱ a waa atɔ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ a nyiŋŋi ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ kakye̱na‑rɔ du‑o si, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a gya abware̱se̱ŋ ke̱kaapo̱ ne̱ mo̱ a kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱‑ɔ se̱.

ref: Now I want you to know that Christ is the head of all men, and a man is the head of a woman. But God is the head of Christ.
text: To, mbe̱yɔmɔ bo̱ le̱e̱ akye̱e̱ kuŋu si ase̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa a mo̱nꞌ nu kaase̱ fe̱yɛ, ɔkye̱e̱ kuŋu e̱ gye̱ ɔnyare̱, ne̱ ɔnyare̱ ke̱maa kuŋu gye̱ Kristoo, ne̱ Kristoo kuŋu gye̱ Wuribware̱.

ref: This means that any man who prays or prophesies with something on his head brings shame to his head.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ɔnyare̱ ke̱maa ne̱ ɔ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ bɛɛɛ ɔ tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a lee bo̱ kaapo̱ mo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ o de atɔ o buŋ mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ kuŋu si‑o de ipeere ɔ mɔɔ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: But any woman who prays or prophesies without something on her head brings shame to her head. In fact, she may as well shave her head.
text: Ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱ ke̱maa mɔ ne̱ ɔ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ bɛɛɛ ɔ tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a lee bo̱ kaapo̱ mò̱‑ɔ, ne̱ sɛye̱ mo̱ŋ buŋ mò̱ kuŋu si‑o de ipeere ɔ mɔɔ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ne̱e̱. Mò̱ ya waa amo̱‑ɔ, a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔkye̱e̱ ne̱ oo see mò̱ ipwii pɛɛɛ bo̱ twe̱e̱‑ɔ bɔ mò̱.

ref: A woman should wear something on her head. It is a disgrace for a woman to shave her head or cut her hair. But if she refuses to wear something on her head, let her cut off her hair.
text: Mò̱ e̱ ma buŋ mò̱ kuŋu si, mo̱nꞌ sa a bo̱ see mò̱ ipwii. Amaa amo̱ a gye̱ apeerese̱ŋ sa ɔkye̱e̱ fe̱yɛ o see mò̱ ipwii‑o si‑o, mo̱nꞌ sa a o buŋ mò̱ kuŋu si.

ref: Men were created to be like God and to bring honor to God. This means a man should not wear anything on his head. Women were created to bring honor to men.
text: Ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ ɔnyare̱ ma buŋ mò̱ kuŋu si‑o e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a twe̱e̱ mò̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ du‑o dɛɛ, na bo̱ bo̱rɔ mò̱ se̱ bo̱ ŋu Wuribware̱ a ke̱dabe̱‑ɔ. Amaa ɔkye̱e̱ ne̱ ba bo̱rɔ se̱ a bo̱ bo̱ ŋu ɔnyare̱‑ɔ ke̱dabe̱.

ref: It was the woman who was made from a man, and not the man who was made from a woman.
text: A mo̱ŋ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ɔkye̱e̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a bo̱ twe̱e̱ ɔnyare̱, amaa ɔnyare̱ gye̱ŋkpɛɛsɛ‑ɔ kibowii ne̱ Wuribware̱ a bo̱ twe̱e̱ ɔkye̱e̱ gye̱ŋkpɛɛsɛ‑ɔ.

ref: He wasn't created for her. She was created for him.
text: A be̱e̱ a mo̱ŋ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ɔkye̱e̱ se̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a twe̱e̱ ɔnyare̱. Amaa ɔnyare̱ se̱ ne̱ ɔɔ twe̱e̱ ɔkye̱e̱.

ref: And so, because of this, and also because of the angels, a woman ought to wear something on her head, as a sign of her authority.
text: Amo̱ katiŋ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ɔkye̱e̱ buŋ mò̱ kuŋu si, na a kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ kuri de mò̱ se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ, na a kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ bo̱nyaa Wuribware̱ mbɔɔ.

ref: It is true that the first woman came from a man, but all other men have been given birth by women. Yet God is the one who created everything.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ɔkye̱e̱ a le̱e̱ ɔnyare̱ kayo̱wɔre̱‑rɔ ne̱e̱, amaa ɔkye̱e̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ ko̱we̱ ɔnyare̱. Ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ sa bamo̱ pɛɛɛ ŋkpa.

ref: Ask yourselves if it is proper for a woman to pray without something on her head.
text: Mo̱ne̱ fɔŋfɔŋ a de̱e̱re̱‑ɔ, a bware fe̱yɛ ɔkye̱e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱, na ɔ mo̱ŋ taa sɛye̱ bo̱ bo̱ŋ mò̱ kuŋu si aaa?

ref: This is how things are done in all of God's churches, and this is why none of you should argue about what I have said. Rules for the Lord's Supper
text: Amaa ɔko̱ e̱ kpa a o gyiiri amo̱‑ɔ ikii fe̱raa, mo̱ e̱ be̱ŋŋaa se̱sɛ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ akye̱e̱ kibuŋ aŋu si‑o gye̱ ane̱ ananatɔ ne̱e̱, ne̱ Wuribware̱ asɔre̱e̱ awuye gya se̱. Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ a ateese ne̱ ba gyaŋŋe̱ ba gyi ba nyiŋŋi mò̱‑ɔ ase̱ŋ (Matiyo

ref: I am told you can't get along with each other when you worship, and I am sure that some of what I have heard is true.
text: Ke̱mo̱ ne̱ ke̱ gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a nu fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ ya yɔ asɔre̱e̱, mo̱ne̱ i ke‑ro ntuŋ si ntuŋ si mo̱ne̱ wo̱re̱ wo̱re̱ ne̱e̱. A du fe̱yɛ amo̱‑ɔ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ.

ref: You are bound to argue with each other, but it is easy to see which of you have God's approval.
text: Mo̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ i gyi fe̱yɛ a tiri fe̱yɛ ikii ba mo̱ne̱ mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ, na e̱ taare̱ sa a ɔke̱maa ŋu bamo̱ ne̱ bamo̱ lee gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ, na bamo̱ ne̱ bamo̱ lee mo̱ŋ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o.

ref: You even start eating before everyone gets to the meeting, and some of you go hungry, while others get drunk.
text: Amaa mo̱ne̱ fe̱raa mo̱ne̱ ya gyaŋŋe̱, mo̱ne̱ ɔke̱maa i gyi mò̱ wo̱re̱ ne̱e̱. Na mo̱ne̱ bo̱ko̱ mɔ e̱ bwe̱e̱, na mo̱ne̱ bo̱ko̱ mɔ te na ako̱ŋ de bamo̱.

ref: If you fail to understand that you are the body of the Lord, you will condemn yourselves by the way you eat and drink.
text: Ɔko̱ ya wo̱ ane̱ŋ a bodobodoo‑o, ne̱ oo nuu ane̱ŋ a nta‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ nu kaase̱ fe̱yɛ a ye̱re̱ bo̱ sa Kristoo ko̱yo̱wɔre̱gyi ne̱e̱, amo̱ fe̱raa ɔ sa ne̱ Wuribware̱ i bu mò̱ ke̱pɔ.

ref: After the meal, Jesus took a cup of wine in his hands and said, “This is my blood, and with it God makes his new agreement with you. Drink this and remember me.”
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱, bamo̱ a gyi lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ taa nta ko̱to̱re̱nkyu bo̱rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ko̱to̱re̱nkyu na nta mɔ ye̱re̱ bo̱ sa e̱taŋ po̱pwɛɛ ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ ba mo̱ mbo̱gya a ɔ bo̱ tii e̱mo̱ kanɔ‑ɔ ne̱e̱. Saŋ ke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ ya gyaŋŋe̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa a mo̱nꞌ nuu mmo̱, mo̱nꞌ waa ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ, na mo̱nꞌ bo̱ nyiŋŋi mo̱.”

ref: I have already told you what the Lord Jesus did on the night he was betrayed. And it came from the Lord himself. He took some bread in his hands.
text: To, ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu fɔŋfɔŋ ase̱ ne̱ mo̱ a nya abware̱se̱ŋ ke̱kaapo̱ mɔ le̱e̱, ne̱ mo̱ a kyɔ tɔwe̱ amo̱ ase̱ŋ gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu a gyi bodobodoo kanye ko̱ ne̱ Yudas Kariyotinyi a gyi mò̱ kidiburo‑o.

ref: Then after he had given thanks, he broke it and said, “This is my body, which is given for you. Eat this and remember me.”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ sa Wuribware̱ aŋsɛ, ne̱ ɔɔ te̱ŋŋe̱ bodobodoo‑o‑ro tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ ko̱yo̱wɔre̱gyi ne̱ n de mo̱ e̱ sa mo̱ne̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱, na mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱ bo̱ nyiŋŋi mo̱.”

ref: But if you eat the bread and drink the wine in a way that isn't worthy of the Lord, you sin against his body and blood.
text: Amo̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ fo̱ko̱ ne̱ fo̱ ya gyi ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ bodobodoo nuu mò̱ nta‑ɔ katɔmaabu‑ro, amo̱ fe̱raa ɔ waa bɔye̱ ɔ bo̱ kye ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ ko̱yo̱wɔre̱gyi na mò̱ mbo̱gya‑ɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: This is why you must examine the way you eat and drink.
text: Pwɛɛ na ɔko̱ bo̱ gyi ane̱ŋ a bodobodoo amo̱, na o nuu nta amo̱‑ɔ, amo̱‑ɔ ɔ gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ de̱e̱re̱ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ ke̱e̱.

ref: This is why many of you are sick and weak and why a lot of others have died.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱‑rɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ mo̱ŋ bo̱ le̱ŋ, ne̱ ba lɔ, ne̱ bamo̱ bo̱ko̱ gbaa a wu.

ref: My dear friends, you should wait until everyone gets there before you start eating.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, mo̱ne̱ ya bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱ a mo̱nꞌ gyi ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ a ateese‑o, mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱ kpa abɛɛ ŋu pwɛɛ na mo̱nꞌ dɛɛ gyi ateese‑o.

ref: If you really are hungry, you can eat at home. Then you won't condemn yourselves when you meet together. After I arrive, I will instruct you about the other matters.
text: Ako̱ŋ i de ɔko̱, mo̱nꞌ sa a ɔ gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ gyi mò̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ pwɛɛ. N gye̱ ane̱ŋ, Wuribware̱ i bu mo̱ne̱ ke̱pɔ. Bo̱ le̱e̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ a saŋ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a bise mo̱ amo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, mo̱ ya ba mo̱ e̱ sa mo̱ne̱ amo̱ se̱ mbraa.

ref: I want you to remember that before you became followers of the Lord, you were led in all the wrong ways by idols that cannot even talk.
text: Mo̱ e̱ kpa a mo̱nꞌ ŋu fe̱yɛ pwɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a bo̱ kii Kristoo awuye‑o, atɔ ko̱ e̱ yaa mo̱ne̱ ikisi ase̱ saŋ ke̱maa, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i suŋ e̱mo̱. Amaa mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nu ase̱ŋ ke̱maa le̱e̱ e̱mo̱ ase̱.

ref: There are different kinds of spiritual gifts, but they all come from the same Spirit.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ aketɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o i ke‑o bo̱‑rɔ e̱wo̱re̱se̱ e̱wo̱re̱se̱, amaa amo̱ pɛɛɛ mɔ a le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o dɛɛ ase̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: and we can each do different things. Yet the same God works in all of us and helps us in everything we do.
text: Ɔke̱maa naa kpa e̱wo̱re̱se̱ e̱wo̱re̱se̱ se̱ o de aketɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a ke mò̱‑ɔ o de o suŋ ɔ sa Wuribware̱. Amaa Wuribware̱ ko̱ŋko̱‑ɔ dɛɛ ya sa bamo̱ ɔke̱maa na atɔ ne̱ o nyi e̱waa‑ɔ.

ref: The Spirit has given each of us a special way of serving others.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o a sa mmo̱‑ɔ bɛɛɛ mmo̱‑ɔ, a bware bo̱ sa ane̱ pɛɛɛ.

ref: Some of us can speak with wisdom, while others can speak with knowledge, but these gifts come from the same Spirit.
text: Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o e̱ sa se̱sɛ ko̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ taare̱ a o nu atɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a bo̱ kwe̱e̱rɔ bo̱ko̱ se̱‑ɔ kaase̱, na ɔ kaapo̱ amo̱‑rɔ. Ɔko̱ mɔ, ɔ sa mò̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ fe̱yɛ o ŋu kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o, na ɔ kaapo̱ kamo̱‑rɔ.

ref: To others the Spirit has given great faith or the power to heal the sick
text: Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o dɛɛ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ sa ne̱ bo̱ko̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ ba gyi ke̱baŋse̱, ne̱ bo̱ko̱ mɔ, ɔ sa ke̱yaale̱ŋ fe̱yɛ bo̱ bo̱ kya alɔpo̱.

ref: But it is the Spirit who does all this and decides which gifts to give to each of us. One Body with Many Parts
text: Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o dɛɛ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ o de bamo̱ o suŋ asuŋ, ne̱ o ke ɔke̱maa mò̱ aketɔ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ waa asuŋ sa Wuribware̱‑ɔ. Kayo̱wɔre̱ a ke‑ro ke‑ro bwe̱e̱tɔ

ref: Some of us are Jews, and others are Gentiles. Some of us are slaves, and others are free. But God's Spirit baptized each of us and made us part of the body of Christ. Now we each drink from that same Spirit.
text: Ane̱ Kristoo awuye dɛɛ ne̱ saŋ ne̱ baa bɔ ane̱ asuu‑o, baa bɔ ane̱ bo̱ waa kayo̱wɔre̱ ko̱ŋko̱‑rɔ. A mo̱ŋ tiri fe̱yɛ ane̱ gye̱ Yudaa awuye bɛɛɛ ane̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye, anya bɛɛɛ agyi, ane̱ a kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe ko̱ŋko̱‑ɔ dɛɛ ne̱e̱ waa ane̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ.

ref: Our bodies don't have just one part. They have many parts.
text: Nyiŋkpasɛ kayo̱wɔre̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ ko̱ko̱ŋko̱, kaa ke‑ro ke‑ro ne̱e̱.

ref: Suppose a foot says, “I'm not a hand, and so I'm not part of the body.” Wouldn't the foot still belong to the body?
text: Fe̱yɛ ke̱yaapapaa ya tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ a mo̱ŋ gye̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ŋ tii kayo̱wɔre̱‑ɔ se̱,” e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ mò̱ ko̱tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ e̱ sa a a waa kase̱ŋtiŋ?

ref: Or suppose an ear says, “I'm not an eye, and so I'm not part of the body.” Wouldn't the ear still belong to the body?
text: Bɛɛɛ ke̱se̱bɔ ya tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ a mo̱ŋ gye̱ ke̱katɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ŋ tii kayo̱wɔre̱‑ɔ se̱,” e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ mò̱ ko̱tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ e̱ sa a a waa kase̱ŋtiŋ?

ref: If our bodies were only an eye, we couldn't hear a thing. And if they were only an ear, we couldn't smell a thing.
text: Fe̱yɛ kayo̱wɔre̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ gye̱ ke̱katɔ ne̱e̱‑ɔ, weetee e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ ka waa a ka nu ke̱tɔ ke̱maa? Ne̱ kayo̱wɔre̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ gye̱ ke̱se̱bɔ ne̱e̱, weetee e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ ka waa a ka nu sɔ?

ref: But God has put all parts of our body together in the way that he decided is best.
text: Amaa kase̱ŋtiŋ si Wuribware̱ ya ba kayo̱wɔre̱‑ɔ ke̱be̱gya ke̱maa bo̱ kye̱na kamo̱ kye̱nakpa mò̱ ke̱pre̱ se̱.

ref: That's why the eyes cannot say they don't need the hands. That's also why the head cannot say it doesn't need the feet.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, ke̱katɔ maa taare̱ a ke̱ tɔwe̱ gywii ke̱sare̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ ase̱ŋ mo̱ŋ tiri mo̱.” Ne̱ kuŋu maa taare̱ a ke̱ tɔwe̱ gywii ayaapapaa fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ase̱ŋ mo̱ŋ tiri mo̱.”

ref: In fact, we cannot get along without the parts of the body that seem to be the weakest.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ fo̱ ya de̱e̱re̱, fo̱ i ŋu fe̱yɛ ane̱ maa taare̱ a ane̱ kye̱na, na ane̱ kayo̱wɔre̱ abe̱gya abe̱gya ne̱ a mo̱ŋ bo̱ le̱ŋ‑ɔ mo̱ŋ te ane̱ se̱.

ref: We take special care to dress up some parts of our bodies. We are modest about our personal parts,
text: Kase̱ŋtiŋ si kayo̱wɔre̱ abe̱gya abe̱gya amo̱ ne̱ ane̱ de̱e̱re̱ se̱ nɛɛnɛɛ. Ane̱ kayo̱wɔre̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ipeere si a mo̱ŋ da kawu‑o ne̱ ane̱ i bu bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: but we don't have to be modest about other parts. God put our bodies together in such a way that even the parts that seem the least important are valuable.
text: Amaa ane̱ kayo̱wɔre̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ a bo̱ daŋ ke̱de̱e̱re̱‑ɔ ase̱ŋ mo̱ŋ tiri ane̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Wuribware̱ a ba kayo̱wɔre̱ abe̱gya abe̱gya bo̱ kya abɛɛ se̱ kpa ko̱ se̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ bu kayo̱wɔre̱ abe̱gya ko̱ ne̱ a mo̱ŋ bo̱ le̱ŋ‑ɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: He did this to make all parts of the body work together smoothly, with each part caring about the others.
text: Ɔɔ waa amo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱ a kayo̱wɔre̱ nya waa kanɔ ko̱ŋko̱, na kayo̱wɔre̱ ke̱be̱gya ke̱maa nya de̱e̱re̱ kamo̱ bɛɛko̱ se̱.

ref: If one part of our body hurts, we hurt all over. If one part of our body is honored, the whole body will be happy.
text: Kayo̱wɔre̱ ke̱be̱gya ko̱ i ŋu ase̱ŋ, amɔ abe̱gya ne̱ a saŋ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ i ŋu ase̱ŋ ne̱e̱. Fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ya kyo̱rɔ kayo̱wɔre̱‑ɔ ke̱be̱gya ko̱, amɔ abe̱gya ne̱ aa saŋ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ akatɔ a gyi.

ref: Together you are the body of Christ. Each one of you is part of his body.
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ du‑o ne̱e̱. Mo̱ne̱ pɛɛɛ a tii abɛɛ se̱ mo̱ne̱ gye̱ Kristoo kayo̱wɔre̱ ne̱e̱, ne̱ ɔke̱maa gye̱ kamo̱ ke̱be̱gya ko̱. Ane̱ŋ a kayo̱wɔre̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ asɔre̱e̱‑ɔ.

ref: What if I could speak all languages of humans and even of angels? If I did not love others, I would be nothing more than a noisy gong or a clanging cymbal.
text: Hare̱e̱ mo̱ i de ke̱yaale̱ŋ e̱se̱ŋsa ke̱sa‑rɔ ne̱ a gye̱ ase̱sɛ lee, bɛɛɛ Wuribware̱ mbɔɔ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ lee ne̱e̱ ooo, ne̱ mo̱ e̱ ma kpa mo̱ bɛɛko̱ ase̱ŋ, n du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ aŋko̱re̱ kpaŋkpaŋ ne̱ o de e̱lawo̱‑ɔ.

ref: What if I gave away all that I owned and let myself be burned alive? I would gain nothing, unless I loved others.
text: Hare̱e̱ gbaa mo̱ ya swii mo̱ ate̱tɔmo̱ŋta pɛɛɛ bo̱ ke atiripo̱, bɛɛɛ mo̱ a sa ne̱ baa kywɛɛ mo̱ de̱e̱kpa‑rɔ mɔɔ, amo̱ fe̱raa mo̱ŋ nya sɛye̱.

ref: rude. Love isn't selfish or quick tempered. It doesn't keep a record of wrongs that others do.
text: Ɔ mo̱ŋ de akatɔle̱ŋ, ne̱ ɔ be̱e̱ ɔ maa saare̱ bo̱ko̱. Ɔ maa ye̱re̱ bo̱ko̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔ kpa‑ɔ. Mò̱ duŋ maa fwii, ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ de ɔko̱ do̱ŋdaa.

ref: Love never fails! Everyone who prophesies will stop, and unknown languages will no longer be spoken. All that we know will be forgotten.
text: Ke̱kpa maa lo̱we̱, ke̱kpa mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ. Amaa ke̱yaale̱ŋ ne̱ bo̱ de ba tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a lee bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱‑ɔ, na ke̱yaale̱ŋ ne̱ bo̱ de ba sa se̱ŋsa ko̱ ne̱ bamo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ maa nu mò̱‑rɔ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ kɛɛ. Ane̱ kanyiase̱ŋ pɛɛɛ e̱ kyo̱ŋ.

ref: When we were children, we thought and reasoned as children do. But when we grew up, we quit our childish ways.
text: Mo̱ a gye̱ keegyi‑o, mo̱ se̱ŋsa na mo̱ mfɛɛre̱ na ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ fa m bɔ atɔ se̱‑ɔ gye̱ keegyi lee ne̱e̱, amaa mo̱ a bo̱ kii ke̱yaafɔre̱‑ɔ, mo̱ a yɔwe̱ ayaagyise̱ŋ‑ɔ.

ref: Now all we can see of God is like a cloudy picture in a mirror. Later we will see him face to face. We don't know everything, but then we will, just as God completely understands us.
text: Kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ atɔ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mbo̱re̱ŋ‑ɔ. A du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ a de̱e̱re̱ diki ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ ke̱e̱‑rɔ nɛɛnɛɛ‑ɔ. Amaa kake nsiŋ ne̱ Yeesuu e̱ ba‑ɔ, ane̱ i ŋu mò̱ akatɔ‑rɔ. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ŋ nyi mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ gye̱ kafwe̱e̱ ko̱ ne̱e̱. Amaa kake nsi amo̱‑ɔ mo̱ i gyii ke̱tɔ ke̱maa, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ nyi mo̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ.

ref: For now there are faith, hope, and love. But of these three, the greatest is love.
text: Aketɔ pɛɛɛ e̱ kyo̱ŋ, amɔ aketɔ asa kpeŋ, amo̱ e̱ gye̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi na te̱maa na ke̱kpa. Amaa amo̱ asa‑rɔ, ke̱mo̱ ne̱ ke̱ gye̱ bre̱sɛ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ke̱kpa.

ref: If you speak languages that others don't know, God will understand what you are saying, though no one else will know what you mean. You will be talking about mysteries that only the Spirit understands.
text: Ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa ko̱ ne̱ ɔ maa nu mò̱‑rɔ‑ɔ maa sa o gywii ase̱sɛ ne̱e̱. Ɔ sa o gywii Wuribware̱ ne̱e̱. Ɔko̱ maa nu ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔ tɔwe̱‑ɔ kaase̱. Ɔ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ kwe̱e̱rɔsɛ ne̱ aa le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o ase̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱, amaa ɔ maa nu amo̱ kaase̱.

ref: But when you prophesy, you will be understood, and others will be helped. They will be encouraged and made to feel better.
text: Amaa ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a lee bo̱ kaapo̱ mò̱‑ɔ e̱ tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ oo ŋu‑o o gywii ase̱sɛ ne̱e̱. Ne̱ a kya bamo̱‑rɔ, ne̱ ba kɔɔre̱ ba gyi bwe̱e̱tɔ, ne̱ a sa bamo̱ ŋkpo̱nɔkya.

ref: By speaking languages that others don't know, you help only yourself. But by prophesying you help everyone in the church.
text: Ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa ko̱ ne̱ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ maa nu mò̱‑rɔ‑ɔ e̱ kya mò̱ wo̱re̱ e̱ye̱e̱‑rɔ ne̱e̱. Amaa ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ e̱ kya mò̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana asɔre̱e̱ agyi‑ro ne̱e̱.

ref: My friends, what good would it do, if I came and spoke unknown languages to you and didn't explain what I meant? How would I help you, unless I told you what God had shown me or gave you some knowledge or prophecy or teaching?
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ, mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, mo̱ ya ba mo̱ne̱ ase̱, ne̱ mo̱ e̱ sa se̱ŋsa ko̱ ne̱ mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ maa nu mò̱‑rɔ‑ɔ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱, ntɔ to̱nɔ gbaa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ nya a mo̱nꞌ le̱e̱ mo̱ ase̱? A maa taare̱ a a sa mo̱ne̱ sɛye̱ pɛɛɛ, amɔ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a lee bo̱ kaapo̱ mo̱‑ɔ, bɛɛɛ mo̱ a ba kanyiase̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ sa mo̱‑ɔ bo̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ.

ref: This is how it is when you speak unknown languages. If no one can understand what you are talking about, you will only be talking to the wind.
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ gbaa mo̱ne̱ du. Mo̱ne̱ ya sa se̱ŋsa ko̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ fɔŋfɔŋ maa nu mò̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ ba waa a bo̱ nu ase̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ kaase̱? Mo̱ne̱ gye̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ sa se̱ŋsa mo̱ne̱ i gywii afwii ne̱e̱.

ref: But if I don't understand the language that someone is using, we will be like foreigners to each other.
text: Amaa mo̱ e̱ ma nu ɔko̱ se̱ŋsa, mo̱ aa mò̱ a kii afɔ abɛɛ ase̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: When we speak languages that others don't know, we should pray for the power to explain what we mean.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa ko̱ ne̱ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ maa nu mò̱‑rɔ‑ɔ ko̱re̱ Wuribware̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ sa mò̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ, na ɔ bo̱ kaapo̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔ tɔwe̱‑ɔ kaase̱.

ref: For example, if I use an unknown language in my prayers, my spirit prays but my mind is useless.
text: Mo̱ ya ba se̱ŋsa ne̱ ma nu mò̱‑rɔ‑ɔ bo̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱, mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ mo̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ wo̱re̱ ne̱e̱. Amaa mo̱ mfɛɛre̱‑rɔ fe̱raa sɛye̱ mo̱ŋ te‑ro.

ref: Then what should I do? There are times when I should pray with my spirit, and times when I should pray with my mind. Sometimes I should sing with my spirit, and at other times I should sing with my mind.
text: Nte̱tɔ ne̱ ŋ waa? Mo̱ e̱ ba mo̱ kakpo̱nɔ na mo̱ mfɛɛre̱ a m bo̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱, na m ba mo̱ kakpo̱nɔ na mo̱ mfɛɛre̱ bo̱ waa iliŋ.

ref: You may be worshiping God in a wonderful way, but no one else will be helped.
text: Hare̱e̱ gbaa fo̱ ya ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱, ne̱ fo̱ a sa aŋsɛ nɛɛnɛɛ, fo̱ se̱ŋsa‑ɔ mo̱ŋ kya fo̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana asɔre̱e̱ agyi‑o‑ro.

ref: I thank God that I speak unknown languages more than any of you.
text: Mo̱ e̱ sa Wuribware̱ aŋsɛ fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ sa se̱ŋsa ko̱ ne̱ mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ maa nu mò̱‑rɔ‑ɔ iluwi bwe̱e̱tɔ n kyɔ mo̱ne̱ ɔke̱maa.

ref: But words that make sense can help the church. This is why in church I would rather speak five words that make sense than to speak
text: Amaa mo̱ ya ba asɔre̱e̱‑rɔ bo̱ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋkpare̱gyi anuu le̱e̱ mo̱ mfɛɛre̱‑rɔ bo̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ atɔ, a bɔ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a sa ase̱ŋkpare̱gyi ŋkpe̱ŋ kanɔ ŋko̱ ne̱ mo̱ aa mo̱ne̱ maa nu mò̱‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: In the Scriptures the Lord says, “I will use strangers who speak unknown languages to talk to my people. They will speak to them in foreign languages, but still my people won't listen to me.”
text: Abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ, baa kyo̱rɛɛ fe̱yɛ, “Wuribware̱ yɛ, ‘Mo̱ e̱ sa a afɔ ne̱ ba le̱e̱ ke̱fɔ‑ɔ ba bamo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ se̱ŋsa bo̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ ase̱ŋ gywii mo̱ ase̱sɛ. Amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ gbaa ooo, bo̱ maa nu mo̱ ase̱ŋ.’ ”

ref: Languages others don't know may mean something to unbelievers, but not to the Lord's followers. Prophecy, on the other hand, is for followers, not for unbelievers.
text: Amo̱‑ɔ e̱ kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ de bamo̱ ne̱ ba sa se̱ŋsa ne̱ ɔko̱ maa nu kaase̱‑ɔ o yii bamo̱ ne̱ baa kine kunu Wuribware̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱. Amaa Wuribware̱ de bamo̱ ne̱ ba tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a lee bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱‑ɔ ɔ kya bamo̱ ne̱ ba kɔɔre̱ ba gyi‑o‑ro.

ref: But suppose all of you are prophesying when those unbelievers and outsiders come in. They will realize that they are sinners, and they will want to change their ways because of what you are saying.
text: Amaa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ ɔke̱maa e̱ tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a lee bo̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ asɔre̱e̱ kigyi‑o ya lweero, ne̱ o nu ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ kaase̱, mo̱ne̱ ase̱ŋkpare̱gyi e̱ kaapo̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ gye̱ e̱bɔye̱ ɔwaapo̱ ne̱e̱. Na ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔke̱maa e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ sa a o nu mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱, na o ŋu mò̱ bɔye̱ ne̱ ɔɔ waa‑ɔ.

ref: They will tell what is hidden in their hearts. Then they will kneel down and say to God, “We are certain that you are with these people.” Worship Must Be Orderly
text: Na mò̱ mfɛɛre̱ ne̱ ŋ kwe̱e̱rɔ‑ɔ lee mmo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ kawu. Na o kpuni aŋurii suŋ Wuribware̱, ŋkee o lee kanɔ‑rɔ a ɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Kase̱ŋtiŋ si Wuribware̱ bo̱ mo̱ne̱ ase̱ mfe̱e̱.”

ref: No more than two or three of you should speak unknown languages during the meeting. You must take turns, and someone should always be there to explain what you mean.
text: Fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ ko̱ e̱ kpa a bo̱ sa se̱ŋsa ne̱ bamo̱ ɔko̱ maa nu mò̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ sa a ase̱sɛ anyɔ bɛɛɛ bo̱ ma kyo̱ŋ asa, na bo̱ sa se̱ŋsa. Amaa bamo̱ pɛɛɛ ma kaŋ kpo̱ra sa se̱ŋsa.

ref: If someone sitting there receives a message from God, the speaker must stop and let the other person speak.
text: Ne̱ saŋ amo̱ Wuribware̱ ya lee ko̱tɔko̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ ɔko̱ ne̱ o te o nu ase̱ŋ‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ sa a mò̱ ne̱ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa‑ɔ laatɔ, pwɛɛ na mò̱ ne̱ o te‑o ko̱so̱ sa se̱ŋsa.

ref: Let only one person speak at a time, then all of you will learn something and be encouraged.
text: Mo̱ne̱ pɛɛɛ de kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱ tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a lee bo̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ ɔke̱maa‑ɔ, na mo̱nꞌ kaapo̱ abɛɛ Wuribware̱ ase̱ŋ, na mo̱nꞌ waa abɛɛ wɔre̱, amaa mo̱nꞌ waa amo̱‑ɔ ke̱gyase̱ ke̱gyase̱.

ref: A prophet should be willing to stop and let someone else speak.
text: Bamo̱ ne̱ ba tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a lee bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱‑ɔ de ke̱yaale̱ŋ fe̱yɛ bo̱ yɔwe̱ bamo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ e̱se̱ŋsa.

ref: God wants everything to be done peacefully and in order. When God's people meet in church,
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ maa kpa ase̱ŋ, amɔ kaye̱e̱yuri.

ref: If there is something they want to know, they can ask their husbands when they get home. It is disgraceful for women to speak in church.
text: Amaa ko̱tɔko̱ e̱ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱ e̱ kpa a o bise, mo̱nꞌ sa a o gywii, na bamo̱ ya yɔ pe̱, na o bise mò̱ kuri. A de ipeere fe̱yɛ ɔkye̱e̱ a sa se̱ŋsa asɔre̱e̱‑rɔ.

ref: God's message did not start with you people, and you are not the only ones it has reached.
text: E̱me̱ne̱ aaa? Mo̱ne̱ Korintoo awuye yɛ abware̱se̱ŋ a le̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ ase̱ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱? Mo̱ne̱ wo̱re̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a taa mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ bo̱ sa aaa?

ref: So don't pay attention to anyone who ignores what I am writing.
text: Amaa ne̱ ɔko̱ e̱ ma sure ase̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ se̱, amo̱ fe̱raa mo̱nꞌ yɔwe̱ mò̱ ase̱ŋ sa mò̱.

ref: But do everything properly and in order.
text: Amaa mo̱nꞌ waa ke̱tɔ ke̱maa nɛɛnɛɛ na kpa timaa si.

ref: You will be saved by this message, if you hold firmly to it. But if you don't, your faith was all for nothing.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ya de̱e̱ kra ase̱ŋ timaa mɔ dɛɛ‑rɔ, Wuribware̱ e̱ de̱e̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ mo̱ne̱, amɔ mo̱ne̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱ amo̱ gyi mo̱ŋ waa sɛye̱ mo̱ne̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ fe̱raa.

ref: I told you the most important part of the message exactly as it was told to me. This part is: Christ died for our sins, as the Scriptures say.
text: Atɔ ne̱ bo̱ko̱ a kaapo̱ mo̱‑ɔ dɛɛ ne̱ mo̱‑ɔ mo̱ a kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱‑ɔ. Atɔ ke̱kaapo̱ ne̱ a tiri bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ Kristoo a wu ne̱e̱ a ɔ taare̱ kpɛɛ ane̱ e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ twe̱e̱, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: He was buried, and three days later he was raised to life, as the Scriptures say.
text: Ke̱mo̱ ne̱ ke̱ gye̱ ke̱nyɔse̱po̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ baa pure mò̱, ne̱ kake sase̱po̱‑ɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kyiŋŋi mò̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: After this, he appeared to more than five hundred other followers. Most of them are still alive, but some have died.
text: Ne̱ oo lee mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱ alafa anuu saŋ ko̱ŋko̱. Ase̱sɛ mɔ‑rɔ kafwe̱e̱ a wu, amaa bamo̱‑rɔ bo̱ko̱ mɔ saŋ bo̱ bo̱‑rɔ mbe̱yɔmɔ.

ref: He also appeared to James, then to all of the apostles.
text: Ne̱ oo lee mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ Yakubu, ne̱ amo̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ ne̱ oo lee mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ mò̱ ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ.

ref: Finally, he appeared to me, even though I am like someone who was born at the wrong time.
text: Ne̱ lalalo̱we̱‑ɔ, oo lee mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ mo̱, mo̱ ne̱ n du fe̱yɛ kayaagyi po̱pwɛɛ ne̱ baa ko̱we̱ mò̱, na mò̱ ko̱ko̱we̱be̱e̱ a kyɔ kyo̱ŋ pwɛɛ ne̱ ba dɛɛ ko̱we̱ mò̱‑ɔ.

ref: I am the least important of all the apostles. In fact, I caused so much trouble for God's church that I don't even deserve to be called an apostle.
text: Mo̱ e̱ gye̱ ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ‑rɔ pɛɛɛ keegyi. Kase̱ŋtiŋ si, mo̱ŋ fo̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ kii ayaa‑rɔ ɔye̱re̱po̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a dɛɛ waa Wuribware̱ asɔre̱e̱ awuye awo̱re̱fɔɔ.

ref: But God treated me with undeserved grace! He made me what I am, and his grace wasn't wasted. I worked much harder than any of the other apostles, although it was really God's grace at work and not me.
text: Amaa Wuribware̱ a ŋu mo̱ e̱wɛɛ, ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ mo̱ a kii ke̱tɔ ne̱ n gye̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ. Ɔ mo̱ŋ ŋu mo̱ e̱wɛɛ kwaa ne̱e̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a suŋ keŋkeŋ sa mò̱ kyo̱ŋ mò̱ ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ ɔke̱maa. Amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ gbaa n gye̱ mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ke̱yaale̱ŋ ne̱e̱, Wuribware̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ a bo̱ waa.

ref: But it doesn't matter if I preached or if they preached. All of you believed the message just the same. God's People Will Be Raised to Life
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, hare̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ a nu ase̱ŋ timaa‑o le̱e̱ mo̱ ase̱ ne̱e̱ ooo, bɛɛɛ bamo̱ ase̱ ne̱e̱ ooo, amo̱ pɛɛɛ gye̱ ako̱ŋko̱ ne̱e̱. A gye̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ane̱ pɛɛɛ e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱. Ne̱ a gye̱ atɔ ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ne̱ i gyi‑o ne̱e̱.

ref: If we preach that Christ was raised from death, how can some of you say the dead will not be raised to life?
text: To, Wuribware̱ kikyiŋŋi Kristoo le̱e̱ lowi‑ro ne̱ ane̱ ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱ pɛɛɛ e̱ tɔwe̱ ane̱ i gywii ase̱sɛ‑ɔ. Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ ane̱ŋ fe̱raa, ne̱ e̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ bo̱ko̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ne̱ baa kyɔ wu‑o maa lɛɛ kyiŋŋi a bo̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro?

ref: If they won't be raised to life, Christ himself wasn't raised to life.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ dɛɛ ke̱ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱e̱, weetee a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ mo̱ŋ kyiŋŋi Kristoo le̱e̱ lowi‑ro.

ref: And if Christ wasn't raised to life, our message is worthless, and so is your faith.
text: Ne̱ amo̱ e̱ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ mo̱ŋ kyiŋŋi Kristoo le̱e̱ lowi‑ro fe̱raa, abware̱se̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ gye̱ kwaa ne̱e̱, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ mɔ mo̱ne̱ a kɔɔre̱ gyi kwaa.

ref: If the dead won't be raised to life, we have told lies about God by saying he raised Christ to life, when he really did not.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ko̱ kyɔ, ne̱ amo̱‑ɔ be̱e̱ a kaapo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ abware̱se̱ŋ ko̱tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kyiŋŋi Kristoo‑o gye̱ kaye̱ba ne̱e̱.

ref: Unless Christ was raised to life, your faith is useless, and you are still living in your sins.
text: Ne̱ amo̱ e̱ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ mo̱ŋ kyiŋŋi Kristoo le̱e̱ lowi‑ro, amo̱ fe̱raa mo̱ne̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi‑o maa sa mo̱ne̱ sɛye̱, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ be̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ saŋ mo̱ne̱ te mo̱ne̱ e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ.

ref: If our hope in Christ is good only for this life, we are worse off than anyone else.
text: Ne̱ amo̱ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ dooo ne̱ ane̱ i de te̱maa Kristoo si, amo̱ fe̱raa ane̱ ase̱ŋ e̱ waa e̱wɛɛ a a kyo̱ŋ ɔke̱maa kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ.

ref: But we must each wait our turn. Christ was the first to be raised to life, and his people will be raised to life when he returns.
text: Amaa n gye̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ i kyiŋŋi luwi ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱e̱. Ane̱ i kyiŋŋi ke̱gyase̱ ke̱gyase̱. Wuribware̱ a kyɔ gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ kyiŋŋi Kristoo bo̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ gya se̱, ne̱ o kyiŋŋi‑o e̱ gye̱ Kristoo awuye, Kristoo ke̱bo̱ ba nyɔse̱po̱‑ɔ.

ref: When the Scriptures say he will put everything under his power, they don't include God. It was God who put everything under the power of Christ.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ, mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ a kare̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ a sa ne̱ atɔ pɛɛɛ bo̱ mò̱ kuwure kaase̱.” Wuribware̱ mo̱ŋ tii ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ bo̱ mò̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ se̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ ya sa ne̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa a lwee Kristoo ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ.

ref: After everything is under the power of God's Son, he will put himself under the power of God, who put everything under his Son's power. Then God will mean everything to everyone.
text: Amaa saŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi Kristoo a nya ke̱tɔ ke̱maa se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ‑ɔ, ɔ taa mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a ɔ bo̱ waa mò̱ se̱ Wuribware̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ, na Wuribware̱ de̱e̱re̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa se̱.

ref: and face death every day? The pride that I have in you because of Christ Jesus our Lord is what makes me say this.
text: Kase̱ŋtiŋ si mo̱ e̱ gyaŋŋaa lowi kake ke̱maa. Ma ba aye̱ba. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ se̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mbe̱yɔmɔ mo̱ne̱ tii ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo ko̱ko̱we̱bɛɛ se̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: Be sensible and stop sinning. You should be embarrassed that some people still don't know about God. What Our Bodies Will Be Like
text: Mo̱nꞌ nu mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱, na mo̱nꞌ kiŋŋi le̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ. A gye̱ ipeere sa mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ bo̱ko̱ saŋ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi Wuribware̱‑ɔ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ ya kyiŋŋi se̱sɛ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro, ne̱ mò̱ kayo̱wɔre̱ i kii‑o

ref: Some of you have asked, “How will the dead be raised to life? What kind of bodies will they have?”
text: Mo̱ne̱‑rɔ ɔko̱ i bise mo̱ fe̱yɛ, “To, e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa wu‑o e̱ be̱e̱ waa a bo̱ kyiŋŋi ba ŋkpa‑rɔ? Nte̱tɔ kayo̱wɔre̱ ne̱ ba nya?”

ref: Wheat seeds and all other seeds look different from the sprouts that come up.
text: N gye̱ kiyii ne̱ kaa daŋ lo̱we̱‑ɔ ne̱ fo̱ i dwii. Ke̱mo̱ kigyi ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱ fo̱ i dwii fe̱yɛ abwaye bɛɛɛ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa.

ref: This is because God gives everything the kind of body he wants it to have.
text: Amaa Wuribware̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ atɔ dwiisɛ ko̱ kwe̱e̱ ko̱ko̱‑rɔ.

ref: People, animals, birds, and fish are each made of flesh, but none of them are alike.
text: Nyiŋkpasɛ kayo̱wɔre̱ kwe̱e̱ mbo̱ lee‑ro. Mbo̱gyii lee kwe̱e̱. Ne̱ ŋkiŋgyi lee mɔ be̱e̱ ŋ kwe̱e̱.

ref: Everything in the heavens has a body, and so does everything on earth. But each one is very different from all the others.
text: Ke̱tɔ ke̱maa mɔ ne̱ ke̱ bo̱ swe̱e̱re̱ mɔ se̱‑ɔ kwe̱e̱ ke̱mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑rɔ. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ akye̱e̱‑e̱‑kpa‑agyi ne̱ a bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ kwe̱e̱ swe̱e̱re̱ mɔ se̱ atɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ ke̱kye̱e̱‑e̱‑kpa‑agyi ke̱maa kwe̱e̱ ke̱mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑rɔ ne̱ ki de ke̱mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ daŋ. Ne̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ atɔ mɔ de amo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ daŋ.

ref: The sun isn't like the moon, the moon isn't like the stars, and each star is different.
text: Kyɔwe̱ de mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ daŋ, ne̱ kiferi de ke̱mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ daŋ, ne̱ ke̱kye̱e̱‑e̱‑kpa‑agyi ke̱maa de ke̱mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ daŋ ne̱ ɔ kwe̱e̱‑ɔ.

ref: That's how it will be when our bodies are raised to life. These bodies will die, but the bodies that are raised will live forever.
text: Mfaanɛɛ dɛɛ ne̱ a waa saŋ ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa wu‑o i kyiŋŋi a bo̱ ba ŋkpa‑rɔ. Bamo̱ ya pure se̱sɛ ne̱ oo wu‑o, mò̱ kayo̱wɔre̱ a kii kifuniŋ, na ku gyo swe̱e̱re̱‑rɔ. Wuribware̱ ya kyiŋŋi kifuniŋ ba ŋkpa‑rɔ, ɔ maa lɛɛ wu daa.

ref: As surely as there are physical bodies, there are spiritual bodies. And our physical bodies will be changed into spiritual bodies.
text: Bamo̱ ya pure se̱sɛ, kifuniŋ ne̱ ki de ayeegyi na abowii ne̱ baa pure, ne̱ mò̱ ya be̱e̱ kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro, amɔ se̱sɛ‑ɔ e̱ nya kayo̱wɔre̱ po̱pwɛɛ, na mò̱ kra be̱e̱ bo̱ kye̱na kamo̱‑rɔ.

ref: The first man was named Adam, and the Scriptures tell us that he was a living person. But Jesus, who may be called the last Adam, is a life-giving spirit.
text: Baa kyo̱rɛɛ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, “Wuribware̱ a twe̱e̱ se̱sɛ gye̱ŋkpɛɛsɛ Adam, ne̱ oo kii nyiŋkpasɛ, ne̱ ɔɔ kye̱na.” Amaa Kristoo, ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ Adam lalalo̱we̱‑ɔ, a yɔ so̱so̱ ya kii ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ sa ŋkpa‑ɔ.

ref: We see that the one with a spiritual body did not come first. He came after the one who had a physical body.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ amɔ nyiŋkpasɛ de mò̱ kayo̱wɔre̱ ne̱ ka de ayeegyi na abowii‑o pwɛɛ na ɔ dɛɛ taare̱ nya mò̱ kayo̱wɔre̱ po̱pwɛɛ.

ref: The first man was made from the dust of the earth, but the second man came from heaven.
text: Adam ne̱ ɔ gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ‑ɔ, e̱se̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a bo̱ pwɛɛ mò̱, e̱se̱‑rɔ ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ ba. Amaa Adam nyɔse̱po̱ ne̱ mò̱ e̱ gye̱ Kristoo‑o fe̱raa a le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ se̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: Everyone on earth has a body like the body of the one who was made from the dust of the earth. And everyone in heaven has a body like the body of the one who came from heaven.
text: Ane̱ se̱sɛ dimaadi, ane̱ a gye̱ kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ane̱ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ e̱se̱‑rɔ ba‑ɔ. Bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ se̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ se̱‑ɔ.

ref: It will happen suddenly, quicker than the blink of an eye. At the sound of the last trumpet the dead will be raised. We will all be changed, so we will never die again.
text: Mo̱nꞌ nu ase̱ŋ kwe̱e̱rɔsɛ mɔ nɛɛnɛɛ, n gye̱ ane̱ pɛɛɛ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ane̱ i wu. Amaa Wuribware̱ kabe̱re̱ ya su kake ne̱ kaye̱ e̱ gye̱ kɛɛ‑ɔ, fe̱yɛ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ mɔ‑ɔ, ane̱ pɛɛɛ i kyurowi puri‑ro puri‑ro. Saŋ ne̱ ane̱ŋ a kabe̱re̱‑ɔ i su‑o, Wuribware̱ i kyiŋŋi bamo̱ ne̱ baa wu‑o, na bo̱ ma lɛɛ wu daa. Na ane̱ pɛɛɛ kyurowi.

ref: Our dead and decaying bodies will be changed into bodies that won't die or decay.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ kaa gyo‑o i kyiŋŋi a ke̱ ba ŋkpa‑rɔ, na ke̱ ma lɛɛ gyo pɛɛɛ. Na ke̱tɔ ne̱ kaa wu‑o kye̱na, na ke̱ ma lɛɛ wu daa.

ref: The bodies we now have are weak and can die. But they will be changed into bodies that are eternal. Then the Scriptures will come true, “Death has lost the battle!
text: Amo̱‑ɔ ya ba, ke̱tɔ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ e̱ ba kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ, “Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Nyaŋpe̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ nye̱ra lowi.”

ref: My dear friends, stand firm and don't be shaken. Always keep busy working for the Lord. You know that everything you do for him is worthwhile.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, mo̱nꞌ ye̱re̱ keŋkeŋ mo̱ne̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi‑ro. Na mo̱nꞌ waa wɔre̱ mo̱ne̱ kusuŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i suŋ mo̱ne̱ e̱ sa ane̱ nyaŋpe̱‑ɔ‑rɔ saŋ ke̱maa, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ asuŋ ko̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i kperi ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ se̱ a mo̱nꞌ waa, na mo̱nꞌ waa amo̱ kwaa.

ref: Choose some followers to take the money to Jerusalem. I will send them on with the money and with letters which show that you approve of them.
text: Mo̱ ya ba‑ɔ, ase̱sɛ ke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a gyaŋŋe̱ lee fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i suŋ a mo̱nꞌ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, mo̱ e̱ sa bamo̱ e̱wo̱re̱ ne̱ e̱e̱ sa bamo̱ kpa fe̱yɛ bo̱ taa atanne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a twe̱e̱twe̱e̱ nya‑ɔ yaa Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ.

ref: If you think I should go along, they can go with me. Paul's Travel Plans
text: Ne̱ amo̱ i bware fe̱yɛ ŋ yɔ, amo̱ fe̱raa mo̱ aa bamo̱ e̱ taare̱ a ane̱ yɔ. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ Pɔɔl a kra ko̱waa‑ɔ

ref: After I have gone through Macedonia, I hope to see you
text: Mo̱ e̱ bo̱rɔ Makedoniya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱, na m bo̱ ba mo̱ne̱ ase̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ a tiri fe̱yɛ m bo̱rɔ mfe̱ŋ pwɛɛ.

ref: and visit with you for a while. I may even stay all winter, so that you can help me on my way to wherever I will be going next.
text: Nsaŋse̱ mo̱ ya ba, mo̱ i kyee mo̱ne̱ ase̱ kafwe̱e̱. Saŋse̱ mo̱ e̱ kye̱na mo̱ne̱ ase̱ afwii ke̱be̱e̱ mɔ pɛɛɛ, na mo̱nꞌ taare̱ kya mo̱‑rɔ a n kya mo̱ kpa‑ɔ se̱ yɔ to̱ŋ ko̱ baŋbaŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ le̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ ase̱ a ŋ yɔ‑ɔ.

ref: If the Lord lets me, I would rather come later for a longer visit than to stop off now for only a short visit.
text: Mo̱ e̱ kpa kuŋu mo̱ne̱. N gye̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ ŋu mo̱ne̱ na n kyo̱ŋ. Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ ya sure, n de te̱maa fe̱yɛ mo̱ i gyi ŋke bwe̱e̱tɔ mo̱ne̱ ase̱.

ref: I will stay in Ephesus until Pentecost,
text: Mo̱ e̱ kye̱na Ifisus maŋ‑nɔ mfe̱e̱ a Pentikos kake‑o bo̱ fo̱.

ref: because there is a wonderful opportunity for me to do some work here. But there are also many people who are against me.
text: Hare̱e̱ ado̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ a bo̱ mfe̱e̱ gbaa ooo, kpa da sa mo̱ mfe̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ waa asuŋ dabe̱ ne̱ amo̱ ase̱ŋ tiri ko̱waa‑ɔ.

ref: When Timothy arrives, give him a friendly welcome. He is doing the Lord's work, just as I am.
text: Timotii ya ba mo̱ne̱ ase̱, mo̱nꞌ sa a mò̱ akatɔ gyi mo̱ne̱ ase̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ o suŋ ɔ sa ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱‑ɔ dɛɛ.

ref: Don't let anyone mistreat him. I am looking for him to return to me together with the other followers. So when he leaves, send him off with your blessings.
text: Ɔko̱ ma kaŋ trasii mò̱, amaa mo̱nꞌ kya mò̱‑rɔ a ɔ kya mò̱ kpa se̱ ŋkpa na alaŋfiya‑ro, na ɔ taare̱ kiŋŋi ba mo̱ ase̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ aa ane̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana ne̱ mo̱ e̱ de̱e̱re̱ kpa.

ref: I have tried hard to get our friend Apollos to visit you with the other followers. He doesn't want to come just now, but he will come when he can. Personal Concerns and Greetings
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ ane̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ Apolos ase̱ŋ. Saŋ ke̱maa mo̱ e̱ waa mò̱ wɔre̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ aa ane̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana amo̱ ba bo̱ ka mo̱ne̱ kanɔ, amaa ɔ mo̱ŋ sure si fe̱yɛ ɔ ba saŋ mɔ se̱. Amaa mò̱ ya kaŋ bo̱ nya kpa, ɔ ba mo̱ne̱ ase̱. Kanɔka ne̱ ka gye̱ kɛɛ‑ɔ

ref: Keep alert. Be firm in your faith. Stay brave and strong.
text: Mo̱nꞌ sa se̱, mo̱nꞌ ye̱re̱ mo̱ne̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi‑o‑ro keŋkeŋ, mo̱nꞌ waa ŋkpo̱nɔ, na mo̱nꞌ waa e̱le̱ŋ mɔ.

ref: Show love in everything you do.
text: Mo̱nꞌ ba ke̱kpa bo̱ suŋ mo̱ne̱ asuŋ pɛɛɛ.

ref: You know that Stephanas and his family were the first in Achaia to have faith in the Lord. They have done all they can for God's people. My friends, I ask you
text: Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, mo̱ne̱ nyi Stifaŋ na mò̱ ka‑ana na mò̱ gyi‑ana ase̱ŋ. Bamo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ baa gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ kii Kristoo awuye Akaya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱. Ne̱ baa lee bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ de̱e̱ suŋ bo̱ kya mfe̱ŋ a Wuribware̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: to obey leaders like them and to do the same for all others who work hard with you.
text: Mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ mo̱ne̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ gya ane̱ŋ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ kanɔ se̱. Na mo̱nꞌ bu ɔke̱maa ne̱ o tii bamo̱ se̱ bamo̱ kusuŋ‑no‑o ane̱ŋ dɛɛ.

ref: Greetings from the churches in Asia. Aquila and Priscilla, together with the church that meets in their house, send greetings in the name of the Lord.
text: Asɔre̱e̱ asɔre̱e̱ ne̱ a bo̱ Asiya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ awuye e̱ ka mo̱ne̱ kanɔ. Akwila na Prisikila na asɔre̱e̱ agyi ne̱ ba gyaŋŋe̱ bamo̱ aye̱ ba waa asɔre̱e̱‑ɔ de ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ ba ka mo̱ne̱ kanɔ.

ref: All the Lord's followers send their greetings. Give each other a warm greeting.
text: Ane̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana pɛɛɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱e̱‑ɔ e̱ ka mo̱ne̱ kanɔ. Mo̱nꞌ gywaa abɛɛ.

ref: I pray that God will put a curse on everyone who doesn't love the Lord. And may the Lord come soon.
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ maa kpa ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo ase̱ŋ‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ da mò̱ yii. Ŋ yɛ, “Maranata!” Kaase̱ e̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ, “O ane̱ nyaŋpe̱, ba!”

ref: I pray the Lord Jesus will be kind to you.
text: Mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo yure mo̱ne̱.

ref: I love everyone who belongs to Christ Jesus.
text: Mo̱ ke̱kpa sii mo̱ne̱ pɛɛɛ ase̱ Kristoo Yeesuu ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ.

ref: From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Jesus Christ, and from Timothy, who is also a follower. To God's church in Corinth and to all of God's people in Achaia.
text: Mo̱, Pɔɔl, ne̱ Wuribware̱ a lee fe̱yɛ ŋ kii Kristoo Yeesuu ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ ɔko̱ na ane̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ Timotii e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ kyo̱rɛɛ wo̱re̱ mɔ ane̱ e̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ mo̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ asɔre̱e̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Korintoo maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ na Wuribware̱ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Akaya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ.

ref: I pray that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! Paul Gives Thanks
text: Ane̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ se̱ Wuribware̱ na ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo i yure mo̱ne̱, na bo̱ sa mo̱ne̱ kaye̱e̱yuri. Ase̱ŋ ya to̱ ane̱, Wuribware̱ e̱ kya ane̱‑rɔ

ref: Praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! The Father is a merciful God, who always gives us comfort.
text: Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱, ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo mò̱ se̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ gye̱ e̱wɛɛŋupo̱ ne̱ ɔ sa ane̱ kakpo̱nɔkya‑ɔ.

ref: He comforts us when we are in trouble, so that we can share this same comfort with others in trouble.
text: Ase̱ŋ ya to̱ ane̱, mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ sa ane̱ kakpo̱nɔkya. Ne̱ ane̱ mɔ ane̱ de kakpo̱nɔkya dɛɛ ne̱ ane̱ a nya le̱e̱ mò̱ ase̱‑ɔ ane̱ e̱ kya bo̱ko̱ ne̱ bamo̱ gbaa, ase̱ŋ a to̱ bamo̱‑ɔ ŋkpo̱nɔ.

ref: We share in the terrible sufferings of Christ, but also in the wonderful comfort he gives.
text: Hare̱e̱ gbaa awo̱re̱fɔɔ suyo ne̱ Kristoo a gyi‑o a to̱ ane̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ ooo, kakpo̱nɔkya ne̱ ane̱ a nya le̱e̱ mò̱ ase̱‑ɔ gbaa kyɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: You never disappoint us. You suffered as much as we did, and we know that you will be comforted as we were.
text: Kase̱ŋtiŋ si, ane̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ ane̱ i gyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ ya gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ gya Kristoo a e̱kpa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ se̱, ɔɔ sa mo̱ne̱ kakpo̱nɔkya dɛɛ ne̱ ɔɔ sa ane̱‑ɔ.

ref: In fact, we felt sure we were going to die. But this made us stop trusting in ourselves and start trusting God, who raises the dead to life.
text: Ane̱ a fa fe̱yɛ ane̱ e̱ kpa a ane̱ wu‑o, ŋkpa ke̱maa mo̱ŋ lɛɛ m bo̱‑rɔ. Amaa amo̱‑ɔ a waa ne̱e̱ a ane̱ ma taa ane̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ ane̱ fɔŋfɔŋ e̱ye̱e̱ se̱, amaa na ane̱ taa e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ Wuribware̱ se̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ taare̱ a o kyiŋŋi bamo̱ ne̱ baa wu‑o ba ŋkpa‑rɔ.

ref: God saved us from the threat of death, and we are sure that he will do it again and again.
text: Ane̱ i dee a ane̱ wu, amaa oo lee ane̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro, ne̱ ɔ be̱e̱ o lee ane̱. Ne̱ mo̱ne̱ ya ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ bo̱ tii ane̱ lee si, amo̱ fe̱raa ane̱ de te̱maa bwe̱e̱tɔ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ de̱e̱ lee ane̱.

ref: I was so sure of your pride in us that I had planned to visit you first of all. In this way you would have the blessing of two visits from me—
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ a kɔɔre̱ gyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ akatɔ a gyi ane̱ se̱‑ɔ se̱, ne̱ mo̱ a gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ kra siraa fe̱yɛ m bo̱ ka mo̱ne̱ kanɔ iluwi e̱nyɔ.

ref: God can be trusted, and so can I, when I say our answer to you has always been “Yes” and never “No.”
text: Wuribware̱ a de kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o si‑o, ke̱tɔ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ ane̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ ɔɔŋ na daabii kanɔlee saŋ ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: This is because Jesus Christ the Son of God is always “Yes” and never “No.” And he is the one Silas, Timothy, and I told you about.
text: Saŋ ne̱ mo̱ aa Silobaanus na Timotii a ba bo̱ tɔwe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo, Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑o, ase̱ŋ gywii mo̱ne̱‑ɔ, ane̱ mo̱ŋ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ gye̱ se̱sɛ ko̱ ne̱, mò̱ ya tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ, ne̱ ɔ be̱e̱ o kyurowi kanɔ‑ɔ.

ref: Christ says “Yes” to all God's promises. This is why we have Christ to say “Amen” for us to the glory of God.
text: Amaa fe̱raa atɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ ɔ waa‑ɔ, Kristoo e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ waa ane̱ŋ a atɔ‑ɔ. Amo̱se̱ se̱ ne̱ mò̱ ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ ane̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ Amɛye̱‑ɔ , na Wuribware̱ nya ke̱dabe̱ na wuraa.

ref: I have decided not to make my next visit with you so painful.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ a waa mo̱ mfɛɛre̱ fe̱yɛ ma ba mo̱ne̱ ase̱ saŋ amo̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ a sa a mo̱ne̱ akatɔ ma gyi.

ref: If I make you feel bad, who would be left to cheer me up, except the people I had made to feel bad?
text: Mo̱ ya sa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ akatɔ mo̱ŋ gyi, ne̱ ŋkee nsɛ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ sa a mo̱‑ɔ mo̱ akatɔ gyi? Amɔ mo̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ a sa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ akatɔ mo̱ŋ gyi‑o dooo.

ref: The reason I want to be happy is to make you happy. I wrote as I did because I didn't want to visit you and be made to feel bad, when you should make me feel happy.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ wo̱re̱ ko̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ mo̱ne̱ tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ŋ ba‑ɔ gywii mo̱ne̱. Ne̱ mo̱ a be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ aa mo̱ne̱ de‑o na, m bo̱ ba‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ a kyɔ lɔŋŋɔ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa. Mo̱ a nyi ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ du‑o si‑o, ne̱ mo̱ a kɔɔre̱ gyi fe̱yɛ, mo̱ akatɔ ya gyi, mo̱ne̱ fɔŋfɔŋ akatɔ mɔ i gyi dɛɛ.

ref: At the time I wrote, I was suffering terribly. My eyes were full of tears, and my heart was broken. But I didn't want to make you feel bad. I only wanted to let you know how much I cared for you. Forgiveness
text: Mo̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ mo̱ne̱ wo̱re̱ amo̱‑ɔ, aa tɔɔraa mo̱, ne̱ mo̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ a nye̱ra. Mo̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ wo̱re̱ amo̱‑ɔ na akyukyu e̱ gyaa. N gye̱ sa ne̱ mo̱ŋ kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ akatɔ gyi. Amaa mo̱ a kpa ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ gyii ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa mo̱ne̱ pɛɛɛ mɔ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱e̱. Mo̱nꞌ taa ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ waa bɔye̱, ne̱ oo nu mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱‑ɔ bɔye̱ bo̱ ke mò̱

ref: I don't want to be hard on you. But if one of you has made someone feel bad, I am not really the one who has been made to feel bad. Some of you are the ones.
text: Wo̱re̱ amo̱‑rɔ ne̱ mo̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ mo̱ne̱ ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ waa bɔye̱‑ɔ ase̱ŋ. Ane̱ŋ a bɔye̱ ne̱ ɔɔ waa‑ɔ a sa ne̱ mo̱ akyukyu a gyaa. Amaa n gye̱ mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ne̱ ɔɔ waa bɔye̱ bo̱ kye. Mo̱ e̱ fa fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ asɔre̱e̱ awuye ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ ipeere a mɔɔ, ne̱ ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ a nye̱ra. Amaa mbe̱yɔmɔ ma kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ pee akatɔ we̱e̱ mò̱ se̱, na a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ.

ref: Most of you have already pointed out the wrong that person did, and this is punishment enough for what was done.
text: Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ a fa mfɛɛre̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ gyiiri mò̱ ke̱se̱bɔ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a gya mò̱ le̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ asɔre̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ mo̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ mò̱ ke̱se̱bɔgyiiri ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a gyiiri mò̱‑ɔ a baare.

ref: When people sin, you should forgive and comfort them, so they won't give up in despair.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, saŋ a fo̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ taa bo̱ ke mò̱, na mo̱nꞌ yuri mò̱ kakpo̱nɔ. N gye̱ ane̱ŋ, mò̱ ase̱ŋ e̱ waa e̱wɛɛ, na mò̱ te̱maa pɛɛɛ lo̱we̱.

ref: You should make them sure of your love for them.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ mo̱ne̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ kaapo̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa mò̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: I also wrote because I wanted to test you and find out if you would follow my instructions.
text: Ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ wo̱re̱ amo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ kyɔ mo̱ne̱‑rɔ mo̱ e̱ ke̱e̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ i bu mo̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa‑rɔ, bɛɛɛ mo̱ne̱ maa bu mo̱.

ref: I will forgive anyone you forgive. Yes, for your sake and with Christ as my witness, I have forgiven whatever needed to be forgiven.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ya taa ɔko̱ e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ ke mò̱, amo̱ fe̱raa mo̱‑ɔ mo̱ e̱ taa mò̱ e̱bɔye̱ a m bo̱ ke mò̱. Ne̱ ko̱tɔko̱ e̱ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ a tiri fe̱yɛ mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ taa bo̱ ke, mo̱ne̱ se̱‑ɔ mo̱ a taa bo̱ ke mò̱ Kristoo akatɔ‑rɔ,

ref: I have done this to keep Satan from getting the better of us. We all know what goes on in his mind.
text: na a taare̱ kuŋ Ɔbɔnsam, na ɔ ma taare̱ nya ane̱ se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ. Ane̱ nyi nɛɛnɛɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Ɔbɔnsam e̱ kpa a ɔ pe̱nna ane̱‑ɔ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Pɔɔl mo̱ŋ nya kaye̱e̱yuri Trowas maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ

ref: When I went to Troas to preach the good news about Christ, I found that the Lord had already prepared the way.
text: Saŋ ne̱ mo̱ a yɔ Trowas maŋ‑nɔ fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ ya tɔwe̱ Kristoo kuŋu si ase̱ŋ timaa‑o, mo̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu a buŋŋi kpa sa mo̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ waa mò̱ kusuŋ.

ref: But I was worried when I didn't find my friend Titus there. So I left the other followers and went on to Macedonia.
text: Amaa na mo̱ mfɛɛre̱ mo̱ŋ da, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ŋ ŋu ane̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ Tiitus. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ a kra ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ a me̱raa se̱ yɔ Makedoniya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱. Ane̱ gye̱ Kristoo kamɛɛ‑rɔ awuye na mò̱ ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱

ref: I am grateful that God always makes it possible for Christ to lead us to victory. God also helps us spread the knowledge about Christ everywhere, and this knowledge is like the smell of perfume.
text: Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ sa Wuribware̱ aŋsɛ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ naa ɔ kaa ɔ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ Kristoo a kɔ gyi Ɔbɔnsam se̱. Mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱, ne̱ ane̱ gye̱ mò̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ awuye. O de ane̱ ɔ tɔwe̱ mò̱ kuŋu si ase̱ŋ to̱ŋ ke̱maa. Mò̱ kuŋu si ase̱ŋ mɔ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ sɔ kɔne̱kɔne̱sɛ‑ɔ.

ref: In fact, God thinks of us as a perfume that brings Christ to everyone. For people who are being saved, this perfume has a sweet smell and leads them to a better life. But for people who are lost, it has a bad smell and leads them to a horrible death. No one really has what it takes to do this work.
text: Ane̱ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ nyoŋku ne̱ Kristoo e̱ kywɛɛ ɔ sa Wuribware̱‑ɔ. Mò̱ sɔ e̱ gya kɔne̱ kɔne̱ sa bamo̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ sa ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ. Amaa mò̱ sɔ gye̱ ke̱bwɛɛ sa bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ maa nya ŋkpa amo̱‑ɔ. Nsɛ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔɔ fo̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ ye̱re̱ mfaanɛɛ a ye̱re̱kpa‑ɔ? Ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ.

ref: You are like a letter written by Christ and delivered by us. But you are not written with pen and ink or on tablets made of stone. You are written in our hearts by the Spirit of the living God.
text: Ne̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ gya Kristoo a e̱kpa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ e̱ kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ ke̱kaapo̱ bo̱ daŋ. N gye̱ pɛɛn ne̱ Kristoo a bo̱ kyo̱rɛɛ mo̱ne̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ se̱‑ɔ. N gye̱ se̱kaŋ ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ kyaŋŋe̱ afo̱re̱ se̱ kyo̱rɛɛ‑ɔ. Amaa Wuribware̱ ne̱ mò̱ wo̱re̱ kpeŋ te‑o kufwiiŋe‑o ya ba mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ke̱sare̱e̱ bo̱ kyo̱rɛɛ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ mo̱ne̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ se̱.

ref: We don't have the right to claim that we have done anything on our own. God gives us what it takes to do all we do.
text: Sɛye̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱ ane̱‑rɔ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ ye̱re̱ amo̱ se̱ ane̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ amo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ a sa ne̱ ane̱ e̱ taare̱ ane̱ e̱ waa kusuŋ mɔ. Ane̱ kanyiase̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ de‑o a le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: He makes us worthy to be the servants of his new agreement that comes from the Holy Spirit and not from a written Law. After all, the Law brings death, but the Spirit brings life.
text: Mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ ane̱ a kii e̱taŋ po̱pwɛɛ asɔfo̱wɔ. E̱taŋ de̱daa‑ɔ na mò̱ mbraa‑ɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a sa Mosis fe̱yɛ ɔ kyo̱rɛɛ bo̱ be̱ya sa Isireelii awuye‑o. Mbraa‑ɔ i bu ane̱ ɔke̱maa ke̱pɔ, na a bware fe̱yɛ ane̱ wu, amaa Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o e̱ sa ane̱ ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: The Law of Moses brought only the promise of death, even though it was carved on stones and given in a wonderful way. Still the Law made Moses' face shine so brightly the people of Israel could not look at it, even though it was a fading glory.
text: Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ fa ane̱ŋ ne̱ kusuŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o i suŋ‑o kyɔ Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ lee‑o ke̱dabe̱ na mò̱ ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ. Wuribware̱ ya kyo̱rɛɛ mò̱ mbraa bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ afo̱re̱ serisɛ paŋpe̱saŋpaŋ anyɔ se̱ ke̱be̱e̱ ko̱ se̱, ne̱ oo lee mò̱ ke̱dabe̱ na mò̱ ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ kawu saŋ amo̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ Mosis. Saŋ ne̱ Mosis a kpo̱ro̱we̱ le̱e̱ ke̱be̱e̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱ akatɔ‑rɔ e̱ to̱re̱ se̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ, ne̱ Isireelii awuye mo̱ŋ taare̱ de̱e̱re̱ mò̱. Amaa kamɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ko̱to̱re̱se̱ amo̱ a yɔwe̱.

ref: In fact, the new agreement is so wonderful that the Law is no longer glorious at all.
text: Kase̱ŋtiŋ si, fo̱ ya de̱e̱re̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Mosis a kusuŋ‑o gye̱ ke̱dabe̱‑ɔ, ne̱ fo̱ ya be̱e̱ de̱e̱re̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ kusuŋ‑o gye̱ ke̱dabe̱‑ɔ, a du fe̱yɛ Mosis a kusuŋ‑o mo̱ŋ lɛɛ ke̱ gye̱ ke̱dabe̱‑ɔ.

ref: The Law was given with a glory that faded away. But the glory of the new agreement is much greater, because it will never fade away.
text: Ke̱dabe̱ ne̱ kaa kyo̱ŋ‑ɔ dɛɛ e̱ ke̱ gye̱ ke̱dabe̱ ne̱e̱, ne̱ ke̱mo̱ ne̱ ke̱ maa kyo̱ŋ pɛɛɛ‑ɔ mɔ ɛ?

ref: This wonderful hope makes us feel like speaking freely.
text: Amɔ a tɛɛ a waa ane̱ wɔre̱ de̱maŋte̱ mɔ‑ɔ, kufu mo̱ŋ de ane̱.

ref: When the Law of Moses is read, they have their minds covered over
text: Hare̱e̱ bo̱ fo̱ ndɔɔ a kake mɔ, ɔko̱ ya kare̱ Mosis a wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ gywii bamo̱, amo̱‑ɔ a du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mprakɛɛ‑ɔ a buŋ bamo̱ akatɔ se̱‑ɔ.

ref: with a covering that is removed only for those who turn to the Lord.
text: Amaa bamo̱ ɔko̱ ya kii bo̱ gya ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu si, amɔ Wuribware̱ i lee kaprakɛɛ‑ɔ a ɔ le̱e̱ mò̱ akatɔ se̱.

ref: The Lord and the Spirit are one and the same, and the Lord's Spirit sets us free.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ, ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ e̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o, ne̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o bo̱‑ɔ, mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ a nya ane̱ e̱ye̱e̱ le̱e̱ mbraa de̱daa‑ɔ se̱.

ref: God has been kind enough to trust us with this work. This is why we never give up.
text: Ane̱ fe̱raa, hare̱e̱ ane̱ a mo̱ŋ fo̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ waa asɔfo̱wɔ kusuŋ mɔ gbaa ooo, Wuribware̱ a taa bo̱ waa ane̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ane̱ maa yɔwe̱ e̱ye̱e̱‑rɔ.

ref: The Scriptures say, “God commanded light to shine in the dark.” Now God is shining in our hearts to let you know that his glory is seen in Jesus Christ.
text: Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a twe̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ laŋŋe̱ kibugyii‑ro,” ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ oo buŋŋi ane̱ akatɔ, ne̱ ane̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ mò̱ akatɔ‑rɔ e̱ to̱re̱ se̱, ne̱ ane̱ a bo̱ gyii fe̱yɛ o de Wuribware̱ a ke̱dabe̱‑ɔ.

ref: We often suffer, but we are never crushed. Even when we don't know what to do, we never give up.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ase̱sɛ ko̱ ya waa ane̱ bo̱rɔkraa bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱, bo̱ maa taare̱ a bo̱ sa a ane̱ yɔwe̱ ane̱ kusuŋ. Ba tɔɔraa ane̱, amaa ane̱ maa yɔwe̱ e̱ye̱e̱‑rɔ.

ref: In times of trouble, God is with us, and when we are knocked down, we get up again.
text: Ane̱ i gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ, amaa Wuribware̱ maa se̱re̱ a ɔ yɔwe̱ ane̱. Ba daye̱ ane̱ ba da swe̱e̱re̱, amaa bo̱ maa taare̱ a bo̱ mɔɔ ane̱.

ref: We face death every day because of Jesus. Our bodies show what his death was like, so his life can also be seen in us.
text: Ane̱ i kperi Yeesuu si ane̱ e̱ gyaŋŋaa lowi kake ke̱maa, na ɔ taare̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ o te, ne̱ mò̱ e̱ de̱ŋ ɔ sa ane̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ.

ref: This means that death is working in us, but life is working in you.
text: Amɔ katiŋ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ lowi ke̱gyaŋŋaa kake ke̱maa‑ɔ a sa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: because we know that God raised the Lord Jesus to life. And just as God raised Jesus, he will also raise us to life. Then he will bring us into his presence together with you.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ane̱ nyi, ne̱ ane̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ ke̱mo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kyiŋŋi ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu baa ŋkpa‑rɔ, ne̱ ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ kaye̱ ya gye̱ kɛɛ, o kyiŋŋi ane̱ aa mo̱ne̱ a ɔ yaa so̱so̱ fe̱yɛ Yeesuu‑o, na ɔ baa ane̱ aa mo̱ne̱ mò̱ akatɔ‑rɔ.

ref: All of this has been done for you, so more and more people will know how kind God is and will praise and honor him. Faith in the Lord
text: Mo̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa e̱ waa ane̱, na, Wuribware̱ a maa se̱ ɔ mo̱rɔwe̱ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ, na bo̱ sa Wuribware̱ aŋsɛ, na bo̱ kyo̱rɔ mò̱ ke̱dabe̱ ne̱ o de‑o. Kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ ane̱ aye̱ ne̱e̱

ref: These little troubles are getting us ready for an eternal glory that will make all our troubles seem like nothing.
text: Ase̱ŋ ne̱ a to̱ ane̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ kyɔ, ne̱ a maa kyee, amaa a sa a ane̱ nya wuraa na ke̱dabe̱ ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ Wuribware̱ ase̱, na a kyɔ ase̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ i ŋu mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: Our bodies are like tents that we live in here on earth. But when these tents are destroyed, we know that God will give each of us a place to live. These homes will not be buildings someone has made, but they are in heaven and will last forever.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ ya wu, ane̱ e̱ nya kye̱nakpa Wuribware̱ ase̱ so̱so̱. Ane̱ kayo̱wɔre̱ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ taŋte̱e̱ ke̱kyaŋ ne̱ baa ba waagya bo̱ waa‑ɔ ne̱e̱, ne̱ waagya‑ɔ ya kyaŋŋe̱‑rɔ, ne̱ taŋte̱e̱ ke̱kyaŋ amo̱ a le̱e̱ da‑ɔ, amɔ ane̱ ɔke̱maa e̱ nya so̱so̱ a ke̱kyaŋ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ ya kye̱na‑rɔ‑ɔ. N gye̱ ase̱sɛ ya waa ke̱kyaŋ amo̱, amaa Wuribware̱ ya waa bo̱ ye̱ra sa ane̱.

ref: While we are here on earth, we sigh because we want to live in that heavenly home.
text: Pwɛɛ na ane̱ dɛɛ fo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ane̱ i su ane̱ e̱ kpa kulwee ane̱ ɔpe̱ po̱pwɛɛ amo̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ.

ref: We want to put it on like clothes and not be naked.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱kyaŋ ne̱ ane̱ ɔke̱maa e̱ nya so̱so̱‑ɔ gye̱ kayo̱wɔre̱ po̱pwɛɛ ne̱ ane̱ kra e̱ ya kye̱na‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: These tents we now live in are like a heavy burden, and we groan. But we don't do this just because we want to leave these bodies that will die. It is because we want to change them for bodies that will never die.
text: Ane̱ a saŋ ane̱ de kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ a ko̱yo̱wɔre̱gyi‑o, ane̱ kakye̱na‑rɔ e̱ tɔɔraa ane̱. Amaa n gye̱ sa ne̱ ane̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ane̱ wu, na ane̱ paŋ ŋkpa pɛɛɛ. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ kakye̱na ne̱ ane̱ te mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ i kyurowi a ka kii kakye̱na ne̱ ka mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ. Na ane̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ a ŋyo̱wɔre̱‑ɔ kyurowi kii ŋyo̱wɔre̱ ne̱ n kye̱na nsu pɛɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: But whether we are at home with the Lord or away from him, we still try our best to please him.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, ane̱ e̱ le̱ŋ e̱ye̱e̱ ne̱e̱, na mò̱ akatɔ nya gyi, hare̱e̱ ane̱ te kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ bɛɛɛ ane̱ yii pe̱ ooo.
