ref: We know what it means to respect the Lord, and we encourage everyone to turn to him. God himself knows what we are like, and I hope you also know what kind of people we are.
text: A le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ Kristoo ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱ se̱re̱ Wuribware̱ se̱, ne̱ ane̱ a le̱ŋ e̱ye̱e̱ ane̱ e̱ po̱pɔre̱ ase̱sɛ fe̱yɛ bo̱ gya mò̱ se̱. Wuribware̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ o nyi ane̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ nɛɛnɛɛ, ne̱ n de te̱maa fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ ɔke̱maa gbaa nyi ane̱ mo̱ne̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ ane̱ŋ dɛɛ.

ref: If we seem out of our minds, it is between God and us. But if we are in our right minds, it is for your good.
text: Ane̱ apo̱rɔpo̱‑ɔ ya po̱pɔre̱ ane̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ, ane̱ e̱ lɔ e̱bo̱ŋ, amo̱ fe̱raa mo̱nꞌ taa fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ se̱ ne̱ ane̱ a kperi ane̱ e̱ lɔ e̱bo̱ŋ‑ɔ. Amaa amo̱ a gye̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ maa lɔ e̱bo̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, amo̱ fe̱raa bo̱ le̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ ane̱ du mfaanɛɛ.

ref: And Christ did die for all of us. He died so we would no longer live for ourselves, but for the one who died and was raised to life for us.
text: Ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ Kristoo a wu sa ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ, ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kyiŋŋi mò̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro baa ŋkpa‑rɔ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ, bamo̱ a saŋ bo̱ te ŋkpa‑rɔ‑ɔ, bo̱ ma lɛɛ kye̱na bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ se̱, amaa bo̱ kye̱na sa Kristoo.

ref: Anyone who belongs to Christ is a new person. The past is forgotten, and everything is new.
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ bo̱ Kristoo a ke̱be̱gya‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ a kii se̱sɛ po̱pwɛɛ ne̱e̱. Mò̱ kakye̱na de̱daa‑ɔ a gye̱ kɛɛ, ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ o te kakye̱na po̱pwɛɛ.

ref: God has done it all! He sent Christ to make peace between himself and us, and he has given us the work of making peace between himself and others.
text: Wuribware̱ ya waa amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ, ɔɔ bo̱rɔ Kristoo si bo̱ lɔŋŋɔ ane̱ aa mò̱ mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ ane̱ gye̱ mò̱ akyarɔpo̱ ase̱ŋ mɔ ko̱tɔwe̱‑rɔ.

ref: What we mean is that God was in Christ, offering peace and forgiveness to the people of this world. And he has given us the work of sharing his message about peace.
text: Ane̱ asuŋ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, Wuribware̱ a bo̱rɔ Kristoo si bo̱ lɔŋŋɔ mò̱ aa kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ase̱sɛ mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ, na ɔ ma nya ba bamo̱ e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ ka bamo̱ ko̱kɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ taa mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ ko̱lɔŋŋɔ mɔ ase̱ŋ ko̱tɔwe̱ bo̱ waa ane̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ.

ref: We were sent to speak for Christ, and God is begging you to listen to our message. We speak for Christ and sincerely ask you to make peace with God.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ane̱ ye̱re̱ Kristoo ayaa‑rɔ ne̱e̱. A du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a bɔ ane̱ se̱ ɔ ko̱re̱ mo̱ne̱‑ɔ. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, ane̱ ye̱re̱ Kristoo ayaa‑rɔ ane̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ aa Wuribware̱ waa kanɔ.

ref: We work together with God, and we beg you to make good use of God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ane̱ ne̱ ane̱ i suŋ ane̱ e̱ sa Wuribware̱‑ɔ, ane̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ mo̱ne̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ atɔ dabe̱ ne̱ ɔɔ waa sa mo̱ne̱‑ɔ ma twe̱e̱ kwaa.

ref: But in everything and in every way we show we truly are God's servants. We have always been patient, though we have had a lot of trouble, suffering, and hard times.
text: Ane̱ a kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ ayaafɔre̱, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kanyite‑ro ne̱ ane̱ a gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ ŋu ase̱ŋ na tɔɔraa ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ kaa waa ane̱‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: We have been beaten, put in jail, and hurt in riots. We have worked hard and have gone without sleep or food.
text: Baa da ane̱, ne̱ baa tii ane̱, ne̱ ase̱sɛ akwii akwii a wo̱re̱ ane̱. Saŋ ko̱ ane̱ a suŋ kpo̱ne̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ, ne̱ ane̱ mo̱ŋ nya saŋ di idi, ne̱ ane̱ a nare̱ ako̱ŋ.

ref: But we have kept ourselves pure and have been understanding, patient, and kind. The Holy Spirit has been with us, and our love has been real.
text: Awo̱re̱fɔɔ de̱maŋte̱ mɔ ne̱ ane̱ a gyi‑o gbaa ooo, ane̱ a ba itimaa na kanyiase̱ŋ na kanyite na ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ bo̱ ka ane̱ ado̱ŋ ko̱kɔ. Ase̱sɛ a ŋu Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a suŋ kusuŋ ane̱ kakye̱na‑rɔ, ne̱ baa ŋu fe̱yɛ ke̱kpa ne̱ ane̱ e̱ kpa bo̱ko̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ.

ref: We have spoken the truth, and God's power has worked in us. In all our struggles we have said and done only what is right.
text: Baa nu ane̱ a gyi kase̱ŋtiŋ, ne̱ baa ŋu Wuribware̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ ne̱ o de‑o. Kase̱ŋtiŋ kigyi ne̱ ane̱ a bo̱ waa ane̱ ado̱ŋtɔ ne̱ ane̱ de ane̱ e̱ kɔ bamo̱ ne̱ ba kɔ ane̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ane̱ de ane̱ e̱ kɔ ane̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ ane̱ e̱ye̱e̱.

ref: Whether we were honored or dishonored or praised or cursed, we always told the truth about ourselves. But some people said we did not.
text: Bo̱ko̱ i bu ane̱, bo̱ko̱ mɔ maa bu ane̱ pɛɛɛ‑ɔ. Bo̱ko̱ e̱ kyo̱rɔ ane̱, bo̱ko̱ mɔ e̱ po̱rɔ ane̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ e̱ waa e̱bɔye̱. Ane̱ i gyi kase̱ŋtiŋ, amaa bo̱ko̱ e̱ po̱rɔ ane̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ gye̱ ape̱nnapo̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: We are unknown to others, but well known to you. We seem to be dying, and yet we are still alive. We have been punished, but never killed,
text: Bo̱ mo̱ŋ de ane̱ ba te̱e̱ sɛye̱, amaa ane̱ ke̱nyare̱ a yɔ to̱ŋ ke̱maa. Ba fa fe̱yɛ ane̱ e̱ kpa a ane̱ wu ne̱e̱, amaa ane̱ saŋ ane̱ te. Ba gyiiri ane̱ ke̱se̱bɔ, amaa bo̱ mo̱ŋ taare̱ mɔɔ ane̱.

ref: and we are always happy, even in times of suffering. Although we are poor, we have made many people rich. And though we own nothing, everything is ours.
text: Ba ŋu fe̱yɛ ane̱ i gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ, amaa ane̱ akatɔ a gyi saŋ ke̱maa. Bo̱ ke̱e̱ kitiri ne̱ ane̱ i wu‑o, amaa ane̱ e̱ sa ne̱ bo̱ko̱ e̱ nya ŋyure. Ba fa fe̱yɛ ane̱ mo̱ŋ de sɛye̱, amaa ane̱ de ke̱tɔ ke̱maa.

ref: Friends in Corinth, we are telling the truth when we say there is room in our hearts for you.
text: Korintoo awuye, ane̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ ane̱ mo̱ŋ taa sɛye̱ bo̱ kwe̱e̱rɔ mo̱ne̱ se̱.

ref: We are not holding back on our love for you, but you are holding back on your love for us.
text: N gye̱ ane̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ane̱ mo̱ŋ buŋŋi ane̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ kpa mo̱ne̱, amaa mo̱ne̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ buŋŋi mo̱ne̱ lee‑ro kpa ane̱.

ref: I speak to you as I would speak to my own children. Please make room in your hearts for us. The Temple of the Living God
text: Mo̱ e̱ sa se̱ŋsa mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ a gye̱ mo̱ gyi‑ana‑ɔ. Ane̱ a tɛɛ buŋŋi ane̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ kpa mo̱ne̱‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ mɔ, mo̱nꞌ buŋŋi mo̱ne̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ kpa ane̱. Mo̱ne̱ ma kaŋ sa a mo̱ne̱ aa bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ de ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi‑o waa ko̱ŋko̱

ref: Stay away from people who are not followers of the Lord! Can someone who is good get along with someone who is evil? Are light and darkness the same?
text: Mo̱ne̱ ma kaŋ sa a mo̱ne̱ aa bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ Kristoo gyi‑o waa ko̱ŋko̱. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ na daa bɔye̱ kpaa ntɔse̱ŋ? Ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ na kibugyii e̱ taare̱ a a kye̱na saŋ ko̱ŋko̱ aaa?

ref: Is Christ a friend of Satan? Can people who follow the Lord have anything in common with those who don't?
text: Kristoo na Ɔbɔnsam e̱ taare̱ a bo̱ waa kanɔ aaa? Ɔkɔɔre̱gyipo̱ na ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ gyi‑o kpaa ntɔse̱ŋ?

ref: Do idols belong in the temple of God? We are the temple of the living God, as God himself says, “I will live with these people and walk among them. I will be their God, and they will be my people.”
text: Wuribware̱ aye̱ na ikisi aye̱ e̱ taare̱ a a tii abɛɛ se̱ waa ko̱ko̱ŋko̱ aaa? Daabii pɛɛɛ! Ane̱ Kristoo awuye ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ ne̱ mò̱ wo̱re̱ dooo i de ŋkpa‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ kye̱na. Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ mò̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, “Mo̱ e̱ kye̱na bamo̱‑rɔ, na mo̱ aa bamo̱ nare̱ saŋ ke̱maa mɔ. Mo̱ i kii bamo̱ nyaŋpe̱, na bamo̱ mɔ bo̱ kii mo̱ ase̱sɛ.”

ref: The Lord also says, “Leave them and stay away! Don't touch anything that isn't clean. Then I will welcome you
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ mò̱ wo̱re̱‑ɔ ke̱be̱gya ko̱‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ ase̱sɛ, mo̱nꞌ dare̱e̱ le̱e̱ bamo̱‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ, na mo̱nꞌ kye̱na ko̱kwe̱e̱! Mo̱ne̱ e̱ ma dabo̱rɔ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ki de iyisi‑o, amo̱ fe̱raa mo̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ne̱.”

ref: and be your Father. You will be my sons and my daughters, as surely as I am God, the All-Powerful.”
text: Ne̱ baa be̱e̱ kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, “Bo̱-e̱le̱ŋ‑ɔ‑kyo̱ŋ‑ɔke̱maa-Bware̱ yɛ, ‘Mo̱ne̱ e̱ te̱e̱ mo̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ se̱, na mo̱‑ɔ mɔ n te̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ agyi nyaŋsɛ na akye̱e̱sɛ.’ ”

ref: My friends, God has made us these promises. So we should stay away from everything that keeps our bodies and spirits from being clean. We should honor God and try to be completely like him. The Church Makes Paul Happy
text: Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, Wuribware̱ a tɛɛ tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ ɔ waa atɔ mfaanɛɛ‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ lee ane̱ e̱ye̱e̱ le̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ke̱ e̱ waa ane̱ ŋyo̱wɔre̱ na ŋkpo̱nɔ iyisi‑o‑ro, na mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ nya ŋkpo̱nɔfwiiri, na ane̱ bu Wuribware̱, na ane̱ se̱re̱ mò̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Tiitus a tɔwe̱ gywii Pɔɔl‑ɔ a waa mò̱ wɔre̱‑ɔ

ref: Make a place for us in your hearts! We haven't mistreated or hurt anyone. We haven't cheated anyone.
text: M be̱e̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ buŋŋi mo̱ne̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ kpa ane̱. Ane̱ mo̱ŋ waa ɔko̱ bɔye̱, ne̱ ane̱ mo̱ŋ nye̱ra ɔko̱ mɔ, ane̱ mo̱ŋ sisii ɔko̱.

ref: I am not saying this to be hard on you. But, as I have said before, you will always be in our thoughts, whether we live or die.
text: Ma tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ a m bo̱ po̱rɔ mo̱ne̱ ne̱e̱. Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ a dɛɛ tɔwe̱‑ɔ, ane̱ a kpa mo̱ne̱ ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ane̱ a waa siraa fe̱yɛ ane̱ aa mo̱ne̱ kye̱na, bɛɛɛ ane̱ aa mo̱ne̱ wu.

ref: I trust you completely. I am always proud of you, and I am greatly encouraged. In all my trouble I am still very happy.
text: Mo̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ i gyi. Mo̱ne̱ se̱, mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱. Mo̱ne̱ se̱, n de wɔre̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Hare̱e̱ ase̱ŋ a to̱ ane̱‑ɔ gbaa ooo, mo̱ akatɔ a gyi.

ref: After we came to Macedonia, we didn't have any chance to rest. We were faced with all kinds of problems. We were troubled by enemies and troubled by fears.
text: Hare̱e̱ gbaa ane̱ a yɔ Makedoniya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ, ane̱ mo̱ŋ nya kaye̱e̱yuri. Kase̱ŋtiŋ si, ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ a to̱ ane̱ to̱ŋ ke̱maa ne̱ ane̱ a yɔ‑ɔ. Ane̱ a gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ, ne̱ kufu a nya ane̱, ne̱ ane̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ a nye̱ra.

ref: But God cheers up people in need, and this is what he did when he sent Titus to us.
text: Amaa Wuribware̱ e̱ gye̱ ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ sa se̱sɛ ne̱ mò̱ abaa‑rɔ a yɔre̱‑ɔ wɔre̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ Tiitus a waa ane̱ wɔre̱.

ref: I don't feel bad anymore, even though my letter hurt your feelings. I did feel bad at first, but I don't now. I know that the letter hurt you for a while.
text: Hare̱e̱ gbaa mo̱ wo̱re̱ a sa ne̱ a mo̱ŋ waa mo̱ne̱ kɔne̱ ooo, mo̱ŋ nu mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ mò̱. Ke̱gye̱ŋkpɛɛ‑ɔ mo̱ a nu mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ ase̱ŋ a nya mo̱ne̱ gya. Amaa aa nya mo̱ne̱ gya saŋ katiŋ ko̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: Now I am happy, but not because I hurt your feelings. It is because God used your hurt feelings to make you turn back to him, and none of you were harmed by us.
text: Aa waa mo̱ kɔne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ. N gye̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ akatɔ mo̱ŋ gyi‑o si ne̱ aa waa mo̱ kɔne̱. Amaa ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ aa waa mo̱ kɔne̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ kusu a sa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a kyurowi le̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ. Wuribware̱ a ba mo̱ne̱ kusu‑o bo̱ waa kitimaa sa mo̱ne̱. Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, ke̱tɔ ne̱ ane̱ a waa‑ɔ mo̱ŋ waa mo̱ne̱ tɔɔraa.

ref: When God makes you feel sorry enough to turn to him and be saved, you don't have anything to feel bad about. But when this world makes you feel sorry, it can cause your death.
text: Kusu ne̱ Wuribware̱ de ɔ sa ne̱ ɔko̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ i kyurowi‑o dɛɛ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ sa a ɔ nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ. Ane̱ŋ a kusu‑o bware. Amaa kusu ne̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ i su‑o e̱ sa a ase̱sɛ na Wuribware̱ mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ nye̱ra nsu pɛɛɛ.

ref: When I wrote you, it wasn't to accuse the one who was wrong or to take up for the one who was hurt. I wrote, so God would show you how much you do care for us.
text: E̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ wo̱re̱ amo̱? Kase̱ŋtiŋ si mo̱ne̱‑rɔ ɔko̱ a waa ɔko̱ mɔ bɔye̱. Amaa n gye̱ bamo̱ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ wo̱re̱ amo̱. Mo̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ wo̱re̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱ a mo̱nꞌ nya de̱e̱re̱ mo̱ne̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ, na mo̱nꞌ tɔwe̱ gywii Wuribware̱ fe̱yɛ kase̱ŋtiŋ si mo̱ne̱ saŋ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa mo̱ ase̱ŋ.

ref: And we were greatly encouraged. Although we were encouraged, we felt even better when we saw how happy Titus was, because you had shown he had nothing to worry about.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ ne̱ aa ba‑ɔ a sa ane̱ wɔre̱ mfe̱e̱. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ki tii si, ne̱ a sa ne̱ ane̱ akatɔ a gyi‑o e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ a waa Tiitus wɔre̱, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a sa ne̱ mò̱ akatɔ a gyi.

ref: We had told him how much we thought of you, and you did not disappoint us. Just as we have always told you the truth, so everything we told him about you has also proved to be true.
text: Pwɛɛ ne̱ ɔɔ dɛɛ ba mo̱ne̱ ase̱‑ɔ, mo̱ a kyɔ kaapo̱ mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ se̱, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ sa a ipeere nya mo̱ mò̱ ase̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ne̱‑ɔ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ne̱ mo̱ a kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ se̱ gywii Tiitus‑o mɔ a waa kase̱ŋtiŋ.

ref: Titus loves all of you very much, especially when he remembers how you obeyed him and how you trembled with fear when you welcomed him.
text: Saŋ ne̱ Tiitus a ba mo̱ne̱ ase̱‑ɔ, kufu a nya mo̱ne̱, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a ko̱kɔse̱ kra mò̱ nɛɛnɛɛ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a waa siraa waa ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ waa‑ɔ. Mò̱ ya nyiŋŋi amo̱‑ɔ se̱, amɔ ke̱kpa ne̱ ɔ kpa mo̱ne̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ a tii si.

ref: It makes me really glad to know I can depend on you.
text: M fe̱raa, aa waa mo̱ kɔne̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ taare̱ a ŋ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ne̱ gyi ke̱tɔ ke̱maa‑rɔ.

ref: My friends, we want you to know that the churches in Macedonia have shown others God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mbe̱yɔmɔ, mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, ane̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ ŋu ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ ke̱kpa a suŋ kusuŋ asɔre̱e̱ awuye ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Makedoniya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: And they did more than we had hoped. They gave themselves first to the Lord and then to us, just as God wanted them to do.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ baa waa‑ɔ, ane̱ mo̱ŋ fa fe̱yɛ ba waa ane̱ŋ. Kase̱ŋtiŋ si, baa waa ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ bo̱ waa‑ɔ, amo̱ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ baa gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ taa bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ sa ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu, ne̱ baa taa sa ane̱ mɔ ane̱.

ref: You do everything better than anyone else. You have stronger faith. You speak better and know more. You are eager to give, and you love us better. Now you must give more generously than anyone else.
text: Mo̱ne̱ kyɔ mo̱ne̱ gye̱ atɔ awuye e̱kpa bwe̱e̱tɔ se̱. Mo̱ne̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi, mo̱ne̱ kanyiase̱ŋ ke̱bo̱ sa se̱ŋsa nɛɛnɛɛ, mo̱ne̱ kanyiase̱ŋ pɛɛɛ kyɔ. Ŋ nyi ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ akatɔ i pee mo̱ne̱ i suŋ Wuribware̱‑ɔ, na ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa mo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ e̱ kpa a mo̱nꞌ kaapo̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa atiripo̱‑ɔ.

ref: I am not ordering you to do this. I am simply testing how real your love is by comparing it with the concern that others have shown.
text: Ma ye̱re̱ mo̱ne̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ waa amo̱‑ɔ, amaa mo̱ a kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Makedoniya asɔre̱e̱ awuye‑o akatɔ a pee a bo̱ bo̱ kya atiripo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ ne̱e̱, na ŋ ye̱re̱ amo̱ se̱ ŋu ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ ke̱kpa‑ɔ de̱ŋ ke̱ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o ne̱e̱.

ref: A year ago you were the first ones to give, and you gave because you wanted to. So listen to my advice.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ ka mo̱ne̱ ase̱ŋ ke̱mo̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱. Suŋno mo̱ne̱ ya gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa a mo̱nꞌ sa atanne̱. Kase̱ŋtiŋ si mɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ e̱sa.

ref: I think you should finish what you started. If you give according to what you have, you will prove you are as eager to give as you were to think about giving.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ lo̱we̱ amo̱ ko̱waa, na ke̱pre̱ ne̱ kaa pre̱ mo̱ne̱, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a bo̱ le̱e̱ kaase̱‑ɔ nya waa kyɛɛkyɛɛ. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ de‑o e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ kaapo̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱nꞌ waa‑ɔ.

ref: It doesn't matter how much you have. What matters is how much you are willing to give from what you have.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ya da mo̱ne̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ se̱ ke atanne̱, Wuribware̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ne̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i ke‑o si. Mò̱ e̱ de̱ŋ o nyi ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ de‑o na ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ taare̱ a mo̱nꞌ ke, na a waa mò̱ kɔne̱‑ɔ.

ref: I am not trying to make life easier for others by making life harder for you. But it is only fair
text: Ane̱ maa kpa a kitiri kra mo̱ne̱, na a taare̱ kya bo̱ko̱ mɔ‑rɔ, amaa ane̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa nya mò̱ ke̱be̱gya‑ɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: for you to share with them when you have so much, and they have so little. Later, when they have more than enough, and you are in need, they can share with you. Then everyone will have a fair share,
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ a saŋ mɔ fe̱raa, mo̱ne̱ a de bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ baa kanɔ ŋke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ de‑o bo̱ kya bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ de kafwe̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ bamo̱ ya bo̱ nya bwe̱e̱tɔ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ mɔ mo̱ne̱ i de kafwe̱e̱, na bo̱ taare̱ kya mo̱ne̱‑rɔ. Mfaanɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ ya waa ne̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa e̱ waa kyɛɛkyɛɛ.

ref: I am grateful that God made Titus care as much about you as we do.
text: Mo̱ e̱ sa Wuribware̱ aŋsɛ fe̱yɛ ɔɔ sa ne̱ Tiitus e̱ kpa mo̱ne̱ ke̱kpa ko̱ŋko̱ amo̱ dɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa mo̱ne̱‑ɔ.

ref: When we begged Titus to visit you, he said he would. He wanted to because he cared so much for you.
text: Tiitus a sure ko̱waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ waa‑ɔ se̱. Ne̱ ɔ be̱e̱ ɔ ko̱kɔse̱ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ke̱pre̱ se̱ ɔ kpa a ɔ kya‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ waa mfɛɛre̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ ba mo̱ne̱ ase̱.

ref: With Titus we are also sending one of the Lord's followers who is well known in every church for spreading the good news.
text: Mò̱ aa ane̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ ko̱ ne̱ asɔre̱e̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ ase̱sɛ e̱ kyo̱rɔ bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ o suŋ ɔ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ timaa bo̱ le̱e̱ Yeesuu kuŋu si‑o e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ba ba.

ref: The churches chose this follower to travel with us while we carry this gift that will bring praise to the Lord and show how much we hope to help.
text: Kase̱ŋtiŋ si asɔre̱e̱ asɔre̱e̱ amo̱ dɛɛ ase̱sɛ ya lee mò̱ fe̱yɛ o kii ane̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ ane̱ kpa mɔ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ yɔ mò̱‑ɔ‑rɔ. Ane̱ de atanne̱ mɔ ane̱ e̱ yaa ase̱sɛ ne̱ ba wu kitiri‑o, na bo̱ nya kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱, na a kaapo̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ kpa a ane̱ kya bamo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: But we want to do what pleases the Lord and what people think is right.
text: Ane̱ e̱ sa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ki bware‑o si bwe̱e̱tɔ, n gye̱ Wuribware̱ a ke̱be̱gya‑ɔ se̱ wo̱re̱, amaa na ase̱sɛ a ke̱be̱gya‑ɔ se̱.

ref: We are also sending someone else with Titus and the other follower. We approve of this man. In fact, he has already shown us many times that he wants to help. And now he wants to help even more than ever, because he trusts you so much.
text: Ane̱ be̱e̱ ane̱ de ane̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ ko̱, ne̱ oo lee mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ ane̱ iluwi bwe̱e̱tɔ na e̱kpa bwe̱e̱tɔ se̱ fe̱yɛ o suŋ Wuribware̱ wɔre̱ se̱ ne̱e̱. Mbe̱yɔmɔ o de wɔre̱ ase̱ŋ mɔ se̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ o de mo̱ne̱ te̱maa bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: Treat them in such a way that the churches will see your love and will know why we bragged about you.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ lee mo̱ne̱ ke̱kpa bo̱ kaapo̱ ase̱sɛ mɔ. Mfaanɛɛ ne̱ ane̱ aa asɔre̱e̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ i ŋu fe̱yɛ ane̱ de kpa fe̱yɛ ane̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ke̱kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱ ko̱kɔse̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa a mo̱nꞌ ke atiripo̱‑ɔ atanne̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ kaye̱ba ne̱e̱.

ref: I don't need to write you about the money you plan to give for God's people.
text: Kase̱ŋtiŋ si, a mo̱ŋ tiri fe̱yɛ n kyo̱rɛɛ mo̱ne̱ twe̱e̱twe̱e̱ mɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ de mo̱ne̱ e̱ sa Wuribware̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Yude̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ ase̱ŋ.

ref: I know how eager you are to give. And I have proudly told the Lord's followers in Macedonia that you people in Achaia have been ready for a whole year. Now your desire to give has made them want to give.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa ke̱kya bamo̱‑rɔ ne̱e̱. Ne̱ n de̱ŋ mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ se̱ mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ Makedoniya awuye‑o mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ le̱e̱ suŋno mo̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ bo̱ Akaya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ a waa siraa fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱ sa atanne̱, amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱ne̱ wɔre̱ a sa ne̱ bamo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ a waa ko̱tɔko̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ twe̱e̱twe̱e̱ amo̱ se̱.

ref: This is why I am sending Titus and the two others to you. I want you to be ready, just as I promised. This will prove we were not wrong to brag about you.
text: Amaa mo̱ i suŋ mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana mo̱ e̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ mo̱ne̱ ase̱, na ane̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ke̱kaapo̱‑ɔ ma buruwi ase̱ŋkpare̱gyi kwaa, amaa mo̱nꞌ waa siraa waa ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱ waa‑ɔ.

ref: Some followers from Macedonia may come with me, and I want them to find that you have the money ready. If you don't, I would be embarrassed for trusting you to do this. But you would be embarrassed even more.
text: N gye̱ ane̱ŋ, Makedoniya asɔre̱e̱ awuye ne̱ mo̱ aa bamo̱ e̱ yɔ Yude̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ ya ba mo̱ne̱ ase̱, ne̱ baa ŋu fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ waa siraa, amo̱ fe̱raa ane̱ aa mo̱ne̱ pɛɛɛ, ipeere e̱ nya ane̱, na a kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ a kɔɔre̱ mo̱ne̱ gyi kwaa.

ref: So I have decided to ask Titus and the others to spend some time with you before I arrive. This way they can arrange to collect the money you have promised. Then you will have the chance to give because you want to, and not because you feel forced to.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ a fa fe̱yɛ a tiri fe̱yɛ ŋ ko̱re̱ mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana mɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ gye̱ mo̱ ŋkpɛɛ ba bo̱ ka mo̱ne̱ kanɔ, na bo̱ kɔɔre̱ atanne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱ sa‑ɔ bo̱ be̱ya, na aa le̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ ke̱kpa se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a sa, amaa n gye̱ sa ne̱ ane̱ e̱ le̱ŋ mo̱ne̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: Remember this saying, “A few seeds make a small harvest, but a lot of seeds make a big harvest.”
text: Abre̱sɛ ya da ke̱kpare̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔko̱ ne̱ o dwii atɔ dwiisɛ kafwe̱e̱‑ɔ, amo̱ ya fo̱ ke̱kpabe̱e̱, ɔ nya amo̱ kafwe̱e̱ ne̱e̱. Ne̱ ɔko̱ ne̱ o dwii amo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ e̱ nya amo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ ne̱e̱.”

ref: Each of you must make up your own mind about how much to give. But don't feel sorry that you must give and don't feel you are forced to give. God loves people who love to give.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ɔke̱maa sa ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ fa mò̱ mfɛɛre̱‑rɔ fe̱yɛ ɔ sa‑ɔ. Mo̱nꞌ sa a a le̱e̱ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ, na a ma ba fe̱yɛ baa le̱ŋ mo̱ne̱. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ e̱ kpa mò̱ ne̱ o ke ɔko̱ ko̱tɔko̱ na a mo̱ŋ bo̱ mò̱ gya kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: God can bless you with everything you need, and you will always have more than enough to do all kinds of good things for others.
text: Wuribware̱ e̱ taare̱ a ɔ sa mo̱ne̱ ŋyure bwe̱e̱tɔ, na mo̱nꞌ nya nya ke̱tɔ ne̱ ki tiri mo̱ne̱‑ɔ kpa ke̱maa se̱, na ŋko̱ saŋ na mo̱nꞌ bo̱ waa ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ e̱wo̱re̱se̱ e̱wo̱re̱se̱ sa bo̱ko̱.

ref: The Scriptures say, “God freely gives his gifts to the poor, and always does right.”
text: Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, “Ɔ da kakpo̱nɔ se̱ o ke atiripo̱ atɔ, na a mo̱ŋ bo̱ mò̱ gya. ɔ maa yɔwe̱ ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ ko̱waa pɛɛɛ.”

ref: God gives seed to farmers and provides everyone with food. He will increase what you have, so you can give even more to those in need.
text: Wuribware̱ e̱ de̱ŋ ɔ sa ɔdɔɔpo̱ atɔ dwiisɛ na ateese gyisɛ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱ne̱ ya waa bo̱ko̱ ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ, Wuribware̱ e̱ sa mo̱ne̱ ateese gyisɛ ne̱ a tiri mo̱ne̱‑ɔ, na ɔ sa a mo̱ne̱ adɔɔrɔtɔ tii si, na mo̱nꞌ taare̱ ba amo̱ bo̱ kya se̱ waa ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: You will be blessed in every way, and you will be able to keep on being generous. Then many people will thank God when we deliver your gift.
text: Ɔ sa a mo̱nꞌ kii atɔ awuye kpa ke̱maa se̱, na mo̱nꞌ taare̱ de̱e̱ ke saŋ ke̱maa, na a mo̱ŋ bo̱ mo̱ne̱ gya ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ. Na Yude̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ a asɔre̱e̱ a atiripo̱‑ɔ sa Wuribware̱ aŋsɛ bo̱ le̱e̱ atanne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a bo̱ bo̱rɔ ane̱ se̱ bo̱ ke bamo̱‑ɔ.

ref: What you are doing is much more than a service that supplies God's people with what they need. It is something that will make many others thank God.
text: Atanne̱ mɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i ke‑o, a maa kya Wuribware̱ ase̱sɛ wo̱re̱‑rɔ ne̱e̱, amaa a baa Wuribware̱ aŋsɛ ke̱sa bwe̱e̱tɔ bo̱ tii si.

ref: The way in which you have proved yourselves by this service will bring honor and praise to God. You believed the message about Christ, and you obeyed it by sharing generously with God's people and with everyone else.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ko̱ kyɔ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ ya ke atanne̱‑ɔ, a kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ Kristoo kuŋu si ase̱ŋ timaa‑o mo̱ne̱ i gyi bwe̱e̱tɔ, na bo̱ ŋu fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱ waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ yɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ne̱ i gyi‑o nɛɛnɛɛ. Na bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ se̱ na ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa kike bamo̱ na ɔke̱maa bo̱ tii si‑o, na a mo̱ŋ bo̱ mo̱ne̱ gya kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, bo̱ sa Wuribware̱ aŋsɛ.

ref: Now they are praying for you and want to see you, because God used you to bless them so very much.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, bamo̱ ya ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ sa mo̱ne̱, ba kpa mo̱ne̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ bo̱ le̱e̱ ŋyure de̱maŋte̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a yure mo̱ne̱‑ɔ se̱.

ref: and every bit of pride that keeps anyone from knowing God. We capture people's thoughts and make them obey Christ.
text: Ane̱ i kpuri aye̱ba ke̱kaapo̱. Na ane̱ sa a ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ kaapo̱ e̱ye̱e̱, ne̱ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa o kye Wuribware̱ a kanyiase̱ŋ‑ɔ pɔɔ. Ne̱ ane̱ i kyurowi ase̱sɛ mfɛɛre̱, ne̱ ane̱ e̱ sa ne̱ ba bu Wuribware̱.

ref: You judge by appearances. If any of you think you are the only ones who belong to Christ, then think again. We belong to Christ as much as you do.
text: A gye̱ fe̱yɛ akatɔ se̱ wo̱re̱ kpeŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ de̱e̱re̱ mo̱ne̱ i gyi ɔko̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ sa a ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ Kristoo e̱ bo̱ mò̱‑ɔ ŋu fe̱yɛ ane̱ mɔ Kristoo e̱ bo̱ ane̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ a bo̱ se̱sɛ amo̱‑ɔ.

ref: Maybe I brag a little too much about the authority that the Lord gave me to help you and not to hurt you. Yet I am not embarrassed to brag.
text: Kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ ke̱yaale̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ de mo̱ne̱ se̱‑ɔ se̱. Amaa ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ mo̱ŋ sa ane̱ ane̱ŋ a ke̱yaale̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ bo̱ lee mo̱ne̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi si, amaa ɔɔ sa ane̱ ne̱e̱ a ane̱ taare̱ sa a mo̱ne̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi tii si. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, ipeere mo̱ŋ de mo̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱.

ref: And I am not trying to scare you with my letters.
text: Mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱e̱ na mo̱nꞌ nya ŋu fe̱yɛ mo̱ŋ de mo̱ e̱wo̱re̱ mo̱ i fuŋte mo̱ne̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: Some of you are saying, “Paul's letters are harsh and powerful. But in person, he is a weakling and has nothing worth saying.”
text: Mo̱ne̱ bo̱ko̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱wo̱re̱ ne̱ mo̱ a bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ mo̱ne̱‑ɔ e̱mo̱‑rɔ bo̱ le̱ŋle̱ŋ, amaa mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ e̱ bo̱ mo̱ne̱ ase̱, mo̱ŋ bo̱ le̱ŋ, ne̱ mo̱ se̱ŋsa maa kaapo̱ sɛye̱.

ref: Those people had better understand that when I am with you, I will do exactly what I say in my letters.
text: Mo̱ i yii ane̱ŋ a ase̱sɛ ne̱ ba tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ e̱wo̱re̱‑rɔ mo̱ e̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ mo̱ne̱ na mo̱ŋ bo̱ mo̱ne̱ ase̱‑ɔ, mo̱ ya ba, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa.

ref: We don't brag about what others have done, as if we had done those things ourselves. But I hope as you become stronger in your faith, we will be able to reach many more of the people around you. That has always been our goal.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, ane̱ maa kaapo̱ ane̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ bo̱ko̱ a waa‑ɔ se̱, na ane̱ ŋaye̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ki bware‑o si. Te̱maa ne̱ ane̱ de‑o e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi a maa se̱ ke̱ e̱ daŋ‑ɔ, ane̱ e̱ taare̱ a ane̱ waa asuŋ mo̱ne̱‑rɔ kyo̱ŋ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ a kyɔ waa‑ɔ.

ref: Then we will be able to preach the good news in other lands where we cannot take credit for work someone else has already done.
text: Na ane̱ nya tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ timaa‑o e̱maŋ ne̱ e̱ bo̱ mo̱ne̱ aye̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ. Mfaanɛɛ ne̱ ane̱ maa nya a ane̱ kaapo̱ ane̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ko̱tɔko̱ ne̱ ɔko̱ a waa, ne̱ ke̱ gye̱ mò̱ wo̱re̱ lee‑o si.

ref: The Scriptures say, “If you want to brag, then brag about the Lord.”
text: Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, “Sa a ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ kaapo̱ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱‑ɔ kaapo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ fe̱yɛ o nyi mo̱, Wuribware̱.”

ref: You may brag about yourself, but the only approval that counts is the Lord's approval.
text: Se̱sɛ e̱ kaapo̱ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ mò̱ awaasɛ se̱, amaa ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Wuribware̱ dooo e̱ de̱ŋ o nyi fe̱yɛ ɔɔ waa atɔ, bɛɛɛ ɔ mo̱ŋ waa.

ref: But now I fear that you will be tricked, just as Eve was tricked by that lying snake. I am afraid that you might stop thinking about Christ in an honest and sincere way.
text: Kufu de mo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ko̱ i kyurowi mo̱ne̱ mfɛɛre̱, na mo̱nꞌ kine Kristoo ke̱gya se̱. Ma kpa fe̱yɛ bo̱ko̱ e̱ pe̱nna mo̱ne̱, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Ɔbɔnsam a buruwi ko̱wɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ waa kanɔrɔkɔne̱ bo̱ pe̱nna ɔkye̱e̱ gye̱ŋkpɛɛsɛ Hawa ne̱ Wuribware̱ a twe̱e̱‑ɔ, fe̱yɛ ɔ ma lɛɛ gya Wuribware̱ kanɔ se̱‑ɔ.

ref: Even when I was in need, I still didn't bother you. In fact, some of the Lord's followers from Macedonia brought me what I needed. I have not been a burden to you in the past, and I will never be a burden.
text: Mo̱ a bo̱ mo̱ne̱ ase̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa atanne̱, mo̱ŋ de̱e̱re̱ mo̱ne̱ akatɔ‑rɔ. Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana ne̱ baa le̱e̱ Makedoniya asɔre̱e̱ asɔre̱e̱‑rɔ‑ɔ a baa mo̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa mɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ. Mo̱ŋ ba mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ mo̱ne̱ se̱, na a waa mo̱ne̱ tɔɔraa kpa ke̱maa se̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ a dɛɛ waa nsaŋ‑ɔ dɛɛ ne̱ m be̱e̱ mo̱ e̱ waa mbe̱yɔmɔ.

ref: As surely as I speak the truth about Christ, no one in Achaia can stop me from bragging about this.
text: Mo̱ i gyi Kristoo a kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ ya tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱ mo̱ne̱ Akaya e̱maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ o kuŋ mo̱ kpa fe̱yɛ m ma kaapo̱ mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ane̱ŋ a kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o si.

ref: And it isn't because I don't love you. God himself knows how much I do love you.
text: Mo̱ a tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ, a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ma kpa mo̱ne̱ ase̱ŋ aaa? Daabii! Wuribware̱ nyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ kpa mo̱ne̱ ase̱ŋ.

ref: I plan to go on doing just what I have always done. Then those people won't be able to brag about doing the same things we are doing.
text: Mo̱ e̱ de̱e̱ kine ko̱kɔɔre̱ atanne̱ ke̱maa le̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ ase̱ bo̱ sa mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱, na bamo̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ te̱e̱ ɛ ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ ma nya sɛye̱ a bo̱ ye̱re̱ se̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ aa bamo̱ ta ne̱e̱.

ref: Anyway, they are no more than false apostles and dishonest workers. They only pretend to be apostles of Christ.
text: Ane̱ŋ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ. Bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ asuŋpo̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ. Ba waa akatɔ se̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ gye̱ Kristoo ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ.

ref: And it is no wonder. Even Satan tries to make himself look like an angel of light.
text: Amo̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ atɔ ne̱ a kpe̱ŋ e̱ye̱e̱‑ɔ. Ɔbɔnsam fɔŋfɔŋ gbaa e̱ taare̱ a ɔ waa mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa o gywii ase̱sɛ.

ref: So why does it seem strange for Satan's servants to pretend to do what is right? Someday they will get exactly what they deserve. Paul's Sufferings for Christ
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ Ɔbɔnsam ayaafɔre̱ ya waa bamo̱ akatɔ se̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ ayaafɔre̱, a mo̱ŋ gye̱ atɔ dabe̱ ko̱ ne̱e̱. Bamo̱ lalalo̱we̱ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ i gyiiri bamo̱ ke̱se̱bɔ bo̱ le̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ baa waa‑ɔ se̱. Pɔɔl e̱ kaapo̱ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ awo̱re̱fɔɔ ne̱ oo gyi‑o si

ref: I don't want any of you to think I am a fool. But if you do, then let me be a fool and brag a little.
text: M be̱e̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ ma kaŋ fa fe̱yɛ n gye̱ se̱sɛ ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ nyi ase̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱e̱. Amaa mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa a mo̱nꞌ fa mo̱ kuŋu si fe̱yɛ mo̱ŋ nyi ase̱ŋ fe̱raa, mo̱nꞌ kɔɔre̱ gyi ane̱ŋ de, na n taare̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ kafwe̱e̱.

ref: And since you are so smart, you will gladly put up with a fool.
text: Mo̱ne̱ a tɛɛ mo̱ne̱ nyi ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ, mo̱ e̱ fa fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ i nyite mo̱ ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi ase̱ŋ‑ɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: In fact, you let people make slaves of you and cheat you and steal from you. Why, you even let them strut around and slap you in the face.
text: Kase̱ŋtiŋ si, bamo̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i bu bamo̱ ke̱dabe̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ a gye̱ Yeesuu ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱‑ɔ ya waa mo̱ne̱ bo̱rɔkraa fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ a gye̱ anya‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ i nyite bamo̱. Bamo̱ ɔko̱ ya kɔɔre̱ mo̱ne̱ atanne̱ pɛɛɛ, bɛɛɛ o gyi mo̱ne̱, bɛɛɛ ɔ waa mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ke̱dabe̱ mo̱ne̱‑rɔ, bɛɛɛ ɔ da mo̱ne̱ e̱se̱bɔ‑rɔ abaa, mo̱ne̱ maa tɔwe̱ sɛye̱.

ref: Five times my own people gave me
text: Yudaa awuye a kra mo̱ iluwi inuu daye̱ mo̱ paara iluwi adusa na akpanɔɔ.

ref: Three times the Romans beat me with a big stick, and once my enemies stoned me. I have been shipwrecked three times, and I even had to spend a night and a day in the sea.
text: Roma awuye a kra mo̱ iluwi e̱sa ba akyo̱kywe̱e̱ bo̱ daye̱ mo̱. Kake ŋko̱ bo̱ko̱ a twe̱e̱ abu bo̱ da mo̱. Iluwi e̱sa ne̱ ko̱re̱e̱ ne̱ n te‑ro mo̱ i tu kpa ɔpo̱o̱ se̱‑ɔ a nye̱ra, ne̱ oo buŋ mo̱. Ne̱ amo̱‑rɔ ko̱ko̱ŋko̱ fe̱raa gbaa mpase̱ na kanye pɛɛɛ na m bo̱ nkyu‑o‑ro pwɛɛ ne̱ baa dɛɛ bo̱ lee mo̱.

ref: During my many travels, I have been in danger from rivers, robbers, my own people, and foreigners. My life has been in danger in cities, in deserts, at sea, and with people who only pretended to be the Lord's followers.
text: Mo̱ e̱kpa kutu bwe̱e̱tɔ‑rɔ, mo̱ a lwee ase̱ŋ‑nɔ bo̱ le̱e̱ nkyu nkyu si, bo̱ le̱e̱ ayu si, bo̱ le̱e̱ mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ase̱sɛ Yudaa awuye na ase̱sɛ ko̱ mɔ se̱. Ase̱ŋ a to̱ mo̱ e̱maŋ se̱ na mbo̱re̱ se̱ na ɔpo̱o̱ se̱. Mo̱ a nya ase̱ŋ le̱e̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ yɛ bo̱ gye̱ Kristoo awuye, amaa ne̱ ba ba aye̱ba ne̱e̱‑ɔ.

ref: I have worked and struggled and spent many sleepless nights. I have gone hungry and thirsty and often had nothing to eat. I have been cold from not having enough clothes to keep me warm.
text: Mo̱ a suŋ Kristoo keŋkeŋ, ne̱ iluwi bwe̱e̱tɔ gbaa, mo̱ŋ taare̱ nya kpa di idi. Ako̱ŋ na burufo a nya mo̱. Mo̱ a paŋ ateese na atɔ buŋsɛ iluwi bwe̱e̱tɔ, ne̱ ako̱ŋ na afwii a nya mo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: When others are weak, I am weak too. When others are tricked into sin, I get angry.
text: Amo̱ ya ba fe̱yɛ asɔre̱e̱ a awuye‑o ɔko̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi e̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱ le̱ŋ, amɔ mo̱‑ɔ mo̱ i su mo̱ e̱ sa mò̱. Bamo̱ ɔko̱ ya lwee e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ, amɔ mo̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ a nye̱ra.

ref: God, the Father of our Lord Jesus, knows I am not lying. And God is to be praised forever!
text: Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo mò̱ se̱ Wuribware̱, mò̱ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ kyo̱rɔ nsu pɛɛɛ‑ɔ, nyi fe̱yɛ ma ba aye̱ba bo̱ le̱e̱ ase̱ŋ mɔ ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱, ne̱ mo̱ e̱ ba a m bo̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ se̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: The governor of Damascus at the time of King Aretas had the city gates guarded, so he could capture me.
text: Saŋ ne̱ mo̱ a yɔ Damasikus maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, gominaa ne̱ ɔ bo̱ owure Areetas kaase̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ a ba akyɔŋŋɔpo̱ bo̱ ye̱ra ye̱ra maŋ amo̱ mbunogyii‑o pɛɛɛ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ weetee bo̱ kra mo̱.

ref: I have to brag. There is nothing to be gained by it, but I must brag about the visions and other things that the Lord has shown me.
text: Mo̱ e̱ de̱e̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱, amaa a maa sa mo̱ to̱nɔ ko̱. Ŋkee fe̱raa mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ aleebo̱kaapo̱tɔ ko̱ ne̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu a lee bo̱ kaapo̱ mo̱‑ɔ ase̱ŋ a n gywii mo̱ne̱.

ref: I am planning to visit you for the third time. But I still won't make a burden of myself. What I really want is you, and not what you have. Children are not supposed to save up for their parents, but parents are supposed to take care of their children.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ mo̱ a waa siraa fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ ba a m bo̱ ka mo̱ne̱ kanɔ sase̱po̱. Mo̱ne̱ ma sa a a tɔɔraa mo̱ne̱. N gye̱ mo̱ne̱ ateese na mo̱ne̱ atanne̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa, amaa mo̱ne̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa. N gye̱ ŋyaagyi ngyingyii e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ba de̱e̱re̱ bamo̱ ako̱we̱po̱ se̱, amaa ako̱we̱po̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ba de̱e̱re̱ bamo̱ ŋyaagyi si. N gye̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱? Mo̱ne̱ ɔko̱we̱po̱ e̱ gye̱ mo̱.

ref: I will brag about that man, but not about myself, except to say how weak I am.
text: Mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ kaa waa mo̱ saŋ amo̱‑ɔ se̱, amaa m be̱e̱ ma lɛɛ kaapo̱ mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱, amɔ bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱ le̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: Yet even if I did brag, I would not be foolish. I would simply be speaking the truth. But I will try not to say too much. That way, none of you will think more highly of me than you should because of what you have seen me do and say.
text: N dɛɛ mo̱ e̱ kpa a ŋ kaapo̱ mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ne̱e̱‑ɔ, weetee mo̱ŋ kii ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ nyi ase̱ŋ‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ a gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱e̱, weetee mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱. Amaa ma lɛɛ kaapo̱ mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ aleebo̱kaapo̱tɔ amo̱ se̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ a mo̱ŋ bware fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ kyo̱rɔ mo̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ atɔ ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ ŋu fe̱yɛ mo̱ a waa‑ɔ, bɛɛɛ ase̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ nu le̱e̱ mo̱ ase̱‑ɔ se̱.

ref: Of course, I am now referring to the wonderful things I saw. One of Satan's angels was sent to make me suffer terribly, so that I would not feel too proud.
text: Amaa Wuribware̱ a sa Ɔbɔnsam kabɔɔ ko̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ taa ko̱tɔko̱ ne̱ ku du fe̱yɛ wu‑o bo̱ yii mo̱ kayo̱wɔre̱, na ka nya mo̱ gya, na m ma nya bu mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ke̱dabe̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ atɔ timaa ne̱ Wuribware̱ a lee bo̱ kaapo̱ mo̱‑ɔ se̱.

ref: Three times I begged the Lord to make this suffering go away.
text: Iluwi e̱sa ne̱ mo̱ a ko̱re̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ fe̱yɛ o kyuwi wu mɔ le̱e̱ mo̱ se̱.

ref: But he replied, “My gift of undeserved grace is all you need. My power is strongest when you are weak.” So if Christ keeps giving me his power, I will gladly brag about how weak I am.
text: Amaa oo lee kanɔ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ke̱kya ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kya fo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ a baare. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ saŋ ne̱ fo̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ fo̱ bo̱ le̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ‑ɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ fo̱ ase̱.” Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, a bo̱ mo̱ kɔne̱ a kyɔ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa fe̱yɛ mo̱ a kaapo̱ mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱ le̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱, na Kristoo ke̱yaale̱ŋ nya sii mo̱ se̱.

ref: Yes, I am glad to be weak or insulted or mistreated or to have troubles and sufferings, if it is for Christ. Because when I am weak, I am strong. Paul's Concern for the Lord's Followers at Corinth
text: Mo̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱ le̱ŋ, bɛɛɛ bamo̱ ya saare̱ mo̱, bɛɛɛ bamo̱ ya po̱rɔ mo̱, bɛɛɛ mo̱ ya gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ, bɛɛɛ ase̱ŋ ke̱maa ya to̱ mo̱, a bo̱ mo̱ kɔne̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Kristoo si ne̱ mo̱ i ŋu ase̱ŋ amo̱ pɛɛɛ. A waa mo̱ kɔne̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱ le̱ŋ, Wuribware̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ sa mo̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ. Ke̱kpa de̱maŋte̱ ne̱ Pɔɔl e̱ kpa Kristoo awuye ne̱ bo̱ te Korintoo maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ

ref: I have been making a fool of myself. But you forced me to do it, when you should have been speaking up for me. I may be nothing at all, but I am as good as those super apostles.
text: Mo̱ a waa mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a mo̱ŋ nyi ase̱ŋ‑ɔ, amaa mo̱ne̱ ya sa ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa ane̱ŋ. Mo̱ne̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱ kyo̱rɔ mo̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ hare̱e̱ gbaa mo̱ a mo̱ŋ gye̱ sɛye̱ mo̱ne̱ ase̱‑ɔ, bamo̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i bu fe̱yɛ bamo̱ a gye̱ Yeesuu ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱ timaa‑o mo̱ŋ fo̱ mo̱.

ref: When I was with you, I was patient and worked all the powerful miracles and signs and wonders of a true apostle.
text: Saŋ ne̱ mo̱ aa mo̱ne̱ te‑o, ne̱ mo̱ a waa akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ, mo̱ a nyite awo̱re̱fɔɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ kigyi‑ro‑o. Atɔ mɔ e̱ kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ n gye̱ Yeesuu ayaa‑rɔ ɔye̱re̱po̱.

ref: So I will gladly give all I have and all I am. Will you love me less for loving you too much?
text: Mo̱ ya kpo̱ne̱ mo̱ne̱ se̱, ne̱ mo̱ a nye̱ra ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ n de‑o na n taare̱ kya mo̱ne̱‑rɔ, weetee a waa mo̱ kɔne̱. Mo̱ a kpa mo̱ne̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa mo̱ kafwe̱e̱ aaa?

ref: You agree that I wasn't a burden to you. Maybe that's because I was trying to catch you off guard and trick you.
text: Amaa mo̱ne̱ bo̱ko̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “To, Pɔɔl fɔŋfɔŋ mo̱ŋ le̱ŋ ane̱, ne̱ ane̱ a sa mò̱ ko̱tɔko̱, amaa ɔɔ ba mò̱ kanyiase̱ŋ bo̱ pe̱nna ane̱, na ɔ kɔɔre̱ atanne̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ ase̱.”

ref: I am afraid when I come, we won't be pleased with each other. I fear that some of you may be arguing or jealous or angry or selfish or gossiping or insulting each other. I even fear that you may be proud and acting like a mob.
text: Amaa kufu de mo̱ fe̱yɛ, mo̱ ya ba mo̱ne̱ ase̱, nsaŋse̱ ma ŋu mo̱ne̱ e̱ waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ki i gyi mo̱ akatɔ‑ɔ, na amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱ne̱ ma ŋu mo̱ e̱ waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ki i gyi mo̱ne̱ akatɔ‑ɔ. Kufu de mo̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ ba a m bo̱ ŋu e̱kɔ na ko̱kwe̱e̱ ke̱ka mo̱ne̱ mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ iduŋ a fwii abɛɛ se̱, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a keke‑ro ntuŋ ntuŋ si, na mo̱ne̱ e̱ sa e̱se̱ŋsa mo̱ne̱ kye abɛɛ, na mo̱ne̱ i kyii abɛɛ, na mo̱ne̱ e̱ waa akatɔle̱ŋ na katɔmaabu.

ref: During my second visit I warned you that I would punish you and anyone else who doesn't stop sinning. I am far away from you now, but I give you the same warning.
text: Mo̱ a ba mo̱ne̱ ase̱ lalalo̱we̱ mɔ‑ɔ, mo̱ a yii mo̱ne̱ se̱, ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ m be̱e̱ mo̱ i yii mo̱ne̱ se̱, na mo̱ŋ bo̱ mo̱ne̱ ase̱ mɔ, fe̱yɛ mo̱ ya ba, ma su bamo̱ ne̱ baa waa e̱bɔye̱ kyee‑o na ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ be̱e̱ o tii si, ne̱ ɔɔ waa e̱bɔye̱‑ɔ se̱.

ref: This should prove to you that I am speaking for Christ. When he corrects you, he won't be weak. He will be powerful!
text: Amo̱‑ɔ e̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ nɛɛnɛɛ fe̱yɛ Kristoo e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ bɔ mo̱ se̱ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa. Kristoo ya de̱e̱re̱ ane̱ ase̱ŋ‑nɔ, kufu mo̱ŋ de mò̱ fe̱yɛ o gyiiri ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ waa bɔye̱‑ɔ ke̱se̱bɔ.

ref: Although he was weak when he was nailed to the cross, he now lives by the power of God. We are weak, just as Christ was. But you will see that we will live by the power of God, just as Christ does.
text: Mo̱ne̱ bo̱ko̱ i gyiiri ikii fe̱yɛ, saŋ ne̱ Kristoo a kye̱na fe̱yɛ se̱sɛ dimaadi kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ mfe̱e̱‑ɔ, ɔ mo̱ŋ kwaye̱ kuwu kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱. Kase̱ŋtiŋ, amaa Wuribware̱ a kyiŋŋi mò̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro, ne̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, o te o de Wuribware̱ a ke̱yaale̱ŋ‑ɔ o suŋ kusuŋ mo̱ne̱‑rɔ. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ sa kpa fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ waa ane̱ ane̱ŋ a bo̱rɔkraa‑ɔ. Amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ gbaa ane̱ de ke̱yaale̱ŋ ne̱ Kristoo a sa ane̱‑ɔ ane̱ e̱ de̱e̱re̱ mo̱ne̱ se̱.

ref: Test yourselves and find out if you really are true to your faith. If you pass the test, you will discover that Christ is living in you. But if Christ isn't living in you, you have failed.
text: Mo̱nꞌ gyi mo̱ne̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ase̱ŋ mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱re̱ ke̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ saŋ mo̱ne̱ gya Kristoo si ne̱e̱, bɛɛɛ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ gya mò̱ se̱. Mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ Kristoo bo̱ mo̱ne̱ ase̱ aaa? Mò̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱ mo̱ne̱ ase̱, a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Kristoo awuye kase̱ŋtiŋ.

ref: I hope you will discover we have not failed.
text: Mo̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ i gyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ i ŋu fe̱yɛ ane̱ fe̱raa ane̱ gye̱ Kristoo awuye kase̱ŋtiŋ.

ref: Even though we are weak, we are glad that you are strong, and we pray you will do even better.
text: Mo̱ne̱ e̱ bo̱ le̱ŋ, ne̱ ane̱ fe̱raa ane̱ ya pɔɔ, ke̱tɔ bo̱ ane̱ kɔne̱. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ ke̱mo̱ se̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ lɔŋŋɔ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ke̱ mo̱ŋ bware‑o.

ref: I am writing these things to you before I arrive. This way I won't have to be hard on you when I use the authority the Lord has given me. I was given this authority, so I could help you and not destroy you.
text: Ke̱fɔ ne̱ m bo̱ mo̱ e̱ kyo̱rɛɛ amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ mo̱ e̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ mo̱ne̱, na, mo̱ ya ba, a mo̱ŋ lɛɛ a tiri fe̱yɛ m ba mo̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ bo̱ pee akatɔ we̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ se̱. Ke̱yaale̱ŋ mɔ ne̱ Kristoo a sa mo̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ n taa bo̱ lee mo̱ne̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi si, amaa n taare̱ sa a mo̱nꞌ nya ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi bwe̱e̱tɔ. Kanɔka

ref: Goodbye, my friends. Do better and pay attention to what I have said. Try to get along and live peacefully with each other. Now I pray that God, who gives love and peace, will be with you.
text: Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, ke̱tɔ ne̱ n de mo̱ e̱ gye̱ kɛɛ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ kye̱na kɔne̱ kɔne̱. Mo̱nꞌ le̱ŋ e̱ye̱e̱ lɔŋŋɔ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ke̱ mo̱ŋ bware‑o. Mo̱nꞌ kya abɛɛ‑rɔ. Mo̱nꞌ waa kanɔ ko̱ŋko̱. Mo̱nꞌ kye̱na kaye̱e̱yuri‑ro. Na Wuribware̱ ne̱ ɔ kpa ane̱ ase̱ŋ, ne̱ ɔ sa kaye̱e̱yuri‑o gya mo̱ne̱ kamɛɛ.

ref: I pray that God the Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
text: Ane̱ se̱ Wuribware̱ na ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo yure mo̱ne̱, na bo̱ sa mo̱ne̱ kaye̱e̱yuri.

ref: Christ obeyed God our Father and gave himself as a sacrifice for our sins to rescue us from this evil world.
text: Yeesuu Kristoo e̱ gye̱ ne̱ oo wu sa ane̱, ne̱ oo lee ane̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ kɔɔre̱ ane̱ le̱e̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ a e̱bɔye̱‑ɔ‑rɔ. Ane̱ se̱ Wuribware̱ ya sa ne̱ ɔɔ waa amo̱‑ɔ.

ref: God will be given glory forever and ever. Amen. The Only True Message
text: Wuribware̱ nya ke̱dabe̱ na wuraa nsu pɛɛɛ. Amɛye̱. Ase̱ŋ timaa‑o maa kyurowi daa

ref: when there is really only one true message. But some people are causing you trouble and want to make you turn away from the good news about Christ.
text: Kase̱ŋtiŋ si, ase̱ŋ timaa ko̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ a bo̱‑rɔ bo̱ tii amo̱‑ɔ ne̱ ane̱ a kaapo̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ Kristoo kuŋu si‑o. Amaa ase̱sɛ ko̱ a lwee mo̱ne̱‑rɔ, ne̱ ba tɔɔraa mo̱ne̱ ba kpa fe̱yɛ bo̱ nye̱ra ase̱ŋ timaa amo̱.

ref: I pray that God will punish anyone who preaches anything different from our message to you! It doesn't matter if that person is one of us or an angel from heaven.
text: Amaa mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ ne̱e̱ ooo, ane̱ ne̱e̱ ooo, bɛɛɛ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ ne̱e̱ ooo, ne̱ mò̱ ya bo̱ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ ko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Kristoo kuŋu si ase̱ŋ timaa‑o gywii mo̱ne̱, ne̱ amo̱ e̱ kwe̱e̱ amo̱ ne̱ ane̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ne̱‑ɔ‑rɔ, amo̱ fe̱raa Wuribware̱ gyiiri mò̱ ke̱se̱bɔ.

ref: My friends, I want you to know that no one made up the message I preach.
text: Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ ase̱ŋ timaa bo̱ le̱e̱ Kristoo kuŋu si ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ne̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ le̱e̱ nyiŋkpasɛ kuŋu‑ro ne̱e̱.

ref: It wasn't given or taught to me by some mere human. My message came directly from Jesus Christ when he appeared to me.
text: Mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ amo̱ le̱e̱ nyiŋkpasɛ ko̱ ase̱ ne̱e̱, bɛɛɛ n gye̱ se̱sɛ ko̱ mɔ ya tɔwe̱ amo̱ gywii mo̱. Amaa Yeesuu Kristoo fɔŋfɔŋ ya lee amo̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ mo̱.

ref: You know how I used to live as a Jew. I was cruel to God's church and even tried to destroy it.
text: To, mo̱ne̱ a kyɔ nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ weetee n dɛɛ n te nsaŋ ne̱ n gya Yudaa awuye a mbraa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ mo̱ a waa Wuribware̱ asɔre̱e̱ awuye awo̱re̱fɔɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ, ne̱ mo̱ a kya se̱ kpuri bamo̱.

ref: I was a much better Jew than anyone else my own age, and I obeyed every law our ancestors had given us.
text: Ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ ŋ ye̱re̱‑rɔ n gye̱ mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana Yudaa awuye a ke̱bware̱suŋ‑o‑ro ŋkpɛɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ akatɔ a pee ke̱gya mo̱ nana‑ana ananatɔ se̱‑ɔ.

ref: to show me his Son, so I would announce his message to the Gentiles. I didn't talk this over with anyone.
text: Ne̱ oo lee mò̱ gyi‑o bo̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ fe̱yɛ n taare̱ tɔwe̱ mò̱ ase̱ŋ timaa‑o gywii bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o. Mò̱ a te̱e̱ mo̱‑ɔ, mo̱ŋ yɔ se̱sɛ ko̱ ase̱ a ɔ kaapo̱ mo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ.

ref: I didn't say a word, not even to the men in Jerusalem who were apostles before I was. Instead, I went at once to Arabia, and afterwards I returned to Damascus.
text: Bɛɛɛ n gye̱ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ ne̱ mo̱ a yɔ ya ka bamo̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a lee fe̱yɛ bo̱ kii mò̱ ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱ ne̱ baa gye̱ mo̱ ŋkpɛɛ e̱ba‑ɔ kanɔ. Amaa mò̱ a de̱ŋ te̱e̱ mo̱‑ɔ, puri amo̱‑rɔ ne̱ mo̱ a yɔ Arabiya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱, ne̱ amo̱ kamɛɛ, mo̱ a kiŋŋi ba Damasikus maŋ‑nɔ.

ref: Three years later I went to visit Peter in Jerusalem and stayed with him for
text: Nsu nsa kamɛɛ, ne̱ mo̱ a yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm na mo̱ aa Peetroo sa se̱ŋsa, ne̱ mo̱ a kye̱na mò̱ ase̱ ŋke kudu nnuu.

ref: And in the presence of God I swear I am telling the truth.
text: Wuribware̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ nyi fe̱yɛ ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ mɔ‑ɔ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱e̱.

ref: Later, I went to the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
text: Amo̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ mo̱ a yɔ Siriya na Kilikiya e̱swe̱e̱re̱ se̱.

ref: But no one who belonged to Christ's churches in Judea had ever seen me in person.
text: Saŋ amo̱‑ɔ asɔre̱e̱ agyi ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Yude̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ nyi se̱sɛ suyo ne̱ n gye̱‑ɔ.

ref: They had only heard that the one who had been cruel to them was now preaching the message that he had once tried to destroy.
text: Amaa baa nu ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ dɛɛ sa ne̱ baa gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ‑ɔ e̱ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ timaa amo̱ dɛɛ ne̱ saŋ‑ɔ weetee o tii amo̱ kpa‑ɔ.

ref: Fourteen years later I went to Jerusalem with Barnabas. I also took along Titus.
text: Nsu kudu nna kamɛɛ, mo̱ a kiŋŋi yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm‑ɔ, mo̱ aa Bar‑Nabas na Tiitus ya yɔ.

ref: But I went there because God had told me to go, and I explained the good news I had been preaching to the Gentiles. Then I met privately with the ones who seemed to be the most important leaders. I wanted to make sure my work in the past and my future work would not be for nothing.
text: Ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ a yɔ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ ya kaapo̱ mo̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ yɔ mfe̱ŋ. Mo̱ a bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ a te̱e̱ asɔre̱e̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱ keri si, ne̱ mo̱ a kaapo̱ bamo̱ ase̱ŋ timaa ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o. Ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ a waa amo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ŋ kpa fe̱yɛ ŋ ŋu fe̱yɛ kusuŋ ne̱ mo̱ a waa le̱e̱ ke̱gye̱ŋkpɛɛ‑ɔ bo̱ fo̱ ndɔɔ a kake mɔ‑ɔ twe̱e̱.

ref: Titus went to Jerusalem with me. He was a Greek, but still he wasn't forced to be circumcised.
text: Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ Tiitus ne̱ mo̱ aa mò̱ naa‑ɔ gye̱ Griikinyi ne̱e̱, amaa bamo̱ mɔ bo̱ mo̱ŋ le̱ŋ mò̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ te̱ŋ mò̱ ko̱twe̱e̱tu, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ Yudaa awuye e̱ waa‑ɔ.

ref: We went there because of those who pretended to be followers and had sneaked in among us as spies. They had come to take away the freedom Christ Jesus had given us, and they were trying to make us their slaves.
text: Ase̱sɛ ko̱ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ba tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ a tiri fe̱yɛ bo̱ waa mò̱ ane̱ŋ a atɔ‑ɔ. Ane̱ŋ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ yɛ bo̱ gye̱ Kristoo awuye ne̱e̱, amaa bamo̱ mɔ bo̱ gye̱ aye̱bapo̱ ne̱e̱. Ba kyɔŋŋɔ ane̱ ba ke̱e̱ ne̱e̱ a bo̱ nya ŋu ane̱ŋ ne̱ Kristoo a kya ane̱ yɔwe̱‑ɔ, na bo̱ nya be̱e̱ sa a ane̱ kii anya.

ref: But we wanted you to have the true message. This is why we didn't give in to them, not even for a second.
text: Amaa ane̱ mo̱ŋ sure sa bamo̱ kpa ke̱maa se̱, na kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱ ka bo̱ ase̱ŋ timaa‑o‑ro‑o ma nya nye̱ra sa mo̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o.

ref: Some of them were supposed to be important leaders, but I didn't care who they were. God doesn't have any favorites! None of these so-called special leaders added anything to my message.
text: Bamo̱ gbaa ne̱ mfe̱ŋ a asɔre̱e̱ awuye‑o nyi fe̱yɛ bo̱ gye̱ bamo̱ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ taa ko̱tɔko̱ bo̱ tii mo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ a kaapo̱‑ɔ se̱. Kase̱ŋtiŋ si hare̱e̱ bo̱ gye̱ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ bɛɛɛ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ ne̱e̱ ooo, a mo̱ŋ tiri mo̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ fe̱raa, ɔ maa de̱e̱re̱ se̱sɛ akatɔ se̱.

ref: They realized God had sent me with the good news for Gentiles, and he had sent Peter with the same message for Jews.
text: Amaa baa ŋu fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ ya lee mo̱ fe̱yɛ n tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ timaa bo̱ le̱e̱ Kristoo kuŋu si gywii bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ kyɔ lee Peetroo fe̱yɛ ɔ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ timaa‑o gywii Yudaa awuye‑o.

ref: James, Peter, and John realized that God had given me the message about his gift of undeserved grace. And these men are supposed to be the backbone of the church. They even gave Barnabas and me a friendly handshake. This was to show that we would work with Gentiles and that they would work with Jews.
text: Ne̱ Yakubu na Peetroo na Yohanee ne̱ mfe̱ŋ a asɔre̱e̱ awuye‑o yɛ bo̱ gye̱ bamo̱ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ a ŋu fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ ya ba ane̱ŋ a kusuŋ mumwii mɔ bo̱ bɔɔrɔ mo̱. Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa gywaa mo̱ aa Bar‑Nabas, na a taare̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ aa bamo̱ pɛɛɛ gya kpa ko̱ŋko̱ se̱ ne̱e̱. Ne̱ baa sure si fe̱yɛ mo̱ aa Bar‑Nabas ya tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ timaa‑o gywii bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o, na bamo̱ mɔ bo̱ sii mfe̱ŋ ya tɔwe̱ amo̱ gywii Yudaa awuye‑o.

ref: They only asked us to remember the poor, and this was something I had always been eager to do. Paul Corrects Peter at Antioch
text: Ke̱tɔ ko̱ŋko̱ kpeŋ ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ gywii ane̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ nyiŋŋi atiripo̱ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ bamo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱. Amo̱‑ɔ mɔ dɛɛ ne̱ saŋ ke̱maa mo̱ e̱ le̱ŋ e̱ye̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ waa. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Pɔɔl a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ Peetroo a waa bɔye̱‑ɔ

ref: When Peter came to Antioch, I told him face to face that he was wrong.
text: Amaa kamɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ ne̱ Peetroo a ba Ante̱yɔke̱ya maŋ‑nɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱ daŋ‑ɔ, amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ mo̱ a yii mò̱ se̱.

ref: But when I saw they were not really obeying the truth that is in the good news, I corrected Peter in front of everyone and said: Peter, you are a Jew, but you live like a Gentile. So how can you force Gentiles to live like Jews?
text: Amaa m fe̱raa, mo̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ baa fo̱ kpa, ne̱ baa kine kase̱ŋtiŋ a ke̱kaapo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ a ko̱so̱ kyuwi‑ro bamo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ sa se̱ŋsa gywii Peetroo fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ gye̱ Yudaanyi, amaa fo̱ te ane̱ŋ ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o te‑o. Ne̱ e̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ Kristoo awuye ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o kye̱na fe̱yɛ Yudaa awuye‑o ne̱e̱?

ref: When we Jews started looking for a way to please God, we discovered that we are sinners too. Does this mean that Christ is the one who makes us sinners? No, it doesn't!
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ fo̱ a waa‑ɔ e̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ Yudaa awuye ne̱, bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ Kristoo ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi dooo si, ane̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ e̱ te̱e̱ ane̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ timaa‑o e̱ waa bɔye̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ fo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ Kristoo a kaapo̱ ane̱ e̱kpa bɔye̱. Amaa ane̱ŋ mo̱ŋ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ pɛɛɛ.

ref: It was the Law itself that killed me and freed me from its power, so I could live for God. I have been nailed to the cross with Christ.
text: Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ fe̱raa, ŋ ya kaapo̱ mo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ a bware fe̱yɛ ŋ wu. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ mo̱ a taa mò̱ mbraa‑ɔ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ ya mɔɔ mo̱ ne̱e̱, na n kye̱na bo̱ sa Wuribware̱.

ref: I don't turn my back on God's gift of undeserved grace. If we can be acceptable to God by obeying the Law, it was useless for Christ to die.
text: Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ŋ sure si fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a dɛɛ ŋu ane̱ e̱wɛɛ bo̱ twe̱e̱ ne̱e̱. Amaa se̱sɛ e̱ taare̱ a ɔ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ e̱ te̱e̱ mò̱ se̱sɛ timaa bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ gya Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, amo̱ fe̱raa a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Kristoo a wu ne̱e̱ bo̱ twe̱e̱. Mo̱ ase̱ŋ a lo̱we̱,”

ref: You stupid Galatians! I told you exactly how Jesus Christ was nailed to a cross. Has someone now put an evil spell on you?
text: Mo̱ne̱ Galatiya awuye mo̱ŋ nyi ase̱ŋ. Bɛɛɛ ɔko̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔɔ kyɔ mo̱ne̱ aaa? Mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyiŋŋi si fe̱yɛ ane̱ a kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱‑rɔ nɛɛnɛɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa da Yeesuu Kristoo bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱‑ɔ ɔɔɔ?

ref: I want to know only one thing. How were you given God's Spirit? Was it by obeying the Law of Moses or by hearing about Christ and having faith in him?
text: To, mo̱nꞌ sa a m bise mo̱ne̱ se̱ŋbise mɔ. Kpa mo̱ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a nya Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o? Bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a gya mò̱ mbraa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ se̱ aaa, bɛɛɛ mo̱ne̱ a nu ase̱ŋ timaa bo̱ le̱e̱ Kristoo kuŋu si ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a kɔɔre̱ amo̱ gyi‑o si aaa?

ref: How can you be so stupid? Do you think that by yourself you can complete what God's Spirit started in you?
text: Mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi ase̱ŋ. Ke̱gye̱ŋkpɛɛ‑ɔ mo̱ne̱ a taa mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o si, amaa mbe̱yɔmɔ fe̱raa mo̱ne̱ e̱ fa fe̱yɛ a tiri fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ ba mo̱ne̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ke̱yaale̱ŋ bo̱ waa, na mo̱ne̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ nya fwiiri.

ref: Have you gone through all of this for nothing? Is it all really for nothing?
text: Wuribware̱ a yure mo̱ne̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ bo̱ twe̱e̱ ne̱e̱ bɛɛɛ? Daabii, mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ gyi fe̱yɛ oo yure mo̱ne̱ bo̱ twe̱e̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: God gives you his Spirit and works miracles in you. But does he do this because you obey the Law of Moses or because you have heard about Christ and have faith in him?
text: Ntɔ se̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a sa mo̱ne̱ mò̱ kufwiiŋe‑o ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ ba ɔ waa akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ mo̱ne̱‑rɔ‑ɔ ne̱e̱? Ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ gya ke̱mo̱ ne̱ mò̱ mbraa‑ɔ e̱ kaapo̱‑ɔ se̱ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱? Bɛɛɛ ŋkɔ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a nu abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a kɔɔre̱ amo̱ gyi‑o si ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱?

ref: And so, you should understand that everyone who has faith is a child of Abraham.
text: Amo̱‑ɔ e̱ kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Abraham mò̱ gyi‑ana kase̱ŋtiŋ e̱ gye̱ bamo̱ ne̱ ba kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ ba gyi‑o.

ref: Long ago the Scriptures said God would accept the Gentiles because of their faith. This is why God told Abraham the good news that all nations would be blessed because of him.
text: Abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ ane̱ be̱e̱ ane̱ e̱ kare̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a fa mò̱ mfɛɛre̱ fe̱yɛ saŋ ko̱ e̱ ba, ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o ya kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi, ɔ te̱e̱ bamo̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ timaa. Ase̱ŋ timaa mɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii Abraham‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Na fo̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ mo̱ i yure kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ.”

ref: This means everyone who has faith will share in the blessings given to Abraham because of his faith.
text: Abraham a kɔɔre̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱‑ɔ gyi, ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a yure mò̱. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ Wuribware̱ i yure ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ kɔɔre̱ mò̱ a o gyi‑o.

ref: Anyone who tries to please God by obeying the Law is under a curse. The Scriptures say, “Everyone who doesn't obey everything in the Law is under a curse.”
text: Amaa bamo̱ ne̱ ba kɔɔre̱ ba gyi fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ya gya Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ se̱, Wuribware̱ e̱ te̱e̱ bamo̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ timaa‑o, kase̱ŋtiŋ si, Wuribware̱ a kyɔ da bamo̱ yii, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔke̱maa ne̱ o kine kibu mbraa‑ɔ ase̱ŋkpare̱gyi‑o, ne̱ ɔ maa gya mmo̱ e̱kpa se̱‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ e̱ lo̱ŋ mò̱.”

ref: And because of what Jesus Christ has done, the blessing promised to Abraham was taken to the Gentiles. This happened so that by faith we would be given the promised Holy Spirit. The Law and the Promise
text: Ɔɔ kɔɔre̱ ane̱ ne̱e̱, na mò̱ se̱‑ɔ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaa awuye‑o nya ŋyure ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ o de ɔ sa Abraham‑ɔ, na ane̱ ya kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu Kristoo gyi, ane̱ nya nya mò̱ kufwiiŋe timaa ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ ɔ sa ane̱‑ɔ.

ref: That is how it is with the promises God made to Abraham and his descendant. The promises were not made to many descendants, but only to one, and that one is Christ.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ, Wuribware̱ maa se̱ ɔ tɔwe̱ o gywii Abraham fe̱yɛ ɔ sa mò̱ aa mò̱ ke̱nanagyi ko̱tɔko̱. Mò̱ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ananagyi‑o, mò̱ ke̱nanagyi ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱ o de. Ke̱mo̱ e̱ gye̱ Kristoo.

ref: If we have to obey the Law in order to receive God's blessings, those blessings don't really come to us because of God's promise. But God was kind to Abraham and made him a promise.
text: Amaa amo̱ i tiri fe̱yɛ ane̱ gya Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ se̱ na ane̱ nya nya ŋyure ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ ɔ sa Abraham‑ɔ, amo̱ fe̱raa a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ n lɛɛ gye̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya‑ɔ se̱, ne̱ ane̱ e̱ nya ŋyure amo̱. Amaa ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kyɔ tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ o yure Abraham‑ɔ se̱ ne̱ Abraham a nya ŋyure amo̱.

ref: What is the use of the Law? It was given later to show that we sin. But it was only supposed to last until the coming of that descendant who was given the promise. In fact, angels gave the Law to Moses, and he gave it to the people.
text: To, amo̱ e̱ gye̱ ane̱ŋ fe̱raa, ne̱ nte̱tɔ ne̱e̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a taa mò̱ mbraa‑ɔ bo̱ sa mò̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ? Ɔɔ sa mò̱ mbraa‑ɔ ne̱e̱ na ase̱sɛ nya gyii atɔ ne̱ a mo̱ŋ bware fe̱yɛ bo̱ waa‑ɔ. Mò̱ mbraa mɔ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱e̱ bo̱ fo̱ saŋ ne̱ Abraham ke̱nanagyi Kristoo e̱ ba‑ɔ, ane̱ŋ a se̱sɛ‑ɔ mɔ dɛɛ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ o yure mò̱. Wuribware̱ a taa mò̱ mbraa‑ɔ bo̱ sa mò̱ mbɔɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ taa bo̱ waa se̱sɛ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ, Mosis ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱‑ɔ, mò̱ ya lɔŋŋɔ Wuribware̱ na mò̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ.

ref: But the Scriptures say that sin controls everyone, so that God's promises will be for anyone who has faith in Jesus Christ.
text: Amaa ane̱ e̱ kare̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ e̱bɔye̱ a kɔɔre̱ kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ aŋu. Amo̱ a gye̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, amɔ ase̱sɛ wo̱re̱ ne̱ ba kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu Kristoo ba gyi‑o, bamo̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi‑o si‑o, ba nya ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ sa Abraham‑ɔ.

ref: The Law controlled us and kept us under its power until the time came when we would have faith.
text: Amaa pwɛɛ ne̱ aa dɛɛ fo̱ saŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a lee ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ kɔɔre̱ Kristoo gyi‑o bo̱ kaapo̱ ane̱‑ɔ, ane̱ dɛɛ ane̱ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ ne̱ ba tii bamo̱‑ɔ.

ref: But once a person has learned to have faith, there is no more need to have the Law as a teacher.
text: Kase̱ŋtiŋ si, saŋ amo̱ a kyɔ ba, amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ n de ane̱ se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ. Mo̱ne̱ pɛɛɛ gye̱ Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑ana ne̱e̱

ref: All of you are God's children because of your faith in Christ Jesus.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ ya kɔɔre̱ Kristoo Yeesuu gyi, amo̱ fe̱raa mo̱ne̱ pɛɛɛ gye̱ Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑ana ne̱e̱.

ref: And when you were baptized, it was as though you had put on Christ in the same way you put on new clothes.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ɔke̱maa ne̱ baa bɔ mò̱ asuu‑o, ne̱ ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, oo tii Kristoo ko̱ko̱we̱bɛɛ se̱‑ɔ a kii ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Kristoo‑o. A du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ a buŋ awaagya po̱pwɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: So if you belong to Christ, you are now part of Abraham's family, and you will be given what God has promised.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ko̱ kyɔ ki tii si‑o e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ a tii Kristoo ko̱ko̱we̱bɛɛ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ gye̱ Abraham ananagyi ne̱e̱, amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ mo̱ne̱ e̱ nya ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ sa Abraham‑ɔ.

ref: This is because children are placed in the care of guardians and teachers until the time their parents have set.
text: Mò̱ a saŋ ɔ gye̱ kayaagyi‑o, anya e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ba de̱e̱re̱ mò̱ se̱ pwɛɛ na ɔ dɛɛ bo̱ fo̱ saŋ ne̱ mò̱ se̱ a bo̱ ye̱ra sa mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ ba bo̱ gyi mò̱ kapo̱tɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: This is how it was with us. We were like children ruled by the powers of this world.
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ saŋ ko̱ ane̱ mɔ ane̱ dɛɛ ane̱ du fe̱yɛ ŋyaagyi‑o, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱tɔ ne̱ kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ e̱ kaapo̱‑ɔ a nya ane̱ se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ.

ref: Now that we are his children, God has sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts. And his Spirit tells us that God is our Father.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ waa bo̱ tii si, na a kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ gye̱ mò̱ gyi‑ana‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ oo suŋ mò̱ gyi a kufwiiŋe‑o bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ bo̱ kye̱na ane̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ. Mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱ o te ɔ te̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “N se̱, n se̱.”

ref: You are no longer slaves. You are God's children, and you will be given what he has promised. Paul's Concern for the Galatians
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ mo̱ne̱ ɔke̱maa mo̱ŋ lɛɛ ɔ gye̱ ke̱nya, amaa ɔ gye̱ ogyi ne̱e̱. Ne̱ fo̱ ke̱maa mɔ a gye̱ ogyi‑o si‑o, Wuribware̱ e̱ sa a fo̱ gyi mò̱ kapo̱tɛɛ. Pɔɔl yɛ saŋ‑ɔ bamo̱ akatɔ a gyi fe̱yɛ mò̱ a bo̱ bamo̱ ase̱

ref: Before you knew God, you were slaves of gods that are not real.
text: Saŋ‑ɔ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi Wuribware̱. Ikisise̱ŋ na atɔ ne̱ a mo̱ŋ gye̱ Wuribware̱ lee‑o dooo ya kɔɔre̱ mo̱ne̱ aŋu.

ref: when I first preached to you? No you didn't, even though you knew I had come there because I was sick.
text: Mo̱ne̱ nyi fe̱yɛ ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ a gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ bo̱ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ gywii mo̱ne̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ saŋ amo̱‑ɔ mo̱ e̱ lɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: My illness must have caused you some trouble, but you didn't hate me or turn me away because of it. You welcomed me as though I were one of God's angels or even Christ Jesus himself.
text: Amaa mo̱ ko̱lɔ amo̱ a tɔɔraa mo̱ne̱ fɛɛ, amo̱ mɔ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ kpo̱raŋ mo̱ se̱. Kase̱ŋtiŋ si, mo̱ne̱ a kra mo̱ nɛɛnɛɛ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a gye̱ Wuribware̱ a mbɔɔ‑ɔ ɔko̱‑ɔ, bɛɛɛ mo̱ a gye̱ Kristoo Yeesuu fɔŋfɔŋ‑ɔ.

ref: Am I now your enemy, just because I told you the truth?
text: Ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ a gyi kase̱ŋtiŋ gywii mo̱ne̱‑ɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ a kii mo̱ne̱ do̱ŋ aaa?

ref: Those people may be paying you a lot of attention, but it isn't for your good. They only want to keep you away from me, so you will pay them a lot of attention.
text: Ase̱sɛ ne̱ ba tɔɔraa mo̱ne̱ ba kaapo̱ aye̱ba ase̱ŋ mo̱ne̱‑rɔ‑ɔ de te̱maa fe̱yɛ ba nya mo̱ne̱ a bo̱ baa bamo̱ ke̱be̱gya‑ɔ se̱. Amaa ke̱tɔ ne̱ bo̱ ye̱re̱ ke̱mo̱ se̱ ba waa amo̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ bo̱ daŋ. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ba kpa a a bo̱ waa‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ba kpa ane̱ aa mo̱ne̱ a brawe̱ abɛɛ se̱ ne̱e̱, na mo̱nꞌ nya gya bamo̱ se̱.

ref: Some of you would like to be under the rule of the Law of Moses. But do you know what the Law says?
text: To, mo̱ne̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ yɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa a mo̱nꞌ gya Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ laatɔ a mo̱nꞌ nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ mbraa amo̱ dɛɛ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ.

ref: In the Scriptures we learn that Abraham had two sons. The mother of one of them was a slave, while the mother of the other one had always been free.
text: Baa kyo̱rɛɛ Wuribware̱ a mbraa wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ Abraham de agyi nyaŋsɛ anyɔ, bamo̱‑rɔ ɔko̱ mò̱ nyi gye̱ ke̱nya ne̱e̱, ne̱ ɔko̱ŋko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ saŋ‑ɔ mò̱ nyi gye̱ ogyi ne̱e̱.

ref: The son of the slave woman was born in the usual way. But the son of the free woman was born because of God's promise.
text: Ɔkye̱e̱ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ ke̱nya‑ɔ a nya kame, ne̱ ɔɔ ko̱we̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ba ko̱we̱ se̱sɛ ke̱maa‑ɔ dɛɛ. Amaa ɔkye̱e̱ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ ogyi‑o fe̱raa, mò̱ a mo̱ŋ taare̱ ko̱we̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ɔɔ nya kame ko̱we̱ keegyi, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ ya tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ a waa ane̱ŋ.

ref: The Scriptures say about her, “You have never had children, but now you can be glad. You have never given birth, but now you can shout. Once you had no children, but now you will have more children than a woman who has been married for a long time.”
text: Baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔle̱ŋmaŋpo̱, fo̱ ne̱ fo̱ mo̱ŋ ko̱we̱ daa‑ɔ, ba kakatɔgyi bo̱ faa‑rɔ keŋkeŋ, fo̱ ne̱ fo̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ duŋwi daa‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ ne̱ fo̱ kuri a se̱re̱ yɔwe̱ fo̱‑ɔ e̱ nya ŋyaagyi a fo̱ kyo̱ŋ mò̱ ne̱ ɔ saŋ o te mò̱ kuri aye̱‑ɔ.”

ref: But the child who was born in the natural way made trouble for the child who was born because of the Spirit. The same thing is happening today.
text: Saŋ amo̱‑ɔ mò̱ gyi ne̱ baa ko̱we̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ba ko̱we̱ se̱sɛ ke̱maa‑ɔ a waa mò̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o a sa ne̱ baa ko̱we̱ mò̱‑ɔ awo̱re̱fɔɔ. Ane̱ŋ a awo̱re̱fɔɔ ne̱ ane̱ i gyi ndɔɔ‑ɔ.

ref: My friends, we are children of the free woman and not of the slave.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, ane̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ ɔkye̱e̱ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ ke̱nya‑ɔ mò̱ gyi‑ana, amaa ane̱ gye̱ ɔkye̱e̱ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ ogyi‑o mò̱ gyi‑ana ne̱e̱.

ref: Christ has set us free! This means we are really free. Now hold on to your freedom and don't ever become slaves of the Law again.
text: Kristoo a mo̱rɔwe̱ ane̱ ne̱e̱ a kase̱ŋtiŋ si ane̱ nya ane̱ e̱ye̱e̱. Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ mo̱nꞌ le̱ŋ e̱ye̱e̱, na mo̱ne̱ ma lɛɛ sa a ɔko̱ sa a mo̱nꞌ kii mbraa ne̱ Wuribware̱ a sa Yudaa awuye‑o anya be̱e̱.

ref: And if you try to please God by obeying the Law, you have cut yourself off from Christ and his gift of undeserved grace.
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ ya gya Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ se̱, Wuribware̱ e̱ te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ se̱sɛ timaa‑o a te̱ŋ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ le̱e̱ Kristoo ase̱, ne̱ oo kine ŋyure ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ kpa a o yure mò̱‑ɔ.

ref: But the Spirit makes us sure God will accept us because of our faith in Christ.
text: Ane̱ fe̱raa, Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ kya ane̱‑rɔ, na ane̱ kɔɔre̱ gyi fe̱yɛ ane̱ de te̱maa ane̱ gywii saŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ te̱e̱ ane̱ lamaŋ‑nɔ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ timaa‑o.

ref: If you are a follower of Christ Jesus, it makes no difference whether you are circumcised or not. All that matters is your faith that makes you love others.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ a tɛɛ tii Kristoo Yeesuu a ko̱ko̱we̱bɛɛ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, hare̱e̱ baa te̱ŋŋe̱ ane̱ atwe̱e̱tu bɛɛɛ bo̱ mo̱ŋ te̱ŋŋe̱ ooo, a mo̱ŋ gye̱ atɔ ne̱ a tiri pɛɛɛ‑ɔ. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ki tiri‑o e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ kɔɔre̱ Kristoo gyi, na ane̱ ke̱kpa ne̱ ane̱ e̱ kpa ane̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana‑ɔ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi‑o bo̱ le̱ŋ.

ref: You were doing so well until someone made you turn from the truth.
text: Weetee saŋ ko̱‑rɔ mo̱ne̱ Galatiya awuye dɛɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱ waa atɔ nɛɛnɛɛ. Ne̱ nsɛ ya fo̱re̱ waa mo̱ne̱‑rɔ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ yɔwe̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o ke̱gyase̱?

ref: And that person was certainly not sent by the one who chose you.
text: Ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ n gye̱ Wuribware̱ ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ bo̱ gya mò̱ se̱‑ɔ ya waa ane̱ŋ.

ref: A little yeast can change a whole batch of dough,
text: Abre̱sɛ ya tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Yiisi kafo̱ŋfwe̱e̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ sa a bodobodoo pɛɛɛ tiŋ.”

ref: but you belong to the Lord. This makes me certain you will do what I say, instead of what someone else tells you to do. Whoever is causing trouble for you will be punished.
text: To, ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ e̱ sa ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ i gyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ i sure ase̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ se̱, na ke̱tɔ ne̱ ki tii si‑o e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ i gyiiri ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ tɔɔraa mo̱ne̱‑ɔ ke̱se̱bɔ.

ref: My friends, if I still preach that people need to be circumcised, why am I in so much trouble? The message about the cross would no longer be a problem, if I told people to be circumcised.
text: Amɔ m maa se̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ atwe̱e̱tu ke̱te̱ŋŋe̱ tiri ne̱e̱‑ɔ, weetee ntɔ se̱ ne̱ Yudaa awuye‑o saŋ ba waa mo̱ awo̱re̱fɔɔ‑ɔ ne̱e̱? Ne̱ ane̱ŋ a ke̱kaapo̱‑ɔ e̱ bo̱ daŋ, amo̱ fe̱raa weetee mo̱ ya tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ Kristoo a wu kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱ sa ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ, bamo̱ iduŋ maa fwii mo̱ se̱.

ref: I wish everyone who is upsetting you would not only get circumcised, but would cut off much more!
text: Mo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ ne̱ ba tɔɔraa mo̱ne̱‑ɔ, bo̱ lee bamo̱ awɛɛ bo̱ tii bamo̱ atwe̱e̱tu ke̱te̱ŋŋe̱‑ɔ se̱.

ref: All the Law says can be summed up in the command to love others as much as you love yourself.
text: Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ pɛɛɛ kuŋu e̱ gye̱ mbraa ne̱ ŋ yɛ, “Kpa fo̱ bɛɛko̱ ase̱ŋ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kpa fo̱ e̱ye̱e̱‑ɔ.”

ref: But if you keep attacking each other like wild animals, you had better watch out or you will destroy yourselves. God's Spirit and Our Own Desires
text: Amaa mo̱ne̱ daa i du fe̱yɛ mbo̱ kpe̱ŋsɛ ne̱ ba kra bamo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana mbo̱ ne̱ ba duŋwi bamo̱ ŋyo̱wɔre̱ ba kyaye̱‑rɔ‑ɔ lee‑o fe̱raa, mo̱ne̱ i kpuri abɛɛ. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ mo̱nꞌ sa se̱. Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o na kinyiŋkpasɛgyi‑o

ref: The Spirit and your desires are enemies of each other. They are always fighting each other and keeping you from doing what you feel you should.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ane̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ane̱ waa‑ɔ kwe̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ mò̱ kufwiiŋe‑o e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ane̱ waa‑ɔ‑rɔ. Atɔ anyɔ mɔ gye̱ ado̱ŋ ne̱e̱, amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ de kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱ waa ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ.

ref: But if you obey the Spirit, the Law of Moses has no control over you.
text: To, Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o e̱ de̱e̱re̱ mo̱ne̱ se̱, n lɛɛ gye̱ mò̱ mbraa‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ n de̱e̱re̱ mo̱ne̱ se̱.

ref: envious. They get drunk, carry on at wild parties, and do other evil things as well. I told you before, and I am telling you again: No one who does these things will share in the blessings of God's kingdom.
text: A mo̱ŋ de kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ de mo̱ne̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana kikisi. A mo̱ŋ bware fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ bwe̱e̱, na mo̱nꞌ gyi a mo̱nꞌ nuu nta bwe̱e̱tɔ, bɛɛɛ mo̱nꞌ waa ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ku du ane̱ŋ‑ɔ. Mo̱ a kyɔ gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ yii mo̱ne̱ se̱, ne̱ m be̱e̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ te ane̱ŋ a kakye̱na bɔye̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ maa nya kpa a bo̱ tii Wuribware̱ a kuwure‑o si.

ref: God's Spirit has given us life, and so we should follow the Spirit.
text: Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o a gye̱ mò̱ ne̱ ɔ sa ane̱ kakye̱na po̱pwɛɛ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ane̱ sa a ɔ de̱e̱re̱ ane̱ se̱.

ref: But don't be conceited or make others jealous by claiming to be better than they are.
text: Ane̱ ma waa akatɔle̱ŋ. Ane̱ ma fwii ane̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana iduŋ, ne̱ ŋkee ane̱ ma waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ mɔɔ ane̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana ŋyo̱wɔre̱‑ɔ.

ref: My friends, you are spiritual. So if someone is trapped in sin, you should gently lead that person back to the right path. But watch out, and don't be tempted yourself.
text: Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana ne̱ mo̱ne̱ gya Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o si nɛɛnɛɛ‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ ya ŋu ɔko̱ e̱ waa bɔye̱, mo̱nꞌ kaapo̱ mò̱ kpa timaa‑o. Amaa mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa a mo̱nꞌ waa amo̱‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ baa mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ kaase̱ se̱sɛ amo̱ ase̱. N gye̱ ane̱ŋ, mo̱ne̱ e̱ sa a Ɔbɔnsam kyɔ mo̱ne̱‑rɔ ke̱e̱ ba mo̱ne̱ bo̱ waa e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ.

ref: You obey the law of Christ when you offer each other a helping hand.
text: Ɔke̱maa kya mò̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑rɔ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa‑rɔ. Mo̱ne̱ ya waa amo̱‑ɔ, amo̱ fe̱raa mo̱ne̱ e̱ waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ Kristoo a tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ waa‑ɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: If you think you are better than others, when you really aren't, you are wrong.
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ fa fe̱yɛ ɔ gye̱ se̱sɛ dabe̱‑ɔ, na mò̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ sɛye̱‑ɔ e̱ pe̱nna mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: Share every good thing you have with anyone who teaches you what God has said.
text: Amaa a bware fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ne̱ ba suye abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ sa bamo̱ akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ atɔ timaa ke̱maa mɔ ne̱ bo̱ de‑o ako̱.

ref: You cannot fool God, so don't make a fool of yourself! You will harvest what you plant.
text: Ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ fa fe̱yɛ ɔ taare̱ a ɔ pe̱nna Wuribware̱‑ɔ e̱ pe̱nna mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ne̱e̱. Ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ se̱sɛ i dwii‑o, ke̱mo̱ dɛɛ ke̱mo̱ ne̱ ɔ nya.

ref: Don't get tired of helping others. You will be rewarded when the time is right, if you don't give up.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ mo̱ne̱ ma sa a ane̱ kpo̱ne̱ itimaa ko̱waa se̱. Ane̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ kiŋŋi kamɛɛ fe̱raa, saŋ ko̱ e̱ ba ne̱ ane̱ e̱ nya ane̱ itimaa kakɔka.

ref: We should help people whenever we can, especially if they are followers of the Lord. Final Warnings
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ saŋ ke̱maa ne̱ ane̱ e̱ nya kpa‑ɔ, a bware fe̱yɛ ane̱ waa ɔke̱maa itimaa. Amaa ane̱ gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ waa ane̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana Kristoo awuye itimaa pwɛɛ. Mò̱ ase̱ŋ lalalo̱we̱

ref: You can see what big letters I make when I write with my own hand.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ fe̱raa, mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ asare̱e̱ ne̱ n de mo̱ e̱ kyo̱rɛɛ wo̱re̱ mɔ mo̱ e̱ gye̱ kɛɛ. Mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱ akyo̱rɛɛgyi dabe̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kyo̱rɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: Those people who are telling you to get circumcised are only trying to show how important they are. And they don't want to get into trouble for preaching about the cross of Christ.
text: Nte̱tɔ ase̱sɛ e̱ gye̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ maa se̱ ba tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ te̱ŋŋe̱ mo̱ne̱ atwe̱e̱tu‑o? Ba kpa fe̱yɛ bo̱ko̱ kyo̱rɔ bamo̱ ne̱e̱. Amaa bo̱ maa kpa fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ waa bamo̱ awo̱re̱fɔɔ bo̱ le̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ kaapo̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ Kristoo kuwu kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: But I will never brag about anything except the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ. Because of his cross, the world is dead as far as I am concerned, and I am dead as far as the world is concerned.
text: M fe̱raa, ma kaapo̱ mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa se̱, amɔ bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo a wu kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ wo̱re̱. Mò̱ a wu ane̱ŋ a lowi‑o si‑o, kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ŋyo̱rɔwe̱ bɔye̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ m bo̱ mo̱ kɔne̱. N du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ ya wu‑o.

ref: It doesn't matter if you are circumcised or not. All that matters is that you are a new person.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ hare̱e̱ baa te̱ŋŋe̱ ane̱ atwe̱e̱tu, bɛɛɛ bo̱ mo̱ŋ te̱ŋŋe̱ ooo, a mo̱ŋ gye̱ atɔ ne̱ a tiri‑o. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ki tiri‑o e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ Kristoo a waa ane̱ ase̱sɛ po̱pwɛɛ.

ref: If you follow this rule, you will belong to God's true people. God will treat you with undeserved kindness and will bless you with peace.
text: Bamo̱ ne̱ bamo̱ fe̱raa bo̱ te ane̱ŋ a kakye̱na po̱pwɛɛ mɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ e̱ sa bamo̱ kaye̱e̱yuri, na o yure bamo̱. Bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱sɛ kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o ne̱ mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ mo̱ e̱ sa‑ɔ.

ref: On my own body are scars that prove I belong to Christ Jesus. So I don't want anyone to bother me anymore.
text: Amo̱ ne̱ a gye̱ kɛɛ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ ma lɛɛ sa a ɔko̱ be̱e̱ tɔɔraa mo̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ taare̱ a ŋ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ alɔpane̱ ne̱ a te mo̱ kayo̱wɔre̱ se̱ ne̱ a kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ Yeesuu e̱ bo̱ mo̱‑ɔ.

ref: My friends, I pray that the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you! Amen.
text: Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo yure mo̱ne̱. Amɛye̱.

ref: I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! Christ Brings Spiritual Blessings
text: Mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ se̱ Wuribware̱ na ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo yure mo̱ne̱, na ɔ sa mo̱ne̱ kaye̱e̱yuri. Ŋyure ne̱ Wuribware̱ na Kristoo a sa ane̱‑ɔ

ref: Praise the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ for the spiritual blessings that Christ has brought us from heaven!
text: Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ kyo̱rɔ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo mò̱ se̱ Wuribware̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔɔ bo̱rɔ mò̱ kufwiiŋe timaa‑o si sa ane̱ ne̱ ane̱ gye̱ Kristoo ase̱sɛ‑ɔ ŋyure ne̱ ŋ ya le̱e̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ane̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a yuri ane̱.

ref: God treated us with undeserved grace because of the Son he dearly loves, and so we should praise God.
text: Ŋyure amo̱ pɛɛɛ ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱rɔ mò̱ gyi Yeesuu ne̱ ɔ kpa mò̱ ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ se̱ ke ane̱, na ane̱ nya kyo̱rɔ mò̱ ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ na mò̱ ke̱dabe̱.

ref: and by what Christ has done, God has shown us his own mysterious ways.
text: Ŋyure‑o ŋko̱ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ɔɔ sa ane̱ kanyiase̱ŋ pɛɛɛ na ane̱ bo̱ ŋu ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ fa hare̱e̱ le̱e̱ saŋ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ ɔ waa‑ɔ.

ref: Then when the time is right, God will do all he has planned, and Christ will bring together everything in heaven and on earth.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ fa, ne̱ mò̱ akatɔ a gyi fe̱yɛ ɔ waa‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ saŋ ya fo̱, a bware fe̱yɛ ɔ sa a ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ke̱ bo̱ so̱so̱ na kaase̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ bo̱ tii abɛɛ se̱, na Kristoo gyi amo̱ se̱ pɛɛɛ kuwure.

ref: I have heard about your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all God's people.
text: Amaa m fe̱raa, mo̱ a nu ase̱ŋ kɔne̱kɔne̱sɛ ko̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ saŋ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu mo̱ne̱ i gyi. Ne̱ mo̱ne̱ be̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa Wuribware̱ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ ase̱ŋ.

ref: I ask the glorious Father and God of our Lord Jesus Christ to give you his Spirit. The Spirit will make you wise and let you understand what it means to know God.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱mo̱ se̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ mo̱ e̱ sa mo̱ne̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ ane̱ se̱ dabe̱‑ɔ na ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo mò̱ se̱‑ɔ sa mo̱ne̱ mò̱ kufwiiŋe timaa‑o, na ɔ nya sa mo̱ne̱ kanyiase̱ŋ, na ɔ nya lee ane̱ŋ ne̱ o du‑o kawu bo̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱. Mfaanɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i gyii mò̱ nɛɛnɛɛ.

ref: My prayer is that light will flood your hearts and you will understand the hope given to you when God chose you. Then you will discover the glorious blessings that will be yours together with all God's people.
text: M be̱e̱ mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ akatɔ buŋŋi, na mo̱nꞌ nu te̱maa ne̱ ɔɔ sa mo̱ne̱‑ɔ kaase̱, na mo̱nꞌ ŋu fe̱yɛ ɔɔ te̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ bo̱ kii mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ lee, na mo̱nꞌ be̱e̱ ŋu ŋyure dabe̱ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ ɔ sa mò̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ.

ref: There Christ rules over all forces, authorities, powers, and rulers. He rules over all beings in this world and will rule in the future world as well.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ a sa ne̱ Kristoo a gyi se̱sɛ ke̱maa na ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ki de ke̱yaale̱ŋ so̱so̱ na kaase̱ na kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ na kamo̱ ne̱ ka ba‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ kuwure. Ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ Kristoo ke̱nyare̱ kyɔ ke̱nyare̱ ke̱maa.

ref: God has put all things under the power of Christ, and for the good of the church he has made him the head of everything.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ a taa ke̱tɔ ke̱maa baa Kristoo kaase̱, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ sa ne̱ oo kii asɔre̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa owure.

ref: In the past you were dead because you sinned and fought against God.
text: To, saŋ‑ɔ mo̱ne̱ gye̱ abɔye̱waapo̱ ne̱e̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ dɛɛ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ de kakye̱na po̱pwɛɛ.

ref: You followed the ways of this world and obeyed the devil. He rules the world, and his spirit has power over everyone who doesn't obey God.
text: Nsaŋ amo̱, amɔ mo̱ne̱ gya kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ase̱sɛ a e̱kpa‑ɔ se̱. Ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i bu ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ afwii‑ro owure‑o. Ɔbɔnsam, ne̱ ɔ gye̱ ibrisi pɛɛɛ‑rɔ ɔbre̱sɛ‑ɔ, ase̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱. Mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ ɔ maa se̱ ɔ waa kusuŋ bɔye̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ maa bu Wuribware̱‑ɔ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ.

ref: Once we were also ruled by the selfish desires of our bodies and minds. We had made God angry, and we were going to be punished like everyone else.
text: Ane̱ gbaa ane̱ dɛɛ ane̱ tii bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ gya dimaadi ke̱pre̱ se̱‑ɔ se̱, ne̱ ane̱ a dɛɛ waa ase̱ŋ bɔye̱ ne̱ ane̱ ŋyo̱rɔwe̱ na ane̱ mfɛɛre̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ. Bamo̱ a ko̱we̱ ane̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ane̱ gbaa ane̱ dɛɛ ane̱ tii bamo̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ duŋ i fwii si‑o si ne̱e̱.

ref: But God was merciful! We were dead because of our sins, but God loved us so much he made us alive with Christ, and God's gift of undeserved grace is what saves you.
text: Saŋ ne̱ ane̱ dɛɛ ane̱ gya e̱kpa bɔye̱ se̱‑ɔ, ane̱ mo̱ŋ de kakye̱na po̱pwɛɛ. Mbe̱yɔmɔ fe̱raa, Wuribware̱ a kyiŋŋi Kristoo le̱e̱ lowi‑ro‑o si‑o, ne̱ ane̱ a nya kakye̱na po̱pwɛɛ. Wuribware̱ a ŋu ane̱ e̱wɛɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ, ne̱ ɔ kpa ane̱ ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ waa ane̱ŋ. Oo su ane̱ se̱ ne̱e̱ se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ mo̱rɔwe̱ ane̱.

ref: God raised us from death to life with Christ Jesus, and he has given us a place beside Christ in heaven.
text: A du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kyiŋŋi ane̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ oo kyiŋŋi Kristoo‑o. Ne̱ a be̱e̱ a du fe̱yɛ mò̱ a sa ne̱ ane̱ a yɔ ya kye̱na mò̱ ase̱ Wuribware̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ a gye̱ ko̱ko̱we̱bɛɛ ko̱ŋko̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ.

ref: God did this so in the future world he could show how truly good and kind he is to us because of what Christ Jesus has done.
