ref: Everything belongs to God, and all things were created by his power. So God did the right thing when he made Jesus perfect by suffering, as Jesus led many of God's children to be saved and to share in his glory.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ Nyiŋkpe̱ŋ-twe̱e̱-atɔ‑pɛɛɛ-Bware̱ a kpa fe̱yɛ se̱sɛ dimaadi ne̱ ɔɔ twe̱e̱‑ɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ nya ke̱dabe̱ na wuraa so̱so̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, a bware fe̱yɛ ɔ bo̱rɔ awo̱re̱fɔɔ ne̱ Yeesuu a gyi‑o si sa ne̱ kpa ke̱maa se̱ Yeesuu a kii se̱sɛ dimaadi ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ na ɔmo̱rɔwe̱po̱.

ref: Jesus and the people he makes holy all belong to the same family. This is why he isn't ashamed to call them his brothers and sisters.
text: Yeesuu ne̱ ɔ gye̱ ɔkpe̱yaa‑ɔ na bamo̱ ne̱ ɔ sa ne̱ ba kii akpe̱yaa‑ɔ gye̱ se̱ ko̱ŋko̱ agyi ne̱e̱. Amo̱se̱ ne̱ ipeere mo̱ŋ de mò̱ ke̱te̱e̱ bamo̱ mò̱ ko̱we̱bɛɛ‑ana.

ref: He even said to God, “I will tell them your name and sing your praises when they come together to worship.”
text: Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, “Mo̱ i lee fo̱ ke̱nyare̱ a n gywii mo̱ aye̱ awuye. Na ŋ waa iliŋ bo̱ kyo̱rɔ fo̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ase̱sɛ e̱ gyaŋŋe̱ ba suŋ fo̱‑ɔ.”

ref: He also said, “I will trust God.” Then he said, “Here I am with the children God has given me.”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ to̱ŋ ko̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ taa mo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a m bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ mò̱ se̱.” Ɔɔ be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ to̱ŋ ko̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ke̱e̱ ɛ, mo̱ aa agyi ne̱ Wuribware̱ a sa mo̱‑ɔ bo̱ mfe̱e̱.”

ref: We are people of flesh and blood. This is why Jesus became one of us. He died to destroy the devil, who had power over death.
text: Agyi amo̱ a gye̱ ase̱sɛ dimaadi‑o si, Yeesuu gbaa a kii se̱sɛ dimaadi. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ aa sa ne̱ mò̱ lowi ne̱ oo wu‑o si, ɔɔ kɔ gya Ɔbɔnsam ne̱ o de ke̱yaale̱ŋ a ɔ mɔɔ‑ɔ.

ref: But he also died to rescue all of us who live each day in fear of dying.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ ne̱ lowi kufu dɛɛ ki de bamo̱ se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ le̱e̱ saŋ ne̱ baa ko̱we̱ bamo̱‑ɔ bo̱ fo̱ saŋ ne̱ baa le̱e̱ gya mò̱ se̱‑ɔ a nya bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱.

ref: Jesus clearly did not come to help angels, but he did come to help Abraham's descendants.
text: Kase̱ŋtiŋ, n gye̱ Wuribware̱ a mbɔɔ‑ɔ ne̱ ɔ mo̱rɔwe̱. Abraham a ananagyi‑o ne̱ ɔ mo̱rɔwe̱.

ref: Jesus was faithful to God, who appointed him, just as Moses was faithful in serving all God's people.
text: Ɔɔ sa ne̱ Wuribware̱ ne̱ oo lee mò̱‑ɔ a kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ Mosis a sa ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kɔɔre̱ mò̱ gyi saŋ ne̱ ɔ de̱e̱re̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱sɛ se̱‑ɔ.

ref: But Jesus deserves more honor than Moses, just as the builder of a house deserves more honor than the house.
text: Amaa Yeesuu e̱ gye̱ ɔdabe̱, ne̱ a bware fe̱yɛ bo̱ bo̱nyaa mò̱, sa mò̱ wuraa kyo̱ŋ Mosis, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ se̱sɛ ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ne̱e̱ kabuno‑o de bo̱nyaa ɔ kyɔ kabuno‑o fɔŋfɔŋ‑ɔ.

ref: Of course, every house is built by someone, and God is really the one who built everything.
text: Kabuno ke̱maa de ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ne̱e̱ kamo̱‑ɔ. Ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ne̱e̱ kabuno gye̱ŋkpɛɛsɛ, ne̱ ɔɔ twe̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa bo̱ tii si‑o e̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱.

ref: It is just as the Holy Spirit says, “If you hear God's voice today,
text: Mo̱nꞌ nu ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ,

ref: For forty years your ancestors tested God and saw the things he did.
text: Mfe̱ŋ, nsu aduna mo̱ne̱ nana‑ana a kyɔ mo̱‑rɔ, ne̱ baa ŋu ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ a waa‑ɔ.

ref: “Then God got tired of them and said, ‘You people never show good sense, and you don't understand what I want you to do.’
text: Amo̱se̱ ne̱ mo̱ duŋ a fwii bamo̱ se̱, ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Bamo̱ mfɛɛre̱ na ŋkpo̱nɔ a le̱e̱ mo̱ se̱, ne̱ baa kine kigyii mo̱ mbraa‑ɔ, yɔwe̱ mo̱ e̱kpa‑ɔ se̱ ke̱gya.

ref: God became angry and told the people, ‘You will never enter my place of rest!’ ”
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ mo̱ a ba kaduŋfwii bo̱ waa e̱taŋ, fe̱yɛ bo̱ maa ba mo̱ kyurekpa‑ɔ daa.’ ”

ref: You must encourage one another each day. And you must keep on while there is still a time that can be called “today.” If you don't, then sin may fool some of you and make you stubborn.
text: Amaa mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱ waa abɛɛ wɔre̱ kake ke̱maa, na e̱bɔye̱ ma nya sa a mo̱nꞌ waa ke̱se̱bɔrɔle̱ŋ. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ya tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ya nu Wuribware̱ e̱ sa se̱ŋsa o gywii mo̱ne̱ ndɔɔ,” ndɔɔ e̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ a ŋke‑o. Saŋ saŋ ɔ bo̱‑rɔ fe̱yɛ ane̱ gya Wuribware̱ kanɔ se̱.

ref: We were sure about Christ when we first became his people. So let's hold tightly to our faith until the end.
text: Amaa ane̱ ya kya se̱ kɔɔre̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ane̱ a le̱e̱ kaase̱ ane̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ ane̱ i gyi‑o wo̱re̱ gyi, amo̱ fe̱raa ane̱ kanɔ tii Kristoo ke̱tɔ ke̱maa si.

ref: Who were those people that heard God's voice and rebelled? Weren't they the same ones that came out of Egypt with Moses?
text: Nte̱tɔ ase̱sɛ ya kine Wuribware̱ kanɔ se̱ ke̱gya‑ɔ ne̱e̱? Ase̱sɛ ko̱ŋko̱‑ɔ dɛɛ ne̱ Mosis a gye̱ bamo̱ ke̱gye̱ŋkpɛɛ le̱e̱ Igyipiti swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: And who did God say would never enter his place of rest? Weren't they the ones that disobeyed him?
text: Ne̱ nsɛ‑ana ne̱ Wuribware̱ a waa e̱taŋ fe̱yɛ bo̱ maa ba mò̱ kyurekpa‑ɔ ne̱e̱? Bamo̱ ne̱ baa kine ke̱gya mò̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: We see that those people did not enter the place of rest because they did not have faith.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ane̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ bamo̱ a yɔwe̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi‑o si‑o, bo̱ mo̱ŋ taare̱ yɔ mò̱ kyurekpa‑ɔ.

ref: The promise to enter the place of rest is still good, and we must take care that none of you miss out.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ, kpa a saŋ ɔ da sa ane̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ nya Wuribware̱ a kukyure ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ ɔ sa ane̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱‑rɔ ɔko̱ ma waa paŋ ane̱ŋ a kukyure amo̱.

ref: We have heard the message, just as they did. But they failed to believe what they heard, and the message did not do them any good.
text: Ane̱ a nu ase̱ŋ timaa‑o ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ane̱ nana‑ana‑ɔ a nu amo̱‑ɔ. Amaa bamo̱ ase̱ŋ timaa ne̱ baa nu‑o mo̱ŋ baa bamo̱ kitimaa ko̱, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ mo̱ŋ kra‑rɔ nɛɛnɛɛ, kɔɔre̱ amo̱ gyi kase̱ŋtiŋ.

ref: Only people who have faith will enter the place of rest. It is just as the Scriptures say, “God became angry and told the people, ‘You will never enter my place of rest!’ ” God said this, even though everything has been ready from the time of creation.
text: Amaa ane̱ fe̱raa, ane̱ ya kɔɔre̱ gyi, ane̱ e̱ yɔ kyurekpa ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ mò̱ ase̱ŋ saŋ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ mo̱ a ba kaduŋfwii bo̱ waa e̱taŋ, fe̱yɛ bo̱ maa ba mo̱ kyurekpa‑ɔ daa”‑ɔ. N gye̱ sa ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ lɔŋŋɔ kyurekpa amo̱ ne̱e̱ se̱ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ. Kase̱ŋtiŋ si fe̱raa hare̱e̱ le̱e̱ saŋ ne̱ ɔɔ twe̱e̱ kaye̱‑ɔ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ kyɔ lɔŋŋɔ mfe̱ŋ bo̱ ye̱ra.

ref: In fact, somewhere the Scriptures say that by the seventh day, God had finished his work, and so he rested.
text: Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ to̱ŋ ko̱ yɛ, “To, amɔ Wuribware̱ a kyɔ lo̱we̱ mò̱ kusuŋ‑o pɛɛɛ, amo̱se̱‑ɔ kake sunoose̱po̱, oo kyure.”

ref: We also read that he later said, “You people will never enter my place of rest!”
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ, Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Bo̱ maa ba mo̱ kyurekpa‑ɔ daa”‑ɔ, mò̱ so̱so̱ a kyurekpa‑ɔ ase̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱.

ref: This means that the promise to enter is still good, because those who first heard about it disobeyed and did not enter.
text: Bamo̱ ne̱ baa gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ nu ase̱ŋ timaa bo̱ le̱e̱ kyurekpa amo̱ se̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ yɔ mfe̱ŋ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ baa kine bo̱ mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ a ase̱ŋ‑ɔ gyi. Amaa Wuribware̱ a sa bo̱ko̱ ne̱ baa ba kamɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ kpa fe̱yɛ bo̱ yɔ kyurekpa amo̱.

ref: But God has promised us a Sabbath when we will rest, even though it has not yet come.
text: Amo̱‑ɔ e̱ kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ ase̱sɛ saŋ bo̱ de kukyure ko̱ ne̱ ku du fe̱yɛ, Wuribware̱ a twe̱e̱ kaye̱ mɔ ŋke nsiye‑ro‑o, kukyure ne̱ oo kyure ke̱mo̱ kake sunoose̱po̱‑ɔ.

ref: On that day God's people will rest from their work, just as God rested from his work.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ yɔ Wuribware̱ a kyurekpa‑ɔ i kyure a ɔ le̱e̱ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ kusuŋ si, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kyure mò̱ lee si.

ref: We should do our best to enter the place of rest, so none of us will disobey and miss going there, as they did.
text: Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ le̱ŋ e̱ye̱e̱ keŋkeŋ, na ane̱ nya taare̱ yɔ Wuribware̱ a kyurekpa amo̱. Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ ɔko̱ ma kaŋ kine Wuribware̱ se̱ ke̱gya, na se̱sɛ‑ɔ fo̱, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ nana‑ana amo̱ a waa ke̱se̱bɔrɔle̱ŋ fo̱‑ɔ.

ref: God's word is alive and powerful! It is sharper than any double-edged sword. His word can cut through our spirits and souls and through our joints and marrow, until it discovers the desires and thoughts of our hearts.
text: Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ amo̱‑rɔ de ke̱yaale̱ŋ. Ne̱ amo̱ ase̱ŋ i lwee se̱sɛ‑rɔ a kyo̱ŋ ane̱ŋ ne̱ gyimbee̱nyɔ se̱kaŋ i furi lwee kiyeegyi‑ro. Abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ e̱ taare̱ a o lwee se̱sɛ mfɛɛre̱‑rɔ yɔ mò̱ kayo̱wɔre̱‑rɔ na abowii‑ro waa kusuŋ, sa a se̱sɛ amerose̱ŋ na kudugyi le̱e̱ ifuri.

ref: Nothing is hidden from God! He sees through everything, and we will have to tell him the truth. Jesus Is the Great High Priest
text: Ko̱tɔko̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a twe̱e̱, ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ ke̱e̱ ke̱mo̱‑rɔ. O ŋu ke̱tɔ ke̱maa, atɔ pɛɛɛ da ifuri si mò̱ ase̱ ne̱e̱. Mò̱‑ɔ mɔ akatɔ‑rɔ ne̱ ŋke ŋko̱ ane̱ pɛɛɛ i lee ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ane̱ a waa‑ɔ kanɔ. Yeesuu gye̱ Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ dabe̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱

ref: We have a great high priest, who has gone into heaven, and he is Jesus the Son of God. This is why we must hold on to what we have said about him.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ kra ane̱ ke̱bware̱suŋ‑o‑ro keŋkeŋ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ de Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ dabe̱ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi Yeesuu, ne̱ o yii so̱so̱ ɔ bo̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ.

ref: So whenever we are in need, we should come bravely before the throne of our merciful God. There we will be treated with undeserved grace, and we will find help.
text: Bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu e̱ gye̱ ane̱ŋ a ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ mɔ‑ɔ se̱, kufu mo̱ŋ de ane̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ yɔ Nyiŋkpe̱ŋ-e̱wɛɛŋupo̱ ase̱. Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ yɔ mò̱ ase̱, na o ŋu ane̱ e̱wɛɛ, kya ane̱‑rɔ, sa ane̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ki tiri ane̱‑ɔ.

ref: Every high priest is appointed to help others by offering gifts and sacrifices to God because of their sins.
text: Amaa Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ ke̱maa ne̱ ba de̱e̱re̱ anyiŋkpasɛ‑rɔ lee‑o fe̱raa, ba lee mò̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ ye̱re̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ ayaa‑rɔ taa bamo̱ aketɔ na mbo̱ ne̱ ba baa‑ɔ sa Wuribware̱ bo̱ lɔŋŋɔ e̱bɔye̱ ke̱maa.

ref: A high priest has weaknesses of his own, and he feels sorry for foolish and sinful people.
text: Ɔ taare̱ o nyite bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi sɛye̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa fo̱ kpa‑ɔ, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ gbaa, n gye̱ e̱kpa ke̱maa‑rɔ ne̱ ɔ waa atɔ ke̱bware̱suŋ‑no.

ref: This is why he must offer sacrifices for his own sins and for the sins of others.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱ ne̱, mò̱ e̱ lɔŋŋɔ Wuribware̱, a tiri fe̱yɛ ɔ lɔŋŋɔ e̱bɔye̱ ko̱lɔŋŋɔ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ kuŋu si, na mò̱ gbaa mò̱ lee‑o si bo̱ tii si.

ref: But no one can have the honor of being a high priest simply by wanting to be one. Only God can choose a priest, and God is the one who chose Aaron.
text: Ɔke̱maa mo̱ŋ de kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ ye̱re̱ ane̱ŋ a ye̱re̱kpa dabe̱ amo̱ waa Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ se̱. Wuribware̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ o lee se̱sɛ fe̱yɛ ɔ bo̱ kii mò̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ oo lee Aarɔŋ‑ɔ dɛɛ.

ref: This is how it was with Christ. He became a high priest, but not just because he wanted the honor of being one. It was God who told him, “You are my Son, because today I have become your Father!”
text: Kristoo gbaa ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱e̱. N gye̱ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ya da mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ se̱ ye̱re̱ Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ ye̱re̱kpa dabe̱ amo̱. Wuribware̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ sa mò̱. Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ mò̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, “Ndɔɔ mo̱ a lee fo̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ gyi e̱ gye̱ fo̱. Ne̱ mo̱‑ɔ, fo̱ se̱ e̱ gye̱ mo̱.”

ref: In another place, God says, “You are a priest forever just like Melchizedek.”
text: Ne̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ to̱ŋ ko̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ i kii Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ nsu pɛɛɛ, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Marikii-Sadukii a kii‑o dɛɛ.”

ref: God had the power to save Jesus from death. And while Jesus was on earth, he begged God with loud crying and tears to save him. He truly worshiped God, and God listened to his prayers.
text: Ke̱be̱e̱ ne̱ Yeesuu te kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, akyukyu ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ ko̱re̱ Wuribware̱ ne̱ o nyi fe̱yɛ ɔ taare̱ a o lee mò̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro‑o. Ne̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ oo bu Wuribware̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ mɔ a nu mò̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱‑ɔ.

ref: Jesus is God's own Son, but still he had to suffer before he could learn what it really means to obey God.
text: Amaa hare̱e̱ Yeesuu a gye̱ Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑o gbaa ooo, ɔ mo̱ŋ kwaye̱ se̱sɛ dimaadi a awo̱re̱fɔɔ‑ɔ. Kase̱ŋtiŋ si, mò̱ a gye̱ se̱sɛ dimaadi‑o si‑o, mò̱ awo̱re̱fɔɔ gyisɛ‑ɔ‑rɔ ne̱ oo suye kibu Wuribware̱ kpa ke̱maa se̱.

ref: This is because God chose him to be a high priest like Melchizedek. Warning against Turning Away
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ mɔ a lee mò̱ fe̱yɛ o gyi Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ ke̱bre̱sɛ fe̱yɛ Marikii-Sadukii‑o dɛɛ. Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ Kristoo awuye a ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi‑o tii si

ref: Much more could be said about this subject. But it is hard to explain, and all of you are slow to understand.
text: Ane̱ de atɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ na ane̱ tɔwe̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ ase̱ŋ mɔ se̱, amaa ane̱ e̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱‑rɔ, a bo̱ le̱ŋ sa mo̱ne̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ maa nu atɔ ke̱kaapo̱ kaase̱ me̱naŋ.

ref: By now you should have been teachers, but once again you need to be taught the simplest things about what God has said. You need milk instead of solid food.
text: Mo̱ne̱ a kii Yeesuu akɔɔre̱gyipo̱ kyee‑o si‑o, weetee mo̱ne̱ a kyɔ kii abware̱se̱ŋ akaapo̱po̱. Amaa a saŋ a tiri fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ nya ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ abware̱se̱ŋ‑nɔ atɔ gye̱ŋkpɛɛsɛ‑ɔ. Mo̱ne̱ ma kpa ateese, amɔ ke̱nyapo̱.

ref: People who live on milk are like babies who don't really know what is right.
text: Ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ taa ke̱nyapo̱ bo̱ kii mò̱ ateese‑o saŋ ɔ gye̱ keegyi ne̱e̱. Ne̱ se̱sɛ amo̱ be̱e̱ ɔ maa nu itimaa ase̱ŋ ke̱kaapo̱‑ɔ kaase̱.

ref: Solid food is for mature people who have been trained to know right from wrong.
text: Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa daŋ‑ɔ i gyi ateese‑o, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa daŋ bamo̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi‑ro‑o a suye kyee kipini ke̱tɔ bɔye̱ na kitimaa.

ref: We must try to become mature and start thinking about more than just the basic things we were taught about Christ. We shouldn't need to keep talking about why we ought to turn from deeds that bring death and why we ought to have faith in God.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, ane̱ yɔwe̱ Kristoo ase̱ŋ ke̱kaapo̱ ngyingyii ngyingyii‑o bo̱ sii kamɛɛ, na mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ de̱e̱ nu abre̱sɛtɔ ke̱kaapo̱. Atɔ mɔ a gye̱ atimaa gbaa ooo, a mo̱ŋ tiri fe̱yɛ ane̱ be̱e̱ suye amo̱ iluwi e̱nyɔ e̱nyɔ. Amo̱ ne̱ n de‑o e̱ gye̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ a kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ nu ane̱ e̱ye̱e̱, na ane̱ kiŋŋi le̱e̱ ke̱bo̱rɔgyi ne̱ ke̱ e̱ baa ke̱se̱bɔgyiiri nsu pɛɛɛ‑ɔ, na ane̱ kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ gyi‑o.

ref: And we shouldn't need to keep teaching about baptisms or about the laying on of hands or about people being raised from death and the future judgment.
text: A mo̱ŋ lɛɛ a tiri fe̱yɛ ane̱ de̱e̱ kaapo̱ asuu ke̱bɔ ase̱ŋ kpo̱ra‑rɔ, bɛɛɛ asare̱e̱ ke̱bo̱dɔŋŋɔ ɔko̱ kuŋu si yure mò̱ ase̱ŋ, bɛɛɛ kikyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro ase̱ŋ, bɛɛɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ i gyi ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ ase̱ŋ, bɛɛɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ bamo̱ kakɔka i gyi nsu pɛɛɛ‑ɔ ase̱ŋ.

ref: A field is useful to farmers, if there is enough rain to make good crops grow. In fact, God will bless such a field.
text: Swe̱e̱re̱ ya nuu mbware̱kyu ne̱ ŋ we̱e̱ mò̱ se̱ saŋ ke̱maa‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ adɔɔteese a baare sa adɔɔpo̱‑ɔ, a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a yure swe̱e̱re̱ amo̱.

ref: But land that produces only thornbushes is worthless. It is likely to fall under God's curse, and in the end it will be set on fire.
text: Amaa mò̱ ya kwɛɛ e̱fa na iwu fe̱raa, ɔ mo̱ŋ de to̱nɔ. Ne̱ a bware fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ lo̱ŋ mò̱. Na lalalo̱we̱‑ɔ, de̱e̱kpa bo̱ kywɛɛ e̱fa na iwu amo̱.

ref: God is always fair. He will remember how you helped his people in the past and how you are still helping them. You belong to God, and he won't forget the love you have shown his people.
text: Wuribware̱ a de kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o si‑o, ɔ maa taŋ mo̱ne̱ se̱. O nyi ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a suŋ sa mò̱‑ɔ na ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a kpa mò̱‑ɔ se̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a kya mo̱ne̱ abɛɛko̱ Kristoo awuye‑ro, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ saŋ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kya bamo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱ ne̱ ɔɔ ye̱re̱ bo̱ ŋu ane̱ŋ a ke̱kpa‑ɔ.

ref: We wish each of you would always be eager to show how strong and lasting your hope really is.
text: Ane̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ kya se̱ pee akatɔ gya Wuribware̱ a e̱kpa‑ɔ se̱ ya bo̱ fo̱ saŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ nya so̱so̱ a kye̱nakpa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ te̱maa dɔŋ se̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱ nya‑ɔ.

ref: Then you would never be lazy. You would be following the example of those who had faith and were patient until God kept his promise to them. God's Promise Is Sure
text: Mo̱ne̱ ma waa wo̱rɔgya. Mo̱nꞌ ko̱te̱ anyitepo̱ ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ gyi‑o, ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ baa nya ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ ɔ sa bamo̱‑ɔ. Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ kra ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ ɔ sa ane̱‑ɔ‑rɔ keŋkeŋ

ref: Then after Abraham had been very patient, he was given what God had promised.
text: Abraham a waa kanyite‑o si‑o, ɔɔ nya ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ ɔ sa mò̱‑ɔ.

ref: When anyone wants to settle an argument, they make a vow by using the name of someone or something greater than themselves.
text: Ase̱sɛ e̱ kpa a bo̱ waa e̱taŋ, ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ kyɔ bamo̱‑ɔ ke̱nyare̱ ne̱ ba ba a bo̱ bo̱ waa e̱taŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ e̱e̱ sa ne̱ ikii ke̱maa ne̱ e̱ bo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ e̱ lo̱we̱.

ref: So when God wanted to prove for certain his promise to his people could not be broken, he made a vow.
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kpa a ɔ kaapo̱ bamo̱ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ ɔ sa bamo̱ ko̱tɔko̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ ɔ maa kyurowi mò̱ kanɔ‑ɔ, ɔɔ waa amo̱ se̱ e̱taŋ, na ikii pɛɛɛ lo̱we̱.

ref: God cannot tell lies! And so his promises and vows are two things that can never be changed. We have run to God for safety. Now his promises should greatly encourage us to take hold of the hope that is right in front of us.
text: Atɔ anyɔ mɔ, abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ na mò̱ e̱taŋ‑ɔ, atɔ ne̱ a maa kyurowi‑o, e̱ kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ maa taare̱ a ɔ ba kaye̱ba. Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ a sa ane̱ wɔre̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ e̱ se̱re̱ e̱naŋ ane̱ e̱ le̱e̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ane̱ e̱ kpa a ane̱ nya ŋyure ne̱ n da ane̱ akatɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: This hope is like a firm and steady anchor for our souls. In fact, hope reaches behind the curtain and into the most holy place.
text: Ŋyure mɔ ne̱ m maa paŋ ane̱‑ɔ te̱maa se̱ ne̱ ane̱ ye̱re̱ keŋkeŋ, ne̱ n sa ane̱ kameroyuri. Ane̱ a bo̱rɔ kyo̱ŋ waagya kuŋsɛ‑ɔ ya lwee suŋkpa do̱dɔ‑rɔ a ke̱kyaŋ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ ane̱ a nya ane̱ŋ a ŋyure‑o.

ref: Melchizedek was both king of Salem and priest of God Most High. He was the one who went out and gave Abraham his blessing, when Abraham returned from killing the kings.
text: Ane̱ a kare̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ de̱daa‑ɔ owure ko̱ e̱ dɛɛ ɔ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ Marikii-Sadukii. Ɔ gye̱ Salɛm maŋ owure ne̱e̱. Ne̱ ɔ be̱e̱ ɔ gye̱ Nyiŋkpe̱ŋ-kyo̱ŋ-atɔ‑pɛɛɛ-Bware̱‑ɔ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱. Amɔ saŋ amo̱ ane̱ nana Abraham a ya kɔ ke̱naa gya awure ana ko̱. Mò̱ a yɔ kiŋŋi ɔ ba‑ɔ, ne̱ Marikii-Sadukii a le̱e̱ bo̱ gyaŋŋaa mò̱ yure mò̱.

ref: Then Abraham gave him a tenth of everything he had. The meaning of the name Melchizedek is “King of Justice.” But since Salem means “peace,” he is also “King of Peace.”
text: Ne̱ Abraham mɔ a ke atɔ pɛɛɛ ne̱ ɔɔ kɔɔre̱ le̱e̱ ke̱naa‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ kudu kudu, ne̱ ɔɔ taa ke̱ke̱maa‑rɔ kare̱ ko̱ŋko̱ sa Marikii-Sadukii. To, ke̱nyare̱ Marikii-Sadukii‑o kaase̱ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ “Mo̱‑owure‑gye̱-se̱sɛ-timaa.” Maŋ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ mò̱ se̱ owure‑o mɔ ke̱nyare̱ e̱ gye̱ Salɛm. Ke̱nyare̱ amo̱ kaase̱ e̱ gye̱ “E̱ye̱e̱‑a‑yuri.” Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mò̱ kuwure ke̱nyare̱ e̱ gye̱ “Nana E̱ye̱e̱‑a‑yuri.”

ref: We are not told he had a father or mother or ancestors or beginning or end. He is like the Son of God and will be a priest forever.
text: Abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ ane̱ mo̱ŋ kare̱ Marikii-Sadukii mò̱ se̱ bɛɛɛ mò̱ nyi bɛɛɛ mò̱ ananagyi bɛɛɛ mò̱ ko̱ko̱we̱ bɛɛɛ mò̱ lowi ase̱ŋ. O du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑o, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ ke̱bre̱sɛ kigyi‑o mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ.

ref: Notice how great Melchizedek was! Our famous ancestor Abraham gave him a tenth of what he had taken from his enemies.
text: Ne̱ ŋkee mo̱ne̱ a ŋu ane̱ŋ ne̱ Marikii-Sadukii amo̱ gye̱ se̱sɛ dabe̱‑ɔ ɔɔɔ? A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Abraham ne̱ ɔ gye̱ ane̱ nana, ne̱ mò̱ ke̱nyare̱ bo̱ dwii‑o a sa mò̱ ke̱naa‑ɔ‑rɔ atɔ ntuŋkare̱ kudu‑ro katuŋkare̱ ko̱ŋko̱.

ref: The Law teaches that even Abraham's descendants must give a tenth of what they possess. And they are to give this to their own relatives, who are the descendants of Levi and are priests.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ ane̱ Isireelii awuye a nsuro‑o, kamo̱ ne̱ ba lee Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ ba le̱e̱ kamo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ Lewii a kasuro‑o. Ne̱ Wuribware̱ mbraa da fe̱yɛ Lewii a kasuro awuye amo̱ kɔɔre̱ bamo̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana Isireelii awuye ase̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ntuŋkare̱ kudu‑ro katuŋkare̱ ko̱ŋko̱, na bo̱ nya nya bamo̱ kusuŋ si ateese na nkyu, le̱e̱ nsuro pɛɛɛ awuye ne̱ bamo̱ nana gbaa mɔ gye̱ Abraham‑ɔ.

ref: Although Melchizedek wasn't a descendant of Levi, Abraham gave him a tenth of what he had. Then Melchizedek blessed Abraham, who had been given God's promise.
text: Amaa ɔnyare̱ mɔ fe̱raa mo̱ŋ le̱e̱ Lewii a kasuro‑o‑ro. Amaa mò̱‑ɔ mɔ a kɔɔre̱ atɔ ntuŋkare̱ kudu‑ro katuŋkare̱ ko̱ŋko̱ le̱e̱ Abraham ase̱ yure mò̱ ne̱ weetee Wuribware̱ a waa e̱taŋ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ bo̱ be̱ya sa mò̱‑ɔ.

ref: Everyone agrees a person who gives a blessing is greater than the one who receives the blessing.
text: Ikii kyɔ e̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ, ɔko̱ ne̱ o yure‑o kyɔ ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ kɔɔre̱ ŋyure amo̱‑ɔ ke̱bre̱sɛ.

ref: Even though the Law of Moses says the priests must be descendants of Levi, those priests cannot make anyone perfect. So there needs to be a priest like Melchizedek, rather than one from the priestly family of Aaron.
text: To, saŋ‑ɔ ane̱ Isireelii awuye a kɔɔre̱ gyi fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ Lewii a kasuro awuye‑o e̱ taare̱ a bo̱ sa a ane̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ fwiiri Wuribware̱ ase̱. Amaa ane̱ a pe̱nna ane̱ e̱ye̱e̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ yɛ a tiri fe̱yɛ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ baŋbaŋ ba. Ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ baŋbaŋ amo̱ maa le̱e̱ Lewii a kasuro‑o‑ro. Mò̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ ke̱bre̱sɛ mo̱ŋ le̱e̱ Aarɔŋ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ gye̱ŋkpɛɛsɛ‑ɔ lee‑o, amaa ku du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Marikii-Sadukii lee‑o.

ref: And when the rules for selecting a priest are changed, the Law must also be changed.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ ya lee se̱sɛ baŋbaŋ fe̱yɛ ɔ bo̱ kii ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ, amo̱ fe̱raa ɔ be̱e̱ o kyurowi ko̱lɔŋŋɔ kusuŋ na ke̱mo̱ mbraa‑ɔ.

ref: The person we are talking about is our Lord, who came from a tribe that had never had anyone to serve as a priest at the altar.
text: Ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ baŋbaŋ amo̱ ne̱ ane̱ a kare̱ mò̱ kuŋu si ase̱ŋ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ e̱ gye̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu. Ne̱ mò̱‑ɔ mɔ a le̱e̱ kasuro ne̱ kamo̱‑rɔ ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ kii ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ daa‑ɔ‑rɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: Everyone knows he came from the tribe of Judah, and Moses never said priests would come from that tribe.
text: Ɔke̱maa mɔ nyi fe̱yɛ Yeesuu a le̱e̱ Yudaa a kasuro‑o‑ro ne̱e̱. Amaa Mosis a kyo̱rɛɛ Mbraa a wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ Yudaa a kasuro‑o awuye mo̱ŋ de kpa fe̱yɛ bo̱ kii ane̱ŋ a alɔŋŋɔpo̱‑ɔ, amɔ Lewii awuye dooo.

ref: The Scriptures say about him, “You are a priest forever, just like Melchizedek.”
text: Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, “Fo̱ i kii Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ nsu pɛɛɛ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Marikii-Sadukii a kii‑o dɛɛ.”

ref: In this way a weak and useless command was put aside,
text: Mfe̱e̱ fe̱raa Wuribware̱ a yɔwe̱ ko̱lɔŋŋɔ mbraa de̱daa‑ɔ bo̱ be̱ya keri si. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ m mo̱ŋ lɔŋŋɔ sɛye̱.

ref: because the Law cannot make anything perfect. At the same time, we are given a much better hope, and it can bring us close to God.
text: Kase̱ŋtiŋ si fe̱raa, mbraa amo̱ maa taare̱ a n sa a ɔko̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ fwiiri lo̱we̱. Mbe̱yɔmɔ Wuribware̱ a sa ane̱ te̱maa timaa ne̱ ane̱ e̱ ba a ane̱ bo̱ sɔŋ tɔ mò̱‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: God himself made a promise when this priest was appointed. But he did not make a promise like this when the other priests were appointed. The promise he made is, “I, the Lord, promise that you will be a priest forever! And I will never change my mind!”
text: Amaa e̱taŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a waa bo̱ taa Yeesuu bo̱ kii ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱. Ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ma kwaye̱ mo̱ e̱taŋ ne̱ mo̱ a waa‑ɔ, ma kyurowi mo̱ kanɔ ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ, fe̱yɛ fo̱ i kii mo̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ nsu pɛɛɛ.”

ref: He is forever able to save the people he leads to God, because he always lives to speak to God for them.
text: Ɔ taare̱ a ɔ mo̱rɔwe̱ bamo̱ ne̱ ba bo̱rɔ mò̱ se̱ ba ba Wuribware̱ ase̱‑ɔ, na bo̱ ma lɛɛ fo̱. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ o te saŋ ke̱maa ɔ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ ɔ sa bamo̱.

ref: Jesus is the high priest we need. He is holy and innocent and faultless, and not at all like us sinners. Jesus is honored above all beings in heaven,
text: Ane̱ŋ a ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ o bware bo̱ sa ane̱. Mò̱ a bware‑o si‑o, mò̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ a fwiiri. Ɔko̱ maa taare̱ a o ŋu mò̱ bɔye̱ ko̱. Ne̱ ɔ kwe̱e̱ ane̱ ne̱ ane̱ gye̱ e̱bɔye̱ awaapo̱‑ɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ o yii so̱so̱ so̱so̱ Wuribware̱ se̱.

ref: and he is better than any other high priest. Jesus doesn't need to offer sacrifices each day for his own sins and then for the sins of the people. He offered a sacrifice once for all, when he gave himself.
text: Ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ ke̱maa ne̱ ɔ gye̱ Lewiinyi‑o e̱ gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ a ɔ lɔŋŋɔ Wuribware̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ e̱bɔye̱‑ɔ se̱ pwɛɛ na ɔ dɛɛ be̱e̱ lɔŋŋɔ Wuribware̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ mò̱ ase̱sɛ mɔ lee‑o si. Yeesuu fe̱raa, ɔ mo̱ŋ du ane̱ŋ. Mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ sa Wuribware̱ luwi ko̱ŋko̱, na Wuribware̱ lee ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ e̱bɔye̱, na a lo̱we̱.

ref: What I mean is we have a high priest who sits at the right side of God's great throne in heaven.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ, amo̱ katiŋ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ɔko̱ ne̱ ane̱ kyɔ ane̱ de‑o. Mò̱ e̱ gye̱ Yeesuu. Mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ o te Nyiŋkpe̱ŋ‑ɔ‑kyɔ-bamo̱-pɛɛɛ-ke̱dabe̱ kigyise si so̱so̱ a kuwure‑o si‑o.

ref: He also serves as the priest in the most holy place inside the real tent there in heaven. This tent of worship was set up by the Lord, not by humans.
text: Ɔ gye̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ‑rɔ ne̱e̱. Mfe̱ŋ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa ne̱ Mosis a ba taŋte̱e̱ ke̱kyaŋ bo̱ waa, na bo̱ taare̱ so̱rɔ nare̱ kimukee‑ro‑o ne̱e̱. Amaa Wuribware̱ suŋkpa ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ suŋkpa kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu a pwɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: Since all priests must offer gifts and sacrifices, Christ also needed to have something to offer.
text: Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ ke̱maa kusuŋ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ ba ateese na mbo̱ bo̱ lɔŋŋɔ Wuribware̱. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ a tiri fe̱yɛ ane̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ‑ɔ gbaa mɔ ba ko̱tɔko̱ bo̱ lɔŋŋɔ Wuribware̱.

ref: If he were here on earth, he would not be a priest at all, because here the Law appoints other priests to offer sacrifices.
text: Ɔ saŋ ɔ dɛɛ ɔ bo̱ swe̱e̱re̱ mɔ se̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ ne̱e̱, weetee ɔ maa waa ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ saŋ bo̱ bo̱‑rɔ ba lɔŋŋɔ Wuribware̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mbraa‑ɔ e̱ kaapo̱‑ɔ.

ref: But the tent where they serve is just a copy and a shadow of the real one in heaven. Before Moses made the tent, he was told, “Be sure to make it exactly like the pattern you were shown on the mountain!”
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ ba de̱e̱re̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ba suŋ Wuribware̱ mò̱ suŋkpa ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱ ne̱e̱ ba suŋ mò̱ mfe̱e̱ a mmo̱‑ɔ‑rɔ. Amo̱se̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a yii Mosis si fe̱yɛ ɔ sa se̱ na ɔ ba taŋte̱e̱ ke̱kyaŋ‑ɔ bo̱ waa Wuribware̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ kaapo̱ mò̱ Sinaa ke̱be̱e̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ.

ref: If the first agreement with God had been all right, there would not have been any need for another one.
text: E̱taŋ gye̱ŋkpɛɛsɛ amo̱ a dɛɛ baare lo̱we̱ ne̱e̱, weetee a mo̱ŋ lɛɛ a tiri fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ be̱e̱ ba e̱po̱pwɛɛ ko̱ bo̱ teere.

ref: But the Lord found fault with it and said, “I tell you the time will come, when I will make a new agreement with the people of Israel and the people of Judah.
text: Amaa ane̱ a kare̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a ŋu mò̱ ase̱sɛ ke̱bɔye̱ ko̱, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ yɛ, “Saŋ ko̱ e̱ ba ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa e̱taŋ po̱pwɛɛ na m bo̱ be̱ya mo̱ aa Isireelii a kasuro‑o awuye na Yudaa a kasuro‑o awuye mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ.

ref: It won't be like the agreement that I made with their ancestors, when I took them by the hand and led them out of Egypt. They broke their agreement with me, and I stopped caring about them!
text: E̱taŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa‑ɔ mo̱ŋ du fe̱yɛ e̱taŋ ne̱ mo̱ a waa sa bamo̱ nana‑ana, saŋ ne̱ mo̱ a kra bamo̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ lee bamo̱ le̱e̱ Igyipiti swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ. Baa yɔwe̱ e̱taŋ amo̱ ke̱gyase̱, amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ mo̱ a yɔwe̱ bamo̱ ase̱ŋ sa bamo̱.

ref: “But now I tell the people of Israel this is my new agreement: ‘The time will come when I, the Lord, will write my laws on their minds and hearts. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
text: Saŋ amo̱ ya fo̱, e̱taŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa na m bo̱ be̱ya mo̱ aa Isireelii awuye pɛɛɛ mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, mfaanɛɛ ne̱ i du. Mo̱ e̱ taa mo̱ mbraa a m bo̱ waa bamo̱ mfɛɛre̱‑rɔ, na n kyo̱rɛɛ mmo̱ bo̱ waa bamo̱ aŋu‑ro, na ŋ kii bamo̱ nyaŋpe̱, na bo̱ kii mo̱ ase̱sɛ.

ref: Not one of them will have to teach another to know me, their Lord.’ “All of them will know me, no matter who they are.
text: A maa lɛɛ ba fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ be̱e̱ ɔ lɛɛ kaapo̱ mò̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ bɛɛɛ mò̱ ko̱we̱bɛɛ fe̱yɛ, ‘Kpa mɔ se̱ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ bo̱rɔ na fo̱ gyii Wuribware̱,’ a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ, abre̱sɛ ooo, ŋyaagyi ooo i gyii mo̱.

ref: I will treat them with kindness, even though they are wicked. I will forget their sins.”
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ taa bamo̱ e̱bɔye̱ a m bo̱ ke bamo̱, na m ma lɛɛ nyiŋŋi bamo̱ ase̱ŋ bɔye̱‑ɔ se̱.”

ref: When the Lord talks about a new agreement, he means that the first one is out of date. And anything that is old and useless will soon disappear.
text: Wuribware̱ a tɛɛ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ waa e̱taŋ po̱pwɛɛ‑ɔ, amo̱ fe̱raa a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ yɛ e̱gye̱ŋkpɛɛsɛ‑ɔ a waa e̱de̱daa, ne̱ e̱ ya wu. Ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ kaa waa ke̱de̱daa, ne̱ kaa wu‑o mɔ maa kyee, na ke̱ fo̱ pɛɛɛ.

ref: The first promise included rules for worship and a tent for worship here on earth.
text: Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ de̱e̱re̱ e̱taŋ de̱daa a ke̱bware̱suŋ a mbraa‑ɔ. Saŋ amo̱‑ɔ se̱sɛ ya waa Wuribware̱ suŋkpa ne̱ bo̱ de‑o.

ref: The gold altar for burning incense was in this holy place. The gold-covered sacred chest was also there, and inside it were three things. First, there was a gold jar filled with manna. Then there was Aaron's walking stick that sprouted. Finally, there were the flat stones with the Ten Commandments written on them.
text: Ke̱mo̱‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ lɔŋŋɔkpa ne̱ baa ba atanne̱ pipee bo̱ me̱ra me̱ra se̱‑ɔ ne̱ ba kywɛɛ nyoŋku mò̱ se̱‑ɔ bo̱. Wuribware̱ e̱taŋ a ke̱dakaa‑ɔ ye̱re̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱e̱, ne̱ baa ba atanne̱ pipee bo̱ me̱ra me̱ra ke̱mo̱ se̱. Ne̱ atanne̱ pipee kalɔ ko̱ ne̱ ka de gyaye̱‑ɔ ye̱re̱ ke̱dakaa‑ɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ ateese ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ fe̱yɛ “Nte̱tɔ‑ne̱e̱?” bo̱ kalɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ. Ateese amo̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a sa bamo̱ le̱e̱ so̱so̱ kake ke̱maa kimukee‑ro. Ke̱dakaa amo̱‑rɔ dɛɛ ne̱ Aarɔŋ yii nare̱se̱sɛ ne̱ ɔɔ kwɛɛ‑ɔ daa‑rɔ. Ke̱dakaa amo̱‑rɔ dɛɛ ne̱ afo̱re̱ anyɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ mbraa kudu‑o bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ se̱ Sinaa ke̱be̱e̱‑ɔ se̱ kimukee‑o‑ro mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ bo̱.

ref: On top of the chest were the glorious creatures with wings opened out above the place of mercy. Now isn't the time to go into detail about these things.
text: Ne̱ abaŋbaŋ awuye ko̱ ne̱ baa ba atanne̱ pipee bo̱ waa‑ɔ ye̱re̱ ke̱dakaa amo̱ aŋu anyɔ. Ne̱ baa ba bamo̱ abaŋbaŋ bo̱ buŋ Nyiŋkpe̱ŋ-e̱wɛɛŋupo̱ a ko̱to̱re̱se̱‑ɔ se̱, na o te mò̱ kuwuregya‑ɔ se̱ mfe̱ŋ. Amaa ane̱ mo̱ŋ de saŋ na ane̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ atɔ mɔ‑rɔ ako̱ ako̱.

ref: But this is how everything was when the priests went each day into the first part of the tent to do their duties.
text: Bamo̱ a lɔŋŋɔ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa bo̱ ye̱re̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ e̱ taare̱ na bo̱ de̱e̱ nya lwee kanɔ se̱ a ke̱kyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ kake ke̱maa waa bamo̱ asuŋ.

ref: However, only the high priest could go into the second part of the tent, and he went in only once a year. Each time he carried blood to offer for his sins and for any sins that the people had committed without meaning to.
text: Amaa do̱dɔ‑rɔ a ke̱kyaŋ‑ɔ fe̱raa, Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ‑ɔ wo̱re̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ o de kpa fe̱yɛ o lwee ke̱mo̱‑rɔ, ne̱ o lwee ke̱mo̱‑rɔ luwi ko̱ŋko̱ kasu ke̱maa, ne̱ o de mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ kuŋu si e̱bɔye̱ ko̱lɔŋŋɔ kabo̱ a mbo̱gya‑ɔ o lweero mfe̱ŋ. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ɔ waa ase̱sɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ kuŋu si a e̱bɔye̱ ne̱ baa waa na bo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi‑o.

ref: All of this is the Holy Spirit's way of saying no one could enter the most holy place while the tent was still the place of worship.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ, Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o de amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ ɔ kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kpa mo̱ŋ tɛɛ di bo̱ sa ase̱sɛ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ lwee ke̱kyaŋ kpe̱yaa kpe̱yaa‑ɔ‑rɔ, na taŋte̱e̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ saŋ ɔ ye̱re̱.

ref: These rules are merely about such things as eating and drinking and ceremonies for washing ourselves. And rules about physical things will last only until the time comes to change them for something better.
text: De̱daa‑rɔ a Wuribware̱ ko̱lɔŋŋɔ mɔ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ gye̱ kigyi na kunuu na ke̱fwe̱e̱ na kayo̱wɔre̱ se̱ atɔ ase̱ŋ ne̱e̱. Amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ bware bo̱ fo̱ saŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ waa mbraa po̱pwɛɛ‑ɔ dooo ne̱e̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Kristoo kusuŋ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ‑ɔ kwe̱e̱‑ɔ

ref: Christ came as the high priest of the good things that are now here. He also went into a much better tent that wasn't made by humans and that doesn't belong to this world.
text: Amaa mbraa po̱pwɛɛ timaa mɔ a kyɔ ba. Kristoo e̱ gye̱ mmo̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ. Mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ oo lwee taŋte̱e̱ ke̱kyaŋ ne̱ ke̱ bo̱ so̱so̱, ne̱ ke̱ bo̱ daŋ ko̱ kyo̱ŋ swe̱e̱re̱ mɔ se̱ a ke̱mo̱‑ɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ ke̱ bware ko̱ kyɔ ke̱mo̱ ne̱ ase̱sɛ a waa‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱ mo̱ŋ tii atɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a twe̱e̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱.

ref: According to the Law of Moses, those people who become unclean are not fit to worship God. Yet they will be considered clean, if they are sprinkled with the blood of goats and bulls and with the ashes of a sacrificed calf.
text: Mbraa de̱daa a mbe̱e̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, te̱e̱re̱ na kpe̱ŋnyare̱ mbo̱gya na kanaate̱bree ne̱ baa kywɛɛ‑ɔ nsɔ ne̱ ba bo̱ ŋo̱nyaŋ asuŋpo̱‑ɔ se̱ bo̱ kpɛɛ bamo̱ iyisi. Bamo̱ ya waa mfaanɛɛ‑ɔ, amɔ bamo̱ ŋyo̱wɔre̱ wo̱re̱ ne̱ baa lɔŋŋɔ.

ref: But Christ was sinless, and he offered himself as an eternal and spiritual sacrifice to God. This is why his blood is much more powerful and makes our consciences clear. Now we can serve the living God and no longer do things that lead to death.
text: Ke̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ amo̱‑ɔ e̱ waa‑ɔ, saŋbo̱to̱ Kristoo a mbo̱gya‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mmo̱ fe̱raa n taare̱ a ŋ kpɛɛ ane̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ lee ane̱ asuŋ bɔye̱ ne̱ a yaa ane̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ke̱se̱bɔgyiiri maa lo̱we̱‑ɔ, na ane̱ nya taare̱ bo̱ suŋ Wuribware̱ ne̱ mò̱ wo̱re̱ dooo i de ŋkpa‑ɔ. Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Kristoo, ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ de e̱bɔye̱‑ɔ, a bo̱rɔ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o, ne̱ mò̱ wo̱re̱ kpeŋ te nsu pɛɛɛ‑ɔ se̱ ne̱e̱, ba mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ lɔŋŋɔ Wuribware̱.

ref: Moses told the people all the Law said they must do. Then he used red wool and a hyssop plant to sprinkle the people and the book of the Law with the blood of bulls and goats and with water.
text: Saŋ ne̱ Mosis a kyo̱rɛɛ Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ pɛɛɛ waa wo̱re̱‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ kare̱ gywii ase̱sɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ taa ko̱kyo̱yaa kapesii na kyipee. Ne̱ ŋkee ɔɔ ba nnaate̱gyii na e̱te̱e̱re̱ mbo̱gya bo̱ waa nkyu‑ro, ne̱ amo̱ pɛɛɛ a lwee abɛɛ‑rɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ taa ko̱kyo̱yaa kapesii na kyipee bo̱ da mbo̱gya na nkyu‑o‑ro, ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ ŋo̱nyaŋ mbraa wo̱re̱‑ɔ na ase̱sɛ‑ɔ se̱.

ref: Moses also sprinkled blood on the tent and on everything else used in worship.
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ ba mbo̱gya‑ɔ bo̱ ŋo̱nyaŋ Wuribware̱ a taŋte̱e̱ ke̱kyaŋ‑ɔ na ate̱tɔmo̱ŋta pɛɛɛ ne̱ ba ba bo̱ bo̱ suŋ bamo̱ ke̱bware̱suŋ‑o si.

ref: The Law says that almost everything must be sprinkled with blood, and no sins can be forgiven unless blood is offered. Christ's Great Sacrifice
text: Kase̱ŋtiŋ si fe̱raa mbraa amo̱‑rɔ, mbo̱gya ne̱ bo̱ de ba lɔŋŋɔ atɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Ne̱ ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ kabo̱ mbo̱gya e̱ mo̱ŋ we̱e̱ swe̱e̱re̱, Wuribware̱ maa ba ɔko̱ e̱bɔye̱ a ɔ bo̱ ke mò̱.

ref: These things are only copies of what is in heaven, and so they had to be made holy by these ceremonies. But the real things in heaven must be made holy by something better.
text: N gye̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa ne̱ ɔ bo̱ swe̱e̱re̱ mɔ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ Kristoo a lweero. Amaa Wuribware̱ suŋkpa ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa de̱e̱re̱ se̱ bo̱ waa mò̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ bo̱ ye̱ra. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, atɔ ne̱ a bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ fe̱raa e̱ kpa ko̱lɔŋŋɔ ne̱ ko̱ kyo̱ŋ swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ lee‑o.

ref: This is why Christ did not go into a tent made by humans and was only a copy of the real one. Instead, he went into heaven and is now there with God to help us.
text: N gye̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa ne̱ baa de̱e̱re̱ so̱so̱ a amo̱‑ɔ se̱ waa swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ ne̱ Kristoo a lweero. So̱so̱‑ɔ fɔŋfɔŋ ne̱ oo lweero. Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ŋkee ɔ ye̱re̱ ane̱ ayaa‑rɔ ɔ sa ane̱ kanɔ se̱ se̱ŋsa o gywii Wuribware̱.

ref: Christ did not have to offer himself many times. He wasn't like a high priest who goes into the most holy place each year to offer the blood of an animal.
text: Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ‑ɔ e̱ taa kabo̱ mbo̱gya, ne̱ o lwee ke̱kyaŋ kpe̱yaa kpe̱yaa‑ɔ‑rɔ kasu kasu. Amaa Kristoo fe̱raa mo̱ŋ lweero ya ba mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ lɔŋŋɔ Wuribware̱ iluwi bwe̱e̱tɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ ko̱.

ref: If he had offered himself every year, he would have suffered many times since the creation of the world. But instead, near the end of time he offered himself once and for all, so he could be a sacrifice that does away with sin.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, amo̱ ya ba ane̱ŋ, amɔ a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔɔ de̱ŋ wu iluwi bwe̱e̱tɔ le̱e̱ saŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a twe̱e̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑ɔ ne̱e̱. Amaa ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ waa‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mbe̱yɔmɔ ne̱ kaye̱ e̱ kpa a ka fo̱ kɛɛ‑ɔ, luwi ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱ oo lee mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ifuri bo̱ lɔŋŋɔ Wuribware̱, na ɔ nya lee ase̱sɛ kuŋu si e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ twe̱e̱.

ref: The Law of Moses is like a shadow of the good things to come. This shadow isn't the good things themselves, because it cannot free people from sin by the sacrifices offered year after year.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ, mbraa ne̱ ŋ kaapo̱ Wuribware̱ ko̱lɔŋŋɔ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ e̱ kaapo̱ atɔ timaa ne̱ a ba a a bo̱ ba‑ɔ ne̱e̱. Mbraa‑ɔ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ diki‑o. Ne̱ ane̱ ya de̱e̱re̱, ane̱ i ŋu fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ e̱ ba kamɛɛ. Bamo̱ a lɔŋŋɔ Wuribware̱ ane̱ŋ‑aaa nsu si nsu si, na a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, a maa taare̱ a a fwiiri bamo̱ ne̱ ba ba na bo̱ bo̱ suŋ Wuribware̱‑ɔ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ.

ref: If there were worshipers who already have their sins washed away and their consciences made clear, there would not be any need to go on offering sacrifices.
text: Ane̱ŋ a ko̱lɔŋŋɔ‑ɔ dɛɛ ke̱ e̱ taare̱ a ke̱ sa a asuŋpo̱‑ɔ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ fwiiri ne̱e̱, weetee bamo̱ mfɛɛre̱ maa waa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ e̱bɔye̱ a sii bamo̱ se̱. Na Wuribware̱ ko̱lɔŋŋɔ lo̱we̱.

ref: But the blood of bulls and goats cannot take away sins. It only reminds people of their sins from one year to the next.
text: Akpe̱ŋnyare̱ na e̱te̱e̱re̱ mbo̱gya a tɛɛ a maa taare̱ a a lee e̱bɔye̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, kasu ke̱maa a ko̱lɔŋŋɔ‑ɔ i nyiŋŋi ase̱sɛ se̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ e̱bɔye̱ a sii bamo̱ se̱.

ref: Then Christ said, “And so, my God, I have come to do what you want, as the Scriptures say.”
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ, ‘O Wuribware̱, ke̱e̱ mo̱. Baa tɔwe̱ mo̱ ase̱ŋ fo̱ mbraa wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ. Mo̱ a ba a m bo̱ waa fo̱ ke̱pre̱.’ ”

ref: The Law teaches that offerings and sacrifices must be made because of sin. But why did Christ mention these things and say that God did not want them?
text: Mo̱nꞌ ŋu fe̱yɛ ɔɔ gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ tɔwe̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ maa kpa ayu mpu ko̱lɔŋŋɔ bɛɛɛ mbo̱ ko̱kywɛɛ ko̱lɔŋŋɔ bɛɛɛ e̱bɔye̱ ko̱lɔŋŋɔ. Hare̱e̱ mbraa‑ɔ a kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ waa amo̱ ooo, ɔɔ tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ.

ref: Well, it was to do away with offerings and sacrifices and to replace them. This is what he meant by saying to God, “I have come to do what you want.”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ bo̱ tii si fe̱yɛ, “O Wuribware̱, ke̱e̱ mo̱, mo̱ a ba a m bo̱ waa fo̱ ke̱pre̱.” Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔ kaapo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ sa a bo̱ yɔwe̱ ko̱lɔŋŋɔ de̱daa‑ɔ, na ɔ waa ke̱tɔ po̱pwɛɛ ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ waa‑ɔ.

ref: So we are made holy because Christ obeyed God and offered himself once for all.
text: Wuribware̱ ke̱pre̱ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ Yeesuu Kristoo a taa mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ lɔŋŋɔ Wuribware̱ luwi ko̱ŋko̱, ne̱ aa lo̱we̱, na a lee ane̱ kuŋu si e̱bɔye̱.

ref: The priests do their work each day, and they keep on offering sacrifices that can never take away sins.
text: Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ ke̱maa ye̱re̱ kake ke̱maa ne̱e̱ ɔ waa mò̱ ko̱lɔŋŋɔ kusuŋ. Na ɔ lɔŋŋɔ Wuribware̱ iluwi e̱nyɔ e̱nyɔ. Ane̱ŋ a ko̱lɔŋŋɔ‑ɔ maa taare̱ a ki lee e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ twe̱e̱ daa.

ref: But Christ offered himself as a sacrifice that is good forever. Now he is sitting at God's right side,
text: Amaa ane̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ Yeesuu mɔ fe̱raa, saŋ ne̱ ɔɔ lɔŋŋɔ Wuribware̱ luwi ko̱ŋko̱ amo̱ bo̱ lee e̱bɔye̱‑ɔ, ko̱lɔŋŋɔ ko̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ ke̱ be̱e̱ ki tiri bo̱ fo̱ ndɔɔ. Amo̱se̱ ne̱ ɔɔ ya kye̱na Wuribware̱ kigyisesare̱e̱ se̱.

ref: and he will stay there until his enemies are put under his power.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ o te na Wuribware̱ baa Yeesuu Kristoo ado̱ŋ pɛɛɛ mò̱ kuwure‑o kaase̱.

ref: By his one sacrifice he has forever set free from sin the people he brings to God.
text: Katiŋ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ a ba mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ lɔŋŋɔ Wuribware̱ luwi ko̱ŋko̱ mɔ bo̱ sa ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ ɔ maa se̱ ɔ waa bamo̱ akpe̱yaa‑ɔ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ a fwiiri.

ref: “When the time comes, I will make an agreement with them. I will write my laws on their minds and hearts.
text: “Saŋ amo̱ ya fo̱, e̱taŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ waa na m bo̱ be̱ya mo̱ aa bamo̱ mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, mfaanɛɛ ne̱ i du. Mo̱ e̱ taa mo̱ mbraa a m bo̱ waa bamo̱ mfɛɛre̱‑rɔ, na n kyo̱rɛɛ mmo̱ bo̱ waa bamo̱ aŋu‑ro.”

ref: Then I will forget about their sins and no longer remember their evil deeds.”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ kya se̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ma lɛɛ nyiŋŋi bamo̱ ase̱ŋ bɔye̱ na bo̱ro̱kɔɔne̱‑ɔ se̱.”

ref: When sins are forgiven, there is no more need to offer sacrifices. Encouragement and Warning
text: Wuribware̱ a tɛɛ taa bamo̱ e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ ke bamo̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ko̱lɔŋŋɔ ne̱ ki lee e̱bɔye̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ ki tiri. Mo̱nꞌ kra mo̱ne̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi‑o‑ro keŋkeŋ bo̱ yɔ

ref: We must hold tightly to the hope we say is ours. After all, we can trust the one who made the agreement with us.
text: Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ ye̱re̱ ke̱yaa ko̱ŋko̱. Mo̱ne̱ a bɔ asuu‑o, te̱maa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a nya fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ e̱ yɔ Wuribware̱ ase̱‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ kra mò̱‑rɔ keŋkeŋkeŋ, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a tɛɛ tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ ɔ sa ane̱ ko̱tɔko̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ɔ maa kyurowi mò̱ kanɔ.

ref: Some people have given up the habit of meeting for worship, but we must not do that. We should keep on encouraging each other, especially since you know that the day of the Lord's coming is getting closer.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ma sa a ane̱ yɔwe̱ ke̱gyaŋŋe̱ suŋ Wuribware̱, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ saŋ ke̱maa bo̱ko̱ e̱ waa‑ɔ. Amaa mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ de̱e̱ gyaŋŋe̱ sa abɛɛ wɔre̱. A tiri fe̱yɛ ane̱ de̱e̱re̱ se̱, na ane̱ pee akatɔ waa amo̱‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kake ne̱ Kristoo i kiŋŋi a ɔ ba‑ɔ a tɔ‑rɔ.

ref: They are God's enemies, and all they can look forward to is a terrible judgment and a furious fire.
text: Amo̱ ya ba ane̱ŋ fe̱raa, amɔ kufu kakye̱na ne̱ ane̱ e̱ kye̱na ane̱ gywii Wuribware̱, na ɔ bo̱ bu ane̱ ke̱pɔ, na ɔ yaa ane̱ mò̱ ke̱de̱e̱kpɛɛtekii ne̱ ɔ ba a ɔ bo̱ kywɛɛ mò̱ ado̱ŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: But it is much worse to dishonor God's Son and to disgrace the blood of the promise that made us holy. And it is just as bad to insult the Holy Spirit, who shows us mercy.
text: Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ twe̱e̱ ke̱yaa a ɔ bo̱ kye Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑o, ne̱ Wuribware̱ i gyiiri ke̱se̱bɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ mo̱ŋ de Yeesuu mbo̱gya ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ tii e̱taŋ‑ɔ kanɔ, ne̱ ŋ ya lee mò̱ iyisi‑o, ɔ te̱e̱ sɛye̱, ne̱ ɔ be̱e̱ ɔ saare̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa ne̱ ɔɔ dɛɛ ŋu mò̱ e̱wɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: We know that God has said he will punish and take revenge. We also know that the Scriptures say the Lord will judge his people.
text: Ane̱ nyi ane̱ŋ ne̱ o du‑o, Wuribware̱ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ ba do̱ŋdaa na m bo̱ kɔ sa mo̱ne̱. Mo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ ka mo̱ne̱ ado̱ŋ ko̱kɔ a n sa mo̱ne̱.” Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Wuribware̱ i gyi mò̱ ase̱sɛ ase̱ŋ.”

ref: It is a terrible thing to fall into the hands of the living God!
text: A bo̱ kufu fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ a lwee ɔse̱ŋgyipo̱ Wuribware̱ ne̱ mò̱ wo̱re̱ dooo i de ŋkpa‑ɔ ase̱ŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ ɔ nya mò̱ kra ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ, gyiiri mò̱ ke̱se̱bɔ.

ref: Sometimes you were abused and mistreated in public, and at other times you shared in the sufferings of others.
text: Saŋ ko̱ baa saare̱ mo̱ne̱, waa mo̱ne̱ awo̱re̱fɔɔ lamaŋ‑nɔ. Saŋ ko̱ mɔ mo̱ne̱ a kya bamo̱ ne̱ baa ŋu ase̱ŋ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: You were kind to people in jail. And you gladly let your possessions be taken away, because you knew you had something better, something that would last forever.
text: Ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a kya bamo̱ ne̱ baa tii‑o‑ro. Bamo̱ a kɔɔre̱ mo̱ne̱ kapo̱tɛɛ‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ sa ne̱ aa tɔɔraa mo̱ne̱, na mo̱nꞌ taa bo̱ waa ase̱ŋ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ nyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ de atɔ Wuribware̱ se̱ na a kyɔ amo̱, ne̱ a be̱e̱ a gyi nsu pɛɛɛ.

ref: Keep on being brave! It will bring you great rewards.
text: Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ ma sa a mo̱ne̱ abaa‑rɔ yɔre̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ wɔre̱ ko̱waa e̱ sa kakɔka dabe̱.

ref: Learn to be patient, so you will please God and be given what he has promised.
text: Mo̱nꞌ nya kanyite, na mo̱nꞌ nya waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ, na mo̱nꞌ nya nya ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ ɔ sa mo̱ne̱‑ɔ.

ref: As the Scriptures say, “God is coming soon! It won't be very long.
text: Fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ mò̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, “A saŋ kafwe̱e̱, ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ ba‑ɔ e̱ ba, ɔ maa kyee.

ref: The people God accepts will live because of their faith. But he isn't pleased with anyone who turns back.”
text: Ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ gyi‑o e̱ nya ŋkpa a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ e̱ te̱e̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ se̱sɛ timaa. Amaa mò̱ ya kiŋŋi kamɛɛ fe̱raa, mo̱ akatɔ maa gyi mò̱ se̱.”

ref: We are not like those people who turn back and get destroyed. We will keep on having faith until we are saved.
text: Amaa ane̱ aa mo̱ne̱ fe̱raa, ane̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ ba kiŋŋi kamɛɛ, na bo̱ ya fo̱‑ɔ. Ane̱ gye̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ ba kɔɔre̱ ba gyi fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ e̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ ane̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: Faith makes us sure of what we hope for and gives us proof of what we cannot see.
text: To, ne̱ nte̱tɔ e̱ gye̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi? Ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ a kɔɔre̱ gyi fe̱yɛ ane̱ e̱ nya atɔ ne̱ ane̱ de te̱maa fe̱yɛ ane̱ e̱ nya, ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ ane̱ akatɔ mo̱ŋ ke̱e̱ amo̱‑ɔ.

ref: It was their faith that made our ancestors pleasing to God.
text: Ade̱daapo̱‑ɔ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi si ne̱ bamo̱ ase̱ŋ a pre̱ Wuribware̱.

ref: Because of our faith, we know that the world was made at God's command. We also know that what can be seen was made out of what cannot be seen.
text: Ane̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi ne̱e̱ se̱ ne̱ ane̱ a nu kaase̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ kanɔ‑rɔ ase̱ŋkpare̱gyi ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ bo̱ twe̱e̱ kaye̱ pɛɛɛ, so̱so̱ na kaase̱. Ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi si ne̱ ane̱ nyi fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ aa ba. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ n gye̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ twe̱e̱ atɔ ne̱ ane̱ ke̱e̱ kaye̱‑rɔ pɛɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: Abraham had faith and obeyed God. He was told to go to the land that God had said would be his, and he left for a country he had never seen.
text: Ne̱ nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ saŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a te̱e̱ Abraham tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ yɔ swe̱e̱re̱ ko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ ɔ sa mò̱ aa mò̱ ananagyi‑o si, ne̱ hare̱e̱ Abraham a mo̱ŋ nyi mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ yɔ‑ɔ gbaa ooo, ne̱ ɔɔ gya Wuribware̱ a kanɔ‑ɔ se̱? Aa le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ gyi‑o si.

ref: Abraham did this, because he was waiting for the eternal city God had planned and built.
text: Ne̱ ŋkee oo nyite o gywii maŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ ba a ɔ bo̱ pwɛɛ so̱so̱, na ɔ lɔŋŋɔ mò̱ dame̱naŋsɛ, na maŋ amo̱ ye̱re̱ nsu pɛɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: Her husband Abraham was almost dead, but he became the ancestor of many people. In fact, there are as many of them as there are stars in the sky or grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ɔnyare̱ ko̱ŋko̱ mɔ ne̱ bo̱ yɛ ɔɔ tɔ‑rɔ iwu gbaa‑ɔ kayaagyi ya baa ke̱nana ne̱ ke̱mo̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ kyɔ fe̱yɛ awo̱re̱‑rɔ akye̱e̱‑e̱‑kpa‑agyi na ɔpo̱o̱ kɛɛ a ke̱se̱pu‑o.

ref: Every one of those people died. But they still had faith, even though they had not received what they had been promised. They were glad just to see these things from far away, and they agreed that they were only strangers and foreigners on this earth.
text: Abraham na mò̱ gyi‑ana a wu, na bo̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ nya swe̱e̱re̱ timaa ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ ɔ sa bamo̱‑ɔ. Amaa hare̱e̱ ane̱ŋ gbaa ooo, baa saŋ kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ a ase̱ŋ tɔwe̱sɛ‑ɔ gyi. A waa ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ a nya swe̱e̱re̱ amo̱ ne̱ bo̱ ke̱e̱ mò̱ a da bamo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ. Bo̱ mo̱ŋ gyiiri ikii fe̱yɛ afɔ e̱ gye̱ bamo̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ.

ref: When people talk this way, it is clear they are looking for a place to call their own.
text: Ane̱ŋ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ e̱ kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ba buwi ba kpa swe̱e̱re̱ ne̱ o kii bamo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ lee‑o ne̱e̱.

ref: If they had been talking about the land where they had once lived, they could have gone back at any time.
text: N gye̱ swe̱e̱re̱ ne̱ baa ko̱so̱ mò̱ se̱‑ɔ se̱ ne̱ ba buwi ba kpa. A dɛɛ a gye̱ mò̱ ne̱e̱‑ɔ, weetee kpa saŋ ɔ da sa bamo̱ a bo̱ kiŋŋi yɔ.

ref: But they were looking forward to a better home in heaven. This is why God wasn't ashamed for them to call him their God. He even built a city for them.
text: Swe̱e̱re̱ ko̱ ne̱ ɔ kyɔ mmo̱‑ɔ ase̱ŋ ne̱ ba kaapo̱, ne̱ ba kpa mò̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Mò̱ e̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ se̱ fɔŋfɔŋ‑ɔ. Amo̱se̱ ne̱ ipeere mo̱ŋ de Wuribware̱ kikii bamo̱ nyaŋpe̱, ne̱ ɔɔ lɔŋŋɔ kye̱nakpa so̱so̱ bo̱ ye̱ra bamo̱.

ref: because he was sure that God could raise people to life. This was just like getting Isaac back from death.
text: O nyi fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ e̱ taare̱ na o kyiŋŋi Isak le̱e̱ lowi‑ro. Kase̱ŋtiŋ si, Wuribware̱ a kuŋ mò̱, na ɔ ma ba mò̱ se̱kaŋ bo̱ mɔɔ mò̱ gyi‑o, a du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Abraham a kɔɔre̱ mò̱ le̱e̱ lowi ase̱‑ɔ.

ref: He chose to be mistreated with God's people instead of having the good time that sin could bring for a little while.
text: Ɔɔ waa mò̱ mfɛɛre̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, mò̱ ya gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱ Igyipiti owure‑o a taa bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ase̱sɛ se̱‑ɔ, a bɔ fe̱yɛ mò̱ a kye̱na kɔne̱ ne̱ ɔ maa kyee‑o‑ro o gyi bɔye̱.

ref: Moses knew that the treasures of Egypt were not as wonderful as what he would receive from suffering for the Messiah, and he looked forward to his reward.
text: Ɔ yɛ, bamo̱ ya kperi Kristoo si saare̱ mò̱, a de ke̱nyare̱ a kyɔ mò̱ a nya Igyipiti swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ a kapo̱tɛɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ. Mò̱ akatɔ dɔŋ nyaŋe̱ ko̱ a ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔ ba a ɔ bo̱ nya‑ɔ se̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: God's people had faith, and when they had walked around the city of Jericho for seven days, its walls fell down.
text: Nte̱tɔ ya waa se̱ ne̱, saŋ ne̱ Isireelii awuye‑o a nare̱ muruwaa Yɛrikoo maŋ ŋke nsunoo‑o, ne̱ kideŋbee ne̱ maŋ‑ɔ awuye a pwɛɛ bo̱ muruwaa maŋ‑ɔ a le̱e̱ da? Aa le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ gyi‑o si.

ref: What else can I say? There isn't enough time to tell about Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets.
text: N kya se̱ tɔwe̱ amo̱ ako̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ bo̱ tii si aaa? Saŋ mo̱ŋ kyɔ sa mo̱ na n tɔwe̱ Gidiyɔŋ na Barak na Samsɔŋ na Yifita na Deefid na Samuwɛl na Wuribware̱ akyaamɛɛ a ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi‑o ase̱ŋ.

ref: Some women received their loved ones back from death. Many of these people were tortured, but they refused to be released. They were sure they would get a better reward when the dead are raised to life.
text: Akye̱e̱ ko̱ mɔ ako̱we̱bɛɛ a kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro. Ase̱sɛ ko̱ mɔ, baa waa bamo̱ bo̱rɔkraa, amaa bamo̱‑ɔ bo̱ mo̱ŋ pɔɔ, na bamo̱ ne̱ ba waa bamo̱ bo̱rɔkraa‑ɔ nya yɔwe̱ bamo̱. Bo̱ yɛ, bamo̱ ya wu, ne̱ bamo̱ ya kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro nya ŋkpa ne̱ n kyɔ mmo̱‑ɔ, a bɔ.

ref: Others were made fun of and beaten with whips, and some were chained in jail.
text: Baa saare̱ bo̱ko̱ mɔ, ne̱ baa da bamo̱ e̱paara, ne̱ baa kra bamo̱ ya tii da bamo̱ e̱kpe̱e̱.

ref: Still others were stoned to death or sawed in two or killed with swords. Some had nothing but sheep skins or goat skins to wear. They were poor, mistreated, and tortured.
text: Ne̱ bo̱ko̱ mɔ, baa ba abu bɛɛɛ apaŋ bo̱ mɔɔ, hare̱e̱ baa ba sɔɔyaa bo̱ te̱ŋ bamo̱‑rɔ atiŋ anyɔ. Bo̱ko̱ mɔ a wu kitiri, ne̱ baa ŋu ase̱ŋ, ne̱ baa gyi tɔɔraa. Te̱e̱re̱ bɛɛɛ sanne̱ e̱wo̱re̱ e̱ gye̱ bamo̱ awaagya.

ref: The world did not deserve these good people, who had to wander in deserts and on mountains and had to live in caves and holes in the ground.
text: Ne̱ bo̱ko̱ mɔ a nare̱ kaa kimukee‑ro na abe̱e̱ se̱, ne̱ baa kye̱na e̱bɔ‑rɔ na amaŋtaŋ‑nɔ. Ane̱ŋ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ dɛɛ bo̱ gye̱ ase̱sɛ timaa bo̱ kyo̱ŋ bamo̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ dɛɛ bo̱ bo̱ kaye̱‑rɔ saŋ amo̱‑ɔ.

ref: This was because God had something better in store for us. And he did not want them to reach the goal of their faith without us.
text: Ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ aa ba ane̱ŋ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kpa ke̱tɔ timaa ko̱ bo̱ ye̱ra bamo̱ so̱so̱. Ke̱mo̱ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ ne̱ ane̱ te ndɔɔ‑ɔ na bamo̱ ne̱ baa dɛɛ kye̱na saŋ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ tii abɛɛ se̱ ya kye̱na so̱so̱ a kye̱nakpa ne̱ o bware ɔ kyo̱ŋ kye̱nakpa ke̱maa‑ɔ.

ref: Such a large crowd of witnesses is all around us! So we must get rid of everything that slows us down, especially the sin that just won't let go. And we must be determined to run the race that is ahead of us.
text: Ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ mɔ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ baa ye̱re̱ bamo̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi‑o‑ro keŋkeŋ. Na ane̱ fe̱raa‑ɔ, ndɔɔ a du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ amo̱ a muruwaa ane̱ ba de̱e̱re̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ se̱re̱ e̱naŋ‑ɔ. Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ lee atɔ na e̱bɔye̱ ne̱ e̱ sa ne̱ ane̱ maa taare̱ a ane̱ se̱re̱ nɛɛnɛɛ‑ɔ, na ane̱ pee akatɔ se̱re̱ ane̱ e̱naŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a sa ane̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ se̱re̱‑ɔ.

ref: We must keep our eyes on Jesus, who leads us and makes our faith complete. He endured the shame of being nailed to a cross, because he knew later on he would be glad he did. Now he is seated at the right side of God's throne!
text: Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ de̱e̱re̱ Yeesuu ne̱ mò̱ fe̱raa a kyɔ gye̱ ane̱ ŋkpɛɛ yɔ ɔ ye̱re̱ e̱naŋ‑ɔ kɛɛ‑ɔ o gywii ane̱‑ɔ. Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔɔ de̱e̱re̱ akatɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ oo ŋu kɔne̱ ne̱ ɔ nya nyaŋe̱ ko̱‑ɔ. Oo gyi kanyite ŋu kaye̱e̱gya na lowi kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱, na a mo̱ŋ tiri mò̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ a lowi‑o de ipeere bɛɛɛ ɔ mo̱ŋ de. Amaa mbe̱yɔmɔ o te Wuribware̱ kigyisesare̱e̱ se̱, na mò̱ aa Wuribware̱ i gyi kuwure.

ref: So keep your mind on Jesus, who put up with many insults from sinners. Then you won't get discouraged and give up.
text: Mo̱nꞌ de̱ŋ de̱e̱re̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a ŋu ase̱ŋ na kikisi le̱e̱ abɔye̱waapo̱ ase̱‑ɔ. Mo̱ne̱ ya taare̱ waa ane̱ŋ, mo̱ne̱ abaa‑rɔ maa yɔre̱, na mo̱ne̱ te̱maa lo̱we̱.

ref: None of you have yet been hurt in your battle against sin.
text: Mo̱ne̱ e̱ kɔ e̱bɔye̱, amaa mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ fo̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ a tiri fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ kɔ wu‑o.

ref: But you have forgotten that the Scriptures say to God's children, “When the Lord punishes you, don't make light of it, and when he corrects you, don't be discouraged.
text: Nsaŋse̱ mo̱ne̱ a taŋ ase̱ŋ ne̱ a bo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ amo̱ gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ gyi‑ana‑ɔ se̱. Ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ gyi, yuri e̱ye̱e̱ na fo̱ nu Wuribware̱ ke̱be̱ra, ne̱ mò̱ ya gyiiri fo̱ ke̱se̱bɔ, fo̱ ma sa a fo̱ abaa‑rɔ yɔre̱.

ref: The Lord corrects the people he loves and disciplines those he calls his own.”
text: Ŋu fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ne̱ ɔ kpa ase̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱ ɔ be̱ra, ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ mò̱ gyi‑o ne̱ o gyiiri ke̱se̱bɔ.”

ref: Be patient when you are being corrected! This is how God treats his children. Don't all parents correct their children?
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ ŋu fe̱yɛ ase̱ŋ mɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i ŋu mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ de ɔ be̱ra mo̱ne̱ ne̱e̱. Ɔ waa mo̱ne̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ a gye̱ mò̱ gyi‑ana‑ɔ. Kayaagyi ko̱ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ mò̱ se̱ maa gyiiri mò̱ ke̱se̱bɔ aaa?

ref: God corrects all his children, and if he doesn't correct you, then you don't really belong to him.
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ be̱ra mò̱‑ɔ i ŋu ase̱ŋ. Amaa Wuribware̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ be̱ra fo̱, a kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ n gye̱ mò̱ gyi kase̱ŋtiŋ e̱ gye̱ fo̱, amɔ ke̱bawo̱re̱gyi dooo.

ref: Our earthly fathers correct us, and we still respect them. Isn't it even better to be given true life by letting our spiritual Father correct us?
text: Ane̱ se̱‑ana ne̱ ane̱ de bamo̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ mfe̱e̱‑ɔ gbaa i gyiiri ane̱ ke̱se̱bɔ, ane̱ mɔ ane̱ i bu bamo̱, saŋbo̱to̱ Wuribware̱ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ ɔke̱maa mò̱ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ane̱ ma yuri e̱ye̱e̱ a ane̱ bo̱ sa, na ane̱ nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ ɔɔɔ?

ref: Our human fathers correct us for a short time, and they do it as they think best. But God corrects us for our own good, because he wants us to be holy, as he is.
text: Ane̱ se̱‑ana e̱ be̱ra ane̱ kafwe̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ba fa fe̱yɛ a bware‑o. Amaa Wuribware̱ fe̱raa e̱ be̱ra ane̱ ane̱ kibaare si ne̱e̱, na ane̱ nya baare fe̱yɛ mò̱‑ɔ.

ref: It is never fun to be corrected. In fact, at the time it is always painful. But if we learn to obey by being corrected, we will do right and live at peace.
text: Bamo̱ e̱ be̱ra ane̱, a bo̱ gya. A mo̱ŋ bo̱ ane̱ kɔne̱. Amaa kamɛɛ‑rɔ ɔke̱maa ne̱ oo nu ane̱ŋ a ke̱be̱ra‑ɔ i baare, na o kii se̱sɛ timaa. Mo̱nꞌ sa se̱ na mo̱ne̱ ma lwii kamɛɛ

ref: Now stand up straight! Stop your knees from shaking
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱nꞌ le̱ŋ e̱ye̱e̱, na mo̱ne̱ ma lɛɛ sa a mo̱ne̱ abaa‑rɔ yɔre̱.

ref: and walk a straight path. Then lame people will be healed, instead of getting worse. Warning against Turning from God
text: Mo̱nꞌ sa a mo̱ne̱ e̱kpa pɛɛɛ waa kyɛɛkyɛɛ, na bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱ le̱ŋ bamo̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi‑o‑ro‑o nya ŋu taare̱ nare̱ se̱.

ref: Try to live at peace with everyone! Live a clean life. If you don't, you will never see the Lord.
text: Mo̱nꞌ le̱ŋ e̱ye̱e̱ na bamo̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ aa bamo̱ te‑o kye̱na kaye̱e̱yuri‑ro. Na mo̱nꞌ kye̱na kakye̱na timaa, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ maa taare̱ a o ŋu Wuribware̱, amɔ mò̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ a fwiiri fe̱raa.

ref: Make sure no one misses out on God's wonderful kindness. Don't let anyone become bitter and cause trouble for the rest of you.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ɔko̱ ma waa paŋ Wuribware̱ a ŋyure‑o. Mo̱ne̱ ɔke̱maa ma sa a kikisi lwee mò̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ daŋ fe̱yɛ kiyii preepreesɛ ko̱ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ daŋ, ne̱ ki de ke̱mo̱ ke̱bwɛɛ‑ɔ ke̱ e̱ ba ase̱ŋ‑ɔ. N gye̱ ane̱ŋ, ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ i kii ase̱sɛ bɔye̱.

ref: You know how he later wanted it back. But there was nothing he could do to change things, even though he begged his father and cried.
text: Amo̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ ne̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ i nyiŋŋi si fe̱yɛ ɔɔ kpa fe̱yɛ mò̱ se̱ yure mò̱, ne̱ mò̱ se̱ a kine. Ne̱ hare̱e̱ mò̱ a nu mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱, ne̱ ɔɔ ba akyukyu bo̱ ko̱re̱ mò̱ se̱‑ɔ gbaa ooo, kpa mo̱ŋ lɛɛ ɔ bo̱‑rɔ, na ɔ bo̱ kyurowi ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ waa‑ɔ.

ref: or trumpet sound. The people of Israel heard a voice speak. But they begged it to stop,
text: Ne̱ baa nu kabe̱re̱ dabe̱ ko̱ kusu, na bo̱re̱ ko̱ a sa se̱ŋsa. Ne̱ kufu a nya ase̱sɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa ko̱re̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ma lɛɛ nu bo̱re̱‑ɔ.

ref: because they could not obey its commands. They were even told to kill any animal that touched the mountain.
text: Bo̱ mo̱ŋ kpa kunu Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ dabo̱rɔ ke̱be̱e̱ amo̱‑ɔ, hare̱e̱ amo̱ e̱ gye̱ kabo̱ gbaa ooo, mo̱nꞌ twe̱e̱ abu bo̱ daye̱ mò̱ mɔɔ.” Bo̱ yɛ amo̱‑ɔ bo̱ le̱ŋ sa bamo̱.

ref: The sight was so frightening that Moses said he shook with fear.
text: Atɔ amo̱ a waa kufu bwe̱e̱tɔ, hare̱e̱ gbaa Mosis yɛ e̱ye̱e̱ a yo̱ŋ mò̱.

ref: You have now come to Mount Zion and to the heavenly Jerusalem. This is the city of the living God, where thousands and thousands of angels have come to celebrate.
text: Amaa mo̱ne̱ ndɔɔ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ fe̱raa, mo̱ne̱ ke̱ba Wuribware̱ ase̱ bo̱ kɔne̱ fɛɛ. Siyɔŋ ke̱be̱e̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a sɔŋ tɔ‑rɔ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ ne̱ ɔ bɔ so̱so̱‑ɔ na Wuribware̱ ne̱ mò̱ wo̱re̱ dooo i de ŋkpa‑ɔ aye̱. Mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ Wuribware̱ mbɔɔ ŋkpe̱ŋ ŋkpe̱ŋ a gyaŋŋe̱ ba gyi akatɔ.

ref: Here you will find all of God's dearest children, whose names are written in heaven. And you will find God himself, who judges everyone. Here also are the spirits of those good people who have been made perfect.
text: Asɔre̱e̱ awuye a be̱e̱ gyaŋŋe̱ mfe̱ŋ, bamo̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ bamo̱ anyare̱ bo̱ be̱ya so̱so̱, ne̱ ɔ te̱e̱ bamo̱ ɔke̱maa mò̱ bregyi‑o ne̱ n de. Mo̱ne̱ a ba Ase̱sɛ-pɛɛɛ‑ɔse̱ŋgyipo̱-Bware̱ ase̱. Mo̱ne̱ a ba mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ase̱sɛ timaa e̱kra ne̱ Wuribware̱ a sa ne̱ bamo̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ a fwiiri lo̱we̱‑ɔ te‑o.

ref: Make sure you obey the one who speaks to you. The people did not escape, when they refused to obey the one who spoke to them at Mount Sinai. Do you think you can possibly escape, if you refuse to obey the one who speaks to you from heaven?
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱re̱ dame̱naŋsɛ. Mo̱ne̱ ma kine kunu mò̱ ne̱ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa o gywii mo̱ne̱‑ɔ. Bamo̱ ne̱ baa kye̱na nsaŋ‑ɔ a kine Wuribware̱ ne̱ ɔɔ sa se̱ŋsa gywii bamo̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ kanɔ se̱ ke̱gya. Bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, bo̱ mo̱ŋ taare̱ kwaye̱ ke̱se̱bɔgyiiri‑o. Amɔ ane̱ fe̱raa, ane̱ ya kine mò̱ ne̱ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa o gywii ane̱ ɔ le̱e̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ane̱ e̱ taare̱ a ane̱ kwaye̱ ke̱se̱bɔgyiiri‑o ooo?

ref: When God spoke the first time, his voice shook only the earth. This time he has promised to shake the earth once again, and heaven too.
text: Saŋ amo̱ Wuribware̱ bo̱re̱‑ɔ a le̱ŋkpaŋ swe̱e̱re̱. Amaa ndɔɔ fe̱raa ɔɔ tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ, “M be̱e̱ mo̱ e̱ le̱ŋkpaŋ swe̱e̱re̱ na so̱so̱ gbaa.”

ref: The words “once again” mean that these created things will someday be shaken and removed. Then what cannot be shaken will last.
text: Mò̱ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ “M be̱e̱ mo̱ e̱ le̱ŋkpaŋ”‑ɔ, ɔ kaapo̱ ane̱ dame̱naŋsɛ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ le̱ŋkpaŋ atɔ ne̱ ɔɔ twe̱e̱‑ɔ, na o lee amo̱, na ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ maa le̱ŋkpaŋ‑ɔ wo̱re̱ nya saŋ nsu pɛɛɛ.

ref: We should be grateful we were given a kingdom that cannot be shaken. And in this kingdom we please God by worshiping him and by showing him great honor and respect.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ sa Wuribware̱ aŋsɛ fe̱yɛ ane̱ e̱ ya gyi Wuribware̱ a kuwure‑o so̱so̱, mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ nya ye̱re̱kpa ne̱ ɔ maa le̱ŋkpaŋ‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ. Na mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ bu Wuribware̱, na ane̱ ba kufu bo̱ suŋ mò̱, ane̱ŋ ne̱ a gyi mò̱ akatɔ‑ɔ.

ref: Keep being concerned about each other as the Lord's followers should.
text: Mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱ kpa abɛɛ fe̱yɛ se̱sɛ na mò̱ ko̱we̱bɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: Be sure to welcome strangers into your home. By doing this, some people have welcomed angels as guests, without even knowing it.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ma yɔwe̱ afɔ ke̱kra nɛɛnɛɛ. Bo̱ko̱ ne̱ baa kra afɔ nɛɛnɛɛ‑ɔ a kra Wuribware̱ a mbɔɔ‑ɔ bo̱ko̱, na bo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi gbaa.

ref: Remember the Lord's people who are in jail and be concerned for them. Don't forget those who are suffering, but imagine you are there with them.
text: Mo̱nꞌ nyiŋŋi bamo̱ ne̱ baa tii‑o si fe̱yɛ sabe̱e̱‑ooo mo̱ne̱ aa bamo̱ ne̱ baa tii‑o. Mo̱nꞌ be̱e̱ nyiŋŋi bamo̱ ne̱ ba tɔɔraa bamo̱‑ɔ se̱ fe̱yɛ sabe̱e̱‑ooo mo̱ne̱ aa bamo̱ ne̱ ba tɔɔraa‑ɔ.

ref: Have respect for marriage. Always be faithful to your partner, because God will punish anyone who is immoral or unfaithful in marriage.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ɔke̱maa de̱e̱ bu awaare̱yɛ. Na anyare̱ na bamo̱ ka‑ana ma kaŋ le̱e̱ abɛɛ kamɛɛ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ i bu ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ waa kakye̱e̱kpa na kanyare̱kpa‑ɔ ke̱pɔ.

ref: Don't fall in love with money. Be satisfied with what you have. The Lord has promised that he will not leave us or desert us.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ma waa katanne̱kpa bwe̱e̱tɔ. Amaa mo̱nꞌ sa a ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ de‑o pre̱ mo̱ne̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ma yɔwe̱ mo̱ne̱‑rɔ, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ mɔ ne̱ ma kine mo̱ne̱.”

ref: This should make you feel like saying, “The Lord helps me! Why should I be afraid of what people can do to me?”
text: Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ iduŋ waa se̱e̱, na ane̱ tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Wuribware̱ e̱ gye̱ mo̱ ɔkyarɔpo̱, amo̱se̱‑ɔ kufu mo̱ŋ de mo̱ anyɔ se̱. Se̱sɛ dimaadi maa taare̱ a ɔ waa ke̱bɔye̱ ko̱ bo̱ kye mo̱, daa.”

ref: Don't forget about your leaders who taught you God's message. Remember what kind of lives they lived and try to have faith like theirs.
text: Mo̱nꞌ nyiŋŋi mo̱ne̱ asɔre̱e̱ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ gywii mo̱ne̱‑ɔ se̱. Mo̱nꞌ nyiŋŋi ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kye̱na‑ɔ se̱ na ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa wu‑o si, na mo̱nꞌ ko̱te̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ gyi‑o.

ref: Jesus Christ never changes! He is the same yesterday, today, and forever.
text: Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu Kristoo du ndee‑o dɛɛ ne̱ o du ndɔɔ de̱ŋ bo̱ yɔ nsu pɛɛɛ.

ref: Don't be fooled by any kind of strange teachings. It is better to receive strength from God's gift of undeserved grace than to depend on certain foods. After all, these foods don't really help the people who eat them.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱ne̱ ma sa a ɔko̱ ba mò̱ ke̱kaapo̱ ne̱ ke̱ gye̱ mo̱ne̱ ase̱ ɔfɔ‑ɔ bo̱ sa a mo̱nꞌ fo̱. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ateese ko̱ kikisi kigyi ke̱gyase̱ maa sa a ane̱ nya ke̱yaale̱ŋ ke̱bware̱suŋ‑no. Ane̱ŋ a mbraa‑ɔ maa kya bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ gya mmo̱ se̱‑ɔ‑rɔ. Amaa a bware ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ taa mfɛɛre̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ ŋyure ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ sa ane̱‑ɔ se̱.

ref: After the high priest offers the blood of animals as a sin offering, the bodies of those animals are burned outside the camp.
text: Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ ya mɔɔ mbo̱ bo̱ lɔŋŋɔ Wuribware̱, na ɔ nya taa ase̱sɛ e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ ke bamo̱‑ɔ, o de mbo̱‑ɔ mbo̱gya wo̱re̱ ne̱e̱ ɔ yɔ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa a ke̱kyaŋ kpe̱yaa kpe̱yaa‑ɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ ɔ taa mbo̱‑ɔ fɔŋfɔŋ ɔ yaa maŋ‑ɔ nlaŋbɛɛ ɔ ya kywɛɛ pɛɛɛ.

ref: This is why we should go outside the camp to Jesus and share in his disgrace.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ yɔwe̱ ane̱ kakye̱na de̱daa‑ɔ bo̱ sii, na ane̱ gya Yeesuu si, ya gyi ipeere ne̱ oo gyi saŋ ne̱ oo wu kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ ane̱ŋ dɛɛ.

ref: But don't forget to help others and to share your possessions with them. This too is like offering a sacrifice that pleases God.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ma kaŋ yɔwe̱ ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ ko̱waa. Mo̱ne̱ aa mo̱ne̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana de̱e̱ ke ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ de‑o‑ro. Ane̱ŋ a Wuribware̱ ko̱lɔŋŋɔ‑ɔ i gyi mò̱ akatɔ fɛɛ.

ref: Obey your leaders and do what they say. They are watching over you, and they must answer to God. So don't make them sad as they do their work. Make them happy. Otherwise, they won't be able to help you at all.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ma kaŋ waa mo̱ne̱ asɔre̱e̱ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ akatɔle̱ŋ, mo̱nꞌ bu bamo̱ ke̱bre̱sɛ‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kake ŋko̱ ba lee ane̱ŋ ne̱ ba de̱e̱re̱ mo̱ne̱ se̱‑ɔ kanɔ Wuribware̱ ase̱. Mo̱ne̱ ya bu bamo̱ ane̱ŋ, bamo̱ akatɔ i gyi. Mo̱ne̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ waa ane̱ŋ fe̱raa, a maa waa bamo̱ kɔne̱, na a sa a mo̱nꞌ paŋ atɔ timaa ko̱ ne̱ a de to̱nɔ‑ɔ.

ref: I especially want you to pray that I can visit you again soon. Final Prayers and Greetings
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ be̱e̱ ki tiri‑o e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ sa mo̱, na ŋ nya kiŋŋi ba mo̱ne̱ ase̱ ŋke mfe̱ne̱ mɔ‑rɔ. Wo̱re̱ mɔ ase̱ŋ ne̱ a tii te̱bɔ‑ɔ

ref: My friends, I have written only a short letter to encourage you, and I beg you to pay close attention to what I have said.
text: Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ mo̱ne̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ ba kanyite bo̱ nu mo̱ wo̱re̱ mɔ‑rɔ ase̱ŋ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ wo̱re̱ mɔ ne̱ n de mo̱ e̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ mo̱ne̱‑ɔ ase̱ŋ e̱ sa mo̱ne̱ wɔre̱, ɔ mo̱ŋ bo̱ e̱swe̱e̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ ko̱.

ref: By now you surely must know that our friend Timothy is out of jail. If he gets here in time, I will bring him with me when I come to visit you.
text: Mo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ nu fe̱yɛ baa lee ane̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ Timotii le̱e̱ tiikpa. Mò̱ ya ba ŋke mfe̱ne̱ mɔ‑rɔ, mo̱ aa mò̱ e̱ ba na ane̱ bo̱ ka mo̱ne̱ kanɔ.

ref: Please give my greetings to your leaders and to the rest of the Lord's people. His followers from Italy send you their greetings.
text: Mo̱nꞌ ka mo̱ne̱ asɔre̱e̱ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ na Wuribware̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ mɔ kanɔ. Kristoo awuye ne̱ baa le̱e̱ Italiya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ e̱ ka mo̱ne̱ kanɔ.

ref: From James, a servant of God and of our Lord Jesus Christ. Greetings to the twelve tribes scattered all over the world. Faith and Wisdom
text: Mo̱, Yakubu, ne̱ n gye̱ Wuribware̱ na ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo ke̱yaafɔre̱‑ɔ e̱ ka Wuribware̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ baa le̱e̱ Yudaa awuye a nsuro kudu ŋnyɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ baa brawe̱‑rɔ bo̱ bo̱ to̱ŋ ke̱maa kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ kanɔ. Ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi na kanyiase̱ŋ

ref: But you must learn to endure everything, so you will be completely mature and not lacking in anything.
text: N gye̱ amo̱ pɛɛɛ ne̱e̱. Mo̱ne̱ ya waa kanyite ase̱ŋ‑nɔ saŋ ke̱maa, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ yɔwe̱ kanyite‑o, mo̱ne̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ i fwiiri, na mo̱nꞌ kii Kristoo awuye kase̱ŋtiŋ, na mo̱ne̱ ma paŋ Kristoo a ŋyure‑o ŋko̱.

ref: If any of you need wisdom, you should ask God, and it will be given to you. God is generous and won't correct you for asking.
text: Ane̱ŋ a ŋyure‑o ŋko̱ e̱ gye̱ kanyiase̱ŋ. Mo̱ne̱ ɔko̱ ne̱ mò̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ de ane̱ŋ a kanyiase̱ŋ‑ɔ, ɔ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱, na Wuribware̱ sa mò̱ kamo̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ de kakpo̱nɔ o ke ɔke̱maa atɔ, na ɔ maa tɛɛ.

ref: But when you ask for something, you must have faith and not doubt. Anyone who doubts is like an ocean wave tossed around in a storm.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ɔke̱maa ya ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱, ɔ kɔɔre̱ gyi fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ e̱ sa mò̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔ kpa‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ ne̱ ɔ maa kɔɔre̱ o gyi‑o du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ afwii a da ke̱laŋkpare̱ ki i muruwi‑ro ɔpo̱o̱ se̱‑ɔ.

ref: If you are that kind of person, you can't make up your mind, and you surely can't be trusted. So don't expect the Lord to give you anything at all. Poor People and Rich People
text: Ɔko̱ ne̱ o du mfaanɛɛ, ne̱ ɔ maa taare̱ a ɔ fa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔ kpa‑ɔ na ɔ kra ke̱mo̱‑rɔ keŋkeŋ‑o maa nya sɛye̱ a ɔ bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu ase̱. Atiripo̱ na atɔ awuye ase̱ŋ

ref: Any of God's people who are poor should be glad he thinks so highly of them.
text: Ɔko̱ bo̱ mo̱ne̱‑rɔ ne̱ kitiri de mò̱ aaa? Se̱sɛ‑ɔ sa a mò̱ akatɔ gyi, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mbe̱yɔmɔ ɔ gye̱ se̱sɛ dabe̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱.

ref: scorched by the burning heat of the sun. The flowers lose their blossoms, and their beauty is destroyed. This is how the rich will disappear, as they go about their business. Trials and Temptations
text: Kyɔwe̱ e̱ kywɛɛ ke̱fe̱raagyi‑o a ɔ mɔɔ, na ke̱mo̱ ato̱to̱o̱ kpo̱ŋŋe̱, na amo̱ daŋ nye̱ra‑ɔ. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ atɔ wuye i wu, na ɔ maa se̱ o suŋ kusuŋ‑o. Mfe̱ne̱ ne̱ ase̱ŋ na awo̱re̱fɔɔ a le̱e̱?

ref: We are tempted by our own desires that drag us off and trap us.
text: To, mfe̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ nyi fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ a ko̱kyɔrɔke̱e̱ a le̱e̱? Se̱sɛ ŋyo̱rɔwe̱ bɔye̱ gye̱ ne̱ n gyiiri mò̱ n le̱e̱ e̱kpa timaa si.

ref: Our desires make us sin, and when sin is finished with us, it leaves us dead.
text: Kayo̱rɔwe̱ bɔye̱ ya nya kame, ka ko̱we̱ e̱bɔye̱ ko̱waa. Ne̱ e̱bɔye̱ ko̱waa ya nya kame, e̱e̱ ko̱we̱ lowi. Mfe̱ne̱ ne̱ ke̱tɔ timaa a le̱e̱?

ref: Every good and perfect gift comes down from the Father who created all the lights in the heavens. He is always the same and never makes dark shadows by changing.
text: So̱so̱ ne̱ atɔ timaa pɛɛɛ mɔ a le̱e̱. Aa le̱e̱ ane̱ se̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱. Mò̱ ya twe̱e̱ kyɔwe̱ na oferi na akye̱e̱‑e̱‑kpa‑agyi. Mbe̱yɔmɔ, ane̱ŋ a so̱so̱ a atɔ mɔ i kyurowi saŋ ke̱maa. Fe̱yɛ kyɔwe̱ a le̱e̱, ne̱ ɔ twɛɛ‑ɔ. Amaa Wuribware̱ fe̱raa maa kyurowi daa.

ref: Obey God's message! Don't fool yourselves by just listening to it.
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ o nu abware̱se̱ŋ, na mò̱‑ɔ mò̱ mo̱ŋ de amo̱ ɔ waa asuŋ timaa‑o e̱ pe̱nna mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: But you must never stop looking at the perfect law that sets you free. God will bless you in everything you do, if you listen and obey, and don't just hear and forget.
text: Amaa ɔke̱maa ne̱ o nu Wuribware̱ ase̱ŋ, ne̱ ɔ maa taŋ ase̱ŋ ne̱ o nu si‑o, ne̱ ɔ gya amo̱ se̱ saŋ ke̱maa‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ i yure mò̱ mò̱ ke̱tɔ waasɛ ke̱maa‑rɔ. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a mbraa timaa amo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ a sa a ane̱ nya ane̱ e̱ye̱e̱ le̱e̱ e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ.

ref: Religion that pleases God the Father must be pure and spotless. You must help needy orphans and widows and not let this world make you evil.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ane̱ se̱ Wuribware̱ yɛ ke̱ gye̱ ke̱bware̱suŋ timaa‑o e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa de̱e̱re̱ aŋɔŋgyi na akpenlekye̱e̱ ne̱ ba gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ‑ɔ se̱, na ɔke̱maa kra mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ le̱e̱ kaye̱ e̱bɔye̱ ne̱ e̱e̱ nye̱ra mò̱‑ɔ se̱.

ref: My friends, if you have faith in our glorious Lord Jesus Christ, you won't treat some people better than others.
text: Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, mo̱ne̱ a kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu Kristoo ne̱ ɔ gye̱ wuraa wuye na ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ dabe̱‑ɔ gyi‑o si‑o, mo̱ne̱ ma kaŋ teŋŋi‑ro pwɛɛ na mo̱nꞌ dɛɛ ŋu ane̱ŋ ne̱ a bware fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ kra ɔko̱‑ɔ.

ref: Suppose a rich person wearing fancy clothes and a gold ring comes to one of your meetings. And suppose a poor person dressed in worn-out clothes also comes.
text: Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ taa fe̱yɛ atɔ wuye ne̱ o buŋ atɔ timaa, ne̱ atanne̱ kapini te mò̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ‑ɔ a ba mo̱ne̱ asɔre̱e̱ ngyaŋŋe̱‑rɔ, ne̱ otiripo̱ mɔ a buŋ mò̱ aprakɛɛ ba mo̱ne̱ ngyaŋŋe̱‑rɔ.

ref: You must not give the best seat to the one in fancy clothes and tell the one who is poor to stand at the side or sit on the floor.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ya bu atɔ timaa wuye‑o, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ yɛ, “Kye̱na kye̱nakpa timaa mɔ”, amaa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ ya tɔwe̱ gywii otiripo̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ye̱re̱ mfe̱ŋ”, bɛɛɛ “Kye̱na swe̱e̱re̱ mo̱ ase̱ mfe̱e̱”,

ref: This is the same as saying that some people are better than others, and you would be acting like a crooked judge.
text: mo̱ne̱ ya waa ane̱ŋ, a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ i teŋŋi‑ro ne̱e̱. Ne̱ mo̱ne̱ be̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ i gyi ase̱ŋ ke̱kpa se̱.

ref: My dear friends, pay attention. God has given a lot of faith to the poor people in this world. God has also promised them a share in his kingdom that he will give to everyone who loves him.
text: Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ timaa‑ana, mo̱nꞌ nu fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ne̱ kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ i bu bamo̱ atiripo̱‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ fe̱raa i bu bamo̱ atɔ awuye ne̱e̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ba kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ ba gyi bwe̱e̱tɔ, ne̱ bo̱ tii kuwure ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ bo̱ be̱ya fe̱yɛ ɔ sa bamo̱ ne̱ ba kpa mò̱‑ɔ se̱.

ref: You mistreat the poor. But isn't it the rich who boss you around and drag you off to court?
text: Amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ gbaa ooo mo̱ne̱ maa bu atiripo̱, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ be̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ de bamo̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ te̱e̱ sɛye̱. N gye̱ atɔ awuye e̱ gye̱ ne̱ bo̱ naa ba yaa mo̱ne̱ iwurekpa, na ba waa mo̱ne̱ awo̱re̱fɔɔ‑ɔ ɔɔɔ?

ref: You will do all right, if you obey the most important law in the Scriptures. It is the law that commands us to love others as much as we love ourselves.
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ gya Wuribware̱ a kuwure a mbraa ne̱ m bo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ ne̱ ŋ yɛ, “Kpa fo̱ bɛɛko̱ ase̱ŋ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kpa fo̱ e̱ye̱e̱‑ɔ,” se̱‑ɔ e̱ waa atɔ timaa ne̱e̱.

ref: But if you treat some people better than others, you have done wrong, and the Scriptures teach that you have sinned.
text: Amaa ɔke̱maa ne̱ o teŋŋi‑ro ɔ de̱e̱re̱ se̱sɛ kayo̱wɔre̱ se̱ pwɛɛ dɛɛ waa ko̱tɔko̱ sa mò̱‑ɔ e̱ waa e̱bɔye̱ ne̱e̱, ne̱ mbraa‑ɔ mɔ i bu mò̱ ke̱pɔ.

ref: If you obey every law except one, you are still guilty of breaking them all.
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ o kye Wuribware̱ a mbraa‑ɔ ŋko̱ŋko̱ kpeŋ‑o pini fe̱yɛ oo gyi ke̱pɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mbraa pɛɛɛ a waa ŋko̱ŋko̱.

ref: The same God who told us to be faithful in marriage also told us not to murder. So even if you are faithful in marriage, but murder someone, you still have broken God's Law.
text: Wuribware̱ ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Fo̱ ma waa kakye̱e̱kpa na kanyare̱kpa”‑ɔ dɛɛ ya tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ ma mɔɔ se̱sɛ.” Ne̱ ɔke̱maa e̱ mo̱ŋ waa kakye̱e̱kpa, ne̱ mò̱ e̱ gye̱ se̱sɛ ne̱ ɔ mɔɔ‑ɔ, ɔ saŋ ɔ gye̱ mbraa‑ɔ ɔfo̱po̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: Do this, because on the day of judgment there will be no pity for those who have not had pity on others. But even in judgment, God is merciful! Faith and Works
text: A kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ ɔse̱ŋgyipo̱‑ɔ maa su ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ su mò̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ se̱‑ɔ se̱, amaa mò̱ ne̱ o su se̱sɛ se̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ se̱re̱ se̱ŋgyi‑o kufu. E̱wɛɛ kuŋu kyɔ ke̱pɔ kibu ke̱yaale̱ŋ. Ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi na ke̱mo̱ kusuŋ

ref: My friends, what good is it to say you have faith, when you don't do anything to show you really do have faith? Can this kind of faith save you?
text: Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, ɔko̱ e̱ yɛ ɔɔ kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ gyi, na mò̱‑ɔ mɔ maa waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ ɔɔ kɔɔre̱ gyi‑o, ntɔ to̱nɔ ne̱ ɔɔ nya? Ane̱ŋ a ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi‑o e̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ mò̱ aaa?

ref: If you know someone who doesn't have any clothes or food,
text: Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ taa fe̱yɛ ane̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana Kristoo awuye ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ de atɔ buŋsɛ na atɔ gyisɛ.

ref: you shouldn't just say, “I hope all goes well for you. I hope you will be warm and have plenty to eat.” What good is it to say this, unless you do something to help?
text: Ke̱tɔ timaa mo̱ ne̱ fo̱ a waa fe̱yɛ fo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Wuribware̱ yure fo̱, na ɔ sa a fo̱ gyi nɛɛnɛɛ”, na fo̱‑ɔ fo̱ mo̱ŋ sa mò̱ ateese? Ne̱ ke̱tɔ timaa mo̱ ne̱ fo̱ a be̱e̱ waa fe̱yɛ kpaŋkpaŋ‑nɔ afwii ya ba, na fo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Wuribware̱ sa a afwii ma nya fo̱”, na fo̱‑ɔ fo̱ mo̱ŋ sa mò̱ atɔ buŋsɛ?

ref: Faith that doesn't lead us to do good deeds is all alone and dead!
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ fo̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ ba fo̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi bo̱ waa ke̱mo̱ kusuŋ, amɔ kaa wu ne̱e̱.

ref: Suppose someone disagrees and says, “It is possible to have faith without doing kind deeds.” I would answer, “Prove that you have faith without doing kind deeds, and I will prove that I have faith by doing them.”
text: Amaa mo̱ne̱ ɔko̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ɔ kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ o gyi, ne̱ ɔko̱ mɔ e̱ waa itimaa. To, se̱sɛ‑ɔ kaapo̱ mo̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ taare̱ a ɔ kɔɔre̱ gyi, na ɔ ma waa itimaa‑o, na mo̱‑ɔ mɔ m ba mo̱ itimaa bo̱ kaapo̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ mo̱ i gyi.

ref: You surely believe there is only one God. That's fine. Even demons believe this, and it makes them shake with fear.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ɔke̱maa e̱ kɔɔre̱ o gyi fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ ko̱ŋko̱ e̱ bo̱‑rɔ aaa? Mo̱ne̱ ya kɔɔre̱ waa‑rɔ fe̱raa, a bware. Mo̱nꞌ pini fe̱yɛ ibrisi pɛɛɛ gbaa e̱ kɔɔre̱ ii gyi fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ bo̱‑rɔ, ne̱ e̱mo̱ ke̱se̱re̱ mò̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, e̱e̱ yo̱ŋ bake̱ bake̱ bake̱.

ref: This is what the Scriptures mean by saying, “Abraham had faith in God, and God accepted him.” That's how Abraham became God's friend.
text: Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ ase̱ŋ ne̱ baa tɔwe̱‑ɔ a ba kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ, “Abraham a kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ a ase̱ŋ‑ɔ gyi‑o si‑o, Wuribware̱ a kɔɔre̱ mò̱ waa‑rɔ fe̱yɛ ɔ gye̱ se̱sɛ timaa,” ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ mò̱ nyare̱.

ref: You can now see that we please God by what we do and not only by what we believe.
text: Ne̱ ŋkee fe̱raa ɔke̱maa a ŋu fe̱yɛ se̱sɛ asuŋ timaa ne̱ ɔɔ waa‑ɔ se̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ mò̱ o gyi, amaa n gye̱ mò̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi‑o si wo̱re̱.

ref: Anyone who doesn't breathe is dead, and faith that doesn't do anything is just as dead!
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ane̱ŋ ne̱ kayo̱wɔre̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ de kra, ne̱ kaa wu‑o, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi e̱ mo̱ŋ de asuŋ timaa, ne̱ kaa wu.

ref: My friends, we should not all try to become teachers. In fact, teachers will be judged more strictly than others.
text: Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ana, mo̱ne̱ ɔko̱ e̱ kpa a o kii abware̱se̱ŋ ɔkaapo̱po̱, ɔ sa mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ se̱. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔse̱ŋgyipo̱ Wuribware̱ i bise ane̱ ne̱ ane̱ gye̱ abware̱se̱ŋ akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ ase̱ŋ a ɔ kyo̱ŋ ɔke̱maa, na ane̱ lee kanɔ gywii mò̱.

ref: It takes strong winds to move a large sailing ship, but the captain uses only a small rudder to make it go in any direction.
text: Mo̱nꞌ be̱e̱ ke̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ko̱re̱e̱ e̱ kyɔ ke̱dabe̱, ne̱ afwii de̱maŋte̱ da kanɔ ŋke̱maa, katɛɛrɛɛ ko̱ŋko̱ e̱ taare̱ ba ke̱baŋ bo̱ kii mò̱ yaa ke̱bare̱ ke̱maa ne̱ ɔ kpa‑ɔ.

ref: Our tongues are small too, and yet they brag about big things. It takes only a spark to start a forest fire!
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ kanɔ gye̱ kataapo̱ gyingyii ne̱e̱. Ne̱ ka kyo̱rɔ kamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kaa waa atɔ dabe̱ dabe̱. Amaa nsɛ e̱ taare̱ a ɔ be̱ra kamo̱? Mo̱nꞌ be̱e̱ ke̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ke̱po̱ŋ kyɔ, ne̱ kafugyee e̱ taare̱ a ka kywɛɛ ke̱mo̱‑ɔ.

ref: The tongue is like a spark. It is an evil power that dirties the rest of the body and sets a person's entire life on fire with flames that come from hell itself.
text: Kanɔ be̱e̱ ka du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ de̱e̱kpa ne̱ ɔ kywɛɛ se̱sɛ kayo̱wɔre̱‑rɔ ɔ nye̱ra kamo̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ ka sa ne̱ ɔ waa e̱bɔye̱, ne̱ e̱e̱ sa ne̱ mò̱ kayo̱wɔre̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ i kii iyisi‑o. Ka sa ne̱ mò̱ kakye̱na‑rɔ e̱ kywɛɛ de̱e̱kpa, de̱e̱kpa ne̱ Ɔbɔnsam fɔŋfɔŋ a kure‑o.
