text: Ne̱ kabo̱ amo̱ a nya kpa, ne̱ kaa fwii ŋe waa ke̱fo̱re̱ ne̱ kaa le̱e̱ mpase̱ wuye‑o e̱mo̱rɔbɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ kaa nya ŋkpa. Ne̱ kaa sa kanɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ mɔɔ ɔke̱maa ne̱ oo kine kamo̱ kusuŋ‑o.

ref: All people were forced to put a mark on their right hand or forehead. Whether they were powerful or weak, rich or poor, free people or slaves,
text: Ne̱ kabo̱ amo̱ a ye̱re̱ abre̱sɛ na ŋyaagyi, na atɔ awuye na atiripo̱, na agyi na anya, fe̱yɛ bo̱ kyaŋ bamo̱ agyiselɔkwɛɛ‑rɔ bɛɛɛ bamo̱ nsɛkpe̱e̱nɔ nkyane̱e̱.

ref: they all had to have this mark, or else they could not buy or sell anything. This mark stood for the name of the beast and for the number of its name.
text: Ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ de kpa fe̱yɛ o fe atɔ bɛɛɛ ɔ sɔɔ atɔ, amɔ baa kyaŋ mò̱ kigyiselɔkwɛɛ‑rɔ bɛɛɛ mò̱ kasɛkpe̱e̱nɔ kakyane̱e̱. Kakyane̱e̱ amo̱ e̱ gye̱ kabo̱ amo̱ ke̱nyare̱ bɛɛɛ akyo̱rɛɛgyi ne̱ a ye̱re̱ bo̱ sa mò̱ ke̱nyare̱‑ɔ.

ref: You need wisdom to understand the number of the beast! But if you are smart enough, you can figure this out. Its number is
text: Ɔko̱ i de kanyiase̱ŋ, ɔ taare̱ a o nu kabo̱ amo̱ akyo̱rɛɛgyii‑o kaase̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ a ye̱re̱ bo̱ sa nyiŋkpasɛ ke̱nyare̱ ne̱e̱. Akyo̱rɛɛgyii amo̱ e̱ gye̱ alafa asiye na adusiye na asiye (

ref: I looked and saw the Lamb standing on Mount Zion! With him were
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a de̱e̱re̱, ne̱ mo̱ a ŋu kasanne̱gyii‑o ye̱re̱ Siyɔŋ ke̱be̱e̱‑ɔ se̱. Mò̱ aa anyare̱ ŋkpe̱ŋ ke̱lafa na aduna na ana (

ref: Then I heard a sound from heaven that was like a roaring flood or loud thunder or even like the music of harps.
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a nu bo̱re̱ ko̱ a su le̱e̱ so̱so̱ fe̱yɛ awuriwuri‑o, bɛɛɛ fe̱yɛ bware̱ a su‑ro gburi gburi‑o. Bo̱re̱ amo̱ a be̱e̱ su ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kwaŋgyi adapo̱ ko̱ a da ikwaŋgyi ba soori‑o.

ref: And a new song was being sung in front of God's throne and in front of the four living creatures and the elders. No one could learn that song, except the
text: Ne̱ anyare̱ ŋkpe̱ŋ ke̱lafa na aduna na ana amo̱ ye̱re̱ kuwuregya‑ɔ na mbo̱ nna‑ɔ na abre̱sɛ‑ɔ akatɔ‑rɔ ba waa kiliŋ po̱pwɛɛ ko̱ ne̱ ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ɔ taare̱ a o suye‑o, amɔ anyare̱ ŋkpe̱ŋ ke̱lafa na aduna na ana amo̱ ne̱ kasanne̱gyii‑o a mo̱rɔwe̱ bamo̱ le̱e̱ kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ wo̱re̱.

ref: All of these are pure virgins, and they follow the Lamb wherever he leads. They have been rescued to be presented to God and the Lamb as the most precious people on earth.
text: Bamo̱ a gye̱ anyare̱ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi akye̱e̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, bamo̱ mfaanɛɛ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ bo̱ gya kasanne̱gyii‑o se̱ to̱ŋ ke̱maa ne̱ ɔ yɔ‑ɔ. Bamo̱ ne̱ ɔɔ gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ mo̱rɔwe̱, ne̱ ɔɔ taa bamo̱ bo̱ waa Wuribware̱ na mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ‑ɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: They never tell lies, and they are innocent. The Messages of the Three Angels
text: Bo̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ ba aye̱ba kake ŋko̱ se̱. Ne̱ bo̱ be̱e̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ de ke̱bɔye̱ ko̱. Wuribware̱ mbɔɔ nsa ko̱

ref: I saw another angel. This one was flying across the sky and had the eternal good news to announce to the people of every race, tribe, language, and nation on earth.
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a be̱e̱ ŋu Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ko̱, na o fuwi afwii‑ro, ne̱ baa suŋ mò̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ timaa bo̱ le̱e̱ ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ se̱ gywii ase̱sɛ ne̱ baa le̱e̱ kasuro ke̱maa‑rɔ na maŋ ke̱maa‑rɔ na kade̱ ke̱maa se̱, ne̱ ba sa e̱se̱ŋsa ke̱maa‑ɔ.

ref: The angel shouted, “Worship and honor God! The time has come for him to judge everyone. Kneel down before the one who created heaven and earth, the oceans, and every stream.”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ faa‑rɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ bu Wuribware̱, na mo̱nꞌ kyo̱rɔ mò̱ ke̱dabe̱‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ saŋ a fo̱ fe̱yɛ o gyi se̱sɛ dimaadi ase̱ŋ. Mo̱nꞌ suŋ mò̱ ne̱ ɔɔ twe̱e̱ so̱so̱ na kaase̱ na ɔpo̱o̱ na e̱bo̱ŋ‑nɔ nkyu‑o.”

ref: A second angel followed and said, “The great city of Babylon has fallen! This is the city that made all nations drunk and immoral. Now God is angry, and Babylon has fallen.”
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ nyɔse̱po̱ a gya kabɔɔ gye̱ŋkpɛɛsɛ‑ɔ se̱, na ɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Baa kpuri opurimakye̱e̱ dabe̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Babilɔŋ‑ɔ. Mò̱ ya sa ne̱ kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ kpa mò̱ ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ, na bo̱ mo̱ŋ taare̱ kine mò̱, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔtabwe̱e̱po̱ maa taare̱ a o kine nta‑ɔ.”

ref: Finally, a third angel came and shouted: Here is what will happen if you worship the beast and the idol and have the mark of the beast on your hand or forehead.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ sase̱po̱ a gya kabɔɔ gye̱ŋkpɛɛsɛ‑ɔ na kanyɔse̱po̱‑ɔ se̱, na ɔ tɔwe̱ keŋkeŋ fe̱yɛ, “A waa le̱ŋ a sa ɔke̱maa ne̱ o suŋ mpase̱ wuye‑o na ke̱fo̱re̱ serisɛ ne̱ kaa le̱e̱ mò̱‑ɔ, na mò̱ ne̱ baa kyaŋ kabo̱‑ɔ kakyane̱e̱‑ɔ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ mò̱ kasɛkpe̱e̱nɔ bɛɛɛ mò̱ kigyiselɔkwɛɛ‑rɔ se̱‑ɔ.

ref: You will have to drink the wine that God gives to everyone who makes him angry. You will feel his mighty anger, and you will be tortured with fire and burning sulfur, while the holy angels and the Lamb look on.
text: Wuribware̱ duŋ a fwii bamo̱ se̱, ne̱ o gyiiri bamo̱ ke̱se̱bɔ. A du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ a se̱sɛ‑ɔ a nuu nta le̱ŋle̱ŋsɛ‑ɔ. Ane̱ŋ a nta‑ɔ e̱ waa preepree mò̱ kame‑ro. Ba ba kafirewuta a bo̱ de̱e̱ bo̱ kywɛɛ bamo̱, na ka ma duŋ, Wuribware̱ a mbɔɔ‑ɔ na kasanne̱gyii‑o akatɔ‑rɔ, na bo̱ gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: If you worship the beast and the idol and accept the mark of its name, you will be tortured day and night. The smoke from your torture will go up forever and ever, and you will never be able to rest.
text: De̱e̱kpa amo̱ ne̱ ɔ kywɛɛ bamo̱‑ɔ e̱bɔɔre̱e̱ e̱ yɔ so̱so̱, na e̱ maa yɔwe̱ kake ŋko̱ se̱ daa. Ɔke̱maa ne̱ o suŋ kabo̱‑ɔ na ke̱tɔ serisɛ ne̱ kaa le̱e̱ mò̱‑ɔ, bɛɛɛ ɔke̱maa ne̱ baa kyaŋ kabo̱‑ɔ kakyane̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ mò̱ se̱‑ɔ maa nya mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a ɔ le̱e̱ de̱e̱kpa‑ɔ‑rɔ kanye na mpase̱ pɛɛɛ.”

ref: God's people must learn to endure. They must also obey his commands and have faith in Jesus.
text: Amo̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ ne̱ bo̱ gya mò̱ mbraa se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ba kya se̱ ba kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu ba gyi‑o, ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ nyite gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ.

ref: Then I heard a voice from heaven say, “Put this in writing. From now on, the Lord will bless everyone who has faith in him when they die.” The Spirit answered, “Yes, they will rest from their hard work, and they will be rewarded for what they have done.” The Earth Is Harvested
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a nu bo̱re̱ ko̱ a le̱e̱ so̱so̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Kyo̱rɛɛ fe̱yɛ, ‘Bamo̱ ne̱ ba wu le̱e̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ bo̱ yɔ, na bo̱ mo̱ŋ yɔwe̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu si ke̱gya‑ɔ e̱ nya ŋyure.’ ” Ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o a be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ, “Kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱e̱. Bamo̱ asuŋ keŋkeŋ ne̱ baa waa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ba nya amo̱ kukyure kɔne̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ i nyiŋŋi asuŋ timaa ne̱ baa waa‑ɔ se̱.” Swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ adɔɔrɔtɔ ke̱kpa

ref: I looked and saw a bright cloud, and someone who seemed to be the Son of Man was sitting on the cloud. He wore a gold crown on his head and held a sharp sickle in his hand.
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a de̱e̱re̱, ne̱ mo̱ a ŋu ko̱wo̱re̱ fufuri. Ɔko̱ te ko̱wo̱re̱‑ɔ se̱, na o du fe̱yɛ ane̱ dimaadi‑o, na o buŋ atanne̱ pipee kuwurepa, na o de loogyi yayasɛ ko̱ mò̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ.

ref: The one on the cloud swung his sickle and harvested the crops.
text: Mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱ ne̱ o te ko̱wo̱re̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ a teyi mò̱ loogyi‑o bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ swe̱e̱re̱ se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ te̱ŋŋe̱ mò̱ se̱ a atɔ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ.

ref: Another angel with a sharp sickle then came out of the temple in heaven.
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a be̱e̱ ŋu Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ a le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa mfe̱ŋ, na mò̱‑ɔ mɔ de loogyi yayasɛ ane̱ŋ dɛɛ.

ref: After this, an angel with power over fire came from the altar and shouted to the angel who had the sickle. He said, “All grapes on earth are ripe! Harvest them with your sharp sickle.”
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ ne̱ mò̱‑ɔ mɔ mò̱ e̱ de̱e̱re̱ lɔŋŋɔkpa a de̱e̱kpa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ a le̱e̱ lɔŋŋɔkpa‑ɔ ase̱ ba, ne̱ ɔɔ faa‑rɔ keŋkeŋ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ne̱ o de loogyi yayasɛ‑ɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ba fo̱ loogyi‑o bo̱ tee kaye̱‑rɔ a bobe ayii agyi‑o, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ aa pee.”

ref: The angel swung his sickle on earth and cut off its grapes. He threw them into a pit where they were trampled on as a sign of God's anger.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ‑ɔ a teyi mò̱ ke̱sare̱e̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ swe̱e̱re̱ se̱, ne̱ oo tee bobe agyi‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ twe̱e̱ amo̱ waa ke̱maŋtaŋ dabe̱ ne̱ ke̱ bo̱ Yɛro̱salɛm nlaŋbɛɛ‑ɔ‑rɔ, na Wuribware̱ nya sa a bo̱ kyikye amo̱ se̱, na ke̱tɔ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ duŋ a fwii abɔye̱waapo̱ se̱.

ref: The pit was outside the city, and when the grapes were mashed, blood flowed out. The blood turned into a river that was about
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa kyikye kyikye bobe agyi amo̱ se̱ ke̱maŋtaŋ dabe̱‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ mbo̱gya a le̱e̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ bo̱ waa fe̱yɛ kufwiiri‑o, ne̱ ke̱mo̱ e̱swe̱e̱ a fo̱ e̱maye̱re̱ alafa anyɔ, ne̱ ke̱mo̱ keŋ i mwiire se̱sɛ so̱swe̱e̱ ke̱maa mɔ.

ref: After this, I looked at the sky and saw something else that was strange and important. Seven angels were bringing the seven last terrible troubles. When these are ended, God will no longer be angry.
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a ŋu afutɔ dabe̱ dabe̱ ko̱ so̱so̱ ne̱ a kyɔ, ne̱ a kpe̱ŋ e̱ye̱e̱‑ɔ. Wuribware̱ mbɔɔ nsunoo ne̱ ɔke̱maa de ase̱ŋ ko̱ŋko̱‑ɔ ye̱re̱. Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ lalalo̱we̱ lee‑o, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ amo̱ ya lo̱we̱, Wuribware̱ kaduŋfwii e̱ lo̱we̱.

ref: Then I saw something that looked like a glass sea mixed with fire, and people were standing on it. They were the ones who had defeated the beast and the idol and the number that tells the name of the beast. God had given them harps,
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a ŋu ke̱tɔ ne̱ ku du fe̱yɛ ɔpo̱o̱, ne̱ ɔ da diŋŋ, ne̱ ɔ to̱re̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ diki‑o, na ɔ kywɛɛ fe̱yɛ de̱e̱kpa‑ɔ. Ase̱sɛ ko̱ ye̱re̱ ɔpo̱o̱ amo̱ kɛɛ. Mo̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ bo̱ gye̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa kine kusuŋ mpase̱ wuye‑o na mò̱ ke̱tɔ serisɛ ne̱ kaa le̱e̱ mò̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa kine fe̱yɛ bo̱ kyaŋ mò̱ ke̱nyare̱ kakyane̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ bamo̱ se̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ bo̱ ye̱re̱ bo̱ de ikwaŋgyi ne̱ Wuribware̱ a sa bamo̱‑ɔ.

ref: and they were singing the song his servant Moses and the Lamb had sung. They were singing, “Lord God All-Powerful, you have done great and marvelous things. You are the ruler of all nations, and you do what is right and fair.
text: Ne̱ ba waa kiliŋ ne̱ ku du fe̱yɛ kiliŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱ Mosis a waa bo̱ kye Igyipiti awuye‑o. Ke̱ gye̱ kiliŋ ne̱ kasanne̱gyii‑o a sa bamo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ba waa. Ba waa ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “Bo̱-e̱le̱ŋ‑ɔ‑kyo̱ŋ‑ɔke̱maa-Bware̱, ke̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ kyɔ, ne̱ fo̱ awaasɛ e̱ kpe̱ŋ e̱ye̱e̱‑ɔ. E̱maŋ e̱maŋ pɛɛɛ Owure, ke̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ e̱kpa bware, ne̱ e̱ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o.

ref: Lord, who doesn't honor and praise your name? You alone are holy, and all nations will come and worship you, because you have shown that you judge with fairness.”
text: Wuribware̱, nsɛ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ maa se̱re̱ fo̱, ne̱ o kine ko̱kyo̱rɔ fo̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ ke̱dabe̱ ne̱ fo̱ de‑o si? Fo̱ wo̱re̱ e̱ gye̱ ɔkpe̱yaa, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ e̱ ba a bo̱ bo̱ kpuni aŋurii fo̱ ayaa‑rɔ, na bo̱ suŋ fo̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ baa ŋu fe̱yɛ fo̱ awaasɛ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱e̱.”

ref: After this, I noticed something else in heaven. The sacred tent used for a temple was open.
text: Amo̱‑ɔ kamɛɛ‑ɔ ne̱ mo̱ a ŋu Wuribware̱ suŋkpa ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ ke̱kyaŋ ne̱ e̱taŋ a ke̱dakaa‑ɔ ye̱re̱‑rɔ‑ɔ a taye̱.

ref: And the seven angels who were bringing the terrible troubles were coming out of it. They were dressed in robes of pure white linen and wore belts made of pure gold.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ a mbɔɔ nsunoo ne̱ bo̱ de ase̱ŋ asunoo‑o a le̱e̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ‑rɔ, na bo̱ buŋ kyefuri e̱kaare̱ pare̱pare̱pare̱ ne̱ e̱ to̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa ba atanne̱ pipee e̱ke̱re̱se̱sɛ bo̱ te̱ŋ te̱ŋ akaŋ se̱.

ref: One of the four living creatures gave each of the seven angels a bowl made of gold. These bowls were filled with the anger of God who lives forever and ever.
text: Ne̱ mbo̱ nna‑ɔ‑rɔ ɔko̱ a sa Wuribware̱ a mbɔɔ‑ɔ ɔke̱maa atanne̱ pipee kakyaŋse̱gyii. Nkyaŋse̱gyii‑o kake̱maa, nta preepreesɛ ya bo̱rɔ, ne̱ ŋ ye̱re̱ bo̱ sa Wuribware̱ ne̱ ɔ maa wu daa‑ɔ kaduŋfwii.

ref: The temple quickly filled with smoke from the glory and power of God. No one could enter it until the seven angels had finished pouring out the seven last troubles.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ ke̱dabe̱ na ke̱yaale̱ŋ a sa ne̱ e̱bɔɔre̱e̱ a bo̱rɔ mò̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ, na ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ taare̱ lweero mfe̱ŋ bo̱ fo̱ saŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ mbɔɔ nsunoo‑o a baa ase̱ŋ asunoo‑o lalalo̱we̱ pwɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: From the temple I heard a voice shout to the seven angels, “Go and empty the seven bowls of God's anger on the earth.”
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a nu bo̱re̱ ko̱ a le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ‑rɔ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa o gywii Wuribware̱ mbɔɔ nsunoo‑o fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ ya twiiri nta preepreesɛ ne̱ ŋ ye̱re̱ bo̱ sa Wuribware̱ a kaduŋfwii‑o, ne̱ m bo̱ nkyaŋse̱gyii isunoo‑o‑ro‑o we̱e̱ swe̱e̱re̱ se̱.”

ref: The first angel emptied his bowl on the earth. At once ugly and painful sores broke out on everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol.
text: Ne̱ kabɔɔ gye̱ŋkpɛɛsɛ‑ɔ a ya twiiri mò̱ lee‑o we̱e̱ swe̱e̱re̱ se̱. Ne̱ e̱lɔ dabe̱ gyagyasɛ a te̱ŋŋe̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ de mpase̱ wuye‑o ke̱nyare̱ bamo̱ nsɛkpe̱e̱nɔ se̱ bɛɛɛ bamo̱ agyiselɔkwɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ ba suŋ ke̱tɔ serisɛ ne̱ kaa le̱e̱ mpase̱ wuye‑o.

ref: The second angel emptied his bowl on the sea. At once the sea turned into blood like that of a dead person, and every living thing in the sea died.
text: Ne̱ kabɔɔ nyɔse̱po̱‑ɔ a twiiri mò̱ lee‑o we̱e̱ ɔpo̱o̱ se̱. Ne̱ nkyu a kii fe̱yɛ mbo̱gya kuri‑o. Ne̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ke̱ bo̱ nkyu‑o‑ro‑o a wu.

ref: The third angel emptied his bowl into the rivers and streams. At once they turned to blood.
text: Ne̱ kabɔɔ sase̱po̱‑ɔ a twiiri mò̱ lee‑o we̱e̱ e̱bo̱ŋ na atiribu pɛɛɛ‑rɔ, ne̱ amo̱‑rɔ nkyu‑o pɛɛɛ a kii mbo̱gya.

ref: Then I heard the angel, who has power over water, say, “You have always been, and you always will be the holy God. You had the right to judge in this way.
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a nu Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ne̱ ɔ de̱e̱re̱ nkyu pɛɛɛ se̱‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ a gyi ase̱sɛ ase̱ŋ kpa se̱, fo̱ ne̱ fo̱ gye̱ Bɔye̱-ko̱-mo̱ŋ‑te-mò̱‑se̱-Bware̱‑ɔ, ne̱ fo̱ a kye̱na le̱e̱ hare̱e̱ de̱daa‑ɔ.

ref: They poured out the blood of your people and your prophets. So you gave them blood to drink as they deserve!”
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ya sa ne̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱sɛ na mò̱ akyaamɛɛ mbo̱gya a twiiri we̱e̱. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ fo̱ a sa bamo̱ mbo̱gya fe̱yɛ bo̱ nuu. Amo̱ ne̱ a bware fe̱yɛ bo̱ sa bamo̱‑ɔ dɛɛ ne̱ baa nya‑ɔ.”

ref: After this, I heard the altar shout, “Yes, Lord God All-Powerful, your judgments are honest and fair.”
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a nu bo̱re̱ ko̱ a le̱e̱ lɔŋŋɔkpa‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ɛhɛɛɛŋ, Bo̱-e̱le̱ŋ‑ɔ‑kyo̱ŋ‑ɔke̱maa-Bware̱! Fo̱ ase̱ŋ kigyi bu ase̱sɛ ke̱pɔ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ!”

ref: The fourth angel emptied his bowl on the sun, and it began to scorch people like fire.
text: Ne̱ kabɔɔ nase̱po̱‑ɔ a twiiri mò̱ lee‑o we̱e̱ kyɔwe̱ se̱, ne̱ baa sa kyɔwe̱ kpa fe̱yɛ mò̱ agyabreŋ na kipeŋpeŋ kywɛɛ ase̱sɛ.

ref: Everyone was scorched by its great heat, and all of them cursed the name of God who had power over these terrible troubles. But no one turned to God and praised him.
text: Ne̱ kyɔwe̱ kipeŋpeŋ a kywɛɛ bamo̱. Ne̱ baa da Wuribware̱ yii, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ o de ase̱ŋ mɔ se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ. Amaa bo̱ mo̱ŋ kiŋŋi le̱e̱ bamo̱ e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ, na bo̱ kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱ a ke̱dabe̱‑ɔ.

ref: The fifth angel emptied his bowl on the throne of the beast. At once darkness covered its kingdom, and its people began biting their tongues in pain.
text: Ne̱ kabɔɔ nuuse̱po̱‑ɔ a twiiri mò̱ lee‑o we̱e̱ mpase̱ wuye a kawuregya‑ɔ se̱. Ne̱ kibugyii a da mò̱ swe̱e̱re̱‑ɔ se̱, ne̱ ase̱sɛ a duŋwi bamo̱ nnɔ bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ a bo̱ bamo̱ gya‑ɔ se̱.

ref: And because of their painful sores, they cursed the God who rules in heaven. But still they did not stop doing evil things.
text: Ne̱ baa da Wuribware̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ yii bo̱ le̱e̱ bamo̱ e̱lɔ na ane̱ŋ ne̱ bamo̱ ŋyo̱wɔre̱ bo̱ bamo̱ gya‑ɔ se̱. Amaa bo̱ mo̱ŋ kiŋŋi le̱e̱ bamo̱ e̱kpa bɔye̱ ne̱ bo̱ gya se̱‑ɔ se̱.

ref: The sixth angel emptied his bowl on the great Euphrates River, and it completely dried up to make a road for the kings from the east.
text: Ne̱ kabɔɔ siyese̱po̱‑ɔ a twiiri mò̱ lee‑o we̱e̱ Yufraatis bo̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱. Ne̱ bo̱ŋ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a wo̱re̱‑rɔ fo̱ŋfo̱ŋfo̱ŋ, na awure ne̱ ba le̱e̱ kyɔwe̱ le̱e̱kpa a ke̱be̱gya‑ɔ nya nya kpa bo̱rɔ‑rɔ kyo̱ŋ.

ref: An evil spirit that looked like a frog came out of the mouth of the dragon. One also came out of the mouth of the beast, and another out of the mouth of the false prophet.
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a ŋu ibrisi e̱sa ko̱ ne̱ i du fe̱yɛ apre̱m‑ɔ, ne̱ e̱mo̱ ɔke̱maa a le̱e̱ daŋkyam‑ɔ kanɔ‑rɔ na mpase̱ wuye‑o kanɔ‑rɔ, na ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ ba aye̱ba fe̱yɛ ɔ gye̱ Wuribware̱ a akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ ɔko̱ ne̱e̱‑ɔ kanɔ‑rɔ.

ref: These evil spirits had the power to work miracles. They went to every king on earth, to bring them together for a war against God All-Powerful. But that will be the day of God's great victory.
text: Ane̱ŋ a ibrisi mɔ mo̱ŋ bo̱ daŋ pɛɛɛ, amaa e̱e̱ taare̱ a e̱ waa atɔ ne̱ a kpe̱ŋ e̱ye̱e̱, ne̱ a sa a kufu nya ase̱sɛ‑ɔ. Ne̱ ibrisi amo̱ e̱ kaa e̱ yɔ kaye̱‑rɔ awure‑o pɛɛɛ ase̱, na e̱ kpɔwe̱ bamo̱ bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱ a bo̱ waa siraa gywii kake dabe̱ ne̱ bamo̱ aa Bo̱-e̱le̱ŋ‑ɔ‑kyo̱ŋ‑ɔke̱maa-Bware̱ e̱ kɔ ke̱naa‑ɔ.

ref: Remember Christ says, “When I come, it will surprise you like a thief! But God will bless you, if you are awake and ready. Then you won't have to walk around naked and be ashamed.”
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a nu bo̱re̱ ko̱ a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ laatɔ a mo̱nꞌ nu! Mo̱ i fu‑ro a m ba fe̱yɛ oyu‑o! Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ kra idi a ɔ kye̱na ke̱e̱ mò̱ atɔ buŋsɛ se̱, na ɔ ma nya nare̱ ke̱te̱bɔŋburoŋ na ipeere nya mò̱ lamaŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ e̱ nya ŋyure.”

ref: Those armies came together in a place that in Hebrew is called Armagedon.
text: Ne̱ ibrisi amo̱ a kpɔwe̱ awure‑o bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱ to̱ŋ ko̱ ne̱ Heebrii se̱ŋsa‑rɔ ba te̱e̱ ɛ Aamagadɔŋ‑ɔ.

ref: As soon as the seventh angel emptied his bowl in the air, a loud voice from the throne in the temple shouted, “It's done!”
text: Ne̱ kabɔɔ sunoose̱po̱‑ɔ a twiiri mò̱ lee‑o bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ awo̱re̱‑rɔ. Ne̱ bo̱re̱ a su keŋkeŋ le̱e̱ kuwuregya ne̱ ke̱ ye̱re̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ke̱tɔ a lo̱we̱!”

ref: The great city of Babylon split into three parts, and the cities of other nations fell. So God made Babylon drink from the wine cup filled with his anger.
text: Ne̱ maŋ dabe̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Babilɔŋ‑ɔ a ke‑ro ntuŋ nsa, ne̱ kaye̱‑rɔ a e̱maŋ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a nye̱ra. Wuribware̱ a nyiŋŋi Babilɔŋ na mò̱ e̱kpa bɔye̱ ne̱ ɔɔ gya se̱‑ɔ se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ oo nuu nta preepreesɛ ne̱ ŋ ye̱re̱ bo̱ sa mò̱, Wuribware̱, kaduŋfwii‑o.

ref: Every island ran away, and the mountains disappeared.
text: Ne̱ ikwii pɛɛɛ yii, ne̱ abe̱e̱ pɛɛɛ a ŋaa.

ref: One of the seven angels who had emptied the bowls came over and said to me, “Come on! I will show you how God will punish that shameless prostitute who sits on many oceans.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ mbɔɔ nsunoo ne̱ bo̱ de nkyaŋse̱gyii‑o‑ro ɔko̱ a ba mo̱ ase̱ bo̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ba, na ŋ kaapo̱ fo̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ba gyiiri opurimakye̱e̱ dabe̱‑ɔ ke̱se̱bɔ‑ɔ. Opurimakye̱e̱‑ɔ ye̱re̱ bo̱ sa maŋ dabe̱ ko̱ ne̱ baa pwɛɛ bo̱ me̱raa e̱bo̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ.

ref: Every king on earth has slept with her, and her shameless ways are like wine that has made everyone on earth drunk.”
text: Mò̱ e̱ gye̱ mò̱ ne̱ mò̱ aa kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ a awure‑o maa se̱ ba waa kakye̱e̱kpa‑ɔ. Kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ e̱ kpa mò̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ, ne̱ bo̱ maa taare̱ a bo̱ kine mò̱, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ɔtabwe̱e̱po̱ maa taare̱ a o kine nta kunuu‑o.”

ref: With the help of the Spirit, the angel took me into a desert, where I saw a woman sitting on a red beast. The beast was covered with names that were an insult to God, and it had seven heads and ten horns.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a ywe̱e̱ mo̱ se̱, ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ‑ɔ a so̱rɔ mo̱ yaa kimukee‑ro, mfe̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ a ŋu ɔkye̱e̱ ko̱ te kabo̱ pipee ko̱ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ anyare̱ bɔye̱ ne̱ a saare̱ Wuribware̱‑ɔ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ mò̱ se̱‑ɔ se̱. Kabo̱ amo̱ de aŋu asunoo na akyɔre̱ kudu.

ref: The woman was dressed in purple and scarlet robes, and she wore jewelry made of gold, precious stones, and pearls. In her hand she held a gold cup filled with the filthy and nasty things she had done.
text: Ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ buŋ atɔ pipee na atanne̱ pipee mpini na agyito to̱re̱se̱sɛ. Ne̱ akukye‑ro ntaŋgyii dame̱naŋsɛ to̱re̱se̱sɛ maa mò̱ atɔ buŋsɛ pɛɛɛ se̱. Ne̱ o de atanne̱ pipee ko̱to̱re̱nkyu mò̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ. Atɔ bɔye̱ ke̱maa te‑ro, ne̱ a ye̱re̱ bo̱ sa kanyare̱kpa ne̱ ɔɔ waa‑ɔ.

ref: On her forehead a mysterious name was written: I AM THE GREAT CITY OF BABYLON, THE MOTHER OF EVERY IMMORAL AND FILTHY THING ON EARTH.
text: Ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ ke̱nyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ nyi ke̱mo̱ kaase̱‑ɔ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ mò̱ kasɛkpe̱e̱nɔ se̱, fe̱yɛ, “Babilɔŋ maŋ dabe̱‑ɔ, apurimakye̱e̱ na kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ a atɔ ne̱ a de iyisi‑o pɛɛɛ bamo̱ nyi.”

ref: I could tell that the woman was drunk on the blood of God's people who had given their lives for Jesus. This surprising sight amazed me,
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ ɔkye̱e̱ amo̱ a nuu nta bwe̱e̱. Nta ne̱ oo nuu‑o gye̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ baa mɔɔ bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ kine ke̱gya Yeesuu si‑o si a mbo̱gya‑ɔ. Mo̱ a ŋu mò̱‑ɔ, ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ mo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: and the angel said: Why are you so amazed? I will explain the mystery about this woman and about the beast she is sitting on, with its seven heads and ten horns.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ‑ɔ a bise mo̱ fe̱yɛ, “E̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ fo̱?” Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ na kabo̱ ne̱ ɔ so̱ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ, kabo̱ ne̱ o de aŋu asunoo na akyɔre̱ kudu‑o, akwe̱e̱rɔse̱ŋ a n gywii fo̱.

ref: The beast you saw is one that used to be and no longer is. It will come back from the deep pit, but only to be destroyed. Everyone on earth whose names were not written in the book of life before the time of creation will be amazed. They will see this beast that used to be and no longer is, but will be once more.
text: Kabo̱ amo̱ ne̱ fo̱ ke̱e̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ, nsaŋ‑ɔ baa ŋu kamo̱, ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ fe̱raa kaa wu, amaa ka le̱e̱ ke̱maŋtaŋ loorisɛ‑ɔ‑rɔ, na a maa kyee ba mɔɔ kamo̱. Ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ te kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ Wuribware̱ mo̱ŋ kyo̱rɛɛ bamo̱ anyare̱ bo̱ waa ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ de ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ hare̱e̱ pwɛɛ ne̱ ɔɔ dɛɛ twe̱e̱ kaye̱‑ɔ, bamo̱ ya ŋu kabo̱ na ka le̱e̱ ke̱maŋtaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ, e̱ye̱e̱ e̱ kpe̱ŋ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kabo̱‑ɔ dɛɛ ka bo̱‑rɔ, ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ fe̱raa kaa wu, amaa ka be̱e̱ ka ba ŋkpa‑rɔ.

ref: Anyone with wisdom can figure this out. The seven heads that the woman is sitting on stand for seven hills. These heads are also seven kings.
text: Fo̱ i nyi ase̱ŋ, fo̱ i nu atɔ mɔ kaase̱. Aŋu asunoo‑o ye̱re̱ bo̱ sa abe̱e̱ asunoo ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ te si‑o ne̱e̱. Ne̱ aŋu asunoo‑o be̱e̱ a ye̱re̱ bo̱ sa awure asunoo‑o.

ref: Five of the kings are dead. One is ruling now, and the other one has not yet come. But when he does, he will rule for only a little while.
text: Awure asunoo‑o‑ro anuu a kpuri, ne̱ ɔko̱ŋko̱ saŋ o gyi kuwure, ne̱ ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ saŋ‑ɔ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ ba. Mò̱ ya ba, o gyi kuwure kafo̱ŋfwe̱e̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: You also saw a beast that used to be and no longer is. That beast is one of the seven kings who will return as the eighth king, but only to be destroyed.
text: Ne̱ kabo̱ ne̱ ka dɛɛ ka bo̱‑rɔ, ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ fe̱raa kaa wu‑o, ye̱re̱ bo̱ sa owure buruwase̱po̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ ɔ gya asunoose̱po̱‑ɔ se̱, ne̱ ke̱tɔ ya waa kafwe̱e̱, o wu kpuri.

ref: The ten horns you saw are ten more kings, who have not yet come into power, and they will rule with the beast for only a short time.
text: Akyɔre̱ kudu ne̱ fo̱ a ŋu‑o ye̱re̱ bo̱ sa awure kudu ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ le̱e̱ gyi kuwure‑o, amaa bo̱ saŋ ba sa bamo̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ fe̱yɛ bo̱ gyi kuwure kabo̱ amo̱ ase̱ dɔŋhwe̱re̱e̱ ko̱ŋko̱.

ref: They all think alike and will give their power and authority to the beast.
text: Awure kudu mɔ pɛɛɛ de mfɛɛre̱ ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱e̱. Bamo̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ ne̱ baa nya‑ɔ pɛɛɛ, ba taa a bo̱ bo̱ sa kabo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: These kings will go to war against the Lamb. But he will defeat them, because he is Lord over all lords and King over all kings. His followers are chosen and special and faithful.
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ aa kasanne̱gyii‑o e̱ kɔ ke̱naa. Amaa kasanne̱gyii‑o na ase̱sɛ ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱, ne̱ oo lee bamo̱, ne̱ bo̱ gya mò̱ se̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o e̱ kɔ a bo̱ gyi bamo̱ se̱. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kasanne̱gyii‑o e̱ gye̱ anyaŋpe̱‑rɔ nyaŋpe̱ na awure‑ro owure.”

ref: The ten horns and the beast will start hating the shameless woman. They will strip off her clothes and leave her naked. Then they will eat her flesh and throw the rest of her body into a fire.
text: Akyɔre̱ kudu ne̱ fo̱ a ŋu‑o na kabo̱‑ɔ i kisi opurimakye̱e̱‑ɔ. Ba buŋŋi mò̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ o de‑o, na bo̱ yɔwe̱ mò̱ ke̱te̱bɔŋburoŋ bo̱ nare̱, na bo̱ wo̱ mò̱, na ŋkee bo̱ kywɛɛ mò̱ to̱ŋ ke̱maa ne̱ aa saŋ‑ɔ de̱e̱kpa‑rɔ.

ref: God is the one who made these kings all think alike and decide to give their power to the beast. And they will do this until what God has said comes true.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a sa ne̱ baa tii abɛɛ se̱, na bo̱ waa ke̱mo̱ ne̱ ɔ kpa‑ɔ, na bo̱ taa bamo̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ‑ɔ sa kabo̱‑ɔ, na ɔ bo̱ gyi kuwure bo̱ fo̱ saŋ ne̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ a waa kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o.

ref: The woman you saw is the great city that rules over all kings on earth.
text: Opurimakye̱e̱ ne̱ fo̱ a ŋu‑o ye̱re̱ bo̱ sa maŋ dabe̱ ne̱ mò̱ owure i gyi kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ awure awure pɛɛɛ mɔ se̱ kuwure‑o ne̱e̱.”

ref: I saw another angel come from heaven. This one had great power, and the earth was bright because of his glory.
text: Amo̱‑ɔ kamɛɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ a be̱e̱ ŋu Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ko̱, na ɔɔ le̱e̱ so̱so̱ ɔ ba. O de ke̱yaale̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa se̱. Ne̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ to̱re̱ se̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ɔɔ sa ne̱ swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ pɛɛɛ a laŋŋe̱‑rɔ.

ref: The angel shouted, “Fallen! Powerful Babylon has fallen and is now the home of demons. It is the den of every filthy spirit and of all unclean birds, and every dirty and hated animal.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ faa‑rɔ keŋkeŋ fe̱yɛ, “Oo kpuri. Babilɔŋ maŋ dabe̱ a kpuri ooo. Mbe̱yɔmɔ fe̱raa, oo kii ke̱taakpaŋ ne̱ ibrisi na ntimani naa‑rɔ‑ɔ, mfe̱ŋ ne̱ mbo̱gyii ne̱ ba kisi bamo̱ ko̱wo̱‑ɔ bo̱.

ref: Babylon's evil and immoral wine has made all nations drunk. Every king on earth has slept with her, and every merchant on earth is rich because of her evil desires.”
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kaye̱‑rɔ nde̱ se̱ awuye pɛɛɛ a nuu opurimakye̱e̱ a nta‑ɔ. Mò̱ nta amo̱ a sa ne̱ kaye̱‑rɔ awure pɛɛɛ a kpa mò̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Ne̱ bamo̱ aa mò̱ a di, ne̱ baa waa kakye̱e̱kpa. Ne̱ mò̱ tɔkpa ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ a sa ne̱ kaye̱‑rɔ ayawo̱gyipo̱ a nya bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱.”

ref: Then I heard another voice from heaven shout, “My people, you must escape from Babylon. Don't take part in her sins and share her punishment.
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a be̱e̱ nu bo̱re̱ ko̱ a le̱e̱ so̱so̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ ase̱sɛ eee, mo̱nꞌ le̱e̱ ba ooo, mo̱nꞌ le̱e̱ mò̱ ase̱ ba, na mo̱ne̱ ma nya waa mò̱ e̱bɔye̱‑ɔ e̱ko̱. Mo̱ne̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ le̱e̱ mò̱ ase̱, ke̱se̱bɔgyiiri ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ sa mò̱‑ɔ e̱ kyaŋŋaa mo̱ne̱ eee.

ref: Her sins are piled as high as heaven. God has remembered the evil she has done.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ e̱bɔye̱ da e̱ dɔŋ abɛɛ se̱, fe̱yɛ le̱e̱ mfe̱e̱ ya bo̱ fo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ Wuribware̱ be̱e̱ o nyiŋŋi mò̱ e̱kpa bɔye̱ ne̱ ɔɔ gya se̱‑ɔ se̱.

ref: Treat her as she has treated others. Make her pay double for what she has done. Make her drink twice as much of what she mixed for others.
text: Mo̱nꞌ waa mò̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ waa mo̱ne̱‑ɔ dɛɛ. Mo̱nꞌ waa iluwi e̱nyɔ bo̱ teere ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ waa mo̱ne̱‑ɔ. Mo̱nꞌ taa nta ne̱ n du preepree iluwi e̱nyɔ n kyo̱ŋ mmo̱ ne̱ ɔɔ sa mo̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a nuu‑o. Mo̱nꞌ sa mò̱ na o nuu.

ref: That woman honored herself with a life of luxury. Reward her now with suffering and pain. “Deep in her heart Babylon said, ‘I am the queen! Never will I be a widow or know what it means to be sad.’
text: Mo̱nꞌ sa a o ŋu ase̱ŋ ne̱ a kyɔ ke̱dabe̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ dɛɛ bu mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ke̱dabe̱‑ɔ. Na mo̱nꞌ waa mò̱ bo̱ro̱kɔɔne̱ ne̱ ɔ ta fe̱yɛ kɔne̱ ne̱ ɔɔ dɛɛ gyi‑o. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ saŋ amo̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱ mo̱ mfe̱e̱, mo̱, wurekye̱e̱. Ma kii kpenlekye̱e̱, na n su kale daa. Mo̱ bo̱re̱‑rɔ maa duŋwi mo̱ kake ko̱ se̱ daa.’

ref: And so, in a single day she will suffer the pain of sorrow, hunger, and death. Fire will destroy her dead body, because her judge is the powerful Lord God.”
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ kake ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱ ase̱ŋ e̱ to̱ mò̱. Ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ i wu, na bamo̱ ne̱ ba saŋ‑ɔ su kale, na ako̱ŋ mɔɔ bamo̱, na de̱e̱kpa kywɛɛ mò̱ e̱lɔŋ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Nyaŋpe̱ Wuribware̱ ɔse̱ŋgyipo̱‑ɔ de e̱le̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ.”

ref: Every king on earth who slept with her and shared in her luxury will mourn. They will weep, when they see the smoke from that fire.
text: Awure ne̱ bamo̱ aa mò̱ a di, ne̱ baa waa kakye̱e̱kpa, ne̱ baa gyi mò̱ kɔne̱‑ɔ ya ŋu de̱e̱kpa ne̱ ɔ kywɛɛ mò̱ maŋ‑ɔ e̱bɔɔre̱e̱, ba su a bo̱ sa mò̱.

ref: Her sufferings will frighten them, and they will stand at a distance and say, “Pity that great and powerful city! Pity Babylon! In a single hour her judgment has come.”
text: Ba ye̱re̱ ke̱fɔ ke̱fɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ maa kpa a bo̱ gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ‑ɔ ako̱. Na bo̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Daane̱ke̱na! Daane̱ke̱na! Babilɔŋ maŋ dabe̱ mumwii mɔ, dɔŋhwe̱re̱e̱ ko̱ŋko̱‑rɔ kpeŋ ne̱ fo̱ a kpuri!”

ref: Every merchant on earth will mourn, because there is no one to buy their goods.
text: Na swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ a ayawo̱gyipo̱‑ɔ mɔ su mò̱ kale, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ ɔ bo̱ maŋ amo̱‑rɔ, na ɔ sɔɔ bamo̱ atɔ.

ref: There won't be anyone to buy their gold, silver, jewels, pearls, fine linen, purple cloth, silk, scarlet cloth, sweet-smelling wood, fancy carvings of ivory and wood, as well as things made of bronze, iron, or marble.
text: Ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ ɔ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ɔ sɔɔ bamo̱ atanne̱ pipee na afufuri na abu yawo̱ le̱ŋsɛ na akukye‑ro ataŋgyi dame̱naŋsɛ na kyefuri ŋkpaŋ na awaagya ne̱ a du fe̱yɛ lɔŋkɔnkyuronkyu‑o, na amo̱ ne̱ aa pee kpɛye̱ kpɛye̱ kpɛye̱‑ɔ, na atɔ dame̱naŋsɛ yawo̱ le̱ŋsɛ ne̱ baa ba ayii ko̱ na kayo̱waa na agye̱re̱tɔ na ko̱nne̱ e̱be̱re̱ na afo̱re̱ fufuri gyorigyorisɛ bo̱ waa‑ɔ.

ref: No one will buy their cinnamon, spices, incense, myrrh, frankincense, wine, olive oil, fine flour, wheat, cattle, sheep, horses, chariots, slaves, and other humans.
text: Na e̱fo̱nɔ na nyoŋku na kakyanne̱ na ndwii na ko̱raa e̱wo̱re̱se̱ e̱wo̱re̱se̱ na nta na mfɔ na mpu na yaabraa na e̱naate̱ na e̱sanne̱ na e̱kpaŋa na itireelaa na anya pɛɛɛ i sii, na a ma nya asɔɔpo̱.

ref: Babylon, the things your heart desired have all escaped from you. Every luxury and all your glory will be lost forever. You will never get them back.
text: Ayawo̱gyipo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ a bo̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Atɔ timaa ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a fo̱ fo̱ se̱. Fo̱ kapo̱tɛɛ na atɔ dame̱naŋsɛ pɛɛɛ a fo̱ fo̱ se̱. Fo̱ maa lɛɛ ŋu amo̱, daa.”

ref: The merchants had become rich because of her. But when they saw her sufferings, they were terrified. They stood at a distance, crying and mourning.
text: Ayawo̱gyipo̱ ne̱ baa gyi yawo̱ maŋ amo̱‑rɔ nya atanne̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ e̱ ye̱re̱ ke̱fɔ ke̱fɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kufu de bamo̱ fe̱yɛ ba gyi mò̱ awo̱re̱fɔɔ‑ɔ ako̱. Na bo̱ su kale.

ref: Then they shouted, “Pity the great city of Babylon! She dressed in fine linen and wore purple and scarlet cloth. She had jewelry made of gold and precious stones and pearls.
text: Na bo̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Daane̱ke̱na! Daane̱ke̱na! Ke̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ase̱ŋ a to̱ maŋ dabe̱ amo̱‑ɔ! Fo̱ ne̱ fo̱ ase̱sɛ a dɛɛ buŋ kyefuri na awaagya ne̱ a du fe̱yɛ lɔŋkɔnkyuronkyu‑o, na amo̱ ne̱ aa pee kpɛye̱ kpɛye̱ kpɛye̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa ba atanne̱ pipee na abu dame̱naŋsɛ yawo̱ le̱ŋsɛ na akukye‑ro ataŋgyi dame̱naŋsɛ bo̱ buŋ bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ se̱‑ɔ.

ref: Yet in a single hour her riches disappeared.” Every ship captain and passenger and sailor stood at a distance, together with everyone who does business by traveling on the sea.
text: Ne̱ dɔŋhwe̱re̱e̱ ko̱ŋko̱‑rɔ kpeŋ fo̱ kapo̱tɛɛ dabe̱ mɔ a fo̱ fo̱ se̱ pɛɛɛ!” Na ntɛɛrɛɛ pɛɛɛ ne̱ bamo̱ kusuŋ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ so̱rɔ ayawo̱gyipo̱ e̱tɔso̱rɔ e̱ko̱re̱e̱‑rɔ le̱e̱ maŋ ko̱ se̱ yaa ɔko̱ se̱‑ɔ ye̱re̱ ke̱fɔ ke̱fɔ.

ref: When they saw the smoke from her fire, they shouted, “This was the greatest city ever!”
text: Bamo̱ ya ŋu e̱bɔɔre̱e̱ a le̱e̱ de̱e̱kpa ne̱ ɔ kywɛɛ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ, na bo̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Maŋ ko̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ, na o du fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ a maŋ dabe̱ mɔ.”

ref: They cried loudly, and in their sorrow they threw dust on their heads, as they said, “Pity the great city of Babylon! Everyone who sailed the seas became rich from her treasures. But in a single hour the city was destroyed.
text: Na bo̱ ba asare̱e̱ bo̱ gya aŋu, na bo̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Daane̱ke̱na! Daane̱ke̱na! Ke̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ase̱ŋ dabe̱ a to̱ maŋ dabe̱ mɔ‑ɔ. Mò̱ kapo̱tɛɛ ne̱ ka kyɔ ke̱baŋse̱‑ɔ se̱, bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ naa bo̱ de e̱ko̱re̱e̱ ba ba ba fe atɔ‑ɔ a kii atɔ awuye! Ne̱ dɔŋhwe̱re̱e̱ ko̱ŋko̱‑rɔ kpeŋ mò̱ kapo̱tɛɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a fo̱ mò̱ se̱!”

ref: A powerful angel then picked up a huge stone and threw it into the sea. The angel said, “This is how the great city of Babylon will be thrown down, never to rise again.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ dabe̱ ko̱ a yase̱ kibu ko̱ ne̱ ke̱mo̱ ke̱dabe̱ ta fe̱yɛ ke̱bwe̱e̱ dabe̱ dabe̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ twe̱e̱ waa ɔpo̱o̱‑rɔ tukum tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ba twe̱e̱ Babilɔŋ, maŋ dabe̱ amo̱, a bo̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱‑ɔ, na bo̱ ma lɛɛ ŋu mò̱ pɛɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: The music of harps and singers and of flutes and trumpets will no longer be heard. No workers will ever set up shop in that city, and the sound of grinding grain will be silenced forever.
text: Bamo̱ ne̱ ba da ikwaŋgyi‑o na bamo̱ ne̱ ba fo̱re̱ mbe̱re̱‑ɔ mɔ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ, hare̱e̱ ba waa iliŋ. Alo̱po̱ na aseripo̱ na apwɛɛpo̱ na abwaye̱po̱ na atɔ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ, hare̱e̱ ba waa kusuŋ. Bo̱ maa lɛɛ kwɛɛ abwaye mfe̱ŋ.

ref: Lamps will no longer shine anywhere in Babylon, and couples will never again say wedding vows there. Her merchants ruled the earth, and by her witchcraft she fooled all nations.
text: Bo̱ maa lɛɛ kyaŋŋe̱ fetiri mfe̱ŋ. Bo̱ maa lɛɛ taa ko̱ko̱fɔ ko̱ mfe̱ŋ. Fo̱ ayawo̱gyipo̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ bo̱ gye̱ ase̱sɛ dabe̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ. Fo̱ maa lɛɛ nya a fo̱ ba asibeŋ na aye̱ba na kanɔrɔkɔne̱ bo̱ pe̱nna kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ.

ref: On the streets of Babylon is found the blood of God's people and of his prophets, and everyone else.”
text: Ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a gyiiri fo̱ ke̱se̱bɔ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ fo̱ a baa mò̱ akyaamɛɛ na mò̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ lowi.”

ref: After this, I heard what sounded like a lot of voices in heaven, and they were shouting, “Praise the Lord! To our God belongs the glorious power to save,
text: Amo̱‑ɔ kamɛɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ a nu e̱lawo̱ dabe̱ ko̱ fe̱yɛ lamaŋ ko̱ a fɛɛ‑rɔ le̱e̱ so̱so̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱. Mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ane̱ ɔmo̱rɔwe̱po̱. Wuraa na ke̱yaale̱ŋ gye̱ mò̱ lee,

ref: because his judgments are honest and fair. That filthy prostitute ruined the earth with shameful deeds. But God has judged her and made her pay the price for murdering his servants.”
text: a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ o gyi ase̱sɛ ase̱ŋ kpa se̱. Opurimakye̱e̱ dabe̱ amo̱ a ba mò̱ kanyare̱kpa bo̱ waa kaye̱‑rɔ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ iyisi‑o si‑o, ne̱ Wuribware̱ a gyi mò̱ ase̱ŋ bu ke̱pɔ. Mò̱ a mɔɔ Wuribware̱ ayaafɔre̱ na mbre‑o si‑o, Wuribware̱ a ba do̱ŋdaa bo̱ gyiiri mò̱ ke̱se̱bɔ bo̱ teere.”

ref: Then the crowd shouted, “Praise the Lord! Smoke will never stop rising from her burning body.”
text: Ne̱ baa be̱e̱ fɛɛ‑rɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱. E̱bɔɔre̱e̱ ne̱ e̱e̱ le̱e̱ de̱e̱kpa ne̱ ɔ kywɛɛ maŋ dabe̱ amo̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ e̱ yɔ so̱so̱, na e̱ maa yɔwe̱ pɛɛɛ.”

ref: From the throne a voice said, “If you worship and fear our God, give praise to him, no matter who you are.”
text: Ne̱ bo̱re̱ ko̱ a le̱e̱ ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ ye̱re̱ kuwuregya‑ɔ ase̱‑ɔ ase̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ŋ yɛ, Wuribware̱ ayaafɔre̱ na mbre eee, mo̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i bu mò̱‑ɔ, abre̱sɛ na ŋyaagyi pɛɛɛ, mo̱nꞌ ba bo̱ kyo̱rɔ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱.”

ref: Then I heard what seemed to be a large crowd that sounded like a roaring flood and loud thunder all mixed together. They were saying, “Praise the Lord! Our Lord God All-Powerful now rules as king.
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a nu e̱lawo̱ ko̱ fe̱yɛ lamaŋ lee‑o, ne̱ e̱ ko̱ŋ i du fe̱yɛ awuriwuri a kyaare̱ wuuu‑o, na fe̱yɛ bware̱ a su‑ro gburi gburi gburi‑o. Ne̱ mo̱ a nu bamo̱ a fɛɛ‑rɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ kyo̱rɔ Wuribware̱, Bo̱-e̱le̱ŋ‑ɔ‑kyo̱ŋ‑ɔke̱maa-Bware̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ e̱ gye̱ owure.

ref: So we will be glad and happy and give him praise. The wedding day of the Lamb is here, and his bride is ready.
text: Mo̱nꞌ sa a a waa ane̱ kɔne̱, na ane̱ akatɔ gyi. Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ kyo̱rɔ mò̱ ke̱dabe̱‑ɔ. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kasanne̱gyii a ko̱ko̱fɔ‑ɔ saŋ a fo̱, ne̱ mò̱ ɔko̱fɔ‑ɔ a kyɔ waa siraa.

ref: She will be given a wedding dress made of pure and shining linen. This linen stands for the good things God's people have done.”
text: Ne̱ baa sa ɔko̱fɔ‑ɔ kyefuri kaare̱ dame̱naŋsɛ ne̱ o fwiiri pare̱pare̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ o buŋ.”

ref: Then the angel told me, “Put this in writing. God will bless everyone who is invited to the wedding feast of the Lamb.” The angel also said, “These things God has said are true.”
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Kyo̱rɛɛ amo̱‑ɔ. Bamo̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ bamo̱ kasanne̱gyii a ko̱ko̱fɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ e̱ nya ŋyure.” Ne̱ kabɔɔ‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ bo̱ tii si fe̱yɛ, “Wuribware̱ a ase̱ŋkpare̱gyi kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o ne̱e̱.”

ref: I knelt at the feet of the angel and began to worship him. But the angel said, “Don't do that! I am a servant, just like you and everyone else who tells about Jesus. Don't worship anyone but God. Everyone who tells about Jesus does it by the power of the Spirit.” The Rider on the White Horse
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a kpuni aŋurii a n suŋ mò̱. Amaa ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Oo daabii, fo̱ ma waa ane̱ŋ! Ŋ gbaa n gye̱ Wuribware̱ a ayaafɔre̱‑ɔ ɔko̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱‑ɔ na fo̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana ne̱ ba tɔwe̱ Yeesuu ase̱ŋ timaa, ne̱ bo̱ maa kiŋŋi kamɛɛ‑ɔ dɛɛ ne̱e̱. Wuribware̱ ne̱ fo̱ suŋ. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o ya lee Wuribware̱ ase̱ŋ bo̱ kaapo̱ mò̱ akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔ bo̱rɔ Yeesuu si o lee ase̱ŋ amo̱ a ɔ bo̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ aa fo̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana‑ɔ.” Kpaŋa fufuri a ɔde̱e̱po̱‑ɔ ase̱ŋ

ref: I looked and saw heaven was open, and a white horse was there. Its rider was called Faithful and True, and he is always fair when he judges or goes to war.
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a ŋu so̱so̱ a buŋŋi‑ro, ne̱ kpaŋa fufuri ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɔde̱e̱po̱ fe̱yɛ ‘Kase̱ŋtiŋ-wuye’ na ‘Ɔko̱ ne̱ kufu mo̱ŋ de mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ tɔwe̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ‑o’ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ. Mò̱ i gyi ase̱sɛ ase̱ŋ, o gyi amo̱ nɛɛnɛɛ, ne̱ ɔ maa gyi ase̱ŋ ke̱kpa se̱. Ne̱ ɔ naa ke̱naa‑rɔ o gyiiri abɔye̱waapo̱ ke̱se̱bɔ.

ref: He had eyes like flames of fire, and he was wearing a lot of crowns. His name was written on him, but he was the only one who knew what the name meant.
text: Mò̱ akatɔ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ agyabreŋ‑o. Ne̱ o buŋ awurepa bwe̱e̱tɔ. Baa kyo̱rɛɛ ke̱ŋasɛnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ nyi ke̱mo̱ kaase̱‑ɔ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ mò̱ se̱, amɔ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ o nyi ke̱mo̱ kaase̱.

ref: The rider wore a robe that was covered with blood, and he was known as “The Word of God.”
text: Ne̱ mbo̱gya a miiri mò̱ waagya ne̱ o buŋ‑o si pɛɛɛ. Ba te̱e̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ Abware̱se̱ŋ.

ref: He was followed by armies from heaven that rode on white horses and were dressed in pure white linen.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ a mbɔɔ anaapo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ buŋ buŋ kyefuri e̱kaare̱ timaa pare̱pare̱pare̱, ne̱ baa de̱e̱ e̱kpaŋa fufuri bo̱ gya mò̱ se̱.

ref: From his mouth a sharp sword went out to attack the nations. He will rule them with an iron rod and will show the fierce anger of God All-Powerful by trampling the grapes in the pit where wine is made.
text: Ne̱ ke̱paŋ yayasɛ ko̱ kuŋu ase̱ te mò̱ kanɔ‑rɔ. Ke̱paŋ amo̱ ne̱ ɔ ba a ɔ bo̱ kɔ gyi nde̱ se̱ awuye pɛɛɛ se̱. Na ɔ ba mò̱ kuwureyii bo̱ bwee bamo̱ e̱maŋ waa kyikpuri kyikpuri kyikpuri. Na o kyikye Wuribware̱ ado̱ŋ se̱ mɔɔ, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ase̱sɛ i kyikye bobe agyi si na amo̱ nkyu nya le̱e̱‑ɔ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ waa na ɔ bo̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ Bo̱-e̱le̱ŋ‑ɔ‑kyo̱ŋ‑ɔke̱maa-Bware̱ duŋ a fwii bamo̱ se̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: On the part of the robe that covered his thigh was written, “KING OF KINGS AND Lord OF LordS.”
text: Ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ ke̱ŋasɛnyare̱ ko̱ mò̱ waagya‑ɔ se̱ na mò̱ gyaŋ se̱ fe̱yɛ, “Awure‑ro‑owure-na-anyaŋpe̱‑rɔ-nyaŋpe̱.”

ref: I then saw an angel standing on the sun, and he shouted to all the birds flying in the sky, “Come and join in God's great feast!
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a ŋu Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ ye̱re̱ kyɔwe̱ se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ faa‑rɔ keŋkeŋ tɔwe̱ gywii mbo̱gyii pɛɛɛ ne̱ ba fuwi so̱so̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mbo̱gyii, mo̱nꞌ bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱ eee. Mo̱nꞌ gyi ateese ne̱ Wuribware̱ a waa sa mo̱ne̱‑ɔ ooo.

ref: You can eat the flesh of kings, rulers, leaders, horses, riders, free people, slaves, important people, and everyone else.”
text: Mo̱nꞌ bo̱ gyi awure na anaapo̱ abre̱sɛ na ayaale̱ŋpo̱ na e̱kpaŋa na bamo̱ ade̱e̱po̱ na ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ, agyi na anya na abre̱sɛ na ŋyaagyi, afuniŋ.”

ref: I also saw the beast and all kings of the earth come together. They fought against the rider on the white horse and against his army.
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a ŋu mpase̱ wuye‑o na kaye̱‑rɔ a awure‑o na bamo̱ anaapo̱‑ɔ a gyaŋŋe̱ a bo̱ kɔ mò̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ na Wuribware̱ a mbɔɔ anaapo̱‑ɔ.

ref: But the beast was captured and so was the false prophet. This is the same prophet who had worked miracles for the beast, so he could fool everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol. The beast and the false prophet were thrown alive into a lake of burning sulfur.
text: Ne̱ Abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ na Wuribware̱ a mbɔɔ anaapo̱‑ɔ a kɔ kra mpase̱ wuye‑o, mò̱ aa mò̱ anaapo̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ baa be̱e̱ kɔ kra mò̱ ne̱ ɔ yɛ ɔ gye̱ Wuribware̱ a akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ ɔko̱‑ɔ, amaa ɔ gye̱ ɔye̱bapo̱ ne̱e̱. Ɔye̱bapo̱ mɔ ya ye̱re̱ mpase̱ wuye‑o ayaa‑rɔ waa akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ bo̱ pe̱nna bamo̱ ne̱ baa kyaŋ kabo̱ amo̱ a kakyane̱e̱‑ɔ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ bamo̱ nsɛkpe̱e̱nɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa suŋ ke̱tɔ serisɛ ne̱ kaa le̱e̱ mò̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ baa kra mpase̱ wuye‑o na ɔye̱bapo̱ kyaamɛɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa twe̱e̱ waa ke̱de̱e̱kpɛɛtekii ne̱ ke̱ e̱ kywɛɛ na kafirewuta tii si‑o‑ro.

ref: But the rest of their army was killed by the sword that came from the mouth of the rider on the horse. Then birds stuffed themselves on the dead bodies.
text: Ke̱paŋ ne̱ ke̱mo̱ kuŋu ase̱ te mò̱ ne̱ ɔ de̱e̱ kpaŋa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ kanɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ mɔɔ mpase̱ wuye a asoogyaa awuye‑o. Ne̱ mbo̱gyii‑o pɛɛɛ a bo̱ gyi bamo̱ afuniŋ‑o kpo̱ne̱.

ref: I saw thrones, and sitting on those thrones were the ones who had been given the right to judge. I also saw the souls of the people who had their heads cut off because they had told about Jesus and preached God's message. They were the same ones who had not worshiped the beast or the idol, and they had refused to let its mark be put on their foreheads or hands. They will come to life and rule with Christ for
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a ŋu ŋwuregya na bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ te mmo̱ se̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ baa sa bamo̱ e̱le̱ŋ fe̱yɛ bo̱ gyi ase̱sɛ ase̱ŋ, na bo̱ bu ke̱pɔ bɛɛɛ ke̱be̱ŋ. Ne̱ mo̱ a ŋu bamo̱ ne̱ baa kperi Yeesuu na abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ ko̱tɔwe̱ se̱ tuwi bamo̱ aŋu‑o. Bamo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ baa kine kusuŋ mpase̱ wuye‑o na ke̱tɔ serisɛ ne̱ kaa le̱e̱ mò̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa be̱e̱ kine ke̱kyaŋŋe̱ mò̱ kakyane̱e̱ bamo̱ nsɛkpe̱e̱nɔ bɛɛɛ agyiselɔkwɛɛ‑rɔ se̱. Ne̱ baa kyiŋŋi, ne̱ baa nya ŋkpa, ne̱ bamo̱ aa Yeesuu a gyi kuwure nsu kakpe̱ŋ.

ref: These people are the first to be raised to life, and they are especially blessed and holy. The second death has no power over them. They will be priests for God and Christ and will rule with them for
text: Bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ tii kuwukyiŋŋi ne̱ ke̱ gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a lee‑o ne̱e̱, na bo̱ nya ŋyure. Ke̱se̱bɔgyiiri nyɔse̱po̱ mo̱ŋ de bamo̱ se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ. Bamo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ba kii Wuribware̱ na Kristoo alɔŋŋɔpo̱, na bamo̱ aa Kristoo gyi kuwure nsu kakpe̱ŋ‑ɔ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kɔ gyi Ɔbɔnsam se̱‑ɔ

ref: He will fool the countries of Gog and Magog, which are at the far ends of the earth, and their people will follow him into battle. They will have as many followers as there are grains of sand along the beach,
text: Na ɔ yɔ kaye̱‑rɔ to̱ŋ ke̱maa ya pe̱nna ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ. Ase̱sɛ amo̱ anyare̱ e̱ gye̱ Gogo na Magogo. Na ɔ ba bamo̱ bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱, na bo̱ ke̱re̱ ke̱naa. Bo̱ de̱ŋ bo̱ kyɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ fe̱yɛ ɔpo̱o̱‑rɔ a ke̱se̱pu‑o.

ref: and they will march all the way across the earth. They will surround the camp of God's people and the city God loves. But fire will come down from heaven and destroy the whole army.
text: Ne̱ baa saŋŋe̱‑rɔ bo̱rɔ swe̱e̱re̱ se̱, ne̱ baa muruwaa Wuribware̱ ase̱sɛ kadaŋ, maŋ ne̱ ɔ kpa mò̱ ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ. Amaa de̱e̱kpa a le̱e̱ so̱so̱ bo̱ kywɛɛ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ mɔɔ.

ref: Then the devil who fooled them will be thrown into the lake of fire and burning sulfur. He will be there with the beast and the false prophet, and they will be in pain day and night forever and ever. The Judgment at the Great White Throne
text: Ne̱ Ɔbɔnsam ne̱ ɔɔ pe̱nna bamo̱‑ɔ mɔ, baa twe̱e̱ mò̱ bo̱ waa ke̱de̱e̱kpɛɛtekii na kafirewuta‑o‑ro mfe̱ŋ ne̱ baa dɛɛ twe̱e̱ mpase̱ wuye‑o na ɔye̱bapo̱ kyaamɛɛ‑ɔ waa‑rɔ‑ɔ. Na ŋkee bo̱ di de̱e̱kpa‑ɔ‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ mpase̱ na kanye nsu pɛɛɛ. Kase̱ŋgyi ne̱ ka gye̱ lalalo̱we̱‑ɔ

ref: I saw a great white throne with someone sitting on it. Earth and heaven tried to run away, but there was no place for them to go.
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a ŋu kuwuregya dabe̱ fufuri pare̱pare̱pare̱ ko̱ na mò̱ ne̱ o te ke̱mo̱ se̱‑ɔ. Mò̱ a te mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ swe̱e̱re̱ na so̱so̱ a ŋaa. Ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ ŋu amo̱, daa.

ref: I also saw all the dead people standing in front of that throne. Every one of them was there, no matter who they had once been. Several books were opened, and then the book of life was opened. The dead were judged by what those books said they had done.
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a ŋu bamo̱ ne̱ baa wu‑o abre̱sɛ na ŋyaagyi, na bo̱ ye̱re̱ kuwuregya‑ɔ akatɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ Wuribware̱ a mbɔɔ‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ a buŋŋi e̱wo̱re̱‑rɔ. Ne̱ baa be̱e̱ buŋŋi wo̱re̱ ko̱‑rɔ, mfe̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ de ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ anyare̱‑ɔ. Baa gyi bamo̱ ne̱ baa wu‑o ase̱ŋ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔke̱maa mɔ a kye̱na kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ bo̱rɔ‑ɔ, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa e̱wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: The sea gave up the dead people who were in it, and death and its kingdom also gave up their dead. Then everyone was judged by what they had done.
text: Ne̱ alowipo̱ pɛɛɛ a kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro, hare̱e̱ bamo̱ ne̱ baa wu swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ ooo, nkyu‑ro ooo. Baa le̱e̱ akye̱ŋaŋpo̱‑rɔ ba bo̱ ye̱re̱ se̱ŋgyikpa‑ɔ. Ne̱ baa ba ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔke̱maa a waa saŋ ne̱ ɔɔ kye̱na swe̱e̱re̱ mɔ se̱‑ɔ bo̱ gyi mò̱ ase̱ŋ.

ref: Afterwards, death and its kingdom were thrown into the lake of fire. This is the second death.
text: Ne̱ baa twe̱e̱ lowi na akye̱ŋaŋpo̱‑rɔ bo̱ waa ke̱de̱e̱kpɛɛtekii‑o‑ro. De̱e̱kpa mɔ‑rɔ e̱ gye̱ ke̱se̱bɔgyiiri nyɔse̱po̱‑ɔ.

ref: Anyone whose name wasn't written in the book of life was thrown into the lake of fire.
text: Ne̱ baa twe̱e̱ ɔke̱maa mɔ ne̱ mò̱ ke̱nyare̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱ ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ bo̱ waa ke̱de̱e̱kpɛɛtekii‑o‑ro.

ref: I heard a loud voice shout from the throne: God's home is now with his people. He will live with them, and they will be his own. Yes, God will make his home among his people.
text: Ne̱ mo̱ a nu ɔko̱ a ye̱re̱ kuwuregya‑ɔ ase̱ faa‑rɔ keŋkeŋ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mbe̱yɔmɔ, ase̱sɛ aye̱ na Wuribware̱ aye̱ a kii ko̱ko̱ŋko̱. Mò̱ aa bamo̱ e̱ kye̱na abɛɛ ase̱, na bo̱ kii mò̱ ase̱sɛ. Bamo̱ aa Wuribware̱ fɔŋfɔŋ e̱ kye̱na, na o kii bamo̱ nyaŋpe̱.

ref: He will wipe all tears from their eyes, and there will be no more death, suffering, crying, or pain. These things of the past are gone forever.
text: Ɔ kpɛɛ bamo̱ akyukyu pɛɛɛ. Na lowi na kabo̱re̱rɔduŋwi na kaye̱e̱gya gye̱ kɛɛ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ atɔ de̱daa‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a kyo̱ŋ a yii.”

ref: Then the one sitting on the throne said: I am making everything new. Write down what I have said. My words are true and can be trusted.
text: Ne̱ mò̱ ne̱ o te kuwuregya‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ yɛ, “Mbe̱yɔmɔ mo̱ e̱ waa ke̱tɔ ke̱maa mɔ ke̱po̱pwɛɛ.” Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Kyo̱rɛɛ ase̱ŋ mɔ ne̱ fo̱ a nu‑o bo̱ be̱ya, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ ase̱ŋ pɛɛɛ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ, ne̱ ɔke̱maa e̱ taare̱ a ɔ taa mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ amo̱ se̱.”

ref: All who win the victory will be given these blessings. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ kɔ Ɔbɔnsam a o gyi si‑o e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ nya ane̱ŋ a ŋyure‑o. Na ɔ te̱e̱ mo̱, Wuribware̱, ɛ mò̱ nyaŋpe̱, na mò̱‑ɔ n te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ mo̱ gyi.

ref: But I will tell you what will happen to cowards and to everyone who is unfaithful or dirty-minded or who murders or is sexually immoral or uses witchcraft or worships idols or tells lies. They will be thrown into that lake of fire and burning sulfur. This is the second death. The New Jerusalem
text: Amaa bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ maa nya ane̱ŋ a ŋyure‑o e̱ gye̱ bamo̱ ne̱ kufu de bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ kii Kristoo awuye‑o, na adiburopo̱, na bamo̱ ne̱ bamo̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ de iyisi‑o, na bamo̱ ne̱ baa mɔɔ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ, na bamo̱ ne̱ baa waa kakye̱e̱kpa na kanyare̱kpa‑ɔ, na bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ de asibeŋ‑o, na akpe, na bamo̱ ne̱ ba suŋ ikisi‑o, na aye̱bapo̱ pɛɛɛ. Ba twe̱e̱ mfaanɛɛ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ a bo̱ bo̱ waa ke̱de̱e̱kpɛɛtekii na kafirewuta‑o‑ro. Amo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ke̱se̱bɔgyiiri nyɔse̱po̱‑ɔ.”

ref: I saw one of the seven angels who had the bowls filled with the seven last terrible troubles. The angel came to me and said, “Come on! I will show you the one who will be the bride and wife of the Lamb.”
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ a mbɔɔ nsunoo ne̱ bo̱ de nkyaŋse̱gyii na ase̱ŋ asunoo lalalo̱we̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ ɔko̱ a ba mo̱ ase̱ bo̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ba a ŋ kaapo̱ fo̱ kasanne̱gyii a ɔko̱fɔ‑ɔ.”

ref: Then with the help of the Spirit, he took me to the top of a very high mountain. There he showed me the holy city of Jerusalem coming down from God in heaven.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o a ywe̱e̱ mo̱ se̱, ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ amo̱ a so̱rɔ mo̱ yaa ke̱be̱e̱ so̱swe̱e̱ ko̱ katwe̱e̱‑rɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ ba Yɛro̱salɛm, Wuribware̱ a maŋ ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ so̱so̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱ ɔ ba‑ɔ, bo̱ kaapo̱ mo̱.

ref: The glory of God made the city bright. It was dazzling and crystal clear like a precious jasper stone.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ e̱ sa ne̱ maŋ‑ɔ e̱ to̱re̱ se̱. Maŋ amo̱ e̱ to̱re̱ se̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kibu yawo̱ le̱ŋsɛ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ gyaasipaa ne̱ ku di kipipee‑o, ne̱ ke̱ e̱ to̱re̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ diki‑o.

ref: The city had a high and thick wall with twelve gates, and each one of them was guarded by an angel. On each of the gates was written the name of one of the twelve tribes of Israel.
text: Maŋ‑ɔ de kideŋbee dabe̱ bo̱ muruwaa mò̱. Ne̱ baa lee abunogyi kudu anyɔ. Ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ye̱re̱ abunogyi‑o ke̱ke̱maa ase̱ ɔ de̱e̱re̱ se̱. Ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ Isireelii awuye a nsuro kudu ŋnyɔ‑ɔ anyare̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ abunogyi‑o si. A kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kasuro ke̱maa de kibunogyi ko̱ŋko̱.

ref: Three of these gates were on the east, three were on the north, three more were on the south, and the other three were on the west.
text: Ke̱be̱gya ke̱maa de abunogyi asa. Asa bo̱ kyɔwe̱ le̱e̱kpa a keri‑o, ne̱ asa bo̱ so̱so̱‑rɔ a keri‑o, ne̱ asa bo̱ kaase̱‑rɔ a keri‑o, ne̱ asa bo̱ kyɔwe̱ twɛɛkpa a keri‑o.

ref: The city was built on twelve foundation stones. On each of the stones was written the name of one of the Lamb's twelve apostles.
text: Maŋ amo̱ a kideŋbee‑o afo̱re̱ kudu anyɔ ne̱ baa ba bo̱ pwɛɛ. Ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ kasanne̱gyii a ayaa‑rɔ aye̱re̱po̱ kudu anyɔ‑ɔ anyare̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ dɔŋŋɔ se̱.

ref: The angel who spoke to me had a gold measuring stick to measure the city and its gates and its walls.
text: Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ne̱ ɔɔ sa se̱ŋsa mo̱ ase̱‑ɔ de atanne̱ pipee kayii kare̱‑atɔ ko̱, na ɔ ba bo̱ kare̱ maŋ amo̱ na kideŋbee‑o na abunogyi‑o.

ref: The city was shaped like a cube, because it was just as high as it was wide. When the angel measured the city, it was about
text: Maŋ‑ɔ a bɔŋ, ne̱ mò̱ e̱swe̱e̱ na ŋkpaŋŋe̱ ta ne̱e̱. Ne̱ kabɔɔ‑ɔ a taa mò̱ kayii‑o bo̱ kare̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ maŋ‑ɔ ta‑ɔ. Mò̱ a bo̱ kare̱‑ɔ, oo ŋu fe̱yɛ maŋ‑ɔ e̱swe̱e̱ gye̱ se̱sɛ e̱swe̱e̱ kakpe̱ŋkɛɛ na ŋkpe̱ŋ alafa anyɔ (

ref: The wall was built of jasper, and the city was made of pure gold, clear as crystal.
text: Ke̱fo̱re̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ gyaasipaa ne̱ ku du kipipee‑o, ne̱ baa bo̱ pwɛɛ kideŋbee‑o, ne̱ baa ba atanne̱ pipee ne̱ a to̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ bo̱ pwɛɛ maŋ‑ɔ fɔŋfɔŋ.

ref: Each of the twelve gates was a solid pearl. The streets of the city were made of pure gold, clear as crystal.
text: Ne̱ abunogyi kudu anyɔ‑ɔ ke̱ke̱maa, baa ba akukye‑ro ke̱taŋgyi dabe̱ dame̱naŋsɛ ko̱ bo̱ pwɛɛ. Ne̱ baa ba atanne̱ pipee ne̱ a to̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ bo̱ waa kabo̱re̱ dabe̱ ne̱ ka da ka bo̱rɔ maŋ‑ɔ nse̱na‑ɔ.

ref: I did not see a temple there. The Lord God All-Powerful and the Lamb were its temple.
text: Wuribware̱ suŋkpa mo̱ŋ bo̱ maŋ amo̱‑rɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Bo̱-e̱le̱ŋ‑ɔ‑kyo̱ŋ‑ɔke̱maa-Bware̱ na kasanne̱gyii‑o fɔŋfɔŋ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ.

ref: And the city did not need the sun or the moon. The glory of God was shining on it, and the Lamb was its light.
text: Ne̱ a mo̱ŋ lɛɛ a tiri fe̱yɛ kyɔwe̱ le̱e̱ mpase̱, bɛɛɛ kiferi laŋŋe̱ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ kanye, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ fɔŋfɔŋ e̱ laŋŋe̱‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ, ne̱ kasanne̱gyii‑o mɔ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ fetiri‑o.

ref: Nations will walk by the light of that city, and kings will bring their riches there.
text: Mò̱ ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ ne̱ kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ e̱ ba a bo̱ bo̱ nare̱‑rɔ. Na kaye̱‑rɔ awure taa bamo̱ mpo̱tɛɛ baa maŋ amo̱‑rɔ.

ref: Its gates are always open during the day, and night never comes.
text: Maŋ amo̱ a e̱po̱ne̱‑ɔ de̱ŋ e̱ ye̱re̱ ne̱e̱. Bo̱ maa tii e̱mo̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mfe̱ŋ mo̱ŋ de kanye.

ref: The glorious treasures of nations will be brought into the city.
text: Kaye̱‑rɔ ase̱sɛ e̱ baa bamo̱ mpo̱tɛɛ na atanne̱ maŋ amo̱‑rɔ.

ref: But nothing unworthy will be allowed to enter. No one who is dirty-minded or who tells lies will be there. Only those whose names are written in the Lamb's book of life will be in the city.
text: Amaa ɔke̱maa ne̱ o de iyisi‑o maa lwee mò̱‑rɔ. Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ waa apeerese̱ŋ‑ɔ, na ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ ba aye̱ba‑ɔ, be̱e̱ ɔ maa lwee mfe̱ŋ. Amɔ bamo̱ ne̱ kasanne̱gyii‑o a kyo̱rɛɛ bamo̱ anyare̱ waa mò̱ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ de ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ dooo e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ba lwee maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: The angel showed me a river that was crystal clear, and its waters gave life. The river came from the throne where God and the Lamb were seated.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ‑ɔ a kaapo̱ mo̱ bo̱ŋ ko̱ ne̱ mò̱ nkyu e̱ sa ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ. Ne̱ n du kre̱ŋkre̱ŋ, ne̱ ŋ ya le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ na kasanne̱gyii a kuwuregya‑ɔ‑rɔ m ba.

ref: Then it flowed down the middle of the city's main street. On each side of the river are trees that grow a different kind of fruit each month of the year. The fruit gives life, and the leaves are used as medicine to heal the nations.
text: Ne̱ nkyu amo̱ a paye̱ maŋ amo̱ nse̱na a kabo̱re̱ dabe̱‑ɔ‑rɔ n kyo̱ŋ m ba. Ayii ne̱ a swɛɛ agyi, ne̱ a sa ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ ye̱re̱ bo̱ŋ‑ɔ ke̱bare̱ ke̱maa se̱, ne̱ a swɛɛ iluwi kudu e̱nyɔ kasu ke̱maa. Bo̱ de amo̱ afe̱raa ba kya alɔgyi ke̱maa ne̱ a tɔɔraa nde̱ se̱ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ.

ref: God's curse will no longer be on the people of that city. He and the Lamb will be seated there on their thrones, and its people will worship God
text: Wuribware̱ maa lɛɛ sa a ase̱sɛ kisi kiyii amo̱ agyi kigyi. Wuribware̱ na kasanne̱gyii‑o kuwure bo̱ maŋ amo̱‑rɔ ne̱e̱. Na mò̱ ayaafɔre̱ suŋ mò̱ mfe̱ŋ.

ref: and will see him face to face. God's name will be written on the foreheads of the people.
text: Ba ŋu Wuribware̱ akatɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ bo̱ de mò̱ ke̱nyare̱ bamo̱ nsɛkpe̱e̱nɔ.

ref: Never again will night appear, and no one who lives there will ever need a lamp or the sun. The Lord God will be their light, and they will rule forever. The Coming of Christ
text: Kanye mo̱ŋ lɛɛ ka bo̱‑rɔ. Ifetiri ase̱ŋ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ a tiri bamo̱, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ kyɔwe̱ ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ mo̱ŋ tiri bamo̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ gye̱ bamo̱ ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ. Na Wuribware̱ sa bamo̱ kuwure ne̱ ke̱ mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ. Yeesuu ke̱ba‑rɔ ase̱ŋ

ref: Then I was told: These words are true and can be trusted. The Lord God controls the spirits of his prophets, and he is the one who sent his angel to show his servants what must happen very soon.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ase̱ŋ mɔ gye̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ, ne̱ ba taare̱ a bo̱ kɔɔre̱ amo̱ gyi. Ne̱ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Wuribware̱ ne̱ oo lee mò̱ ase̱ŋ bo̱ kaapo̱ mò̱ akyaamɛɛ ne̱ baa dɛɛ kye̱na‑ɔ a suŋ mò̱ kabɔɔ fe̱yɛ ɔ ya kaapo̱ mò̱ ayaafɔre̱‑ɔ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ ba a ke̱ bo̱ waa mbe̱yɔmɔ dɛɛ‑ɔ.”

ref: Remember, I am coming soon! God will bless everyone who pays attention to the message of this book.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ nu mo̱ ase̱! Mo̱ e̱ ba mbe̱yɔmɔ dɛɛ. Bamo̱ ne̱ ba gya mo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ ne̱ a bo̱ wo̱re̱ mɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ e̱ nya ŋyure!”

ref: My name is John, and I am the one who heard and saw these things. Then after I had heard and seen all this, I knelt down and began to worship at the feet of the angel who had shown it to me.
text: Mo̱, Yohanee, e̱ gye̱ ne̱ mo̱ a nu, ne̱ mo̱ a ŋu amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ. Ne̱ mo̱ a kpuni aŋurii kabɔɔ‑ɔ ayaa‑rɔ, na n suŋ mò̱.

ref: But the angel said, Don't do that! I am a servant, just like you. I am the same as a follower or a prophet or anyone else who obeys what is written in this book. God is the one you should worship.
text: Amaa ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Oo daabii, fo̱ ma suŋ mo̱, suŋ Wuribware̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ m fe̱raa, n gye̱ Wuribware̱ a ayaafɔre̱‑ɔ ɔko̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ aa fo̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana akyaamɛɛ na bamo̱ pɛɛɛ ne̱ bo̱ gya ase̱ŋ ne̱ a bo̱ wo̱re̱ mɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ.”

ref: Evil people will keep on being evil, and everyone who is dirty-minded will still be dirty-minded. But good people will keep on doing right, and God's people will always be holy.
text: Saŋ amo̱ ya fo̱, ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ gye̱ ɔbɔye̱waapo̱‑ɔ kya se̱ waa e̱bɔye̱, ne̱ mò̱ ne̱ o de iyisi‑o me̱raa se̱ waa iyisi. Ne̱ mò̱ ne̱ ɔ waa ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ‑ɔ kya se̱ waa ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ. Ne̱ mò̱ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ se̱sɛ timaa‑o kya se̱ waa itimaa.”

ref: Then I was told: I am coming soon! And when I come, I will reward everyone for what they have done.
text: Yeesuu yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ nu mo̱ ase̱! Mo̱ e̱ ba mbe̱yɔmɔ dɛɛ! Mo̱ e̱ ka ɔke̱maa ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ waa mò̱ asuŋ‑o ko̱kɔ.

ref: God will bless all who have washed their robes. They will each have the right to eat fruit from the tree that gives life, and they can enter the gates of the city.
text: Bamo̱ ne̱ baa fwe̱e̱ bamo̱ atɔ buŋsɛ waa siraa‑o e̱ nya ŋyure, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ba nya kpa a bo̱ lwee maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ, na bo̱ gyi kiyii ne̱ ke̱ e̱ sa ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ agyi‑o.

ref: I am Jesus! And I am the one who sent my angel to tell all of you these things for the churches. I am David's Great Descendant, and I am also the bright morning star.
text: Mo̱, Yeesuu, ya suŋ mo̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ nare̱ tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ gywii asɔre̱e̱ asɔre̱e̱ awuye. Mo̱ anyare̱ e̱ gye̱ ‘Owure-Deefid-mò̱-ke̱nanagyi,’ na ‘Ɔto̱re̱.’ ”

ref: Here is my warning for everyone who hears the prophecies in this book: If you add anything to them, God will make you suffer all the terrible troubles written in this book.
text: Mo̱, Yohanee, i yii ɔke̱maa ne̱ o nu abware̱se̱ŋ ne̱ a bo̱ wo̱re̱ mɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱ fe̱yɛ, ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ taa ko̱tɔko̱ a ɔ bo̱ tii ase̱ŋ mɔ se̱‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ mɔ e̱ sa a o tii bamo̱ ne̱ ba gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ, ne̱ mo̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ amo̱ ase̱ŋ wo̱re̱ mɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱.

ref: If you take anything away from these prophecies, God will not let you have part in the life-giving tree and in the holy city described in this book.
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ o lee ko̱tɔko̱ a ɔ le̱e̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱ mɔ‑rɔ a ase̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ maa sa a o tii bamo̱ ne̱ ba nya kpa na bo̱ kye̱na mò̱ maŋ ne̱ mo̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱. Na ɔ ma nya gyi kiyii ne̱ ke̱ e̱ sa ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ agyi‑o.

ref: I pray that the Lord Jesus will be kind to all of you.
text: Mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Yeesuu Kristoo yure mò̱ ase̱sɛ. Amɛye̱.
